description:
Theme

Unicoin is a whimsical, unicorn-themed game where players harness luck and strategy to rid themselves of coins while navigating magical surprises. Set in a playful world of unicorns the game brings charm, chaos, and excitement to every turn.

Gameplay

Players take turns rolling dice to place coins on numbered spaces. If a roll lands on an occupied space (a bust), they must collect all the coins there. Special Power Coins introduce unique abilities, adding twists like doubling placements, skipping turns, or sending coins straight to Unicorn Paradise —a mystical realm where coins are removed from play. A secondary die introduces extra risk and reward when collecting coins.

Goal

The goal is to be the first one to get rid of your herd of Unicoins.

On November 25, 1950, the Chinese People's Volunteer Army launched the second campaign. After the Dechuan battle, the 38th Army of the Volunteer Army advanced rapidly towards Kaesong. In order to plug the gap in the battle and prevent the Volunteer Army from advancing westward, the Turkish Brigade built defensive positions at Gariling and Yangzhan east of Kaesong and tried to hold on. The 114th Division of the 38th Army of the Volunteer Army launched a fierce attack and engaged in a fierce battle with the Turkish Brigade from the 27th to the 29th. They finally broke through the Turkish Brigade's defense line and inflicted heavy damage on it, successfully realizing their intention of infiltration. However, the tenacity of the Turkish Brigade in this battle left a deep impression on the Volunteer Army, and from then on they regarded the Turkish Army as the main force of the United Nations Army alongside the US and British Armies.

There are two widely circulated stories about this battle. One is that before the Volunteer Army and the Turkish Brigade exchanged fire, the Turkish army mistakenly took the retreating South Korean troops as the Volunteer Army and severely damaged them, which became a famous misunderstanding in the second battle. The other is that many years later, before the start of the self-defense counterattack against India, the People's Liberation Army used the Turkish Army as a reference for its estimate of the strength of the Indian Army, believing that the Indian Army's combat effectiveness should be inferior to the Turkish Army and stronger than the Kuomintang Army. Of course, in actual combat, the Indian army was not even as good as the national army, but this also shows from one side that the Turks in Korea were indeed brave.

1950年11月25日、中國人民志願軍發起第二次戰役 ,德川戰鬥以後 ' 志願軍第3 8軍向價川急進 為了堵住戰役缺口 ,阻止志願軍西進 土 耳其旅在價川以東的嘎日嶺、陽站構築了防禦陣地試圖堅守。志願軍3 8 軍1 1 4師所部勇猛衝擊 ,與土耳其旅展開激戰,從2 7日戰至2 9日, 終於突破土耳其旅防線 ,並對其予以重創 ,成功實現了穿插意圖 ,但此次 戰鬥中土耳其旅的頑強給了志願軍深刻的印象 ,從此將土軍與美英軍並列 視為聯合國軍主力 。 關於這場戰鬥 , 還有兩個廣為流傳的故事 , 一是志願軍與土耳其旅交 火前 ,土軍誤將潰退的南朝鮮部隊視為志願軍井予以重創 ,成為二次戰役 中一個著名的烏龍 二是多年以後的對印自衛反擊戰開戰前 ,解放軍對印 軍的實力估計還使用了土耳其軍作為參照 , 認為印軍戰鬥力應該不如土耳 其軍强於國民黨部隊 。當然 ,實戰正面了印軍甚至不如國軍 ,但這也從一 個側面說明瞭當年在朝鮮土耳其人的確堪稱勇猛
 更多精彩的中文版桌面戰棋請淘寶搜索 : 戰鼓遊戲

The Battle of Chipyong-ri was an offensive battle launched by Chinese People's Volunteer Army troops against a fortified field position in Chipyong-ri, Yangpyeong County, Gyeonggi Province, in the early stages of the fourth campaign to resist U.S. aggression and aid Korea. After paying a heavy price, the Volunteer Army once broke through the UN's defensive positions, but due to poor coordination and lack of targeted tactics, they were eventually forced to withdraw from the battle under the resolute resistance of the UN Army. The fighting situation of this battle was unprecedentedly fierce, and it was the first time that the volunteer army failed in its attack on the US military in a large-scale combat in the War to Resist U.S. Aggression and Aid Korea. This battle served as the dividing line, marking the end of the Volunteer Army's large-scale mobile offensive phase, and the Korean battlefield then entered the positional defense phase.

砥平裏戰鬥 ,是抗美援朝第四次戰役初期 ,誌願軍部隊對 京畿道楊平郡砥平裏進行的壹次對堅固野戰陣地進攻戰鬥 。 誌願軍在付出重大犧牲後,壹度突破聯合國軍防禦陣地,但 因協同失調、戰法針對性不強 ,在聯合國軍堅決抵抗下 ,最 終被迫撤出戰鬥 。這場戰鬥戰況空前激烈,是抗美援朝戰場 上誌願軍在較大規模作戰中第壹次對美軍攻擊失利 。以此戰 為分界線 ,標誌著誌願軍大規模運動進攻階段的結束,朝鮮 戰場此後轉入了陣地防禦戰階段 。

F1 Roll & Race: Street Circuits is the expansion of F1 Roll & Race. It incorporates:

     7 New circuits (Bakú, Las Vegas, Mónaco, Valencia, Miami, Marina Bay and Yas Marina).
     1st Player Card.
     6 cards for solo mode
     New default driver card.


—description from the designer

A campaign of 6 scenarios in which the rules and victory conditions will change, to achieve greater replayability.
Add new heroes with new abilities and objectives.
Feat cards give you access to new objectives to increase your score. 
Now with booster cards, players can interact with each other using an ability at the end of the turn, but giving points to the opponents next to them.

In Spy Guy Germany you become a detective trying to find the person responsible for a series of crimes against Germany. Find on the map as many clues as possible, which will help you find the villain Dr. Moritz before he flee. Spy Guy is a cooperative game that tests your observation skills and collaboration between players. If you manage to catch up Doktor Moritz you win the game!

Behold, Princess Beauty "Deluxe" and her power of Kingmaker!


     This version of Beauty is only compatible with Rebel Princess Deluxe Edition.
     Beauty Deluxe is not a card; She's a punchboard tile exactly like the other 12 princesses in the Deluxe Edition.
     Beauty will have embossed art elements allowing sunlight to shimmer on her beloved roses.


Kingmaker: Immediately after you play a card, switch any 2 cards (except the Frog) that have been played.

boardgame: Li-La-Laune-Bär's Natur- und Umweltspiel für Kinder
description: The youngest player starts. He rolls a dice and selects one of the 3 Li-La-Launebär tokens on the board to move by the corresponding number. When the token ends on one of the coloured  fields, the child gets some questions about animals, flowers and environment. Whenever they can answer them correctly, they get the card. The child who first has five cards, wins.

boardgame: Junior Escape Game: Der verschwundene Kürbis
description:
Somebody has stolen the squash from grandma Tina. Help her to find the thief!
The players work together to solve some puzzle and logic games. If everything is solved correctly, you win all together!

Introducing Rummy Snap – a strategic rummy game unlike any you have ever seen. Rummy Snap utilizes 55 octagonal-shaped, firm rubber, interlocking tiles that mimic the behavior of the playing cards in a classic 52 card deck. Challenge your friends and family to be the first one to use all your tiles by forming gin rummy style sequences and sets. Rummy Snap is an amalgamation of numerous table game classics: part Gin Rummy, part Uno, part Mexican Train Dominoes, and part jigsaw puzzle. Aspects of Gin Rummy: Players create or build upon sequences of consecutive tiles of the same suit (a run) or sets of two or more tiles of the same rank (a set). As in Gin Rummy, players strategize their tile (card) play to maximize their run and set creations while minimizing the number of turns to rid themselves of their tiles and thus claim Gin (Rummy Snap).

Trice is a 2 players game. The player who first make a Trice with the dice win the game. For making a tris you must align horizontally, vertically or diagonally three dice with only one of this characteristic: same color, same number, three numbers in order. The player must place the dice choosed by the opposite player.

Choose your hero (or villain), along with their accompanying troops, then fight it out in fast-paced, tactical conflicts that can be played in around one hour. Use special commands courtesy of your Hero to turn the tide or crush your foe as you fight to claim scenario objectives.

Introducing a fun, fast, and frantic Kart Racing tabletop game that captures the spirit of your favorite classic and modern video games. With over 200 abilities to choose from, you can customize your deck to tailor your playstyle. The highlight of the game is the endless tracks—create them yourself!

Play as quirky characters that parody iconic video game legends. Each character comes with unique special abilities and Ultimates, giving you the chance to outshine your rivals. Start with four legends, and look forward to many more being added in the future.

Navigate tracks using a unique drive card system that allows you to drift and boost around corners. It's easy to pick up, yet offers plenty of depth for strategic gameplay!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to New Droptown, a city pulsating with neon lights, urban graffiti, and an unbreakable passion for street soccer.

Born from Incident Zero, a mysterious blackout in the summer of 1987 when the global AI system S.O.C.C.E.R. (Sports Observation, Coordination, Control & Evaluation Routine) crashed and reshaped the world, this town lives and breathes for street soccer and games.


How It Plays
In Street Side Strikers, each Player controls a Team of Kids in a fast-paced, arcade-style street soccer brawl, powered by a wild and glitchy AI System called S.O.C.C.E.R.

At the start of every Round, S.O.C.C.E.R. drops a new Event onto the Field, spawning Items and unleashing unpredictable rules that shift the flow of the match and create new opportunities.

Each Player then takes a Turn to perform Actions, collect Items, and fight for control of the Ball.

As you string together successful Actions, you will build your Combo Meter. Cash in your momentum at the perfect moment to unleash powerful EX Actions, breaking through defenses and turning the tide in an instant!

If at any point, a Team has scored 3 Goals, the match ends immediately and that Team wins.

Otherwise, after four Rounds, the match ends and the Team with the most Goals wins.


Replayability & Depth
Street Side Strikers blends 90s arcade energy with tactical depth, featuring original art by Athanasios Bathas and guest artists from around the world. Every card, every character, every corner of New Droptown is packed with personality, lore, and soul. With dozens of Event combinations, reactive defense, and a combo-driven action system, no two matches are ever the same.

This is street soccer like you've never played it.
A city born from beats, neon dreams and the kids who never stopped playing.

The roar of the crowd. The tension in the locker room. The weight of every decision resting on your shoulders. Football Headcoach puts you in the headset of a professional football coach, where every choice you make shapes the destiny of your team.

Build your franchise from the ground up—scout and draft future superstars, negotiate contracts, and set the philosophy that will define your legacy. But the real test comes on game day. Will you play it safe on fourth down or roll the dice for a championship moment? Will you outmaneuver opposing coaches with tactical adjustments, provide an edge with strategic half-time adjustments, or crumble under the pressure?

With a deep, dynamic simulation, every game is an evolving chess match. Call the perfect play, rally your team in crunch time, and etch your name among the legends. The stadium lights are on. The headset is yours.

Are you ready to lead your team to glory?

—description from the designer

The strategic card game inspired by mythological creatures from all around the world.

Mystical spirits and terrifying monsters have converged, and are now ready to join forces.

From eastern European tales, to Asian mythology, through African voodoo folklore and ancient pre-Colombian fables, dive deep into myths from all around the world and (re)discover their mysterious creatures.

https://boardgamegeek.com/thread/3518111/everbeasts-strategic-card-game-on-kickstarter-july

—description from the designer

Tilted is a game of balance and strategy for 1 to 4 players. Players take turns earning coins by ‘tilting’ the balance beam with wooden blocks. Place a block near the fulcrum and earn more coins, but fail to ‘tilt’ the balance beam and earn none! Spend coins to buy yourself more blocks. The last player that has not been eliminated wins!

Hekka was created by Marino Carpignano in 2025. It is a variant of the game Barrier by Karl Scherer (2002). Players take turns moving their knight across the board, leaving obstacles on all the squares they pass through. The first player unable to move loses the game.

The game consists of a board of variable size, generally between 5x5 and 9x9, although the 8x8 format is the most commonly used. It also includes two pieces (one white and one black) and a number of neutral pieces used as obstacles.

The game is played by two players who take turns. Each player has a single move per turn. The initial setup is shown in Figure 1 (empty board). White moves first, or alternatively, either player may start.

On the first move, each player places their single piece on an empty square. From the second turn onward, each player has two movements per turn, both in an orthogonal direction: one move of one square and another move of two squares. These two moves, combined, form an "L"-shaped movement, similar to the knight's move in Chess. Every square traversed by the piece, including the starting square, is occupied by a neutral piece ("obstacle"). The piece may jump over both the opponent’s piece and obstacles but must always land on an empty square. There is no capturing in this game.

The first player must initially place their piece in a square within the area a4-d2 or e7-g5. The second player must place their piece in any square within the remaining empty area.
From the second turn onward, the move consists of the following phases:
a) Move in an "L" shape, landing on an empty square (even jumping over obstacles or the opponent’s piece);
b) Place an obstacle (green piece) on each empty square traversed (including the starting square); obviously, obstacles must not be placed on already occupied squares;
c) Remove one obstacle from a square orthogonally or diagonally adjacent to the opponent’s piece, excluding those just placed during the same turn. If no such obstacle exists, the player must remove any other obstacle present on the board. In the first few moves, there may be no previously placed obstacles; in this case, the obstacle to be removed may be one of those just placed.
The game continues as long as moves are available. The first player unable to move loses the game.

—description from the designer

Dice Diver is a theme board game in which players become divers and explore the deep sea to discover and score several sea creatures.
 Mechanically, it is a party game that combines push-your-luck and set collection using dice,
It has a position as a warm-up or filler game at the start of the meeting with easy and fun rules and fast playtime.

Kuhmilchschafkäsefuß is a card game consisting of 110 cards. One round takes about 20 minutes. To win, you need to be fully concentrated and alert, as well as having quick reflexes.

In turn, each player turns over the top card of their own deck. Whenever a compound term (such as “cow's milk”) is revealed at the same time as its individual terms (in this case ‘cow’ and “milk”), the cow's milk sheep must be struck at lightning speed. Whoever reacts first has their teammates eat cards!

The slowest or most inattentive player receives all the cards already revealed. The aim is to get rid of all your cards.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Kuhmilchschafkäsefuß ist ein Kartenspiel, bestehend aus 110 Karten. Eine Spielrunde dauert ungefähr 20 Minuten. Um zu gewinnen, bedarf es vollster Konzentration und Aufmerksamkeit, sowie schneller Reflexe.

Reihum deckt jeder Spieler die oberste Karte seines eigenes Kartenstapels auf. Wann immer ein zusammengesetzter Begriff (wie z.B. "Kuhmilch") gleichzeitig mit seinen Einzelbegriffen (in dem Fall "Kuh" und "Milch") aufgedeckt ist, muss in Windeseile auf das Kuhmilchschaf geschlagen werden. Wer zuerst reagiert, lässt seine Mitspieler Karten fressen!

Der langsamste oder unaufmerksamste Spieler erhält alle bereits aufgedeckten Karten. Ziel ist es, alle seine Karten loszuwerden.

—description from the publisher

6 To Win is a fast-paced dice race, where you roll, lock and match dice on your way to victory!

Setting a D12 to 6, each player rolls 2x D6 dice trying to match the current D12 value. If successful, tick the D12 value up +1 and continue rolling. The first player to reach 11 wins!

boardgame: Bible Brawl: Not Safe For Sunday School Expansion
description:
The Not Safe For Sunday School Expansion Pack adds 30 more cards to your Bible Brawl base game. These 30 (15 Unique) cards are most likely the stories you have never heard of before in Sunday School and stories that we couldn't add to the base game.

The cards range from the over-the-top violence like bears attacking disrespectful teenagers to the ridiculousness of someone cooking bread over poo. You're guaranteed to laugh or cringe.

Starleap is an intergalactic race between space-faring animals in a prestigious affair hosted by King Wa from planet Amphibia.
 Each player is in charge of a team of 3 frogs who are racing back home. The objective of the game is to have the most points. Points are gained by collecting bugs along the way and by finishing before the other players.

Each player has an identical hand of movement cards, 1 interrupt card, and 1 special card unique to them. On your turn, you play a card and resolve its movement, leap frogging over occupied spaces. Each space you land on triggers some effect: rockets let you move further, portals teleport you, stars allow you to draw powerful boost cards, asteroids earn you bugs, and planets give you one of 5 permanent upgrades. Timing is crucial in the game, so each player's single interrupt card allows them to make a movement on their off-turn to capitalize on the positioning of other player's frogs.

When you play a card, you keep it in front of you and don't have access to it again until you pick all your cards back up by ending your turn with no cards in hand. The game ends when one player has made it home with all 3 frogs. The frogs earn points based on their relative positions, with the frogs in last losing points. The player with the most points wins!

—description from the publisher

The threat of The Dark One has never loomed larger over the realms of the Grimm Fairy Tales Universe in Zenescope’s first deck-building card game!

Combine heroes from the realms of Earth, Oz, Wonderland, and Neverland to defeat The Dark Horde!  Work together to stop them before time runs out and the universe is lost to darkness forever!

This is a self contained deck building game with TCG elements. To win the game you must defeat the all of the Villains and he Dark One that will appear throughout the game as you play.  Once you have defeated all four of the Villains, the game is complete and you are victorious!
 —description from the publisher

boardgame: Codex: La malédiction de l'Abbaye de Thélème
description:
You receive a strange package. Inside, a file is addressed to you, the person chosen for this mission. An isolated abbey in Germany, dusty documents, a medieval book-box and a diary by a certain Brother William... These are the pieces of a puzzle waiting to be put together.

For centuries, strange events have plagued this place. Will you dare to discover what the abbey is desperately trying to hide?

Take part in building Aetherya, shaping its fields, forests, mountains and swamps. Claim territories and efficiently gather their indigenous tribes while sparingly collecting precious gems and avoiding the greedy dragons.

Players impersonate gods taking turns shaping the world by placing a tile, then optionally claiming one of its territories: adjacent fields, forests, mountains or swamps. A claimed territory allows its owner to climb a step on the corresponding land track for every piece of land in the territory. Furthermore, every time a claimed territory is enlarged in the future, its owner climbs on the corresponding track accordingly.

Land tracks have several thresholds: some granting victory points for every player reaching them and other granting special bonuses for the first player to reach it. These special bonuses are creature tribes (human, elves, dwarves and goblins), gems or dragons.

At the end of the game, creature tribes grant victory points if placed in a 2x2 square of its living habitat (fields, forests, mountains and swamps, respectively) but give a penalty at the end of the game if they were not placed. Gems sum up and score only their unit number (i.e. 5 points for 15 in gems) but each dragon first steals its player's most valuable gem. Eventually, players score victory points according to their domination over each type of land. The player with the most victory points wins.

boardgame: 단청 : 카드로 하는 윷놀이 디즈니 에디션 (Dancheong: Yut-nori with cards – Disney edition)
description:
단청은 단청토큰이라는 새로운 시스템으로 전통 윷놀이의 전략과 난이도는 유지한 채 현대적인 룰로 재해석해 만든 보드게임입니다.

Dancheong: Yut-nori with cards (Disney edition)

Dancheong - Beautifully decorated by drawing patterns in various colors on traditional korean wooden structures.

It is a card game that reinterprets the traditional Korean game of yutnori in a modern way.

When it's your turn, you can open up to five yut cards.
Move the meeple as much as the opened cards. You can't move the meeple if you get a Yut card of the same color during the opening! Instead, the left player moves the meeple.

If you get the same color card, except for the last card you opened. left player moves as much as the rest of the players.

Players who move meeple place dancheong tokens in the moved area and hand over the case. Place as many Dancheong tokens drawn on the last opened yut card.


     You need to put a lot of Dancheong tokens on top to win!


The score depends on the area where you place the Dancheong token.

Points vary depending on the area where you place the dancheong tokens. If all the players have gone through one turn, the round ends.

If you've gone all the way to round 8, or if someone completes all the meeples, the game will be closed.

The player with the highest score of the finished meeples and the Dancheong token score wins.


     Calculate 10 points for each meeple completed.

Each round, a card is revealed listing items from a particular category. Every player tries to place the listed items in the correct order (peppers by Scoville Heat Units, Brad Pitt movies by release date) on their dry erase board. Points are scored for each item listed in the correct order and additional points for a perfect round.

Players have 2 minutes per round. Play continues for 10 rounds. The player with the highest score wins the game.

Battlegrounds of ALTA is a fast-paced, tactical hero brawler set in the fantasy world of ALTA—a land inspired by the diverse geography of Alberta, featuring towering mountains in the west, vast central plains, and icy waterways in the north. Heroes from across this world converge in the battlegrounds, each wielding unique decks filled with weapons, equipment, and action cards. Whether they fight for glory, to protect their homeland, or for the thrill of combat, they all share the same goal: survival.

The objective of Battlegrounds of ALTA is simple—be the last player standing. Players battle across a dynamic, customizable game board, using their hero’s abilities and tactical movement to outmaneuver and eliminate opponents. Through clever positioning, well-timed attacks, and strategic defense, players must manage their stamina and resources to wear down their rivals' health. In the end, only one hero will remain, claiming victory as the champion of ALTA.

—description from the designer

OOF-DAH! INTERMEDIATE is the expansion to OOF-DAH! LITE, introducing all new components and rules, but does not also include the components in OOF-DAH! LITE.  Thus, if a person owns both OOF-DAH! LITE and OOF-DAH! INTERMEDIATE, they have a complete set of OOF-DAH! The Organic Farm Strategy Game. The goal of the game is to earn Hunger Tokens by growing vegetables on a farm, as well as trading, negotiating, making purchases and collecting rent.

In Vitruvius  board game... you are an academic competing in the biggest architecture contest in Rome to have the honor of being the outstanding student of the master Vitruvius, an Ancient Roman architect from the 1st century BC. Vitruvius left as a legacy the work "On Architecture", a comprehensive work composed of 10 volumes on various subjects related to education, the architect, structures and nature.
So, prepare your strategic skills and dexterity to win this game. Build structures obeying concepts of stability, utility and beauty. But be careful, your project must not only be beautiful, it must withstand each new floor created.

The basic building components are 3: column, capital and architrave. All components have four distinct colors: yellow, green, blue and red. The gray color, represented on the project cards, can be assigned to the component of any other color.  On your turn, it is mandatory to place one piece, following the sequence: column, capital and architrave; and one more from the following five actions: PLACE +1 piece of any shape or color. EXCHANGE PIECES of the same shape ( platform/platform or platform/deposit). RAISE a set already positioned on the platform, of a column and a capital, to a higher level. REPOSITION 1 character of any academic. BUY three cards from the pile and choose one. Then, exchange the chosen card for one from your hand and discard the remaining three. At the end of your turn, the academic who has completed projects must announce, count the score and walk with his character on the Roman Scoring Road.

Projects completed at LEVEL 1: +1 point; at LEVEL 2: +2 points, and so on. Two cards of projects completed during the turn, regardless of level: +1, three or more cards of projects completed during the turn: +2.
If the academic knocks down the structure, all the others move 5 spaces on the Scoring Road and all pieces that were not left standing are discarded.

The game ends when one of the three conditions is met: 1) the first academic reaches the goal described according to the score, by the number of players (40, 35 or 30 points); 2) pieces in the storage and reserve of each academic run out; 3) it is no longer possible to place or move the pieces on the Construction Platform. The player with most points is the winner!

This exclusive expansion introduces a new environment, new thematic enemies, and Torment cards that can be added to any Massive Darkness game.

In the vast underground networks of Shadowreach lies countless underground lakes, rivers, and seas. Who knows what you might find in their depths? A lost civilization holding a wealth of treasure or a leviathan lurking in the abyss, ready to consume you…

Sunken Temple is an expansion for Massive Darkness: Dungeons of Shadowreach. This complete expansion will come with a new area to explore, new enemies, its own set of quests which can be played as a mini campaign in this brand-new adventure! It even adds a brand-new mechanic, Torments, which can be added to any core box of Massive Darkness to add punishing trials the Lightbringers must undertake for each quest.

—description from the publisher

A variant of Giantkind wrought by the mad Wizard Kings in ancient times, Fire Giants hold an affinity for flame like few other species. They walk through intense flames with nary a flinch, and conjure forth shields and walls of searing fire in their own defense. Shadowreach has few Giantkin wandering its caverns, but Fire Giants often undertake a pilgrimage to reach the world's 'heart flame', meaning caverns of magma and molten rivers. Such a quest would be innocuous enough, if they did not also pledge to make war on every living creature they find.

Adds 1 Roaming Monster and 6 cards.

Add iconic creatures into your games of Massive Darkness for epic encounters!

Sometimes while exploring the vast cavernous networks of Shadowreach, travelers will come across ruins containing various marvels from a bygone era. However, these ruins also hide unknown dangers and may be home to creatures waiting for their next meal…

Creatures of Legend is an expansion for Massive Darkness: Dungeons of Shadowreach, which offers a variety of new enemies that can be added to any Quest to spice up your Massive Darkness experience. It also includes 1 epic Quest that ends with an encounter with an epic Red Dragon!

—description from the publisher

Introducing a brand new expansion that introduces a new environment, new thematic enemies and the Horror Mechanic that can be added to a Massive Darkness Core box.

In the deepest, most forsaken reaches of Shadowreach, far from the towering grandeur of the Underspire, lies a realm of poisoned swamps, seething with dark magics and the lingering stench of undeath.

Wicked Hamlet is an expansion for Massive Darkness: Dungeons of Shadowreach. This complete expansion will come with a new area to explore, new enemies, and its own set of quests which can be played as a mini campaign in this new adventure! It even adds a brand-new mechanic, Horror, which can be added to any game of Massive Darkness to bring an extra layer of challenge and intensity to your gameplay.

Dr. Grange has uncovered information about an old laboratory facility deep in the Northwoods.

As whispers of her intention to find this place spread around town, some of the local teens decide to follow her.

The perfect first expansion for DLB. This set includes:

     4 New PVC Character Miniatures
     1 New PVC Killer Miniature
     New Card Terrain Expansion (Facility 86)
     A New Narrative Trilogy (in a printed booklet)
     4 Character Cards
     1 New Killer Visage Card
     New M.O.s and Trait Cards
     New Environment Cards
     New Item Cards

boardgame: Don't Look Back: Summer Camp Slasher – Card Expansion
description:
A card expansion for Don't Look Back themed around expanded a Summer Camp game.

A set of highly themed expansion cards for games set in the iconic location of a Summer Camp.

     New Environments
     New M.O.s
     New Traits
     New Boons
     New Curses
     New Items
     New Stashes

boardgame: Don't Look Back: Suburban Slaughter – Card Expansion
description:
The ultimate suburban themed deck expansion for DLB! Suburban Slaughter! Featuring 36 new cards!

A set of highly themed expansion cards for games set in the familiar locals of the Suburbs.

     New Environments
     New M.O.s
     New Traits
     New Boons
     New Curses
     New Items
     New Stashes

Bollywood Musiquence is a digitally integrated card game for Bollywood fans! A thrilling party card game for adults combining music and fun from the past 75 years of Bollywood. Listen to the actual songs from 1949 to 2024 while playing the game.

First player or team to discard all their music cards is the winner.

How to Play

     Select a music card from your pile and get ready for a nostalgic adventure. DJ (i.e. your neighbor) launches the free QR code reader app.
     DJ uses the app to scan the QR code on the music card, and voila! The song will start playing automatically on YouTube music. Let the music beats and rhythm fill the air and set the mood for an epic game bout.
     Enjoy the music but at the same time channel your inner Sherlock. Take a shot at guessing the year the song was released. Trust your instincts and place the card in the correct spot on the music timeline. Will you nail it?
     Now comes the moment of truth! DJ turns the music card over to see if you guessed the year right. If you did, congratulations! You just discarded a card in the music timeline. Continue building the timeline.
     First player or team to discard all their music cards is the Winner!


—description from the designer

The crew of Hypatia I, a multidisciplinary, intergenerational, all-female crew from Catalonia, Spain, comprised of nine intrepid astronauts, has just arrived on Mars. Help the intrepid astronauts complete all missions in this cooperative card game.

Players take turns in a draft and play dynamic to assemble sets of cards, which will be scored based on the number of cards of each type drawn. Memory and intuition are required, as players do not compete with each other, but must collaborate so that they all manage to score the maximum possible points and that everyone completes their mission. In addition, an optional final phase called "control mission" allows you to modify the missions and scores obtained during the game.

Experience a fast-paced cooperative draft game that challenges your memory and teamwork skills, where every choice brings the crew closer to mission success!

The Breakthrough of Kampfgruppe Peiper is a solitaire game about the Piper Battles in the Battle of the Bulge. Players form a Piper Combat Team and aim to break through to the Meuse River by December 20 PM. However, as in history, U.S. resistance (mainly engineers) and fuel shortages will be major obstacles.

Historical Note: Piper was completely encircled at La Gruez on the 22nd, but no further advance was possible as the 82nd Airborne Division launched a counterattack from the west on the 19th and the 3rd Armoured Division from the north on the 20th. Piper told the American prisoners that if they had crossed the river somewhere in Trois-Pont, they could have reached the Meuse in 19 days, so they set a time limit of one day for the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: History: World War II (Battle of the Bulge)

description:
The seventh game of the Gold & Steel series, La Conquête de la Hongrie, 1526 is an Operational level historical simulation featuring one of the numerous Ottoman attempts to conquer the Kingdom of Hungary which conducts to the famous battle of Mohacs, August 29, 1526, where the army of Suleyman The Magnificent will slaughter most of the Hungarian army, their allies army and their King Laszlo II.

One player plays the role of the Hungarian Kingdom and its allies (Transylvania/Walachia,  Croatia, Moldavia, Poland-Lithuania, Germany, Bohemia) the other player plays the Ottoman Empire and its allies (Serbia, Bosnia, Crimean Khanate).
The objectives are to take control of the different territories (Pest, the main capital), to win pitched battles or major sieges, to eliminate units, leaders and the King!
This is both a strategic & tactical game with garrisons and armies evolving on the main strategic map showing the Balkan countries and units fighting on a tactical map featuring a typical XVIth century battleground.
Christians units are gendarmes (heavy cavalry), hussars (light cavalrymen), mounted archers, arquebusiers, bowmen, militia, pikemen, sword & bucklers  and cannons. The Ottoman units are Janissaries, Sipahis (medium cavalry), Kapikulu (heavy cavalry), mounted arches, militia from Anatolia and Roumelia and many cannons.
The ambience of that period is added with tactical chits with specific application for the fights – shooting and close-combat – (cavalry charge in line od deep ranks, bulwark shield, fight to death, waggonberg (enchained chariots), cannon battery, cannon fires, feint, arrows harassment, surround) and events such as rain, disagreement between chefs, betrayal, janissaries mutiny, ineffective artillery…
This a medium to high complexity 2 players game. One turn is 2 weeks, and the game is played in 12 turns, 120 minutes of play.

The complete sequence of a turn:
I/ Ottoman Empire
1) Reinforcement, replacement, withdrawal, recruitment
2) Strategic move
3) Attrition phase
4) Combat phase (battles & sieges)
II/ Hungarian Kingdom
 Same sequence
III/ Diplomatic phase
 Change of alliance
 Check for victory

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Tricky Seasons takes trick-taking, tic-tac-toe and bidding and blends them together! The rules are easy to grasp, but each round offers a unique challenge that will need strategic thinking. Immerse yourself in this delightful journey through the Finnish landscapes as you compete in the Battle of Seasons.

The aim in each game round is to score as many points as you can by getting your player number into complete lines of dice of the same colour on the Gameboard and correctly bidding to predict your score for the round.

End of the game
The standard game lasts for as many Rounds as there are players.
Alternatively, you may play until someone has scored an agreed number of VP. In lieu of any other decision we recommend 13VP, which typically takes 2-3 hands. 
Whoever has the most VP wins.
In the case of a tie, whoever scored more VP in the final Round wins. If it’s still a tie, it is a joint victory.

Food Forest is a versatile set of playing cards that can be used for playing many exciting games. As with ordinary playing cards,
games are based on matching. Unlike ordinary playing cards, you match "inputs" and "outputs". [polination, nitrogen , extra water…] Inputs are on the left side of the card(with the arrow pointing in) outputs are on the right (with the arrow pointing out). These inputs and outputs can be matched by comparing the little black icons on the left hand side of one card to the same black icons on the right side of another card.

Games:
Picklet
A simple matching game for 2 to 6 players ages 6 and up. Picklet is a very simple matching game suitable for kids and beginner adults. Children will be happy to play this game indefinitely. Adults may soon feel the need to graduate to more complex games. Either way, this game is an exciting and energetic game for the entire family.

Multi-Player Solitaire
Multi-player solitaire pits you against yourself, to build an efficient, regenerative homestead.

House is Wild
A competitive card game loosely based on Crazy Eights, wherein players try to match the cards in their hands with the cards at the top of the deck. The first player to discard her entire hand wins the game!
Click Here

Homesteader
A game of Regenerative Homesteading for 2 - 4 players
Stake a claim and rearrange the landscape to make it as productive as possible! This is a mildly competitive game where land acquisition, pest problems and natural disasters provide opportunities either for competition and cooperation.

Chicken
A poker-style game for 2 - 6 players
Chicken is a guild-building, poker-style card game wherein you race against your opponents. You must balance the need to finish the hand quickly with the desire to acquire more points. By holding out for more points, you play a metaphorical "game of chicken" with your opponents, hoping that they do not beat you to winning the hand!

Guilding the Dandelion
A fun, grouping game for 2 to 6 players ages 8 and up
A relatively new and untested game, Guilding the Dandelion is a game where players build "guilds" of plants (or other garden elements) by satisfying all of the input needs of at least one of the cards in the guild. Please help us improve this game!
 —description from the publisher

3 GAMES, 2 PLAYERS, 1 CHEST!

The Game Chest is a limited edition collection of three unique, pocket-sized games from Pocket Fox Games: Luchamania, Heraldry, and Showdown. Each game features distinct themes and mechanics — Luchamania offers fast-paced lucha libre action, Heraldry focuses on tactical medieval strategy, and Showdown delivers Wild West shootouts. Perfect for 2 players, these easy-to-learn, quick-to-play games come in a portable chest wallet, ideal for gaming on the go.

With only 500 copies available, The Game Chest offers a rare, exclusive experience that’s perfect for collectors and social gamers. Each game provides engaging gameplay with plenty of depth, making it suitable for both casual players and strategy enthusiasts.

—description from the publisher

Step into the world of Tegula, where strategy and a bit of luck help you create a stunning mosaic while racing to clear your tiles.

Inspired by Roman tile work, this game is all about skillful tile-matching and clever actions that give you an edge — or set your opponents back. Match hexagonal tiles to build the mosaic, and use action cards to swap tiles, give tiles, or play extra tiles, giving you a strategic advantage in every move. Be the first to get rid of all your tiles to claim victory.

—description from the publisher

Embark on a thrilling jungle adventure with friends and family in this immersive animal rescue game!

Together, you'll race against the clock to save jungle animals by reaching into the Grab Bag, feeling for the right animal, and placing it on its matching habitat card. With 8 unique challenge modes, there's a perfect game for every skill level.

Includes 24 beautifully shaped wooden animals, 12 habitat cards, 4 challenge cards, 2 timer cards, a sand timer, and instructions to guide your journey. Let the animal rescue mission begin!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Step into a whimsical gardening adventure, where strategy and a bit of luck help you cultivate the ultimate dream garden!

In Swapple, players switch, swap, and swipe cards to plant their perfect garden while outsmarting fellow gardeners. Watch out for sneaky snails and mischievous caterpillars that might spoil your plans, while helpful bees and beautiful butterflies bring your garden to life.

Players race to match the their garden cards pattern on their playing boards.  Action cards allow players to swap cards and boards, bringing an element of take that into the game.  The first player to match their board, wins.

Illustrated with designs from the Royal Horticultural Society, each card celebrates the beauty of nature. The game is perfect for quick 20-minute rounds and is travel-friendly, with a compact box that’s easy to carry along wherever you go.

—description from the publisher

The Rogue Agent Module and Sergeant Module, while only released in the Kickstarter combined expansion and in the the plot thickens expansion, was released in 2015 for the Japanese market as a separate version.

boardgame: Harry Potter: The Triwizard Maze Game – 2nd Edition
description:
Players compete to race around the Triwizard Maze from Base to Home. Watch out for the Spell spaces - if you land on one, you'll have to draw a Spell card, which could give you an advantage or set you back! If you plan your moves just right, you can capture your opponents and send them back to their Base! Strategize and sneak past the Triwizard Cup for a quick shortcut, but be careful not to get stuck. There are endless ways to play and you never know who'll win! The first player to get all of their pawns home, wins!

—description from publisher

Dive into our colorful world of fun with this duck-shaped board game, perfect for kids and families! It's a delightful blend of sorting colors and stacking shapes. With its cute duck design, it's sure to capture the hearts of young ones while providing endless entertainment for the whole family!

How to play:

     The youngest player kicks off the excitement by flipping over a game card, and then it's a race to match your nest to the card.
     Arrange ducks and eggs in the nest to match the card illustrations. Shout "Duck, Duck, Goose" when you're done, and if you're the first to finish, you win the round! 
     Keep the fun going by flipping over a new card for the next challenge. Stack and shout for victory to collect 5 cards for the victory. 


—description from the publisher

boardgame: ¡Arriba España! 1936-37:  A simulation of the first year of the Spanish Civil War
description:
Those are the rules for "¡Arriba España!" (Spanish Civil War), a strategic wargame that simulates the first year of the Spanish Civil War (July 1936 - June 1937).

It is based on the great Game of Phil Kendall and Lee Brimmicombe-Wood Angola with many differences and upgrades.

Game Overview

Concept: This is a game for four players divided into two alliances that control different geographic sectors of Spain during the military uprising.

Factions:

Nationalist Alliance:
Blue (Franco's Nacionales) - Andalusia
Yellow (Mola's Nacionales) - Northwest
Republican Alliance:
Red (Popular Front) - Catalonia and Madrid
Green (Basque Country/Asturias) - North

Main Mechanics
Activation System

Operations Cards: Each player creates a secret deck of cards to activate their units
Columns: Groups of units that can attack and conquer territory
Fifth Columns: Forces without columns that can only move (not attack)

Game Phases (12 turns total)

Planning: Creation of operations decks, determination of first player
Operations: Play of cards to activate units
Endgame: Victory points, reinforcements, recruitment, events

Combat

Complex system with:

Preliminary phases: Aviation, artillery, mines, anti-tank weapons
Ground combat: Based on balance of power and die rolls
Modifiers: Terrain, support, special equipment

Reinforcements and Growth

Basic Recruitment: 4 units per turn per faction
Foreign Aid: The losing alliance receives foreign reinforcements
Bid System: Competition for additional support
Event Cards: Modify specific situations

Strategic Elements
Territorial Control

Cities: Sources of reinforcements and victory points
Capitals: Seville (Blue), Barcelona (Red), A Coruña (Yellow), Bilbao (Green)
Special Objectives: Toledo (Alcázar), Oviedo (Fortress)

Victory Conditions

Decisive Victory: Exceed specific victory point thresholds during the game
Final Victory: Whoever has the most victory points at the end of turn 12
Victory Points: Awarded by conquering cities and completing objectives

Historical Features

Transport from Ceuta: Franco's forces must pass naval checks
International Aid: Germany/Italy vs. USSR/International Brigades
Historical Events: Cards reflecting key moments of the conflict

System Complexity

The game combines:

Covert planning (hidden operational decks)
Resource management (units, equipment, reinforcements)
Diplomacy (coordination between allies)
Military tactics (combination of weapons, terrain, logistics)

It is an intermediate-to-advanced wargame that requires long-term strategic planning and tactical coordination, offering a detailed simulation of the critical first year of the Spanish Civil War.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Based on the Lone Ranger series, this board game was manufactured in Greece by Remoundo toy company and released in 1971.

It is played by 2-6 players, who are divided into two teams, one is the Lone Ranger team while the other team consists of bandits.

The movement on the board is done using dice and in each round only one player from each team plays, who can move only one pawn.

When a player of one team finds himself in the spot where a player of the opposing team is, he can capture him, and there is also the possibility if they are in close proximity to shoot the opponent with certain numbers on the dice.

The winner team is the one that captures or kills all of its opponents.

In Rebel Princess, the princesses fought off unwarranted marriage proposals from pesky princes. Twenty-eight days later, the princes returned...as zombies!

The zombie horde is growing. It began with the princes, but it has spread to the local villagers, the adorable woodland creatures, and even our noble knights! The rebel princesses are the last of us. If they fail, the kingdom will fall forever into darkness — a true and horrific Zombieland!

Zombie Princess is the sequel to Rebel Princess. Just as Rebel Princess was a thoughtful modern spin on the classic trick-taking card game Hearts, Zombie Princess is a spin on the classic trick-taking card game Spades.

As legendary princesses in Zombie Princess, players compete individually or as teams! Each princess is armed with a unique player power, giving her or her teammate much needed flexibility or information. Zombie Princess takes place over four rounds, and each round has a special rule, making each game feel fresh and unique. Princesses bid on how many hordes of zombies they will eliminate... er... save each round. Will you eradicate the resident evil of the land, or will this be the dawn of the dead?

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Goldilocks and the Three Bears
Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood
Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Sleeping Beauty
Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Frog Prince

description:
Black and White alternate putting disks on empty squares from an initial opening position. Each disk must be placed so that it sandwiches one or more enemy disks between itself and another friendly disk, with no empty squares in-between.

The sandwiched enemy disks are flipped, changing color:

 + When a player flips an enemy stone, it becomes gray. If a player flips a gray stone, it becomes one of his own stones.
 If one player can't move, he must pass his turn.

The game ends when neither side can move. The winner is the player with most pieces of his color. Gray disks are not accounted.

—description from the designer

Portgas.D.Ace, 2nd Division Commander of the Whitebeard Pirates, has been captured by the Marines! They are planning his public execution on the island of Marineford.

Whitebeard has united an enormous fleet of Pirates to attack the island and free his adopted son, Ace.

Choose your side - The Great War is about to begin!

Whitebeard and his allies must find the key to Ace's handcuffs, free him, and help him escape. The Marines must eliminate Whitebeard and Ace.

Which side will be victorious at the end of the day?

boardgame: SCUM: An Eclipse Phase Board Game
description:
SCUM is a co-op/competitive board game where players fight to defend their habitat from a mysterious threat bent on its destruction. Use your skills and rep to build a powerful tableau, then blow it all up to unleash an epic turn! Based on the RPG Eclipse Phase, SCUM doubles down on the evocative world-building of the original game.

Manipulating your rep, commanding your AI muse, and calling in favors with the denizens of the Scum swarm, you must uncover the mastermind behind the plot and hatch a crazy plan to defeat them before the multiplying threats unleashed upon the swarm overwhelm it and force the unthinkable. Swap morphs, fabricate black market gear, run illicit software, and inflate your rep. From the very first turn, challenging tactical decisions and an intricate web of resource and hand management will have you walking a razor-thin line between victory and defeat.

The core game concept is to upgrade your player board and then unleash it. SCUM is driven by action selection mechanics building up your playerboard with volatile morphs, fabricated schematics, and inventive AI shenanigans, all while managing your rep upon the swarm to gain favors with its denizens.

If the players manage to reveal the mastermind through enough investigation, and complete a set number of objectives on the 3 random Plan cards, the mastermind is defeated, and the Scum swarm has been saved. The winner is the player that has the most Renown at the end of the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kódem TCG: "Titans of the Crust" Expansion
description:
Roar Through Time! After surviving a dystopic future, you're thrust into the prehistoric past! Command mighty dinosaurs and engage in fierce card battles against your rivals. Unleash the raw power of ancient creatures and dominate the primordial battlefields.

This expansion combines strategic card play with the awe-inspiring theme of dinosaurs, offering a unique and thrilling tabletop experience. Prepare for epic clashes where only the strongest survive!

—description from the publisher

Flores y Tumbas: A Post-Apocalyptic Día de Muertos. In a world torn between nature and technology, the Yokoa tribe, sheltered within Geodomes, honors their ancestral traditions while battling the armored Catrin Squad. This expansion introduces a unique Día de Muertos theme to the game's post-apocalyptic setting, enriching the narrative and gameplay.

Flores y Tumbas enhances card interaction with the introduction of "Spectre cards." These new cards deepen the connection between your deck and the Extinct, opening up strategic possibilities and tactical depth. Explore new card synergies and mechanics as you navigate the conflict between nature and technology.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Su le Teste! La Nipote di Vecchie Battaglie
description:
Su le Teste! ("Up the heads!") is a historical miniature wargame for Napoleonic battles and campaigns from 1805 to 1815.
It is perfect for integrating a series of Supplements to deepen and recall all the battles of the Napoleonic decade.
La Nipote di Vecchie Battaglie is the first supplement; it allows you to recall the Battle of Raab, which took place on 14 June 1809, near Győr, in present-day Hungary, where Franco-Italian forces clashed with Austrian forces. It took place on the anniversary of the battles of Marengo and Friedland, hence the nickname given to it by Napoleon.

description:
The Full Duplex expansion introduces an additional card to the Glitches game, offering an extra scoring opportunity for the player who owns it at the end of the game.

Full Duplex cards have distinct illustrations and are distributed separately, although their rules are identical.

Rules:

When a player places a card on the matrix and manages to score points in both the row and the column , he receives the “Full Duplex” card. This card is always transferred to the player who scores points in the row and the column simultaneously. At the end of the game, whoever holds the card earns 2 extra points.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Klaw and M'Baku
description:
Two new characters with ties to the kingdom of Wakanda enter Marvel: Crisis Protocol in this new pack! Existing as a sentient sound-form, Ulysses Klaw terrorizes the world with a sound generator capable of creating high-volume sonic waves and blasts of concussive force. He is joined by the Wakandan chieftain M'Baku, whose prodigious strength often puts him at odds with T'Challa, but whose influence cannot be denied. The beautifully sculpted miniatures in this pack help round out the Wakanda affiliation, giving players even more options for their squads. On top of that, this pack also includes three Team Tactic cards that help Klaw and M'Baku unlock even more abilities in the thick of battle.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tenpenny Parks: Winter Lights & Spooky Nights Expansion
description:
The holiday season is fast approaching Fairview, and Mayor Tenpenny is preparing festivities for the town’s best amusement parks. In the crisp autumn air, compete to construct the spookiest rides to thrill your visitors. When winter arrives, prepare for the season of giving and fill every guest’s heart with the glowing lights of holiday cheer!

Winter Lights & Spooky Nights is two expansions in one box for Tenpenny Parks, the worker and tile placement game that tasks you with building the best amusement park in town. Play these expansions* separately or together, and discover new ways to inspire your park’s seasonal visitors with 12 new attractions, wooden Ghost and Christmas Tree tokens, advancement tracks, and fresh mechanics to challenge your skills.

Trick, or treat? In Spooky Nights, prepare heart-pounding new rides for Halloween! Nothing sells tickets like a fright fest. Haunt your attractions with helpful ghosts, scaring up varied bonuses during the Advertising step. Offer up creepy concessions too, and you’ll give your guests goosebumps like never before.

As autumn ends, deck the halls in your park with Winter Lights. Decorate your trees and build festive new attractions sure to light up the faces of your patrons. Bask in the warm glow of the holiday spirit as you collect Cheer to advance up the new Holiday Board, sharing your gifts in the spirit of giving.

Featuring the unmistakable art of Vincent Dutrait, Tenpenny Parks is more beautiful and varied than ever before. Discover new tactics and new joys galore as you compete for Fairview’s visitors!

*These expansions are also compatible with the Innovation expansion and require the Tenpenny Parks base game to play.

- Description from Publisher

A neat little set of extra cards for Corvids, including the elusive Treepie as well as five copies of the 'tweezers' card.

Available at PAX Unplugged 2024 and online for a short time after.

—description from the publisher

A party game where every word sounds like "dick" – Ideal for pun lovers and word enthusiasts!

Step into the world of Dicktion, the wickedly witty party game where sharp minds and quick tongues collide!

Test your genius as you dodge forbidden words, unleash clever puns, and outwit your friends to claim the ultimate title: The Master of Dicktion!

Predickt the word
Predict the word on the card while your teammate acts it out. Try to guess as many cards before the time runs out.

Judge Richard
Don't say a forbidden word or your opponents can activate Judge Richard. If they do your turn ends.

Master of Dicktion
Outwit your friends to claim the most points and become the Master of Dicktion!

—description from the publisher

A malevolent chill spills forth from a long abandoned dungeon. An unshakable cold that has woven it’s way into every home and heart of the nearby village. Emerging from the tavern, Heroes driven by pride, desperation and drink, make their way to unearth the source. However, to rid the land of evil, sometimes it must first be unleashed.
   
   Take on the roll of two adventurers, delving into a dungeon created thru card draws. Find and activate all three alters to summon a powerful creature that you must then vanquish.
   
   A solo print and play game that consist of only six-teen cards and printed tokens. Also requires two dice and a pencil.

—description from the designer

Deluxe SUN GOD EDITION with Traders Expansion.

Invoke the power of the sun god!

Take on the role of a line of ancient Egyptian kings, ruling over your vast kingdoms. You will work to improve the culture of Egypt and the strength of your royal lineage, all while following the will of the gods.

Time is limited, so you must wisely decide how to spend your days.

In the end, will you have pleased the gods enough to move on to the Field of Reeds in the afterlife?

One of Reiner Knizia’s most loved games, RA is a classic auction game for 2-5 players, now in a new edition with revised artwork and deluxe components.

This version has the acrylic tiles and includes the Traders expansion inside the box.

2-5 Players | Ages 12+ | 30-60 Minutes
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
It's the matching game you love, now featuring iconic characters from the 2017 Fast & Furious 8 movie! Players race to get rid of all their cards by matching a card in their hand with the current card shown on top of the deck. Watch for the special wild card with the Fast & Furious rule! Play that card, and all other players race to slap the top of the discard pile. The player whose hand is on top is last and has to draw four more cards! Two customizable wild cards deliver even more game-changing moments and help defeat opponents! Score points by being the first to get rid of all the cards in your hand. First player or team to 500 wins. When you're down to one card, don't forget to yell "UNO!" Contains 112 cards plus instructions. For 2 to 10 players, ages 7 and older. Easy to pick up, impossible to put down.

The classic matching card game, now with a Fast and Furious theme!

Players take turns matching one of their cards with the color or number card shown on the top of the deck.

Score points by being the first to get rid of all the cards in your hand. First player or team to 500 wins.

Includes special Fast & Furious wild card and two customizable cards for even more intense play!

UNO is easy to pick up, impossible to put down!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Employee of the Month Card Game
description:
A card game about sneaky colleagues sabotaging each other in order to avoid getting fired.

Retrenchment's near. The pressure's tight! Sabotage your colleagues to stay in the fight!

Play Work Cards to show how hardworking you are. Play Politics Cards to trip up your colleagues and get ahead. If you work the least, your boss will get angry. You're fired when your boss gets angry at you one too many times.

The last employee standing is the winner!

—description from the designer

Snow Planner: New Season enriches the base game with co-op play, additional guest cards, event cards, talent cards, and project cards, enhancing strategic options and depth. This expansion offers new ways to optimize hotel operations and attract guests without altering the fundamental gameplay.

Players continue to strategically manage an inn in snowy mountains but now have more tools at their disposal. The new talent cards offer unique abilities that broaden strategic choices, and the new project cards introduce compelling goals that keep each playthrough fresh and exciting.

While the core game mechanics remain unchanged, the expansion offers strategies that can be employed at players' discretion, ensuring that even those familiar with the base game will find new challenges and enjoy replayability.

—description from the designer

Rotisserie Tricken is a quick and tasty 18-card trick-taking game for 3 players, where your bidding skills will earn you the juiciest points! Each round, players aim to take tricks and hit their bid, with the lowest scorer picking the flavour of the next round (herb, garlic, barbecue, or "classic" chicken).

This clever twist keeps everyone in the game, ensuring no one gets left behind as the flavour shifts. With short, engaging hands, simple bidding and plenty of strategy, will you come out on top, or will you be stuck with the scraps? Only the best biddings will carve out a victory!

—description from the designer

Weretigers is a fast-paced cooperative dice defense game infused with Southeast Asian aesthetics and a vibrant paper-mâché art style. Players take on the roles of mystical weretigers defending their village from escalating chaos under the light of a shifting moon.

Each round, players roll dice and assign them strategically to combat threats triggered by the unpredictable Moon Deck. With teamwork, careful planning, and a bit of luck, you'll adapt to ever-changing events and use your characters' unique powers to survive the lunar onslaught.

As the game progresses, pressure mounts—survive until the final Moon Card, and you'll face the endgame challenge together. Quick to learn and packed with dynamic action, Weretigers blends light strategy with exciting cooperative gameplay perfect for family game night or party tables.

Sketch is a card game that combines strategy, memory, and fast-paced action. Draw, discard and play action cards to sabotage your opponents!

In Sketch! you win by being the player with the lowest score at the end of the game, but you know only some of your cards, so you have to remember them carefully!!

"Sketch!" as often as possible to get rid of your cards.
Making a "Sketch!" is the key mechanic of the game!
Here's how it works:
When there's a card on top of the discard pile that you know you also have, you can discard it at any moment by shouting SKETCH!
This way, you'll get rid of a card and lower your score!

And on top of that, you can use action cards at the right moment to sabotage your opponents and turn the game in your favor!

Games last about 10 minutes, making it the perfect quick-play for parties or game nights. Once you play, you'll want to go again and again!

—description from the designer

Think you know movies? Prove it!
Reel Talk is a fast-paced and conversation-driven card game for movie buffs, quote lovers, and party game fans. Each card features a legendary film quote. Your task: deliver the line in character — or guess the film, role, or actor when someone else does.

Whether you're a die-hard cinephile or just love shouting “I’ll be back” in a bad Austrian accent, Reel Talk turns your favorite movie lines into an interactive challenge of memory, wit, and performance.

OBJECTIVE
Be the first player (or team) to correctly guess six movie elements and fill your PERSONAL game board:

     Title of the Movie
     Character Name (Who said the quote)
     Year of Release
     Director of the Movie
     Main Star of the Movie
     Studio/Production Company 


The first to complete their board wins the FrogDog Film Award!

STARTING THE GAME

     The youngest player goes first OR team with the lowest combined age
     That player/team draws the first card from the deck


PLAYING A TURN

     Read the Quote out loud, bonus if you imitate the original delivery!
     The player/team chooses one of the six categories to answer (Title, Character, Year, Director, Star, or Studio)
     If the answer is correct, they keep the card and place it in the matching slot on their game board
     If the answer is wrong, the other players/teams get one chance to steal by answering the same category
         If correct, they win the card and place it on their board
         If no one guesses correctly, the card goes to the bottom of the deck
    


SPECIAL RULE

     If a player/team already has a category, they can ANSWER THAT!
     This blocks other players from stealing, but they do not gain a new card
     Strategy tip: Use this wisely to stop others from winning!


WINNING THE GAME

     The first player/team to collect all six movie elements wins the game
     They receive the prestigious FrogDog Film Award 
     Want to keep playing? Shuffle the deck and go again!


ALTERNATIVE GAME MODES

     Speed Round: Set a 30-second time limit per turn for fast-paced play!
     Movie Pitch Mode: After winning, describe what kind of crazy movie you made based on your collected cards!
     Team Battle: Work in pairs for a more strategic game!


—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Romance Dawn
description:
A super strong card and character line-up to power up your Starter Decks!
Artwork includes card game exclusives, anime and manga scenes, and original illustrations!
  
By using multi-color cards as leader cards, you can build a multi-color deck.

Parallel cards with different illustrations and designs! A valuable must have card for your collection.
Box topper cards also included!

Parallel cards are included as a box topper! One card is included at random from a total of 6 types.

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Paramount War
description:
The second booster pack is finally here!
Experience the fierce battle with One Piece Cards!
The popular Paramount War Saga themed Booster Pack is getting a Vol.2!
The new color "Black", which makes its first appearance in Starter Deck "Absolute Justice [ST-06]", is getting into the battle! New Multi Color Leader cards are also in the lineup! Increase the number of techniques at your disposal!
A Box includes a bundled bonus card

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Pillars of Strength
description:
New "Yellow" cards are here!
Mighty enemies from fierce battles now gather in the ONE PIECE CARD GAME!
 With the East Blue, Water Seven, and Whole Cake Island as the theme, a wide variety of enemies the Strawhat Pirates fought are included!
"Yellow" is making its presence known! Card types include new leader cards that are not only multicolor, the color combinations are new too.
A defining feature is cards with effects that let you look at life cards and includes characters belonging to the "Big Mom Pirates", making it perfect to strenghten the starter deck "Big Mom Pirates [ST-07], which is being released at the same time.

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Kingdoms of Intrigue
description:
The fierce battles of Alabasta and Dressrosa come to life!
All Leader Cards are multi-colored!
It includes a lot of popular characters from Arabasta Arc and Dressrosa Arc!

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Awakening of the New Era
description:
To commemorate the 1st Anniversary of the ONE PIECE CARD GAME this release includes a card that features an original illustration by Eiichiro Oda!
Get this ultimate card we are presenting to all ONE PIECE fans here!!
The all-powerful God Enel and Sabo, Ranked No.2 in the Revolutionary Army, are two of the many characters from Skypia and the Revolutionary Army making their debut in the game with this release!
At last, the strongest stage, "GEAR5" is reached! GEAR5 Luffy makes its debut in this Booster lineup. Even Kaido who fought fiercely in Land of Wano arc is also in the lineup!
Manga illustration leader cards from "ROMANCE DAWN" and "PARAMOUNT WAR" are getting parallel versions and will be included in the lineup for "AWAKENING OF THE NEW ERA"
Previous booster pack only included 1 type of Super Parallel cards but, this time there are 3 types in the lineup! Be sure to get the Super Parallel cards of the 3 captains!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Wings of the Captain
description:
The 6th Booster adds Fishman Island, Thriller Bark, and Germa as a theme!
These cards introduce new tactics which expands the battle options even further!
Introducing new FILM, Fish-Man, Thriller Bark, GERMA, and Land of Wano Leaders!
Also features many cards that will bolster existing decks!
Includes many cards that complement the STARTER DECK -Zoro & Sanji- [ST-12] released in October!
Fortify your deck with new cards from both wings of the captain!

—description from the publisher

Ancient tales, passed down over the ages, tell of wondrous places lost to time, far across the sea. Soon you’ll add your own chapter to these legends.

Add fantastic new marvels to discover, as well as a new ship (with new journey effects), with this 6-card expansion. Swap out marvels from the base game with new, mythical locations or add them in directly for an epic journey!

Forgotten Worlds 6-card expansion adds two optional components: a new ship with unique journey effects, and five marvels featuring distinctive costs and placement restrictions. Each can be used on its own with the base game or combined for greater variety. The expansion also includes a new Epic Mode, offering a longer journey and tougher scoring targets to conquer.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Memorial Collection
description:
The first Extra Booster is here!
These cards are designed to strengthen previously released decks!
3 all-new multicolor leaders join the battle!
Cards that power-up older decks are also included!
Seize victory using new strategies granted to older decks by these new cards!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: 500 Years in the Future
description:
This time the feature is characters appearing in the Egghead Arc!
In addition, it will also include cards to strengthen characters that are part of the Revolutionary Army, the Supernovas, and the Seven Warlords of the Sea! 
Features many Characters from Egghead Arc!!
Make sure of OP-07-500 Years in the Future to construct Dr. Vegapunk Deck!!
New Deck from Existing Theme will debut!
Construct brand new Revolutionary Army, Supernovas and Seven Warlords of the Sea decks!

—description from the publisher

Dive into a game of tactics, bluff, and risk-taking, where every decision counts. Your mission: recruit creatures, make them fight, and build the most formidable team to triumph in the final showdown and win the game.

Sfæra is played from 8 years old, either solo or one-on-one, with games lasting about 20 minutes.

GAME OBJECTIVE: 
Build the most competitive team of creatures to win round 4, the final round of the game, by reducing all the creatures of the opposing team to 0 health points.

Game Round Process (Quick Explanations):


     Draw 1 item card secretly.
     Recruit a creature (except in round 4).
     Secretly choose your first creature.
     Reveal a creature.
     Position the life cubes.
     Combat: Check the color of the opponent's creature and roll the 3 combat dice.
     Finish the combat between two creatures.
     End the round when all the creatures on a player’s team are at 0 health points.


—description from the designer

"The game where the wrong answers are the right ones!"

The wild game show by Or Butbul and Omri Hacohen – now in a crazy card game version that will have you laughing out loud!

The goal? Answer as many questions as possible with incorrect answers – but stay within the category. For example, if the question is "What is the capital of Israel?", answering "Watermelon" is terrible, but "Tokyo"? Brilliant!

You can play head-to-head, in teams, or even in an intense one-on-one duel. The game includes 50 cards with 750 questions – some straight from the show, some brand new – plus 75 extra-challenging questions to stump even the biggest nonsense experts.

Play a quick round or make a whole evening out of it, perfect for parties or even mid-meeting chaos! Great for friends, families, kids (9+), adults, and anyone who loves to laugh at pure nonsense.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Two Legends
description:
Silvers Rayleigh, who was the right hand of the Pirate King finally joins the battle!
You'll also be able to make new decks themed around the Whitebeard Pirates, Animal Kingdom Pirates, and Big Mom Pirates! 
Silvers Rayleigh's powerful effect allows him to decrease the power value of the opponent's character card,
then KO up to 1 character card with a power value below 3000!
Characters from White Beard Pirates, Animal Kingdom Pirates and Big Mom Pirates are now here as Leaders!
Many characters will be featured in different colors than before!
Utilize powerful leader cards to construct entirely new decks!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: One Piece Card the Best
description:
Many popular cards from previous​ booster packs are included!
Lots of them are also Alt-Art cards with brand new illustrations!
Featuring a selection of the most popular cards from the past!!
New alt-art cards that are also the full art design!
Strengthen your deck with these powerful cards!
Sanji’s brand new Leader card is here!
Use his powerful effect to seize victory!
Don!! Cards with gold foiling will also appear!!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Emperors in the New World
description:
The 2nd anniversary brings the first new alt-art leader cards since awakening of the new era!
The new four emperors are also all available as super alt-art cards!
A long-awaited character joins the fray!
Gold roger, the "king of the pirates," had achieved it all.
Wealth, fame and power had all been his.
Not surprisingly, the final words he spoke before they lopped off his head inspired adventurers throughout the world to sail the seas.
My treasure?
Why, it's right where I left it...
Lim and nico robin make their first appearance as leader cards, along with alt-art leader cards featuring their original manga art!

—description from the publisher

Players are picky kids sitting at the dinner table. Each has 2 types of food they don't like, and they must keep them secret from other players.

Once a player is forced to eat the food they're picky about, they're eliminated from the table. Stay on the table to win the game!

Each player has a character that appears on a grid – only they know their character. Each character has two foods they won't eat.

On a turn, players put a food card on the row or column. Any characters in that row or column will then flip. When a character receives a Food circled on his dining card:
a. If only one of the Food is received, then flip his dining card.
b. If two or more are received, eliminate that character.

If your character is eliminated, you're out of the game. The last character left is the winner.

boardgame: Super Mario: Mushroom Mania – Balancing Game
description:
Don’t Drop The Mushrooms!

Pipe shaped Balancing Game inspired by the world of Super Mario! Place the mushrooms onto the pipe shaped balancing base. Be careful! The Pipe is unstable and easy to topple!

—description from the publisher
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
UNLEASH THE POWER OF WORDS!

Everyone knows that in the world of magic, no magician ever reveals their tricks. But what fewer people realize is that the true magic lies in the words they use to perform them.

Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious!
Alohomora!
Bibbidi-Bobbidi-Boo!

Wizards and witches around the world invent wild and whimsical words to cast their spells, keeping their most guarded secret hidden from the rest of humanity. That any word can be magical. House, badger, sternocleidomastoid…

In Abra Palabra, your goal is to get rid of all your cards by adding them to the table, summing them to the ones already placed, while saying a word that includes the syllables exactly as they appear.

Any word! Are you brave enough to put it to the test?

—description from the publisher

¡DESATA EL PODER DE LAS PALABRAS!

Todo el mundo sabe que en el mundo de la magia nadie rebela sus trucos. Lo que no sabe tanta gente es que la verdadera magia reside en las palabras que usan para realizarlos.

¡Ábrete Sésamo!
¡Supercalifrágilisticoespialidoso!
¡Alohomora!
¡Abanibí Aboebé!

Los magos y magas del mundo se inventan palabras muy locas para realizar sus hechizos y conjuros, tratando de ocultar al resto de la humanidad su secreto más secretísimo. Que realmente cualquier palabra puede ser mágica. Casa, tejón, esternocleidomastoideo...

En Abra Palabra tendrás que intentar quedarte sin cartas añadiéndolas a la mesa, sumándolas a las que ya estén colocadas mientras dices una palabra en las que encajen las sílabas tal y como están colocadas

¡Cualquier palabra! ¿Te atreves a comprobarlo?

—description from the publisher (Spanish)
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Welcome to the exciting world of Dinosaur Number Eggs, where dinosaurs and learning numbers equals fun!!

This captivating early number game invites children to spin the spinner and match eggs to hatch adorable baby dinosaurs, enhancing their counting skills and number recognition. Players race to hatch the most dinosaurs while staying alert for the T-Rex wild card, which adds a twist by requiring them to return an egg.

Dinosaur Number Eggs promotes social interaction and friendly competition while captivating the imaginations of young dinosaur enthusiasts. Dinosaur Number Eggs is more than just a game, once players have enjoyed the game they can use the egg cards for additional activities including creating a number, matching pairs and more.

Featuring vibrant illustrations and engaging gameplay, Dinosaur Number Eggs is perfect for children aged 3 and up — a must-have addition to your game collection!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Les Animaux fantastiques: la valise des créatures de Norbert Dragonneau
description: Players swap face-down cards from their playing area with those from the draw pile, or with each other and with other players' cards using special cards. Once the animals are organized by size and color, Wingardium Leviosa! they go to Norbert's suitcase. The first player to have arranged all of their animals wins the game.

boardgame: Legends of Expedition: The Crumbling Pyramid
