description:
A diverse team of adventurers find themselves trapped inside a colossal, ancient  pyramid. Their initial exploration was driven by the promise of untold treasures, but they soon discover the pyramid is cursed and starting to collapse around them. 
As the pyramid begins to crumble, the team must navigate the chambers of the structure, testing their wits and bravery. 
With the pyramid rapidly deteriorating, the adventurers must decipher clues that hint at a hidden escape route.

Players will reveal cards as they explore. Each card will come with new challenges.

Roll your team member dice against expedition dice. Players must utilize strategy and a little press your luck in order to pass the card. Your supplies are limited but can be used to mitigate dice.

Reveal all rooms of the pyramid in order to reveal the escape path. This is a grand finally, roll against a lot of expedition dice.

—description from the publisher

Poohsticks: The Card Game is a race game using a standard deck of 52 cards. It is based on the original game of Poohsticks from Winnie the Pooh, in which players drop their sticks over the side of a bridge and watch them race down the river.

Your river of cards is different every time and can be customized with bridges, rocks, and narrows. Each player uses an Ace as their stick boat, drops it into the river, and then plays cards from their hand to mathematically match cards in the river and move along it. First one out the other end wins!

—description from the designer

The ONUS! system is extended into a complete new stand-alone game that can be combined with the other games in the series.
With ONUS! Sengoku you can play battles in ancient Japan, or even historical scenarios. The Sengoku period is a very long period of civil war in Japan, starting in 1467 and lasting until the final peace in 1615, the beginning of the Edo era.
 Traianus is a historical battle wargame, similar to a miniatures game, in which two sides engage in battle. This game focuses on the armies of the great factions in Japan. In this game, different action cards recreate various types of battle orders and events, allowing each game to be unique and any combat to be epic, unpredictable and exciting. ONUS! can be played anywhere, but for the best gaming experience we recommend a playing area as large as possible, i.e. 100 x 90 cm (3.2 x 2.9 feet).

Units are represented by cards that can represent different scales, from units of 30 soldiers, to 100 or 500 soldiers.

Units have their own set of traits to represent permanent attributes of the unit, like Melee Combat, Range Attack, Defensive capacity, Morale and Life, and Movement capacity. This set of traits gets bonuses and penalties depending on the ongoing situation and combat engagements. Units also have their unique set of skills to represent specific abilities they can perform: from defensive formations to extremely effective techniques to attack enemies. Some of these skills need to be activated; meaning only one of them can be used in an engagement, while others are inherent to the units.

Leadership is represented in the game by the figure of the General. A General’s token represent his HQ and its own escort. In addition to the leadership benefit, a General grants bonuses to nearby units, boosting the morale of the troops and providing additional benefits when they are engaged with the enemy in those bloody encounters.

—description from the publisher

1812! War on the Atlantic Seaboard is a card-driven strategic wargame that simulates the naval and land actions fought on and around the Eastern Coast of North America and the Gulf of Mexico during The War of 1812. This is a stand alone sequel to 1812! War on the Great Lakes Frontier.

This is a two-player game with either player commanding the naval and land forces of the United States of America or the British Empire. It can be played with four players when combined with the original game into an entire war campaign.

Players will alternate playing Strategy cards, spending Operation Points and causing historical events to occur. Operation Points are the currency used to move and fight battles with military units.

The scale of fighting units is regimental and single ship with some naval units grouped or removed to create solid gameplay. Each leader, regiment and naval vessel has been researched to determine its relative strength and weaknesses based on battle performance, unit size, and type. With limited land armies and naval battles mostly confined to single ship frigate and privateer actions the forces are manageable although challenging to coordinate compared to the original game.

Also contains expansion content for the original Great Lakes Frontier game including new regiments, errata updates and the counters, cards and order of battle for a new scenario.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Step into the world of Cute and Aggressive Capybaras, where you become a mischievous capybara journeying through the forest in search of fruit. 


You’ll visit Hot Baths, collect (or even steal) Fruit, use powerful Items, and navigate unexpected Events on your quest. Whoever collects all the
Fruit and returns to the Sacred Hot Spring wins!

Be the first capybara to gather all Fruit cards from every Hot Bath tile (or steal them from other players) and make it back to the Sacred Hot Spring tile to claim victory!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A War of Whispers: Expansion of Influence
description: The Orca clan, a new island nation of raiders, threatens coastlines across the map, disrupts the most well-laid plans and adds a new player option. Gain unique advantages for your agent through the Guild. The Sparrow Underground adds a fifth player to the game and a double sided board offers customized scenarios for even more ways to play.

Players set up a 4x4 grid of cards, face down in front of them. Based on the reveal of a round card, players then select, reveal, and move, slide, and swap letter cards, attempting to get them into certain positions in the grid. The first player to spell BING or BONG in either a row or column wins!

—description from the publisher

An engine-building, worker-placement board game where players manage their home bakery, trying to bake the most delectable desserts!

Each turn place your worker to manage a different piece of your bakery, and accumulate valuable experience that accelerates your operations.

Carefully manage your resources, and be sure to bake enough to pull out ahead by the end!

—description from the publisher

There isn’t enough space on the ice, but our balancing penguins are all determined to settle there, even if they make everything topple over!

This balance game is made up of a natural wood iceberg and 15 very cute penguins in polar colours. Children aged 2 and over will be able to defy the laws of gravity by trying to squeeze as many penguins as possible onto the ice!

This game can be played solo or with friends.

There are tons of trivias around, but, what's the point of learning about the capital of Kyrgyzstan (?) if instead you can learn about books bound in human skin, or about the Germans-versus-Germans battle in World War II? Why get caught up in the Oscars for a film only your grandmother remembers (???) when you can do it for that Pope who put his predecessor's corpse on trial? For pure trivia, there are encyclopedias. For fun, you have Trivia Freak.

Trivia Freak contains 700 questions of seven categories. Each one includes three alternatives, all difficult to believe, but one—you've got to be kidding me—is true. The other two make you wonder what was smoking the guy that invented them. There are also questions that give the option to invent an extra false alternative on the fly, a creative effort that is well rewarded if a rival chooses it.

It's a turn-free game, in which the fastest player on either team can always answer. The board is flexible, with multiple path options to squeeze the categories you feel most strongly about. You can make it small for a quickie or cover the entire table if you have all five extensions.

Suitable for the whole family (I mean, we also have 100 sex questions, but that's in an extension). Each question comes with a QR code, in case any skeptics want to check the sources (we know that sometimes the correct answers are hard to believe), or to read more context, or see photos or videos.
If you like trivia, this is the one for you. Except, of course, you would rather learn about the world's largest producer of tungsten instead of that Rolling Stone who became the son-in-law of his own son.

—description from the designer

Imposters are causing mayhem in Canada—race your friends in this fast-paced party game by reacting quickly the cards by doing silly actions, but be careful—some players are sneaky imposters trying to throw you off! Catch them before it’s too late - Eh!

—description from the publisher

Will you be the king of the Jungle?
Who knows all about animals?  You may think you do, but chances are you'll get stumped!  Answer trivia questions about animals, and race around the board to try to win!  The winner claims the title "King of the Jungle"

Gear up for an exciting adventure of money management with  Money Bunch - the game of learning money for kids! Join the neighborhood bike ride where players have the chance to earn income while learning the invaluable skills of saving, sharing and spending money. At the end of the game, work together with fellow players to pool your shared money and invest in new playground equipment for the entire town to use and enjoy. This engaging cooperative game not only teaches kids about smart money habits but also instills the importance of teamwork, collaboration and making a positive impact. Get ready to pedal your way to financial literacy with this interactive game!


     This counting money game for kids teaches the essential skills of money management as they ride bikes around town, earning money that they learn to save, share and spend.
     Develop financial literacy, critical thinking, cooperative skills, teamwork and collaboration while fostering a sense of responsibility and making a positive impact.


—description from the publisher

A highly requested add-on, Redemption III unlocks the ability to play as some badass bosses from Street Masters III: Tide of the Dragon, and Street Masters IV: Lament of the Blood Moon, including Train, Tiger Ip, Blood Moon and Swiftclaw.

But that’s not all — Redemption Pack III also includes limited edition foil Fighter Profile cards for every fighter in the game (over 50 cards in total), with a reflective finish powered-up side!

—description from the publisher

We all have that friend who loves to rank things: top five songs of middle school, top ten superheroes who can't fly, top three snacks after 1 a.m. But what if you had to rank random, seemingly unrelated things?

Introducing Rank! — the game of listing and listening, shouting and scoring, and debating things you didn't even know you cared about! It's everyone for themselves in this revealing, fast-paced card game. Play as individuals or play as teams to get more people in on the fun.

Each round, one player will be the "Ranker." This player's job will be to rank the things submitted by the other players. The Ranker will select an "adjective" card and decide the direction of the ranking using the included "MOST/LEAST" card. The remaining players will submit a "thing" card from their hands that they each think best matches that category.

For instance, if the Ranker chooses "MOST legendary," players might submit "Alexander the Great," "milkshakes" (going for the "dairy" pun!), and "your favorite artist's latest album drop." Which one is actually most legendary? That's for the ranker to decide!

After players have submitted their choices, the Ranker will place the cards in an order based on his or her preference (or from LEAST to MOST). Players should try to influence the Ranker as much as they like, but in the end, the Ranker has final say! Players receive points based on where their cards fall in the ranking.

Then it's time for a new Ranker and a new round!

—description from the publisher

Looming high above a forgotten village stands the remains of a forgotten cathedral. On cloudy nights such as this, only the flickers of a few candles light the empty aisles, bringing warmth to the five sole inhabitants: Parm, Brie, Gouda, Jaq, and Colby.

With only embers of light to shield them from darkness, the purring wind wraps and winds its way through the pews, fueling the fears of our five little mice. Spine-chilling specters of carnivorous creatures haunt their dreams. Images of cat claws, glistening snake fangs, and ominous owl eyes lurk in every corner of their minds.

Will their fears bring endless hours of restless sleep or will they shed these ghastly nightmares? Be the first mouse to wake up and grab the most cheese from the kitchen. As we all know: the early mouse gets the cheese!

Haunted Mouse is a ladder climbing/shedding game where mice not only shed their own fears (cards), but can use the fears of others! After all, why work hard shedding your own fears when your opponents can do it for you? The earlier you get rid of all your fears, the more cheese you get! Be the first mouse to complete their cheese wheel wins the game and never goes hungry again!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dead by Daylight: The Board Game – Lightbringer Expansion
description:
Enhance your Dead By Daylight: The Board Game experience with this expansion!
 Play as one of the new Survivors and utilize their unique perks to escape from a new cabal of terrifying Killers with horrifying abilities you've never seen before!

Each turn, players will plan their moves by secretly selecting paths on the board to move along. Each time a player moves, they will reveal new features of the board, called props, which they can interact with to further their objectives or to hinder their opponents.

Survivors are able to utilize many different props to aid in their survival and navigation of the trial. Killers can make use of fewer props—instead, they interact with the Survivors using their attack or their unique power. Each Killer has a different power that will completely change the way the game plays.

Both Killers and Survivors have perks which grant them special abilities. Experienced players can customize their characters with various perk configurations, offering even more replayability.

The Lightbringer Expansion introduces an additional 3 Killers and 4 Survivors:
6 new Killer miniatures: x4 The Unknown, The Singularity, The Skull Merchant
Four new Survivors: Thalita, Renato, Gabriel, Sable
New Perks for these characters.

—description from the publisher

What's a triptych? you might ask. "Triptych" is a fancy term referring to three like things, originally relating back to 15th-century art pieces — a set of three associated works intended to be appreciated together. Today, the word is used more broadly and includes any three pieces of art observed in sequence, such as three musical works or even a series of three videos.

Triptych is a fast-paced matching game. The object is to discover groups of three cards (Triptychs) that share identical or complementary aspects. When you see a Triptych, be the first player to tap those three cards and shout "Trips!" If you're right, collect the group of cards. If players cannot find a Triptych, the dealer will add more cards, three at a time, until a player can identify a Triptych.

When no more matches can be made, the player with the most cards wins!

—description from the publisher

A Chinese Checkers-like race to get your pegs (ships) to the other side of the board, navigating along routes between various types of stars, and sending other players' pieces into the black hole, from which they need to escape.  Landing on certain types of stars forces a die roll, the result of which will make sections of the board rotate - forcing you to change plans and routes on the fly.

Designed by James Lynn, self-manufactured and only released as a Collector’s Series, the board was made out of 100% recycled wood, hand-built, hand-painted, signed and numbered with a Certificate of Authenticity. This was made with a large central disc under the main board, that you rotate based upon rolls of a custom die, causing four smaller inlaid discs, to also rotate, changing the map.

A simple roll the dice and move board game based on the famous cartoon, for 2-4 players.The winner is the first player who reaches number 100 on the board.
The board game was manufactured in Greece during the 80's

Operation Mata Hari transports you into a world of espionage and secrets, where players assume the role of cunning and unscrupulous spies. Immersed in an atmosphere of tension and mystery, the game challenges you to gather incriminating evidence, items and equipment essential to complete your secret mission. Your ability to maintain cover and remain incognito will be crucial to success. Only the Master of Deception, the one who can skillfully play his or her cards and reveal the moves of his or her opponents, will emerge victorious.

How to Play Operation Mata Hari
In Operation Mata Hari, players must stick to their objectives and hide their weaknesses from their opponents. Spies trade resources critical to the success of their mission, in a delicate balance between truth and lies. Cunning is essential to gain advantageous exchanges by making truthful statements and trying to identify who is lying during the counter-offer phase. But beware, resources also include your opponents' allies, which may allow them to lie through their teeth or request a specific resource you are saving for a strategic moment.

Operation Mata Hari is a unique gaming experience that combines strategy, deception and social skills. Perfect for fans of board games and role-playing games, it offers a challenge that stimulates the mind and engages gamers.

—description from the publisher

Asteriated Illusion is a game setting on Asteriated World. You should play the role of God of Fate, using the character cards to collect Grail Shards and fuse Grails to get victory. You can interrupt the characters' destiny to weaken your competitor in the game, so don't forget to take advantage of this trick!

The game consists of two major parts. One is the Reality, and each player has a Reality of their own. There are three zones(red, blue and green) in each Reality. Cards played by players usually enter the Reality. The other one is Illusion, in which all players share.

Players act in turn. They should choose a card in their hand and play it. Depending on that card's name, there are four kinds of playing ways: 1. Entering. If there's no other card with the same name in all players' Reality, you put it into an empty zone of your Reality. This is considered that this card "enters". 2. Bolstering. If there's at least one card with the same name in your reality, you put it onto that card(s). This is considered that this card "bolsters". Both entering and bolstering you will gain a Gem referring to color of that zone. 3. Intervening. If there's at least one card with the same name in other players' reality, you put it onto that card(s). You can take a Gem referring to color of that zone from that player. This is considered that this card "intervenes". 4. Flashing. If these three conditions cannot be meet, you discard it and gain any kind of Gems equal to that card's rarity. This is considered that this card "flashes".

After playing cards, for each zone in your Reality where no new cards are put in, you decide what to do from three actions: 1. Awakening. Pay some Gem with the same color of that zone to put all cards sideways in that zone. 2. Resting. Remove any number of awakened cards to get equivalent Gem with the same color of that zone. 3. Skip. After this phase, you draw cards to 5.

Then the next player acts. In the beginning of your next turn, your characters(cards) in Illusion will give you Shards cards and your completely awakened cards in your zones will go to the Illusion, which also gives you Shards cards. There are four kinds of Shards card, and you will fuse a Grail if you collect 4 Shards cards of the same kind(and then discard them). In the 3-player standard mode, the player who gets 5 Grails wins! Additionally, there are usually up to 4 characters in Illusion. When the limit is reached, other three characters will leave the Illusion and be discarded.

In the game players may gain Crystal as a resource. They can use 3 Crystals to gain an extra action or replace a Shards card of any color. So value the Crystals!

Each character with different name will have unique skills and effects so that the game will be exciting! There are also game variants for more fun.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In a New York devastated by a zombie outbreak, survivor communities fight to endure the chaos and rebuild civilization. As the leader of one of these groups, you must navigate the city's iconic neighborhoods with your caravan, making critical decisions along the way: deploy your team strategically to gather vital resources, conquer and reactivate key locations, and eliminate imminent threats.

With a blend of area control, resource management, and tactical decision-making, No More Dead: New York will make you witness either the rebirth or the downfall of civilization, all within 90 minutes. But beware, a threat even greater than the zombies lurks in the shadows - other survivors!

The fate of New York is in your hands. Will you ensure your community's survival, find a cure, or succumb to the chaos?

—description from the publisher

With this mini-expansion, you can nurture plants in your new greenhouse. Through skillful watering, they even bear exotic fruits. Plant them in your garden at the right moment to leave your guests so enchanted that they forget everything else.

Only playable with expert-variant.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: SCP Foundation: The Board Game – Confidential Crisis
description:
This is a cooperative, story-driven board game set in the SCP Foundation universe.

In this game, players take on the role of the O5 Council, also known as O5 Command, which is the highest authority in the SCP Foundation. The Council is responsible for all operations and decisions within the Foundation. Players must respond to an unknown enemy that has breached the security systems of an SCP site.

As a member of the O5 Council, you will face critical decisions: Should you contain the SCP, secure the world, or destroy it?

Each round consists of three phases, with the ultimate goal of increasing your security level and developing your character to confront powerful SCP entities. This game offers multiple missions/scenarions and victory conditions.

Explore savage lands full of dangers and mysteries, meet fascinating characters and enjoy epic adventures.

Players are competing to get their hands on the crown in order to claim the lands as their own.
Travel through the map, exploring dangerous caves with narrative stories, pack your bag with items and weapons in the tavern and repair or upgrade your weapons by visiting a blacksmith.
You will need to get equipment to become more powerful and go down the dungeons to get one (or more) relic. Having a relic is mandatory to fight the demon lord and get the crown back.
The first player to reach the town with tje crown will be proclaimed king.

Explorez des contrées sauvages emplies de mystères et de dangers, rencontrez des personnages fascinants aux destins entrelacés, et vivez des aventures épiques où chaque choix compte. Que vous soyez un vétéran des jeux de rôle ou un novice curieux, Terres Sauvages promet des heures de divertissement captivant.

Meritorious is a cross-genre tabletop game that combines mechanics from TTRPG, hexcrawl, 4X, and roll & write. Inspired by the lives of notable English kings such as Alfred the Great and Harold Godwinson, Meritorious calls on you to lead one of four noble families in a bid for the throne to an island kingdom. Welcome to a medieval world of fantastic creatures, kingdom building, diplomacy, war, and adventure.

—description from the designer
Roll the dice and race around the track on a photo safari without getting delayed on the way!

Dans la cour d’école ("In the schoolyard") is a quick and interactive card game for 3 to 6 players, ages 6 and up. It recreates the nostalgic feeling of school recess, where players secretly embody a character and try to collect the best combination of playground treasures hidden beneath a pile of fallen leaves.

Each turn, players draw three cards — marbles, toy cars, bricks, or chestnuts — and must choose wisely: one card is kept, one is given to another player, and one goes back under the pile. Some cards trigger fun mini-games like Rock-Paper-Scissors, adding surprises and disrupting strategies. Once everyone has 7 cards, players reveal their hand and score stars based on their collections and their secret character.

Accessible and full of surprises, Dans la cour d’école is ideal for families, schools, and game libraries. It encourages observation, interaction, and light strategy in a joyful and replayable format.

—description from the publisher

In this city, ruthless criminals operate in the shadows beyond the reach of the police. As a detective, your mission is to prove their crimes and secure definitive evidence. However, these criminals will relentlessly obstruct your investigation and attempt to escape with cleverly fabricated alibis.

Veiltrace, a competitive game for 1-4 players, is divided into two phases:


     Evidence Card Placement: Players take turns placing evidence cards in their area to construct an investigation board.
     Investigation: Players navigate the investigation board like a maze to collect evidence (VP).


At the end of the game, the player who has gathered the most evidence (VP) wins.

In Squash Banana, players race to squash the Banana Bandit—but not the innocent citizens of Squashville! With 90-second rounds, it’s a fun, high-energy card game where speed and laughter are the keys to winning. Perfect for kids ages 5 and up, this game guarantees excitement for family game nights or kids’ parties.

Game Features:

     90-Second Rounds: Fast and furious fun!
     Squash the Bandit: Only the Banana Bandit is fair game.
     For Kids & Families: Easy to learn, loads of laughter.


A quick, fun game for kids and families, Squash Banana brings tons of action and laughs in every round!

—description from the publisher

Kraken’s Cove is a strategic, memory-driven game where players take on the role of rival captains navigating a treacherous cove in search of fish, crabs, and shrimp. Each player deploys boats to catch sea creatures while avoiding the Kraken lurking beneath the waves. On your turn, you’ll flip sea tiles to catch creatures and replace them with Kraken tiles, making the cove more dangerous with each flip.

Keep track of where the Kraken are hiding, because knowing their locations may come in handy for avoiding disaster—or using them to sabotage your fellow captains later! The first captain to catch 3 creatures, regardless of type, wins the game, but the Kraken’s constant threat means every decision could mean triumph or failure. Kraken’s Cove is a fast-paced, fun mix of strategy, memory, and a little bit of sabotage!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Great War at Sea Jutland: Coast Defenders
family: Series: Avalanche Press Gold Club Golden Journal Supplements
Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
Four expansions for Ratland (Pirats, Goals, Worship and Port) in a single box. With illustrations inspired by classic tales, they bring new experiences (with new rules and new additions)to your games. You can use each expansion separately or combinee them to enjoy a different experience in each game.

In Pirats you replace 1 of your rat with a Pirat Captain token. In the resolution of combat (phase 3) or when you looking for cheese (phase 6), count a Pirat Captain who has been deployed to a location whre there are no other Pirat Captains as twice as strong.

In Goals, you place face up  Goal cards on the table as the number of players. At the end of a game, check if any players have completed the Goals and adds the points from complete goals to their final score.

In Worship, you deal 1 Worship card to each player, and place it between you and the player to your right. During the game, when you receive 1 cheese or 1 rat, you may place it as an offering on the next empty space of any of your two adjacent Worship cards. Each player, at the end of the game scores the points on both their right and left worship card, and only scores the points on the last completed row of each card.

In port, during phase 1, reveal a port card, aong with the event and food cards. Diuring phase two, you can place rats between your nursery and your pantry to mark that those rats will go to the port. At the star of phase 6, first resolve the port card and get the reward.

A Spirit likes broadleaf trees colored with fall breeze, and another Spirit likes evergreen trees.
Who would attract more attention from the animals this year? 
Be one of these Spirits and decorate Saryeoni with the colors of fall!

You can either bring a Leaf Card to your hand, or place a Leaf card to the Forest Way Board. 
When placing Leaf Card on Forest Way, you can cover other Leaves! To cover Leaves, it should be either Same color ot Same icon.

If Forest Way space has more than 3 straight line or diagonal line with same color, You can get 1 Animal Token for each Leaf Ways. If other Spirit has their Leaf Way as well, both get an Animal Token.

Whoever has 4 Animal Tokens at the end of the turn, wins! Or, when Spirits used all the Leaf cards and the game can not be progressed anymore, the game ends. In this case, the Spirit which has more Animal Tokens wins!

Expand your copy of Rolling Realms or Rolling Realms Redux with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Tokaido.

To play this realm you write numbers at locations along the Tokaido path and gain or pay resources along the way. Can you visit the right locations to earn your way to 6 stars?

—description from the publisher

Slice - A Pizza Game is a high-energy card game where players race to grab matching pizza slices and complete their orders before anyone else. Whether you’re blocking opponents with Hot Sauce cards or using Combo cards to get ahead.

In Slice, players try to complete randomly drawn pizza cards form ingredients in the Draw pile.  Once a new ingredient card is drawn, players race to be the first to cover the card with their hand.  If they are first, they claim that ingredient card. But be careful - claim an ingredient you don't need and you lose ALL your ingredients.  Block opponents with Hot Sauce cards or using Combo cards to get ahead.

The player who completes three pizza cards first, wins!

—description from the designer

Orléans: Neue Ortskarten N°6 is a set of three new building tiles for use in the Orléans base game or the deluxe edition of the game. The tiles feature a tinkerer's wagon, a bookbindery, and a flea market.

boardgame: 당신은 사건 현장에 있습니다 (You area the crime scene)
description:
You're at the scene of the incident
(Original Title : Crímenes ilustrados: ¿Quién es el asesino?)

Suspicious events occurred in various places, such as hotels, airports, cruises, and offices. Now you have to show your skills as a detective. Keep an eye on even the smallest clues at the scene and sometimes collect evidence with your smartphone to uncover the truth of events beyond your imagination!

Incident 
Case 1. Airport, coffee and death
Case 2. Doctor Must Die
Case 3. The gunfire that rang out from room 1104
Case 4. Secrets of Monte Mayor Mansion 
Case 5. The mysterious actress' dressing room 
Case 6. Strange Suicide Case in the Office
Case 7. Tragedy of Santa Maria 
Case 8. Dying message left at the police station 
Case 9. A strange robbery with nothing missing
Case 10. Murder at the Pharaoh Museum in Egypt
Case 11. Frozen human found in cellar 
Case 12. Man on fire in kitchen

A way to solve a case 
1. Analyze the scene. Follow the page and check each piece of detailed evidence from the scene, as if you are at a real scene.
2. Write down the clues. Some events require notes rather than memorizing them. Unlock the hidden codes. 
3. Search for clues. Some clues hide information that is not easily known in everyday life. Search everything with your smartphone and find out.

호텔, 공항, 크루즈, 사무실 등 다양한 장소에서 의심스러운 사건이 발생했습니다. 이제 당신은 탐정으로서의 실력을 발휘해야 합니다. 사건 현장의 작은 단서도 눈여겨보고 때로는 스마트폰으로 증거들을 수집하며 상상을 뛰어넘는 사건의 진실을 밝혀내세요!

사건 1. 공항, 커피 그리고 죽음
사건 2. 죽어야만 하는 의사
사건 3. 1104호에서 울려 퍼진 총성
사건 4. 몬테마요르 저택의 비밀 
사건 5. 미스터리한 여배우의 분장실 
사건 6. 사무실의 이상한 자살 사건
사건 7. 산타마리아호의 비극 
사건 8. 경찰서에 남겨진 다잉 메시지 
사건 9. 사라진 것이 없는 기묘한 강도 사건
사건 10. 이집트 파라오 박물관 살인 사건
사건 11. 지하실에서 발견된 냉동 인간 
사건 12. 주방에서 불타오른 남자

사건을 해결하는 방법 
1. 현장을 분석하세요. 마치 여러분이 진짜 현장에 있는것처럼 페이지를 따라가 각각 현장의 디테일한 증거들을 하나씩 확인하세요.
2. 단서를 기록하세요. 어떤 사건들은 암기보다는 메모가 필요합니다. 숨겨진 암호를 풀어보세요. 
3. 단서를 검색하세요. 어떤 단서들은 일상에서 쉽게 알지 못하는 정보를 숨기고 있습니다. 당신의 스마트폰으로 모든 것을 검색해서 알아내세요.

A lightweight board game for 2-6 players where you control a regional airline and expand your destinations over time by unlocking cities of your choice through dice rolls. Earn points by claiming Routes from the open pool of Route Cards on your turn using whimsical Plane Cards of varying values. Whoever has the most points after all Routes are claimed wins.

The main strategy component is deciding which cities to unlock when you have the opportunity. You begin with 5 cities unlocked that are all in one geographic region. Players must take chances and look ahead multiple turns (and get a bit of luck) to get ahead of the competition.

—description from the designer

Flip a card and start the 60-second timer! Players must quickly draw 3 things using the shape on the card, excluding the object mentioned. If your drawing matches someone else’s, neither of you earn a point. At the end of the minute, everyone shares their drawings; each unique drawing scores one point. The player with the most points at the end wins!

It’s Not It challenges your creativity, not artistic skills! To score big, draw something no one else does. But beware — if your drawing matches another, it’s a bust, and no points are earned. Think fast, think different, and outsmart your opponents!

How to play:

     Flip the game card over! (Nobody can draw the object mentioned on the card!)
     Set the timer to 60 seconds!
     Draw 3 things using the shape on the card! (The shape MUST be part of the doodle!)
     Reveal doodles and tally scores!


—description from the publisher

Balkan Fury 2 is the eagerly anticipated revision and expansion of the second game in the TSWW series. Balkan Fury is an exceptionally fun game to play… and now includes an much extended timeline, partisans, partisans and more partisans… plus of course all the original elements of our top selling game! In the revised edition the game rules, order of battle booklets, and at start booklets are perfect or stitch bound (depending on booklet size), whilst the charts are printed on the high quality card stock so beloved of owners of Day of Infamy, Barbarossa, and Singapore! Of course, we still provide you with 2 D10 dice (we can delete them on request if you have enough dice to start a shop already) and the usual TSWW refinements ranging from “what if” forces to a game system that gives historical results if you play using historical decisions.

Balkan Fury is the all encompassing operational game covering the campaigns in Albania (39), Greece (40 and 41), Yugoslavia (1941), the Balkan partisan wars (1941-43) and the Aegean. The game mates with Barbarossa, Blitzkrieg and Mare Nostrum seamlessly to provide coverage of the War in the West from 1939-43…

Balkan Fury includes:

MAPS - 2 TSWW Standard size maps (each about 18" x 26") covering the Balkans. On map area includes Albania, Greece, Yugoslavia, plus parts of Italy, Romania, Hungary, Bulgaria and Turkey. Maps are to our latest graphic standards and are updated to include the very latest information.

COUNTERS - 2240 counters on 8 countersheets with the forces that fought in the Balkans, representing Albanian, Croat, Serbian, Yugoslav, Yugoslav Communist, Slovenian, Bosnian, Macedonian, Montenegran, Italian, Greek, Bulgarian, German, British and Hungarian forces, with 2 in game modules at a 5 day rather than a half month scale to permit a super detailed approach to operations in Albania (1939) and Crete (1941) to be shown.

ORDERS OF BATTLE - The OBs are dramatically expanded with 20 plus modules and scenarios provided to help players learn the game system, ease into the fury of combat in the Balkans and finally master the Grand Campaign overall. Highlights include various small learning modules, the Italo-Greek War, the planned but never attempted Italian invasion of Yugoslavia, Operation Marita, the German invasion of the Balkans, Operation Merkur, the catastrophic assault on Crete by the Luftwaffe’s crack paratroops, Balkan Fury – the over arching grand campaign, and much, much more.

RULES AND CHARTS - Fully updated to include the very latest TSWW rules and Chart elements, like the rest of Balkan Fury 2, they include all known FAQ and Errata elements from the first release of the product, and build upon the success of the original game.

2 D10 DICE!

—description from the publisher
family: Series: The Second World War (Diffraction Entertainment)

boardgame: Corps of Discovery: Tariff Map Pack
description:
This is a digital product only!

In here you will find 6 new maps for Fauna and 6 new maps for Flora!

boardgame: Cyberpunk 2077: The Board Game – Stretch Goal Box
description:
This box contains all the stretch goals unlocked during the Gamefound crowdfunding campaign. The over 60 Stretch Goal components are contained within one box, but some are divided into 4 expansions (which may be sold individually at retail in the future). The expansions are called "Warlords", "Threat Protocol", "Legendary Crew" and "Neon Paths". The Stretch Goal Box also includes the unlocked Gamefound Exclusive components which will only be available in this box for supporters on Gamefound.

The Stretch Goal Box is available in two primary versions; Standard and Deluxe. Both versions include all the critical components needed to play the game, but the Deluxe version also includes 23 miniatures and higher-quality materials for certain game components.

boardgame: Cyberpunk 2077: The Board Game – Garry the Prophet
description:
Mysterious, unpredictable, and perhaps even a little mad... Garry the Prophet arrives in Night City, and who knows - maybe there's a hint of truth in his ramblings.

Garry the Prophet is a unique exclusive that weaves all four expansions together. You can bring Garry into the mix whenever you play with any of the expansions. Place his miniature on the board, and from that moment on, you can attempt to uncover his cryptic insights. But be warned: each prophecy comes with a challenge intricately tied to the expansion you're playing.

Complete the challenge, and Garry will reward you with the prophecy card for that expansion. You can "fulfill" these prophecies at any point in the game. By unlocking a prophecy, you gain access to special components hidden within the Prophecy Envelope, each enhancing your gameplay experience.

From now on, you can use it in your game (not only with that expansion), enhancing your experience.

Step into the unknown and see where Garry's Prophecies will lead you!

Note: This game component was available exclusively to the biggest spenders during the Gamefound campaign. It won't be available at retail, nor could it be acquired separately during the crowdfunder.

boardgame: Cyberpunk 2077: The Board Game – Female V with Mantis Blades
description:
Grab your deadly Mantis Blades and face the incoming enemies in epic combat. Get close and look deep into their eyes - let them know who got them!

Includes 1 Miniature and 2 Action Cards. Use this miniature in place of the standard Female V character included in the base game while switching out two of her Action Cards with these two. This allows Female V to begin the game with a different weapon, one that is deadlier in melee combat.

boardgame: Cyberpunk 2077: The Board Game – V with Mantis Blades
description:
Grab your deadly Mantis Blades and face the incoming enemies in epic combat. Get close and look deep into their eyes - let them know who got them!

Includes 1 Miniature and 2 Action Cards. Use this miniature in place of the standard Male V character included in the base game while switching out two of his Action Cards with these two. This allows Male V to begin the game with a different weapon, one that is deadlier in melee combat.

Space Station is a cooperative action-economy game, set inside of a space facility. Disaster looms, and time is running out. Players must analyze their surroundings to choose the best options in order to accomplish their goals.

The game features some unusual concepts: the principle of simultaneous actions requires players to carefully consider how they organize their actions. All characters perform their actions simultaneously, and their consequences are only applied during the resolution phase.

A free app is required to play Space Station. It handles both narrative and gameplay aspects: it reports events, provides information on the state of the environment, records movements and actions, and determines their consequences.

—description from the publisher

The second grand celebration at the palace begins, and new princesses have been invited. They all want to enjoy the festivities without the persistent charming princes, so they will have to avoid them for five more days. Additionally, a new type of festivity has been introduced at the palace: the all-female dance! And some wicked guests have also joined in on the fun.

This expansion includes:


    Team mode: new rules that may apply all the original princess and round cards.
    13 new round cards (6 to be played specifically with the team mode).
    8 new princess cards (6 to be played specifically with the team mode).
    In some languages, this expansion also adds the 8 additional cards from Bezier Games' Deluxe version of the base game
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Dominate the field, crush your foes, and pierce the veil before other aspirants can consolidate their power.

Your fiend has newly risen, fresh and eager to do your bidding.

What will you pursue?

Knowledge of the abyss, a path to another plane of reality, or dominion over this mortal realm?

Set against a backdrop of Art Deco and Art Nouveau styling, Occultis places you in the role of an occult aspirant, playable solo or competitively with up to eight players.

Players must evolve their monster, upgrading its powers as it levels, while gathering relics, arcane knowledge, and ritual components to achieve victory.

Beware, this is not a passive world. Your opponents will actively seek to undermine your progress using their own fiends, attacking, stealing, scrying, mutating, and employing other tactics to gain the upper hand.

Occultis is a game of strategy, not needless complexity.

Hours spent on setup and rules explanation? Not here.

Occultis is easy to learn but rewards forward-thinking strategies.

Think you're pulling ahead? Your opponent might just be waiting for the perfect moment.

—description from the designer

After capturing New York in 1776, Crown forces led by General Howe set their sights on Philadelphia, the heart of the rebellion. After a long and grueling journey, his army landed in August 1777. George Washington positioned his troops between Philadelphia and the British forces, leading to a clash at Brandywine on September 11, 1777.
The game puts both players in the roles of Washington and Howe. The British general’s mission is to seize Philadelphia, the political stronghold of the rebels. And if, in the process, he can destroy the rebel army, the revolution itself could be crushed!

Meanwhile, General Washington seeks to use the Brandywine River as a natural defense. His goal is either to destroy Howe’s army or at least inflict enough damage to cripple its operational capabilities.

Brandywine: A Time for Heroes puts you in the shoes of Howe or Washington.
The decisions are yours to make…

The game is based on the activation of British and American player Formations.

Each side has Formation Activation Cards, and each Formation has a limited number of activations among these cards. During each Activation Phase, players choose between movement/combat, building a redoubt, or doing nothing.

Each Formation has a limited amount of Cohesion. With every activation for movement or combat, Cohesion decreases.

Activating Formations generates momentum, represented by Momentum Cubes. These can be used to purchase Tactical Cards, which simulate various campaign and battle variables.

Victory is achieved by destroying enemy Formations or occupying strategic locations.

Of course, nothing went as planned! But… where are the Redcoats?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: When Dreadnoughts Ruled the Seas: Russo-Japanese War Edition (1904-1905)
description:
When Dreadnoughts Ruled the Seas is a fast-playing, 4-page set of naval rules written by Brian DeWitt. This is the Russo-Japanese edition of the game, specifically tailored for the naval battles between the empires of Russia and Japan from 1904 to 1905. The 31-page PDF includes everything needed to play--rules, quick reference sheet, movement templates, 3 historical scenarios, and ship record cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Bolted!: The Game of Creative Necromancy
description:
Bolted! is a puzzle game where players race to assemble a bizarre creature -- or "make friends" -- by piecing together mismatched body part cards.

Players are "creature-crafters" apprenticing under the famous Doctor von Gravenstein. Each player builds one Friend by playing body part cards onto the creature they’re assembling in front of them.

In the Build phase, play as many part cards as you can onto any one limb of your Friend. New parts must connect to an existing part with the same socket color. In this way, you can create branching groups and chains of parts on your Friend. Part cards may also be scrapped (ground up into fuel) in order to power Tools, which give you advantages and allow you to build in different ways. Scrapped cards become resources you can hoard to power big moves and combos at crucial moments.

Following each Build phase, there is a Parade. Your creations in-progress are displayed to the Doctor's gallery of billionaire Patrons, each of whom have expressed a desire to see very particular patterns of parts on your Friends. Any Patron whose pattern you have matched will pay you in coins matching the number of parts you used in that pattern.  Having earned these points, players return to Build again, attempting to modify their Friend in order to satisfy additional patterns before the next Parade.

Every time a Patron pays a player, they leave the gallery and are replaced. After six Patrons have paid any player, all the Patrons will help activate the Doctor's giant electrical switch to bring the Friends to life! This triggers the final round of the game, after which all players compare their accumulated points.

The player with the most points has built the Best Friend, and wins the game. (The other players are recycled into new body parts to make more Friends.)

boardgame: Photo Tour: North America – Photo Projects Expansion
description:
A photographer may experience a variety of events on the way to a new landmark and it is not uncommon for inspiration to strike along the way.

Components: 3 photo project cards: US Route 66, Coastal Photography, and Aircraft Spotting.

Pack available during the first kickstarter period of the main game.

Enhance your Photo Tour: North America experience with three exciting expansions. The Postcards expansion offers region-specific cards with one-time bonuses. The Road Events expansion introduces tokens with surprise bonuses, ready to be claimed as you travel. The Photo Union expansion adds Wildcard tokens, allowing you to substitute any Photo Equipment token. Dive into these expansions to make every turn more strategic and thrilling!

The expansion was available during the kickstarter period of the main game.

Components:

ROAD EVENTS EXPANSION
12 Road Event tokens

PHOTO UNION EXPANSION
5 Photo Union tokens
1 Gear Sharing card

POSTCARDS EXPANSION
7 Postcard cards

Watch your plants grow and propagate clippings to share with your friends in this cozy auction and tableau game for 2 to 4 plant lovers. Acquire the most valuable collection to make your friends green with envy!

In Propagation Station, players cultivate a thriving house plant collection by bidding on a variety of plants and essential equipment. Strategic auctions let you outbid opponents — but winning means sharing your bid with them! Balance growth and inventory while battling pests and optimizing your setup with grow lights and equipment. Each turn presents choices: nurture your plants, expand your collection, or disrupt rivals. The goal? Build the most efficient propagation station and prove your green thumb reigns supreme!

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
How many days since March 14, 2020?
That’s how long the lockdown has lasted.

Swiss-Canadian author Derek Schraner presents Hikikomori, an immersive “lucky lockdown life simulator” inspired by the concept of social isolation. It offers a unique approach to balancing daily activities. Players complete a series of simple minigames representing different aspects of day-to-day life. What makes the gameplay challenging are the ever-evolving rules. In this interactive experience, decisions made each day reveal how those rules change over time.

Players will need six-sided dice and something to write with, as they record their progress and track various activities.

At its core, Hikikomori is a meditation on balance. It explores the tension between conflicting obligations and the pursuit of fulfilment in routine. It is the culmination of the author’s experience living this way for five years, offering personal reflection, and inviting you to share in the challenges and insights of the journey.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Operation CanCon: Canadian Eighties Music Shopping Gamebook
description:
A Mission to Save the Music and the Future of a Nation

Dive into the interactive world of Operation CanCon, where your mission is to collect Canadian Content. Navigate Eighties music stores in search of iconic songs. The hope of a country’s future will depend on preserving its cultural artifacts.

Using dice and a checklist, take your chances with the Sweep minigame, a treasure hunt where every roll reveals another hit. It’s not all random chance, however. Your decisions really matter. Choose which tracks to target, which stores to visit, and how long to search. How will you spend your limited time in Toronto? Whether you play a single game or a multi-day campaign, you’ll discover opportunities for tactics and strategy. Advanced modes, tweaks, and New Game Plus provide even more challenges.

Perfect for gamers, music lovers, and fans of Canadiana, Operation CanCon is a new take on nostalgia. With an interactive narrative, flexible gameplay, and customizable goals, it’s more than a game, it’s a retro celebration.

—description from the designer

A simple roll the dice and move board game based on the anime series Candy Candy, It can be played from 2 to 4 players.The winner is the first player who reaches number 100 on the board.

The board game was manufactured in Greece during the 80's

The harmonious God Crystal, responsible for maintaining the natural barrier between universes has shattered. Spread far and wide into thousands of pieces, these fragments have been termed Power Crystals, for they have provided different realms the means to move beyond their plane of existence. While the cause of the great shattering is unknown, the ramifications are very clear-chaos.

Players choose one of the first five Realms to cross the rift, battling against one another for survival. Each of these realms has Champions and items whose playstyles dictate their strengths, weaknesses, and quirks, so experiment to discover clever combinations to bolster your forces. Manage your resources wisely, build your battlefield, summon your army, & leverage your realm’s talents.

Compete against your friends, destroy their Power Crystals, and show them why your Realm is supreme as you spiral towards a thrilling conclusion in this strategic combat board game.

In Robo Rescue, homesick robots race for home in this turn-based, programmed movement strategy board game.

2047. The human scientists have left. Silence echoes through the deserted Martian Research Facility. Four robots abandoned, embark on an adventure to reunite with their human creator back on Earth. Who will be the first one to repair the rocket engines of their spacecraft and blast off? This competition is fueled by homesickness, ingenuity, and the unwavering bond between robots and their creators.

Each player becomes an engineer, guiding their robot companion through the abandoned facility in search for resources using remote commands. Be the first to repair all 4 of your rocket engines to claim victory and blast off!

Each player’s turn consists of:

     Movement - Guide your robot with each carefully chosen command. Movement cards become your words, directing your robot to scoot forward, turn left, or take a daring right. Throw in a Blast! to blow up a Container Box, or activate a Levitate to glide over obstacles.
    Board Actions - Energy cells hidden in the old labs, spare parts scattered among the workbenches, and maybe even some leftover oil to grease those rusty gears. Scavenge for resources needed to repair your rocket engines.
    Conversions - Convert your collected resources into the essential components needed for your spacecraft.

In the near future, people are sick and tired of pushing buttons to get on the elevator. With all this AI technology, why not have an AI that predicts which floor you are going to so that you have more time to do important things, like looking through your phone!

In Elevator Operator you play as the AI installed by the hotel to get people where they want to go. Your programming, while limited, does allow you to sense the floor customers want to visit. Your goal is to get all customers where they are going before they get angry and fill up the hotel with guests. If too many customers take the stairs you will be shut down, and replaced. Do you have what it takes to become the best AI Elevator out there? Find out in Elevator Operator!

Elevator Operator is solitaire dice game that seeks to condense a Pick Up and Deliver game to its smallest denominator. Using two elevators, pick up customers and deliver them to other floors. While choices are limited, players will have to focus on delivering customers fast enough as to not run out of dice, but not so fast that they anger customers. Taking 15-30 minutes to play, this game will have you thinking, "can I afford to upset that customer this round?"

—description from the designer

boardgame: The NecroNomNomNom: A Card Game of Dark Deliciousness
description:
A card game of omnivorous peril and delectable danger. This luxuriantly appointed deck of 100+ cards to feed insanity compels you and a cohort of fellow cultists (2 to 4 players) to stir up the foul fixin’s of H. P. Lovecraft's Cosmos and compete in a grimly hilarious, ravenous race against fate, luck, your opponents, and the Stars themselves. Are you ready to play with your food?
Within the disturbing depths of this bloodcurdling box lie four subdecks: Eldritch Horrors from beyond time and space that affect play for each cultist holding that card; Rituals (recipes) to satisfy the unearthly appetites of the Ancient Ones; Offerings (ingredients) to assemble for a given ritual; and The Stars, which lambast celebrity chefs and give players a task for that round.
Be the first cultist to complete a ritual that quells the hunger of your Eldritch Horror—before you become the main course. You don’t have to know how to boil water or skin a nightgaunt to make these recipes, though. You merely need to answer that age-old question posed by unhinged necromancers everywhere: “Now that I’ve summoned Ghatanothoa, what to feed it?” Friends make a fine choice, but can you consign them to their just desserts?

The Two Towers is a game for two players using a standard deck of 52 cards. Each builds a tower in runs of cards, but an exact match on top of your opponents tower swaps them. The player with the tallest tower at the end wins!

—description from the designer

Playing as the sun or the moon, two players compete to get three of their tiles of the same color face up on the board at the same time. Though both the sun player and the moon player start with three of each color of tile, before beginning the game each player discards one of their six tiles face down. This adds an element of deduction to the game, as players don't know which color their opponent can actually win with.

Players take turns placing tiles on the 3x3 grid, utilizing the game's unique flipping mechanics to try and set up scenarios where they can win. When a tile is placed face up (the color is visible), all other tiles in the same row or column flip over depending on the color of the tile placed (row for blue tiles, column for red tiles). Tiles may also be placed face down, though this does not cause any flips.

Placing your own tiles face down can be used to hide information from your opponent, though doing so puts the tile itself in a more vulnerable position; players can replace one of their opponent's face down tiles with one of their own tiles, returning the face down tile to their opponent in the process. Be careful, as this is the only way tiles can be retrieved once placed. By placing your tiles carefully, it is possible to set up scenarios where you forcefully flip over one of your opponent's tiles on one turn, then replace it with one of your own the next. Just try to avoid accidentally flipping your own tiles face down when you're going for the win!

The Anomie tile is a special tile that both players have access to - when placed, it flips all tiles on the board, though it does not count toward either player's win while face up. It can be useful for shaking things up in a pinch, or to instantly win if you have three tiles of the same color face down already. Unlike other tiles, if the Anomie tile is flipped face down at any point, it gets removed and returned to its position next to the board.

- User Summary

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Nuka-Cola Caps Set
description:
This is a set of 50 distressed Nuka-Cola bottle caps, made by a British manufacturer with an approved graphic design to resemble authentic Nuka-Cola caps!

This set also includes stickers to allow you to use the caps as tokens. Plus an exclusive weapon card "Bottlecap Mine" for use in your game of Fallout: Wasteland Warfare. Available for the first time, these are the only official Nuka-Cola caps!

Cat Spotting is a fun and fast series of games where the objective is simple: be the first player to spot the hidden cat in the crowd! These games are best played with 2 players, but can be enjoyed with more.

Race to spot cats the fastest or work together to find them all!

There are 4 sets of Crowd Cards, 120 unique, hand-drawn Cat Cards and 7 ways to play.

Each game requires a set of Crowd Cards and some Cat Cards, depending on the game.
 A cat will ONLY be on 1 of the 3 Crowd Cards.

They will look exactly as pictured on the Cat Card, although part of it may be hidden behind other cats.

Have fun Spotting Cats!

—description from the designer

Spindel 4 is a fast-paced strategy game for 2 to 4 players, suitable for ages 8 and up. With simple rules and a playtime of just 15 minutes, it offers the perfect mix of tactics and fun.

Each player chooses a color and takes turns coloring a space connected to the heart of the web or to a previously colored space of the same color. There can never be an empty space between the heart and any colored space. The first player to create a winning Spindel of four—straight, circular, or diagonal—wins the game. The last player to have seen a real spider goes first.

Color spaces, block opponents, and outsmart your rivals to win! Perfect for family game nights and fans of clever, competitive gameplay.

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Royal Blood
description:
Introduction of the new theme "Punk Hazard"!
Greatly enhances existing "Supernovas" and "Donquixote Family" themed decks!
Packed with numerous cards to support popular themes like “Supernovas,” “Dressrosa,” and “Donquixote Pirates”!
Use these powerful cards to strengthen your deck! 
Popular characters appear as new leader cards!
Harness their powerful effects to turn the tide of battle in your favor!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Anime 25th Collection
description:
A special booster released to commemorate the 25th anniversary of the anime!

All cards will feature new illustrations drawn by artists from the TV anime "ONE PIECE"!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Starter Deck – Monkey D. Luffy
description:
For the first time starter decks themed around a character are here!
The new Leader for this one is "Luffy" and this deck lets you take control and attack while also aiming the attack!!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Starter Deck – Yamato
description:
For the first time starter decks themed around a character are here!
The new Leader for this one is "Yamato" and is a defensive deck letting you solidly stop enemy attacks!!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Ultra Deck – The Three Captains
description:
All the included cards are holographic!
That’s not all, this pre-constructed deck is also powerful with the three captains of the “Worst Generation” being used as the theme.
It even features the first Red-Purple multicolor Leader card! 
The three captains Law, Luffy and Kid assembles to form an unstoppable force!
Includes many characters that have strong ties with the captains.

—description from the publisher

Imitation is the sincerest form of flattery that mediocrity can pay to greatness - but it seems that the second half of that phrase gets forgotten. As other companies rise up and attempt to compete in the space tourism industry, it has become more important than ever to show the world that Galactic Cruise is the premiere destination for luxurious space cruising. In Galactic Cruise: Achievements, players will work to accomplish special feats set forth by the Board of Directors, receiving a special reward (and an even more daunting challenge) each time one of these goals is achieved.

This expansion includes new content including a Zoe meeple and rules for including her in your game. However, the bulk of the expansion is within the 12+ boxes of locked content. Each time a player accomplishes one of the 12 achievements, a new box of content is unlocked for all players, with the achieving supervisor etching their name into the annals of history. Some of these rewards are new renditions of familiar things, such as new Agenda Cards and special cockpits and engines. Others are brand new innovations, including secret objective cards, special ability tiles, and asymmetric characters.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 8: Snake Men
description:
As the Snake Men enter the battle, the stage is set for the Ultimate Battleground! Claws and fangs slash against swords and axes, while lasers meet spells in a whirlwind of conflict. Will Hing Hsss and his army of evil minions emerge victorious? The outcome lies in your hands.

Snake Men lurked in the shadows, patiently waiting for the perfect moment to strike. That time is now! Brace yourself for an entirely new force of evil – one that strikes when least expected.

Snake Men is an expansion to Masters of the Universe: Battleground, in which a brand new faction, terrain pieces, and battleground setups are introduced.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 10: Preternian Heroes
description:
The conflict expands, and now the Preternian Heroes join the battle. Heroes from the past, together with mighty creatures, come and will remove any evil from the land. Use new, powerful characters to take control of the battle and claim victory.

The battle isn't going to cease. The Preternian Heroes hear about the neverending conflict and join in to stop the evil that has spread over the lands.

Preternian Heroes is an expansion to Masters of the Universe: Battleground in which a brand new faction and battleground setups are introduced. Are you ready?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 11: Moss Man & Turbodactyl Pack
description:
When evil threatens to conquer Preternia, nature itself will rise to defend its homeland. The entire jungle seethes with rage, fueled by Moss Man's magic, while high above, the mighty Turbodactyl patrols the skies. Should the forces of evil dare to assail this land, they will face dire consequences.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 11: Roboto Pack
description:
His metal frame renders him invulnerable to attacks that would incapacitate an ordinary warrior. His computerized mind endows him with remarkable intelligence. His right hand is a mechanical claw that can be replaced with an axe or a laser gun. He is Roboto, and he stands as the guardian of this world against the forces of evil.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 9: Faker Pack
description:
Is he a friend or foe? The mightiest man in the universe or an evil robot? It's difficult to tell. Faker infiltrates the ranks of the Masters of the Universe, wrecking havoc from within. Will this nefarious scheme succeed? Faker is almost as strong as He-Man, which means that this time, Castle Grayskull may be lost.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 9: Clamp Champ Pack
description:
The forces of evil lured Clamp Champ away from Castle Grayskull to an abandoned junkyard, hoping to vanquish the isolated hero. However, they underestimated him. Even alone, Clamp Champ proved himself strong enough to overcome the forces of Evil Warriors. One can only imagine the strength this warrior will wield with allies by his side.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Zodac with Throne
description:
Embark on a cosmic journey with Zodac Zur, once a member of Eternia's Council of Elders and King Grayskull's friend. Now a Cosmic Enforcer for the Overlords, he maintains universal balance. Yet, the battle for Grayskull's Sword of Power calls him back to Eternia. Join him in the Second Ultimate Battleground! The balance of the universe is in your hands!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Land Shark
description:
Terror roars to life with the Land Shark, monstrous mechanical menace! With its chomping jaws and rolling reads, it devours everything in its path. Will its iron bite crush the forces of good, or can the defenders of Grayskull stop this beastly juggernaut?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Roton
description:
Unleash terror with Roton, the whirling war machine of evil! Armed with spinning blades and twin blasters, it tears across the battlefield in a vortex of destruction. Will you harness its power to spread chaos, or will the forces of good grind it to a halt? Eternia's fate spins with Roton!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Talon Fighter
description:
Soar into the skies of Eternia with the might Talon Fighter, a two-seated aerial marvel crafted for speed and power. Equipped with advanced sensors and powerful blasters, it patrols the realm, defending against the forces of evil. Will you command the skies and protect Eternia, or let darkness take flight? The choice is yours.

—description from the publisher

Techu is a card game that we’ve played  with friends for over 15 years. Techu was our cat, and used to love to watch us play this game from her chair…back in Osaka, Japan.

Our idea was to create a fairer card game with a 5×5 grid, similar to a mini-shogi board.

It’s a strategic card game for those who love wit and luck. Having the deepness of chess and the luck of cards, it’s a perfect 2 player game. Those who get it…are hooked.

—description from the designer

Each player takes half a deck in a single color, and takes turns playing cards into the grid with the goal of occupying the most territory. Higher ranked cards can cover and capture enemy cards, but you can only play if your cards connect back to your home row. Many different strategies are possible, and every game ends in a unique map of its own particular battle.

Festmaker is the ultimate board game for rock and metal lovers!

As a festival organizer, you’ll compete to create the most legendary music festival by securing the best venues, managing crowd capacity, and bidding to sign top bands.

Each player starts the game with the same amount of money. During their turn, they can use this money to purchase venues and stages of their choice. These venues will determine the festival's capacity, which corresponds to the maximum score they can obtain.

They must place bands on each stage, which will attract more or less people depending on the time they play.

The player who attracts the most people to their festival wins.

—description from the designer

In the 20th century, quantum physics revolutionized our understanding of the atomic and subatomic world, paving the way for quantum technologies. These promise breakthroughs in communication, sensing, simulation, and computation.

However, to engineer these futuristic technologies, our labs and industries must master the art of controlling single atoms and manipulating them to create specific states characterized by unique quantum properties, known as entanglement. This is the quest ahead of us, already undertaken by many researchers and innovators worldwide, which Quantum Quest represents.

During each turn, players may:


     Take a card from the display
     Play a card in the Quantum Technologies Pyramid
     Convert Qubits to advance on the Entanglement Scale


Join us in the challenge of developing quantum technologies, increasing your research capability, creating thousands of qubits, and entangling them faster than your adversaries. Be the first to climb the Pyramid of Quantum Technologies and claim your space in the history of science and technology!

Quantum Quest is an educational board game developed by the Department of Physics and Astronomy at the University of Padua. It explores key elements of quantum physics, such as qubits and entanglement, aiming to make the principles behind the race for Quantum Advantage accessible to everyone—all through an engaging and immersive gaming experience.

—description from the designer

Favor: A Game of Royal Succession is a thrilling card-drafting game for 3-5 players, plunging you into a kingdom teetering on the edge after the King’s demise. As one of 10 royal siblings, you’ll vie to win the Queen’s favor and seize the crown across three dramatic Acts, each brimming with tension and lasting 45-90 minutes. Pick your champion—perhaps the sly Bandit, the noble Knight, or the enigmatic Magician, each with their own flair and challenges—and outmaneuver your kin through cunning card play, daring missions, and epic realm-wide melees. With its gorgeously illustrated cards and immersive theme, Favor delivers a delicious mix of strategy and storytelling for those who crave rivalry and glory.

Each Act unfolds in three pulse-pounding phases: the Gameplay Phase, where you draft and play cards to hone skills like Charisma, Power, or Alchemy; the Missions Phase, where you embark on royal quests to curry the Queen’s favor; and the Melee Phase, a grand tournament where you unleash your might to claim coveted Prestige Tokens. Cards—from Endeavors that sharpen your talents to Weapons, Artifacts, and Potions that spark dazzling effects—open myriad paths to triumph, whether you’re conquering missions, dominating melees, or chasing legendary combos like Blind Faith, Heaven’s Blade, and Archeus for a mighty edge. Choices abound: borrow skills from a rival for a price, tweak your character’s proficiency slider for a clever advantage, or trade cards for gold to fuel your rise.

The stakes soar in Act 3, where the final melee offers a dazzling prize of Prestige, and the Queen casts her discerning eye over each sibling’s deeds. Victory hinges on a grand tally of Completed Missions, Character Favor (gained through skills like Survival or Knowledge), Prestige, and the bitter sting of Scorn Tokens that can mar your reputation, with gold breaking any deadlock. The sibling with the greatest score ascends as Ruler of the Realm. Favor’s clever mechanics reward sharp wits—whether you’re a Fiddler hunting wildcards or a Duchess weaving a web of generosity—while its enchanting card effects, like the Mind’s Eye or Philosopher’s Stone, sprinkle magic into every turn.

Crafted for strategy lovers, Favor blends approachable rules with endless replayability, thanks to its colorful cast of characters and intricate card interplay. The game’s lavish design, from its ornate artwork to its evocative symbols, sweeps players into a realm of royal intrigue where every choice carves your destiny. Will you outfox your siblings and bask in the Queen’s favor, or will your dreams of the throne crumble in this exhilarating game of wit and valor?

—description from the publisher

Brushstroke Mayhem is a card game of chaotic creativity. Players build abstract artworks while outmaneuvering each other in a strategic mess of color and control.

Players choose a Painting from the Open Market at the beginning of the game.  They gain new ones later on.

On their turn,  players layout cards from the Brushstroke draw deck into either a shared canvas or their personal canvas.  They then use these cards to match the abstract painting cards.

The first player to complete 3 paintings, wins the game.

Advanced rules bring extra mechanics like color effects and disruption to add more spice.  The game is designed to be color blind friendly with each brushstroke having its own color symbol.

Who’s Meowing? is a fast-paced party game full of blindfolds, meowing, and cat chaos!

Players use Who’s Meowing? mini-game cards to trigger challenges where they must guess which player is secretly meowing - if they guess correctly they win a tuna card.

Action cards add even more excitement, allowing players to steal tuna, trick opponents, or cause total chaos. The goal is to collect the most tuna cards by bluffing, stealing, and outwitting your opponents.

Designed for cat lovers and party game enthusiasts alike, Who’s Meowing? is easy to learn and endlessly replayable. The mix of deduction, bluffing, and absurd cat noises keeps each game fresh, making it the perfect choice for gatherings and game nights.

—description from the designer

As the last greater dragon falls, another seismic wave of Ether unfolds from The Tear. It shakes the foundations of the Tower, and while it doesn’t kill any mages, it does infuse the very stone with the pulsating light of elemental magic. Like a shining beacon, the Tower calls out to the corporeal and ethereal alike. To humans and monsters. And to far worse…

Darkness descends on Vale as dragons attack the Tower! With the irresistible pull of Ether emanating from the mages’ academy, the Tower is under siege. The monsters of Etchinstone are trying to take control of The Tear, and brave Ether Mages must fight to protect the realm. The fate of Vale hinges upon this climactic battle!

In Dragons of Etchinstone: Siege, players act as Ether Mages defending against powerful siege attacks. An expanded hand allows players to create two Action Sets per turn, while new Structure Cards offer valuable abilities to use in the battle. These new mechanics bolster the Dragons of Etchinstone experience and create an ultimate challenge for even the sharpest gamers. Gut-wrenching hand management and brain-burning strategy will satisfy even the greatest Ether Mages.

Bloodied by the fight for freedom in Vale? Curious what lies beyond The Tear? The Siege expansion also offers players a chance to take on the role of the monsters in the Return to Etchinstone variant. Pursue victory at all costs and return to your lost home through the Tower’s Ether rift!

Siege will make you rethink everything you thought you knew about minimalist game design with a city-sized achievement in a pocket-sized game.

—description from the publisher

Parallel Stories is a series of horror adventure card games.

Technopolis is engulfed in an epidemic and drowning in a bloody uprising. Two people are trapped in a labyrinth of ruins. One is a soldier searching for his family. The other — a spy on her mission. How will their stories end? Follow the narrative and pay attention to details — your choices at key moments will determine the fate of the characters.

Welcome to this fur-mily showdown of adorableness, where you, esteemed members of the animal kingdom, have been given the critically important (and utterly charming) mission of determining which animal is the cutest of them all. That’s right—who’s the top of the cuteness charts, and who’s hanging out at the “not-so-cute” end?

SETUP:
Each player picks their favorite animal paw. Place the Cuteness Meter in the center of the table. Line up the 3 blobfish tokens face down. Shuffle the cards and place them face down. Flip the top card over and place it above the center of the Cuteness Meter.

PLAY:
The player who most recently played with
a really cute animal goes first as the
