Cuteness Boss. The Cuteness Boss flips the top card from the deck and places it below the Cuteness Meter where everyone can see it. Without telling anyone, the Cuteness Boss decides if the new animal is more cute or less cute than the card at the center of the Cuteness Meter.

If the Cuteness Boss thinks the animal
is more cute, they rank it to the right side of
the Cuteness Meter. If the Cuteness Boss thinks the animal is less cute, they rank it to the left side of the Cuteness Meter. Once the Cuteness Boss has made their decision, they keep it secret and declare “I’M FEELIN’ CUTE!”
The other players then place their paws where they think the Cuteness Boss ranks the new animal on the Cuteness Meter.

After the players have guessed the new animal’s location on the Cuteness Scale, the Cuteness Boss places the new animal in its rightful spot, extending the Cuteness Lineup. Each player who guessed correctly scores a point. The Cuteness Boss also gets one point if any player correctly guessed the animal’s
cuteness rank.

Then, the person sitting to the left of the Cuteness Boss takes over as the new Cuteness Boss. Play continues with the new Cuteness Boss drawing a new card from the deck. The Cuteness Boss is able to rank an animal’s cuteness at the far ends of the Cuteness Lineup or between any two animals.
As the line of animals gets longer, the guessing gets trickier!

WINNING:
When no one guesses correctly, one blobfish is turned over. When all 3 blobfish have been turned over, the game ends. The player with the most points is the champion!

2 PLAYER/COOPERATIVE VERSION:
Don’t want to keep score? Only have 2 players? No problem! Play the game cooperatively where all players work together to create the longest Cuteness Lineup possible before all 3 blobfish make their grand entrance!

Raise a paw if you're Feelin' Cute!

Deadwood Game’s first board game is a 4X game set in the American West. Each player’s objective is to gain a set number of Pioneer Points, or have the last Pioneer standing.

Welcome to the Wild West. It took you a few years, but you finally convinced your partner to make the trek. You’ve arrived at your destination and now it’s time to search the frontier, learn new skills, find resources, and start a family. The only problem? Living this far west of the Mississippi comes with its dangers. Death is not uncommon in these parts, and neither is famine, wild animals, and getting robbed. In order to survive, you must claim land and build on it, process resources, hire help, and grow your family to help with the tasks at hand. Don’t forget you need to be quick on the draw. If not, you’ll end up being just another Boot Hill in these parts.

boardgame: Эволюция: Новый мир – Круговорот (Evolution: New World – The Cycle)
description:
Эволюция: Новый мир – Круговорот ("Evolution: New World – The Cycle") is the second big expansion to the Evolution: New World.

A variety of microorganisms inhabit habitats and enter animal populations. Now you need to carefully choose food for your creatures. New breeding mechanics and new traits will help animals resist mutations and pathogens.

There are three new features in the Cycle expansion:

     Microbiota. Players can populate areas with various microorganisms that affect animal nutrition.
     Change Phase. Mutagens and Pathogens can affect animal properties and population.
     Cross-breeding. The basic action allows players to create new animals with desirable properties during the Feeding Phase.


Also, with this expansion, you can increase the number of players to 6.

boardgame: Эволюция: Новый мир – Метаморфозы (Evolution: New World – Metamorphoses)
description:
Эволюция: Новый мир – Метаморфозы ("Evolution: New World – Metamorphoses") is the mini expansion to the Evolution: New World.

The expansion contains 24 cards with six new traits. Just add these cards from this expansion to Evolution Deck.

Metamorphoses cards have a caterpillar symbol in the upper-right corner of each card.

Rumipoo is a light-hearted, family-friendly card game that blends classic rummy mechanics with a whimsical twist: adorable animals, magical unicorns… and unexpected poop!

Players take turns drawing and discarding cards to form melds—sets or runs of matching animal cards—to fill their personal Animal Sanctuary. Special cards like Poop disrupt opponents' progress by blocking their sanctuary spaces, while action cards let you steal from rivals, clear away poop, or play extra melds. Unicorns are rare and valuable creatures that can complete difficult melds or offer unique advantages.

The game continues until one player successfully fills their entire sanctuary with completed melds, winning the game and rescuing the most animals.

Easy to learn and packed with playful interaction, Rumipoo is designed for 2–4 players and is suitable for ages 7 and up.

Cowlien is a cooperative board game that aims to add up to the total by expressing the big and small numbers in cow calls so that they are all within the range of numbers indicated by small cards.

In the game, each player aims to evolve the cow into a cowrian (the evolved appearance of the cow in this game) by properly hitting the cow with a beam.

The start player will surface one card from the deck of the cow card, and all will check the numbers.

Starting with the first player, check the range of numbers on the cards clockwise, and release the beam cards back with a "Moo!" sound, which gives you a rough idea of what the loud and small numbers of the calls are.

When one person makes a cry and confirms it, all other players also cry "Moo!" When the power issues a beam card and everyone else cries, each player surfaces the beam card to confirm the result.

If the numbers on the beam cards are summed up and within the range of the number of cattle cards, the cow succeeds in evolution.

If the numbers are out of bounds, unfortunately, the beef becomes a steak. Place the steak on the cattle card and step aside.

Fox Two!! is a card-based aerial combat game set in the final years of the Cold War.

Designed for 2 to 5 players, each player takes command of a squadron of fighter aircraft, engaging in intense dogfights against their rivals in a bid for aerial supremacy.

In the basic game mode, the objective is a free-for-all battle royale where the player who shoots down the most enemy aircraft wins. However, surviving aircraft also contribute to your final score, and attracting too much attention can lead to concentrated attacks from other players. Striking a balance between offense and survival is key.

There are three types of attacks you can use against enemy fighters:

RHM (Radar Homing Missiles),
 IRM (Infrared Homing Missiles), and
 Guns (Autocannons).

Each aircraft has its own strengths and weaknesses with these weapons. For example, the F-14A Tomcat excels at long-range RHM attacks, while the F-16 Fighting Falcon is formidable in close-range IRM dogfights. The Soviet side boasts the powerful R-73 (AA-11 Archer) missile, which outperforms the Sidewinder and gives them a significant edge in IRM-based engagements.

Advanced scenarios introduce more strategic, war game-like gameplay. Rather than a simple battle royale, players can divide into Eastern and Western blocs and fight for control of the skies. Game styles include head-to-head duels, 2v2 team battles, and even hidden allegiance scenarios where you won’t know who’s friend or foe.

Despite covering many aspects of jet-age aerial warfare, Fox Two!! boasts remarkable playability: the rulebook is just three A4 pages long, and a full game can be played in under 30 minutes. The game is easy to enjoy even for players unfamiliar with modern air combat, while enthusiasts will find it easy to expand with custom aircraft and rules.

—description from the designer

🌿 Overview

Nestled within the Imperial City of Huế, Kien Trung Palace once stood as one of the grandest landmarks of the Nguyễn Dynasty. Its architecture was uniquely adorned with Khảm sành sứ, a traditional Vietnamese mosaic art made from broken ceramic and porcelain shards, handcrafted by artisans to decorate the palace’s exteriors with shimmering patterns.
Destroyed in 1947, it lay in ruins until its real-life restoration began in 2019.
Restoration: Kien Trung Palace invites players to relive that journey — to rebuild, innovate, and preserve this cultural treasure.

🧱 Gameplay

Across three years of construction, players act as restoration experts, balancing planning, craftsmanship, and influence.
Each year consists of two main phases:

Construction Phase: Place workers or artisans at warehouse gates (five interconnected rondel boards) to trigger actions and advance progress on various building projects.

Evaluation Phase: Compare influence across warehouse zones and community support to recruit new artisans and determine the next starting player.

Earn Achievement Points through completed buildings, technological advancements, management cards, and your growing team of artisans.

⚙️ Key Mechanics

Worker Placement & Area Control: Every placement impacts both your actions and your standing in key areas.

Five Interconnected Rondel Boards: The warehouse network forms the heart of the game — each rondel connects to others in many ways, creating replayability, dynamic chain reactions, and strategic movement puzzles.

Development Tracks: Advance in Technology for efficiency and new workers, or in Community Support to gain re-recruit ability

Management Cards: Provide flexible instant effects for strategic timing.

🏆 End of the Game

After the third construction year, players total Achievement Points from:

Completed construction boards

Both development tracks

Management cards bonuses

Recruited artisans (3 pts each)

The player with the highest score becomes the Master Restorer of Kien Trung Palace — the one who most faithfully revives history through skill, precision, and vision.

Players are a research team searching for fairies in Alfheim.
In the game, players place square cards like a puzzle, to create sets of 2 to 4 cards in one line.
Since the cards are connected vertically and horizontally, players try to arrange them effeciently.
Each player places their cards on their own field. Then, place the same kind tile that the active player drew on their field. This means that each player can enjoy the puzzle without being disturbed at all.

When a specific set is created, players place the corresponding fairy in the own area. This is a score.
After placing 12 rounds, the score is calculated.

The "Expert Cards" for finding a set of fairies faster, "Footprint Cards" that must be placed near the special clue cards side by side, and special characters, the King and Tomte (who help Santa in a nordic legend).

Furthermore, if you don't like the cards that you must place, you can randomly change the cards. This is another great feature of this game.

At the end of the game, you will find a large number of fairies painted with transparent watercolors spread out before you. Let's all enjoy the puzzle together.

—description from the designer

A card game for 2-5 players where you compete to collect the most loot points by the end of the game. Play powerful action cards to block opponents, steal treasure and shift the tides in your favour. Quick to learn and endlessly replayable.

Each player starts with a hand of 5 cards - 2 loot cards and 3 random cards from the deck. The rest of the cards form a central pile called the standing deck. On their turn a player must take a card from the standing deck then play an action card.

The action cards allow the player to steal cards from opponents, take extra cards from the standing deck or change the direction of play. Look out for the blue whale card which lets you block an attack from another player, and beware the swordfish that forces you to discard a loot card!

When the standing deck is empty, each player counts up the value of the loot cards in their hand and the player with the most loot wins.

Carrot Patch is a family friendly board game, where 2 to 4 rabbits race to collect carrots from the garden.

Using d6 dice; players roll and strategically advance towards the next carrot. By chance, a 1 or 6 is rolled, the player draws an Event Card and takes an action, or not.

Once all carrots have been picked up, the Player with the most carrots, wins. Tie game? Play again!

Sounds easy enough but beware, your carrots will get stolen!

—description from the designer

"This alley ain't big enough for all of us!"

Go claw to claw in a 2 to 5 player free for all card battler.

Every player draws from the same deck, fighting which ever cat you think deserves the beat down. Claws, teeth, or a pouch of catnip, everything is legal in this alley. Or maybe distract your opponent with a, LAZER!

Last cat standing owns the alley.

—description from the designer

Frontlines: Korea is a game of ground combat from 1950 through 1953. This set of rules simulates company to brigade sized actions of combined arms forces.

—description from the introduction

Frontlines: Korea 1953-includes the following:

     Rules for ground combat in the 1950s, including air support, artillery, and amphibious landings.
     Data for 25+ vehicles, 30+ aircraft, and 4 boats
     Complete combat charts and templates
     Organization charts for 30+ US, UN, and Communist units
     4 scenarios to get you started
     Scale is one model represents one vehicle, infantry units can be fire team size and up
     Ground scale is 50 meters to the inch


—description from the back of the book
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Chikki is a competitive ladder-climbing game using dominoes for 2 players. The objective is to play all of your tiles first.

In Chikki, players can match, or play higher ranked combos to shed tiles. The combos can suddenly escalate to higher combination types when a six suited meld is played. Also, players will have to draw tiles during the play and struggle to keep the lead.

—description from the designer

On the 18th of June 1815, Napoleon was defeated at Waterloo. The allies decided to exile him to the remote volcanic island of Saint Helena in the South Atlantic. 
A few plots were made to save the former Emperor from his exile between 1816 and 1820, but none of them succeeded. But what if one of them had succeeded? Here is YOUR chance to rewrite history by rescuing the Emperor from his exile! 
Needless to say: it won't be an easy task.

Release the Eagle is a hand management/deck building card game. In their turn, the players will be able to draw, play or reveal certain cards on one of the 7 locations of the island. They must beware, because some cards are disadvantageous and are best kept hidden. The players will attempt to collect 4 types of cards which serve as minimal requirements in order to attempt a rescue: a financier with money, means of transportation, an internal contact and an event to profit from. In order to be able to play them, the players must influence the Hudson Lowe mood track. From the moment that governor  Lowe lets his guard down (allowing you to play at least 4 cards), the Royal Navy and British patrols are not present on one of the 6 locations, then it is your moment to ensure the prisoner's escape...

Beware though: time is limited, because Napoleon is slowly being poisoned!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Tales of Derring Do: Rules for Adventures from Dime Novels and Pulp Magazines
description:
Pulp action rules.

Fancy some Pirate; Cowboy; Gangster; Superhero Jungle or Mysterious Orient action? 
Want to Fight Nazis, Dinosaurs and Vampires?

Then you'll need the latest in this very popular rule system.
 Includes scenarios, examples of play, and action cards to cut out.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Very Bad Lands: Therizinosaurus Promo Card
description: Long Claws: Every time I lose a life, I draw a Disaster card and remove it from the game without showing it to others.

Use the Christmas cards and your festive actions to be the first to reach twelve drummers drumming, or knock others out of the round. Can you be the last player standing or the first to do the victory drum roll?

The deck consists of numbered cards ranging from 1-11. Each player receives a 12 Drummers Drumming card which sits in front of them. Each player is dealt 6 cards, with the remainder making the draw pile. The player with a 1 card begins.
Each turn you have 3 options.

     play up to 6 cards, they must either match the last played card or be +1/-1 from the card below it.
     play a single action card (Nominate, Letter to Santa, Naughty List, Mistletoe).
     regift your hand (once per round) by flipping over your 12 Drummers Drumming card, discarding your hand and redrawing, ending your turn.


If you are unable to play a card or regift your hand, you are knocked out of the round.

The round ends when either
1 - you are the last player standing
or
2 - you are the first player to play you 12 Drummers Drumming card, by placing it on top of a number 11 card.

Play continues until one player has won 2 rounds.

Step into the exhilarating world of Flip N Forecast, where you harness your inner meteorologist and navigate the whimsical world of weather prediction. This thrilling card game tests your forecasting prowess as you predict whether the next card will be higher or lower, but remember the weather can change in a flash! Use special power cards to steer the forecast to your advantage and see if you can clear your pile to become the Round's Weather Champion! Flip N Forecast is a perfect blend of strategy, luck, and a touch of meteorological magic, with simple rules yet engaging gameplay. It's a fantastic game for friends and family, and with three compelling rounds, each match brings fresh excitement and challenges. The cumulative scoring system ensures the competition remains fierce until the final card is played. So, will you ascend to the title of Supreme Meteorologist, or will you be left struggling against the stormy weather? Only the flip of a card will decide in this captivating weather themed venture.

In the swirling currents of a feeding frenzy, even the smallest fish can rise to power… if they stick together. In this vibrant, underwater card game, schools of fish compete to dominate the feeding grounds by grouping up and chasing each other off. Outsmart your rivals, time your plays, and try not to be the one left behind when the food runs out.

Gunjo Gradation is a climbing game where cards of the same number can be grouped to form stronger plays, letting weaker fish gang up on bigger ones. But leftovers matter: unplayed cards cause penalties at the end of each round, and only matching numbers can be stacked to reduce the pain. Bonus points are awarded based on who escapes cleanest, with simple rules that escalate into a dynamic tactical puzzle.

The task of the "storyteller" is to choose one card and come up with an association for it, so that not everyone can solve it. The players first choose the most suitable association from their cards, and then try to guess which of all the cards belongs to the "narrator".

The game perfectly develops imagination, gives a ton of pleasure and teaches you to think unconventionally. One of the advantages is the opportunity to better understand the people who play with you, to feel their emotions more subtly, and to learn more about them through cards and associations.

The peculiarity of this version is that it is a travel box: the box is very small — it can easily fit not only in a backpack, but even in an ordinary bag. The box itself completely replaces the playing field: the chips are "put on" the side walls. This not only saves space, but also makes the game even more convenient: even if the transport suddenly shakes during the trip, not a single chip will budge.

Unlike the original Imaginarium, this version can be played by up to three people.
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play
Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
The board is build up of 11x11x special pins, which can be adjusted in three different hights: up = red marking, middel = white, down= black marking. Starting position of all pins is white. One player plays the red markings and in his turn he pulls up one pin. Then is the other players turn and he pushes one pin down to the black marking.

Winner will be the player with 5 of his markings in one row (also possible as diagonal).

An escape game set in the "Ostfriesenkrimi" world (popular East Frisia crime novels). Work through a deck of cards by solving logical, number and story puzzles to save a life.


Kult-Krimi aus Ostfriesland: Das originale Escape-Krimi-Spiel in der praktischen Kartenbox

Ein spannendes Escape-Krimi-Game an der Wirkungsstätte von Ann Kathrin Klaasen mit vielen bekannten Figuren und Schauplätzen aus der Erfolgsserie des Bestseller-Autoren Klaus-Peter Wolf. Eine interaktive Ergänzung zum Krimi-Kosmos der beliebten Ostfriesland-Krimis. Zum Um-die-Ecke-denken: Hier sind Kreativität und Kombinationsgeschick gefragt

Ein interaktiver Krimi für 1-4 Spieler ab 12 Jahren: Spaß für einen allein oder die ganze Familie
    50 spannende Karten im handlichen Format: Reichlich Rätselspaß für eine Stunde kriminelles Vergnügen. Dank der praktischen Kartenbox im Format 9,8 x 15,4 cm  ist dieses spannende Escape-Game überall spielbar – ob daheim oder auf Reisen
    Mehrfach spielbar: Im Spielverlauf bleiben die Karten intakt
    Tipps als Hilfestellung: Kommt man doch einmal nicht weiter, gibt es Hinweise auf der Website von arsEdition

Rätseln mit Zeitlimit: Nur wer zielführend ermittelt, löst den Fall innerhalb der vorgegebenen Zeitspanne. Die ultimative Herausforderung für Abenteurer, Spiele-Fans, Rätselprofis und Superhirne. Das Erfolgsrezept der Escape-Room Bücher von arsEdition als kniffliges Kartenspiel. Live-Escape-Feeling für Erwachsene: Nehmen Sie die Challenge an und schaffen es, den Ausgang zu finden?

Color Creeps is an exciting speed and dexterity game where players take on the roles of mages trying to capture colorful magical creatures. You'll put on different colored bracelets on both hands and try to be the fastest capturing cards of your colors. The player with the most cards when the deck is empty wins!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Village Pillage: Robin des Bois – 3 Cards Bonus Plato
description:
To diversify your games, Origames and Plato Magazine offer you three new village cards:

YEOMAN (Clorinda):
If the opponent is RICH, win 8 turnips
or Win 4 turnips

THIEF (Stephanie Gamwell):
Steal 3 turnips. Then, if the opponent is RICH, steal all the turnips of their Bank

REGENT :
Buy a Relic. Otherwise, if the opponent is RICH, gain 2 cards from the Market

Cards included in KS version but exclu (goodie) for French edition

Welcome to Joker Jailbreak! This is a single-player, physical card game you can play with a standard 52 card deck.
Created by Ramon Huiskamp (roofkat).

Setup:
1. Remove jokers from your set.
2. Place a joker on the table.
3. Shuffle the remaining cards.
4. Place stacks of six cards top-down on each of the four sides of the joker, forming the jail walls.
5. Place two cards top-down on each of the joker's four diagonals, forming the corners.
6. Place an open card on each wall and corner stack (you should end up with 8 open cards surrounding the joker).
7. The remaining cards are called the "Remainder".

Goal:
Clear out one of the four walls around the joker, so they can be free! Hurray!
(Clearing out a corner does not let the joker escape, they can't squeeze through the walls like that.)

How to play:
- Play black cards against red cards of the exact same score to get rid of them
  E.g.: a black 6 can be played against a red 6, or a red 4 and a red 2.
- Turn around the cards that were underneath the cards you played against each other.
    Repeat!

Rules:
- The Ace counts as 1, the Jack as 11, Queen as 12 and the King as 13.
- You can play cards from the Remainder on top of the joker, with a limit of three cards in a stack.
- These cards can be useful if you're stuck, and can be played anytime.
- However, they have to be cleared out in order to win, the joker can't escape with any card on top of him.

Fail state: 
You lose when you are no longer able to play any cards against each other.

Strategy:
Try to play away the walls as much as possible, try to avoid clearing out corners as it will limit your options, which i turn lowers your chances of clearing out a wall.

Alterations:
To change the difficulty, add or remove cards from the corners. More cards in the corners makes it easier, less cards in corners make it harder.

boardgame: War in the Pacific: A WW2 Roll & Write Game
description:
The year is 1942. Japan has rapidly expanded across the Pacific, bombing Pearl Harbor, capturing key territories, and threatening to dominate Asia. The United States, determined to halt this advance, begins a daring island-hopping campaign to weaken Japan’s hold and force their surrender. Can you lead the American forces to victory, or will the Japanese resistance force the United States to a negotiated settlement?

In War in the Pacific, you'll allocate limited resources to launch attacks, conduct strategic bombing, maintain supply lines, and seize key islands. Your strategy must be swift and decisive, targeting objectives like Guadalcanal, Iwo Jima, and Okinawa.  As the war intensifies, you can divert critical resources to the 
Manhattan Project, researching and developing nuclear weapons. The race to build atomic bombs could be the key to ending the war. Can you balance the demands of the Manhattan Project with the immediate needs of your campaign?

Once you've gained a foothold, you’ll face a crucial choice: invade mainland Japan in a costly land assault, or use strategic bombing and nuclear weapons to force Japan’s surrender. Each option is risky as an invasion could end the war quickly, but at great cost, while bombing and nukes may hasten Japan’s 
surrender, but could come too late.

With fast-paced dice allocation, resource management, and mounting pressure, every decision could mean the difference between victory or failure in your quest to force Japan’s capitulation.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Эволюция: Подарочное издание (Evolution: The Origin of Species – Gift Edition)
description:
Эволюция: Подарочное издание ("Evolution: The Origin of Species – Gift Edition") includes:

     the main game Evolution: The Origin of Species
     the expansion Time to Fly
     the expansion Continents
     18 additional cards with 3 traits ("r-Strategy", "Flighty", "Homeothermy")
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

boardgame: Gnomes vs Trolls: The Sneaky Fun Card Game
description:
Award-winning Gnomes vs Trolls card game has lots of sneaky fun to treasure! Enjoy a delightful mix of beginner strategy and unsuspecting surprises with this loveable family card game.

Gather the most treasure and save it from the tricky trolls to win!

Tricky trolls are lurking, turning up when they’re least expected. Flip a card to find your fate: a gnome, treasure, or a TROLL! Nine thieving trolls surprise unsuspecting gnomes, take their treasure, and put an end to their gathering games.

It's up to players to surround their treasure cards with gnomes to protect it, but remember, when the 9th troll card turns up, time's up!

Unique, three-sided cards with storybook illustrations give players lots of fun surprises to spot. The inventive pyramid "troll timer" creates suspense – no two rounds of the game play the same way! Quick setup and play makes Gnomes vs Trolls card game perfect for birthdays, parties, gatherings, and family game nights too.

—description from the publisher

What is banananbo? How many bananas? Judge the number of bananas faster than anyone else!

This real time card game challenges players to quickly play banana cards that are one rank higher or lower than the two that are in the center of the table. The challenge is that the cards have a variety of different ways of how they express the number that you are supposed to recognize. Some of the cards have banana slices and certain numbers, some of them have bananas, some of them have bananas with gorillas, holding the bananas, some of them have the number of bananas spelled out, and even there are some cards that have the numbers of bananas written out in Japanese.

Whoever plays all of their banana cards into the center of the table 1st is the winner.

Before After is a quick-thinking word-linking game. Players divide into two teams, each is given a stack of cards with three words on them. In the center of the table, 10 cards with two letters, each displayed in a way to make it easy for both teams to see it are placed on the board. You leave a space in the center to put a word card and the player that puts it out decides which of the three words colors that they’re going to use before flipping the card. Players then have to call out a word that can compound with the selected active word before it, or after it as either a compound word, an expression, or some other saying that is easily recognizable. If they do come out a word that uses one of the four letters that are on The side of the word, CRDE, they get to take that letter card and continue with the newly accessible letter card on one side and the previous one on the other side. Once a team or a player has acquired three letter cards, the word card is awarded to the Person or Team who got the three cards. This continues until 15 cards are collected and that player or team wins.

“Where pictures are worth a thousand words” - Players take turns being the Photobomber. The game starts with the Photobomber choosing a SECRET picture card and writing down a clue to describe the card! Everyone else can only see the clue "In Trouble"! Now they anonymously submit a card from their hand they think best matches the clue. All the cards are shuffled & everyone has to vote on who they think the Photobomber was!
Tally up your scores + move your pieces across the board!

boardgame: World War III: Team Yankee – Israeli
description:
56 page Hardback Book
     Backgrounds on the Israeli forces in World War III: Team Yankee
     Instructions on how to build Israeli forces including Merkava 3, Merkava 2, Merkava 1, Magach 6, and Sho’t Tank Companies, as well as Mech Infantry, Reserve Infantry, and Paratrooper Companies.

     Also includes FREE redemption codes to unlock the lists on Forces and PDF download on FOW Digital

This theme deck features the Metallic Splatterkins and the visionary Tazara Smith, coming with a full deck of 39 cards and Inspiration and Paint tokens, plus two Gem tokens for Tazara's ability.
This features the Monochrome Splatterkins, led by visionary Collette Nox, coming with a full deck of 39 cards and Inspiration and Paint tokens.

boardgame: 2D6 Swashbuckler: Solo Adventures in the Age of Honor
description:
Solo Gaming in the Age of Honor
"You should have stayed in the tavern, monsieur," the masked rogue sneered, parrying with effortless grace.
"And let you escape with my purse and my honor? Never," the nobleman replied, driving forward with a sharp lunge.
A flick of the rogue’s wrist sent the noble’s hat flying, but a moment later, the rogue stumbled as his opponent’s riposte struck true.
"First blood is mine," the nobleman declared, leveling his rapier. "Shall we see who leaves this square on their feet?“

2d6 Swashbucklers is a solo LiteRPG of high adventure set in the Era of the Swashbuckler—an age of duels, intrigue, and daring escapades. 
From the courtly halls of Paris to the streets of London, every encounter is driven by fast-paced 2d6 mechanics that let you carve your legend in a world of swordplay and romance. 
Will you fight for glory, gold, or revenge?

Inside you’ll find:


     A complete RPGLite THW system
     New Battle Board movement and action system
     More combat choices and weapons that influence in-game mechanics
     A full campaign and adventure generator
     A Dueling system for "Affaires d'honneur"
     New Tokens and Battle Boards


Draw your steel, step into the fray, and live by the blade… or die by it!

Powered by the Chain Reaction System by Two Hour Wargames

—description from the publisher

boardgame: La Copa de Las Casas Harry Potter
description:
There are four Houses in Hogwarts: Gryffindor, Ravenclaw, Hufflepuff and Slytherin, and they all compete for the House Cup throughout the school year.

In this game, the achievements and failures of each House can earn them points or lose them points. Points are tracked with gems for each House.

Students gain points for successfully navigating the board, rolling dice when casting spells, and even bid all their points on a single roll. Also, players will try to recover the golden snitch by bidding a number from 1 to 6 and rolling a single die to match it.

The first player to get to 300 points is declared the winner.

Have you ever wanted to resurrect the ancient beasts that were extinct? Imagine the thrill of reviving ancient beasts that disappeared centuries ago - would you recover what was lost through time? Now, the power to make this a reality is in your hands. Grab your gene mixer and complete the fragmented dna. With each recovered gene strain, you'll get closer to recreating these magnificent beings, bridging the gap between past and future. Your mission: to recover and reconstruct the genetic code from ancient, fragmented strands and assemble them into an unparalleled miracle of science. It's time to unlock the secrets of life itself and breathe new existence into the extinct.

Dino Lab is a puzzle game where you are tasked with completing genes of various dinosaurs but to finish a gene pool you will have to use common aspects of different beasts. Experiment and solve the biological puzzle of life

The game is divided into 25 rounds. Each round players simultaneously fill test tubes in the DNA mixer in order to create dinosaurs. The goal of the game is to create as many complete sentences of dinosaurs' DNA as possible.

Players will get points depending on the accuracy of a DNA. The game ends when all test tubes in a DNA mixer are filled. The player with the most points is a winner.

—description from the publisher

Dowsing Mine / ダウジングマイン is a logic puzzle game played using the digital representation of the alphabet.

The object is to guess the letters used in your opponent's word (3-5 letters) before they do by alternating questions to find out where the letters are located. Use your puzzle skills (and a little English) to decipher the letters faster than your opponent!

—description from the publisher

Se-kata is a card game about syllables. To play it, all you need to do is come up with a word as fast as you can with the syllables provided before the other players can do the same. This game is great for children who are learning to read, but is also a lot of fun for adults! Have fun and test your knowledge of the Indonesian language.

Get ready for ludicrous guesses, ridiculous comparisons and argument-inducing approximations in this fast-paced party game.

Quick to answer and hilarious to quarrel over, these aren't your regular quiz questions. With 300 cards and 900 questions to choose from, challenge your opponents to wacky guesstimations like how high the Eiffel Tower is in adult male ostriches, or how long the longest ever human hair was in French fries. Who will get roughly the right answers to win the game?

—description from the designer

In Jack, you’ll explore the foggy streets of 1800s London, uncover clues, and follow a dark trail of mystery. Your mission: catch Jack before he strikes again. Trace the path on the map, defeat the criminals you encounter, and find the clues that will lead you to the place where Jack will strike. Stop him before he succeeds in his evil plan.

To play, print the map, the 8 cards, and the 8 tokens. You’ll also need a pencil and two six-sided dice. Shuffle the cards to create the deck and place it face down on the table.

Start from the designated starting point on the map and trace the paths you take as you explore. When you reach a point of interest marked with a number, draw a card and try to capture the enemy using your skills and the dice. If you succeed, you’ll earn a clue.

Once you’ve collected 3 different clues, compare your combination with the table in the rulebook. It will tell you where to go on the map to face the final boss, “Jack.” Defeat Jack to win the game!

—description from the designer

An engaging quiz board game that introduces children to prehistoric topics by exploring the most fascinating dinosaurs and the prehistoric era of dinosaurs.

The game board is double-sided: one side depicts prehistoric times when dinosaurs lived, and the other shows the Ice Age and the appearance of the first mammals.

The game can be played in 3 different ways and also includes quiz cards, offering an easy and enjoyable way to learn.

The game is designed for school-aged children and presents information included in the curriculum in a pleasant and fun way.

Learning through play!

—description from the publisher

On a turn a player spins a spinner that shows 6 options: 1/2/3/4/wild/stop. The numbers 1-4 tell you how many fields you move your dino standee on a 1-dimensional path. Wild is number you choose from 1 to 4. Stop means you lose a turn. After landing on a filed you usually answer a trivia question or are presented with a riddle made from dino tokens. If you answer correctly, you move again, otherwise is the nex player's turn. The path on the board is divided into three time periods. When a player finishes the first period receives that period's token and all other players are teleported into this space and the game moves on. When the last period token is claimed the game ends and the player who collected the most period tokens wins.

—user summary

In the game of wizarding you will be constructing a dungeon maze to help wizards escape. Along the way the wizards will battle monsters, learn unique spells and fortell the future through prophecy.

You will score points for the number of exits you connect to the center of the dungeon, the number of wizards you help escape, the number of monsters they defeat, and the number of shrines found, along with any prophecies that are fortold.

—description from the designer

description:

Join the starters by collecting pledges while overcoming the challenges of the route!

Choose a scenario card, create teams and move the Easter procession in time to the music, trying to catch pledges (wooden tokens). The team that reaches 25 points first wins.

Will you be able to keep up with the pace to his temple?

New empire is created in the Whispering Grove as the settlers from all across the land swarm into the forest to start a new life. Dense, enchanted woodland deep in the territory of the Wood Elves hidden between the highest mountain ranges of the land. Over the years this place became a home of nomadic tribes like Felines, Taurens or Blood Elves. After the war torn the rest of the continent, the grove became a source of hope for the settlers from the broken lands.

Whispering Grove is an expansion for Kingdom Reborn. New map will bring more replayability to your game as well as three Tribe Cards to add to your future games of Kingdom Reborn. Divide the land for your future citizens and bring forth the era of peace to the land that only knew conflict.

—description from the publisher

Largest of the empires shares their land with those seeking refuge and new life. Land of Ursa Tribe, a place known for its hunters and warriors is vast, filled with lush forests and rich fields only waiting to be sown. Tribes from all across the Miralune swarm onto the Great Empire in a search of new opportunities.

Great Empire is an expansion for Kingdom Reborn that brings a new map with modified rules as well as Ursa Tribe Card to bring more variety to your future games of Kingdom Reborn. Divide the land for your future citizens and bring forth the era of peace to the land that only knew conflict.

—description from the publisher

On the North of the continent, adventurers of the Gnome Tribe discovered a new archipelago that stretches across the horizon. Land without the scars of war and a promise of a new beginning fills the Tribes with hope. Teleportation crystal is already placed and the magic swirls across the island awaiting the first settlers to make it their new home.

Forgotten Isles is an expansion for Kingdom Reborn that brings two whole new maps as well as Gnome Tribe Card to bring more variety to your future games of Kingdom Reborn. Divide the land for your future citizens and bring forth the era of peace to the land that only knew conflict.

—description from the publisher

Settlers from a wide array of tribes embark on a journey to the new land, bringing with them the rich traditions, customs, and wisdom of their respective cultures. As they arrive, these traditions become the cornerstone of their adaptation and survival in the unfamiliar environment. As they settle in, the blending and sharing of these diverse practices weave together a new, vibrant community, built on the strengths of their cultural heritage.

Tradition Cards is an expansion for Kingdom Reborn that brings the whole new module to the game. Draw helpful tradition cards from the deck of 9 cards and use them to create the greatest empire that will be known for centuries. Divide the land for your future citizens and bring forth the era of peace to the land that only knew conflict.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Meowtopia!

Before you lies a unique space, a room dedicated to feline contentment, centered around the most satisfying carpet a cat could dream about. This is Meowtopia, a sanctuary taking shape, and you are the architects of its joy. Cats have gathered, watching with anticipation as you begin your work.

Your thoughtful creations will transform this room into a tapestry of climbing delights and restful retreats. Piece by piece, you'll create environments where cats can indulge their natural instincts to climb, perch, and observe.

Plan your actions, collect necessary Cat Tree pieces, place them wisely, and watch the purrs (and points!) accumulate. Let's create the most wonderfully happy cat environment ever conceived!

In Meowtopia, players are tasked to create the most joyful, purrfect room imaginable. A place that every cat would call heaven.

On their turn, players take actions involving collecting colorful Cat Tree pieces from the market and using them to create Cat Trees within the shared room. Players can also improve a Cat Tree by adding pieces upon an existing Cat Tree and an Happy Cat piece on top of it, meaning that this cat is fully satisfied and can now observe the world around him.

—description from the publisher

Klondike TrickTaker is a solo trick-taking game based on the classic solitaire game of Klondike. In Klondike TrickTaker, you must try to clear the tableau of cards by playing tricks.

Klondike TrickTaker is played as a may-follow trick taking game, and is set up like classic solitaire with seven columns of cards. Players hold an hand of six cards at all times! Each turn, you’ll play the frontmost card from your hand to beat any card played by your opponent: the face-up card in any column! As long as the card you played is a higher rank and the same suit, you’ll discard your played card and add the defeated card to the back of your hand. If you clear all the columns, you’ve won! But as in solitaire, if you can’t beat any of the showing cards, you’ll have to discard from your hand to draw new cards.

Get the highest score you can by clearing columns with as few draws as possible!

7th century BC. Majestic Greek triremes full of wine and jewels are docking at the shores of the future Bosporan Kingdom. Ashore are coming the settlers, including you, young architects from Miletus, who are here to build the future capital, the city of Pantikapaion. The new city is just beginning to emerge, and it is longing for your skills and talent.
You are observing the new lands around you: the inhabitants of Pantikapaion need houses to live in, aqueducts to irrigate vineyards and arid lands, the hills are the best location for watchtowers, and the top of Mount Mithridat is perfect for a majestic temple of Apollo. Hire workers and spend your wealth to speed up the construction of the city. After all, only one of you will get the glory of the  greatest urban planner!

How to play
1. Choose a location tile and add it to your city. You can either expand your city or raise it.

2. Perform 1 of the 3 possible actions.

Construct a building
Choose a building, return the required resources to the common supply and construct this building in your city. After that you advance on the development board, thus gaining valuable bonuses and raising your chance of scoring more points at the end of the game.

Sell resources
Return the chosen resources to the common supply and gain coins. Do not forget to advance your markers on the corresponding development board tracks and gain bonuses.

Gain 2 coins or 1 resource

3. To get resources from location tiles in your city you can either cover them with other tiles and building or send workers to collect them.

4. A game is played over 5 rounds. At the end of the game, you will get score victory points for your buildings, resources and coins in your reserve and probably lose points for visible drought spaces.

The player who has gained the most victory points becomes the winner. Honor and glory to the great architect!

—description from the publisher

Have you ever had a place you wanted to visit? To conquer the highest mountains, race with wind over the vast fields or to find the secrets of the deepest jungles? Pick your objective and explore places you have always wanted to see.

Destination Cards is a module for The Wayfarer that consists of nine objective cards giving you more things to account for while playing the game as well as map of Kenya for you to test the module on. This module is compatible with each of the Wayfarer Expansions.

Kenya's landscape is a stunning mix of savannas, mountains, and coastal beauty. The iconic savannahs of the Maasai Mara and Amboseli National Park are home to vast herds of wildlife, with the towering Mount Kenya providing a striking backdrop. To the west, the Great Rift Valley offers dramatic escarpments and expansive lakes, such as Lake Nakuru and Lake Naivasha. The coastal region along the Indian Ocean features pristine beaches, coral reefs, and palm-lined shores. Kenya’s landscapes are a captivating blend of wilderness, mountain ranges, and tropical coastlines.

Travel across different countries and see the beauty of the landscape often taken for granted. Pick up a bundle and create your own path.

—description from the publisher

Grab your ticket and make you way through countries with trains, boats and buses. Enjoy your sightseeing adventure and travel in style.

Transit Pass is a module for The Wayfarer that will change the randomness of the dice for the deck of 12 cards as well as the map of Iceland for you to test the module on.. This module is compatible with each of the Wayfarer Expansions.

Iceland’s landscape is otherworldly, characterized by dramatic volcanic terrain, geothermal hot springs, and expansive glaciers. Majestic waterfalls like Gullfoss and Seljalandsfoss cascade down cliffs, while black sand beaches, such as Reynisfjara, stretch along the coast. The island’s volcanic activity has created lava fields, rugged mountains, and geothermal geysers, including the famous Geysir. Iceland’s remote beauty is accentuated by vast fjords, sprawling tundras, and the stunning Northern Lights, making its landscape both raw and enchanting.

Travel across different countries and see the beauty of the landscape often taken for granted. Pick up a bundle and create your own path.

—description from the publisher

Travel through the islands of Japan. Make your way through Kyushu, Honshu and Hokkaido creating your own path. This Expansion for The Wayfarer consists of three different maps that have modified rules.

Explore Hokkaido, Japan’s northernmost island, known for its breathtaking landscapes. Vast fields of wildflowers stretch across expansive plains, bordered by rolling mountains and volcanic peaks. In winter, the region transforms into a snow-covered wonderland, with powdery snow blanketing forests and coastal cliffs. Hot springs, serene lakes, and expansive national parks further highlight Hokkaido’s natural beauty.

Honshu, Japan's largest island, offers a diverse and dynamic landscape. The island is home to towering mountains like the iconic Mount Fuji, lush forests, and fertile plains, especially around regions like Kansai and Kanto. Along its coastline, you’ll find dramatic cliffs, sandy beaches, and serene bays. Honshu is also dotted with bustling cities, tranquil temples, and rice paddies, offering a stunning contrast between city and nature.

Kyushu, Japan's southernmost main island, is known for its lush, mountainous terrain and active volcanic landscapes. The island features hot springs like those in Beppu and volcanic peaks such as Mount Aso, one of the world's largest active volcanoes. Its coastlines are dotted with beautiful beaches, rugged cliffs, and serene bays. Dense forests cover much of the interior, while the subtropical climate along the southern coast nurtures vibrant plant life.

Pick up a bundle and create your own path.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Stadt Land Vollpfosten: Extrem – Classic Edition
description:
Now it's getting EXTREME! We've given STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN a nasty twist. You're looking for a suitable word for a category. You know ... But! Certain letters have to be in the word ... and others are strictly forbidden. And sometimes it's the other way round. Who can find the right words?

—description from the publisher

Jetzt wird‘s EXTREM! Wir haben STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN noch einen fiesen Dreh verpasst. Ihr sucht ein passendes Wort zu einer Kategorie. Kennt man ... Aber! Bestimmte Buchstaben müssen im Wort stecken ... und andere sind streng verboten. Und manchmal ist alles umgekehrt. Wer findet die richtigen Worte?

—description from the publisher (German)

Visit Europe and all of it’s history and glory. Make you way through England, Germany and France. Some landscapes though are so beautiful you’ll have to see them twice.

Europe Map Pack is an expansion for the Wayfarer with three completely different maps for you to play on and new rules that you will have to strategise around while marking every sight.

England's landscape is a picturesque blend of rolling hills, lush green meadows, and charming countryside. The countryside is dotted with ancient stone villages, winding rivers, and expansive farmland. To the north, rugged moors and sweeping highlands, like those in the Lake District and the Yorkshire Dales, contrast with the gentle, pastoral beauty of the south. The coastline offers dramatic cliffs, sandy beaches, and secluded coves, while historic forests, such as Sherwood Forest, add a sense of enchantment to the land. England’s landscapes feel timeless, where nature and history intertwine seamlessly.

See the incredibly diverse landscape of Germany, featuring dense forests, rolling hills, and majestic mountain ranges like the Bavarian Alps. The country is known for its expansive rivers, such as the Rhine and Elbe, which carve through fertile valleys and picturesque vineyards. To the north, the coastline offers sandy beaches and striking cliffs along the North Sea and Baltic Sea. The countryside is dotted with charming villages, ancient castles, and lush meadows, while the Black Forest in the southwest and the vast plains in the east provide a variety of scenic beauty. Germany’s landscapes combine natural splendor with a rich cultural backdrop.

France offers a stunning variety of landscapes for you to experiance, from the rugged coastlines of Normandy and the Mediterranean to the lush vineyards of Bordeaux and Burgundy. The French Alps and Pyrenees provide dramatic mountain scenery, perfect for hiking, skiing, and breathtaking views. In contrast, the rolling hills of Provence are dotted with lavender fields, while the Loire Valley boasts elegant châteaux set against a serene, river-lined backdrop. The country also features picturesque countryside, charming villages, and vast agricultural plains, making France’s landscapes as diverse as they are beautiful.

Travel across different countries and see the beauty of the landscape often taken for granted. Pick up a bundle and create your own path.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Wayfarer: North America Map Pack
description:
Travel through the continent of North America. Mark your print in Texas, Colorado or Quebeck. Places so large you will have to set up your own campsites to explore it all.

North America Map Pack is an expansion for the Wayfarer with three completely different maps for you to play on and new rules that will change the way you play.

Texas offers a vast and diverse landscape, ranging from sweeping deserts to lush forests. The western part of the state is dominated by rugged deserts and dramatic mountain ranges like the Davis Mountains, while the central region features rolling hills and grasslands. The eastern part of Texas is rich in dense forests, with tall pine trees and swamplands, especially near the Gulf Coast. Along the coast, you'll find sandy beaches and wetlands. Texas’s landscape is expansive and varied, blending arid plains, vibrant wildflowers, and scenic river valleys.

Make way through the Colorado landscape is dominated by the stunning Rocky Mountains, with towering peaks, rugged cliffs, and deep valleys. The state features alpine lakes, dense forests, and expansive meadows, offering breathtaking views in every direction. To the east, the land transitions into high plains, while the western part is marked by deserts and canyons, such as those found in the Colorado Plateau. Colorado's diverse geography also includes wild rivers, scenic mountain passes, and areas of vibrant wildflowers, making it a paradise for outdoor enthusiasts and nature lovers alike.

Explore the diverse landscape of Quebeck, blending lush forests, rolling hills, and expansive lakes. The province is known for its vast boreal forests that stretch across much of the land, as well as the majestic Laurentian Mountains to the north. Along its southern border, the St. Lawrence River winds through fertile valleys, while in the east, the Gaspé Peninsula offers dramatic cliffs and coastal beauty. Quebec's landscape also includes picturesque villages, vibrant fall foliage, and remote wilderness areas, making it a place of natural wonder and tranquility.

Travel across different countries and see the beauty of the landscape often taken for granted. Pick up a bundle and create your own path.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vineyard: A Winemaking Game – Local News Mini-Expansion
description:
The Local News Mini-Expansion adds random events that make the Paperwork action more exciting for all players. Add 20 cards that bring interesting challenges and bonuses to the vineyard!

—description from the publisher

L'enclume - Mots & Images is the return of the already iconic game L'Enclume. Discover 200 brand-new "Enclume" (anvil) cards and 100 "Enclume" (anvil) image cards.

The goal of the game remains the same. You’ll need to use your wit to become the heaviest by guessing the most offbeat puns and visual wordplays.

The player who first guesses the pun or image correctly wins the card and the weight assigned to it.

boardgame: Unsolved Case Files: Wallet Case – Tara Noble
description:
MYSTIC POINT, T.X. – A wallet lost six months ago has just been found at Mystic Point Train Station – its contents untouched, its owner nowhere to be found. It looks like a simple case of lost property… but it isn’t.

Inside, you’ll find personal items, cryptic notes, and clues that don’t quite add up. The deeper you dig, the stranger it gets.

Your investigation begins with the wallet in hand, but the trail leads to unsettling connections, unanswered questions, and people who claim to see more than what’s in front of them.

Can you follow the clues, separate fact from illusion, and uncover the truth?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Irregular Miniatures Pike and Shot Rule Box
description:
The aim of these rules is to produce an enjoyably fast yet realistic game through the Late Medieval, Renaissance and Marlburian periods. They can also stretch to refighting Clive's exploits in India and the Jacobite Risings.

The rules are designed for use with the Irregular Miniatures 6mm strips. Having said that, they can be used with any scale of figures with the relative changes to ranges and groundscale.

—description from the introduction

A lethal disease and meltdown from abandoned nuclear power plants due to the pandemic have caused the demise of civilization. Individuals who did not succumb to radioactivity or disease survive in scattershot communities. Some of these survivors get by a shopping mall but, as supplies fall short, they are forced to scavenge the ruins of their city for sustenance.

Set in a post-apocalyptic scenario, Apocalypcity is a game where players explore the ruins of their city and compete to gather resources that will grant them leadership of their community.

The goal of the game is to explore the city by revealing unexplored zone cards and obtaining resources, recover supplies and return them to the shopping mall.

Players will recover supplies that will help them explore the city faster, overcome other player´s attacks and zone effects or steal recourses from other players.

—description from the publisher

WordSnap, the word-building, puzzle game that doesnt restrict your play with the confines of a gameboard.  Octogonal soft-snap pieces let you craft words vertically, horizontally, and diagonally; creating a crossword puzzle-like grid. As you build words, unique patterns emerge, offering players unlimited scoring opportunities.  Whether you are a seasoned wordsmith or a newcomer to word games, WordSnap promises a challenging, immersive experience that invites you to flex your vocabulary from multiple angles.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Last Night at the Lucky Lad Casino
description:
Are you feeling lucky?

Last Night at the Lucky Lad Casino is a fast-paced party game, where you’ll need to outwit and gamble your way to victory against up to 7 other players on only the fairest of slot machines!

Players compete to be the last person standing at various slot machines that are put up for retirement by the casino. If a player is ever the only one at a slot machine, they get a free pull!

Gameplay revolves around a player's decision to remain at their current machine or to go to the newest machine revealed that turn.

This decision is simultaneously revealed by all players at the table!

—description from the publisher

Push yourself to the brink of bankruptcy in a high stakes game of spelling and debt!

Players compete to win tricks against one another, which in turn adds letters to your spelling tableau. The more letters you win, the more words that you can spell, but be careful, as all unused letters put you further and further in debt at the end of each round.

—description from the publisher

Sammies! is a game where sandwich shop owners compete to make the biggest and most coveted sandwiches at their local food court!

Each player has a limited amount of "counter space" where they'll be preparing 5 and only 5 sandwiches... So, it'll be up to you to stack them high with as many matching ingredients as you can, all while getting rid of the ingredients you don't need!

Players secure their sandwich ingredients from a randomly drawn market and through trades with other players. You'll need to be shrewd judge of character, however, as sometimes players will be forced to complete trades with ingredient cards that are face down and hidden from view...

Can you really take them at their word that they're giving you two Philly Cheesesteaks for your one Roast Beef? Or are they ACTUALLY just trying to offload a bunch of useless Tuna Salad?

Tough to say... but it's a cutthroat business when you're slinging sammies for a living.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kamigami Battles: Astaroth, The Great Duke Promo Card
description:
Released via the Japanime Games Patreon.

Astaroth, The Great Duke is promo card # KBP-017

Act: Banish a Warrior from your hand to reduce a God's energy by 2.
React: Banish any Disciple to add +1 to an Attack.

React: Pay 1 Energy to Discard a Defending Disciple.
