boardgame: Oshi Push: Origins Video Promo Card
description:
Available through the Japanime Games Patreon.

OPDD/PC1-144

3 Coins, Experience, 6 Stars

When you play an Experience Content card (including this one): This card gains +2 Stars this round.

This is a tongue-in-cheek mini game from Excalibur Games that was produced as a convention giveaway which included a list of some games for sale.

I am told that they had different sheets that represented local teams of the particular convention area. The names of the teams were changed to a local game club and an opponent at the time of the convention.

This particular one is the Minnesota Minnesota Vikings and the Oakland Raiders and was handed out at an early Pacificon Game Expo, Santa Clara, CA, circa 1980. Printed on silver card stock.

A little research reveals that the only time the Oakland Raiders played the Minnesota Vikings on Labor Day weekend, which is when the Pacificon game expos are traditionally held, was 1980. I believe we can conclude this is a good way to date this particular game.

The game is meant as a joke. However, there is a actually a very simplified version of a football game listed on this page. They don't identify how mark the Scrimmage line, a square block. Die roles determine the play, the penalties, the field goal and the extra point. Allowances are made for penalties and a rough copy of a football field is used. There are penalties.

ACE 187 is it two sided, three player, four unit game simulation or war game depending on which term you prefer. A fifth piece is also included.

This is a tongue-in-cheek game/catalog was handed out at Origins '78 between July 13-16, 1978. Realistically, it does appear to be more of a clever way to make people look at the companies list of games.

This has to be the simplest game, ever of the raid at Entebbe. There is a counter for the hostages, the PLO unit, ugandan, headquarters, a Meg, and Israeli commandos. The map is seven hexes with six wrapped around the center seventh.

At the start of the game, all counters, except the Israeli commandos are put on the map. The Israeli unit lands on the airport. Combat is simple. The PLO, Meg, hostage and HQ units cannot move the Israel unit may attack on landing. Combat is after landing. Israel's win on a roll of 1 to 6 and free the hospital. Israel always wins. However, the Ugandan player may use the radio to announce that murders are not being committed and is proof foreign reporters may come to you again and look to see if there are nobody's laying around.

Each player is given two Conclusion cards, and the first player draws a card from the trivia deck, picking one of the three statements to read aloud. The other players decide whether the statement is true or false, placing the corresponding Conclusion card on the table. Each correct guess earns 1 point, and after the deck is exhausted, the person with the highest score wins.

boardgame: What Do You Meme?: Ancient Memes Expansion Pack
description: Even more memes! 115 Cards (90 caption cards & 25 photo cards) to be added to core game.

In a themed Anglo-French deck, Heroes (kings of all suits) wield  Spears (spades) to battle Monsters (clubs) to gain Treasure (diamonds) and attract the most Followers (hearts). Players draw up to five cards on their turn, adding one Follower to their troop, and playing any Monsters drawn in front of them or in front of other players. Then they attack the Monsters by playing Spears: if these total equal or more than the value of the monster, they can place a Treasure card from their hand to their horde. If the monster is still alive, it counterattacks by taking its remaining point value in Followers or cards still in hand to the discard pile.

When the draw pile is depleted, the Hero with the highest point value in Followers and Treasures wins.

Seek and find! Be the first to discover over 1200 objects in 8 different and messy places! Can you find a pin or a helicopter amidst the chaos that prevails in an old kitchen or an old office before the other players catch up with you? But be careful because your opponents may turn off your lights, make you search from a distance or even search… upside down!

Ψάξε βρες το! Ανακάλυψε πρώτος πάνω από 1200 αντικείμενα σε 8 διαφορετικά και ακατάστατα μέρη! Μπορείς να βρεις μια καρφίτσα ή ένα ελικόπτερο ανάμεσα στον χαμό που επικρατεί σε μια παλιά κουζίνα ή σε ένα παλιό γραφείο πριν σε προλάβουν οι υπόλοιποι παίκτες; Πρόσεξε όμως γιατί οι αντίπαλοί σου μπορεί να σου κλείσουν τα φώτα, να σε κάνουν να ψάχνεις από απόσταση ή ακόμα και να ψάχνεις… ανάποδα!

In Smugglers of Kaimas, players rely on bluffing and push-your-luck strategies to transfer resources from their Inventory to their Stockpile, enabling the completion of vital Projects and the accumulation of enough Influence to secure victory.

The game’s thrill lies in the ever-changing dynamics of the Checkpoint, where the pace can accelerate or slow down unpredictably, compelling you to adapt your tactics. Smuggle more resources than you declare to outsmart your opponents, but tread carefully — random inspections may expose your deceit, causing you to forfeit your shipment and fall behind in the race for dominance.

Objective: Be the first to reach the required Influence threshold based on available time to play:
    
Quick Game: 7 Influence. Full Game: 10 Influence.

—description from the publisher

Incredible underground images full of small and large hidden objects await you. How observant are you? Choose the image you like the most and get ready, an underground journey begins!

A player draws a card and leaves it in the center of the table. Together you see the word and try to find the object on your card. Each object is depicted differently on each card. Find it first, press the bell and win the card! Be careful, action cards can turn the game upside down. Make your opponent search from a distance or upside down and make it even more difficult for him!

Απίστευτες underground εικόνες γεμάτες μικρά και μεγάλα κρυμμένα αντικείμενα σε περιμένουν. Πόσο παρατηρητικός/ή είσαι; Διάλεξε την εικόνα που σου αρέσει περισσότερο και ετοιμάσου, ένα underground ταξίδι ξεκινά!

Ένας παίκτης τραβά μία κάρτα και την αφήνει στο κέντρο του τραπεζιού. Όλοι μαζί βλέπετε τη λέξη και ψάχνετε να βρείτε το αντικείμενο στην καρτέλα σας. Κάθε αντικείμενο απεικονίζεται διαφορετικά σε κάθε καρτέλα. Bρες το πρώτος/η, πάτα το κουδούνι και κέρδισε την κάρτα! Πρόσεχε, οι κάρτες ενεργειών μπορούν να ανατρέψουν το παιχνίδι. Βάλε τον αντίπαλο να ψάχνει από απόσταση ή ανάποδα και δυσκόλεψέ τον ακόμη περισσότερο!

Warning! Don't pee on yourself. Clean pants not included. The ultimate game to have a great time on your night, your birthday or an entire weekend! An easy, fast, extremely challenging game! If you hate reading rules, this is made for you!

The boss asks a question with a black card and the rest answer with their funniest white card. The boss will decide (as always happens!) which is the funniest answer and the player who gave it wins the black card. The player with the most black cards at the end of the game wins! Of course, you decide when the game will end, it could be never!

But why is the game called Talk to the Hand? 10 fantastic white cards with the same name are included in the set. If you hold one of these cards and can't find another one that matches the question being played, then luck is on your side! Play this card and with the well-known Talk to the Hand gesture show the others how much of a loooser they are! All you need is humor. It's that simple!

The game includes

85 black cards
415 white cards
Instruction booklet

—description from the publisher (translated)

Προσοχή! Μην κατουρηθείτε πάνω σας. Δεν περιλαμβάνεται καθαρό παντελόνι. Το απόλυτο παιχνίδι για να περάσεις όμορφα το βράδυ σου, τα γενέθλιά σου ή ένα ολόκληρο Σαββατοκύριακο! Ένα εύκολο, γρήγορο, άκρως προκλητικό παιχνίδι! Αν σιχαίνεσαι να διαβάζεις κανόνες, είναι φτιαγμένο για σένα!

Το αφεντικό ρωτά με μία μαύρη κάρτα και οι υπόλοιποι απαντούν με την πιο αστεία λευκή τους κάρτα. Το Αφεντικό θα αποφασίσει (όπως συμβαίνει πάντα άλλωστε!) ποια είναι η πιο αστεία απάντηση και ο παίκτης που την έδωσε κερδίζει τη μαύρη κάρτα. Ο παίκτης με τις περισσότερες μαύρες κάρτες στο τέλος του παιχνιδιού, κερδίζει! Φυσικά, εσείς αποφασίζετε πότε θα τελειώσει το παιχνίδι, μπορεί και ποτέ!

Μα γιατί το παιχνίδι λέγεται Talk to the Hand; 10 φανταστικές συνονόματες λευκές κάρτες περιέχονται στο σετ. Αν κρατάς μία τέτοια κάρτα και δεν βρίσκεις κάποια άλλη να ταιριάζει με την ερώτηση που παίζεται, τότε η τύχη είναι με το μέρος σου! Παίξε αυτή την κάρτα και με τη γνωστή χειρονομία Talk to the Hand δείξε στους υπόλοιπους πόσο looosers είναι! Το μόνο που χρειάζεται είναι χιούμορ. Τόσο απλό!

Το παιχνίδι περιλαμβάνει

85 μαύρες κάρτες
415 λευκές κάρτες
Φυλλάδιο οδηγιών

—description from the publisher

Grasse Roll and Write is the compact and dynamic version of the acclaimed award-winning Grasse game, now in roll and write format. Ideal for those looking for faster strategic games, without giving up the fun and elegance of perfumery.

Why choose Grasse Roll and Write? 

     Short and Engaging Games: In about 30 to 45 minutes, you immerse yourself in the art of perfumery and explore all the strategic paths available. 
     Simplified Portability: Grasse Roll and Write fits perfectly in your backpack or purse. Take it on trips, meetups with friends or even for a strategic coffee break! 
     Adaptable Strategy: The randomness of the dice combined with intelligent decisions makes each game unique, without requiring long planning sessions. Turn Any Moment into Fun: Whether you’re a fan of the original Grasse or a lover of compact, fast-paced games, Grasse Roll and Write is perfect for those looking for smart strategy anywhere, anytime.


Discover the joy of playing wherever you want!
With lightweight, intuitive components, Grasse Roll and Write is the ideal choice for taking your strategic skills out into the world. Craft perfumes, advance through the Grasse Fields, and become the master perfumer – at your table or wherever your imagination takes you!

—description from the designer
family: Versions & Editions: Roll- or Flip-and-Write Versions of Non-Writing Games

description:
Elemental Tempest is a trick taking, point scoring card game for 3 to 5 players. Each round, each player in turn plays a card from their hand, following suit if possible. If you can not follow suit, you can play a card of the Element that trumps that current suit changing the suit and nullifying the previous suit. The person who played the highest ranking card takes all the cards played that round and sets them aside for later scoring. Rounds continue until all cards are played, then they are scored. The first player to end a round with over 60 points is the winner.

—description from the designer

Duck That Goose Invites players to outsmart opponents in a comical quest where fortune flips with the cards. With a deck of sixty-nine whimsically designed cards, players navigate the twists of fate, all while evading a wild good chase. Will you float on cloud 9? Or shoot yourself in the foot? Risk the ire of the table… and you may get thrown under the bus or have your bubble burst. Every round is an unpredictable riot!

—description from the publisher

The game is made up of 69 cards with values ranging from -9 to +13 and even some the multiply or divide your score. Some have varying actions as well.

Deal four cards out in front of each player face down. each player can look at only two of their cards without showing any other players, memorize them and place them back face down. On your turn you either draw from the top of the deck, or the top of the discard pile. If you like the card you picked place it face up in front of you and discard one of your face down cards. If not, discard it and turn one of your face down cards up. if the new face up card has an action you do it now.

After all players have all face up cards in front of themselves the round is over, add up all the card values, the player with the lowest score wins and negative scores count. It is suggested that you play multiple rounds adding each one to make your final score.

Strategic board game with RPG-inspired adventures. Battle with polyhedral dice in an exciting, immersive, and strategic journey!
A strategic 4-player adventure board game!
Murgo is a thrilling dungeon-crawling board game where players take on the role of brave heroes fighting to seal the gateway to hell. When the forces of darkness break through, monstrous fiends begin their invasion, threatening to consume the world in chaos. Players must battle through three infernal levels, facing terrifying enemies, collecting powerful spirit cards, and upgrading their gear to survive.

—description from the designer

Halo-Halo is a short and lighthearted card game for 2 to 4 players together in a delicious competition to create the most mouthwatering halo-halo, a Filipino shaved ice dessert with numerous toppings, by drafting ingredients and scoring the most "yummy points" or "yummies."

On your turn, you pick from among four random cards in the center, known as the "chiller." You place the card above your glass in the form of a one-dimensional tableau, placing cards "above" each preceding card, mimicking the stacking of a halo-halo. The card's ability is activated upon drafting.

Some cards have abilities that simply gain yummies based on other cards or their positions in the halo-halo, while other cards have abilities that allow the cards to swap around the table.

Players keep going until everyone has 6/4/3 ingredients (depending on the number of players in the game). Then, whoever has the most yummies at the end of the game wins.

If there is a tie, the tie is broken by looking at which player has the highest numbered card (cards are numbered 1 to 14, separate from their yummies). Whoever has the highest-numbered card among the tied players wins.

Spora takes the concepts like group, liberty and territory, known from territorial games like Go, and adds support for stacks. Stacks can sow their pieces to adjacent intersections, capturing enemy pieces or supporting near friendly structures. The total number of pieces is limited, so players need to manage their finite budget to reach endgame controlling as much territory as possible.

Spora is the Greek σπορά, meaning 'sowing'.

Read the complete rules.

—description from the designer

Breakfast is served... if you can survive!

In Dino Path, 2 to 4 cavemen race into the jungle to steal dinosaur eggs and carry them back to camp—physically holding the egg tokens on their backs!

But watch out! You must avoid angry T-Rexes, navigate carnivorous plants, and outwit meddling rivals trying to steal your loot. Use action cards to dash, block, or even control the dinosaurs to clear your path.

With its unique tactile components and fast-paced gameplay, Dino Path is a chaotic, fun-filled family game perfect for casual players and dino lovers alike.

New Dark Ages. Colony is an asymmetrical strategy of character, resource and territory control based on the Ukrainian bestseller of the same name by Max Kidruk.

The year is 2141. Scientists detect a neutrino burst of incredible power near the Earth. Methane begins to be massively released from the bottom of the North Ocean. A strange new pathogen appears on land, infecting pregnant women and rewriting the genome of babies in their wombs. At the same time, on Mars, the confrontation between the super-specialists arriving from Earth and the Mars-born, who are forced to work like slaves in low-skilled manual labor, is escalating.

Choose one of 12 factions, recruit a team of heroes, engineers, and soldiers familiar from the book, and implement your own secret strategy in the fight of all against all!

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Kingdom Builder: Family & Friends Compact Edition
description:
This is the new compact version of the Spiel des Jahres of 2012 that might be smaller in size but actually offers more variety than the original game!

Instead of 8 game board sections there are only 4 now - usable on both sides for putting together the play area. The location tiles are not fixed to one game board section but can be put on any board, giving people more options during setup.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Talk To The Hand: Cringe Expansion Pack
description:
More cards for longer and more cringe-worthy situations! Don't forget that the basic version of Talk To The Hand is required to play.

The boss asks a question with a black card and the rest of the players answer with their funniest white card. The boss will decide (as always!) which is the funniest answer and the player who gave it wins the black card. The player with the most black cards at the end of the game wins! Of course, you decide when the game ends, it could be never!

The game includes:

30 black cards
180 white cards
Instruction booklet

Περισσότερες κάρτες για μεγαλύτερη διάρκεια και πιο cringe καταστάσεις! Μην ξεχνάτε ότι απαιτείται η βασική έκδοση Talk To The Hand για να παίξετε.

Το αφεντικό ρωτά με μία μαύρη κάρτα και οι υπόλοιποι απαντούν με την πιο αστεία λευκή τους κάρτα. Το Αφεντικό θα αποφασίσει (όπως συμβαίνει πάντα άλλωστε!) ποια είναι η πιο αστεία απάντηση και ο παίκτης που την έδωσε κερδίζει τη μαύρη κάρτα. Ο παίκτης με τις περισσότερες μαύρες κάρτες στο τέλος του παιχνιδιού, κερδίζει! Φυσικά, εσείς αποφασίζετε πότε θα τελειώσει το παιχνίδι, μπορεί και ποτέ!

Το παιχνίδι περιλαμβάνει:

30 μαύρες κάρτες  
180 λευκές κάρτες
Φυλλάδιο οδηγιών

boardgame: Talk To The Hand: Spicy Expansion Pack

description:
Dead Man’s Deck is a fast-paced, zombie-themed card game that challenges your strategy, tests your memory, and throws in a few devilish twists along the way.

Your goal? End the game with the least number of points—but in a world crawling with the undead, nothing is ever that simple.

Outwit your opponents by swapping, discarding, or revealing cards at just the right moment. Use your memory wisely—every decision counts!

Highlights:

     A unique blend of memory and strategic deduction
     Perfect for 2–6 players, ages 13+
     Optional Zombie Drinking Game Mode for late-night shenanigans
     Quick to learn, addictive to master


Whether you're creeping through quiet gameplay or unleashing undead party mode, Dead Man’s Deck promises a brain-hungry twist on your next game night.

—description from the publisher

Watch your legion grow in strength as the battle unfolds! Plan ahead of enemy assaults, power up, and emerge triumphant to assert your dominance!

Objective: Outlast all rivals and be the last one standing.

Mechanics: Tame Animas to build your Deck, optimize your strategy, engage in thrilling clashes against other players, and emerge as the ultimate survivor! By assembling 4 Animas around your Core, you can unleash the power of the Khroma and inflige devastating blows upon your opponents.

—description from the publisher

Players race to extinguish fires in a small town by playing cards containing complex number operations. The player who extinguishes the most fires wins.

A new building is set on fire each round, and the firefighters can only move around town by playing movement or special ability cards. Movement cards move a firefighter along the real or imaginary planes. Special ability cards can e.g. rotate a firefighter along a plane or jump to the nearest point on the real or imaginary line.

After moving, an area around the firefighter is extinguished and the player gets any fire tokens that were extinguished. The player with the most fire tokens at the end of the game wins.

boardgame: Ik hou van Holland: Zeeland editie
description:
Play the special Zeeland edition of the I love Holland board game now!
The game is based on the well-known TV program I love Holland. The game components are completely tailored to the province of Zeeland. Show that you are a real Zeeland expert in the categories basic knowledge, history, spelling, the dictionary game and cheese throwing. Compete in teams against each other and win!

Speel nu de speciale Zeeland editie van het ik hou van Holland bordspel!
Het spel is gebaseerd op het bekende tv-programma ik hou van Holland.De spelonderdelen zijn helemaal afgestemd op de provincie Zeeland. Laat in de categorieën basiskennis, geschiedenis, spelling, het woordenboekspel en het kaaswerpen zien dat jij een echte Zeeland-kenner bent. Strijd in teams tegen elkaar en win!

The hunt begins! In this card game, it's not about having the smallest or largest number - no, you need all 6 of each KIBU character! Listen and combine to grab as many sets of characters as possible and secure them for yourself. Pay close attention to the KIBU JUMPERs that are collected. Be careful and use clever tricks to win the game!

The aim of the game is to collect all 6 cards of a card family in order to secure them as one point. Whoever has the most points at the end wins. To collect a card family, you must ask your fellow players and claim cards from them. Address the players by name and state the character and number of the card you want.

—description from the publisher

Die Jagd beginnt! In diesem Kartenspiel kommt es nicht darauf an, die kleinste oder größte Zahl zu haben - nein, du brauchst von jedem KIBU Charakter alle 6! Schnapp dir durchs Zuhören und Kombinieren so viele Sets der Charaktere wie möglich und sichere sie dir. Achte genau auf die KIBU JUMPER, die gesammelt werden. Sei wachsam und setze clevere Finten, um das Spiel zu gewinnen!

—description from the publisher (German)

In Nightslain, you'll assume the role of a Nightslayer tasked with cleansing locations from the horrific creatures which ravaged the kingdom.

During the course of the game, you'll fight your way through 2 Territories, collecting vital equipment which will increase your stats. Clear the second Territory to win the game.

The Nightslayer

Your character has 3 stats: Vitality, Physique and Knowledge.

Vitality determines your max health; if it falls to 0, you lose the game.

Physique governs your overall strength, used during combat.

Knowledge increases your arcane, which is used to cast spells.

Territories and The Encounter Card

Territories are faced one at a time. With a Cleanse action, you may reduce its Threat level by 1 per strength point spent. If this reduces its threat to 0, you have cleared the location. You may also try to loot during a turn, but it will come at a cost. Each territory presents 3 locations with different looting costs.

Whenever you reduce a location's threat level, you'll also move the Encounter cube on the Encounter card down one space. During the subsequent phase, you'll have to resolve the effect of the current space. Some effects may be mitigated, while others are inopposable.

Tools and The Spellbook

Tools cover various roles: they increase your stats, and most of them provide with an active or passive ability. There are 3 tool categories, but you may only carry one tool from each category.

Spells can be unlocked by increasing Knowledge: they cost Arcane to cast, but do not need to be readied after use. The Spellbook card has 2 sides, each with a different spell set.

Looming Destruction

During the final phase of each round, you'll have to increase destruction on the territory: if it reaches 9, you lose the game. Be careful as the more remaining threat a territory has, the more destruction will be caused; not only that, but high destruction will hinder your Nightslayer with negative effects.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Hands in the Sea: Deus Ex Machina Cards
description:
A mini-expansion containing a set of 8 optional cards (4 per side), with powerful one-time events that can be activated at the cost of losing Victory Points.

This set was free for all backers who followed the Hands in the Sea campaign before it was launched, and then backed the game.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
You win this game together as a team! Agree on a secret sign and collect all the taggs without anyone noticing. But be careful! If you're not clever and quick enough, you'll play into your opponents' hands and lose your taggs. So be careful and keep an eye on your opponents!

—description from the publisher

Dieses Spiel gewinnt ihr gemeinsam im Tagg-Team! Vereinbart ein geheimes Zeichen und sammelt unbemerkt alle Taggs. Doch Vorsicht! Seid ihr nicht clever und schnell genug, spielt ihr euren Gegnern in die Karten und verliert eure Taggs. Also passt gut auf und behaltet eure Widersacher im Auge!

—description from the publisher (translated)
Automa solo mode for Tokaido. Includes solo mode rulebook, cards and player aid. Also includes panorama cards for all player counts. Already included in the Stonemaier release of Tokaido.

The Playfoam Game is squishy, squashy, shaping fun for EVERYONE! Teams take turns sculpting and guessing kid-friendly clues in a race against the clock. Since Playfoam never dries out, the family fun never ends!

Objective: Guess the clues sculpted by your teammates to win the round. The first team to win 5 rounds wins the game.

General Rules: No talking. No sound effects. No sculpting words.

Setup: Split players into 2 teams. Choose a place to play. It should have space for acting out clues and a flat surface for sculpting.

How to play:

     The first sculptor draws a clue card from the deck.
     The timer is flipped over, and the sculptor begins molding while their teammates attempt to guess the clue. If it is helpful, sculptors may also use their Playfoam to mold a prop and act out using it. Teammates can shout out as many guesses as they want, until they correctly identify the clue, or time is up.
     Stealing - If the sculpting team does not correctly guess a clue before time runs out, the opposing team can make one guess, and if correct, steal the point.
     Scoring - The team who guesses the clue card first wins the round and keeps that card. The first team to collect 5 cards wins the game!


—description from the publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
Get ready for this rapid fire card game where posh pooches behave badly. The grande dames of canine high society have gathered for a deliciously vicious martini lunch- and they’re trading barbs between sips. Can you throw shade quick enough to be the top dog?

You Shady Pooch! is played over several rounds. You'll start with 4 bones that you'll wager each round. The first player to empty their hand wins that round's pot. Once someone's lost all of their bones, the game ends, and the richest pooch wins!

Every copy of You Shady Pooch! features 54 fabulously illustrated cards by artist Joshua Heinsz, an instruction booklet, drinking game variant rules, and 24 delightful wooden bone tokens to help the shady pooches at your game table keep score.

—description from the publisher

Developed and Selfpublished by: Mystic Shelter

Factsheet: 
Competetive 1v1 Expandable Cardgame

- PvPvE
- 2 Players
- 14 Years or older
- 30+ Minutes per Play (Deckbuilding not included)

Includes:
- 305 Cards in total (112 unique Cards in the Amount to use in a deck + Game Accessoire Cards)
- Pre-punched Tokensheet
- Rulebook

Recommended Retail Price: 44,- Euro

Perfect for:
- Boardgame Experts who love to tinker with Synergies while building their Deck.
- Story Enthusiasts who enjoy new original Universes and want to dive into the Story and Lore of the Game itself

Gather 40 Essence by Generating them with Cards, fullfilling Quests,
destroying enemy Cards or attacking the Enemy.

You take turns with your Enemy and choose between different possible
Actions you can take in your turn instead of having a fixed Turn Process.

Quests come up in your self-built Questdeck, giving them different objectives while playing against each other, 
you can build your Questdeck as well as your Main Deck yourself before the Game starts.

While playing, the Corruption – an environmental Threat – rises, 
once it reaches the highest Stage both players lose, so they have to keep an eye on it and manipulate it if necessary. 
Different Cards interact differently with the Corruption.

There are 5 upcoming Expansions planned, each with roughly 20-30 new unique Cards each (60-70 in Total).
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description: This is a solo expansion set that allows Otaru1899 to play alone. Play against the robot "Inazoo," which has three levels of strength, and compete for the highest score.

This set of cards adds five new heroes—Archer, Assassin, Beastmaster, Oracle, and Sorceress—each with it's own unique power. You can swap any of these in to replace one of the four original heroes in the Creatures and Cards deck.

Also included is a wild card, the Djinn, to shuffle into the draw deck. It's power to grant three wishes adds a new twist to the game!

—description from the publisher

Purring Hearth is a cozy 1–4 player dice and bag-building game set in a charming woodland tavern run by the Catmother and her lively kitten helpers. Guests arrive with whimsical, specific requests, and your job is to assign the right kittens (represented by dice) to meet their expectations. Some guests seek perfect pairs, others prefer playful randomness or poetic symmetry — and each kitten has their own quirks.

You'll upgrade staff, serve guests across six thematic roles, unlock tavern expansions, and discover your own path to success. Focus on wealth, specialization, wide growth or deep mastery — Purring Hearth offers multiple strategies and rewards creative play. Every game tells a slightly different story of furry hospitality.

A free print-and-play version will be available to download, intended to share the game with players and gather feedback from the community. Publishers are welcome: the final version can be adapted and developed further for professional release.

—description from the designer

VOLATILITY - Investing Made Fun is a fast-paced board game about investing in the stock market.

The game can be played in two modes: one featuring a Progressive Tax system, and another where investing within your Circle of Competence provides added benefits. In both modes, Companies are selected to represent the stock market, and players choose Character Cards that influence their investing strategies.

Each round of gameplay includes collecting income and dividends, rolling dice to determine share price changes, buying and selling shares, and drawing an Event Card that introduces new risks or rewards.

The goal is to navigate economic events and make strategic investing decisions to finish the game with the largest net worth.

—description from the publisher

Discover the most fascinating Italian chicken breeds with Hens – Italian Breeds, the new expansion for the game that has captivated everyone with its feathers and strategy!

With this expansion, you can replace the breeds in the base game with 3 new Italian breeds, maintaining the same card color to seamlessly integrate everything into your game.
Also included are 2 new Goal cards to add variety and new challenges.
You can use them with both the Italian breeds and the original breeds from the base game.

BeFree for Starters is a fast-paced financial literacy card game where players simulate real-life money decisions—like investing, taking loans, and managing cash flow. In each turn, players draw cards to either invest or borrow, aiming to grow their net worth. They receive passive income, pay interest, and can buy, sell, or repay strategically.

The game is played in cycles. After two simulated years, players update their net worth. The first player to reach $1,000,000 in net worth (Cash + Assets – Loans) wins.

Perfect for teens, families, and educators, the game teaches smart financial habits through fun and dynamic gameplay.

—description from the publisher

Abstract game, no luck involved.
On a rectangular board with colored spaces, players move their pawn around leaving a piece behind. If you have 2 or more pieces on the same space, you are entitled to build a tower with them. You can increase your range of movement with the help of towers.
Game ends when one player is blocked or all 40 pieces are on the board. The player who controls most of the colors wins. You control a color, if you have more pieces of your towers there than your opponent.

—description from the designer

A Card Game on SouthEast Asian Ghost Culture. When the sun sets, many strange ghosts arise in The Lion City. Growing up in the multicultural country of Singapore, I was exposed to many traditions and myths from various cultures. One of the lesser talks about topics was ghost stories, ranging from the haunted building of Old Changi Hospital to the small islands such as Pulau Ubin and St John Island. I was so intrigued in these stories, I've decided to make it into a simple card game that promotes this ghost culture to many others who aren't aware of it.

Battle it out with fellow hunters to catch as many spirits as you can!

Draw cards, summon or sacrifice ghosts, play action cards

Goal: Reach a total of 12 fear factor from the ghosts you collect.

Caution: Friendships might be broken through this game.

Rock Paper Scissors GOAT! (as in Greatest Of All Time) is a card game with Rock, Paper, Scissor suits that beat one another - and the GOAT suit that “always” wins. Based on the schoolyard classic, you already know some of the rules. Your only choice now is which ruleset to play first.

     ‘Get Your GOAT!’ is a trick-taking game for 2 to 6 players, ages 10 and up. Plays in 20 to 30 minutes. Arrange GOAT meeples points) on the box, claim points each time you take an even number of tricks. Take an even number of tricks to score. Score 10 points to win.
     ‘Last GOAT!’ is a family-weight card shedding game for 2 to 6 players, ages 6 and up. Plays in 5 to 15 minutes. Play one or two cards. Play two cards of the suit as long is it exceeds the card(s) you cover. Must play one card if you change suit. To win, be the first player to play all your cards.


There are 4 special cards that change or alter the rules of play - 3 Lock Suit cards and 1 Black Sheep card.

—description from the designer

BeFree for Champions is a strategic financial board game where players pursue wealth, purpose, and personal growth. Designed for teens and adults, it simulates real-life money decisions—from investing in real estate to developing life skills and launching purpose-driven businesses.

Players start with a set amount of money and build passive income through smart investments. Along the way, they face life challenges, make donations, and take steps toward meaningful goals.

To win, a player must:

Generate at least $120,000/year in passive income

Complete a personal Life Goal worth $300,000

Save $120,000 in cash reserves

Donate $1,000,000 to society

The first to fulfill all victory conditions becomes the Champion. BeFree for Champions is perfect for families, educators, and future-minded players who enjoy games with depth, realism, and impact.

—description from the publisher

BeFree for KIDS is a fun and interactive financial board game designed for children aged 7+ to explore real-world money skills in an age-appropriate, hands-on way. Through smart choices and playful competition, young players learn how to earn, save, invest, and manage cash flow while enjoying a dynamic family-friendly experience.

In the game, players choose a financial path — Enjoy Now or Plan for Tomorrow — then roll the die, move along the board, and face decisions like working jobs, making deposits, covering expenses, or investing. Strategic choices help them build wealth or learn from setbacks.

The first player to reach $10,000 and fulfill their Dream card wins the game. Perfect for families, classrooms, and young future investors.

—description from the publisher

Flip to a random “most likely to ___” prompt, decide in your head who among you best matches it, and 3…2…1…point to the person you decided best fits! If you think you best fit the prompt, raise your hand instead of pointing. If you pointed at someone who raised their hand or you raised your hand and someone else pointed at you, you receive points! The first person to get to 3 points wins in this quick and easy-to-carry game.

—description from the publisher

「○○そう」とみんなに思われてる“と思っている“人を当てるゲーム！

めくるだけで遊べる！「意外」がたのしい価値観ゲーム！

description:
Reveal the game cards one by one and choose the dice that match their conditions to build your own dice tower. Maintain a delicate balance amidst the thrilling tension and keep your tower from falling until the end to win. Who can build the tallest dice tower? Show off your strategy and focus.

—description from the publisher

A twenty-card trick-taking game for two players that fuses with civilization-building games by making each card representative of a city one is trying to add to one's empire. Winning a trick is decided in the usual way of highest-ranking card, but it is bonuses applied to the leaders of one's cities and the buildings they contain that determines the winner after three rounds, with the most points declaring the dominant empire.

Take a stand for liberty or the Crown in Black Powder Epic Battles: Revolution!

This essential tome gives you all the rules required to fight the battles of the American War of Independence with Black Powder in Epic Battles scale, alongside historical background information, artwork, and gorgeous miniatures photography, as well as eight scenarios for crucial engagements.

Small but mighty, the A5 softcover format makes it perfect for slipping into a gaming bag!

–description from the publisher

You have received an exclusive invitation from the legendary forest wizard, Elixirus, to participate in his Magical Mixing Contest, held annually at his charming hut tucked away in the heart of the enchanted forest. There, apprentices from all corners of the realm will come together to explore the forest’s magical wonders, and gather ingredients to concoct delightful potions in an effort to impress the whimsical wizard. It’s a celebration of creativity, discovery, and a touch of forest mischief - so bring your skill, imagination, and a pinch of wonder. Master Elixirus can’t wait to see what you brew up!

In Elixirus, you'll compete with your fellow players to make 5 different elixirs. The game is played in rounds, each consisting of 2 simple phases. In the Gather phase, venture out and explore various mystical forest locations to find the best ingredients. The higher their potency, the more valuable your elixirs become. In the Mix phase, turn your ingredients into magical elixirs to score points. The game ends after a player completes any 4 of their elixirs, at which point the player with the most potent potions wins.

Think your journey through Neo Eden is already over? Far from it! With the Nanolith Deluxe Erweiterung, you'll face even more dangerous missions and encounter powerful allies. But beware: To play this expansion, you'll need the Nanolith base game. Only then will you be able to use the new miniatures, player boards, and special dice that will test your tactical skills and take the thrill to a new level.

The additional Arena game mode ensures heated battles in which you and your friends can engage in thrilling duels, team battles, or epic boss fights. The reworked heroes and sidekick not only rely on their unique abilities, but also on modern acrylic tokens and special dice that allow you to make tactical moves. Whether you prefer to play cooperatively or go head-to-head, the Deluxe Expansion opens up unimagined strategies and deepens the game mechanics of the base game.

What makes this expansion special is its attention to detail: The high-quality acrylic miniatures and standees, colorful player aids, and new special dice perfectly capture the futuristic atmosphere. The included game mode book also provides a quick introduction to the new rules, so you can get started right away. Those who are already enthusiastic about the main game will appreciate the fresh ideas and high replay potential of the Deluxe Expansion.

The Nanolith Deluxe Erweiterung is ideal for anyone who wants to delve even deeper into the world of Nanolith. Whether you're an experienced fan looking for new challenges or just want to complete your collection, you'll find hours of fun and constantly new tactical possibilities here. And if you need a change from the usual campaign, just try out the Arena Mode and engage in a thrilling sci-fi skirmish with your fellow players.

—description from the publisher (translated)

The masterful edition of the official card game from STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN
In this fast-paced card game, each player receives 8 letter cards and discards matching terms to given categories, with the fastest player scoring points in each round and the aim being to get all the cards off, while action and joker cards provide additional excitement.

boardgame: Las Ligas de la Ñ: Los Ases del Destino
description:
Las Ligas de la Ñ: Los Ases del Destino ("The Ñ Leagues: The Aces of Destiny") is an eñeist-style tarot card game for 1 to infinite players.

In this new Ñ game (the eleventh in the series), two distinct modes can be played: a competitive mode and a free mode (multiplayer or solo).

Each card in Los Ases del Destino contains the following information: 1. Name 2. Image 3. Description 4. Origin (birth and death dates for historical figures, and author, work, and date for fictional characters) 5. League (group of power, faction, or order within the universe of Las Ligas de la Ñ) 6. Arcane (number and name of the arcane according to the archetypal tradition of tarot) 7. Ñ Value (represents the transcendence within the universe) 8. S Value (represents the wisdom) 9. F Value (represents the strength—mental, physical, technological, political, or magical) 10. M Value (represents mobility—either the number of spaces moved or type of movement) 11. Other elements (planets, zodiac signs, Viking runes, chakras, etc.).

In the competitive mode, each game consists of 5 rounds, and each round follows 7 phases:1. Card Draw (3 arcanes and 3 leagues)2. Storytelling (the storyteller narrates a real or invented story) 3. (Re)Cognition (each player may ask one question to another player) 4. Selection (players secretly choose their principal, complementary, and opposite arcana and leagues) 5. Voting (players try to guess the arcane and league choices of three players of their choice) 6. Scoring (each correct guess about another player's card earns 1 point, and each time another player guesses one of your choices, you also earn 1 point—rewarding both interpretative ability and clarity of expression) 7. Purgatory (each player may make a judgment or comment about any aspect of the game, the story, the selections, or the participants—and may also pose another question to a player of their choice). At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins—although the true goal is to explore the symbolic, personal, and narrative connections between the cards and players.

In the free mode, players take on the roles of heroic travelers or visionary sages who must draw arcane, league, and even Eñesutra cards (Las Ligas de la Ñ: Eñesutras) to receive infinite lessons and make crucial decisions. In the solo mode, the player must face the Ñ in a masterful and ancestral card duel.

Additionally, this deck is compatible with other games in the series, such as Las Ligas: Reinas Arcanas, Las Ligas de la Ñ: Eñesutras, Las Ligas de la Ñ: Antología, and Las Ligas de la Ñ: La Gran Guerra.

—description from the designer

A fast-paced miniatures game of whimsical fantasy jousting.

While the Hairfoot halflings typically prefer eating and drinking to strenuous exercise, one thing that is sure to stir them from their armchairs is the noble sport of Hairfoot Jousting. Small teams take to the field on the backs of various unruly mounts – pigs, turkeys, sheep, and more – to tilt against their rivals, striving to knock them to the ground with their blunt lances. For the victors: fame, glory, and perhaps a new pair of gardening gloves. For the defeated team… well, everyone loves an underdog!

Hairfoot Jousting is a quick and simple tabletop miniatures game in which players seek to lead their teams of three to victory in tournaments or over the course of a season. Play on some of the most famous pitches to grace the sport, such as Baker's Run, The Puddles, and Barrel Bottom, each with their own special challenges. Point your jousters in the direction of their opponents and hope their mounts don't have their own ideas! In Hairfoot Jousting, sportsmanship and a sense of humour are as valuable as skill or luck – after all, it's joust a bit of fun.

—description from the publisher

A streamlined wargame for cinematic space dogfights. Strap in rookie. Power up your I-Drive and punch it into the void!

Voidfighter is a wargame of space-fighter combat where players pilot their squadron, perform special maneuvers – including barrel rolls and Immelmann turns – and unleash a barrage of laser blasts, all in an effort to defeat their foes.

Using a simple set of ship characteristics based around differing die types, as well as a variety of additional traits and weapons, players can create and customize the ship of their dreams from nimble fighters all the way to mighty, but cumbersome, corvettes. For players that want to jump straight into the cockpit and get to the action, the book also contains a great number of existing ship types based upon classic sci-fi tropes and ideas, from space empires to plucky mercenaries.

Also contained in Voidfighter are rules for a selection of celestial phenomena and other terrain types, a variety of differing scenarios, and a campaign system enabling pilots to increase in skill over the course of several games.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Burrows & Badgers: Second Edition – A Skirmish Game of Anthropomorphic Animals
description:
A new revised and expanded edition of the popular wargame Burrows & Badgers.

The Kingdom of Northymbra is a land in turmoil. King Redwulf is missing, and his son rules as regent in his stead, facing threats from within and without: growing dissention among the knights and nobles of the realm, whispers of revolution from the Freebeasts, Hillfolk raiding the borders, and bandits of all stripes making the most of the chaos.

Burrows & Badgers: Second Edition is a revised and expanded version of the popular tabletop skirmish game set in the ancient realm of Northymbra, a kingdom where mice, badgers, toads, and other animals wear armour, wield swords, and cast magic spells. This updated edition contains new content including additional background material, new species, warband types and a whole host of new spells, skills, and equipment.

Each model in Burrows & Badgers: Second Edition represents an individual character, and can be selected from a wide range of species – from the humble mouse to the mighty badger – and armed and equipped as desired. Scenarios link into ongoing campaigns, where heroes and villains may make their names and the assistance of infamous mercenaries might mean the difference between victory and defeat. Lead your warband from battle to battle, and uphold the name of your faction, whether you stand with Reinert's Royalists, the Freebeasts of the Fox Families, or simply for your own glory or survival.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dragon Rampant: Second Edition – Fantasy Wargaming Rules
description:
A revised and updated edition of the popular Dragon Rampant ruleset.

Whether you're a nameless Dark Lord looking to conquer the known world, a Champion of Light holding out against the forces of evil or a Northern barbarian facing claimants to a stolen throne, Dragon Rampant: Second Edition allows you to bring those battles to the tabletop in this easy-to-learn but tactically rewarding fantasy wargame.

Dragon Rampant: Second Edition is a new, updated edition of the hit Osprey Wargames series title whose miniature-agnostic, large-scale skirmish rules brought the great battles of fantasy fiction to the tabletop. Featuring revised core rules, substantial additions to the magic and unit upgrade sections, as well as extensive sample army lists and a range of scenarios, Dragon Rampant: Second Edition allows players to command unruly orc warbands, raise armies of the undead, campaign across an antediluvian world as the warchief of a barbarian tribe, or exploit the power of mighty creatures and extraordinary sorcery.

—description from the publisher

description:
This is a board game designed for 5-10 players.  In this game, players will immerse themselves in a medieval world full of chaos, where they are separated into two factions, the Loyalists and the Traitors, against each other.  In order to seize the victory of game, players will have to use a variety of tactices, including concealment, deception, and accusations.  Cooperation and betrayal are the norm.  Remember: in the game, no one can be trusted except you...

—description from the back of the box

This game includes mild verbal violence and content related to sexuality.

This game employs a satirical and ironic approach, allowing players to take on the roles of either a dictator puppet ministers. The intention is to explore certain absurdities of human nature through the gaming experience and provoke reflections among players.

Return to the City of Angels. This expansion will have three new cases available: The Curse of the Jade Jaguar, A Twist of the Knife, and Hollywood Heartbreak. The cases in this expansion were designed by Evan Blewett, with art by Vincent Dutrait, Snow Conrad, and Agnieszka Dąbrowiecka. So dust off your fedora and get ready to step back into the world of Detective: City of Angels.

—description from the publisher

description:
First we will choose one of the emotion cards and that will be our character. In addition, it will be an emotion that we will not be able to use to score.

Throughout each turn we will try to empathize, or not, with the player in turn and the situation he describes.

Roll a die to choose a situation, read it aloud to all the players and clarify if we will play with the intention of feeling apathy or empathy. As the case may be.

Each player will choose one of the 7 emotions and show them at the same time. Depending on the matches they have with each other, players will earn rewards.

The first to complete 4 different emotion tiles wins. But remember, you can't earn tokens of the same emotion as your character card.

Α simple roll the dice and move board game with the theme of James Bond 007. This game was manufactured in Greece and it was released in the late 60's as a gift for consumers who purchased the product under the name ZIT.

It can be played from 2 to 4 players. Each player chooses a pawn and starts by crossing whichever path thinks that is best. The move is made using dice. When a player lands on a spot that has a number on it, he must follow the order given to the agent corresponding to that number. If a player reaches the same spot another player is standing on, he has to go 6 spots back.

The winner is the player who reaches Dr Γιελ first on an exact roll of the dice.

Players are office workers competing for promotion. Hard work often pays off, but not always with office politics.

Can you manage your efforts wisely to win the most credit while taking the least blame?

Each player takes a set of 12 Work Cards as their starting hand. The game takes 12 turns as 12 months a year. Each turn, Reveal 1 Boss Card which sets this month's work assignment. Each player secretly selects and plays one card from their hand. Once all players have decided, they reveal their cards simultaneously.
Compare the sum of effort values on all players' cards against the task number on the revealed Boss Card. If the task is completed,the best performer gets the performance point. If the task is uncompleted, the worst performer gets the complaint.

After 12 months(rounds), the player with the highest total score wins.Total Score = Performance Points - Complaint Points

Pigeons are having a party, yet the traffic is terrible.

Pigeons hate waiting for others, but enjoy having others wait for them. To have the most fun, you must be the latest to the party. But if you're too late, others won't let you in!

Navigate a treacherous city commuting environment, to become the party king among pigeons!

After 7 rounds of "route-drafting", route points are tallied. Among the pigeons who keep their total under 21 points, The one with the highest points wins the game and is crowned the King of Pigeon!

The bugs are out in full force in this expansion that introduces several new insect-themed petals and meeple to your favorite flower bouquet!

Bugs in The Garden introduces a new "place insect" sub-action to its classic fast-paced gameplay that allows players to:


     Position caterpillars on other players' petals to eat away at their points!



     Use Spiders to swap previously locked petal positions!



     Place down butterflies that can barricade your opponent's progress!


Bugs in the Garden is meant to add an extra layer of strategy to the Bouquet experience and spices things up with an extra dose of "take-that" gameplay.

—description from the publisher

Nanobots have taken over the flower Bouquet! Do you have what it takes to stop them and restore natural order?

Bouquet: Cybernetic Assault features a fully scripted Automa that let's you enjoy all the strategy of Bouquet with just 1 or 2 players.

(Now featuring the unreasonably difficult "Nightmare Mode" due to popular request!)

—description from the publisher

Cosmic Run: Mission One is a solo space racing game that is included within Dr. Finn's Solo Strategy and Word Games Book (vol. 1). The game consists of a single sheet and requires 4 dice and a pencil to play. Although it is a solitaire game, multiple players can play at the same time to see who gets the best score.

In the game, you are racing your ships toward 4 planets to see if you can get to each planet before ships from an alien race. As in Dr. Finn's Cosmic Run, you roll dice and place them on planets or use them to recruit allies (who can give you special powers to manipulate the dice).

On a turn, you roll 4 dice and place 1 or more of them on allies or planets.  Any remaining dice must be rolled and again, you must place 1 or more dice on allies or planets.  Continue in this manner until all 4 dice are placed.  The dice are resolved and you may move ships and/or gain ally powers.  if you reach a planet, you score victory points for that planet.

On the alien's turn, you roll 1 die and move an alien ship based on the die roll.  If any alien reaches a planet before you, you lose points based on how far you are from the planet.  The game ends when all planets are reached. You are trying to score the most points.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Roll- or Flip-and-Write Versions of Non-Writing Games

description:
Aeon’s End: Beyond the Breach expands on the beloved expedition system with a brand new FIGHT FIVE. This means a brand new tier of nemesis difficulty and a brand new type of treasure cards players can earn for surviving this gauntlet of challenges. This box is a standalone product with new mages, nemeses, supply cards, friends and foes, and treasures that will enhance anyone’s collection.

—description from the publisher

"A simple two-player game where you flip over diamond-shaped cards.

When a butterfly flies by, roll the dice. Will there be a big reversal? Will you be the one to get the big reversal?

Choro comes from the Japanese word chorotto, which means a little. Creatures are chorotto peeking out from the holes on diamond-shaped cards. The rules of the game are simple, like Reversi. But when butterflies flutter into the game, everything changes!"

—description from the publisher

Rocca Choro is like a card-based kid's version of Othello that adds randomness through Special Cards (which cannot normally be fliped to the other color) and Chance Cards (which depending on a die roll  will allow one player a free flip of one of their opponent's cards). Includes one six-sided die.

The supernatural conflicts of Idols of Torment rage across a perilous afterlife called the Echo. This ruined landscape combines the remnants of Heaven and Hell following their apocalyptic collapse, forging a single, shattered realm inhabited by wandering souls.

Idols of Torment is a cosmic horror miniatures game for two players. Each player takes on the role of a dark entity called an Eternal. As an Eternal, you command eldritch manifestations called Idols as they descend upon the Echo. Your sole purpose is to pit your Idols against your enemy for control over the tormented souls. These souls are a precious resource that must be reaped, thus releasing their energy from the Echo and returning it to the stars. But reaping them is not an easy task — you will need all the tools at your disposal to chase them down, influence their movements, manipulate the landscape, and fend off your opponent.

The overall system takes familiar skirmish gameplay and blends it with various tabletop roleplaying game and board game concepts, delivering a classic, turn-based, strategy game experience.  '

—description from the designer
An action dexterity football game mixed with some card play. On your turn you draw a card and arrange your player disks before attempting to throw a small beanbag to land on one of your player disks.

This game is contained within Dr. Finn's Solo Strategy and Word Game book (volume 1). This is a solo game that requires 3 dice and 1 game sheet from the book.

In the game, you fill 3 grids with numbers, based the dice rolls, trying to meet scoring conditions of the rows and columns in the grids.  Each grid has 4 rows and columns.  On each turn, your roll 3 dice and enter a value from each die into each of the 3 different grids.  When you complete a row or column, you may score points and possibly earn a power to be used to manipulate the dice.

After 16 turns, the 3 grids are filled in and you calculate your final score.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Little Flower Shop: Open for Business
description:
This game is in Dr. Finn's Book of Solo Strategy and Word Games.  The game requires 4 dice and 1 game sheet from the book.

In the game, you add items to your inventory and sell them, hoping to maximize your profits.  On each turn, perform 3-4 steps.  In step 1, you roll 4 dice and choose 3 of them to increase your inventory. In step 2, you roll 2 dice, which affect the market values of the items.  In step 3, you must sell all items of 1 inventory type at its current market value. In step 4 (which happens twice in the game in specified turns), you choose a custom order that you can try to fulfill at game end for additional profit.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Magical World of Disney Trivia: Kids Edition
description:
The Magical World of Disney Trivia: Kid's Edition is the perfect game for your next family game night! This game features questions about a wide array of Disney and Pixar characters, including classic characters like Mickey Mouse and Disney Junior favorites like Doc McStuffins! Each card includes a picture of a character on one side and questions about that character and their movie on the other for a trivia experience that's perfect for younger players. Race to be the first player to earn a Mickey Head Token in each color and cross the finish line to win!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
In this game of Strategy and luck, you play as pet rats, trying to get the most Pats and Snacks. You play in turns, in which each player uses their actions to move, get pats, take naps, do tricks, start tussles and pick up Snacks to put in Snack Stash! You can steal Snacks from other players...but they can also steal yours!

—description from the designer

This is a game in Dr. Finn's Book of Solo Strategy and Word Games (volume 1). This game requires 2 dice, a pencil, and a game sheet.

This solo game consists of three main parts: Fill, Forget, and Find.

In the first part, you roll dice to fill in a 7x7 grid of letters which has 14 empty squares and 35 pre-determined letters. Each die roll determines a letter, which you enter into any empty square.  After 14 rolls, the grid is filled.

In the second part, you roll dice to determine letters that you can cross out.  You take 10 turns, crossing out 1 letter in the grid at a time.

In the third part, you make words using the remaining letters in each row and column.

Your score for each word depends on its length, the letters in the word, special letter bonuses, and a word bonus if you use all the letters in a row or column.

—description from the publisher

This solo game is in Dr. Finn's Book of Solo Strategy and Word Games. It requires 1 die, a pencil, and a game sheet.

In this game, you roll the die to determine "tile" options which shows different city features in different polyomino shapes.  For each roll, you pick one of the tiles to draw into the "city" (which is a 6x6 grid of empty spaces).

You score points at the end for meeting the zoning requirements of the different city features.

—description from the publisher

This is a game in Dr. Finn's Book of Solo Strategy and Word Games.  It requires 2 dice, a pencil, and a game sheet (in the book).

In this game, you enclose various animals using fence pieces, whose shapes are determined by the dice roll. You are trying to collect sets of animals in each enclosure and trying to make sure there is one water bucket in each.

—description from the publisher

This is a game in Dr. Finn's Book of Solo Strategy and Word Games.  It requires 1 die, a pencil and a game sheet (in the book).

In this solo game, you roll the die to determine a letter that you enter into a crossword-like puzzle grid.  Your goal is to fill the crossword with legal words, but ones that can also score you a lot of points.

—description from the publisher

This is a game in Dr. Finn's Book of Solo Strategy and Word Games. It requires 1 die, a pencil, and a game sheet (in the book).

In this solo word game, roll a die to determine a polyomino shape that is used to "capture" letters in a grid.  These letters are then used to spell a variety of words.  You score points by spelling legal words and by putting valuable letters in bonus spaces.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: Vermilion's Mortal Battle
description:
This is a doujin game that was produced for sale at Game Shows in Japan (such as Tokyo Game Marker).  To get one you needed to preorder it in advance and then pick it up at the show.

This game is set in the Legend of the Galactic Heroes Universe, an anime series that is very popular in Japan.  The game deals with the Battle of Vermilion, which occurred during the Empire's invasion of Alliance territory.

The game's system is based on Command Magazine issue 53's game called Grunwald 1410.

Game Scale:
1 hex is about 28 light seconds (about 8.3 billion km) 
1 turn is about half a day
1 unit is about 500 ships

The game is a doujin and requires assembly.

—user summary
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

boardgame: FLUSTER: The Game of Unusual Questions for Deeper Connections
description:
FLUSTER is a social card game that uses unusual questions to inspire deep conversations, hilarious stories, and memorable moments (all while trying to win, of course).

In each round, one player draws a question from the deck. Questions range from simple, like, "what's the luckiest thing that's ever happened to you?", to introspective, such as "what's something you wish more people understood about you?", to funny, like "what moment or phase of your life do you look back on now and cringe?"

All the other players answer the question, and the player who drew the question picks their favourite answer, based on vulnerability, honesty, humour, or whatever criteria they choose. The player that won the round gets a point. The game ends when a player reaches five points. If a player turns up a FLUSTER card, they can use it to block a player from answering a future question.

There are several other ways to play FLUSTER, some of which use special cards, like the FLUSTER card, which turns a question back on the asker.

A game where tiny cars battle it out on the tabletop. A book that asks you to cut it up with scissors.

Obliteration Derby is a miniature wargame intended for quick and brutal, free-for-all battles. 
Players each build a deck of cards and navigate their vehicles around the table as they lob grenades and fire machine guns at their competitors. Only one player will be left standing, so drive fast, kill hard and leave a good-looking crater!

Note: It's highly recommended that you sleeve the game's cards in standard TCG sleeves, as the paper is relatively thin.

This book provides enough cards for one player, along with all the rules they will need. There is also a two-player edition with one set of rules and double the number of cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Grimm World: Jakob and the Giants
description:
Discover the secrets that lie hidden on Garnet Mountain.

This 18-card expansion for Grimm World brings you a new character, Jakob the one-armed soldier, a powerful magical artifact, new encounters and a new ending.

Simply add the cards to the respective decks and start a new campaign.

—description from the publisher

MIDT is a strategic card game primarily designed for 2 players, centered around intense head-to-head battles. The goal is to place your number card exactly in the middle—based on card position, not value.

Each player starts with 10 number cards ranging from -1 to 20. Players take turns playing one card at a time. Played cards are arranged in ascending order on the table. Once all cards have been placed, players count from both ends of the row, and the player whose card lands in the middle scores  points.

The game combines simple arithmetic (addition and subtraction) with strategic card effects, including cards that can remove others from the field. It features a high-level two-player battle mode as well as a four-player party mode for lighter gameplay.

MIDT is language-independent and accessible for all ages, offering not only fun but also educational value through number sense and logical thinking.

boardgame: Books of Time: Our Family Plays Games Promo
description: 3 double sided tiles for Books of Time giving alternative effects on the Civilization board.

Can you find a pair of words-one from each card-that are connected in some way? Your challenge is to choose two words you believe others will also select, and craft a one-word clue to link them. In this semi-cooperative game, success depends on aligning your brain waves with at least one other player.

—description from the publisher

Build Your Own Lodge in a Hidden Forest Retreat!

Chilltime is a strategic 2-player board game that blends area control, dice rolling, and pick & delivery mechanics.

Each turn, players roll four dice and allocate three of them to move two different types of pieces:

Craftsman pieces, which collect lodges from the Build Area and transport them to scoring spots called CHILL tiles.

Jyamamon pieces, which serve as defenders—placing fences to block opponents or support artisans.

Every round presents a dilemma: should you invest more dice into offense or defense? Your goal is to build more lodges on CHILL tiles than your opponent.

The game ends when either player has built four lodges, and the player with more on CHILL tiles wins.

Chilltime features a fine balance of strategy and luck, with special die effects and blocking mechanics creating constant interaction.

boardgame: Лорды Приграничья: Время Перемен (Lords of Frontier: Time of Change)
description:
The addon “Time for Change” will allow you to look at the familiar factions of the “Lords of the Frontier" from a new angle! 
There are several modules that can be used to replace or supplement the basic rules of the game.

     The "Alternative Factions" module gives players the opportunity to get new versions of the base factions by replacing some creatures and development branches that radically change the strategy and experience of playing for this faction.
     The "New Neutrals" module includes Basilisk Rider, Wild Giant and Arachnid, as well as a new hero, Aleandra, for interesting and challenging battles.
     The "Special Buildings" module adds 2 unique buildings to each faction, which will give useful bonuses when built.

2 Die 4 is a solo roll-and-write game played with two dice.
The aim of the game is to best fill out a grid of boxes to score as many points as possible.

But there's a twist: The grid obeys Sudoku rules, that is to say only one of any given number can appear in any row or column.
If you can't fill in the numbers right, you need to put in an X.

—description from the designer

ClusterRoll is a solo roll-and-write game where you roll dice to fill in a grid of numbers.

The twist: The dice have to be used as two halves of a domino and have to be entered adjacent to each other.

You score points based on the biggest cluster of each number you make.

—description from the designer

Players manage a row of four face-down cards, secretly viewing only their edge cards at the start. Each turn, choose to either take a visible card from the discard pile or draw blindly from the deck. When drawing, decide whether to keep the new card by swapping it with one of your hidden cards, or discard it immediately.

The challenge lies in remembering which edge cards you've seen while strategically improving your hand through careful swaps. Special action cards add tactical options: peek at your own cards, steal from opponents, or force unwanted exchanges.
The round ends when a confident player shouts "MEOW!" believing they have the lowest total. All other players get one final turn before revealing cards and counting points. The player with the fewest points wins the round.
Multiple rounds determine the overall winner. Success requires balancing memory of your known edge cards, risk assessment, and timing your final call.

—description from the publisher

Tornscape: Damned Hike is an exciting tabletop game for two players, where each commands a squad from one of the opposing factions. The game not only offers unforgettable tactical battles but also immerses players in the multilayered world of Thorn, providing a great foundation for expanding their armies.

If you enjoy strategic battles where every unit matters and victory depends not only on strength but also on completing missions and objectives, Tornscape: The Damned Hike will deliver an unforgettable experience. All components of this game are fully compatible with Tornscape: Aether Expeditions, making it ideal for further battles with other factions of the Westorn world, such as Angeln, Castilia, the Priory of Hope, and the Abyss.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Sengoku Jidai (Warring States Period) is a solitaire strategy game set during the final decades of Japan’s Warring States Period, leading up to the decisive Battle of Sekigahara.

As the player, you will take on the roles of Oda Nobunaga, Toyotomi Hideyoshi, and Tokugawa Ieyasu in turn, guiding their rise to power. Your goal is to conquer territories controlled by rival clans, overcome political and military challenges, and ultimately unify Japan under a single rule.

GAME COMPONENTS


     Game Rules
     One map (60x46 cm)
     30 Event cards
     37 Tactics cards
     40 Battle cards
     12 Clan cards
     167 counters (20x20mm)

Have you always wanted to raise a wolf cub?

This 36-card mini-expansion for Grimm World allows you to experience the game cooperatively. One player continues to play the character, while the other player plays Isegrim the wolf. 
Finally experience fairytale-like dark stories together.

Simply add the cards to the respective decks and start a new campaign.

—description from the designer

The Scooter Expansion will replace the after-hours scooter use in the base game and replace it with a much more robust delivery system using scooters. In this expansion, you will place your scooters out onto the city board for delivery bonuses. We will also introduce a NEW signature dish!

—description from the publisher

The Skills Expansion will give your workers asymmetrical skills. Each player will have the opportunity to give their workers skills to enhance their play. These skills are unique and once acquired you get to keep the power for the remainder of the game. This will add another crunchy layer of gaming goodness to the Night Market Experience.

—description from the publisher

The Arcade Expansion will highlight the stall games that are in night markets. From Shooting Hoops to Dart to scoping Shrimp there are all kinds of games. In this expansion, the player will have the chance to place these game stalls in their markets for bonuses during and at the end of the game.

—description from the publisher

The MiniMart Experience is truly special in Night Market. If you are a solo player and enjoy euro-style games this is one you do not want to miss. The solo player is very challenging and the replay on this solo version is high. It also includes a fabulous Multi-Player Competitive Mode against Automa and Co-Op against Automa. There are Multiple Modes of play.

—description from the publisher

The Cultural Expansion celebrates the four largest festivals that happen throughout the year. The Dragon Boat Festival, Lantern Festival, Mid-Autumn (Moon) Festival, and The Spring Festival. The Add-On Pack will come with different setups and scenarios for the players based on the festivals.

—description from the publisher

The Stretch Goal Pack from the Kickstarter campaign.
It consists of 7 small addons for the base game of Night Market:

     Formosa Tour
     Bonus Seasonel Attractions
     Fruit Stands
     Metro
     Objectives
     Wild cusomers
     Sous Chefs


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Sous Scellés: Les Maudits de Fosse-aux-Ronces
description:
You're the detective!
Everyone knew about this abandoned house in Fosse-aux-Ronces. Some even believed it to be cursed. Perhaps that's what drew this group of high-school students there on that evening in 1997. How could a young man barely out of his teens kill one of his companions in cold blood?

The ensuing trial shakes up the peaceful daily life of the village, but the murderer's conviction seems to appease everyone.

Years later, the deaths linked to this group of friends multiply, rekindling questions about this alleged curse.

Note: Internet connection required.

Cette maison abandonnée de Fosse-aux-Ronces, tout le monde la connaissait. Certains la croyaient même maudite. C’est peut-être ce qui a attiré ce groupe de lycéens à s’y rendre en cette soirée de 1997. Comment un jeune homme à peine sorti de l’adolescence a-t-il pu tuer de sang froid l’un de ses compagnons ? Le procès qui s’en suit secoue le quotidien paisible du village mais la condamnation du meurtrier semble apaiser les esprits. Des années plus tard, les morts liées à ce groupe d’amis se multiplient et ravivent les questionnements sur cette prétendue malédiction.

You're the detective!
The large Fréchard family reunites in their parents' Burgundy home. house. But during the course of the evening, tensions are revealed around the siblings and the family business. The dinner takes a macabre turn when, in the early hours of the morning, the body of Daniel, the youngest brother, is discovered in the cellar. discovered in the cellar.

A tense family retreat, the same evening 8 different points of view: who's telling the truth?

Note: Internet connection required.

La grande famille Fréchard se réunit dans la maison bourguignonne des parents. Mais au cours de la soirée, des tensions se révèlent autour de la fratrie et de l’affaire familiale. Le dîner prend un tournant macabre lorsqu’au petit matin, le corps de Daniel, le frère cadet, est découvert dans la cave. Un huis-clos familial sous tension, une même soirée décrite sous 8 points de vue différents : qui dit la vérité ?

boardgame: Sous Scellés: La fille du troisième
description:
You're the detective!
In life, everything smiles on Sarah. She's got her dream job, lives in a lovely apartment in Lyon and has a dog she adores. The only thing missing is love, so in the meantime, she's making the most of her single life via dating apps. On this particular evening, it's late at night when she arrives home from a surprise birthday party organized by her friends at her favorite bar. She's careful not to make any noise so as not to disturb her neighbors.

But it's another wake-up call that awaits them in the early hours of the morning: the din of the fire department sirens under their windows. Sarah's body lies at the foot of the building, having fallen to her death from the 3rd floor.

Did she jump? Was she pushed? Did she flee from an assailant?

Note: Internet connection required.

Dans la vie, tout sourit à Sarah. Elle a le job de ses rêves, vit dans un joli appartement à Lyon et a un chien qu’elle adore. Il ne lui manque plus que l’amour, alors en attendant, elle profite à fond de son célibat via des applis de rencontre. Ce soir là, c’est tard dans la nuit qu’elle passe la porte de chez elle, après une soirée d’anniversaire surprise organisée par ses amis dans son bar préféré. Elle veille à ne pas faire de bruit pour ne pas gêner ses voisins.Mais c’est un autre réveil qui les attend au petit matin : le vacarme des sirènes des pompiers sous leurs fenêtres. Le corps de Sarah gît au pied de l’immeuble, après une chute mortelle du 3e étage. A-t-elle sauté ? A-t-elle été poussée ? A-t-elle fui un agresseur ?

Fake Crime is an intuitive yet innovative game where the art is part of the gameplay! Play clue cards to exonerate suspects by naming an object that appears on both cards. Other investigators must follow suit as everyone scrambles to be the first one to catch the killer. Pick your suspect and pit your sleuthing skills against your rivals in this simple yet sophisticated game of mystery and murder. Don't have any rivals? Try the solo mystery challenge mode.

These cards can be used as a traditional tarot deck to examine the evidence locker of your mind. Structurally similar to the Rider-Waite tarot, with a thematic twist. The Guidebook has concise, approachable interpretations with a forensic flair. Sometimes we hide information from ourselves, these cards can help you uncover the clues.

—description from the publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description: Arquilínea is a game that challenges observation, knowledge, strategy, and memory with competitive and collaborative variations. The objective in all its forms of play is to chronologically organize works from different periods in the history of architecture.
