Cults of Underghast is a fast paced deck builder, where you have to be the first to collect enough victory cards to declare yourself the leading cult of Underghast. Influence from the population of Underghast is required to achieve this goal. Buy cards and do actions that works towards what your belief believes in.

—description from the designer

A 10-card booster pack that includes 7 additional solo Commissions and 1 bonus Guest Judge.

—description from the publisher

Vihon: The Age of Domination is a fully modular role-playing card game set in a mythic world of conquest, rebellion, and survival. Create your hero from a self-contained Character Box, selecting from diverse races and rich specializations, then take on thrilling story-driven adventures using Adventure Boxes packed with narrative events, brutal combat, and powerful loot.

Explore the war-torn realm of Vihon—where Elves rule through colonial might, Dwarves feud over mystical runes, Humans return to tribal roots, and Orcs cling to survival on a floating city. Each race offers four unique paths, from Elven assassins and Dwarven rune-warriors to Human herbalists and tribal hunters, allowing for deep customization and strategic variety.

Every game is shaped by the cards you bring and the choices you make. Build your deck, equip your character, and face off against enemy forces and fate itself as you forge your legend across Vihon’s ever-evolving landscape. Whether solo or with friends, Vihon blends character-building, tactical cardplay, and immersive storytelling into an endlessly replayable tabletop experience.

The city's upcoming commercial district is looking for local businesses. The work is on you to seek out potential businesses and customers before we open up.

To maximize your benefits, you must take advantage of other players' placement of businesses, as you work out the best combination of customers.

The commercial building at 86 Jianshe Road is now open!
Players act as building operators, collaboratively establishing shops in this shared skyscraper while competing to attract VIP customers. After 5 turns, the player with the highest total Popularity Points from guests at game end claims victory.

In Theos, you are a Greek deity who wants to capture some mythological creatures! Aim well, and flick your dice with the right amount of force to win the best cards or steal your opponents’ cards. If you score more points than everyone else, you will reign over all Olympus!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Elden Ring: Realm of the Grafted King – Iron Fist Alexander
description:
Iron Fist Alexander (Exclusive)

I am the great jar warrior, Iron Fist Alexander! Lend me strength, O warriors within! Let us become one champion, together!

A community favourite and exclusive NPC, Iron Fist Alexander won't be sold at general retail.

Card game similar to Mau Mau or UNO with funny action cards from the school environment, belongs to the book of the same name.

-- German --
Herr Schröder ist ein ganz normaler Lehrer im ganz normalen Wahnsinn zwischen Notenvergabe und Pausenklingel.
In diesem Spiel bist du Herr Schröder – und musst deine Kollegen vertreten, die es nicht aus den Ferien zurück in die Schule geschafft haben. Dabei gibt es zu allem Überfluss noch die ein oder andere Hürde: Da sind Besserwisser-Streberin Anastasia, Justin, dessen Verhaltensoriginalität dich um Stunden zurückwirft, und natürlich die Landesschulbehörde, die mit tollen neuen Ideen deine To-dos potenziert.
Schaffst du es, deine Handkarten als Erster loszuwerden und somit #korrekturensohn
des Jahres zu werden?

A collection of five plastic miniatures for use in the classic BattleTech tabletop and Alpha Strike games. The box also provides dry-erase cards intended to directly support Alpha Strike gameplay as well as Battlefield Support cards.

—user summary

FORTIFY YOUR PERIMETER!
Any long-term campaign requires a secure base of operations. Emplacements are a perfect way to secure that perimeter, providing fire support for BattleMech and vehicle units.

Unleash Battlefield Support: Emplacements! Included are five different emplacements, with turrets that may be swapped between any of the bases and towers. This mix-and-march flexibility allows for a variety of emplacement types and adjustments to line of sight for any type of mission.

—description from the back of the box

Bird Brain is a fast slap game for quick-winged birds. You're a bird trying to grab your grub while avoiding harmful trash and junk food. Junk food can net you big points, but eat too much and you'll be sick! Watch out for other birds that will try to steal your well earned meals. Play fast and smart to be the brainiest bird of them all.

Rallye is a two-player tile laying game where players place, in turn, various segments of roads on their own board in order to create the largest network, but also on their opponent's board in order to block them. New segments must coincide with previous ones.
The player with the most occupied spaces is the winner.

What Does the Cat Say? Moo? Meow?

Get ready for a fast, fun, and wildly silly game of animal mix-ups!

Flip a card, find a match, and race to be the first to shout the right sound or name—but be careful, your brain might play tricks on you! Stay sharp, react fast, and collect the most cards to win.

Tactical minigame where players will amass their army of undead and fight with others for domination.
Every move matters, every card has its role, and one mistake can make a difference between glory and defeat.

boardgame: Las Ligas de la Ñ: Eñesutras
description:
Las Ligas de la Ñ: Eñesutras ("The Ñ Leagues: Eñesutras") is an existential card game for 1 to infinite players.

In this new Ñ game (the twelfth in the series), each card contains one or more quotes, either from the universe of The Ñ Leagues or from classic authors of Spanish and world literature. These quotes are accompanied by the title of the work, the author, and the historical period, and are classified by various themes (philosophy, religion, history, politics, art, science, love, humor…).

Eñesutras stands midway between the sutras of Indian tradition (brief and concise texts from Buddhism, Hinduism, and Jainism that convey philosophical, religious, or spiritual teachings) and the I Ching (a Chinese work of sapiential and oracular literature filled with philosophical, ethical, and symbolic messages).

In the multiplayer mode, players must dedicate quotes from the cards in their hand in the most justified and persuasive way possible, aiming to convince the rest of the players during the end-of-round voting. In turns, each player reads their chosen eñesutra aloud and dedicates it to another player (first stating the recipient, then reading the quote). The choice of recipient is entirely free, and the person receiving the card can choose to remain silent or deliver a comeback to gain popularity. At the end of each round (once all quotes have been dedicated), players vote in secret for their favorite player—without being allowed to vote for themselves. Each vote is worth 1 point, and the winner(s) of the round receive 3 bonus points. After the agreed number of rounds (1, 3, 5, 7…), the player with the most points wins.

In the solo mode, the player must read ten full cards and reflect on their existence—without falling into an existential crisis.

An alternative mode allows for anonymous dedications, where the quotes are written on paper or read in a neutral voice so that the rest of the players must guess who the dedication was meant for. In the case of the solo mode, you may always send a message by phone—or even indulge in nostalgia and send a romantic letter by traditional post.

Moreover, this deck is compatible with other games in the series, such as The Ñ Leagues: Arcane Queens or The Ñ Leagues: Aces of Destiny, where quotes, arcana, and leagues intertwine and function as narrative oracles.

For 47 years, King Char Kol brought justice and prosperity to the realm. Following his mysterious disappearance near the ancient city of Redford, several rivals have staked their claim to succeed him, each seeking to consolidate their power by accumulating wealth and resources.

In this epic strategy game, you'll choose from five unique leaders, each with their own distinct abilities and playstyles. With twenty different unit types and eighteen event cards that can dramatically shift the course of the game, no two playthroughs are ever the same.

Gather scarce resources, choose followers to muster and promote, and strategically build your forces to secure your claim to the realm. Will you peacefully expand your dominion or ruthlessly 
attack your rivals?

Will you choose harvest or conquest?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
An eighteen-card tile-laying game played on a five-by-five grid, where players take turns drawing double-sided cards from three stacks. Each turn the player chooses which card to place terrain-side up to the center of the grid, and which to play as a young adventurer (with pet dinosaur in tow) on the side, trying to match the icons on the respective cards to gain victory points. When the grid is filled players tally their VP points, with the highest total winning.

—description from the publisher

In ぽいよね (poiyone), you'll work together with others to determine which among a random group of item cards is most like a given person in the group.

For example, do you have a friend who's kind of like a calculator? Maybe a bit Christmas tree-esque? Even if not, you can use a wide variety of justifications to connect the person to the item - the clothes they're wearing that day, their personality, their hobbies...

The game has two variations, a short game and a long game. For the short game, the lead player will look at the drawn item cards (equal to the player count plus one) and place cards representing each player face-down next to the item card they think that player is most similar to. The other players must then work together to line up their name boards next to the same items the lead player did. If the name boards match the representative cards the lead player placed, it's a win! If any are switched around, it's a loss.

The long game provides even more of a challenge to get through, but the overall idea remains the same. Players must try their best to align their way of thinking in creative and humorous ways to clear the game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Acorn Delivery （どんぐりデリバリー）
description:
Acorn Delivery is a sugoroku game in which players play cards and move one acorn piece forward together. However, you cannot go too far to the "Acorn has dropped" square. Aim for the goal without dropping the acorns!

—description from the publisher
publishers: パニーニゲームズ（Paninee Games）

description:
美國革命：華盛頓 ("American Revolution: Washington") is a 1-4 player strategic level wargame to simulate the American Revolution in 1776 to 1783 in north America.

There are 8 rounds in one game. Player commands the British army to conquer the Continental army in north America. British armies may move into the revolt areas and roll the die to attack rebel armies. Be careful, the rebels may ambush you randomly. In the end round, you need to score at least 7 vps by capturing the Continental commanders and sweep the rebel armies.

American Revolution: Washington is the seventh game of the Revolt & Conquer series. You can combine Washington and Greene maps together for playing a Revolutionary War campaign game with 3-4 players.

—description from the publisher

美國革命：格林 ("American Revolution: Greene") is a 1-4 player strategic level wargame to simulate the American Revolution in 1776 to 1783 in north America.

There are 8 rounds in one game. Player commands the British army to conquer the Continental army in north America. British armies may move into the revolt areas and roll the die to attack rebel armies. Be careful, the rebels may ambush you randomly. In the end round, you need to score at least 7 vps by capturing the Continental commanders and sweep the rebel armies.

American Revolution: Greene is the eighth game of the Revolt & Conquer series. You can combine Washington and Greene maps together for playing a Revolutionary War campaign game with 3-4 players.

—description from the publisher

A supplement for Stargrave that explores the ideas of artificial intelligence run amok, soulless cyborg killers, and cyberpunk hacking.

Welcome to the Outlaw Technology Sector, a vast region of space cut off from the rest of the Ravaged Galaxy. Once a haven to criminals, political dissidents, and rogue scientists, it was overrun by an artificial intelligence intent on wiping out all organic life. Only through the combined might of the pre-Last War great empires was this menace brought down, and their retribution was terrible. Now the Outlaw Technology Sector is a dead place, a nightmare place. Navigational systems refuse to chart a course there, calling such paths 'death vectors'. But the independent crews survive by going where others fear to tread, and the Sector contains technology not found anywhere else…

Death Vector is a supplement for Stargrave, containing 8 new scenarios set inside the Outlaw Technology Sector. Instead of the pirate fleets, the players contend with the legions of cyborg automatons that still prowl the region, hunting organic life. Paired with these scenarios is a new campaign element, 'The Network', which brings cyberpunk hacking into the game. This book also includes a new space encounters table to be used for adventures in the OTS, a new advanced technology table, and a bestiary filled with cybernetic horrors.

—description from the publisher

You are settlers in search of new lands. You have just discovered uninhabited islands! Each player takes control of one of them. It is up to you to decide how your islands will develop and how you will manage them.

Pierwsza osada ("First Settlement") is a card game in which players, on their turn, will choose cards from the market and their hand and add them to their play area—their settlement on the newly discovered island. The better you plan your settlement’s development, the more points you will earn—each type of card grants points in a different way. You can also gain additional points if merchants visit your island.

At the end of the game, each player’s settlement will consist of 16 cards. The player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

On the outskirts of the city there is the Grand Casino. It was a hive of activity long ago and well stocked with potential life-changing supplies, for there were restaurants, cafes and facilities for thousands of visitors. The route is more dangerous but the prize might be worth it, especially when it comes to food, water and medical supplies.
This is the third expansion for Rad Zone Totality. It includes a new large building (the Casino) to explore, journey charts and a set of search area descriptions, as well as a new character to encounter.

—description from the publisher

A fun, fast play ruleset for Skirmish games of no more than 50 to 100 miniatures. Games should play in 3-4 hours, perfect for a morning or afternoon wargame session.

The 80 page book Includes a movement and event deck to allow for random events, full army lists and historical notes.

—description from the publisher

Dive deeper into the haunting depths of the Spire with Spire's End: Siren's Song. This expansion features 16 cards, including previously released favorites like the Rat King, the Mega Mushroom, and the Siren—all available together for the first time! Enjoy additional art and new story cards that shake up gameplay with tougher challenges, facing menacing foes like Pipkin & Thistlewick.

—description from the publisher

Eukao – The Hill of Ukāo is a simple abstract board game.

Players take turns selecting a stack of tokens on the board where their color is on top. They then pick up the entire stack and redistribute the tokens one by one in a straight line, starting with the bottom token. The first player to create a stack of three consecutive tokens of their color wins the game.

To win, players must strategically plan their moves while thinking ahead. Despite its simple rules, the game offers deep tactical play, making it enjoyable for both children and adults.

Part of the “Landscapes of Japan” Series – Volume 1

Eukao is the first release in Sui Works’ Landscapes of Japan series.

The theme of this first game is Biei Town in Hokkaido. Located in the heart of Hokkaido, Biei is known for its gently rolling hills and lush, vibrant nature. With colorful patchwork fields of wheat, sugar beets, legumes, and potatoes, the town is a visual delight and is officially recognized as a member of “The Most Beautiful Villages in Japan” alliance. Efforts continue to preserve and share its scenic beauty for future generations.

This game uniquely expresses Biei’s landscape and culture through the mechanic of stacked tokens. It’s easy to learn, even for beginners, yet offers enough depth to satisfy seasoned board gamers.

Enjoy a unique gaming experience while taking in the serene charm of Biei’s hills.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: World of Tanks: The Card Game
description:
World of Tanks: The Card Game brings the legendary armored warfare of the video game franchise to the tabletop in a fast-paced, strategic card battle format. Developed by Archon Studio in collaboration with Wargaming, this game puts players in command of iconic tanks in tactical duels that demand careful planning, smart positioning, and a solid grasp of battlefield momentum.

At its core, World of Tanks: The Card Game is a dynamic deck-builder where you construct your arsenal from a wide variety of tanks—each with unique traits, strengths, and roles. Choose your approach: swarm the enemy with agile light tanks, unleash the raw power of heavy armor, or strike with precision from mid-range. The flexibility of the system encourages experimentation and rewards creative strategies.

Victory isn't limited to simply eliminating your opponent's forces. Multiple paths to success ensure that each match plays out differently, whether you're holding objectives, outmaneuvering your foe, or executing a well-timed combo.

Featuring immersive artwork, intuitive mechanics, and deep tactical choices, World of Tanks: The Card Game captures the thrill of tank combat in a compact, replayable format. Whether you're a longtime fan of the franchise or a newcomer to the battlefield, prepare for an explosive fight to the finish.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In Intelliwar, players will employ all their tactical and strategic skills to claim their rightful place on the Silver Throne. To do that, you will move units around the board, activate special abilities, upgrade your forces, and strike with calculated attacks. The goal is to eliminate the enemy Prince or wipe out all enemy units except the Prince. The first player to fulfill at least one of these two victory conditions wins.

At the beginning of the game, units are drafted. All of them are unique, so choose carefully!

On their turn, a player places Activation tokens on two different Unit Cards that don’t have Activation Tokens, with the player-colored side facing up. When you place an Activation Token on a card with the player-colored side up, you “activate” this unit. On a turn, each Unit may perform up to 2 of the following actions: Move, Attack, or Use a Special Ability.

A player immediately wins the game when they fulfill at least one of the following victory conditions: 
eliminate the Enemy Prince OR eliminate all the enemy Unit Tokens except for the Prince.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Parks (Second Edition): Haleakalā National Park Promo Card
description:
A collectible holofoil park promo included as a preorder bonus for both the Base Camp and Summit Edition of Parks (Second Edition)

This card includes alternative art and unique requirements to visit the national park.

boardgame: 新銳桌遊小買家 (New Boardgame Buyer 2024)
description:
Choose a board game store to shop at, take the game (card) from the store.  After choosing one, the next player repeats, going to a store that hasn't been visited this turn.

Wait until everyone has chosen and then put them in front of you. Repeat until all players have collected 6 cards.

Half-time bonus: The player with the most hearts on the collected cards can pick one of the remaining cards from the first half and remove the rest of the cards from the game.

In the second half, refill the stores and play again.

Final rewards: The first and second players with the most hearts on the collected cards will pick one card from the remaining cards in the second half and take it away. The game ends and scoring begins.

There are five scoring methods in the game

Family game: Collect as many different kinds of letters as possible.

Children's game: Stars are points, which are helpful for mid-game rewards

Party game: There are 4 colors in total. The more of the same color you collect, the better.

Strategy game: The product of the brain symbol and the hourglass symbol is the score

Other games: You will get extra points based on the other 4 board games you have collected, 1 point for each.

Similar to tic-tac-toe but with the need to think several moves ahead and with each piece having a particular power and control over your opponent. Try to create the most lines of 3 counters in your color.

The standard game is played on a 4 x 4 square grid. Each Player has eight pieces with two of four different symbols. Each piece when played determines where your opponent can play their next piece.

Each piece you play instructs your opponent to play in a straight line (either orthogonally or diagonally) from the piece just played, to play away from or to play adjacent to the piece just played. The aim is to form lines of 3 but with your opponent controlling where you can play this is harder than it sounds. If you can play so your opponent is unable to go you get a free play anywhere on the board.

CGXpress is a hidden identity, social deduction game where the player playing as Mr Huang, along with his helpers, must deduce where the Mayor is to deliver the package.

How to play

Set up
Use cards based on number of players
3P = 3 players and above
6P = 6 players and above

Choose a player to be Mr Huang! Deal the rest to others

Leave the last card in the center

Mr Huang then closes his eyes while the rest reveal their cards to each other before placing them face down again
Once done, Mr Huang can open their eyes again!

And the game can begin!

Mr Huang needs to ask each player 1 of 3 questions to find out who is Mayor Har!

Players can choose to lie or tell the truth!

Once all questions have been answered,
Mr Huang must choose a card to reveal.

The revealed card determines the winning icon!

—description from the publisher

Each player takes on the role of a leader of a small kingdom, intent on becoming the premier ruler of the land.

Through strategy and chance, you must raise armies, battle your enemies, make alliances among the factions (and possibly each other), gather advisors, and seek out ancient artifacts to gain the gods' favor.

Your ultimate goal is the collection of victory points. Whoever has the most when the Conquest Stone is claimed and used wins the game.

You gain victory points by building and taking castles, guarding ancient standing stones, and finding advisors to curry the gods’ favor.

There are two ways to play a game of Lands of Conquest. You can play until the Conquest Stone shows up on the map through the random drawing of fate tokens, or you can decide the rough length of the game you want to play (we suggest at least 2-3 hours, but it could easily be 2-3 sessions if the players are willing), and set a timer, with the Conquest Stone arriving when the timer indicates.

Either way, the arrival of the Conquest Stone signals the beginning of Endgame.

ENDGAME

The ruler who captures the Conquest Stone and successfully returns it to their city can choose when the game ends by calling upon the gods and claiming the high throne. The stone itself is worth 7 Victory Points. Each turn it remains in your city, you gain an additional victory point, giving your opponents only a limited time to alter the outcome.

—description from the designer

World Domination! A card game for those power hungry psychopaths amongst us. Gather your Minions, build awesome Weapons and turn your rivals into teeny tiny pieces!

Collect 3 matching components to build a Doomsday Device. Charge it up and unleash it upon your opponents. But beware! Your opponents will be doing the same. Stop them by using minions to raid their lab or Sabotage their ambitions with a sudden loss of power.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: Battle of the Corridor
description:
Battle of the Corridor is a set of scenarios for Tsukuda's Legend of the Galactic Heroes series.  To play it, you need at least one of these games: Lippstadt, Vermilion, Lost Complete Victory, or Iserlohn.

The game is a doujin and was only for sale at game shows in Japan, such as Tokyo Game Market and Comiket.  This meant that you needed to preorder the game before you came and then pick it up at the show.

Battle of the Corridor's description from the author Nishiki:

It consists of multiple scenarios with reference to the description in the designer's note published in the Tsukuda "Vermillion" manual.

Scenario 20
"Battle of the Corridor Part 1: Victory and Invincibility: Sorcerer vs. Fierce General" The outpost battle, the clash between the El Fasil Revolutionary Reserve Army (Yang Fleet) vs. the Black Lancer Fleet + Fahrenheit Fleet.

Scenario 21
"Battle of the Corridor - Kaleidoscope - 〈1〉 Sorcerer vs. Golden Lion" Battle with the main force of the Imperial Army. Head-to-head confrontation between Jan and Reinhardt. It covers everything from the invasion of the Iserlohn corridor by the main force of the Imperial Army to the death of Senior General Steinmetz.

Scenario 22
"Battle of the Corridor - Kaleidoscope - 〈2〉 Sorcerer vs. Ironclad The third round of the battle of the corridor. Consecutive battles with the Müller fleet and the Eisenach fleet. Yang's all-out offensive and the battle to the death of the Müller fleet that greeted it.

Scenario 23
"Battle of the Corridor - Part 2 - The End of the Great Parent Expedition - The Last Battle of the Sorcerer" The final round of the Battle of the Corridor. A fierce battle between the fleet under Mittermeier under Bayerlein and the reorganized Black Lancer Fleet. Covering the repulsion of the Imperial Army's third offensive, the reverse salient of the Yang fleet, and the death of Vice Admiral Fisher.

The game requires assembly.

—user summary
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description: From a deck of 32 cards each player starts with a Hazel tree, using the rest of the cards to create a 3x3 grid: two columns of nature-themed dominoes featuring various animals and undergrowth plants, and one column of trees. Players take turns picking a card from the grid, replenishing from the deck, trying to collect the creatures matching the icons on the trees in their grove to score points and hopefully win a special garden card. When the deck is used up or when the players' groves can support no more life, VP points are tallied, the person with the highest number winning.

boardgame: Victory by Any Means: The Strategic Game of Interstellar Exploration, Diplomacy, & Conquest
description:
A NEW AGE DAWNS IN THE GALAXY

Two hundred years ago, a coalition of alien civilizations known as the OLD EMPIRE banded together to fight back against a relentless machine intelligence known only as the HARBINGERS. These mechanical monstrosities had erupted out of the Sagittarius Arm of the Milky Way, burning every colony they encountered and threatening to end all life in the galaxy. The Harbinger’s superior technology outmatched anything the Old Empire had in its own arsenal and they found themselves losing ground, their colonies burnt to cinders as the Harbinger fleets advanced across the stars.

In a final desperate, climactic battle, the alliance narrowly succeeded in destroying the Harbinger “brainworld” using a PRECURSOR planeteering bomb. However, the detonation of this ancient superweapon triggered a hyperspace shockwave that began to systematically shutdown the galactic jump lane network until interstellar travel was impossible. The GREAT HYPERSPACE COLLAPSE had begun.

It has been two centuries since the jump lanes disappeared, but your empire’s long range monitoring stations are showing that the jump lanes have returned. It’s now time for you to lead your empire back to the stars.

Victory by Any Means is the strategic game of interstellar exploration, diplomacy, and conquest where each player takes control of an alien empire to help it rebuild after the end of the Great Hyperspace Collapse. Choose from any of ten different alien species, each with their own unique abilities and force lists. Explore strange alien worlds, establish new colonies, expand your fleets and armies, develop new technologies, perform covert operations, and engage in diplomacy and warfare with the other major and minor alien civilizations you encounter along the way.

This book includes all of the rules you need to run a strategic science fiction campaign in the VICTORY BY ANY MEANS Harbinger Wars universe, but you can easily adapt the rules to run a campaign in your favorite science fiction setting. The modular nature of the game rules makes it easy to customize your campaign experience using the selection of optional rules that are included with this book.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Эволюция: Иммунитет. Мини-дополнение (Evolution: The Origin of Species – Immunity Mini Expansion)
description:
Эволюция: Иммунитет. Мини-дополнение ("Evolution: The Origin of Species – Immunity Mini Expansion") is a mini-expansion that is compatible with all games in the "green" Evolution series.

It includes 3 new traits: "Horns", "Genomic Instability" and "Immunity".

boardgame: Эволюция: Жизненный цикл. Мини-дополнение (Evolution: The Origin of Species – Life Cycle Mini Expansion)
description:
Эволюция: Жизненный цикл. Мини-дополнение ("Evolution: The Origin of Species – Life Cycle Mini Expansion") is a mini-expansion that is compatible with all games in the "green" Evolution series.

It includes 4 new traits: "Spores", "Cyst", "Mammal" and "Stressful".

Pixel simulates the world of scientific research, specifically Astrophysics, the study of the Universe. The goal of the game is to measure astrophysical observables with ever-improving resolution. The player will have to manage the research center, build an observational strategy using telescopes from the ground and from space, upgrade instruments, publish papers, lead his team but also find economic resources to fund new projects. At the end of the game, the player with the most scientific prestige win.

boardgame: Эволюция: Случайные мутации – Сверххищник. Мини-дополнение (Evolution: Random Mutations – Apex Predator Mini Expansion)
description:
Эволюция: Случайные мутации – Сверххищник. Мини-дополнение ("Evolution: Random Mutations – Apex Predator Mini Expansion") is a stretch goal mini-expansion for crowdfunding the Evolution: Random Mutations.

It includes 3 new traits: "Apex Predator", "Sociality" and "Skinny" (4 cards each).

Add them to the Traits deck to try out new survival strategies.

boardgame: Bomb Busters: La cité de la peur Mission
description:
Promo card given at the 2024 Cannes Board games Convention (FIJ) a mission titled "La cité de la peur" in french, "The City of Fear" in English (reference to the beloved French comedy movie "La cité de la peur”).

The mission:
In the middle of the Cannes Film Festival, a poorly measured glitter bomb threatens to explode on the red carpet. This bomb therefore has a lot of red wires... Fortunately, they are not hidden under the carpet, you know (almost) where they are!


     Deal 2 red wires per support face down to each Minesweeper, who places them on their relevant support(s) then takes the corresponding Info tokens and places them next to this/these support(s).
     Exceptionally, do not place any red counters on the board.
     Each Minesweeper places an Info token as usual, from those remaining available

In Звездопад ("Starfall") game, the objective is to find a hidden gem's color faster than your opponents.

The game is played with cards that have colored gems of various sizes and numbers. Each card has a specific color, indicated by its border.

At the start of each round, 5 cards are revealed for all colors. A task card is then revealed, showing a pattern of gems players must match. Players search through the stack to find the matching card, following these steps: 

     The color of the first gem on the task card determines which card to check first.
     The size and number of gems help narrow down possible matches.
     Players must mentally trace the clues from one card to another until they find the exact match (if this card was trace twice).


The first player who correctly tap the right-colored gem wins it, along with any linked cards and task cards. Each player may only take one gem.

The game ends when you can't open 5 cards. The player with the most cards wins. If there's a tie, all these players are winners.

—user summary

Upgrade your Achtung Panzer! experience with Iron Coffins!

Packed with special rules, missions, and lots of tank statistics, Iron Coffins is an essential addition to your gaming library. Designed to expand and build upon the core rulebook, this tome contains everything you need to take your armoured warfare to the next level. From expanded campaign rules, new historical scenarios, and tank aces, to over a hundred new asset and event cards and guidance on taking Achtung Panzer! into every theatre of World War II, this book has something for everyone.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Great War at Sea Jutland: Portugal's Armada
description:
From the Avalanche Press website…

“ Several times a year, when we feel like it, the Golden Journal brings Gold Club members some fun extras for their Avalanche Press games. Our new-model Golden Journal features a “real” booklet, just like a small magazine, and a small set of die-cut, silky-smooth playing pieces.

Golden Journal No. 59: Portugal’s Armada is for Great War at Sea, with 23 new die-cut and silky-smooth pieces. Portugal was a desperately poor country, and this influenced naval development more than any other factor. They desired a fleet to protect their colonies from British and German encroachment, but could not really afford it.

There are four distinct eras of Portuguese naval construction covered in Golden Journal No. 59, only one of which actually happened. First, you get the existing fleet; this has two “long” ship pieces: an ironclad re-built into a coast defense ship. They went cheap and instead just wasted the money. They also have a crapulent Elswick-built cruiser.

The 1907 program is sometimes described as including two “dreadnoughts,” but the design the Portuguese chose was a coast-defense ship, because it was cheaper. They also had cruisers (in reality, small coast-defense ships) to accompany them.

The 1911 program went to three larger ships, but still smaller and cheaper than contemporary dreadnoughts. Vickers Design 702 looked a little like the new British super-dreadnoughts, but had 12-inch rather than 13.5-inch main guns and was much smaller. Again, the program had cruisers as well.

Finally, in July 1921 the U.S. Navy’s Second Battleship Squadron visited Lisbon, with the dreadnought Utah attached and its own six Connecticut-class pre-dreadnoughts. Portuguese naval and political leaders toured the ships, and at some point during a formal dinner squadron commander Charles Frederick Hughes passed along an offer to sell the dreadnought and five of the pre-dreadnoughts. Once again, the Portuguese passed due to the costs.

How do you get it? Only by joining the Gold Club – join up and we’ll tell you how to get it.”
family: Series: Avalanche Press Gold Club Golden Journal Supplements
Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
Morph is a customizable card battle game that blends the accessibility of a board game with the depth of a TCG.

Its first innovation is immediately visible: every card has a detachable illustration. Slipped into sleeves, Morph cards can be overlaid with any standard-sized card (63×88 mm) — from another TCG, a board game, a photo, or a printed drawing — giving players endless creative freedom without altering gameplay.

In the Starter Pack, each player chooses one of six Prodigies, dual-colored leaders that define your strategy. You then combine two factions to create a ready-to-play 24-card deck. Games last about 20 minutes, in duel or solo mode, with streamlined mechanics designed to eliminate common frustrations of TCGs:

- No mana death (each turn you sacrifice one card to generate mana).

- No top-decking (you always draw back to four cards).

- No snowballing (creatures return under their owner’s deck after dealing damage).

The Initiation Pack includes 4 of the game’s 6 factions, each with a distinct archetype:

- Instinct – speed and direct damage

- Chaos – destruction and reanimation

- Flux – regeneration and card advantage

- Swarm – multiplication of small creatures

Each Prodigy card is double-sided (masculine front / feminine back) and, like Creature cards, features a detachable illustration for full customization.

Morph is designed with inclusivity in mind: all cards are printed with icons on the left side of the front and mirrored on the back, ensuring perfect readability for both right- and left-handed players. The compact 60 × 35 cm play area works equally well face-to-face or side-by-side.

Morph positions itself as a “hybrid TCG” — a modular, accessible game to enjoy as a quick, strategic duel or as a gateway into deeper deckbuilding when the full cycle launches in 2026.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Data Chain is an abstract strategic card game with a light cyberpunk theme for 2-4 players about knowing when to save and when to spend your resources. Players take turns collecting chips to purchase cards that are each worth a point from the central Data Row. When you purchase cards, you also receive a reward which increases with each subsequent card you purchase, thus creating a powerful Data Chain! The player with the most points at the end of the game wins! It’ll take both your wits and some luck to master the Data Chain!

The player who most recently used a spreadsheet or had the lowest score in a previous game of Data Chain becomes the first player. Each turn you can either take chips or purchase cards from a shared central Data Row.

A "Data Chain" occurs when a player purchases multiple cards in a single turn. Chain your purchases together and potentially buy all 4 cards in the Data Row! Each card has a cost located on the bottom half of the card which will show how many chips of each color it takes to purchase the card. Each card also shows a reward on the top half of the card which is taken when the card is purchased. If this is your first purchase of the turn, take 1 chip of that color from the bank. If this is the second card you’ve purchased this turn, take 2 chips of that color, etc. Since the reward on each card increases for each link in your Data Chain and you can also spend the reward on purchased cards, planning ahead and strategically chaining your purchases together is the key to success!

The game ends when the last card in the deck is flipped into the data row. After this happens, play continues until all players have taken an equal number of turns. After the game has ended, all players count all of their cards purchased throughout the game (both face up and face down) and whoever has the most cards is the winner!

—description from the designer

Animals are on their way to the ark!
The flood is coming and it’s up to you to rescue the animals before Noah’s Ark sets sail. Work together to pair up the animals, but watch out! Every time you make a wrong match a flood card is added to the grid. Can you find all of the animal pairs and get them on the boat before it floats away?
Three Levels of Play!
Level 1: Pair up the animals and race the flood!
Level 2: Match water animal powers to help fill a bigger boat!
Level 3: Strategize with a combo of rooms from the power deck for the largest boat yet!

Controlled Chaos is a fast-paced party game that blends humour, social strategy, and bold decision-making. Players take turns spinning the Control Centre, which directs them to one of four decks — Hit List, At Risk, Ick, or Outlast — each triggering a different kind of chaotic challenge, callout, or group interaction.

From sparking debates and playful rivalries to testing how well your friends can think under pressure, the game is built to keep everyone engaged, laughing, and slightly on edge. The goal? Complete the prompt, survive the spotlight, and embrace the chaos.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Above and Below: Haunted – Museum of Ghosts
description: Museum of Ghosts is a print-and-play expansion for Above and Below: Haunted. Museum of Ghosts includes an additional storybook and a printable Museum Board that allows for an alternative Banish action where players can donate groups of ghosts to the museum for various rewards and to compete for Top Donor status.

TRAQ is a quizz game where no cultural knowledge is required.

The game master reads 9 wild questions in a row, you must try to remind them. Each player on its turn must answer one of the 9 questions, without telling which one.

The aim of the game is to find enough food for your dinosaur so that it can found a nest and ensure that its species will survive. It is possible to collect food but also you can fight for it with other dinos. As well as in prehistoric times in this game dinos get into dangerous situations. They can be threatened by cruel Tyrannosaur Rex or by lava coming out of volcano. Pick your favorite dino and start a journey to prehistory together.

boardgame: 범인은 이 안에 있다 (The Criminal Is In Here)
description:
범인은 이 안에 있다 ("The criminal is in here.") is a "cooperative mystery game" in which the whole story of the case is revealed by opening cards one by one, collecting evidence related to the case or testimony of suspects, and discussing it together.

There is no fixed method for investigation! At every moment, players must make their own judgments and selections to lead the investigation, and the outcome of the investigation will vary accordingly.

Episode 1 Stuff went to the owner. 
Episode 2 Merry Christmas, Santa! 
Episode 3 Lived happily ever after.

Each episode consists of about 80 main(incident) cards and about 20 item cards.

You open the main card one by one and proceed with the investigation. Based on the clues gathered one by one, your ultimate goal is to accurately reveal the whole story of the case and receive good investigative evaluation.

If there is no more main card to open, the investigation will end immediately, and the "investigation report" of the episode should be prepared from the "investigation report and commentary paper." Even if the main card remains, if you believe you have already learned all about the case, you can always end the investigation and answer questions from the investigation report.

When the investigation report is completed, a 'investigation evaluation' will be conducted for the episode. The investigation will be conducted according to the evaluation items below, and the epilogue of the case will vary depending on the final score.

IntroductionLonsi (from Old Norse: lǫng sýn, meaning "distant sight") is a strategy game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of a hexagonal board, initially set up with white stones, black stones, and empty cells, arranged in such a way that each stone is adjacent to enemy stones but not to others of the same color. The recommended size is 8 cells per side, although boards of 6 or 10 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A visual connection is a maximal set of empty cells in a straight line between two friendly stones.
     The value of a visual connection is the number of cells it contains. An empty cell can be part of several visual connections and is counted separately in each of them.


				
				
					Turns:White plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, move one of your stones in a straight line, jumping over a single enemy stone; there may be empty cells before or after the jumped stone, and the move must end on an empty cell adjacent to friendly stones. Upon landing, any friendly stones adjacent to the occupied cell are removed from the board. If you cannot move any of your stones, you must pass your turn; otherwise, passing is not allowed.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when players pass in consecutive turns. At the end of the game, points are calculated as the sum of the values ​​of the friendly visual connections, plus half a point for the player with the longest visual connection. If there is a tie, the next longest connections are compared until a difference is found. If they all match, the half point goes to the player who passed first. The player with the highest score wins.

—description from the designer

Experimental Technical Readout: RISC samples BattleMechs (and a vehicle) used as testbeds by the Republic Institute of Strategic Combat (RISC). Most were tested in false flag operations around former Republic worlds during the 3130s. Many were deployed in the Republic’s desperate defense against encroaching Great House and Clan formations during the 3140s. All units featured in this book incorporate some of the technologies developed by the RISC (found in Interstellar Operations: Alternate Eras). Statistics and Record Sheets are included for 13 new experimental BattleMechs (and 1 vehicle!), ready for play in advanced BattleTech games.

—description from the publisher

Trivia Game with Star Wars theme. 140 question cards with 4 multiple choice options. It's for you to decide the difficulty level: difficult (ask the question only), medium (ask the question with all 4 choice options) or easy (ask the question with just the 50:50 choices).

The team with more points at the end wins.

A film has been moving from drawer to drawer of a large production company for years. There are several scripts, the original, the one brought by the producer, the one brought by another producer who came later, the one brought by another scriptwriter that no one really knows where it came from... and the production company has had enough. He needs a final script for this film and he has brought all parties together so that you can agree once and for all.

Screenplay is a narrative game in which you and your friends are going to tell a movie that you are going to invent as you go.

A movie can have different tones: Action, Drama, Romanticism, Mystery, Comedy and Horror. Every good movie has a little bit of each of them! At the beginning of the game, players secretly choose which of these tones they prefer to boost.
Taking turns, each player introduces an actor into the film and describes his character or narrates a scene in which several actors advance the plot of the film. In this way the tones of the film gain points.
At the end of the movie, the preferences of each player are revealed. The one who best suits the final result will have won the game! It will probably be time to play another one as revenge.

Face the Infinite in this multi-stage boss fight.
The Infinite is designed for true heroes only - it can only be attempted once you've defeated all three Episodes.

If you win, you'll be rewarded with a surprise that adds a new dimension of replayability!

—description from the publisher

DO66-0 is your mechanical canine companion, ready to help you by granting powerful upgrades. DO66-0 comes equipped with 3 playable game modes:

Solo Mode
Play as one Hero with DO66-0 at your side!

Beginner Mode
Play as DO66-0 and join other Heros.

Boost Mode
Play without DO66-0, but gain its upgrades to help with tough boss fights.

—description from the publisher

In this epic multiplayer adventure, you'll take turns navigating through a richly detailed world filled with intrigue, danger, and opportunity. With 79 pages and 54 cards of dynamic story content offering a variety of narrative possibilities, each playthrough is an exciting and unpredictable experience.

Explore the Western outpost and its surrounding wilderness, where you can forge alliances with townsfolk or betray them for your own gain. As you journey through this wild frontier, you'll be tasked with making tough choices that will impact your standing in the community, your wealth, and your honour.

Compete against other players to earn Legend Points as you rise in fame and notoriety. But as your reputation grows, so does the danger. Can you rise to the top and secure your status as a legend of the Old West, or will your rivals surpass you?

With rich and immersive storytelling, this game is perfect for a small group looking for adventure in a narrative that adapts to their choices. Whether you're seeking fortune, fame, or revenge, the Old West is waiting for you to carve your path.

—description from the designer

Step into the role of an investigator in this two-part mystery, where you’ll examine evidence, analyze suspect statements, and follow the trail of clues as the story unfolds. When you’re ready, open the solution PDF to check your deductions and evaluate how well you uncovered the truth.

Lukas Hoffman, a 19-year-old Berlin resident, begins receiving unsettling notes. As the messages grow more threatening, he reports them to the authorities, and you take over the investigation. As the case progresses, the situation escalates, revealing that it is far more complex than it initially seemed.

This is our most challenging case yet, so for new players, we recommend starting with our other mysteries to get familiar with the investigative process before taking on this complex case.

—description from the designer

In the wake of global conflict, powerful countries fight for supremacy. Territories shift hands, alliances crumble and soldiers await their commands. Enter Blitz Creed where players engage in intense battles and use their wits to emerge victorious in this world of relentless warfare.

Blitz Creed  is a fast-paced, military-themed card game where players battle for global dominance. Set in a world of strategic warfare, players control countries and deploy soldiers to engage in battles and conquer regions. The game combines elements of military strategy, outsmarting opponents and anticipating the moves of others in a contest of wits and power.

In Blitz Creed, you use a deck of 110 cards that include countries, soldiers, dispute cards and reinforcements. You’ll strategically play your cards to deploy soldiers, attack other countries while protecting your regions. Each country has a strength which determines it's ability to win or lose a battle. There are also special cards like Regional Strike, which let you unleash powerful attacks on your enemies.

The goal is simple: conquer and secure 3 regions by collecting the required number of countries. The first player to secure 3 regions becomes world leader & wins the game.

—description from the designer

The year is 1834 and you are the commander of an East India Company expedition headed into the mountain kingdom of Coorg, in southwest India, with orders to depose an uncooperative raja. Or, if you prefer, you are the commander of the Raja's troops, a Kodavan guerrilla fighter defending your land (and by default the Raja, too) against an incursion of the East India Company.

The Coorg War is an asymmetric war-game, one player commanding five columns of troops marching on fixed paths toward the center of the map, and the other player manoeuvring guerrilla bands to block and ambush the columns, trying to wear them down and run out the clock. Success is measured by how long it takes for the Raja's Morale to drop to a point that he is willing to come to terms.

The combat system is based on that old standby, rolling dice to score hits. Hits on the Raja's warbands take them out of the fray temporarily; the trick is to manage the 'recycling' process. Hits on the Expedition cause Fatigue, which slows their operations. To recover from Fatigue the Columns must periodically Rest or risk being forced to Retreat. The Raja's forces are represented by wooden cubes and the Expedition's forces by cards laid out in a predetermined tableau.

The game is highly suitable for solitaire. There are no ‘bots’, only suggested guidelines for the opposing side. At the same time, there are enough tense decision points to make the game enjoyable for two players. It is also a short game. With experience, probably no more than two hours, and possibly as short as 90 minutes. (The game covers 9 days or 27 turns, but unless the Expedition player fumbles the ball it should end after 3-4 days.) Table space required is only the typical 22”x34” map sheet.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pole Position: Circuits Pack 2 Monaco & Spain
description:
This is an expansion with two circuits. The base game is required for playing.
The circuit Monaco is based on the real Circuit de Monaco. The circuit Spain seems to be inspired by the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya.

The Wild Card Game is a trick-taking game. The player who plays the highest card wins the trick, but if two high cards match, then the next highest card wins the round. Play your cards carefully and try to outwit your opponents in this totally roarsome game!
It's a trick-taking safari game where players must collect the most animal cards. Play the highest card to win the trick... BUT if two high cards match, the next highest card triumphs! Play your cards carefully and try to outwit your opponents in this fun card game that's totally roarsome!

—description from the publisher

Bid Millions of dollars on hilariously bad investments and hope for the worst.  if you win the bid, flip the Profit of Loss coin to see if the invisible hands of the free market will leave your investments a success or failure.

Lose the bid and you'll have to take your chances to see if you'll strike it Rich or be in Dire Straits.  Losing money never felt so good!

SPACE GITS is a dexterity-based miniatures skirmish game about drunk-ass space orcs lootin', shootin' and scootin'.

You play as a gang of drunk-ass space orcs rioting through the megacity of Gnork. You are out for a thumpin' good time, and to loot whatever isn’t nailed down.

SPACE GITS features a host of unique and innovative dexterity mechanics, supports 2-5 players and plays out in under an hour. It welcomes and encourages kitbashing, and can be played as a drinking game. It includes rules for drunken orc-on-orc combat, a full-on campaign system, and vehicle rules. (Planet Smasher Games does not condone drink-driving, except in 28mm scale). It is extremely fun.

—description from the publisher

Geomandering is a strategic, print-and-play board game where rival wizards reshape the world of Terralithia to gain control over the populace. At the heart of the game is a tug-of-war for influence — each player aims to outmaneuver the other by manipulating influence across a political landscape that can be reshaped by casting various Geomantic spells.

Gameplay unfolds across two areas: a central map, where players place Influence to control areas and a personal Wizard tome, were players will learn to harness and improve their spell casting prowess.

Each turn, players draft dice from a shared pool and spend them to cast spells from their Wizard’s Tome. These spells allow you to place influence, reshape the geography or enhance your own spellcasting engine. As you draw connections in your tome, you unlock stronger spells and multipliers that let you act more efficiently over time.

The central challenge of Geomandering is balancing immediate tactical moves (like locking down a key region) with long-term planning (building up your tome for powerful combos later). The geography of the board is ever changing through the actions of each wizard. New rivers can be carved, dwellings erected, and lands scorched. Through these actions, regions can change hands multiple times over the course of the game.

Includes solo mode.

Monolith's Call: Arena Pack One is an unofficial, fan-made expansion for Monolith Arena which adds three brand new armies and an optional play mode for Monoliths.  Command Elemental creatures with the Primordial Kin, otherworldly crystals with Stallos Enclave or trans-dimensional creatures with the Aberrant Legion.

Spawn fearsome insects with Apex Hive, marshal your non-human legions with Southlands Coalition and consume the battlefield with the monstrous Quivering Mass.

Includes 105 army tiles, 38 tokens, 4 Reference Sheets and one Booklet with strategies and examples.

Monolith Arena is required to play this product.

Draw chips from a bag and move forward on the path to catch the bear. But if you draw the wrong chips, the bear will move instead... and catch YOU!

A lil' game of cooperation and action!

---

Tire des jetons et avance sur le chemin qui te mène à l'ours. Mais si tu tombes sur les mauvais jetons, c'est l'ours qui risque d'avancer... et de t'attraper !

Un p'tit jeu de coopération et d'action !

This expansion adds two more Guardians to the core game, plus Dante himself. It includes 3 miniatures, 2 large cards and new cardboard components: 9 Bells and 8 Desks. It Includes a QR with the rules in 11 languages.

Plutus: Form an alliance with the god of wealth, and he will allow you to set up customs offices in Hell to charge passage fees to your rivals.
Place one of your two Desks below any shield. Every time a player passes through, they must pay you one drachma.

Phlegyas: Summon him by ringing a Bell. Phlegyas the ferryman will navigate out of the muddy waters of the River Styx and come to your aid.
Ring one of your Bells and place Phlegyas on a circle. Before playing your Hell phase, you may move the soul laterally to any empty shield in that circle before making it descend to a lower level.

Dante: Replace the wooden meeple with this miniature.

(Both these guardians were part of the Deluxe Edition of inferno.)

boardgame: The Coming Wave: Storm Over The Yellow Sea
description:
Storm over the Yellow Sea is the first installment of The Coming Wave series, enveloping potential conflicts around the area of Yellow Sea, Sea of Japan, Korean Peninsula and Japanese islands.

The Coming Wave series are a simulation system that portrays hypothetical conflicts in the near-future. The system focuses on modern and next-generation warfare, where the tempo and lethality of modern warfare are supplemented by a wide array of additional layers: from electronic warfare and cyber warfare, all the way to space warfare. Challenging the players to make decision to utilize all of their assets to the fullest in combined air-land-naval warfare.

The game covers multiple potential conflicts: from a naval-air conflict between China and the Korea-US alliance, to an attempt of Japan to reclaim the long-disputed territory from Russia. There are scenarios for one-on-one battles between two belligerent powers, and also full conflict between multi-country alliances. In these scenarios, the game allows players to command the most advanced assets within the region: China’s J-20, U.S.’s F-35, Russia’s Su-57, Japan’s ASM-3 armed F-2, and Korea’s Sejong the Great class to name a few. Challenging the players to conduct combined arms operation with their air, naval, ground elements to the fullest effect and outwit their opponents.

The game includes 7 scenarios, with 5 single-map scenarios, 1 double-map scenario and 1 full 3-mapper. Portraying a story arc where a surprise preemptive attack by South Korea sparked the start of a full regional conflict which eventually draws in all major power around.

Components:
Three 841x594mm Maps
One 420x280mm Korea overlay Map
One Decision Book (Rulebook)
One Game Book
One Scenario Book
One Chart and Table Manual
One pad of fleet display
139 Airfield, port and command display cards
100 command point markers (Acrylic cubes)
180 plastic cube markers
1116 counters (486 18mm, 630 15mm)
Four Player aid cards
Three 10-sided dice

Quest in a Box is a lighthearted, fast-paced storytelling game where imagination is your greatest weapon and laughter is your ultimate reward.

In this whimsical, cooperative tabletop experience, 2 to 8 players take on the roles of quirky characters with unique talents, quirks, and catchphrases. Together, your party must overcome bizarre challenges and complete a randomly generated quest, all while staying in character and keeping the laughs coming.

Whether you're battling a sandwich-stealing goblin, delivering a letter to a dragon, or trying to convince a wizard to try therapy, every game is wildly different and endlessly replayable.

What Makes It Special?

     No Winners, No Losers. Just Laughs: It's all about collaborative storytelling and having fun with your friends and family.
     Perfect for All Ages: Designed for players 9 and up, with just the right mix of cleverness for adults and simplicity for kids.
     Quick to Learn, Always Fresh: Set up in minutes, play in under an hour, and never have the same adventure twice.
     Independently Made in the U.S.A.: Lovingly crafted by indie creator Luis, every copy is handmade and assembled right here in the United States.
     Kickstarter-Exclusive Bonuses: Backers get special content you won’t find in any future retail edition, including extra characters and bonus quest cards.


In Quest in a Box, players complete the quest by working together to overcome challenges using their characters' unique quirks, talents, and a bit of roleplaying flair. Each round, a player is chosen as the Hero and must respond to a Challenge Card in character. After acting out their response, they roll a die to determine success or failure.

Other players vote on the Hero’s performance, awarding Roleplay Points that can unlock bonus abilities like rerolls or character swaps.

The team wins by completing 5 challenges before they fail 3. If they fail 3 challenges, the quest ends in a humorous disaster and the game is lost.

There are no pawns or movement—this is a storytelling card game focused on imagination, interaction, and laughs.

—description from the designer

Move one tile at a time, play power cards and challenge your opponents in olympian duels.
In this Odyssey where mythology and handicap meet, count on the unsuspected talents of five gods like no others.

The first player to reach the top of Olympus wins.

The game is accessible to people with prehensile troubles, colour-blind people and people with "dis-" troubles.

Three funny games to learn the colours with colourful bears and perls. 

     collecting perls: each player is trying to be first player who has collected one perl of each (5x) colour by rolling the colour dice.
     coloured bears: each player is trying to be first player who has collected the coloured perls in the defined order on the board by rolling the colour dice.
     Random-Bears: each player is trying to be first player who has collected one perl of each (5x) colour by rolling the regular dice and moving the mentioned steps on the board.

Theme
This game is a Russian version of Monopoly, created and published in Leningrad in 1988.
Players become investors who buy shops and develop them. The cash flow in the game consists of taxes (for players staying on others' possessions) and events from "Chance" cards (fines, taxes, misfortunes). In a difficult situation, players can pledge their properties – otherwise, the player bankrupts and loses the game.

Goal
The goal of the game is to be the last player remaining with any money – after everyone else's bankruptcy or after established time is over.

Gameplay
Game mechanics is almost exactly the same like in Monopoly.

Each turn, the player rolls two dice, moves around the board and buys shops grouped by their products.
1) If a player lands on a new shop, he can buy it or allow the bank to auction it to other players.
2) If he lands on someone's shop, he must pay its owner a rent (proportional to the value of the land and its buildings).
3) If the property belongs to the player and he has all the shops for this product, he can build houses or buy cars which increase rent for visitors.
There are also other places on the board that you can't buy, but they require the player to draw a "Chance" card, pay taxes, collect income, and even go to jail. Each player also has regular income everytime they pass the starting field.

This game is an abstract placement game in which at least 2 players take turns to place diamonds on a pattern. The aim is to reproduce the shapes shown on their task cards. However, the player only completes his task if another player does not beat him and places the last diamond on the given pattern. This keeps the game exciting until the very end.

The creative edition for all fans of versatile Scattergories-games. Every hobby drawer will get worked up by 23x creative and funny categories. This time it's not only about finding a word in the category with the mentioned letters, you also must be able to draw it.
Every Player gets a page of the big log and places it in front of him.  The innovative letter-bar helps for finding a letter to be played. Then the players try to fill out their log as fast as possible.

This is a continuation of the world-famous party game Drinkopoly. It is a social and interactive board game designed to provide hours of fun, laughs and mischief at home, parties or outings.

The board has 40 fields with various tasks requiring you to answer questions, perform tasks and finish sentences. The winner is the one who traverses through the social challenges and reaches the end of the journey first.

When it's your turn, you roll the dice and move the corresponding number of spaces forward. When you pull a card, you have to complete the task or take a sip of a drink to advance further. Some of the tasks are funny, some are sexy and some are simply craaazy! You can be sure whichever card you pick, you will remember it (or not).

boardgame: Escape Game: Die Abenteuer des Sir Pascal
description:
A game with cards and many puzzles waiting to be solved so that Sir Pascal can win the knights tournament.

Just like in a real Escape Room! A journey through history; full of puzzles that help develop logical skills, mathematical thinking, observation skills and language. The cards hide useful clues to continue the adventure and win the tournament. The game includes a set of puzzle cards, a game board and useful items to find the clues.

boardgame: Escape Game: Schatzsuche im alten Ägypten
description:
You learned during your adventures that there is a treasure of the first pharao which is hidden in a secret pyramid. Therefore, you go to Alexandria to find more information.

A game with cards and many puzzles waiting to be solved so that you can find the treasure of the first pharao - just like in a real Escape Room! A journey through history; full of puzzles that help develop logical skills, mathematical thinking, observation skills and language. The cards hide useful clues to continue the adventure and find the treasure. The game includes a set of puzzle cards, a game board and useful items to find the clues.

The legendary secrets of the breathtaking city of Berlin are finally to be uncovered. For this, you are needed as an investigator! Explore every corner of the mysterious city and solve the numerous tricky puzzles and secrets you encounter along the way. Only in this way will there be an escape at the end to pass the exciting adventure.

The game includes a set of puzzle cards, a game board and useful items to find the clues.

It's neck or nothing (es geht um die Wurst). Learn more about German/Austrian sausages.
In this running game you have to collect the four different sausage types of one row (horizontal, vertical, diagonal) which are mentioned in the finish area (Wursthimmel) sausage heaven in clouds.
Within this game you can throw out the other players. Some fields will guide you on fast lane, some will advise you to draw a card to follow the instruction, some will bring you back to the starting point. 
Once one of your four meeple comes to the end of the board, you have to show the sausage card for the field of cloud this meeple wants to be placed. If you can not show a correct sausage card, you have to wait in the waiting area, where you can exchange cards with other players.

The Palatine slang word Uffbasse! has the meaning of “Watch out!”.
Uffbasse! is a trick-taking card game in which the players take on the role of chefs. They play to gain ingredients and use them to “cook” original Palatinate dishes. The chef who has cooked the most stars after four rounds becomes the new Palatinate Star Chef!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
You have just arrived at the library "Benjamin Franklin" to find some information about history. But the librarian gives you a suspicious look.... Does he want to hide something?

As in an actual escape room, you are locked in the library and have to find the exit!

The game includes a set of puzzle cards, a game board and useful items to find the clues.

Welcome to BombArctic, the competition that every polar champion dreams of winning by reaching the top of the iceberg before the others. To do this, they will have to be cunning to deceive their opponents... and avoid falling!

At the beginning of their turn, the player reveals a Bet card with different Paths, with various positive and negative consequences. The player chooses one of the accessible Paths (not all of them are accessible, if you don't have any fish to pay for the passage, for example...), while their opponents try to guess the choosen Path.

If the player manages to trick their opponents, they
move forward on the Iceberg board, in addition to any other effects indicated on the Bet card, and may then decide to reveal a new Bet card. On the other hand, if their opponents intercept him, it's a failure: he applies the consequences and their turn ends immediately. As soon as a player reaches the top of the Iceberg, they win the game!

...a few games later... Maybe will you use unique Champion abilities and Polar Patrol tiles?

With games lasting around twenty minutes and all along played simultaneously, BombArctic offers an experience in which anticipation is paramount and plot twists are always possible!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Junior Escape Game: Die Insel der Piraten
description:
A game with cards and many puzzles waiting to be solved so that you can uncover all mysteries of pirate island - just like in a real Escape Room! 
A journey through history; full of puzzles that help develop logical skills, mathematical thinking, observation skills and language. The cards hide useful clues to continue the adventure and exit the island. The game includes a set of puzzle cards, a game board and useful items to find the clues.

boardgame: Escape Game: Die Prüfung im Parthenon
description:
The legendary secrets of the breathtaking Parthenon in Athens are finally to be uncovered. For this, you are needed as an investigator! Explore every corner of the mysterious building and solve the numerous tricky puzzles and secrets you encounter along the way. Only in this way will there be an escape at the end to pass the exciting adventure.

The game includes a set of puzzle cards, a game board and useful items to find the clues.

In this educational board game developed by the Danish United Nations Association you have to setup two to four teams (max 4 persons per team) and each team tries to solve their global goals of their mission at first. 
The questions are coming from the supporting App. In each round there is only one question to be answered by each team, selected by the position of team meeple on the board. Before you ask the app for question you can move by rolling the dice.

It's princess party time, the music starts and all guest want to be immediately at the dance floor. 
In your turn you try to place one figure on the unstable dance floor. If no figure was falling down within your turn, the next player has to place a figure until all 10 figures are placed. If a figure will fall down, the other player wins immediately.

Dracula has died! Roam Dracula's castle as a vampire socialite trying to win Dracula's eccentric curios.

This is a light auction game for families and friend groups that like a little foul play in their games.

Each turn you will move into a new room, place a bid token facedown, and perform an action. At the end of the game, reveal all the bid tokens to see who won each curio.

The player with the best collection wins.

Who among us didn't play knight when we were little? Become a brave swordsman, a mysterious witch or a skillful robber! Never mind, if you've grown up a bit, now you can go back to the world of childhood fantasy and become a knight (and maybe a bit of a rascal).

Choose a valiant child hero equipped with a wooden sword and a shield made of pot lid and measure your strength against others. But you must fight with discretion.

Every attack, cover or lunge costs you as much strength as in a real duel. When you run out of breath, there's nothing to do but rest. The important thing is right timing. Conserve yourself, combine your fighting moves cleverly and knock your opponent down before he does.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

This is a quiz game with 66 questions and multiple choice answers about the Bavarian dialekt. 
The one who answers first 5 questions correctly wins the game.

This is a fast reaction game! Put the poster with the 24 Pippi images in the middle of the table. Shuffle the 24 cards and put 12 of them face down on the table. The start player takes a card and looks at it. He does not show it to the other players, but give them some hints on what he sees. The other players try to guess the right image on the poster by putting their hand on the image. Every player has to guess another image. 
The player who guesses the correct image gets the card. When all 12 cards have been played, the player with the most cards wins.

Players take turns placing their game pieces onto the grid. The rules are simple - Pieces of the same color MUST touch, and pieces of the same shape must NOT touch. Whoever places the most pieces onto the grid wins the game. Or, whoever has the most open pegs surrounding their game pieces wins the game!

Located in one of the most biodiverse regions in the world, the Giant Panda National Park safeguards over 70% of the country's wild pandas. However, the animals here are also in crisis. They are trapped on "islands" in fragmented habitats, and some small populations may even be at risk of extinction! Take on the role of the park's animal conservationists, creating and linking habitats for rare animals (Giant Pandas, Musk Deer, Snow Leopards, Eurasian Otters), stitching together stunning landscapes, protecting biodiversity, and completing your unique national park!

In this game, each player act as a conservationist for four different animals. Players play square tiles of four different habitats in turn to create a wonderful park. After placing the tile, players may score points if a tile is surrounded by four tiles and put the animal meeple they protect on that tile. When a player's tile or meeple library is empty, the game will end after one more round. The player who has the most points wins!

—description from the publisher

A funny, accessible and replayable cooperative card game.

1 to 4 players. Play with or without randomness. Print & Play format – download, print, play!

Goal
Clean a zombie-infested house, one room at a time. Each player takes on the role of a survivor with a unique power. Explore, survive encounters, loot helpful items… and try to make it out alive.

Two gameplay modes:
Without randomness – easy, smooth, great for families
With randomness – adds tension, replayability and hilarious chaos!

Game includes:


     54 printable cards (Characters, Items, Rooms, Encounters, Loot…) 
     Full rulebook 
     Player aids
     Cute & retro pixel art style


Bonus:
Fully solo-playable
Great for families, casual gamers, or zombie fans with a twisted sense of humor

—description from the designer

Un jeu de cartes coopératif drôle, accessible et rejouable.

De 1 à 4 joueurs. Avec ou sans hasard, c’est vous qui choisissez ! Format Print & Play – téléchargez, imprimez, jouez !

Le but
Nettoyez une maison infestée de zombies, pièce après pièce. Chaque joueur incarne un personnage unique avec une capacité spéciale. Explorez, survivez aux rencontres, fouillez pour trouver des objets utiles… et tentez d’en ressortir vivant (ou pas trop mordu).

Deux modes de jeu :
Sans hasard : règles simples, parfait pour débuter ou jouer en famille
Avec hasard : pour plus de tension, de rejouabilité… et d’effets absurdes !

Contenu du jeu

     54 cartes à imprimer (Personnages, Objets, Lieux, Rencontres, Loot…)
     Livret de règles complet
     Cartes aide-mémoire
     Illustrations en pixel art fun et rétro


Bonus
Jouable en solo
Adapté aux familles comme aux amateurs de zombies décalés

—description from the designer (French)

A tactical family game for 2 to 5 players!

Players arrive to the Veggie Islands! A resort full of delicious (and not so delicious) vegetables; use your wits and harvest the best veggies! But beware of the always changing value!

Players must administrate their time, cause time is key in this game! The player behind on the time track will take the turn!

On you turn you must do 1 of 4 Actions:


     Explore: Take 1 to 5 Cards from the Deck and/or Display and advance that many spaces on the Time Track.
     Harvest: Play 1 to 5 Cards from your Hand and advance that many spaces on the Time Track. This cards will score for you at the end of the game!
     Move: Discard 1 Card  from your Hand and swap that awake Color Mouse with another awake Mouse in an adjacent space. Then move to the front of the line on the Time Track.
     Take a Nap: Discard 2 Cards of the same color and that Color Mouse takes a nap for the rest of the game! That mouse can't be move for the rest of the game.


The game immediately ends when the Deck runs out or 5 Mice are asleep! Players score the Veggies they Harvested (Cards played in front of them) and the player with the most points wins the game!

Pay Attention! Each Mouse is link to a Veggie and the space where they are shows the value of that Veggie. So the value of the Veggies can change at any moment!

—description from the publisher
