boardgame: Keep the Heroes Out!: Very Jolly Pack of Promos
description:
4 physical promo cards from the 2024 "Keep the Heroes Out - Digital Game" Kickstarter.

Scroll

     Portal
     Ambush 2 into Swords room and Coin


Potion

     Reinforce (Equipment)
     Heal


Beast

     Draw and Set Trap
     Melee Attack and Melee Attack


Equipment

     Move and Portal
     Swarm (Move)

boardgame: Keep the Heroes Out!: The Quijote Promo Pack
description:
Four promo cards available during Essen SPIEL 2024 and in the 2024 "Keep the Heroes Out - Digital Game" Kickstarter.

Scroll

     Portal
     Book, Move and Melee Attack


Potion

     Melee Attack
     Reinforce (Book)


Beast

     Draw, Book and Activate


Equipment

     Defend
     Swarm (Book)

Build the biggest and most charming city. To achieve this, you'll need to choose wisely from the three cards in front of you and complete colorful buildings while collecting as many elements as you can. But be careful—smart strategy and a bit of luck will be key if you decide to take risks! In the end, the player with the most impressive city will claim victory. Ready to build your dream?

How to play:

     Place three cards in the center.
     Choose your favorite one to build your city.
     Complete buildings, collect elements, and… win the game!

With everything on the line, head out on new Special Ops to turn the tide. This new game mode can be played on any board and with any other expansion content to bring your game to the next level.

This modular expansion for Sniper Elite: The Board Game introduces a series of new Special Ops, new equipment, squad upgrades and more that is compatible with the entire Sniper Elite range.

New Sniper Modules give the sniper new tricks such as multi-kill kill shots, his trusty “Good Boy” dog companion, or lots of new Loadout cards include some with particularly potent or versatile affects.

New Defender Modules add tons of variety from Flares, Elite Officer Cards, and a host of “Superiority Cards” that each offer unique approaches to the many maps in the Sniper Elite range. You can even ally yourself with “The Hunter”, a special tenth soldier with heavy armour who is especially skilled at finding and eliminating the sniper.

—description from the publisher

An ambitious operation to end the war, the Axis are launching an experimental super carrier across the Atlantic. Find these plans before it can launch, or infiltrate it to bring down this war machine from within.

This expansion for Sniper Elite: The Board Game introduces two new maps, 2 vehicles with miniatures, new loadout and objective cards, a dozen new tokens and new Solo Mode cards.

Infiltrating the 'Norman Village' map is a real challenge for players as armoured cars patrol the area and crucial intelligence needs to be found to help bring about the downfall of the enemies’ '“Super Carrier”'.

From the wide-open fields surrounding the village to the air ducts, tight corridors, and multiple decks of the Super Carrier map itself, players will find diverse challenges each time they play. New gameplay mechanics from tools such as TNT or Bolt Cutters will be crucial for the sniper’s success while the defenders will be able to utilise reinforced walls and locked doors to turn the ship into a nightmarish maze.

—description from the publisher

Bluestone is played by placing playing stones on a special pentagonal grid marked by "blue stones" on the perimeter. The goal is to connect to as many blue stones as you can using your own playing stones.

BLUESTONE RULES IN BRIEF (rules with detailed examples are available here)

Placement: The first player on turn 1 places a single stone onto any empty intersection. Thereafter, starting with turn 1 for the second player, each player on his/her turn places two stones onto empty intersections, with the following restriction: the second stone may not be placed into a group that contains the first stone. (If it is impossible to place the second stone in a different group, then that stone is forfeited.)

Group score: A group of connected same-color stones has a score equal to the number of blue stones it connects to. (IMPORTANT: The number of stones in the group itself is irrelevant. A group of 20 stones that connects to 5 blue stones scores 5 points; a group of 6 stones that connects to 5 blue stones scores 5 points; etc.)

Capture: If a group is surrounded by an enemy group in a way that makes it impossible for it to connect to at least FOUR blue stones, then that group is immediately captured and its stones are removed from the board.

Winning: Play continues until the board is full or both players pass in succession. At that point, the player with the highest scoring single group wins. If both players' highest scoring groups are equal in score, then the players look to their remaining groups; the player with the highest scoring single group from among these remaining groups wins. If there is still a tie, then players keep comparing their highest scoring groups "down the line" until the tie is broken. (It is impossible for group scores to be tied "all down the line.")

Final Scoring Example: Player A ends the game with groups scoring 10, 8, and 5; Player B ends the game with groups scoring 10, 8, 4, and 4. The players' first-highest and second-highest scoring groups are tied at 10 and 8 respectively. Player A wins the game with his/her third-highest scoring group, since this group scores 5 for A compared to 4 for B. (Note that the total score of all of a player's groups summed together -- in this case, 23 for A and 26 for B -- is irrelevant.)

Board Sizes: The standard Bluestone board has five blue stones per side and 76 playable intersections. For a deeper and longer game, Bluestone can also be played on a board with seven blue stones per side and 141 playable intersections. (The game requires an odd number of blue stones per side, otherwise draws are in principle possible.)

—description from the designer

Türkiye, with its rich historical and cultural heritage, attracts millions of tourists annually. While most visitors know only the world-famous Hagia Sophia Grand Mosque in Istanbul, few realize that multiple structures bearing this sacred name exist throughout the country. "Hagia Sophia" - meaning "Holy Wisdom" - isn't unique to Istanbul. Türkiye is home to several lesser-known Hagia Sophia structures, each with its own historical significance and architectural beauty. These hidden gems represent the country's layered history of faith, conquest, and cultural transformation.

Hagia Sophia is a print and play, roll and write game.  In Hagia Sophia, you step into the role of an Urban Planner competing to impress the Mayor with your city development skills. Just as many discover there's more to Hagia Sophia than Istanbul's grand monument, players discover there's more to urban planning than simple construction. Will you create a balanced city that fulfills your promises, or will your ambitious plans crumble under practical constraints? Can you outplan your opponents to become the Mayor's favored urban visionary?

Your mission is to transform your district by strategically placing essential facilities while navigating opportunities and constraints. Your blueprint must include plans to build schools with roads, hospitals, fund critical allocations, provide free Wi-Fi, and assign Civil Servants to hospitals and schools. Every building needs civilians, and unemployed civilians will cost you points.  Even when construction seems impossible, you must continue developing - building roads that follow the curve dictated by the dice.

Each turn, you'll:


     Roll a pool of dice that includes region dice, promise dice, and a road die 
     Select a region die to claim on your player sheet and draw icons from promise dice onto your city map 
     Connect your city with strategic road or bridge placements 


—description from the designer

Build beautiful palaces in this expert level Euro game. Place your overseers in different areas, but also pay attention to the order for the upcoming rounds. Acquire resources and decorate your buildings to create a masterpiece.

Prestige is a strategic game that offers many different possibilities.  We are in the time of the Rococo. The nobility is vying for the most prestige and we take on the role of lords of the castle. In the course of the game, we want to expand and decorate our small castle. We have 5 instructors at our disposal to do this. We use the instructors to create building contracts and hire ambitious craftsmen who gain experience and improve their skills, which are important for the further expansion of our castle. The craftsmen will be used to carry out different building contracts with varying degrees of efficiency.

The game takes place over 3 years, each with the 4 seasons. The seasons can have an influence on construction and the procurement of resources. For this reason, it is very important to plan your individual actions carefully and to keep a close eye on your fellow players.

In each season, we have various actions available to us by deploying our instructors:
        • Acquire resources from the resource plant
        • Place a building order
        • Buy, sell or exchange resources on the market and buy workers
        • Expand the land
        • Hire a new craftsman
        • Become the starting player for the next round
        
The placement of the instructors also controls the player order in the next season. It can and will happen that a player has several consecutive turns. This allows for many strategies.
        
Only those who cleverly expand their castle, adapt to current events and keep a close eye on their fellow players will win this game.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Rooms: Interiour Design, Home Decoration, Tidying

description:
In Carta Imperia: Rise of Rome, you play the leading Families of the Roman Republic wheeling and dealing with one another to become the most prestigious Family in Rome.  The Family with the most Prestige in the form of ephemeral Dignitas & more permanent Auctoritas at the end of the game wins.

The mechanics of the game are designed to create a bullpit for negotiation, because this game is all about dealmaking.  Almost everything in this game is legitimate currency that you can use to get ahead: spend your Dignitas to buy alliances; sell Governorships for political advantage; bribe lawyers to prosecute your rivals; give Rome’s generals command of your private armies for a cut of the spoils. The only things you can’t sell are Auctoritas, the Legions of Rome and your political Legacy.  The rest is negotiable.

The game is played over 7 Eras and each Era starts by revealing a Mapcard which shows the areas of the Mediterranean that became part of Roman history in that Era.  Players choose 1 or 2 Action Cards which depict what their Family will prioritize in that Era.  Each Action Card is then played out in the numerical order of the card.

—description from the publisher

Foragers is a multi-player game that is won using strategy and cunning to claim resources and trap unwary opponents. Players compete to claim resources on the hex grid and fence in their opponents.

—description from the publisher

During the celebrations of Bastille Day, somehow the dead came back to life. Amidst the chaos, six survivors meet a mysterious character that might have an answer for the zombie outbreak — and perhaps a solution to stop it. In their way stand hordes of the undead, plus a delirious billionaire who sees an opportunity in the end of the world.

PariZ is a new campaign expansion for Zombicide: 2nd Edition that features six new survivors and a brand-new set of Parisian zombies. Set in Paris, the story unfolds through ten missions as new challenges and opportunities arise with each choice players make. Brave the Catacombs brimming with (un)life, make crucial choices using objective cards, explore new game mechanisms, and bring the lights back to the undead city.

Every year, a pack of wolves makes the perilous journey across acres of snow and timberwoods in search of food, shelter and safety. Start as a lean pack and face the daunting voyage filled with threats, tough choices and harsh weather. Co-ordinate who hunts, who leads and protect your pups. The pack must survive!

Wolfpack Echoes is a co-operative survival game for 1-4 players. In the game, you start with a small pack of 4 wolves and traverse various terrains with different challenges and threats. Individual members form a shape that determines whether the pack hunts, moves forward or defends. During the rounds, the wolves will age, reproduce and elect an alpha. Surviving till the last round with certain number of wolves ensures a win for all.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Midnight Sons Affiliation Pack
description:
Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Midnight Sons Affiliation Pack recruits the champions of darkness from across the Marvel Universe to face off against any shadowy foe. With Blade, Moon Knight, Ghost Rider, and Doctor Voodoo, these characters are joined together in this repackaged Affiliation Pack.

Including their thematic miniatures, Character Stat and Team Tactic Cards, this pack includes everything needed to pick up these existing characters in Marvel: Crisis Protocol.

A 2004 abstract game designed by Bill Taylor and João Pedro Neto, inspired by Christian Freeling's Havannah.

The game is played on an hexhex board with alternating sizes 8/9 and where no edge hex has special status, i.e., corner hexes are not treated differently than any other hexes at the edge. That is achieved by modelling the board perimeter as a circle approximation.

—description from the designer

In this delightful game, players compete to complete orders for ice creams, sweets, and cookies. The objective is to be the first to fulfill all 6 dessert orders.

At the start of each turn, a player selects one of their tokens that represents a specific dessert in demand.
The player then rolls a die. The number rolled indicates how many pieces of that particular dessert are needed to fulfill the order.
Next, the player flips over two tiles from a double-faced set of dessert tiles. They must reveal missing or hide surplus desserts so that only the required number of desserts is left of the table as indicated by the die roll.

If the remaining desserts match the number rolled, the player successfully completes the order.

The first player to complete all 6 orders wins the game!

—user summary

Zapraszamy na lody! Albo inne słodkości...

Ola zamawia 2 pączki, a Ala 3 kawałki ciasta. Odwróć kafelki i przygotuj słodycze zgodnie z zamówieniem! W tej grze ćwiczymy pamięć, logiczne myślenie oraz podstawowe działania matematyczne.

—description from the back of the box
family: Series: Dobra gra w dobrej cenie (Egmont Polska)

description:
Banana Magnate is a chill, slightly twisted economic card game for 2–4 players, where you run your own banana plantation… and make sure your rivals regret ever planting anything. Build stuff, upgrade it, harvest your sweet yellow goods, and scrump like there’s no tomorrow.
Oh yeah — and then scrump even more..

What’s inside this fruity madness:

     A banana-oriented economy that’s easy to pick up and oddly satisfying.
     Your very own banana plantation — totally wacky and different every single time.
     12 eccentric characters with wild abilities, all fighting for the title of Banana Magnate.
     Sabotaging your neighbors? 100% socially acceptable here.
     A goofy, cartoonish vibe that makes everything feel light and fun — even when you’re being robbed blind.


“scrump”, in game dev lingo, means sneakily “borrowing” someone’s crops — without asking, without telling, and definitely without giving them back. Ever.

—description from the publisher

Lewis and Clark are tasked not only with exploring America, but with ridding the land of numerous invasive monsters that have appeared.

Corps of Discovery: A Game Set in the World of Manifest Destiny is a co-operative deduction game in which players each take the role of one of the crew on the expedition and set out to explore the land. The game board allows for different maps to be inserted into it, so you have many adventures ahead of you.

In addition to finding and killing monsters, you must also complete numerous daily challenges that require specific resources that you can find on the board. You have to use logic and deduction to reason out where the resources you need are located. Ally yourselves with Sacajawea and the indigenous people of the area to help you on your quest.

The game comes with two chapters: Fauna and Flora. Each has new mechanisms, a different goal, and new components to give each chapter a different feel.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

boardgame: Clue: Advent Calendar – Secrets Under Snowfall
description:
Six guests arrive at Tudor Mansion in the dead of winter. They expect a sparkling winter party but receive a cold welcome from host Boddy Black, who threatens to expose their secrets. By the end of the night, Black is dead and everyone’s a suspect.

Families can piece together what happened in the Clue Advent Calendar: Secrets Under Snowfall! The mystery unfolds over 24 days. Each day of this countdown calendar reveals a fresh puzzle to solve - and more of the story.

Solve the puzzle to find out which door to open next. Then use the collected clues to deduce WHO killed Black, WHERE, and with WHAT! Advent calendars and family games are fun holiday gifts for kids and adults, and this 2025 Clue advent calendar is a great gift for families who enjoy playing family board games together!

Difficulty: 3/5

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)
Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

boardgame: Bonaparte: le wargame – jeu de bataille avec figurines pour la période 1792-1802
description:
Focusing on the early days of the man who would become master of Europe, this wargame explores the tactical and structural revolutions of the late 18th century.

Bonaparte allows two or more players to recreate the battles of the revolutionary period, from skirmishes to major battles, embodying iconic generals, with their strengths and weaknesses.

In this book you will find:

     The rules of the game
     A selection of scenarios
     Army lists
     Useful tips


—description from the publisher

Axé sur les débuts de celui qui deviendra maître de l’Europe, ce wargame se propose de mettre en jeu les révolutions tactiques et structurelles de la fin du XVIIIe siècle.

Bonaparte permet à deux joueurs ou plus de reconstituer les combats de la période révolutionnaire, allant de l’escarmouche à la grande bataille, en incarnant des généraux emblématiques, avec leurs atouts et leurs faiblesses.

Dans ce livre vous trouverez :

     Les règles du jeu
     Une sélection de scénarios
     Des listes d’armées
     Des conseils utiles


—description from the publisher (French)

A NSFW expansion pack for the game Lineup. Featuring 50 double-sided cards that include 100 more questions about adult topics (and a few OSHA violations, to truly make it 'not safe for work'). Intended for use with the Lineup base game's other components.

—description from the publisher

In this cooperative game, players battle a nameless Darkness consuming the world by telling each other uplifting Stories. Each player picks a hero class (druid, warrior, etc.) and receives a starting hand of Class, Character, and Study cards as their "knapsack." Over four rounds, Darkness cards are drawn followed by Story cards used to fight against them, which must be completed by players adding Trait, Light, Skill, Activity and Ability cards that match the Story's requirements, while also trying to level up their characters by acquiring skill tokens and Treasure cards.
On the fourth round comes the End Battle, in which all Stories must be finished to defeat the Darkness.

In this 18-card connection game, players draw from a deck featuring origami animals (fox, swan, giraffe, elephant, frog, lion), endeavoring to make six-in-a-row of one type and laying claim to cards so that other players can't move them.

Embark on a legendary adventure with Legends of Enderas, the latest 5-in-1 Variety Expansion! Add 120 brand-new cards to your game -- including new traits, ages, and mechanics that unlock new strategies and chaos! Mix and match it with other expansions--like the original Mythlings, Techlings and Dinolings from our Upgrade Pack and Deluxe Bundle -- for more strategic possibilities!

Legends of Enderas includes:


     Treasures Expansion (26 Card Expansion) - The Treasures expansion adds a new level of strategy to Doomlings. Each round, draw a treasure card that allows you to play a special ability!                  
     Mythlings Expansion (27 Card Expansion) - A collection of Doomlings known for their mischievous tricks! Watch out for spells that cast enchanting effects when discarded.
     Techlings Expansion (27 Card Expansion) - Doomlings from a bygone era, known for their helpful innovations and long-lost attachment ability. Techlings thrive on support, enhancing and strengthening other Doomlings.
     Dinolings Expansion (27 Card Expansion) - The gentle giants of the Legends of Enderas collection, these Doomlings can help you score big points, but can also easily make other traits go extinct from trait piles. 
     Fuzelings Expansion (13 Card Expansion) - Fuzelings combine the art and mechanics of Mythlings, Techlings, and Dinolings. They can help you get bigger bonuses from Drop of Life effects and trigger new combos!
     1 Mystery Holofoil Collectible Card from the Legends of Enderas Collection


—description from the publisher

Players pick a Voodoo Mambo as their character and a set of three zombie romance themed dice, then each player draws a card from the zombie deck. The goal is to collect 5 zombies by matching the icons on the cards, either from the deck or stealing a card from another player.

In this cookie-themed game, players take turns drawing one card from a 36-card cookie deck, and choosing one to play-either face down to hide its type, or face up to activate its special ability. If a matching cookie is drawn, the player must discard them. When the deck is exhausted, the player who has the most cookie cards in front of them wins.

On a 16-space, diamond-shaped grid cards are arranged, each featuring several train tracks, with the goal being that players take turns rearranging and rotating the cards to create one, continuous track from the Council Bluffs station to the Sacramento station.

VARZ TCG is a tactical trading card game set in a modern world where urban legends come to life. The game is played with custom card decks, a hex-based battlefield map, and tokens used to track life, damage, and positioning. It is primarily designed for two players, though alternate formats allow up to six.

In a standard match, each player brings a Hero, a 30-card Main Deck, a 9-card Skill Deck, and two Portal Decks placed in the opponent’s territory. At the start of the game, players set up the hex-grid battlefield, place their Hero, and position their Portals. Players then take turns drawing cards, summoning spirits known as Spills, casting Commands and Slashes, and moving units across the map.

Each turn focuses on gaining control of territory, defeating enemy Spills, defending your own Portals, and attacking your opponent. Positioning is critical, and many effects are triggered through movement, targeting, or timing. A player can win by either reducing one of the opponent’s two Portal Decks to zero cards, or by bringing the opponent’s Life to zero.

The game features six elemental factions, each with unique mechanics and synergies. As cards are revealed and abilities are activated, the battlefield shifts dramatically, leading to unexpected combos and comebacks. A typical game lasts 20 to 120 minutes, with each match telling a unique tale of spirits and strategy.

The game is scheduled for release in 2025.
The story world behind the game has already been introduced through the live-action drama series VARZ, now publicly released.

—description from the designer

Conquerors and Conquests is a dice rolling game where you will compete to solve quests, battle monsters, and even fight other players. Cast spells and move around the game board to become the next champion of the land.

With a starting hand of spell cards you can upgrade throughout gameplay, each player becomes more powerful, giving more opportunity to battle stronger monsters and complete harder quests. Make each game unique by choosing a different hero with special abilities, different starting spells, and even change the game board by rearranging the tiles that make up the play space.

Conquerors and Conquests is a Dungeons and Dragons campaign without the roleplaying, choices only being able to be made from the spell cards in front of you and the items in your hand, entirely played in 90-120 minutes. The pixel art on each card brings an element of 1990s video games to life, bringing old arcade games and dungeon crawlers to your tabletop.

—description from the publisher

In Gymkhana x Girona, players must chart the best route to visit as many neighbourhoods as possible and buy postcards. You'll need to manage your money and energy wisely, use power cards strategically, and prevent other players from obstructing your progress.
This game is designed to introduce Girona in a fun and educational way, sui-table for both locals and visitors curious about the city

—description from the designer

Con Gimcana X Girona, tendremos que hacer la mejor ruta para visitar más barrios y comprar postales. Deberemos administrar nuestro dinero, gestionar la energía para ir más lejos, usar las cartas de poder en el momento adecuado y evitar que el resto de participantes nos aparten de nuestro camino.
Este juego quiere presentar Girona de una manera divertida y didáctica, tanto a quienes viven allí como a las personas que tienen curiosidad por ella.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

Amb Gimcana x Girona haurem de fer la millor ruta per visitar com més barris millor i comprar-hi postals. Caldrà administrar els nostres diners, gestionar l’energia per arribar més lluny, utilitzar les cartes de poder en el moment adequat i evitar que la resta de participants ens apartin del nostre camí.
Aquest joc vol donar a conèixer Girona de manera divertida i didàctica tant als qui hi viuen com a la gent de fora que sent curiositat per la ciutat.

—description from the designer (Catalan)
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Mind Worlds is a Card Game in which you desperately try to gain back control over your Mind.

Using Thoughts, your goal is to reach the highest number of Influence before the clock runs out.

In Conquest For The Capital, 4 asymmetric factions fight to control the capital city and its sorcerous throne. 
For eons, the human sorcerer-kings have banished you from your homeland. But with the death of the last sorcerer-king, you now return to reclaim these ancient lands. 
You have only five turns before neighbouring kingdoms intervene—or a new bloodline of sorcerers rises. It’s up to you to lead your faction to supremacy.

Play as one of these 4 factions, the Reckless Orcs, Cunning Elves, Fortifying Dwarves, and Inevitable Undead in a deathmatch against the Humans and each other.
With simultaneous turns and hidden movement, you must use your cunning and strategy to be the first to Conquer The Capital!

boardgame: ワードジャンクション (Word Junction)
description:
In this game, each player writes their set (four-letter hiragana) on the play area to create as many words as possible.
Words can be created not only from top to bottom or left to right, but also from bottom to top or right to left. (It is surprisingly difficult to create words in the reverse reading order.)
The first and last letters of a word can be used multiple times, so use this to your advantage as you progress through the game.
You can earn points based on the number of letters in the word, the Shiritori Bonus, and junctions, and the player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner.

—description from the designer

このゲームでは、各プレイヤーの作るセット（4文字のひらがな）をプレイエリアに書き込んで、多くのワードを作ることを目指します。
ワードは、上から下・左から右の読みだけではなく、下から上・右から左の読みでも作ることができます。（通常とは逆の読み順で作るのは意外と難しいものです。）
ワードの最初と最後の文字は複数回使用することができるので、これを活かしてゲームを進めましょう。
ワードの文字数、しりとりボーナス、ジャンクションから得点を得ることができ、ゲーム終了時、最も得点が高いプレイヤーが勝者です。

—description from the designer (Japanese)

Set in the heart of a Louisiana bayou, this game invites players to compete for fame by fulfilling music label contracts.
To earn points, you'll need to collect the right instruments — but there's a catch: you’ll get them by skillfully tossing frogs onto lily pads!

Simple to learn, full of chaotic fun, and bursting with swampy charm, this is a game where rhythm, aim, and a bit of luck come together for a hopping good time.

—description from the designer

Landis is a cooperative space adventure where teamwork, precision, and daring landings will decide your place in the stars. As members of an interstellar crew, your mission is to journey across distant planets, performing perfect descents to build your legend among the galaxies.

Captain Laika must check each crew member’s battery level before choosing the next mission. Based on your available energy, she’ll decide which challenge to take on.

Now, it’s all about precision: travel between planets without overshooting or falling short — or risk damaging the ship.

Plan your routes carefully, manage your energy wisely, and adapt as a team to each mission’s demands.

Complete as many successful landings as possible to earn your place in history as the galaxy’s greatest crew.

—description from the designer

Periodic table game

Who will be the first to put all their elements in the right places and master the periodic table?

The game is for the whole family and you don't need to know the periodic table before you start. But we promise you'll be both entertained and much smarter along the way.

Can be played in three ways, as a quick card game, as a cooperative game and as a competition.

In the midst of the French Revolution, the government was organized around the Committee of Public Safety, the Committee of General Security, and the Revolutionary Tribunal. But the parliamentarians of the Mountain, the Plain, and the Gironde were politically divided and threatened by military interventions from European monarchies. Fear of a return to the ancien régime led to a hunt for anti-revolutionaries.

Terror is a card-based programming game with effects and initiative, worker placement, and resource management. Earn the most victory points by contributing to the war effort, gaining seats on the Committee of Public Safety, obtaining majorities in Parliament, and condemning anti-revolutionaries at the Tribunal. Be careful not to end up on the scaffold yourself.


En pleine Révolution Française, le gouvernement s’organise autour du Comité de Salut Public, du Comité de Sûreté Générale et du Tribunal Révolutionnaire.Mais les parlementaires de la Montagne, de la Plaine et de la Gironde se déchirent politiquement et sont menacés par les interventions militaires des monarchies européennes. La peur du retour à l’ancien régime entraîne une chasse aux antirévolutionnaires.

Terreur est un jeu de programmation de cartes à effets et à initiative avec de la pose d’ouvrier et de la gestion de ressources. Obtenez le plus de points de victoire en contribuant à l’effort de guerre, en gagnant des places au Comité de Salut Public, en obtenant des majorités au Parlement et en condamnant des antirévolutionnaires au Tribunal. Attention à ne pas vous retrouver vous-même sur l’échafaud.

—description from the publisher

At this luxurious doggy motel, your pup is in for the adventure of a lifetime!
Far from a simple vacation, this stay is all about exploration, friendship, and discovery.

Your dog will sniff out hidden treasures, forge bonds with fellow furry guests, and uncover the secrets of the motel’s lush surroundings. Each day brings new surprises and playful challenges that will test their curiosity and courage.

Only the most adventurous and clever dogs will rise to fame — will your pup shine as the top scouter of the motel?

—description from the designer

Bring out your inner coach and take charge of your team in this football board game.

Dominate the game with a strong defence or dictate your pace with an unstoppable attack.

Will you control possession or surprise with a lethal counterattack?

With each play, the tension will grow... the last play can decide everything!

Demonstrate your mastery of total football, impose your style and lead your team to glory.

—description from the designer

Matica Quest is a fantasy themed card game where players complete quests by solving math equations with addition, subtraction, multiplication and division. Play digit cards from your hand to complete the quests, or action cards to mess with your opponents. Class cards give additional powers, and also allow for better competition between players of different ages or math abilities.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Field of Bees, the buzzing new card game that lets you experience the world of these essential pollinators. Your mission is to explore the wildflower field, collecting different flowers to lure endangered bee populations to your bee sanctuary. As the game unfolds, you'll need to employ a blend of memory and tactical decision-making to gather the right flower combinations, redeem bee cards, and use their special abilities.

Honey bees are often the first bee we think of when we hear the phrase, “Save the Bees”. However, native bees may be threatened by habitat loss, pathogens, and perhaps managed bees such as Honey Bees. (Worldwide, the use of managed bees for crop pollination and honey production has increased dramatically.) After playing this game, research these flowers and native bees. Then consider planting one of these flowers in your own garden if it supports your region’s ecosystem.

Here are a few resources to start with: XERCES SOCIETY for Invertebrate Conservation, WWF, IUNC Redlist

Overview
At a high level, during the game players will flip then collect flower cards from the wildflower field. Players will need to remember which cards were flipped by other players, kept or remain in the flower field as new flowers are added to the field in order to strategically collect flower cards. (If you struggle with memory games, or are tight on space, Field of Bees offers a simple drafting structure as well!) Matching flower cards can be redeemed to collect bees. Collected bees can be saved in a player's sanctuary or garden to utilize an action. (Each bee type has a special ability that can be played if a bee is in a garden. Once the action is played, the bee is then saved in the player's sanctuary.) The game ends when the first player or the group has collected the designated number of bees for the chosen gameplay. The various gameplay modes are outlined in the field guide, along with guidelines around flipping flowers, trading flowers, collecting bees, luring bees, dangerous knapweeds and more!

—description from the designer

Tokyo Ghoul District Battle is a frenetic and asymmetrical dice game.

Choose a character and compete by frantically rolling your dice. Certain results will grant you special powers that inflict damage on your opponents.


     Each player chooses a Character board and a corresponding Ally tile and places it in front of them.
     Each player takes 1 black and white die and 4 colored dice.
     Everyone plays at the same time without waiting for a turn to be the first to roll the required faces for one of their abilities. Each roll, players can roll all or some of their dice, as often as necessary.
     Once a player rolls the 5 symbols corresponding to their Power tile, they must hit the tile in the center of the table with their hand. All players must then hit this tile as quickly as possible... the slowest player takes damage! 5. When a power is activated, players suffer injuries... When all the injury slots on your tile are filled, you faint! If this happens, don't panic: just pull off a special combo at the back of your tile to heal yourself and rejoin the battle!
     The game ends when all but one player is knocked out or eliminated. The last character standing wins!


—description from the publisher

In Adrinkras, players must create a beautiful fabric using Adinkra symbols, connecting them to score as efficiently as possible through matching pairs, triplets, quadruplets, and quintuplets. However, the placement of each tile is crucial in this fun and tactical game.

—description from the designer

Em Adrinkras os jogadores devem produzir um lindo tecido com os símbolos Adinkras, conectando cada um deles para pontuar da melhor forma possível, através de pares, trincas, quadras e quinas iguais, porém o posicionamento de cada peça é fundamental nesse jogo tático e muito divertido.

—description from the designer (Portuguese)

Welcome to Small Adventure, a dungeon exploration game inspired by the classic role-playing game quests. Whether solo or with friends, explore the depths of a sinister dungeon or a mysterious forest as you search for treasure and face off against dangerous foes!

Between your adventures, take a break at the tavern and enjoy traditional card games like Canasta, Poker, Truco, and Pife — a unique feature of Small Adventure is that its decks can also be used to play these games! Pack your adventure bag, summon your courage, and let's dive together into this epic journey.

—description from the designer

In Elch Dorado ("Elk Dorado"), the 6 rolled dice indicate how many Elk Pesos the player who played the highest bid card on that die receives. It's extremely important that identical values ​​cancel each other out.
The Elk and the Owl play a major role. If you capture the Elk along the way, you can increase the value of one of your bid cards, potentially transforming a 6-value card into an unassailable 7. And the Owl allows you to reroll the dice and thus increase the value of the loot.
There are also a black and a white dice as an extension. With the black dice, everything is different: While you only achieve something if you alone played the highest card, now the rule is: Two identical bid cards score points! However, with the black dice, you lose points, and the affected players must pay the corresponding number of Elk Pesos. This is a source of joy – but for the others.
And the white dice ensures that not just one player gets something, but two.
The few rules are quickly understood and many actions provide surprises and lots of fun.

—description from the publisher

Bei Elch Dorado zeigen die 6 geworfenen Würfel, wie viele Elch Peso derjenige erhält, der zu diesem Würfel die höchste Bietkarte gelegt hat. Dabei ist enorm wichtig, dass gleiche Werte sich auspatten.

Eine große Rolle spielen Elch und Eule. Wer nebenbei den Elch erobert, kann damit den Wert einer seiner Bietkarten wertvoller machen, möglicherweise eine 6er Karte in eine unangreifbare 7 umwandeln. Und die Eule erlaubt, den Würfel neu zu werfen und so den Wert der Beute zu steigern.

Dazu kommen als Erweiterung ein schwarzer und ein weißer Würfel. Beim schwarzen ist alles anders: Während man eben nur dann etwas erreicht, wenn man alleine die höchste Karte gespielt hat, gilt nun: Zwei gleiche Bietkarten bringen Punkte! Allerdings sind es beim schwarzen Würfel Minuspunkte, die betroffenen müssen entsprechend viele Elch Pesos abgeben. Da kommt Freude auf –  aber bei den anderen.

Und der weiße Würfel sorgt dafür, dass nicht nur ein Spieler etwas bekommt, sondern gleich zwei.

Die wenigen Regeln sind schnell verstanden und viele Aktionen sorgen für Überraschungen und viel Spaß.

—description from the publisher German)

boardgame: Puppet Master: Return to Bodega Bay Inn
description:
When you and your friends decided to spend the night in this old hotel, you had no idea about the murders that had taken place years prior, and you certainly never could have imagined the animated puppets that caused it all. But you did accidently find the trunk that imprisoned those puppets for all these years, and you just couldn’t help yourself when you pried it open. The puppets are now trying to complete their dead master’s work by bringing his soul back, and all the puppets need are a few key items and an unwilling victim to be the body donor.

Puppet Master: Return to Bodega Bay Inn, is a cooperative, cat-and-mouse-style, survival game for one to four players, that uses Hand Management and Deck Hunting to influence its claustrophobic, skirmish gameplay. Explore the hotel as a modular game board you design each time, so every game is different. Each puppet has a unique personality and the “Puppet Behavior Dial” changes their strategy throughout the game. Having the right cards in your hand and a few doors strategically locked might just give you the time you need to escape but it’s a race against your quickly dwindling Deck to collect the information you need to stop the puppets before the puppets complete their agenda.

—description from the designer

Santa isn't the only one sneaking around this Christmas...

Get ready for holiday mayhem with Really Bad Santa, the hilarious dexterity game from Right Wrong Games! It brings the same addictive speed and laughter as our best-selling game, Tiger Banana Pancake — now wrapped in pure Christmas cheer!

Players race to act out silly Christmas-themed movements as fast as they can. But watch out! Santa’s brought some imposters to not only ruin Christmas, but to throw you off your game! Be the first to slap them and shout 'Bad Santa!' before anybody else. 

Catch the Imposters, watch out for the Snowman, and don’t end up with a pile of Christmas cards — or you'll be on the naughty list!

Really Bad Santa is perfect for lively holiday parties and makes an ideal stocking stuffer. But be warned: only the quickest will survive!

Puutarha (Garden) is an educational board game about gardening, recommended for ages 7+. The game teaches you real information that can also be applied to gardening. You will learn to identify different flowers, plants, herbs and root vegetables. The tools in the game are real tools used in traditional gardening.

To succeed in the game, you need a little luck and a keen eye. Your task is to cultivate a garden plot, grow succulent root vegetables, sweet berries, fine herbs, tasty apples and beautiful flowers. Also remember to preserve your products according to the annual cycle. The player who collects the highest score based on the plants grown and the preserved harvest wins the game.

At the heart of Hive Fissilicus, the citizens of Volkus prepare for an event of ritual significance – the firing of the Great Gun. The macabre Goremongers, followers of Khorne intent on sabotage, rise from the depths of the hive on a tide of blood and violence. Opposing them, zealous Sanctifiers seek to defend the holy weapon in the name of the God-Emperor. At stake is the fate of the Great Gun and Volkus itself.

This expansion includes two multipart plastic kill teams, an imposing piece of shrine scenery, rules, datacards, and accessories for your games of Kill Team. It continues the ongoing narrative on Volkus, and introduces fresh ways to play, more operatives, and strategies – it's an ideal way to add even more variety to your games.

This boxed set contains:


     56-page softcover Blood and Zeal Dossier: Background, artwork, and rules for the two kill teams in the box, plus an overview of the ongoing conflict on the planet Volkus.
     Sanctifiers Kill Team: A complete kill team of 11 multipart plastic miniatures, consisting of 1x Confessor, 1x Cherub, 1x Death Cult Assassin, 1x Salvationist, 1x Miraculist, 2x Missionaries, 1x Drill Abbot, 1x Persecutor (which can alternatively be built as a Preacher), 1x Conflagrator (which can alternatively be built as a Preacher), and 1x Reliquant (which can alternatively be built as a Preacher).
     Goremongers Kill Team: A complete kill team of 8 multipart plastic miniatures, consisting of 1x Blood Herald, 2x Aspirants, as well as 1x Bloodtaker, 1x Impaler, 1x Skullclaimer, 1x Stalker, and 1x Inciter (all of which except the Blood Herald can alternatively be built as Aspirants).
     Killzone Upgrade: Shrine Statue: A terrain feature designed to be used with the rules in the book.
     Sanctifiers Datacards: 37 datacards covering operative and faction rules, abilities, ploys, and equipment.
     Goremongers Datacards: 40 datacards covering operative and faction rules, abilities, ploys, and equipment.
     Blood and Zeal Card Pack: 32 cards are included in the book for use with the Blood and Zeal Mission Pack.
     Token Sheets: One double-sided cardboard token sheet, containing 38x Goremongers tokens, and 39x Sanctifiers tokens.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Friends: The Television Series – Trivia Game
description:
Friends Board Game! How Much Do You Know About Them?

Show how much you know about the Friends series with this incredible trivia game!
Answer questions from 4 categories, face the lightning round, and accumulate points to win the department. A game full of laughter and memories!
Features:


     Questions based on the iconic Friends series.
     Team mode to share with friends.
     Various categories and a lightning round to test your memory. Recommended age: 14+.


The ideal game for Friends fans and unforgettable nights!

Seafood Chain is a trick-taking game about food-chain and survival-of-the-fittest in the sea.
Seafood Chain has no suits, no trumps, no special cards, no biddings and no minus points, but food-chain.
As usual trick-taking game, each player play a card from his/her hand, but following players can’t play a card with the same number as any card played in the trick. If you don't have a playable card, you must reveal your hand and the round ends immediately.
If all cards played in that trick form a serial number and you played the last number of the serial number, you win 3 victory points.
If all cards played in that trick don't form a serial number and you played the biggest number in the trick, you win 1 victory point.
The player with the biggest victory point total wins.

—description from the designer

Collect Emotions and Win: Be the first to collect 3 sets of Emotion cards or 6 Anxiety cards to claim victory and complete your emotional journey.
Strategic Action Cards: Play Action cards to outsmart opponents, shift your strategy, or disrupt the game with twists at every turn.
Pixar’s Anxiety Figure Power-Up: The player with the most Anxiety cards gains control of the Anxiety mini figure, unlocking extra card draws and unique gameplay advantages.

Monsters & Mandates is a 3-in-1 expansion. 
Each expansion adds new mechanics you can play separately or combine together for the ultimate experience!


     Monsters introduces new Demigod cards and unique monsters cards that have invaded your Landscapes. Drive them off or defeat them for a powerful boon! 
     In Mandates, Hades is jealous of Zeus' control over you and demands you meet his mandate. Push your luck to complete his request for a reward, or suffer his wrath if you fall short.
    Influence of the Immortals - Use your god's unique power to intervene directly

boardgame: MutantSlayers: Post-apocalyptic skirmish game in wrecked lands
description:
Create your Warband and hunt the mutants, fighting them in epic battles to gain fame. Accumulate experience, and get new weapons, equipment, and skills. Fight against other warbands to become the strongest of them all.

MutantSlayers is a skirmish game for two players, set in a post-apocalyptic world devastated by a great cataclysm that caused catastrophic earthquakes. From chasms created in the ground, large quantities of a deep green Gas, containing a deadly virus, emerged. Anyone who inhaled it turned into a mutant, with terrifying features and sprinkled with spores that spread the same Gas, which has now also become a valuable source of energy for the survivors. In MutantSlayers, you will form a Warband of 5 models and clash with your opponent in epic battles, where the mutants will be a constant and active presence, increasing to the level of hazard throughout the game. You will have to retrieve Gas from the mutants' bodies to survive, avoiding attacks, hiding from their sight, or moving silently, while always remaining alert to your opponent. The mutants are divided into three categories, each with different methods of activation and attack, offering an intense challenge. The game includes a campaign system in which the player decides how to specialize each model according to the fame gained in battle and equips them according to their specialty. Your Warband will be customized. Furthermore, the higher the experience level of your Warband, the more dangerous the mutants you will have to face. In addition, the rulebook includes rules for playing solo, with six complete scenarios, one cooperative scenario, and one for multiplayer play.

This book contains the game's rules, useful resources, 6 detailed scenarios, and a campaign system that allows you to create engaging narrative matches.  
It includes rules for playing solo with six complete scenarios,  which can be played individually or in a campaign. 
Additionally, you will find a scenario for cooperative and multiplayer games.

Main Features:

     Agnostic miniatures game: you can use any miniature for both the warbands (no. 5) and the mutants (minimum no. 9).
     Rules for playing solo with 6 scenarios.
     One cooperative scenario and multiplayer.
     Simple and customized creation of Warband statistics.
     Four factions with their abilities.
     Profiles and levels of mutants with varying abilities and characteristics.
     Quick matches and setup.
     Small game table: 2' x 2' ( 60 x 60 cm).
     Campaign system that allows you to customize each model.
     6 complete scenarios to play in narrative campaign mode or individually.
     Counters, tokens, and game resources are included in the rulebook, which you can reproduce and print out.


—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Have you ever wanted to explore the jungle and discover what’s hidden deep inside? Picture yourself photographing the exotic animals of the Amazon Rainforest, from colorful parrots to carnivorous piranhas.

Now’s your chance to step into the role of a true explorer—charting paths through untouched land, setting up camps in places no one else has reached yet, and immersing yourself in the raw, breathtaking beauty of the Amazon Rainforest. But this journey isn’t just about discovery—it’s about making a difference.

You’ll be part of vital research in the world’s largest and most complex ecosystem, collecting data that could help protect endangered species and preserve fragile environments. Every sample you gather, every observation you make, adds to the bigger picture and could push you closer to becoming the most influential researcher of the entire expedition. Leave your mark as the most important researcher of the expedition.

One Great Expedition is an exploration board game where you will place camps in various, uncharted parts of the Amazon Rainforest. Discover various animals and set your camps in smart positions, before other explorers beat you to it.

—description from the publisher

The object of Brain Tame is to form words from the letters on the cards.  It uses the rummy mechanic of drawing a card from the draw or discard pile, making and adding to words from the hand, and discarding a card, with a round ending when a player empties their hand.

Distinctive features include: the deck includes cards for word endings "ed", "er", and "ing", as well as all alphabet letters and wild cards; the dealer chooses how many cards to deal per player (5+ recommended); words of even 1 letter (A, I) are allowed; the art reflects the company Lily Frilly's target market of young girls.

When scoring words at the end of a round, most cards are 10 points each; X, Y, Z are 20 each; ER, ED, ING are 15 points each; Wilds are 50 points. There is no penalty for cards left in the hand. The winner is the first to reach the goal total agreed on at the start; recommended is 250-300 for kids, 350-500 for teens and adults.

Notorious Pirates is a 2-4 player game that pits players against each other. The game is played in two phases with three rounds in each phase. The first phase is called Buccaneer's Bones. Each player secretly rolls their own set of dice. In turn order players make a bid on the dice they think are under everyone's cup. The bids are increased as players take their turns. Once a player thinks the previous bidder has overbid their hand, they call the bidder a cheat and a liar. The dice are then revealed to determine the winner of that round. The winner gains reputation and the Pirate Booty. The goal of this phase is to arm your fleet of four ships the best you can for the second phase of the game.

The second phase of the game called Battle on the High Sea is where players battle it out to determine who is the most Notorious Pirate. Players take turns either ramming or launching their cannons at the other players fleets. The game features a deck of historical pirate captains to help players along the way. There is also a deck of pirate cards allow players to cheat, trick or steal their way to victory. The unique replay ability of the game is nearly limitless even though the goals are the same. As players get to know the Notorious Pirates board game, they will be able to capitalize on the many strategies built into the game to help them win it.

The winner is determined by who has the most reputation at the end of the third round of Battle on the High Sea. Reputation is gained in both phases of the game. 

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dice Cup / Dice Tower / Dice Tray

description:
Not A Real-Word Game is a victorless game of making and using hyper-pleas-ify-ing words.

In just a few minutes, you and your fellow neo-log-ists, will have created 15 or so of, your very own neo-log-isms to add to the Not A Real-Word Dictionary!

Flippable domino-like cards allow you to choose which Word Particle(s) to align with word(s) already in the Play Area. Once you have built your word, read it out loud, then tell everyone what your word means.

If you're stuck, no worries, hot potato your deck to a neighbor. There's no need for stress, this isn't your typical word game with a need to know-it-all.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Battles for the Ardennes: Deluxe Edition
description:
Battles for the Ardennes simulates the battles that occurred in the Ardennes area in World War II with new artwork and mounted map boards. Individual combat units represent between 1,000 and 7,000 men and supporting weapons. Armored units represent between 40 to 180 tanks or Panzers. Artillery units represent between 60 and 100 guns or rocket projectors.

Eight Scenarios:
St. Vith: 6th Panzer Army Attack, 16 Dec.–21 Dec. 1944
Clervaux: Breakout 5th Panzer Army, 16 Dec.–21 Dec. 1944
Celles: Battle Before the Meuse, 23 Dec.–26 Dec. 1944
Sedan 1940: Across the Meuse, 11 May–15 May 1940
Blitzkrieg to the Meuse: 10 May–15 May 1940
The Battle of the Bulge: 16 Dec. 1944–2 Jan. 1945
The Opening Guns: 16 Dec.–20 Dec. 1944
22 December 1944: 22 Dec.–25 Dec. 1944

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Basic Training: ASL Starter Kit Magazine
description:
Basic Training: Issue One is a support magazine for the Advanced Squad Leader Starter Kit series. It contains 48 pages with several articles, 12 new scenarios for ASLSK, 3 player aid cards, and a half countersheet with 90 half inch counters and 32 5/8th inch counters.

Clarification on S115 Task Force Sparks: The German Balance should be - Add a PSK to the German OB.

Articles include:
-Guides to Learning SK has tips for learning SK from Craig Renier.
-S2 War of the Rats Scenario Replay by Andrew Maly, Greg Schmittgens, and Perry Cocke
-In Behind the Numbers Andrew Maly looks at some statistics on dice rolls from the replay.
-Run Away! is a quick primer on routing from Ken Dunn.
-Basic Rout Training by Neal Ulen and Stephen Stewart provides some good options when routing.
-In Two Halves Make a Hole Vincent Maresca gives tactical tips on using half squads.
-If … Then … Elst gives a tactical perspective on Decision at Elst by Neal Ulen and Scott Rowland.
-Solo ASL Starter Kit provides some tips by Mark Stevens on playing SK by yourself.
family: Series: Advanced Squad Leader Starter Kit

description:
The Clown Gang (Gangue do Palhaço) is an urban legend involving a clown and a ballerina in a van who kidnapped people and terrorized the greater São Paulo area in the 1990s. The city is in panic and the chase will be frantic. The police need to catch the criminals, protect the people and unmask the gang.

Step into the shoes of the protagonists of this urban legend: play as a fearless police officer and her partner, who seek to save lives and capture criminals, or take on the role of the legendary gang and sow chaos throughout the city.

—description from the publisher

Vapor Willie is a clever and colorful river adventure game where players compete to become the most skilled steamboat captain on the waterways. Sail between islands and ports, gather quirky supplies like hay, barrels, ducks, and goats, trade them for sailor’s whistles, and complete personal objectives—all while racing to be the first to reach the end of the river.

But the river isn’t calm: each island holds surprises, resources are scarce, and the weather can change your fortune. You'll collect and manage supplies, expand the river, and dock at ports to trigger special events or open up wild player-to-player negotiations. Use your whistles wisely—they’re both currency for movement and points at the end!

Choose from four beloved characters (Mouse, Rat, Captain, and Parrot), each with their own one-time powers like free movement, bonus draws, or port privileges. Extend the river to new tiles and seize opportunities before your rivals beat you to them. Just don’t forget to complete your objectives—or risk a point penalty.

With charming artwork, tight resource management, strategic movement, and fast-paced interaction, Vapor Willie is perfect for fans of tactical games that combine planning, negotiation, and a dash of chaos on the open river.

Strategy, tactics, daring and luck are the right elements to save the children, or capture them. Get ready for the chase!
 Véio do Saco is a versatile game and, if played by 2, 3 or 4 people, each person has the opportunity to play a double journey: take on the role of a child trying to escape from the Old Man and also play as the Old Man himself, trying to catch the children.

In each game, excitement is guaranteed with the traps spread throughout the city. Collect sweets and treats, earn stars and avoid having your child captured. If you prefer, reverse the game, quickly capture the little ones, taking them to the hiding place. In both situations, the participants can win the game! And for those who prefer group play, the cooperative mode allows the children to defeat the villain together!

—description from the publisher

The music game that will jog your memory and make everyone sing along!

Are you ready to face the biggest musical challenge you'll find today? Toca Raul is the perfect game for those who love music, enjoy having fun with friends and have a Raulzito in their heart! Here, it's not enough to know who sings or the name of the song – you have to have strategy and courage to shout out loud: Toca Raul!

The expression “Toca Raul” began to be used in bars and concerts throughout Brazil, especially in the 80s and 90s, as an irreverent request from the audience to play Raul Seixas' songs, regardless of the artist or the context of the performance. Nowadays, Toca Raul is much more than a request or a game: it's a celebration of music and pop culture in the best "Maluco Beleza" style.

—description from the publisher

In the city of Mariana, Minas Gerais, in the early hours of Good Friday, five minutes before the beginning of Holy Saturday, a pagan ritual takes place. People dress in white, hiding their faces under a hood, while carrying a bone and a candle, singing a song and praying for the souls of people who died without confession.

At the front, one person carries a cross covered by a black veil, and others carry chains, skulls, a wicker basket and bird feathers, to recreate two iconic stories from the region.

Some say that there is always a dead person accompanying the procession.

The procession leaves from the Archconfraternity of Saint Francis and the Church of Saint Francis of Assisi, in the so-called Procession of Souls.

—description from the publisher

October 31, 1987, the night the legend awoke… Are you ready to face the Boogeyman?

A group of kids wearing Halloween masks decide to carry out small acts of vandalism in a competition between the neighborhood gangs for the title of Master of Mischief. However, they end up discovering, in the worst possible way, that the Boogeyman, a being that feeds on disobedient children, is not just a legend!

—description from the publisher

Crisis is a card-based party game set in a fictional, unstable world where nations are constantly pushed to the brink of collapse. Instead of competing for dominance, players lead countries desperately trying to survive waves of unpredictable global and local crises. The setting is grim and dramatic, yet the tone of gameplay remains light and accessible, focusing on clever interaction and tactical card play.

The game supports 2 to 4 players, each managing their own country represented by population and territory cards. Players draw from a shared event deck, which contains both useful cards and dangerous Crisis cards. Crisis cards hinder players and stack up, triggering negative effects like losing population or territory. Players use action cards to sabotage others, protect themselves, or manipulate the flow of crises—often forcing opponents to take on crises instead. Strategic card management is essential, as many actions require discarding cards with specific symbols or types to activate powerful effects.

The objective is simple: be the last player with any population and territory cards remaining. If a player loses all of either, they are eliminated. Success requires careful hand management, clever use of action cards, and knowing when to press your luck.

It's a Zoo is a 20-minute strategy card game for 2-4 players, ages 8 and up.

Players will act as zookeepers organizing animals in a zoo while managing cards in their hands. At the end of the game, players will evaluate the zoo to find the largest grouping of each animal type. Those with the most cards of the matching animal types in their hand will score points for those animals in the zoo.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Battles of the American Civil War August 1862 to April 1865: A Solitaire Wargame Expansion
description:
This is an Expansion of my original game called, “Battles of the American Civil War, June 1861 to July 1862: A Solitaire Wargame”. You need that original game in order to play the Battles in this Expansion because that book contains the rules.

The American Civil War was a conflict between the United States (the Union or the North) and the eleven states that had seceded from the union and formed the Confederate States of America (the Confederacy or the South). It lasted from 1861 to 1865. One of the key issues fought over was slavery and the power of the federal government to prohibit it in certain territories. Abraham Lincoln, the first Republican president, promised to keep slavery out of the territories, which was seen as hostile by the South. At the time, the North had well-established industries, but in the South there was far more reliance on large-scale farming, which itself relied on enslaved people. War began in 1861 when Confederate forces opened fire on Fort Sumter in Charleston, and ended in 1865 when the Confederates surrendered.

In the game, the Player commands various units, often representing many hundreds or thousands of men each, seeking to achieve a wide variety of Objectives. The original game included Battles from the years 1861 and 1862.

This Expansion plays in exactly the same way as the original game and adds Battles from 1862 to 1865. This book is not therefore a standalone game and you need the original rules (and preferably the Counters) to play it.

This game is a solitaire wargame. You play the game, and the enemy is controlled by the game. You will need a good handful of standard six-sided dice to play (nine is ideal, although fewer will do).

It is highly recommended that the counters provided on the final page of the original game are used to play this game. Simply stick them to card and cut them out. A video demonstrating how generally to do this can be found on the Mike Lambo Solitaire Book Games YouTube channel.

On the same channel you will find an instructional playthrough video for the original game, which walks players through the rules of the game. The best way to learn the game is to read through the rules and then watch that video.

20 full colour Battle Maps are included in this Expansion.

—description from the publisher

The game is played by two players with three dice. The movement points earned with one throw can, at the player's discretion, be distributed either:

     entirely to the ball alone, or
     entirely to the team alone, or
     arbitrarily between the ball and team. However, the ball always moves the assigned points ahead of the team.


Type of movement of the ball: All points marked by circles on all lines count as movement points for the ball.
Type of movement of the team: Only the intersection points of all lines count as movement points for the team.

The ball is stopped by one player of the opposing team and therefore cannot pass over them. The ball can pass over a player of the own team, in which case the point occupied by that player is counted as zero. If the ball is fixed between two players of your own team in such a way that the opponent can only move the ball on the line between these two players, the opponent must transfer the entire score of his throw to his team. The team that fixed the ball must transfer the entire score of his throw to the ball after fixing it, thus releasing the ball. However, if the ball is fixed between two opponents, the fixing team must release the ball on the next throw. 
Goal scoring: The goalkeeper keeps all shots under 12 points. A goal is scored when the ball lands behind the goal line instead of on it with 12 or more points. The losing team takes the kickoff. The goalkeeper may not leave the goal area.
The game ends after an agreed on time. The player who scored the most goals wins the game.

—user summary

Make like a mycelium and grow in unique geometric patterns, connect with both resources and fellow mycelial networks, and react to unexpected challenges and constantly changing conditions with one goal in mind: sporing to survive.

Shroomscape is a strategic board game for 4 players where each player takes on the role of a different species of mushroom, represented by a growing mycelial network. The goal is to grow your network across a forest board, collect resources, sprout fruiting bodies, and release spores — all while responding to unpredictable events that can shift the balance of the game.

Each mushroom species has its own strengths and limitations. Players grow by placing pieces in specific geometric patterns, aiming to reach resource pieces and trigger actions. The game includes both competitive and cooperative elements — sometimes it’s in your interest to help another player, and sometimes it’s better to block them.

Gameplay involves piece placement, pattern building, and managing limited actions each turn. Random events introduced through spore releases create unpredictable shifts, forcing players to adapt and rethink their strategies. The game ends when a set number of spores have been released wins.

—description from the publisher

Ten new tenants for your High Rise building w/ teal letters EXP in the upper right corner:

Contents:

10 Tenant Tiles
- Upscale Organic SuperMarket - Downtown (teal)
- Movie Production Company - City Center (red)
- Federal Investigative Office - City Center (red)
- Megachurch - Downtown (teal)
- Building Inspector (#23) - Bayside Heights (beige)
- Publishing House (#24) - Harbourside (gray)
- Private Space Program (#25) - East Gardens (green)
- Mutual Fund Firm (#26) - Bayside Heights (beige)
- Office of Tourism (#27) - East Gardens (green)
- Vaguely Named Charity (#28) - Harbourside (gray)

24 Tenant Cards
- 4x Building Inspector (#23) - Bayside Heights (beige)
- 4x Publishing House (#24) - Harbourside (gray)
- 4x Private Space Program (#25) - East Gardens (green)
- 4x Mutual Fund Firm (#26) - Bayside Heights (beige)
- 4x Office of Tourism (#27) - East Gardens (green)
- 4x Vaguely Named Charity (#28) - Harbourside (gray)

1 Extra Tenants Appendix (that explains 9 of the 10 tenants but leaves out Movie Production Company)

A co-op game for 2 to 4 players.
Your task is to drive out the ghosts that haunt the Abbey through a complex Alchemical Ritual.
Explore the interior and exterior of the Abbey, find the Alchemical Elements and combine them with the help of Manuscripts. Avoid the ghosts in a frantic race against time: will you be able to drive away the spectres before their terrifying manifestations drive you to panic or madness?

In qualità di eroi appartenenti alla Lega degli Alchimisti, il vostro compito è scacciare i fantasmi che infestano l’Abbazia tramite un complesso Rituale Alchemico. Ognuno dei 4 Fantasmi Principali, per essere scacciato, richiede una combinazione di 5 Carte Elemento Alchemico con lo stesso simbolo (Acqua, Aria, Terra, Fuoco) ma di tipologia diversa.

I giocatori devono quindi raccogliere le Carte Elemento Alchemico e scambiarsele tra loro fino a creare le combinazioni giuste e poi correre nell'Area infestata da ciascun Fantasma Principale e sconfiggerlo.

Ogni giocatore al suo turno ha la possibilità di compiere 3 azioni a scelta tra:
- muoversi da un'Area ad un'altra Area adiacente;
- "passare a" o "ricevere da" un altro giocatore presente nella stessa Area una Carta Elemento Alchemico purché essa sia dello stesso simbolo dell'Area in cui si trovano i giocatori;
- scacciare un Fantasma (se possiede le Carte richieste ed è nell'Area infestata dal Fantasma);
- scartare una Carta Elemento Alchemico per spostarsi immediatamente in un'Area caratterizzata dal simbolo della Carta scartata;
- calmare un altro giocatore facendogli calare di un punto o la Follia o la Paura.

In certe Aree dell'Abbazia sono consentite anche azioni speciali come:
- Pregare per abbassare la propria Paura;
- Lavorare la terra per abbassare la propria Follia (cioè per tornare...con i piedi per terra!);
- Prelevare un Manoscritto (i Manoscritti sono degli aiuti fondamentali per semplificare la dura vita degli Eroi).

Infine, ciascun Eroe ha anche a disposizione un'azione speciale tipica del proprio personaggio.

Compiute le 3 azioni, il turno del giocatore si conclude pescando 2 nuove Carte Elemento Alchemico e scoprendo un numero di Carte Attacco Fantasma pari al numero di Fantasmi ancora in gioco diviso per 2 arrotondato per eccesso. Le Carte Attacco Fantasma sono le manifestazioni terrificanti dei Fantasmi nelle varie Aree dell'Abbazia: tutti i giocatori coinvolti in un Attacco Fantasma subiscono un incremento nella Paura e/o nella Follia come indicato sulla Carta stessa.

Se anche un solo Eroe supera il punteggio massimo consentito di Paura o di Follia, egli scappa terrorizzato determinando l'immeditata sconfitta di tutti i giocatori.

Se i giocatori terminano uno dei due mazzi Carte Elemento Alchemico o Carte Attacco Fantasma prima di aver sconfitto i 4 Fantasmi Principali, essi perdono la partita.

Se, invece, vengono sconfitti tutti 4 i Fantasmi Principali, i giocatori vincono.

Nel mazzo delle carte Elemento Alchemico sono nascosti 4 Fantasmi Secondari la cui uscita innescherà una accelerazione nel tempo di gioco e una ulteriore difficoltà crescente per i giocatori. La sconfitta dei Fantasmi Secondari, sebbene non obbligatoria, fornisce dei Punti Vittoria aggiuntivi.

Happy DayZ is a trick-taking game in which you play Communities trying to survive in a world overrun by Zombies. Win tricks with Survivors to trigger their powers and grow your Community. Leave strategic tricks for your opponents to avoid taking damage from Zombies!

The originality of Happy DayZ lies in its board: if the trick is played on the + side, the highest card value wins. If played on the - side, the lowest card wins. It’s up to you to play your cards in the right spots to gain the advantage! The Community with the most Survivors at the end wins the game.

Play a single round for quick games, or use campaign mode to play for 4 rounds or until any Community loses its last Survivor.

In Woodstock Fever, you play with 6 factions: Hippie, Dog, Children, Scientist, Preacher and Military !

American Dream & Woodstock Fever are compatible standalone!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Dragon's Tweak: A Game of Strategic Summoning
description:
In a fractured realm of floating hexes and flickering magic, only the cunning will survive — and only one will tame the Dragon Ruler.

The Dragon’s Tweak is a modular strategy game for 2–4 players that blends tactical movement, resource management, and dynamic elemental combat. Each player commands a pair of dragon summoners, vying to gather powerful eggs, control the field, and ultimately challenge the mighty ruler at the heart of the realm.


     Hex-Based Battlefield – Build the arena from elemental hexes, each with unique effects  
     Summon and Sacrifice – Play dragons from hand, pay with eggs or burn older allies to power stronger ones  
     Color Shifts Everything – Battles and token pickups depend on the ever-changing elemental color wheel  
     Duels of Wits and Power – Engage in direct confrontations where timing and color dominance decide the victor  
     Portals, Movement Boosts, and Tactical Turns – Every tile is a potential opportunity or trap


With every turn, you'll face choices: advance toward the center or fortify your forces? Block your rival or bait them into overextending? And when the moment is right — will you strike for the throne?

The Dragon’s Tweak is a tense, ever-evolving contest of positioning, bluffing, and clever combos. Only the most adaptive summoner can rule the dragons.

—description from the designer

Recórcholis is a fast-paced card game in which players try to be the first to get rid of their face-down deck of cards. Players take turns playing a card from their deck openly onto a pile, making the sound indicated on the card.
However, if the sound is the same as the previous player's, they must invent a different one.

When a player plays a Recórcholis card, everyone must immediately put a hand on the table.

Whoever makes a mistake or is the last to put their hand down takes the entire deck of cards.

Some special cards add a special touch to the game.

Recorcholis is the reimplementation of ¡Caiaaate!, the game by Gianina Della Gaspera and Emanuel Sánchez, with a new comic-style aesthetic and updated rules.

YOUR MISSION:
SCAN THE SECTORS OF THE GALAXY TO LOCATE AND DESTROY ALL ENEMY SHIPS

You may well be an ace pilot but this mission could be your most difficult one yet. As if duelling it out with alien ships wasn't enough, you'll also need to negotiate dangerous asteroid fields. Your ship's shields will only protect you up to a point, although you can always attempt to land on one of the friendlier planets for repairs.
BEST OF LUCK ACE!

Completing your mission will indeed require a fair bit of luck - but also some strategic choices and forward planning, based on your dice rolls, to work out how best to locate the enemy fighters, land on planets and steer clear of asteroids.

The game consists of a single card with the rules printed on the back.

You'll also need 4 dice and a dry erase pen to cross out the enemy ships when you destroy them.

—description from the designer

A single card game where 2 to 4 wordsmiths try to make a word from their 5 letters generated by The Letterator.

TO START place the Letterator so that each player is facing one of its quarters.

ON EACH TURN the active player selects a letter from the quarter facing them and writes it down, announcing which section of the quarter it is in (e.g. ‘red’, ‘yellow left’, ‘purple centre’). The other players locate the letter in the corresponding section of their quarter and write it down.

The active player then rotates The Letterator. The player on their left becomes the new active player and a new turn begins.

THE ROUND ENDS after 5 turns. Players then make a single word using their 5 letters, scoring points for the letters used in the word and bonus points for a 5-letter word.

THE GAME ENDS after 4 rounds (3 for a 3-player game). Each player then creates a grammatically correct sentence that includes all of the words they've made, reads it aloud and announces their total points. The player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Age of Steam Deluxe: Expansion Volume V
description:
Seven additional expansion maps for Age of Steam Deluxe.

Expansion Volume V includes:

     Portugal: Madeira & Açores
     Spain & Portugal
     Moon with Satellite  
     Greece 
     Montréal Métro  
     Austria  
     D.C. Metro


The Expansion Volume include updated versions of classic Age of Steam maps adapted to the new Deluxe art style. These maps cover a wide range of play counts and styles - each with its own unique twists and gameplay. The Expansion Volumes also includes a storage box to store the maps.

--information from publisher

Players become chocolatiers seeking to share truly delicious chocolate with the world by building their very own chocolate brands.

In order to grow your brand, you’ll need to source quality cacao from cacao farmers, develop new confections to sell at events, local markets, and your own brick-and-mortar stores, recruit specialists to help with your operations, collaborate with local confectionery shops, and participate in an annual chocolate contest.

—description from the designer
publishers: analog lunchbox (アナログランチボックス)

description:
The four different "ferry timetables" are thoroughly shuffled and placed face down on the table. The topmost timetable is then placed on the lighthouse (clearly visible to all players). After the enclosed ferry (see ferry map) has been placed at port "1" according to the color shown there, and each player has placed their pieces in the starting position corresponding to their color, the game can begin.
