Now, everyone takes turns rolling the dice. They first turn over the timer located at the lighthouse and simultaneously make their first roll. They then move one of their pieces toward the ferry by the number rolled. Players take turns rolling the die, creating a race between the individual pieces to the ferry. Once the timer runs out, the ferry will immediately depart, even if a player is still on their turn, and head for the next port indicated on the ferry board.
If a player believes they can no longer get their piece to the ferry at port "1" within the current time limit, they should wisely try to immediately move to the next indicated port and wait for the ferry there. However, they may also be "thrown out" by an approaching opponent while waiting. If a player manages to get a piece onto the ferry, they may "deposit" it in the lighthouse, i.e., the piece is thrown into the top of the lighthouse. The first player to deposit all of their pieces in the lighthouse is the winner.

In addition to the large lake ferry, there are two small passenger express ferries on the game board that can be used to quickly cross the lake. One can cross the lake lengthwise from point 3 to 2 (or vice versa) and one can cross the lake crosswise from point 6 to 6.

—user summary

WORDMINGLE is a word puzzle game, which presents you with 10 letters of two words jumbled together, and the object is to determine what the two original words are.  Its contents are: 
- 100 Scrabble-like letter tiles
- 22 tile holders: 10 beige, 6 white, 6 black
- 1 wooden box, with the URL of the site (wordmingle.app) which has the game's instructions, and the generator of the puzzles.

The app's front page lets you choose DAILY for the daily puzzle, RANDOM for a random puzzle, and GUIDE for the rules.  To play, you bring up a daily or random puzzle, and it shows you 10 letters. After putting their tiles into the 10 beige holders, tap on I'M READY, and the solving screen has spaces to say the length of the two words (one black and one white, either 5 and 5, 4 and 6, or 6 and 4), to enter word guesses, and to request a hint. Then you shuffle the letter tiles around until you have one or two words, which you can enter, and the app tells you whether the guess is correct.  To get a hint, you click on a letter and it shows whether it's in the white or the black word, so you can transfer its tile to the holder of its color. You get 1 point for each hint and each incorrect guess, and the object is to have the lowest score when both words have been guessed.

WORDMINGLE is mainly a solitaire game, but it can be played cooperatively by a group, as well as competitively between people with their own tile sets.  You can share the URL for random puzzles so people can compare their efforts to solve the same puzzle.

WORDMINGLE is deliberately designed to use the physical act of moving tiles around, based on research that shows that activity that uses both the mind and the body helps preserve mental acuity more than those that just use the mind alone.

Holds over 1000 sleeved, cards includes "extension board" which makes the game 5-6 players. Also includes 24 exclusive cards.

—description from the publisher

All content from all Boss Monster content up to Super Boss Monster fits in the big box.

—description from the back of the box

The classic "what's on my head" conversational game, themed around Canadian comedy series The Red Green Show.

Your identity is displayed on a card on your head, visible to everyone in the lodge but you. You have to discover who, what, or where you are by asking questions of the other lodge members. The answer's on your head....and everybody knows but you!

Players push their luck on the Great Lakes in a pickup and deliver epic. In this one to five player game, players take on the roll of industrial shipping captains racing throughout the Great Lakes to make money, upgrade ships, and protect investments.

During a player's turn, they must decide whether to make progress in their journey or to remain safely in port. Storms run from West to East across the board, enabling some weather predictability. However, the weather changes faster than the boats move, so an uncalculating captain can easily find their boat in the middle of a lake, with a storm beating down upon it.

It is a game of knowing when to take a chance and when to play it safe. As a boat in port makes no money, risks will need to be taken. Lives will need to be chanced.

The game incorporates the traditional pick-up and deliver system, but with a more modern opportunity for strategy. Contracts are determined by a draft, which allows players to chart an advantageous course. Captains may also hedge their bets by purchasing insurance for a voyage, which cuts painfully into profits, but also covers a replacement vessel if it is sunk.

While profit and competition may be the goal, every captain knows that the weather and the illusion of invulnerability are the only true foes.

—description from the publisher

Topple Tanks is a highly strategic abstract strategy game designed by Neural Forge. Manoeuvre your tanks around the board to line up your shot, topple pillars to cut off escape or crush opposing tanks. Customise your play style by selecting from three tank variants. First to 3 kills wins.

—description from the designer

Take part in more important sieges of the Napoleonic era with this expansion for Siege Works. Take part in battles of:
San Sebastian -  A costly Victory! The heavily fortified city resists British assaults as the war nears its end. With limited time Difficult terrain and fierce resistance. Can you storm the walls and end French control of the city?
Copenhagen -  A bold preemptive strike! The British besiege Denmark’s capital to seize its fleet before it can fall into Napoleon's hands. Can you bombard the city into submission and capture or destroy the Danish fleet?

Will you claim victory, or be forced to abandon the siege in defeat? The fate of the fortress and your army rests in your hands.

—description from the publisher

SMOL TACTICS is a quick tactical cardgame for 2-5 players. The secret? Each card can play on two colors! On the table are three central rows with objectives that everyone plays on in turn. Fulfilled the objective? That stack of cards is for you! But watch out: each card you play can be the perfect setup for the other. 
Do you win the most cards?

With different objectives and a powerful, one-time joker that you must use tactically, it remains exciting until the last card. Perfect for fast, competitive fun.

—description from the designer

You are the commander of a Star Fleet. In this two-player competitive dexterity game, you’ll place your fleet tokens onto a rotating disc—representing the main screen of your command center. The goal is to outmaneuver your opponent so that, when the game ends, your faction’s color dominates the lower of the disc."

A fast moving flashcard game for 2-4 players.

Flashtracks is a fast moving game that provides the right balance between fun and learning. Spin the dial, answer a question correctly, and move your train along the colorful track. The first player to reach the end wins.

boardgame: Impractical Jokers the Game: Box of Challenges II
description:
Box of Challenges 2 has all new cards! Play as a stand alone game set or combine with your first Impractical Jokers Game: Box of Challenges for even more straight-up hilarious and extremely awkward fun!

This game has three phases. First, players go undercover and attempt to pull of challenges in front of unsuspecting guests as home or at the office. Later, all players join in on the fun and face off in a charade-style game to pair the most awkward actions with hilarious scenario cards. And of course, it wouldn't be a Jokers Gamer without the third phase of the game: Punishments.
The goal is to be the first player awarded 5 scenario cards.

—description from the publisher

The Party Game of HALF-GUESSES that's FULL of LAUGHS!

...But That's Only HALF the Story! uses a mechanic seen in games like Eat Poop You Cat and Scrawl, as players have to try to guess what is happening based on pictures provided by previous players.

The twist here is that you are together creating STORIES (not cohesive ones - frankly they're not going to make a helluva lotta sense - but stories nonetheless)!

Players are each handed booklets (that can be easily adapted to player counts from 4-12!), and then are given a story starter to write on the first page (e.g., 'The Mouse began sniffing the cheese in the trap'). They then each turn the page, and draw a picture of what they think should happen NEXT in the story (e.g., a picture of a mouse trying to pull the cheese OUT of the mousetrap). Next, all hand their books to the people clockwise from them, careful to only show the PICTURE that they drew. Players must now attempt to GUESS what is happening in the pictures, and write the description below them. Those players then draw what would happen NEXT in THOSE stories, and so on.

After a set number of pages (and stories that have likely changed plots and characters about seven times!), the stories are collected and read aloud! Laugh as plots that began with UFO's landing in the woods end up with werewolves winning their ballet recitals! Or tales of absent-minded zookeepers ending up starring time-travelling accountants!

...But That's Only HALF the Story!: give it HALF a CHANCE, and you'll DOUBLE-OVER with LAUGHTER!

—description from the publisher
Adds 5 new double sided Guardian cards and 35 new Nawal cards, including all previous promo cards!
Códicus is a fast-paced deduction game for up to 6 players where you race to crack your unique 6-digit code using logic and clever clue-asking. Be the first to solve it all and win!

Arcanite is a tactical card game where your deck is more than just your toolkit. It is also your life. Each time you take damage, you lose cards into the mysterious Void. But the Void is no ordinary discard pile. It becomes a source of energy, recovery, and strategy.

Each card in your hand can be played, consumed as a resource, or held for later. Every turn, you must weigh your options and act with precision. Every card is a decision that could shape the outcome of the match.

With multiple elemental Avatars to choose from and countless ways to build your deck, no two games play the same. Arcanite offers several modes of play, including one-on-one duels, team battles, cooperative challenges, and even a solo mode.

—description from the designer

With the dragons weakened by the Phyrexian invasion of Tarkir, the five clans rebelled, throwing off the yoke of their draconic masters and reclaiming their lost heritages. Now the dragonlords have been defeated, and clans and dragons are working together to build a brighter future for the plane—but danger still brews in the skies, as the dragonstorms are growing in strength, spawning new broods of wild dragons that threaten to overwhelm civilization across Tarkir. Narset, the Waymaster of the Jeskai clan, must join forces with Elspeth Tirel to discover the source of these new storms and restore peace.

Tarkir Dragonstorm features the long-awaited return of the three-color clans previously seen in Khans of Tarkir and Fate Reforged. Each clan has a new mechanic. The Abzan have learned to Endure, which gives a you choice of +1/+1 counters or a creature token. The Jeskai have Flurry, which rewards you when you cast your second spell in a turn. The Sultai have Renew, allowing them to exile creatures from the graveyard to buff up creatures on the battlefield. The Mardu can Mobilize, granting temporary bonus creatures to attack with for a turn. And the Temur have Harmonize, which allows an instant or sorcery spell to be cast from the graveyard, with the option to tap a creature to do it at a discount. Additionally, the dragons have a mechanic of their own as well: new Omen cards give you the choice of a powerful dragon creature or a cheaper instant or sorcery, but if you choose the instant or sorcery mode, the card will shuffle back into your deck, so your opponent will never know when that dragon might be coming back!

Epic Battles Scenario: The military clash between the French forces of King Louis XII, and the Venetian army that shattered the armies of the Republic.

Epic Battles is the only wargaming rulebook that makes it possible to relive all the great battles fought from the dawn of history up to the beginning of the XX century, incorporating the development of tactics and weaponry throughout more than five thousand years of history.
In fact, the tactics the players will need to use to win the game are remarkably well aligned with the historical progress and limitations of the Armies that have written the course of history and with the terrain features of the battlefields on which they fought.
The rules - innovative to the world of wargaming - are intuitive, and easy to learn, understand and play - making any battle both a new and engaging experience.

Epic Battles, one history and one rulebook to play out history!

—description from the publisher
family: History: War of the League of Cambrai

description:
Dare to Ding is an exhilarating game of riddles and quick reflexes, where every turn brings unexpected challenges and fast-paced fun. Roll the die, draw a card, and race to solve riddles before your opponents. Will you be the first to ding and claim victory? Don’t just think it, ding it!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Operation CanCon: Canadian Nineties Music Shopping Gamebook
description:
It's Time to Take "Shopping Local" to the Next Level!

Welcome back to the Nineties. Canada’s music scene is rocking, and the store shelves are packed with winners, if you're ready to roll. In Operation CanCon, you’re not just flipping through dusty bins, you’re chasing priceless cultural artifacts. As a lone collector on a mission, your goal is to target a checklist of tracks scattered in iconic Toronto shops.

Part interactive fiction, part solo roll-and-write game, every visit is a gamble, whether for ten minutes or a campaign. All you need to play is three six-sided dice and something to write with.

Operation CanCon is your passport to an era of gone-but-unforgettable Canadian Content. The music that inspired a generation is ready to rise again, but it needs YOU to bring the decade back to life. Fully steeped in nostalgia, this standalone sequel to the Eighties original lets you relive a time when finding the right album seemed like fate.

—description from the designer

For information on how to play, see the Operation CanCon: Eighties video section at https://boardgamegeek.com/video/557062/operation-cancon-canadian-eighties-music-shopping/operation-cancon-how-to-play

A fast paced card game where competing burrow fight for scraps of food and territory.

Select your favorite kind of badgers and lead them to conquer their favorite food. Play the right badger at the right time to either defeat a competing badger or to strengthen your position.

Use special abilities or tunneling as you chose to conquer the field and gain your meal before the game ends.
Each burrow has unique special abilities and badgers based on real types of badger.

At the end, who owns the most food wins!

—description from the publisher

A Mini expansion for Space Lion: Divide and Conquer. This Solo mode includes an A.I. version of all 5 armies to play against or add into your team game.  Note: This expansion is included in the Deluxe Edition.

Includes:

     5 A.I. Army decks
     Achievements List
     Rules cards
     38 total cards


—description from the publisher

A Mini expansion for Space Lion: Divide and Conquer. These insane Lion cards bring mechanics from one army to another.  Note: This expansion is included in the Deluxe Edition.

Includes:

     10 unique Lions which can be part of two armies. ( You receive 2 copies, each with the army specific card back for a total of 20 cards). 


—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Massive-Verse Fighting Card Game: Team Up!
description:
A lightning quick game of snappy strategy and big reads. Take their HP down to 0 or deliver a carefully placed Execute to take your opponent out in an instant! With this team up box, expand the Massive-Verse Fighting Card Game to intense 4 player action! Synergize with your ally to gain access to powerful team up abilities!

Can be played standalone or combined with any other Massive-verse or Pocket Paragons characters.

Includes

     4 character decks
     30 Teamup cards
     4 Dials


Featuring

     Radiant Red
     Shift
     Radiant Yellow
     Radiant Pink


—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Massive-Verse Fighting Card Game: 5yndicate Promo Pack
description:
A bonus playable character that plays like several characters at once! The 5yndicate are a villainous team that can adjust their tactics on the fly. You can never be fully prepared!  A Kickstarter exclusive - not available in retail.

—description from the publisher

This box features 8 Paragons from previous promotional events. Includes: 6 Pirate themed Paragons from the Six Sided Seas and 2 Bonus characters from the indie video game, A Robot Named Fight!

CONTENTS

     8 Character decks ( Each 8-10 cards)
     10 Dividers


Featuring:

     Mari, the Sea's Charm 
     CaLee, the Swashbuckler
     Noodles, the Brine
     Malakos, the Mad Captain
     Elles Tehra, the Enticer
     Obninsk, the Deepsea Lure
     Fight! #XXXX (Every copy of Fight is uniquely numbered! Every single Fight! is unique!)
     Tutorial Smith


—description from the publisher

boardgame: 7th Sea: City of Five Sails – Fate and Fortune
description:
Fortune only favors fools, and Fate is friend to no one.  Every faction within the City of Five Sails has mustered their forces, laid out their plans, and anxiously cast their die.   All that's left to do is grit your teeth and fight to your last breath.

This 149 card expansion for 7th Sea: City of Five Sails introduces new Leaders for each faction which radically change the way each faction plays.  New Gambling Maneuvers and Gambling Techniques will allow you to press your luck for greater rewards!  Explore new ways to play with the new First Mates play variant.

—description from the designer
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Be Herd is the joyful, cooperative game which brings friends and family together and asks "how strong is your herd?"

Vote on insightful questions about each other and work together to build your 'Herd Tower'. Are you a Flamboyance of Flamingos or a Gaggle of Geese?

—description from the publisher

The Leader of the herc choose a card, then the players secretly vote, then build your tower, but watch out, if the tower is falling then you lose the game!

Luminous is a 2 player collectable card game with tradeable cosmetic elements designed for ages 14+. The game takes approximately 10 minutes to learn and 20-40 minutes to play. Players purchase “faction” decks, which contain a complete set of cards to deckbuild with, allowing them to be competitive right out of the gate without the need for randomized pack hunting. Play orbs to cast spells, relics, and units from your hand to reduce your opponent's life to 0. Sequencing and careful decision making is important to make sure you aren't overwhelmed by your opponent.

—description from the designer

Bandits boarded the 3:15 to Fort Worth. Sheriff Amun Carter has rounded up a posse with two leaders. The leader who best achieves the goals of stopping the Train Robbery in progress will be the new Deputy.

In The Great Train Rescue, players will join opposing posses to save as many passengers as they can from the train scoring for the cards that have been chosen. Each choice will help players score or will set them up for future success. Turn order changes when players choose cards with the lower time value letting that player go first on the following turn.

Players will note the Wanted cards, some of the time they will earn the players more points or at other times earn the players fewer points. Players will also have to consider passenger color, tools, and loot! Each choice of a card will allow the opponent more cards to access and so taking just the right card can really get wild in the old west!

Hop aboard the train and see if you can thwart the robbers in the best ways each round. Cunning and clever play across the 18 cards of this game will challenge the players. Do you have what it takes to be the new Deputy?

In just 18 cards, experience the Wild West as you stop a Great Train Robbery!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Эволюция: Трава и грибы – Промо-набор "Ванильный трюфель" (Evolution: Herbs and Mushrooms – Vanilla Truffle Promo Set)
description:
Промо-набор "Ванильный трюфель" ("Vanilla Truffle Promo Set") was a gift for pre-ordering the expansion Evolution: Herbs and Mushrooms on Crowd Republic.

Vanilla Truffle is both an herb and mushroom. As a herb, it receives a red food token during extinction phase; as mushroom, it can get a red food token from owner of dead animal.

When animal takes red food token from a Vanilla Truffle, owner gets a Nirvana mark.

Player with a Nirvana mark skips turn until end of feeding phase. In extinction phase, player gets rid of a Nirvana mark.

Set includes:

     2 Vanilla Truffle cards
     4 Nirvana effect marks
     rules


•••

Промо-набор "Ванильный трюфель" шёл в подарок за предзаказ дополнения "Эволюция: Трава и грибы" на Crowd Republic.

"Ванильный трюфель" считается одновременно и травой, и грибом. Как трава он получает красный жетон еды в фазу вымирания; как гриб он может получать красный жетон еды от владельца погибшего животного.

Когда животное берёт красный жетон еды с "Ванильного трюфеля", его владелец получает метку "Нирвана".

Игрок с меткой "Нирвана" пропускает свою очередь хода до конца этой фазы питания. В фазу вымирания игрок избавляется от метки "Нирвана".

В комплект входят:

     2 карты "Ванильный трюфель"
     4 метки последствий "Нирвана"
     правила

During the Renaissance, much regard was paid to reviving the arts: sculpture, painting, fabric, architecture, stage, and music. As a patron, your goal is to inspire the artists in your work shops to create Masterworks!

In Renaissance and Write, players will be choosing one six-sided die which they will attempt to use in all seven areas of their player sheet. Some of the choices are easier such as working to complete a Masterwork, while others may be more challenging such as which lesser work is the best choice for that round.

As players place their dice, they will find benefits with the Blessings area where a wide selection of dice yields a chance to change a die they chose into a different value, while lesser works are more specific in their choices, they can lead to points or other luck modifiers.

The game progresses and the players will have to decide is their star Artist is going to remain with them as the bonus card or if they will transition out of the workshops and allow the player to have an additional die of any value they choose for that turn only.

Clever play awaits 1 to 100 players, just print additional player pages and bonus cards to add as many players as you wish. Do you have what it takes to be the best patron in the land?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroes and Rogues: Out for Gold
description: Also called "MiniSkull Crew," after adding it to his MiniSkull world of games, in this 18-card set collecting game, players must hire 3 heroes to go on a quest, by matching either elemental quality (air, water, etc.) or race (elf, orc, etc.). Six cards are dealt in the center of the table-three face-up, three face-down, with the remaining cards dealt equally to the players, who then take turns replacing a card in the center with one in their hands, performing the action on it (take another turn, exchange a card with another player, etc.). The game ends when one player finishes collecting a set, and then all players add the gold value of their heroes, with the highest value winning.

Pike the Penguin comes with the board and rules, players will have to supply two tokens for the whale and penguin, another two for the fish, and a coin.
One person is the orca, which moves around the edge (ocean) of the board, while the other plays the penguin, which can move anywhere on the 3x4 board (iceberg). The two fish are placed on opposite sides of the board.
Players take turns moving their pieces around the board: the penguin moves one space along connected lines, while the orca moves by coin flip (either one or two spaces) displacing any fish it lands on one space.
The penguin's goal is to get 3 points by eating fish (which are added back to the board), while the orca's is to eat the penguin.

Sometimes, you know you just want to insult someone and you find that you don't have the vocabulary to do so… well now with Badmouth, that's going to be a thing of the past. With possibilities such as "filthy curtain munching prick" and "strange mirror stroking creep" we're sure that your vocabulary for insults will be improved upon in no time! Badmouth is an insult building card game for adults, for people with loose lips and looser morals.

The format of the game is quite simple and very quick to learn. There are 4 groups of cards (numbered 1-4!) which together would make a sentence and a group of Badmouth cards to keep to the side. You start with one card from each pile. You then choose your favourite word of insult from that selection and place it down. Then, pick up another card of the same number and pass that to your mate, whose cards you take. Then choose an insult word that would go well with the first card (but of a different number) and keep doing this until you have 4 chosen cards (each of a different number) that make your funny sentence.

Once you've had fun doing this, you all take it in turns to reveal your sentence. We're sure that hilarity will ensue and that there will be some down right insulting sentences being flung about. When everyone has had their turn to utter their insult, the group chooses whose was the funniest. This person is awarded a coveted Badmouth card and then the whole process starts again until all the Badmouth cards are won. When this happens, the person with the highest number of cards wins!

There are some naughty words in this game, like, really naughty. Because of that, and the nature of the game, we recommend that this is only played by adults and not kids. This game would make a great ice breaker for tense situations but we all know that this is the perfect game for a drinks night, summer BBQ or party with your friends. Best of all, this game can only be found in the UK at MenKind so you know you won't find it anywhere else! If this sounds like something you'd be interested in, or think you know someone else who would, then why not order Badmouth: The Insult Building Adult Party Game today!

Dragons are powerful and nearly indestructible, as well as highly territorial. But when they flock together, look out! Two opposing clans of dragons seek to devastate the inhabitants of each others' territories.

Dragonflock was originally designed for two full monochrome stashes of five trios each (two medium pyramids from each stash will be unused), to be played on a Volcano board.

Those with a lean pyramid supply can still play with three similar five-color Treehouse sets: Use green and blue against red and yellow (or cyan and purple against orange and clear), with no distinction between paired colors.

More attractively, for those wealthy in pyramids: Use white, black, and/or clear pyramid stashes for the larges, another two colors for the mediums, and two further translucent colors for the smalls.

The two dragons that start in the center of the board are trapped there, and neither can move until a third dragon intervenes to resolve the dispute. It is their dispute that has touched off the long-slumbering animosity between the two clans.

Flow is an abstract two-player competitive game. To win, line up four of your pieces on the game board in a row, whether diagonally or orthogonally. Each turn, you must perform two actions in this order:


     Place one of your player pieces onto the game board.
     Push one of your player pieces, as well as every other adjacent piece in the direction you push.


Because pushing a piece can also move your opponent's pieces, you can foil their plans at creating a line, but if you are not paying attention, you are just as likely to inadvertently create a line from opponent pieces and win the game for them!

—description from the publisher

Attack of the drones! This expansion introduces nimble drone units to Snap Ships Tactics. These class 2 units bristle with specialized weaponry and operate independently or swarm in coordinated attacks. 
Includes 4 complete drone units ready to be built and deployed, along with 10 new bonus pieces. All parts can mixed and matched with existing Tactics ships for awesome combos and gameplay.

—description from the publisher

The FALX SC-41 Escort and the WASP K.L.A.W. Heavy Fighter join your Snap Ships Tactics battles with 6 different builds.
These class 5 ships are designed for experienced pilots seeking to dominate the battlefield with advanced weaponry and larger ships!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Flash Point: Legacy of Flame – Reset Pack
description: Includes everything needed to reset the game and reply the original campaign. Originally part of the add-on options for Flash Point - Legacy of Flame.

Central Solutions has selected you to participate in The Game, a test of strategy, determination and mental fortitude.

Participation is mandatory.

In The Game, players control a team of contestants who are participating in different events, all with a singular goal: survive and advance. Using a simple D10 system, you must obey the rules of each game while building (and breaking) alliances and sabotaging your foes. After 3 events, the player with the most contestants still alive is the winner.

boardgame: 1918: A First World War Miniatures Game
description:
1918 is an alternating-activation historical tabletop wargame where players refight the battles of the last year of the First World War using miniatures. A typical game uses around 30-60 miniatures on each side and lasts 2-3 hours. The game focuses on combined arms (i.e. infantry, cavalry, tanks, artillery and aircraft working together) and the command challenges faced by junior officers leading men in the first truly industrial war.

Within the game, you will find:

     Epic setting - the last year of the First World War is crammed full of scale and drama. In 1918 the Kaiser's Germany makes one huge last roll of the dice, launching its Spring Offensive as fresh but inexperienced U.S. troops flood into France to support the war-weary British Empire and French Republic. The question is - who will crack first?
     Changing technology - by 1918, new innovations such as tanks, aircraft, light machine guns and accurate, indirect artillery were being used at scale to crack open the previously near-impenetrable trench lines. 
     Evolving combat - increasingly, success depended on fire and manoeuvre at the small unit level, and combined arms at higher levels. The days of 'running at machine guns' (if they ever really existed) were long over. 
     Varied battlefields - the battles of 1918 were highly mobile and varied; sometimes fighting took place in the open fields, woodlands or towns, at other times, in the shell-pocked trenches.



     Full colour, 215 page rulebook, illustrated throughout with period accurate artwork, high-quality miniature photography and licensed historical photography from the Imperial War Museum London.
     The 1918 Core and Advanced rules, including rules for vehicles,(off-board) artillery, buildings and fortifications. 
     Eight varied scenarios designed to represent the German Spring Offensive.
     Full force selection rules and four Force Lists: 
         American Expeditionary Force Infantry Division: AEF Rifle Platoon (Early) & USMC Rifle Platoon (Early), plus Battalion and Divisional Support.
         British Infantry Division: British Rifle Platoon & British Machine Gun Platoon, plus Battalion and Divisional Support.
         French Infantry Division: Rifle Platoon (Early) & Veteran Rifle Platoon (Early), plus  Battalion and Divisional Support.
         German Intervention Division: Intervention Rifle Platoon & Stormtrooper Platoon, plus Battalion and Divisional Support.
    


—description from the publisher

It's Mathive is simple to learn and addictive once you’ve got the hang of it.
By taking two decks of cards, a playing deck and a winning deck, and using cards from the playing deck in order to mathematically ‘create’ the cards from the winning deck, the player with the most cards from the winning deck takes the crown once all the winning deck cards have been played.

Players must attempt to create the value of the winning card that was picked, using two of their own playing cards. For instance, if the face-up winning deck card displayed has a value of 12, then a player might place down a 3 and 4 from their own hand, as 3 multiplied by 4 gives a total of 12. Or they could use a 7 and a 5 to add them together to reach that value of 12.

Times are hungry in the middle ages. You are monks in the Order of the Famished Friar, tending your garden to put food on the table. Plant your crops carefully to be the first to fill the monastery's menu.

Players must garden and rotate crops within a 3x3 grid, planning ahead to satisfy the menu before other players. But the Chicken is always fouling up your plots! You must chase it to another plot each turn, confounding the other gardeners or protecting a crop you need on a coming turn.

The Order of the Famished Friar is a compact card game in a mint tin, for 1-3 players. Most games take just 15-20 minutes. Once a player completes the Menu, they must immediately announce "Serve it Forthe!" and are the winner.

—description from the designer

Sunspot is a loop-forming game played on a regular hexagonal board by placing and flipping stones. The game is guaranteed to end with a winning loop. The recommended board size is 5 or larger. The pie rule may be used for balance.

				
				
					GoalForm a loop of your stones around at least one enemy stone or empty site.

				
				
					Definitions
     Edge stone: a stone on the board edge, or a stone that can "see" the board edge through a straight line of empty spaces.
     Interior stone: a stone that is not an edge stone, i.e. it’s not on the edge, and does not "see" the edge.
     A group, consisting of adjacent stones of a single colour, can be an edge group (having only edge stones), an interior group (having only interior stones), or a combined group (having both).
     A combined group has one or more edge subgroups, i.e. mutually adjacent edge stones.


				
				
					On your turnYour turn can consist of a placement action, which is mandatory as long as the board isn’t full, and a flip action, which is optional if you just did a placement action, and mandatory if no placement action was possible. There is no passing.

     Placement action: place one stone on an empty site.
     Flip action: flip an interior stone that is part of a combined group from the opponent’s colour to your colour. If the flipped stone is now part of a combined group of your colour, you must choose one edge subgroup of this group and flip it to the opponent’s colour.


You can only win after performing the complete flip action, so if you momentarily create a loop but then have to destroy it again you don’t win. If on your turn you create a loop for both yourself and the opponent, you win. If you only create a loop for the opponent, the opponent wins.

—description from the designer

L'Appuntamento di Isidoro & Sonja is an exciting board game with a moving board, played by 2-4 players. Isidoro(aka Heathcliff) has a date with Sonja at her house and each player has to cross the city to collect the gifts he will offer her.

The gifts needed to be collected are a bouquet of flowers (one card), a bottle of milk (two cards) and a fish (two cards).

While attempting to collect the gifts, each player will encounter many obstacles and opportunities to overcome his opponents.

The winner is the player who reaches Sonja's house first with an accurate roll of the dice having collected all the gifts.

Play a real game of tennis, respecting the rules of the real game. here, cards replace rackets. Winning shots, faults, challenges, audience...
All the events of a center court are brought together to play a grand slam.

—description from the back of the box

The players take on the roles of investors, buying and selling shares in global Companies seeking market opportunities across the world.

By investing in a company, you provide capital for investments and profits. When the game ends, the player with the most shares in the most profitable companies wins.

Each company is run by the player with the most shares (the "President"). One group of companies produce resources, another group manufacture them into consumer goods, and a third group provide transports between the markets around the world.

Using a rondel selection mechanic, the President issues shares or take loans to raise capital, invests the capital in facilities, workers and R&D, and operates the company to maximise the profits for the shareholders.

The economy of the game is strongly interconnected. Not only must the companies buy and sell from each other but their operations also affect the workers’ salaries and buying power. This means that market opportunities will open and close across the world, forcing the players to constantly reassess their strategies.

—description from the designer

Wire is an abstract two-player competitive game. To win, each player — red and blue — takes turns placing or rotating wire tokens on the board, trying to create a connection from one side of the board to the other.

On a turn, a player either:


     Places their wire token diagonally on an open space on the board, or
     Rotates one of their previously placed wire tokens 90º.


Play continues until a line of wire tokens in a single color connects two opposite sides of the game board. While players can place and rotate only their own wire tokens, they can take advantage of how their opponent's tokens are aligned to create the circuit they need to win the game.

A new exclusive Merchant to play, given free to backers of the Kickstarter.

Includes rulebook, miniature, 44 cards and a role selection card.

Additional cards to add to your decks. Includes:
3 Beasts of the Bay cards
6 Rogue Cards (3 of them are misprint replacements)
24 Townsfolk Cards (12 pets, 12 inventors)
32 Corruption cards

This Loot Pack was a Kickstarter Exclusive from the Master Craft stretch goals given free to backers of the campaign.

IntroductionSlepli (from Old Norse: sleiplykkja, meaning “slippery loop”) is a loop game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 5 cells per side, but boards with 4 or 6 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of connected stones of any color. A single isolated stone is also a group.
     A loop is a closed path of stones of any color, with no branches and one stone wide along its entire length.
     You control a loop if more than half of its stones are your color. If a loop has the same number of stones of each color, neither player controls it.
     A claimed loop is a loop made entirely of one color.
     To flip is to replace enemy stones with friendly stones, which is optional only when it involves flipping stones from claimed loops.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and players alternate turns. On your turn, perform the following actions in order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty cell.
     Flip all enemy stones contained within any controlled loop that forms with this placement, until all match your color.
     Remove all friendly stones adjacent to the new loop, except those belonging to claimed loops.


At the end of your turn, there must be no friendly stones adjacent to friendly loops.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when all empty cells are adjacent to loops controlled by a single player; in that case, the player who controls those loops is the winner.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on any empty spot, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

The Campaigns in Europe series features graphically updated War in Europe maps with a more detailed terrain analysis. Game counters have been upgraded as well and are presented with historical identifications while full-color PACs provide tracks, lists and setup maps.
The system uses move/fight or fight/move phases followed by a variable mechanized phase, elegantly portraying the doctrinal differences among contending armed forces. Headquarters, depots, and a rail system are used to project logistics and combat support. Air power is depicted providing ground combat support, interdiction capabilities, supply, and transport, while naval power provides transport, amphibious assault, and gunfire support.

Contents:

     1 Expansion Rules booklet
     1,120 ½-inch die-cut Expansion counters (4 sheets)
     16 Expansion Player Aid Cards (#10–25)
     Storage bags


Note: The Campaigns in Europe Expansion Pack (ziplock) is not a stand-alone game. It requires the games from World at War game edition issues 97, 98, 99 & 100 to play.

—description from the publisher

The Galactic Empire is crumbling as the outer systems break free. As the leader of one of them, you use economy, politics and science to challenge the Empire and lay the foundation of a new one.

Each game has three hidden goals (which may be economic, political, or scientific) that must be researched. Once you know a goal and fulfill it, you claim the victory.

Each player has an action board with six actions and a number of action tokens. In your turn, you may either add a token to your board and ”bump” previous tokens to higher levels or retrieve a token to take the token’s action. The higher the level, the stronger the action, provided that you researched that action level. There are six actions:


     Space Travel: Extend links to planets.
     Colonize: Place link on planet for benefit.
     Produce: Place resource on planet.
     Trade: Exchange resources between planets.
     Research: Pay to advance on tech track.
     Battle: Pay to remove Empire link.


All planets have a certain supply and demand of resources and all players may take actions on their behalf to earn rewards. Those rewards let them extend their networks and advance on the tech tree to unlock more powerful actions. Eventually they may peek at hidden goals and fulfill them to claim the victory. However, they may also manipulate the Empire to block each other.

—description from the publisher

Fart Drop is a hilarious and fast-paced party card game where players pass around a dangerous secret: the dreaded fart card. Each round is filled with bluffing, laughter, sabotage, and the constant fear that the stink might land in your lap.

Players take turns drawing, passing, or playing cards, all while trying to avoid ending the game holding the fart. But watch out—special cards can flip the game at any moment! Use gas masks to block it, unleash a backdraft to bounce it back, or drop a stink bomb for an instant win.

The game features a cast of over-the-top characters and 50 special cards that add chaos, strategy, and variety to every match. With only a few used per game, no two playthroughs are ever quite the same.

Designed for quick rounds, big laughs, and endless replayability, Fart Drop is the ultimate game for families, friends, and anyone who isn’t afraid to play dirty.

—description from the designer

サイdo男カード (Rōmaji: Saidōmenkādo, English translation: Men of Saidōsho Cards) is a collectible card game featuring real-life elderly men of the small community of Saidōsho. The cards feature artwork of the men and abilities themed based on their occupation.

Players battle teams of six men each, arranged in a three-row pyramid: one face-up attacker, two face-down on the frontline, and three face-down on the bench. The attackers battle one another using their attacks and special powers to reduce the other's hit points to zero. Powers may require players to complete certain tasks, such as complimenting their opponent or saying a tongue twister. When an attacker is defeated, a card from the frontline takes their place, and in turn a card from the bench moves to the frontline. The player who defeats all their opponent's men first wins.

Saidōmen is designed and handmade by staff at Saidōsho Community Center to address the local generational divide. Saidōsho is a small community built around a rural train station, in which working-age adults are few due to better employment opportunities in big cities. As such, the population of Saidōsho is largely two socially-distant groups: the elderly and children. The game has fostered interest and engagement between the two demographics.

MEMORY21 is a fast-paced memory game where players collectively count to 21, but there's a twist: each round, numbers are replaced with prompt cards. Every round adds a new layer of chaos as players must remember which card replaced which number.

Aside from the counting, this game is about strategy, bluffing and razor-sharp memory. Challenge your opponents when they slip up, but be careful: one wrong move and its your turn to be called out!

Whether you are playing with friends or foes, this game is a true test of recall. Only one can be victorious, will it be you?

MEMORY21 is a fast-paced memory game where players collectively count to 21, but there's a twist: each round numbers are replaced with picture cards. Every round adds a ew layer of chaos as players must remember which card replaced which number.

Aside from the counting, this game is about strategy, bluffing & razor-sharp memory. Challenge your opponents when they slip up, but be careful: one wrong move and its your turn to be called out!

Whether you are playing with friends or foes, this game is a true test of recall. Only one can be victorious, will it be you?

—description from the publisher

Cambio: Into the Wild is a fast-paced strategy card game that blends the reflexes of Snap with the tactical edge of Poker. Designed for 3–6 players, it's quick to learn and wildly replayable, with intense 10–15 minute rounds full of bluffing, bold moves, and primal showdowns. Perfect for game night, travel, or parties, Cambio brings wild energy and competitive fun to your pocket.

? Learn more or get your copy here: https://www.cambiothegame.com/

—description from the designer

A chicken race where you can either increase the number of dice or roll a dice.

If you roll a lot of dice, your score will increase, but if you increase too much, your opponent will roll the dice.

You’ve embarked on a mission with Dr Idaho Bones, celebrated adventuring archaeologist, to locate and safeguard the lost treasures of an ancient temple, deep in the rainforest. But when Dr Bones accidentally triggers a booby trap and seals you inside, it’s down to you to solve the temple’s fiendish puzzles, secure the treasure, and escape.

Only once you’ve solved all 12 puzzles will you be able to unseal the temple’s exit door and escape with the lost treasure. Can you outwit the temple’s cunning traps, or will you become an archaeological find yourself?

Expand your spellbook with Advanced Spellcraft, featuring new options for wizards and a ten-scenario competitive campaign.

While today's wizards are not as gifted as those who ruled Felstad, the recent discovery of the fabled Bibliosepulchre promises to change all that. It was in this great library-crypt that ancient librarians were interred, each clutching a rare grimoire. Who knows what strange energies have seeped from those tomes over the centuries or what shadows and ghosts stalk the dark catacombs? Only those truly obsessed with magic and the power it offers would dare to find out!

Advanced Spellcraft offers numerous new rules and ideas that bring more spellcasting possibilities to games of Frostgrave. Critical successes and failures for each basic spell are introduced, as are spell-specific flourishes that can be learned to enhance your magic. Create a Furious Quill that leaps from target to target, cast a Draining Word that affects an entire school of magic, or ensnare multiple zombies with Control Undead. Such flourishes take their toll, however, and to even learn such secrets will require you to venture into the Bibliosepulchre itself in a ten-scenario competitive campaign.

With hundreds of new tricks for wizards to learn, Advanced Spellcraft is the largest supplement to date, and is presented in celebration of Frostgrave's 10th anniversary.

—description from the publisher

In the mystical Huangshan Mountains, Clever Tibetan macaques play a  thrilling survival game. As one of them, your interactions are filled with hidden choices—cooperate or betray? Every decision could change the clan's fate!
This game is perfect for family gatherings and parent-child bonding. The vibrant artwork captures macaque life, blending beauty and simplicity. Easy-to-learn rules, fun interactions, and multiple play modes maike ite a strategic yet entertaining nature-themed game!
The game is co-created with Shanghai Natural History Museum.

In Swearmints: Spicy NSFW Edition, you receive two cards that each feature four saucy words. Choose a word from each card to create a new swear that wins votes from other players.

During each round, The Mint Master starts by dealing out 2 cards to all other players. Each card has 4 silly words on each side, so each player will have 16 words to play with. All players must combine one word on each of their cards to form a new slang phrase and arrange it face up in front of them. (They cannot combine two words on the same card). Once everyone is ready, each player must introduce their new slang phrase and tell the Mint Master what it means.
Scoring

After each player has defined their word, the Mint Master awards 1 Mint to their favorite new slang phrase plus and bonus mints.

Once the winner is chosen, all of the used cards are discarded, the Mint Master passes the tin clockwise, and you go back to the start. Re-shuffle as needed. Play continues until the tin runs out of mints, and the person with the most mints wins.

Over five quick rounds, you'll trade rum, gather weapons, and tame wild creatures in your quest for infamy!

Each round has two key phases: drafting cards to plan your strategy, then building to expand your fleet and compete for Infamy cards. Every card you build strengthens your fleet, unlocking new opportunities and expanding your reach.

Simultaneous play keeps games fast and lively, even with up to five players.

—description from the designer

A Tricky Escape is a cooperative trick-taking game with the heart-pounding tension of an escape room. Your team is trapped with just 60 minutes on the clock and a series of puzzle-filled rooms standing between you and freedom. Each room presents a unique trick-taking twist. If you succeed, you seal it off and press on. If you fail, you must choose whether to retry the puzzle or risk potentially taking on an even more difficult one. After unlocking three rooms, you will face a final scenario-specific challenge to complete your escape. Need help? You can use hint cards, but they will cost you precious time. Make every trick count or risk getting out by the skin of your teeth.

—description from the designer

The elite have pushed France to the edge of chaos. But the power that was taken from the people must return to the people. Relive the struggle for liberal democracy as you fight for liberty, equality, and fraternity.

—description from the publisher

The cities of the Indus Valley had their own written language, trade systems, centralized drainage, and housing. Relive the unlikely rise and fall of one of the cradles of human civilization.

—description from the publisher

Lizard's Lair is a solitaire tile - laying puzzle mint tin game. Take on the role of a slithering lizard moving throughout its lair. The goal of the game is to collect eggs, dodge snakes, and avoid getting trapped in this ever changing underground.

Suspenseful choices: Select which card you will play wisely, timing is crucial.

Decisive roll and move mechanics: A refreshing take on roll and move games. Each movement impacts the next round.

Simple yet strategic and replayable gameplay: 17 lair cards creates a wide range of variety and card combinations each gameplay.

Push Your Luck with King Snakes: Will you fall in a trap? Run into a predator? Collect precious eggs?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Skara Brae: KS Promo Action Tiles – Barter & Forage
description:
A set of 2 special action Tiles, first offered in the 2024 Kickstater for Skara Brae.

Barter
Gain 3 Skaill Knives. May pay 1 Skaill Knife at the end of each Round to purchase the Resources shown directly below the Trader Marker. This is free once flipped. If a player has not moved their Trade Marker at least one space, this has no effect.

Forage
Resolve 1 Gather Action Tile up to 2 times (3 times when flipped), just as if Workers had been placed there. Also gain 1 Skaill Knife. For Gather Action Tiles that have a choice of rewards, choose from any combination.

A set of 6 plunder tiles and 10 beer tokens, first offered during the 2024 Kickstarter for The Anarchy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: I, Napoleon: The Limits of Glory
description:
I, Napoleon is a solo biographical card game that puts you in the shoes of Napoleon Bonaparte in 1793 and allows you to explore the paths his life might have taken until his death in 1821.

I, Napoleon: The Limits of Glory offers new events, people, politics, and possibilities, but it also adds some complexity, more cards to shuffle and keep track of, and as much as two hours of additional playtime. You will meet new Commanders (Suchet you say?), new lovers, new political allies, and new enemies (including the Russian Imperial Guard!). The cards increase the importance of Political Points in the game as you will need them to deal with unrest from both Jacobins and Royalists. The Spanish Ulcer will be a bigger problem, with three new Expedition cards to deal with. And the Portugal Box on the map will finally get some use with Portugal’s Royal Family.

As a Commander, Napoleon may have the opportunity to carry out either his attempted March on Constantinople (historically thwarted by defeat in Syria) or his dream of a March on India. Both very risky but promising large rewards.

The possibility of Escape to America after Waterloo now exists, opening up the chance for a final act of Glory by the fugitive French Emperor. Will you collude with Aaron Burr and perhaps create an American Empire? Or be defeated by Andrew Jackson? Or perhaps serve under the Americans against the British in an ongoing War of 18--?

Comrades Conundrum is a game about the bureaucracy and backstabbing of the Supreme Soviet, shortly before the Fall of the Iron Curtain. You are a minister of the Soviet Union, seeking to seize the position of Premier as you acquire ever more grandiose Titles. By seizing Resources, and strategic use of your Powers, you might just end up at the top of the heap! Of course, your rivals won't just lay down like the capitalist running dogs they are— it's up to you to outmaneuver them before Perestroika brings everything crashing down.

Comrades revolves around two main mechanics: trading of randomly distributed Resources (5 of each type, 10 types overall), and the use of Power cards to disrupt this trade. Resources (Nukes, Party Memberships, etc.) are traded blindly between players, opening up the potential to bluff. The more matches of a particular resource you hold, the more potential value they have, as you trade them in to declare Titles, which are worth points at the end of the game.

Power cards come in two types, Action and Instant. Action cards are more powerful than Instants, but force the player to choose between declaring a Title, or altering the balance of play. Instants, on the other hand, can be played at any time, and are generally offensive in use.

Optional rulesets provide for longer games, and for a "Judge" that influences things round to round.

—description from the publisher

Fate-Fortune-Djinn is a two player card game, and a conversation. The game is completed when one player Djinns. The player who has the Djinn hand is in the Initiator phase, the player who does not Djinn is in the Receiver phase. To Djinn a player must draw and add one card to their hand of 11 cards to make a 12 card hand of four cards of one Mortals suit, three cards of a second Mortals suit, 2 cards of a third Mortals suit, one card of a fourth Mortals suit, and a pair of Celestials cards. The Djinn hand must have the number of Mortals cards in the suit indicated by the player's elements die roll. The game can be played to win by having the Djinn hand, or for fun and personal insight. The choice is the players. Website: www.fate-fortune-djinn.com

—description from the designer

TomeBound is a fast-paced, spell-slinging dueling game where strategy reigns supreme. Disrupt your foe with a deluge of wind and water, build and demolish with powerful earth spells, and reduce your enemy to ashes with an onslaught of fire and lightning. TomeBound is a game of planning, timing, clever counter play, and epic battle magic!

Tomebound is a deckless deckbuilder that approaches the classic concept of dueling wizards from a totally new and unique perspective!

—description from the designer

An exploration and building long-form game for one civ-building enthusiast, played on an origami fortune teller (aka: a cootie catcher). World In My Pocket is a civ-building game specifically designed to fit in your pocket for you to play in short bursts over a long period of time (days or weeks). Excluding a pen, the entire game (randomizer, play field, and play reference) is 2" x 2" x 1/4". Includes expansions and variant rules to play co-op, co-petitively, or competitively with any number of players (though 5 players total is a good upper limit).

—description from the publisher

Railheads: Switzerland is a cube rails game about the development of the Swiss rail network and how the strategic geographical location of Switzerland, nestled among the Alps, made its rail transport essential for connecting northern and southern Europe.

Through the use of a highly interactive and cascading action selection mechanism the players compete by laying tracks, constructing tunnels, developing industries, making investments,and managing loans made available by Credit Suisse with one ultimate goal in mind, to be the wealthiest magnate when the game ends.

Railheads: Switzerland is the third installment in a series of cube rails games that all share a prime action selection mechanism as well as a central theme of both railroad network and industry refinement getting developed in tandem.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Hadrai'da: Ascendant World of Kylia TCG
description:
Join the esteemed ranks of the Hadrai’da, Kylia’s most renowned mercenary guild! Battle against other combatants in an attempt to rise above all others!

Experience the thrill of combat in this first person TCG as you strategize, collect powerful cards, and challenge opponents. Game modes support 2-6 players.

Use a combination of cards & dice rolls to simulate the unpredictability of battle, adding an exciting element of chance and strategic decision-making to every encounter.

In this game, your character's skills and abilities are represented by a 40-60 card deck. Find matching cards to play them on the battlefield and turn the tides in your favor!

—description from the designer

This expansion pack for the social card game FLUSTER takes you deeper into the minds of your friends or significant other with sex-and-relationship themed questions.

The expansion also includes Blind Date cards, which allow you to trade hands with another player.

Ask the questions you've always wondered about, while competing to give the best answers. Maybe you'll learn something fun, or maybe it will open up a vulnerable conversation you've been wanting to have.

—description from the publisher

This expansion pack for the social card game FLUSTER adds some comic relief with questions that evoke funny stories and explore your friends' senses of humour while competing to give the best answers.

The expansion also includes Rewrite cards, which allow you to change one word in a question for maximum laughs.

Questions in the Laugh Pack include "What's a recent thought that would have made you sound like a stoner?" and "What's something you can't believe you used to find funny?"

—description from the publisher

Strataugury is a fantastical card game of strategy, magic, and war.  All a player requires is a standard deck of cards and the Strataugury Manual.

Each player takes turns summoning units and casting spells using combinations of cards from their hand.  The goal of the game is to reduce the opponent's health to 0, or to increase their own health to 60.

The game is split into Basic and Advanced Gameplay elements to ensure that players are not immediately overwhelmed by the game's complexity.

Solo players are also welcome as Single-Player Campaigns offer narrative-driven challenges to test their skills and experience epic legends.

—description from the publisher

Play dice with this famous family game. With Mega Yatzy Jr. you can play together all evening! With the extra large wooden dice and fun animals, the game is easy to understand. The score pad contains enough pages for many rounds of this game and it has an easy scoring system. Guaranteed fun for the whole family!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
The game can be played by 3–5 players. After determining the order of play, all players start from the "OBSERVATION CENTER" square.
Players may agree to start from a different position if they wish.

Each player directs their spaceship toward a "NUMBERED STATION," choosing any route they prefer to reach it.

Players take turns rolling the die, moving forward by the number of spaces shown.
Some spaces contain various symbols, such as "ROCKETS," "MEASURING WELLS," "CLOCKS," "COPY," etc.
These symbols mean the following:

Landing on a ROCKET space speeds up the player’s spaceship—they may immediately move to the space indicated by the rocket.

Landing on a MEASURING WELL means the player must skip one turn—they may only move again on their next roll.

A CLOCK space benefits the player, allowing them to move forward two extra spaces.

Landing on a COPY space means the player must copy the position of the previous player—moving back to the space that player is on.

Furthermore, the game leader resolves any unusual situations or disputes that may arise during gameplay.

The goal of every player is to reach the final point of outer space, located in the center of the board.
The first player to reach this point is the winner of the game.



JÁTÉKSZABÁLYA játékot 3–4–5 személy játszhatja. A sorrendet megállapítva, minden játékos a „MEGFIGYELŐ KÖZPONT” mezőről indul.
A játékosok megállapodnak, ettől eltérően is lehet elindulni.
Minden játékos egy „SZÁMRAJELZŐ ÁLLOMÁS” felé irányítja űrhajóját, melynek megközelítéséhez a játékos tetszésének megfelelő útvonalat választhat.

A játékosok a velük jelzett dobókockát felváltva dobják, és a dobott pontszámnak megfelelő mezőn haladnak tovább.
Egyes mezőkön különféle szimbólumok láthatók, mint pl. „RAKÉTÁK”, „MÉRŐKUTAK”, „ÓRÁK”, „MÁSOLAT” stb.
Ezek jelentése:

A „RAKÉTA” mezőkre lépő játékos űrhajója gyorsabban halad, azonnal továbbléphet a rakétával jelzett mezőre.

A „MÉRŐKÚT” mezők egy körből való kimaradást jelenthetnek, a játékos csak a következő dobás után haladhat tovább.

Az „ÓRA” mező a játékos előnyére szolgál, két mezőt előreléphet.

A „MÁSOLAT” mezőre érkező játékos „lemásolja” az előző játékos helyzetét, azaz visszalép az ő mezőjére.

Továbbá! A játékvezető ezt akkor válaszolja meg, ha bármiféle különös eset fordul elő, vagy a játékos vitás ügyekben nem tudnak megegyezni.

Minden játékos célja: elsőként eljutni a világűr legvégső pontjára, amely a játékmező középpontjában található. Ez a pont a „CÉL”.
Aki ezt először eléri, az lesz a játék győztese.

You have been with us through the deep, dark Soulstone mines of First Edition; out into the bloody streets of Second Edition; and finally ventured with us into the vast wilds of Third Edition. With each step, the game has built on the editions before it, making Third Edition the best iteration yet. Through it all, Malifaux has always emphasized putting Fate directly into the hands of the players.

Now, we ask you to pull on those threads of fate once again by joining the Malifaux Fourth Edition open beta! Our plan is to begin the open beta next week on April 21st and run it for the next several months, finalizing the new edition by Gen Con 2025!

—description from the publisher

Malifaux is a card-driven miniatures skirmish game set in the otherworldly titular city, where players compete to execute "schemes" and "strategies" rather than necessarily killing the opposition. The fourth edition brings improved and streamlined gameplay, with particular focus on speed of play and meaningful interaction with terrain.

—user summary

In Pyramire, players compete to become the next Deity by gathering the favor of the gods and invoking powerful creatures. Set in an ancient world, players use strategic movements and resource management to assert their dominance.

The player who accumulates the most power points by the end of the game is crowned the Chosen One.

In each round, a player has three phases:

Pyramid Phase: Rotate one pyramid up to a quarter turn to gain resources.

Action Phase: Perform various actions, including recruiting creatures, moving pyramids, activating geometric powers, and trading resources.

Regeneration Phase: Regenerate all creature cards and draw a new card.

The game features a unique mechanic where the geometric arrangement of pyramids is crucial for activating creature powers. Players must carefully plan their pyramid movements and resource management to create powerful combos and assert their vision to triumph 1.

The game ends when a player reaches at least 40 points or has 8 creature cards in their play area. The player with the highest total points wins.

In Kissa Naapurissa ("Cat Next Door"), 16 double-sided window tiles are set up in a grid, some of which show a cat and others a colour. The players' task is to find and match a coloured cat token the correct combination of cat and colour by flipping the window tiles. The player chooses either the same cat or the same colour as the one on the cat token and checks if they got it right. If both match, the player earns a cat token as a reward.

The memory tiles are not removed from play, but they keep turning over by each flip/turn.

boardgame: TroubleZ Run! Revenge of the Jellynator
description:
TroubleZ Run! Revenge of the Jellynator is push-your-luck game for kids of age 6 and up, designed by Giorgio Galbusera, illustrated by Petr Štefek and set in the same colourful zombie universe as card game TroubleZ.

It’s for 2 to 4 players and you will be able to play it in 15 to 30 minutes. It has very simple rules and fast pace, so the kids will love it. In the role of TroubleZ, you will be fleeing from a monster called Jellynator and rescuing zombie animals that would otherwise be trapped by a Jellynator.

The catch is that you don’t want to get too far ahead – the last player is rewarded each round, but at the same time you don’t want the Jellynator to catch you, because that results in minus points. The game is dice-driven, but there is still a lot of meaningful choices to make, so I think if the kids will bring you to the game, you won’t regret.

—description from the publisher

Probably the best party game of all time. 60 cards with various instructions and fun tasks for a sociable and unforgettable games evening with your “team”.

—description from the publisher

Das wahrscheinlich beste Partyspiel aller Zeiten. 60 Karten mit verschiedenen Anweisungen und lustigen Aufgaben für einen geselligen und unvergesslichen Spieleabend mit deiner "Mannschaft".

—description from the publisher (German)

Monsters are massing into huge armies that threaten to destroy the cities the heroes have so laboriously built. Elite units need to be summoned, new weapons forged, and the battle against evil must be waged once more.

Karak 2: Chaos is the biggest expansion ever created for a Karak game. It introduces ten new building types, of which you draft up to two at the start of the game, allowing you to tailor the shape of your city to your strategy. Five of the new buildings serve to train new elite units - each has only two dice, but even just one of them will yield an entire army. 
There's a new merchant tile on the game board, where players can pay resources to buy items that will help them deal with situations when there's no good loot left.

Finally, from the cursed cities that have fallen victim to the rampage of the Chaos Riders' armies, mighty warbands march out with one goal in mind - to raze your cities.

Karak 2: Chaos also introduces a pair of new heroes, the Alchemist and the Acrobat, that are coming to bring the number of available heroes – including promos – to 18.

—description from the publisher

Karak: Labyrinth is a real-time drawing game by Petr Vojtěch. Labyrinth is designed for 1 to 4 players aged 6 and up, and the playing time is around 10 to 15 minutes, making it a true lightning-fast game.

Karak: Labyrinth is the most accessible game from the world of Karak. Players simultaneously try to find the best path through the labyrinth of Karak, and to gain the most points maximizing the completion of three objectives, randomly selected from a vast pool of objective cards.

Players must not retrace their own steps and to pass important points of interest – such as a sword to be gained – they must solve the corresponding puzzle mini-game, of which there are several different kinds in the game. And it all happens within a time limit.

Rules-wise everything is as simple as possible, and the game will include a tutorial campaign, which means you can start playing as soon as you unpack the game.

—description from the publisher

The core element of the game, designed by Olda Rejl, Ondra Cigánek and Ivan Dostál, is a fresh and rewarding system of three gears which determine what actions you can or must perform in each round.

Crash Derby is being developed in collaboration with Prusa Research and it's a title explicitly designed with the intention of allowing the community to create new content for it, like additional cart models, obstacles or entirely new gameplay elements.

At launch, a range of expansion elements will already be available to print at home on the Printables website, with more to come. That way, soon there won’t be a two copies of Crash Derby that would be the same.

Crash Derby is designed for 2 to 4 players aged 8 and up, and one game will take approximately 45 minutes to play.

—description from the publisher

The Puer expansion enhances the game with Court of Nobles, enriching your options with two new main actions - bringing the total to seven, and the special Imperial decrees then bring even more complexity to the game and expand your strategic choices.

As a first new action you can visit the Court, for which you'll use the new Courtier meeples. The halls of the Court not only bring immediate bonuses but also points at the end of the round if you hold the majority there.

The second new action is the puer market - featuring pieces that can be linked together to earn bonuses not only from newly added pieces, but also from previous ones. Careful planning is therefore much desirable. In addition, completed puers also help to earn points at the end of the game.

It is necessary to be prepared for the fact that the complexity of the Tea Garden increases quite significantly with this expansion, and the time required to play the game is also a bit longer than without it. The Puer expansion also contains components for Solo play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 何食べたい (What Do You Want to Eat?)
description:
何食べたい (What Do You Want to Eat?), a.k.a., Guessing Foodies in Taiwan, is a deduction card game themed around delicious Taiwanese cuisine. Use clues of color and number to guess which dish your opponent has secretly chosen!

In more detail, each turn one player selects a dish card from their hand and places it behind their preference card, hidden from others. In turn, the other players place a card from their hand face up, and the player who chose the dish places the card by "Like" or "Dislike" depending on whether the target dish matches in color or number with the one presented. Using clues from these hints, the other players try to guess the target dish. Whoever guesses correctly first wins a point, and whoever scores the most wins.

Enjoy a flavorful deduction battle with family and friends as you explore Taiwan's tasty treats! Who will be the first to uncover what their opponent really wants to eat? Take the challenge and find out!

The boardgame Napoleon was manufactured and released in Greece in the 70's by EPA toy company. It is an educational boardgame for children and adolescents from 6 - 18 years old and can be played by 2-6 players. Through the game players learn about the life and major historical events in which Napoleon Bonaparte was involved.

Each player has a pawn and the movement on the board is done using dice. During the game, each player will fall on spots that have specific orders on them that they must be followed. These orders will either slow him down or speed up his path.

The winner of the game is the one who reaches the finish line first.

The board game comes with a lovely little 64-page booklet with the story of Napoleon.

Sugar Rush is a speed- and memory-based game, where players take on the roles of different desserts. The objective is to remember everyone else's dessert identities and to be the quickest at calling out another player's identity when needed.

In Sugar Rush, players start by picking a dessert identity and attempt to remember everyone else's selected dessert identity. Players then take turns opening a card from the main deck, which also consists of desserts. When two players have matching open dessert cards, they are in a duel. The duelling players then try to remember and call out their opponent's dessert identity. The first player to do so correctly wins their opponent's top card and scores it.

When the main deck runs out, the game is over. At that point, the player with the most scored cards wins the game.

The game can be played with advanced variants where players have once-per-game abilities to exchange their dessert identity with another player or with the unselected identities.

Similar to:

     Anomia - Sugar Rush is a significant twist on Anomia. While Anomia requires players to quickly call out general trivia, Sugar Rush needs players to remember other players' identities and call those out instead.

In Kitten's Kitchen, the entrepreneurial kitties have set their paws on the food business.

Can you fulfill orders and earn more coins?

Choose one of your kittens. If you correctly select the next player, use the effects you earn to accumulate orders that give you more coins.

Whoever accumulates the most coins at the end of the game wins the game of Kitten's Kitchen.

PWNED! is a two-player abstract strategy game where the cyberpunk theme meets tactical depth. Set in a digital battlefield inspired by hacker culture, players command opposing hacker factions—Red vs. Blue—in a race to infiltrate each other’s servers. The narrative pits two hacker armies, led by the legendary Hacker Pangolin, in an explosive clash of logic, deception, and domination.

Players take turns performing two out of four possible actions: move, copy, zip, or unzip their pieces across a network of nodes. Strategic placement is essential, as players can stack three pieces into a powerful compressed “zip file,” which can later be unpacked to dispatch pieces on free directions. Along the way, Trojan bombs add layers of bluffing and chaos, capable of triggering chain reactions when revealed or deliberately detonated.

Beyond positioning and movement, players must remain connected—literally. Each side must maintain a network path to their own server at all times, or risk violating the “disconnection” rule. Meanwhile, deleting enemy pieces through strategic flanking is a key part of control and sabotage.

The objective is clear: be the first to send any of your pieces into the opponent’s server and shout “PWNED!” to declare victory! With its sleek mechanics, hidden traps, and layered decision-making, PWNED! delivers a highly replayable battle of minds, ideal for fans of tactical board games and digital warfare alike.

—description from the designer

In this beautiful card game you can win by building your art collection. Art Jam showcases work from 64 of the world’s greatest artists, dating from medieval to modern times.

In Art Jam players aim to create matching sets of 3 cards based on themes and colors. Themes included in the game are portraits, animals, woman & child, music, and sports. The first player to create 3 sets wins the game. Art Jam is a card game that combines card playing and set collecting in a nice way.

The world is on the brink of destruction during the fourth “cold war”. The world has congealed into 5 ruling nations. Each nation sits poised to strike with their full nuclear arsenal. In these trying times one thing is for certain… there will be mutually assured destruction.

During a game of M.A.D - Mutually assured destruction, players take the role of a nuclear superpower. Each player will join the arms race, building missiles, defences and bunkers to ensure they survive the coming armageddon.

A game of M.A.D. involves playing many game rounds, made up of player turns. During a players turn, they will be performing actions that involve playing cards, consisting of either missiles, defences or special cards. When all players have had their turn, there will be a missile phase that occurs simultaneously across all players.

The goal of M.A.D. is to destroy all other nations while keeping your nation safe from missile bombardment. Failing that, ensure your nation saves the most of population in nuclear bunkers.

—description from the designer

Step into a micro world of medieval conquest with Mint Kingdom - A compact mint tin solo game where you take on the role of a Lord - managing resources, building an army, and expanding your realm!
