The game begins with the building of your kingdom. Grow your population and assign them roles — farmers to cultivate food, blacksmiths to forge metal, and peasants to generate gold through taxes. Each turn brings new events that could either aid your progress, like grand Tournaments, or challenge it, such as devastating famines. Once your resources are secured, raise an army of knights, archers, pikemen, and peasants to lay siege to the castles of three neighboring Lords. Conquer all three territories to claim ultimate victory!

—description from the publisher

Live the dream with Barbie! In The Game of Life: Barbie Edition board game, players dream big; cruise up to a Malibu Beach Party in a Dream Convertible; explore a Dream Career; collect cat, dog, and friend pegs; and set fashion trends. Along the way, earn Dream Points. Draw Action cards to collect more Dream Points for helpful deeds — or pay them to other players. The player with the most Dream Points when everyone reaches the party wins!

LIVE THE DREAM: Dream big and take a spin around Malibu in your Dream Convertible, choose your Dream Career, find your Dream House, set fashion trends, and bring friends along for the ride.

DREAM CAREERS & DREAM HOUSES: Pop Star or Marine Biologist? Getaway House or Dream Boat? Players draw Dream Career and Dream House cards to choose their dream job and home.

DREAM CONVERTIBLES, PETS & FRIENDS: Choose a Dream Convertible in one of 4 exciting colors! Then collect cat, dog, and friend pegs (with unique hairstyles!) to take along for the ride.

SET TRENDS: Ballet Core, Coastal Cowgirl, Boho Chic… Land on a Trendsetter space and set a new fashion trend! Earn Dream Points every time another player spins that trend’s number.

–description from publisher

boardgame: Revengeance (fan expansion for Cosmic Ecounter Duel)
description: This fan expansion by Chris "Messianic" Oliveira adds more aliens and envoys into the mix, and 6 Artifacts and rule to spice up the reinforcements deck!

Warring States: China is a 2-player area control/war game set in warring states period (481/403 BCE - 221 BCE) China.

Fast playing and easy to learn, at the start of each turn you will toss 3 coins to represent consulting the I-Ching, which will determine which combinations of actions that are available for you to take. Should you go on the attack, or hold your ground and recruit more armies?

Recruit, move and use tactics cards to sway the battles in your favour as you fight to control the most regions.

—description from the designer

Shadow has fallen over Lorehelm. The Denizens lay defeated. You, a lone Paladin, are what's left of the heroes of the Riverwide Alliance. Mere days remain until the evils of Nul Gador take permanent hold of the crystals. Their plans at the Castle must be stopped before all is lost.

You look Northward, squinting against the burning shadow. You whisper a prayer and take the first steps of your final quest.

Pocket Paladin is an epic solo overland adventure designed to be stored in a single card sleeve. Work your way across the landscape, interacting with map sites like storms, wandering enemies, Mage and Monster towers, secret tunnels, towns and caravans. Unlock new abilities, recruit allies and learn new skills to be prepared to take on more challenging enemies and--ultimately--the enemy's Castle. Complete your quest before time runs out to lift the cover of shadow and bring peace back to Lorehelm!

—description from the publisher

From a sacred mountaintop in central Borneo stretches what will be the world’s last and greatest space elevator: the Mahkota Langit. Surrounding it is Earth’s newest megacity, Kota Kalimantan, which grew rapidly to serve the space elevator’s construction and anticipate its operation. Kali sprawls across central Borneo, ignoring borders and absorbing cities in its path.

The elevator is the project of the loosely allied Oceanic and Southeast Asian Nations. OSEAN has poured its resources into what was hoped to be a space elevator free of megacorporation control and able to completely surpass Earth’s two other elevators with its scale and technological superiority.

---

Elevation builds upon the groundwork laid by System Gateway to broaden the core Netrunner card pool and round out the thematic and mechanical identities of each of the three Runner and four Corp factions.

System Gateway and Elevation together constitute the core card pool that forms the basis for Null Signal Games Standard and Startup game formats, and is the ideal foundation for all levels of Netrunner play.

Elevation is about raising the stakes and reaching new heights, all set against the backdrop of a space elevator under construction and the megacity that surrounds it.

Whereas expansions in cycles (such as the Liberation Cycle) deliver one strong narrative, Elevation instead provides a setting in which players will tell their own tales. Kota Kalimantan has dozens of stories waiting to be discovered, and no pre-set conclusions.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Zugzwang is a crazy, captivating complement to Chess, where practitioners play callous cards to force folly upon their furious foes.

Perfect for chess enthusiasts looking for fresh excitement, this innovative game introduces a unique card mechanic that challenges your tactical skills. Each player wields a deck of cards that dictates the movements of their opponent's pieces, adding an unexpected twist to every match.

With Zugzwang, no more dull moments over the chessboard! Players are forced to think creatively as they navigate the repercussions of their opponent's moves.

Transform your chess experience and keep your game nights engaging with Zugzwang—a captivating blend of strategy and chance that will have friends and family hooked!

—description from the designer

15 card expansion pack for Oh Fruck!

Contains 13 new special rule cards and 2 more blank house rule cards.

13 Heroes is a card game with core mechanics based on Rock-Paper-Scissors, making it easy for anyone to  pick up and play. The addition of card abilities deepens the strategy of trying to be the first person to play your entire hand and win the game.

The goal of the game is to be the first play to play all the cards in their hand. Each card had a number between 1 and 13 and a symbol representing rock, paper, or scissors.

At the start of a round, the current player can play any card. From that point, the next player will need to play a card from their hand that either:

A)Has the Rock-Paper-Scissor symbol that would beat the current card in play

OR

B)Is the same number as the card in play.

If no cards in their hand meet either condition, then they draw a card from the deck and pass to the next player

While these core mechanics are all you need to know to play the Simple Mode of the game, which is recommended for learning the game, the Standard Mode of play incorporates the abilities on the cards which increase the depth of the gameplay.

Each of the 13 cards have unique abilities that allow you to do things like skip other players, play successive turns, reverse the power balance of the card numbers and more.

13 Heroes is a fast paced card game with the iconic wacky design and art style of "ito" game designer Mitsuru Nakamura that breathes new life into a classic childhood game everyone is already familiar with, making it the perfect choice for parties and groups with casual gamers.

Shin Cucco is a new take on the classic game Cucco. Players will have to be mind full of a shifting power balance and what cards their opponents may have or else they risk losing all their heart tokens and getting knocked out of the game.

Each player starts the game with 3 heart tokens, and at the start of each round, every player is dealt a single card. After everyone checks what card the have, the Start Player chooses if turn order should go clockwise or counter clockwise. Then on each players turn they decide to do ONE of the following:

Stay: Keep their current card

OR

Change: Switch their current card with the card held by the next player in turn order. (The last player in turn order would discard their card and draw from the deck.)

Once all players have had their turn, it is time to reveal the card they hold in their hand. The player left holding the lowest numbered card is determined to be the loser of that round and must remove one of their heart tokens.

In the simple version of the game, players simply compare the numbers on their cards, which can range between 1 and 18. However, when playing Special Mode the abilities on the cards are also taken into consideration. These abilities allow players to turn the tide back into their favor, or inconvenience other players. It is even possible to change the power balance mid-round and make lower value numbers stronger than higher value ones.

Gameplay continues until one player has lost all 3 of their heart tokens. At the end of the game, remaining players reveal the point value on the back of their tokens for a final point total. The value on the heart tokens are random and unknown even to the players until the end of the game, so even if you end up with less total tokens than other players at the end, there is still a chance that luck will be on your side and you can end up with enough points to be the winner.

boardgame: Bloomchasers: Birds and Bees Expansion Deck
description:
The Bloomchasers base game is a fun, exciting experience on its own, with enough competitive strategy to make it replayable over and over. But, the Birds and Bees Expansion Pack was made for those wanting deeper strategic mischief, higher scores, and to grow a bigger tree!

The Birds
These feathered frenemies fly around the tree as it grows, and savvy players can use them to protect their own buds that might be vulnerable to getting stolen, or to block an opponent's bud that's on the verge of blooming! Be careful, though, because Birds have a habit of getting comfortable and might stick around where they're not wanted — you can only move them when a "Bird" weather card is available on your turn.

The Beehives
Beehive Branches come in a new, tricky shape that slots into the tree like a normal branch. But, Bloomchasers who make their flowers bloom on a Beehive Branch will double the score of any flowers touching, so expect a hive of activity around them as players fight to grow onto them!

—description from the publisher
publishers: Evan and Josh's Very Special Games Company (Very Special Games)

description:
Netherwatch is a cooperative horror deduction game where players take on the roles of elite agents investigating supernatural disturbances, uncovering cryptic clues, and confronting a terrifying Demon. With unique agent abilities, unpredictable adversaries, and escalating tension, Netherwatch blends strategic deduction, survival horror, and tense cooperation into an intense gaming experience.

Each mission unfolds in two stages: first, players must investigate and deduce the demon's identity while managing their sanity and avoiding deadly manifestations, then they must perform a high-stakes banishment ritual or escape before the Demon claims them.

—description from the designer

A hilarious dating boardgame! Players roll the dice and the first one to the finish line wins. The challenge, however, is that players must spot the red flags or end up going back to therapy. The game involves Red Flag Cards where players vote on different behaviours; whether they signify a Red or Green Flag.

This game draws parallels to the actual dating culture here in Australia, where players could get ghosted or receive nudes. If players land on Confession Corner they are instructed to confess to a time when they were the red flag. This game creates great conversation between trusted friends, and a good laugh!

This game is suitable for adults given the relationship themes involved, and comes with a light warning given the sensitive nature of the topic.

—description from the designer

boardgame: らくらく冒険者の幸せ異世界ライフの独立拡張～旅仲間の冒険盟約～ (Rakuraku Boukensha no Shiawase Isekai Life no Dokuritsu Kakuchou ~Tabi Nakama no Bouken Meiyaku~)
description:
らくらく冒険者の幸せ異世界ライフの独立拡張～旅仲間の冒険盟約～ is a standalone expansion for らくらく冒険者の幸せ異世界ライフ, that can be played with 1-2 players by itself, or combined to play 1-6 players in a single game.

The players are reincarnated into a fantasy world, where they have one year to compete for the happiest life with their partner. Each round/month, players choose to either go on a quest and acquire resources, or to spend those resources to build and upgrade facilities. At the end of each season, players can spend resources to go on seasonal events with their partner. Once a full year has passed, players add up their victory points, with the winner being declared as having experienced the most fulfilling time with their partner.

This expansion carries the identical theme and gameplay as the base game, but includes an array of new content to increase variety, such as new partners and jobs, more monsters, and a set of special events exclusive to each partner, including the ones from the base game.

This expansion also includes the new partners as NPC adventurers for games with lower player counts, integrating with the NPC adventurers introduced in the 冒険者たちの間奏曲 mini-expansion.

After the first space base was built, it didn’t take long for the corporations backing the space travel program to start eyeing bigger opportunities. With humanity finally stepping beyond Earth, these companies weren’t just thinking about exploration anymore, they were thinking about influence. And profits. Lots of profits.

Once a few planets were terraformed and early colonies started to take shape, the race was on. Everyone wanted a piece of the new frontier. It wasn’t just about sending rockets and building habitats. It was about who would control the future out there.

Pretty soon, word started to spread. Quiet at first, just whispers in the right circles: two new research stations were in the works, planned for construction in a recently discovered galaxy. Not much was known about the area, only that it was full of potential—and mystery. The kind of place that could lead to major scientific breakthroughs... or massive power grabs.

For the corporations, it was a golden opportunity. For everyone else, it raised some serious questions. Who’s really in charge out there? What are they looking for? And what happens when they find it?

Galactic Contract is an expansion for Void Ventures consisting of two new bases awaiting to be built. Bring more variety to your game stepping onto two different construction sites in space. The race for power, wealth, and control over the future of space exploration has only just begun.

—description from the publisher

Preparations for the new line of specialized space stations are finally complete. Years of planning, testing, and refining have led to this moment, but there’s a catch. The blueprints for the most critical modules, the ones that make the entire system work, are still being kept under tight lock and key. And they’re not just sitting in some corporate vault, they’re being guarded by the very inventors who designed them.

These aren’t your average engineers. They’re pioneers, brilliant minds who’ve spent decades pushing the limits of what’s possible in space tech. And while they were brought into the project for their expertise, they’ve made it very clear they aren’t just going to hand over their life’s work to the highest bidder. To them, it’s not just about the tech. It’s about control, and principles. They’ve seen what happens when powerful interests get their hands on breakthrough technology.

So now there's a standoff. The inventors are demanding something in return, respect, recognition, maybe even a seat at the table when the big decisions are made. Until then, the blueprints stay buried.

Base Modules is an expansion for Void Ventures that will present you with more challenges to overcome during the game. This expansion consists of a mission card as well as a modified gamesheet. The race for power, wealth, and control over the future of space exploration has only just begun.

—description from the publisher

An ambitious project just landed on your desk and it’s unlike anything you've ever seen before. The mission? Build the Atlas Station. Not just another outpost or research hub, but a massive, self - sustaining habitat designed to hold the entirety of humanity. This isn’t some distant dream, it’s the next step in our survival. Earth is no longer a guarantee, and Atlas is our second shot. A lifeline. The destination: a distant alien planet, light-years from home. Untouched, unpredictable, and filled with both danger and opportunity. Everything about this mission depends on cutting-edge innovation, relentless precision, and the kind of leadership that doesn’t flinch when the stakes are at their highest. This isn’t just another job. This is the future of our species and every decision from here on out could tip the scales between hope and extinction.

And here's the twist: you’re not the only one with your eyes on this station. The corporations are circling, each one hungry for influence, for control. Whoever takes the lead on Atlas won’t just be building a structure, they’ll be shaping the next era of civilization.

So, the question is: will your corporation rise to the occasion and seize that influence? Or will someone else claim the future before you even get started? The countdown has already begun.

Atlas Station is an expansion for Void Ventures that will give you a way to play in a group of 4-6 players. The race for power, wealth, and control over the future of space exploration has only just begun.

—description from the publisher

Building a research station on an alien planet is no small feat. It’s not just about putting up walls and plugging in equipment, it’s a massive, complex process that depends on materials Earth can’t easily supply, and certainly not fabricate on the fly in an unfamiliar, possibly hostile environment.

Most of the specialized components needed, reinforced alloys, advanced composites, precision instruments, can’t be made on-site. The planet simply doesn’t have the infrastructure, let alone the right conditions. That’s why everything has to be delivered from orbit, carefully prepared and shipped down in controlled drops from an automated satellite stationed above the atmosphere.

To make this work, engineers came up with a solution: an orbital elevator. It’s a towering structure built to bridge the gap between space and surface. But this isn’t your typical elevator, it's more like a high-tech catcher’s mitt. It’s designed to intercept and guide the cargo pods coming down from orbit, ensuring that even the most delicate materials land safely and exactly where they’re needed.

Without it, there’s no way to guarantee the precision or safety of the supply chain. One bad landing, and a whole shipment of vital equipment could be lost, or worse, cause damage on impact. With the orbital elevator in place, however, every drop is controlled, every delivery accounted for.

It’s the backbone of the entire operation. Without it, construction would be impossible. With it, the research station stands a chance of becoming reality.

Resource Market is a module for Void Ventures that gives players a way to acquire additional resources during the game with a deck of orbital elevator cards. The race for power, wealth, and control over the future of space exploration has only just begun.

—description from the publisher

A player play the Mime Master he don't mime, but he have to give instructions to his teammates to get them to mime situations.

There is 3 words on the card. He chooses one word and describe what all the other players have to mime.

The guy who find win one point represent by the card and become the new Mime Master.

If someone score 3 points the game end (for 7 players).

Throw a cube into the box.

If it doesn't end up flat on the bottom, you're out.

Win by placing all 10 of your cubes or by being the last one standing!

The Captains draw a Word card.

The other players give a number between 1 and 5. The Captains secretly consult the corresponding word and tell the other players the theme (Person or Character, Occupation, Sport or Hobby, Object, Food or Drink, Geography, Animal or Plant).

The Captains announce the number of letters in the word and place that many erasable "Letter" cards in a row in the center of the table. This row of cards represents the word to be found.

To guess words, like in hangman, suggest a letter. If it's in the word, write it. If it's not, receive a clue that begins with that letter.

Railheads: The Midwest is a cube rails game about the railroad expansion through the American Midwest and how it enabled efficient transportation of goods from the hinterland to major cities eastward, connecting agricultural regions and emerging industries to urban markets, facilitating trade and economic growth.

The federal and state governments played a pivotal role by offering considerable subsidies and land grants to railroad companies, incentivizing them to build tracks through less populated areas and into states that would benefit from improved access. This investment not only accelerated the construction of rail lines but also stimulated regional economies, ultimately weaving the fabric of a national market and contributing to the United States' rapid industrialization.

Through the use of a highly interactive and cascading action selection mechanism the players compete by laying tracks, crossing state lines, developing industries, and making investments with one ultimate goal in mind, to be the wealthiest magnate when the game ends.

Railheads: The Midwest is the second installment in a series of cube rails games that all share a prime action selection mechanism as well as a central theme of both railroad network and industry refinement getting developed in tandem.

—description from the designer

The Everbloom Promo Pack 1 expands Evergreen includes 1 landmark tile, 5 wildlife cards, and 10 hybrid cards.

—description from the publisher

Immerse yourself in the epic world of one of the most influential RPGs of all time - Gothic. In Gothic: A Shadow's Quest, slip into the role of a “Shadow”, an aspiring member of the Old Camp who wants to make a name for himself in the Colony. Risky tasks and deadly opponents await you in the dangerous Valley of Mines. Alone or cooperatively with up to three other players - and an optional Game Master – fight against aggressive scavengers, sneaky minecrawlers, cruel hordes of orcs, and other bloodthirsty creatures. Explore, complete quests, and develop your character; master swordplay, unleash magical powers, or wield a bow to defeat enemies from afar. The choice is yours!

Customise your Shadows according to their skills and abilities and stock up on gear in the Old Camp. Then take a Quest from one of the NPCs and your party can set out on their Journey! Explore the valley of Mines where you will find treasure and encounter characters that might help or hinder your adventures depending on your decisions. The Journey ends with a Final Encounter where you might battle a big boss monster, have to solve a series of puzzles, or destroy a magical artefact. Then return to the Old Camp to cash in your loot and level up!

—description from the publisher

To score points in LENGTH, you care only about the number of cards that you've played in your groups, whether in sets or runs.

To start, each player has a hand of eight cards from a deck with cards numbered 1-9 and 10/0, with ten copies of each card. Reveal a random score card from each of the four decks, as well as the "single line" score cards. On a turn you either:


    Draw two cards.
    Play/score/play and score.


To play, place cards in front of you in sets or runs (i.e., groups). You can have at most four groups in front of you and can discard groups at will. After you play, draw as many cards as the number of groups you played.

To score, discard groups from in front of you that match the groups depicted on a visible score card, e.g., six cards (whether in a set or run); a group of three cards and another group of three cards; or a group of three cards and a set of two cards. Each score card has a point value on it, and you draw this many cards after scoring. (Additionally, if you score only with sets instead of a mix of runs and sets, the point value doubles, but you draw only the listed number of cards.)

Your hand limit is ten cards.

In a two-player game, when the number deck runs out, whoever has the most points wins. In a game with 3+ players, whoever first scores 10+ points wins.
publishers: 新ボードゲーム党 (New Board Game Party)

description:
To win CHARGE, all players must use smart play and card abilities to make their way through the entire deck.

To set up, place ten cards face down on the table as coins, then deal each player a hand of four cards. Each card has a number and letter on it, and most have an ability. On a turn, either:


    Play a card to the table.
    Use a card ability.


When playing, if the table is empty, play any card; if a card is on the table, your card must have the same letter as or a number that's not greater than the card in play. Flip the card already in play face down as a coin, discarding it if ten coins are already in play.

To use an ability, place the card in front of you, discard coins equal to the number on the card, carry out the ability, then discard it. Abilities include clearing the table, discarding a card from hand, revealing a card in hand, exchanging cards with another player, and using the ability of the table card.

Refill your hand to four cards. If players play out all the cards in the deck and their hands, they win.
publishers: 新ボードゲーム党 (New Board Game Party)

description:
Ready, Aim, Unleash the DINOSAURS!

The Dino Pack brings two epic dino-powered weapons to help SMASH your opponent’s castle. Harness the might of the T-Rex and the Triceratops for battles that will go down in history or should we say prehistory?!

Compatible with Catapult Feud and Jurassic Feud. Includes mini game for target practice!

Inside the box you will find two dino launchers, a rubber boulder, cards and standees. Use the dino launchers instead of catapults when playing Catapult Feud, or you can use the cards and standees for target practice!

—description from the publisher

You play as rival investigators with only so many cases to go around. You must choose to play honest or play mind games with fellow players in this easy-to-pick-up game using a standard 52-card deck.

Players start with 4 cards in hand and take turns doing moves like Check the Scene or Find a Lead. These moves grant you and other players new cards to hone in on a specific set of cards, called a case. You’ll also need to use your skills as a detective to deduce what other players are trying to collect, either by reading their intentions or even looking directly at their hand! Finding out what other players are up to will keep you from going on a goose chase or tell you ahead of time how many points they may get. Information is power in this game.

If you have a valid case, you can submit it to lock the cards in. The game ends after one player submits their third case and every other player gets a final turn to submit. The highest total scoring player wins!

Moka is a board game that tests your wit and memory.

In this game, players compete to create as many words as possible from the letters arranged on a grid. But be careful, these letters will be flipped, and you'll need to remember their placement to form your words. The game is played over 5 captivating rounds, and the player with the most points at the end wins.

Designed for 2 to 4 players, Moka is ideal for word game and puzzle enthusiasts.

—description from the publisher

Carmony is a competitive and educational card game that turns music theory into an engaging challenge for 2 to 4 players, ages 12 and up. Whether you're a seasoned musician or just starting out, Carmony invites you to explore the relationships between notes, intervals, chords, and scales in a playful, strategic way.

With a unique visual system based on the chromatic wheel, players use NOTE cards and MELD cards to build musical structures. Each MELD card corresponds to a specific interval, chord, or scale, represented by a numbered sequence that can be aligned with the CHROMATIC WHEEL to visualize and assemble the correct combinations.

How it’s played:

Each player starts with a chromatic wheel, 5 MELD cards, and a set number of NOTE cards, depending on the number of players. On their turn, players draw NOTE cards and try to complete one or more MELDs—musical structures like a minor third interval, a diminished seventh chord, or a Lydian scale. Melds are played by laying down the matching NOTE cards along with the corresponding MELD card.

Players may also "eat" melds previously played by others—if they can play a more powerful structure using their own MELD card. This mechanic adds a layer of strategic depth and player interaction.

Each turn ends by discarding a NOTE card, and the round continues clockwise.

How to win:

The game is played over 3 rounds. Points are scored based on the complexity of the melds played—more notes mean higher scores—and penalties are given for unused NOTE cards. Going out (i.e., playing all your NOTE cards under certain conditions) earns bonus points. At the end of the third round, the player with the highest total score wins the game.

—description from the designer

Your dog LOVES to eat your HOMEWORK! Pick a Homework Sheet, put it in the dog’s mouth, turn his ear, then collect the shreds out of his rear end. Unscramble the shredded Homework and correctly guess the image. Turn it in before the deadline to win! Every correct guess wins a Doggie Treat.

The player with the most Doggie Treats after three rounds wins the game!

How to play:
Step 1.) Feed him the homework. The Dog Ate My Homework comes with 100 Homework Sheets with more Homework Sheets available online for download at the University Games website.

Step 2.) Turn his ear. The doggie mechanism safely shreds
Homework Sheets into 7 strips and mixes them up as they exit the dog’s rear end.

Step 3.) Unscramble the picture. You have 30 seconds to unscramble the picture and win the Doggie Treat Token.

—description from the publisher

For as long as man and beast have been companions, one crucial question has haunted them both: Who, if anyone, is a good boy? Choose your dog and begin doing sins and good deeds to show your friends your true character. Gathering positive and negative traits to discover who your dog really is. Who's a Good Boy will let you gather your friends and family to ask important questions like "Are we inherently good or evil?" or "Can people change?" or "What if a dog did sins?"

In Who's A Good Boy, you begin the game by selecting a dog character card with a unique ability. From there, each round consists of a trait being flipped. Each trait has a point value that is either positive or negative. Then, players bid on these traits using Action cards that describe sins or good deeds. Positive traits are awarded to whichever dog was best and negative traits are awarded to whichever was worst.

Once a player reached five traits, their game is over. The game continues until only one player is left without five traits, at which point players count up their trait scores, resolve any remaining events, and declare which dog is the Goodest Boy.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Am I the ASS?: The Game
description:
Step into the world of Am I the ASS?, the provocative party game that dives into real-life dilemmas and public judgment! This game features 150 authentic and often awkward scenarios, each drawn from real situations that challenge you to determine: Was this person really an ass, or did they just find themselves in a tough spot?

The twist? You're not just giving your opinion; you're predicting how 500 people from around the world reacted! Armed with your Ass-o-Meter, you'll gauge the public's sentiment on each scenario. Scenarios include dating/relationships, family dynamics, workplace conflicts, money, parenting, roommates, social etiquette, and downright bad behavior. After the reader shares the real-life situation, everyone secretly guesses: What percentage of our respondents thought the person was an ass: 25%, 50%, 75%, or... everyone? Once the results are revealed, players who guessed correctly earn tokens. The game continues until one perceptive player expertly navigates the scenarios and collects five tokens to win!

—description from the publisher

Morphid is a two-player connection game played on the spaces of a triangular grid of triangles.

On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty space or replace a weak enemy stone with a friendly stone. A stone is weak if it occupies one of the two central spaces in a four-cell rhomboid and the other three spaces in it are occupied by stones of the opposite color. A stone is also weak if it is adjacent to two stones of the opposite color and the fourth space that would complete the rhomboid is outside the board.

Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

You win if there is a chain of connected stones of your color touching all three edges of the board. Two stones are connected if the spaces they occupy share a full edge with each other. Likewise, a chain touches an edge of the board if at least one of the spaces it ocupies shares a full edge with the edge of the board. Draws are not possible.

To make the game fair, the second player will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with their opponent instead of making a regular move.

				
				
					Variants
     Triptide: To further empower defensive play, instead of flipping the weak enemy stone in a rhomboid, swap it with the friendly, non-adjacent stone in the same rhomboid. If there is no such stone because the space it would occupy is outside the board, just flip the weak enemy stone. If, after the swap, the swapped enemy stone is still weak, replace it with a friendly stone.



     Pymorph: You may flip an enemy stone to your color if it is adjacent to at least two stones of your color.


				
				
					NotesMorphid is the triangular version of the square game Morphos. Both games feature temporary interlocking deadlocks that are guaranteed to be resolvable at the latest when they reach the edge of the board. Morphid was inspired by Myramid, which is in turn one possible triangular adaptation of the Morphos variant with simplified capture formations. (Pymorph, mentioned above, is a purer triangular adaptation of this simplified Morphos. Morphid is to Morphos as Pymorph is to simplified Morphos.)

By delaying the resolution of capture fights, the more complex capture formations in Morphos and Morphid make better use of the board and provide for more sophisticated defensive play than their simplified counterparts.

—description from the designer

boardgame: L'Assemblée des Villains: Yzma & Kronk and Lama
description:
A new villain team to add to the game:

Yzma:
Yzma is awakened at the end of the night.
Once per game, you can transform another player into a llama. This player loses their power and their right to vote, but can still participate in the debate.

Kronk:
Kronk has no special skills.
Only vote against Yzma if you are sure of her alignment.

Potion/Llama:
Discard your character card. You are now a Llama. You can no longer vote, wake up at night, or use your special skill, but you can still participate in the debate.

Stomp Stomp Dino! is a cooperative survival game where players take on the role of adorable, tiny, squishy Aquilops dinosaurs trying to make it through a prehistoric panic—together. A massive volcanic eruption has plunged the valley into chaos, and now enormous adult dinos are stampeding in every direction. You and your brood must dodge danger, gather supplies, and help each other escape before the valley turns to ash.

Each round, players move through an ever-shifting landscape filled with hazards, item pickups, and wild dino behaviors. You’ll mold your own custom dino minis, move around the board, and work as a team to outmaneuver unpredictable stampede events, navigate terrain, and reach safety before you run out of lives and get turned into dino pancakes! With variable card draws, a dynamic board, and escalating stakes, no two games play out the same.

Stomp Stomp Dino is easy to learn and full of heart. Its tactile, playful components make it instantly fun for kids and families, while layered decisions and teamwork keep things engaging for more strategic players.

—description from the designer

Glass blocks were a hit! But now customers want glass-only patterns in their mosaics too...

The Vidraçaria (Glassworks) expansion adds 4 new cards with patterns that are made only with glass blocks. The chosen pattern is added to the game (totaling 4 pattern cards to be completed) and the rest are used as cobogó cards.

—description from the publisher

The client just bought this beautiful window for the mosaic! How it will fit in is not my problem...

The Janelão (Big Window) expansion adds 2 new cards with a new element: a window that is twice the size of a normal cobogó. The window pattern is used as one of the 3 patterns in the game, which must be filled with the same color of cobogó of your choice.

—description from the publisher

Fame has finally arrived! New special clients have arrived looking for exclusive mosaics!

The Clientes Especiais (Special Clients) expansion adds 3 new pattern cards inspired by other games: Capivárias, Malabares (from the Piquinim Collection) and Vomitland (from Cyber ​​Rhino Studios). These patterns are larger than the ones in the base game, but can be made with any color of cobogó.

—description from the publisher

Safari Spaceship is a fast-paced, card-driven game set on the vibrant world of Safaria, where diverse animal species have united for a bold mission: deep space exploration.

As a newly appointed Commander of a state-of-the-art vessel, your mission is to recruit the right combination of crew and passengers to claim a new planet — one that’s perfectly suited for a select few species under your care. But you’re not alone in reaching out for the stars and competition is fierce!

Each turn, you'll attempt to steal cards from opponents to assemble your team, balance your ship’s advanced systems, and prepare to launch to your destination planet. Choose wisely — each animal brings unique strengths, and matching the right passengers to the right planets is key to victory. From brilliant sloths and manatees to daring penguins, tigers, pandas, and hippos, your ship will be filled with personality, charm, and tactical potential.

Safari Spaceship blends intuitive icon-based gameplay with quick decisions and high replayability. Rules scale in complexity to allow for younger players to have fun, while still offering an approachable structure that makes it easy to teach, while offering strategic depth that evolves with each play.

GOOD LUCK! And may the cosmos guide you.

—description from the publisher

A western boom town falls square onto a poker table!

     Most points wins!
     Worker placement with poker chips!
     Multi use cards!
     Super streamlined design!
     Generous doses of engine-building and player interaction!


follow the design journey on Instagram! @theLimestoneCowboy

—description from the designer

Capybaras vs Pelicans is a lighthearted card game in which players guide cheeky capybaras safely to a hot spring while avoiding other players' pesky pelicans.

Players can play capybaras from their hand or use a pelican to claim one from an opponent. But beware - until the capybaras make it to a hot spring, other players can steal capybaras back. Once all cards have been played, the player with the most capybaras safely at a hot spring wins the game.

Strategic use of pelicans and timely hot springs can make the difference between victory and defeat. With a simple setup and fast-paced play, the game is suitable for a wide range of players.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In this competitive tower-building game, players construct towering structures while strategically placing monsters to gain abilities and earn points. The game features a single type of card, which serves a dual purpose: Monster Cards on the front and Tower Cards on the back. Players must carefully decide how to use each card to maximize their tower’s strength and scoring potential.

Gameplay Overview:
Card Usage: Each card has two sides—one representing a monster, the other forming part of the tower. Players must strategically choose whether to play the card as a monster to gain special abilities or use it to build their tower for structural stability.

Monster Abilities: Monsters offer unique effects that enhance the tower, disrupt opponents, or increase scoring potential.

Scoring System: Players earn points based on the monsters they place and the stability of their tower.

Victory Condition: The game ends after a set number of turns The player with the highest score is crowned the Master Tower Builder!

The Heroclix Yu-Gi-Oh! Starter Set combines the strategic depth of Heroclix with the vibrant world of the Yu-Gi-Oh! anime and trading card game. Players take on the roles of iconic duelists and monsters, represented as collectible miniatures, to engage in tactical battles on a grid-based map. Each figure has unique stats and abilities, reflecting their traits from the Yu-Gi-Oh! universe, making team-building an essential part of the strategy.

The gameplay revolves around positioning, resource management, and timing. Players use action tokens to move, attack, or activate special abilities of their figures. Combat is resolved using dice rolls and a variety of attack and defense stats, making every battle a blend of skill and chance. Players must balance offensive and defensive strategies while navigating terrain and line-of-sight mechanics.

The goal of the game is to outmaneuver and defeat the opponent’s team by strategically using each figure’s abilities to deal damage, control the battlefield, and achieve scenario-specific objectives. Victory can be achieved by eliminating all opponent figures or fulfilling alternate win conditions, depending on the game setup.

This starter set serves as an excellent entry point for new players, offering a self-contained experience with pre-assembled teams and all necessary components. It also provides a gateway for fans to expand their collection and dive deeper into the wider Heroclix community.

boardgame: Light in the Dark: Fires of Thokaia
description:
Fires of Thokaia expands the world of Light in the Dark with a gripping new chapter. A mysterious faction known as the Cult of Ember has emerged in the region of Thokaia, preaching salvation through flame and sacrifice. Their influence is spreading rapidly - corrupting townsfolk, manipulating the desperate, and waging a silent war against the healers who stand in their way.

As the plague doctor, you must navigate this new treacherous land filled with dark rituals, indoctrinated followers, and corrupted beasts. Unravel the cult's secrets through new narrative-driven quests, face off against unique enemies, and acquire powerful new abilities that test your resolve and strategy in fresh, challenging ways.

With more content, deeper choices, and heightened danger, Fires of Thokaia adds a darker edge to your journey. The Cult of Ember burns from within - will you extinguish the flame or be consumed by it?

—description from the publisher

Players race to collect either four cards with the same number or five cards of the same color by drawing from a shared display row. Each turn, take a card and choose whether to keep it or place it in the display for others to potentially claim.
The tension comes from the "O!" rule: when one card away from winning, players must shout "O!" or forfeit the game if caught. This creates a delicate balance between progressing toward victory and revealing your position to opponents.

Action cards add strategic depth - steal cards with Thief, peek at opponents' hands with Wise Vision, or protect yourself with Defense cards. The Joker acts as a wild card for any color or number, while Penalty cards can derail your progress.
The scoring system rewards finishing order across multiple rounds, with points allocated based on player count. Success requires careful hand management, timing your "O!" declaration, and using action cards to disrupt opponents while protecting your own collection.

—description from the publisher

DuetO! is an extraordinary family game that challenges players to use their observation and reflex skills. The game revolves around finding matching symbols between any two cards as quickly as possible. More than just a game, it's an interactive learning experience that introduces players to interesting facts about the three Baltic States: Lithuania, Latvia, and Estonia.
Players race to identify common symbols across cards, creating an engaging and fast-paced gameplay that tests both speed and perception. The game consists of 31 unique cards, each featuring 6 different pictures related to the Baltic States, making it both an entertaining game and an educational tool.
The game ends when the last card is taken, with the player who has collected the most cards declared the winner. DuetO! offers a unique blend of quick thinking, cultural learning, and family fun.

—description from the publisher

Players compete to quickly find words matching both a category and specific letter requirements. Each round presents a category card and three letter requirement cards - one vowel and two consonants that the word must contain.
The tension lies in balancing speed with accuracy. Players who shout words meeting fewer than all three requirements trigger a 30-second timer, during which others can only propose words with more letter matches. Meeting all three requirements wins instantly, but partial matches create dramatic countdown moments.

Double-sided cards provide scalable difficulty: blue sides offer common categories and frequent letters, while green sides challenge players with rare letters and obscure categories. This allows mixed-skill groups to compete fairly.
Victory requires quick thinking under pressure as players mentally scan their vocabulary for words that thread the needle between category constraints and letter requirements. The first to collect five category cards wins.

—description from the publisher

Aventure Around the World is game number 13 in Bandai's Part Joy series.

This is a game where the players go on an adventure and travel to different destinations around the world. While doing so they maintain their supplies, avoid accidents, and take chances, all in an attempt to be the first player to reach Tokyo by Space Shuttle.

Chessetto is a new card-based chess game. Chess is a game of strategy and mental skill played between two players on an 8 x 8 space board. However, in Chessetto, there is only one space as the starting board, and the players themselves build the board by adding spaces turn by turn (build). Furthermore, there are no pieces on the board at the start of the game, so players gradually add their pieces to the board throughout the game (recruit). Finally, opponents move their pieces from one locker to another, as is normally done in chess (move).

—description from the designer

boardgame: Ultimatum: can you turn the tide?
description:
In Ultimatum the world is on the brink of disaster. As members of the global climate prevention team, your mission is to combat the most severe natural disasters of our time. Floods, hurricanes, and bushfires threaten cities worldwide, and every decision you make could be the difference between survival and catastrophe. This is your ultimatum - can you turn the tide?

The game board represents various cities across the globe. On each turn, players must choose between taking preventive actions in their current city or traveling to assist teammates elsewhere. The unpredictability of Mother Nature means that disasters can strike anywhere, at any time, with devastating effects. Players must collaborate, adapt their strategies, and act swiftly. Each turn presents new challenges, requiring players to reconsider their plans and work together to save the world. Only with the right strategy, you can prevent the destruction and ensure a thriving future for humanity.

To win the game, your team must secure three regions on the board and complete the selected mission, before three regions are lost or the mission becomes impossible to complete.

Every game is unique, demanding fresh strategies and teamwork. Are you ready to turn the tide and save the world?

—description from the publisher

Cross Clues is a cooperative party game about making connections between clues. Work together to fill in the grid of code words by giving single word clues that represent the crossing of two different code words in the grid. Consider your clues carefully and think as a team to fill in as much of the grid as possible! Thousands of different combinations guarantee endless fun!

In this special version, get ready for 200 new words that delve into the universe of science, history and technology - all with Super's creative and striking touch. Want more? Combine different versions of Cross Clues and create your own game!

—description from the publisher

SETI: Space Agencies, an expansion for SETI: Search for Extraterrestrial Intelligence, introduces powerful asymmetric organizations — each with unique abilities, strategic advantages, and starting conditions that redefine how you approach the search for alien life.

As the leader of one of these institutions — ranging from private tech companies to global research networks — you'll bring a distinct perspective and specialized tools to the mission, unlocking bold new strategies and rewarding long-term planning.

In addition to these organizations, the expansion adds three new alien species, each with their own unique twists, and over forty new project cards inspired by real-world initiatives. These integrate seamlessly into the main deck, providing even greater variety and strategic options.

"Quick Start" cards, designed for use with the organizations, let you skip the slower opening round and dive straight into the action. They introduce an extra layer of asymmetry during set-up and can be used to further accelerate games in lower player counts.

Finally, the expansion introduces signal tokens, a subtle but meaningful new mechanism that enhances scanning efficiency.

Whether you're adapting to your organization's strengths or navigating fresh interstellar challenges, SETI: Space Agencies deepens the strategic landscape of your mission to discover life beyond Earth.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Are you a fearless trailblazer or perhaps a complete daredevil? If so, Corp Incorporated invites you to...do what?! More trucking!

In Galaxy Trucker: Do What?!, you'll get the chance to undertake missions that might — quite literally — blow your mind. Escort aliens with special powers, build crazy ships beyond human imagination, place bets, and laugh in the face of danger. Win the annual Transgalactic Trek — the most prestigious trucking achievement in the known universe — and join Club Gold! Or at least cross the finish line on a toilet seat, but alive.

If hauling boombastic or suspiciously glowing cargo isn't wild enough, take it up a notch with some supercards! After all, what's a galactic mission without a good old meteor shower, eh?

We all know how important connections are in life. On your adventures, you'll meet many high-profile VIPs and even have the opportunity to earn some serious cash by becoming their space taxi driver! Some of the prominent passengers just want to have fun, even if it means smuggling goods in pipes, boosting weapons, or flying away with a part of your ship!

P.S. Having quality life insurance is highly recommended...just in case.

Galaxy Trucker: Do What!? is the second expansion for the new edition of Galaxy Trucker, offering even more opportunities to gain riches and new ways to destroy your run-down ship. This expansion features two big modules for seasoned truckers: Missions and VIP Astronauts, as well as a turn-based mode for a more laid-back experience.

The Galaxy Trucker (Second Edition) base game in required for play, and this expansion is compatible with Galaxy Trucker: Keep on Trucking.

—description from the publisher

Leuchtturm voraus! (Beacon Patrol) is a co-op tile-laying exploration game in which you navigate the coast of the North Sea to secure its beacon buoys, lighthouses and waterways.

You are captains of the Coast Guard. Together you check beacon buoys and lighthouses to ensure the safety of the North Sea coast. You place your tiles next to tiles that are already placed, move your ships, and explore the sea.

Your goal is to explore as many tiles as possible. A tile is considered explored when it’s connected to other tiles on all four of its sides.

—description from the designer

This German edition includes the base game and the expansion Ships & Shores.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Indomitable Survivor: Tachyon Nodachi Rubi
description:
Tachyon Nodachi - Rubi is packaged in a graphic box that includes:

1 x Tachyon Nodachi - Rubi hard plastic miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
1 x Neutron Seed Phoenix indomitable resource card
1 x Tachyon Nodachi pattern card
1 x Tachyon Nodachi gear card
1 x Install guide card
1 x Large matte art print

Description
Infinite reflections of kaleidoscopic colors blinded her eyes. She shut them to the painful light, feeling the strange current carrying her through the monster's mystifying bowels. Visions of her own death visited her panicked mind, replicating, rewinding, rewriting her gruesome ending until the epiphany froze her body. Its sudden stillness loosed her, slipping faster and faster for interminable ages until the pain of impact upon the stone-faced ground woke her. The monster wheeled around, straining its neck to peck open her heaving chest. The fatal wound was, but then was not. She struck back, spilling the monster's shimmering guts at her feet. From the mess she plucked a seed that blinked in and out of existence even as she held it fast in her fist.

The game consists of 100 double-sided cards with images on them. Depending on the number of players, 8, 10, or 12 of them are laid out on the table. Each player divides them into pairs by whatever criteria they want and writes down the pairs. When everyone is done, pairs are compared. Each pair that at least one other player also noted (for whatever reason) yields 1 point. 
The game ends at the end of the round in which a player has at least 12 points. Whoever has most points, wins.

boardgame: Dr. Finn's Book of Solo Strategy and Word Games
description:
Dr. Finn's Book of Solo Strategy and Word Games contains eight solo games that can be played in the book without cutting them out. Each game uses one sheet of paper and requires only 1-5 dice and a pencil. The book contains eight copies of each game, as well as a score chart to track your scores. The games are:


     Cosmic Run: Mission One
     Spell It Out
     Pen Pals
     Crunch the Numbers
     My Perfect City
     Leftover Letters
     The Little Flower Shop: Open for Business
     Word Wrap


All of the games except Cosmic Run: Mission One are also playable as multiplayer solitaire. In this case, the player sheets need to be cut out of the book and/or copied.

Taiwan’s diverse terrain and climate have nurtured over 400 species of butterflies, including more than 50 endemic species. Records of butterflies in Taiwan date back to the Qing Dynasty, and during the 1950s, large-scale collecting and exports earned the island the nickname “The Kingdom of Butterflies.”
In this game, players travel back to early 20th-century Taiwan as butterfly catchers, collecting and preserving specimens while racing to publish their research before others.
The game is played over several rounds. Each round, players use their starting hand of butterfly cards and try to form as many specimen sets as possible by drawing cards or using discarded cards from other players to exchange for prestige points.
The first player to turn their entire hand into specimen sets ends the round and also earns bonus prestige points from the other players.

—description from the publisher

Consiglio is a fast-paced card game of subtle influence, calculated risks, and shifting loyalties set in a fictional Renaissance city inspired by Venice at its peak. Players assume the roles of ambitious nobles, guildmasters, and social climbers vying for power within the Grand Council — the Consiglio. In this world of masked intentions and whispered deals, only the most cunning can rise to the top.

Gameplay centers around playing cards in ascending order, with the option to bluff by playing a card face-down and declaring its rank. Players may challenge each other's claims, but doing so comes with risk: expose a liar, and they must pick up the stack — but if you wrongly accuse, the penalty is yours. Matching the previously played number unlocks powerful special effects, such as stealing cards, skipping rivals, or manipulating the hand of fate.

The goal is simple: Be the first player to play all of your cards and emerge as the dominant force in the council chambers.

Consiglio offers sharp strategic play, social deduction, and elegant interaction in a compact format.

Touché: The Swashbucklers is a fast-paced, two-player showdown in which legendary swashbucklers from history and film clash steel in cinematic duels. Use strategic fencing maneuvers, deliver cutting taunts to throw your opponent off balance, and fight across dynamic locations — from bustling town squares to perilous pirate ships. Outsmart, outmaneuver, and out duel your rival in a game of wit, style, and steel.

Squillo, the irreverent game that, with thousands of copies sold each year, makes politicians and consumer associations tremble, returns in a definitive and celebratory edition: Squillo Deluxe – Trilogy, ready for its third parliamentary questioning!

Squillo is an extreme-spirited card game where you take on the role of a madam or pimp, managing your stable blow by blow, made up of escorts, streetwalkers, and rising stars—each with their own traits, rates, and final earnings in case of... organ trafficking.

This new edition features the 110 best cards, selected from the base game and its first three expansions. It includes events (situations and mishaps) and power-ups (health points and enhancements). The goal is to defeat the other players by maneuvering your squillo within the limits and abilities dictated by their character cards. The game offers two modes: the standard level, ideal for newcomers, and the advanced level, designed for experienced players seeking to add a strategic layer to the pure fun.

The Urban Calculus (TUC) is a wargame and a playable, competitive systems model. It allows players to examine and consider some of the linkages between what is happening in the urban battle(s) and what the effects are on the civilians, infrastructure, society (of all combatants), global opinion and government will.

Initially 4 scenarios have been developed for TUC:

An introductory scenario -  a relatively balanced potential conflict with the defender heavily invested in success
An asymmetric scenario – where not all parts of the model exist, and some linkages may be non-existent
Two linked alliance scenarios, one with an army defending its own city, and one where another one country is helping to defend the city.

The aim with TUC is very much to allow people to modify not just the values on the linkages between the different factors and capabilities, but also to modify the core model to reflect their own needs and concerns.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Blood Red Skies: Heinkel HE 111H Squadron
description: The HE 111 was the Luftwaffe's most numerous medium bomber at the opening of World War 2. Later, the HE 111 was moved into various roles including maritime patrol and transport/logistics

You have traveled to the Fair Trade Town of Kamakura! As your train home rapidly approaches, you must hurry and buy souvenirs. You want to buy sets of 3, but whenever you spot a Fairtrade product, you can't resist picking it up! Along the way, kids, cats, and all kinds of unexpected trouble arises... In this traditional-style board game, made with environmentally friendly paper, you can also learn about Fairtrade.

Fairtrade Souvenirs is an adaptation of Oink Games' 2024 release, Souvenirs from Venice. Roll the dice and move your piece while searching for souvenirs. Your goal is to collect 3 of the same souvenir (from 6 different types) before all of the tiles have been flipped over.

Up to here, it's the same as "Souvenirs from Venice, but in Fairtrade Souvenirs special souvenirs with the Fairtrade Mark*¹ have been added. These special souvenirs cost 2 coins to purchase, but if one is included in a set of 3 matching souvenirs, you earn bonus points. You might be collecting a different type of souvenir, but then come across a souvenir with Fairtrade Mark and want to buy it. Or you might notice when another player secretly buys something for 2 coins. This brings a new layer of fun to the game.


    1 Products that meet standards such as fair payment to manufacturers and protection of working conditions carry The FAIRTRADE Mark.

Enter a world of cunning strategy and ruthless ambition, where the throne hangs in the balance and every choice could lead to triumph or ruin. Royal Gambit is a dark fantasy game of combat and deception for 2–6 players, blending social deduction, resource management, and tactical decision making.

Will You Reign Supreme?

Lead your Royal—A mighty King or Queen and command an array of unique characters with powerful abilities and counteractions. Bluff, bargain, and betray your way to victory by eliminating your rivals to secure your place on the throne.

—description from the designer
Principati + is an expansion for Principati Duello. It includes the components for the 3rd, 4th, 5th, or 6th player. It can only be played with Principati Duello and is not a standalone game.

boardgame: Oshi Push: Birthday Special Promo Card
description:
Available through the Japanime Games Patreon.

OPDD/PC1-119

1 Coins, Creative, 3 Stars

While this card is on an Independent Platform:
This card gains +3 Stars

boardgame: Kamigami Battles: Oberon, King of the Fairies
description:
Released via the Japanime Games Patreon.

Oberon, King of the Fairies is promo card # KBP-021

Act: Pay 1. Energy Reveal the top 3 cards of your Draw deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.
React: Pay 1 Energy to Discard a Defending Disciple.

Pilastri is an abstract game for two players played on a square board with an even number of spaces. Each player controls one of the two colors of pieces, which start in a checkered pattern on the board.

The goal of Pilastri is to outlast your opponent. A player without a legal move on their turn loses the game. Passing is not allowed.

A game of Pilastri on a 6x6 board takes about 15-20 minutes to play.

On your turn, take any one of your pieces from the top of a stack, leaving behind any pieces that it may have been on top of, and move it left, right, up, or down any distance, placing it on top of the first stack of pieces it encounters.

The following must also be true for the move to be legal:

     The piece must end higher up than where it started.
     A piece that starts without anything beneath it may only be placed on top of a stack containing exactly one piece.


—description from the designer

Principati - Cacciatore is an expansion for Principati duello. It adds new terrain tiles, the hunter's house, and new game components to the game. The box contains components for two players, along with separate rules. It can only be played with Principati duello and is not a standalone game.

Step into the warm, fragrant air of a bustling Hong Kong bakery, where golden baked goods await. Armed with tongs and a plastic tray, weave through the aisles, choosing between pineapple buns, egg tarts, and sausage buns. Once you skip a baked good, there’s no turning back. Don’t dawdle! Race to the end and pick your favourites. With tongs in hand in the bustling heat, the bakery’s flavours are yours to greet. Will your tray hold bites so sweet, or will you be left with nothing to eat?

The goal of the game is to score the most points. Points are earned by collecting cards, forming combos, or grabbing limited special cards. You can travel as far down the line of cards as you like—but once you pass a card, there’s no going back. With a limit of twelve cards, every choice counts.

—description from the publisher

Visit your local Hong Kong ice parlour and cool off with a refreshing drink. Assemble your favourite milk tea, mango sago, or red bean ice! Scoop up ice cream, drizzle condensed milk, and toss in some grass jelly—but stay sharp! Every choice counts, as one quick swap can change everything. With each step, you’ll need memory, strategy, and a little luck to stay ahead. Outmaneuver your rivals and watch them slip up. Do you have what it takes to keep your cool?

A card game with a twist, you hold a bunh of cards in your hand which you cannot use. You have to use the cards from your opponents hands! As you take turns picking tools from each other, you slowly collect the right ingridients to complete your drink. The first person to finish all their drinks first will win the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Age of Steam Deluxe: Expansion Volume VI
description:
Seven additional expansion maps for Age of Steam Deluxe.

Expansion Volume VI includes:

     Ireland 
     Sicily  
     Belgium
     Warsaw
     Roundhouse - a rethemed version of Steampunk
     1830's Pennsylvania  
     Atlantic City 


The Expansion Volume includes updated versions of classic Age of Steam maps adapted to the new Deluxe art style. These maps cover a wide range of play counts and styles - each with its own unique twists and gameplay. The Expansion Volumes also includes a storage box to store the maps.

--information from publisher

boardgame: Attack on Gihren: One Year War Solitaire
description:
Attack on Gihren is a solitaire game set in the Gundam Universe.  It is based on the Nobunaga solitaire game from K2 Publishing.

The player takes charge as the head of the Zabi Family and commander in chief of the Zeon forces during the One-Year War. To win, the player must utilize their finances to build weapons and acquire technological might, and then with that, utilize their forces to overthrow the Federation.

The game is a doujin, which was only produced for sale at Japanese game shows, such as Tokyo Game Market.  The owner must assemble the component, in the case the map and counters, while the cards are already cut and laminated.

The kit includes an 8-page rulebook, an arrival table, events table, a sheet of counters, two maps, and 30 cards.

Two and a Half Truths is a party game for everyone from long-time friends to first-time acquaintances. Teams of two work together to determine two unique things the partners have in common, then choose a third thing that is only true for one of them.

The pair secretly writes their "two and a half" truths on the game's large (15" x 21") game board with the included dry erase markers, out of sight of players.

Then, all other players have two guesses for which is which, leaving a mark under their guesses. Points are scored based on whether players figured out which truth was a lie, with additional points awarded if they can guess which player it was false for.

Then it's time to pass the board to the next team of two and see how well you know them!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Предназначение: Дополнения "Ведьмин лес: Всё то золото", "Препятствия", "Способности" (Destinies: Modules)
description:
Предназначение: Дополнения "Ведьмин лес: Всё то золото", "Препятствия", "Способности" ("Destinies: Modules") includes:

     the expansion Destinies: Witchwood – All That Glitters Scenario Pack
     the expansion Adversity Module
     the expansion Character Traits Module


•••
Предназначение: Дополнения "Ведьмин лес: Всё то золото", "Препятствия", "Способности" включает в себя:

     дополнение "Ведьмин лес: Всё то золото"
     дополнение "Препятствия"
     дополнение "Способности"

Outwit rival dragons and build a hoard of glittering gems in this fast double trick-taking game for 2-6 players!

Play cards from your hand that match at least one dragon on the Lead Card. Each round offers new chances to collect glittering games through Crests and the value of the dragons you play - high or low. But beware... your fellow dragons are dreadfully tricky.

—description from the publisher

Can someone please tell those on the river that the babies are here?

The Capicreche (Capydaycare) expansion adds new types of capybaras to the river. Capiticos (baby capybaras) accompany adult capybaras of their herds and protect them from being pushed, while the Capissor (capybara teacher) pulls a Capitico and its guardian back to the starting segment.

The great clown Piolin took a break from the circus to meet the Capivárias! But he's different...

The Capiolins expansion adds a new type of capybara to the game: the Capiolins gives positive or negative points to the side of the river where it ends the round, but each push makes them change sides. This expansion is inspired by the game Malabares, which is also part of the Piquinim Collection.

—description from the publisher

As if there weren't enough onças in this river, now there are more? You better watch out!

The Capirigo (Capy-peril) expansion adds 3 new capionça cards to the game, with new effects. They can be used in place of or in conjunction with the capionças from the base game.

—description from the publisher

The ticket booth attendant also wants to try his luck as a clown! With more competition, who will star alongside Piolin as the new juggler?

The Quarto Malabarista (4th Juggler) expansion adds 2 new cards to allow Malabares to be played with 4 players.

—description from the publisher

Isn't this cobogó too heavy to juggle? I don't know if that will work, huh?

The Cobográn Finale expansion brings a new cobogó card, which takes the place of the club in the base game. Using the cobogó, the Piolin Move becomes riskier, but it also guarantees an automatic victory for whoever manages to do it. This expansion is inspired by the game Cobogó, which is also part of the Piquinim Collection.

—description from the publisher
The Foundations expansion takes the towers of Rise of Babel and condenses them onto a unique two-sided game mat.

Throughout the modular expansion you can: Gain a prophecy and fulfill it at the end of the game, obtain a scroll resource and craft unique gifts.

—description from the publisher

Summit is a playable, tradable, and collectable government themed card game where players manage their own fictional Country using a variety of cards to implement strategies. Summit aims to simulate all the responsibilities a government has when managing a Country.

Economy: ​

Each player starts with a balance of 25 GP, Each card you play, except for random event cards, has an associated GP cost to play.​

Trade:​

Players simulate trade by implementing supply chains and exchanging or selling cards to one another.​

Conflicts:​

Players use a wide range of Military Assets to implement offensive and  defensive strategies against one another.​

Alliances:​

Players can form trade and military agreements/alliances using certain cards.

boardgame: Godzilla: Kaiju on the Earth LEGENDS
description:
Kaiju on the Earth LEGENDS (Godzilla) is a competitive and cooperative game between one monster player and a team of 1 to 4 humans.

The monster attacks the people of Tokyo while destroying famous places all over the city.
Its gigantic body destroys everything just by walking, and the radiation from its mouth causes great damage all over the place.

On the human side, the governors of Tokyo, Saitama, Chiba, and Kanagawa form a team and work together to deal with the monster disaster.
They predict the monster's invasion and secure evacuation routes while avoiding damage. They help evacuees to the surrounding prefectures.
By helping the reporters, their reports touch the heart of Hiroshi Serizawa, leading to the use of the ultimate biological destruction weapon, the Oxygen Destroyer.

Godzilla or a scientific weapon? What will be the outcome of this epic battle of fear and terror?

Titan Zero is a cooperative game for 1-5 players about heroes fighting gigantic Titans to save Earth. It includes evolving missions, a customizable hero setup and an exiting new dice selection mechanic called Dice Dillema.

The Dice Dilemma means that you roll all dice and assign them to the Titan´s actions. Each player then steals 1 die from the Titan - what you leave to the Titan, it uses for its own actions! It´s not just luck - but consequences.

There are no standard hero classes in TITAN ZERO, but rather unique toolkits. Every hero brings unique mechanics, that no other character offers. Each combination opens new strategies - and new ways to survive and win.

Players must wound the Titan six times to achieve victory. If the Titan rampages across the globe, evolves beyond control, or three heroes fall in battle, it's game over!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Crime Files: Fallakte – Verliebt, verlobt, verwitwet
description:
A young woman is found dead on the morning after her wedding. The husband, who was apparently drunk, hardly has any memories of what happened the night before. 
The players are a team of investigators that follows evidence, analyzes testimonies and tries to find out who among the wedding guests is behind the tragic death of the bride.

A series of strange burglaries occurs in a small town, but only items of low value are stolen. 
Over the course of 24 days, the players follow the traces of the burglar, listen to witness reports and analyze documents to find out what is behind the mysterious crimes.
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
Artificial intelligence (AI) is a hot topic and the discussion about its capabilities is roaring. But what is the hype really all about? Find out in the card game AI-Compass! Take on the role of ultimate AI experts and help citizens with their queries. Your mission: find out which problems can actually be solved by AI systems. But beware! Not every task is suitable for AI. It's up to you to decide which challenges might be best solved without artificial intelligence. Whoever proposes the most convincing solutions and in the end has dealt the most cards will become employee of the day and win the game! Let your imagination run free and find creative ways through the uncharted territory of artificial intelligence.

The AI-Compass is not just about fun and entertainment, but also offers a realistic perspective on active AI systems and their importance for the common good. Learn about the many ways AI can positively impact our society and where we might in fact not need it.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Künstliche Intelligenz (KI) ist in aller Munde und die Diskussion um ihre Fähigkeiten ist groß. Doch was ist wirklich dran an dem Hype? Findet es im Kartenspiel KI-Kompass heraus! Hier schlüpft ihr in die Rolle von ultimativen KI-Expert*innen und helft Bürger*innen bei ihren Anfragen. Eure Mission: Herauszufinden, welche Probleme wirklich von KI-Systemen gelöst werden können. Doch Vorsicht! Nicht jede Aufgabe ist für KI geeignet. Es liegt an euch aufzudecken, welche Herausforderungen sich ohne Künstliche Intelligenz am besten bewältigen lassen. Wer von euch die überzeugendsten Lösungen vorschlägt und am Ende die meisten KI-Karten vor sich ausgespielt hat, wird Mitarbeiter*in des Tages und gewinnt das Spiel! Lasst eure Fantasie sprudeln und findet gemeinsam kreative Wege durch das Neuland der Künstlichen Intelligenz.

Beim KI-Kompass geht es nicht nur um Spaß und Unterhaltung, sondern auch um einen realistischen Blick auf aktive KI-Systeme und ihre Bedeutung für das Gemeinwohl. Lernt die vielfältigen Möglichkeiten kennen, wie KI unsere Gesellschaft positiv beeinflussen kann und wo wir sie vielleicht auch gar nicht brauchen.

—description from the publisher
publishers: Alexander von Humboldt Institut für Internet und Gesellschaft gGmbH

description:
Another game in the Uzzle Games series: 

     Puzzle + Stacking Challenge
     Takes Only 1 Minute To Learn
     4 Difficulty Levels For All Ages
     Perfect For Family Game Night


How to Play:

     Step 1: Choose a Difficulty - Mix the structure cards for your chosen difficulty and place them in the center. Players can choose different levels based on their skill.
     Step 2: Get Your Blocks - Each player receives two blocks of each color, ensuring no two blocks of the same color share a pattern.
     Step 3: Draw and Place Cards - Draw a challenge card and place it face down.
     Step 4: Start Building! - At the countdown, flip your card and start building the structure. Match your blocks to the pattern on the card.



     Scoring and Continuing: The first to finish shouts "Blockwork!" Others verify the structure. Correct builders set their card aside for points, incorrect attempts continue the round.
     Winning: Collect three correct cards in your score pile to win.
     Special Blocks: Silver blocks on cards mean any colored block can be used if it matches the required pattern.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Crime Files: Fallakte – Atemlos im Allgäu
description:
In a small town on the edge of the Alps, a bar owner is stabbed to death. The next day, another corpse is found floating in a nearby lake. Is there a connection between the two deaths?

Players are a team of investigators and examine photos, testimonies and forensic reports to solve the case(s).

In a small town on the German North Sea Coast, a real estate agent is found dead. He leaves behind a wife, a lover, a former business partner and disgruntled customers. Who could be behind the murder?

Players are a team of investigators and examine photos, testimonies and forensic reports to solve the case.

Zen e Raiva is an educational game that helps children with emotional development in a therapeutic environment.

We all have dragons inside of us. They show up when we feel angry. They are not villains, but guardians of something that is important to us. In Zen e Raiva, you embark on an emotional journey where you learn to recognize, understand and turn these dragons into allies.
