In this engaging and thought-provoking game, players use the "Zens", wise little characters who surround the dragons in order to calm down the anger before any havoc is wreaked. The mission? To achieve emotional balance and victory, both in game and in real life.

Players will be rolling dice and using their dice in order to add their Zens to the board and surround the dragons that are in there. When a dragon is surrounded, it is removed from the board and the player with the most Zens around it gains a Necessity card. The first player with 3 of the same type of Necessity cards or 4 different types wins the game.

—description from the designer

Going Viral is a competitive tableau-building game where you and your fellow players become up-and-coming travel influencers racing to gain likes and gain fame during your whirlwind trip across Europe. Your goal? Hit one million likes before anyone else—and leave your digital footprint all over Social Media.

In Going Viral, each turn is a new opportunity to grow your brand. Strategically post your content from real-world destinations:  Show your followers the mouthwatering meals, stylish boutique stays, and thrilling local attractions you experience in your journey. Post with Purpose - optimize your tableau for maximum engagement, and crush the competition with your creativity and timing.

Beware—trending events can throw your perfect feed into chaos. One minute you’re crushing it with food posts in Paris, and the next you’re opponents hiking challenge in the Alps is stealing the spotlight.

Earn Victory Points for hitting content goals, building unique feeds, and mastering the ever-changing algorithm. When any player reaches one million likes, the game ends—but only the most savvy influencer will claim ultimate victory through their consistent on brand posting and the accumulation of vital victory points.

Featuring vivid artwork, modern mechanics, and a theme that’s as timely as it is entertaining, Going Viral is perfect for fans of games like Earth, Wingspan, and Trekking the World. If you’ve ever dreamed of going viral without turning on a computer, now’s your chance.

—description from the designer

Championship for tiny geniuses.
With questions on sports and entertainment.

Il campionato dei piccoli geni.
Con domande anche su sport e spettacolo.

First detective case released by Paupio Detektyvai.
A pretty easy case, perfect for introduction of to "Paupio Detektyvų" games.

Story:
Detective, we need your help.

A horrific crime shook the village of Jurgaičiai when archaeologist Airidas Girinskas was found murdered in his home.

—description from the publisher

An easy to learn family/party card shedding game with artwork modelled after the classic inspector comic books like Dick Tracy.

Hide the Body is a fast-paced card game where players race to dispose of their victims’ bodies while trying to direct the Inspector and the Cadaver Dog to discover and dig up bodies hidden by their opponents!

Each player starts with six bodies. Players (the villains) have to dispose of them and have a limited number of holes in which to hide the bodies.  They can work together to get their bodies safely buried or get the Inspector or his cadaver dog to dig up their opponents’ bodies!

The first player to get rid of all six bodies is the winner.

—description from the designer

A fast paced, easy to learn family/party card game with game play and artwork modeled after George Orwell’s “Animal Farm”.  Like in spades or hearts, players race to shed cards in suit-based runs.  If a player can close a run using Napolean, Snowball or Squealer (i.e. any pig but Major) that player may set a rule.
While the pigs set the rules, other animals have special abilities that keep the game chaotic and unpredictable.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Возрождение галактики: Набор мини-дополнений (Galactic Renaissance: Expansions Set)
description:
Возрождение галактики: Набор мини-дополнений ("Galactic Renaissance: Expansions Set") includes:

     the expansion 5th Player
     the expansion Cosmic Rabbit Game Up
     the expansion Warmonger Game Up
     the expansion Pocket Dimentionalist Game Up
     the expansion Astute Diplomats Game Up
     the expansion Micro-Portal Expert Game Up


•••
Возрождение галактики: Набор мини-дополнений включает в себя:

     дополнение "5-ый игрок"
     дополнение "Космокролик"
     дополнение "Разжигатель конфликтов"
     дополнение "Исследователь карманных измерений"
     дополнение "Коварные дипломаты"
     дополнение "Эксперт по микропорталам"

Up or Down? is a fast-paced trivia game where players combine general knowledge with clever guessing. One player answers a trivia question with their best guess, and the others must decide whether the actual answer is up (higher) or down (lower) than the given response. With a wide range of topics, the game keeps everyone on their toes.

Players score points by answering correctly and making accurate predictions, with bonus points for risky but correct guesses. Easy to learn and quick to play, Up or Down is perfect for casual game nights, offering a fun mix of knowledge, intuition, and interaction.

Flint's Chest: The Pirates' Treasure is a strategic board game set in the Golden Age of Piracy. Players command legendary pirate captains such as Blackbeard, Anne Bonny, and Calico Jack in a race to claim the fabled Treasure Island.

The game features a unique three-ring island structure, with players starting on the outer ring and progressing inward toward the central Treasure Island. Each ring offers increasing rewards but greater dangers. Players must build structures on conquered islands to generate resources, recruit pirates, and construct naval improvements.

Strategic naval combat allows players to battle other pirates and the patrolling Royal Navy ships. Each player also has a secret objective that provides an alternative path to victory beyond simply controlling Treasure Island.

Key Features:

     Historically inspired pirate captains with unique abilities
     Dynamic island conquest and resource management
     Tactical naval combat with cannons and boarding actions
     Secret objectives for varied gameplay
     60-90 minute play time for 3-6 players


—description from the designer

Multitasking is a fun party game where players must complete various challenges while multitasking. Each card presents a task, like answering a question while jumping on one leg or singing in a funny voice. The goal is to perform these tasks successfully, creating chaos and lots of laughs as players juggle mental and physical challenges.

Perfect for 4-8 players, Multitasking is all about quick thinking and coordination. With simple rules and plenty of opportunities for hilarity, it’s a great choice for groups looking for an entertaining and fast-paced game that keeps everyone on their toes.

Roll-and-Move Mushroom Pachinko! Mushrooms, Monkeys, Gnomes.

Mushinko is a 3-5 player traditional dice-based roll-and-move. Players move around an outer track and collect mini magnets and 'fungi' pearls. When entering the middle track, players build the Mushinko drop game using the included magnetic box.

When enough pins are added to the grid, players stop rolling dice, and drop their pearls. The highest score wins in this hybrid adventure.

Mushinko is a game of mini games, including Fungi Jack, Mystery Spores, and Memory Match - all games which use the multi-use resource cards from the game.

—description from the designer

WellMess is a card game designed to promote wellness habits in a fun and engaging way. Players take on the role of wellness seekers navigating through challenges related to physical, emotional, mental, and nutritional health.

Each card represents a behavior, choice, or situation, some healthier than others. Players must collect healthy habits, avoid sabotaging behaviors, and face “crisis” cards that shake up the game. The ultimate goal is to be the first to complete your wellness path by building the healthiest routine.

Easy to learn and perfect for game nights with friends, WellMess mixes strategic decisions with hilarious situations, sparking real conversations about self-care.

Not all hands are created equal but in this game, you get to rewrite your fate. In a high-strategy twist on classic Daifugō, players use special “segments” to alter the digital numbers on their cards, transforming weak hands into powerful plays. Whether you're adding bars to a 1 to make it a 7, or trimming a 9 into a 3, the key to victory lies in clever rewiring.

Segnum is a ladder climbing and shedding game inspired where players manipulate the numeric values of their cards using physical segment chips. Each turn, players may play cards, pass to earn segments, or spend them to modify card values in order to form valid sets. But there’s a cost to hoarding resources: unused segments at game end result in penalties, while perfect efficiency brings bonus points. Planning when to pass, how to reshape your hand, and when to strike adds a layer of tactical depth that rewards foresight and flexibility.

Atomorph is a drawless connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

				
				
					DefinitionsA stone in a corner of the board is weak if it is orthogonally adjacent to two enemy stones.

A white stone is also weak if there are three black stones such that one of them is vertically adjacent to the white stone, one of them is horizontally adjacent to the white stone and one of them is diagonally adjacent to the white stone, provided that the latter black stone is adjacent to exactly one of the other two black stones. Likewise, with colors reversed, for weak black stones.

A weak stone is contested if an enemy stone in its place would also be weak.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point or replace (flip) a weak enemy stone with a stone of your color. You cannot flip a contested stone if your opponent just flipped a contested stone anywhere on the board.

Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

				
				
					Variants
     For a simpler but still drawless game, never flip contested stones.



     For a simpler game with exactly two drawn positions (whether flipping contested stones is allowed or not), never flip stones in the corners of the board.



     To make defense stronger, when you remove an enemy stone and there are empty points on the board, place the removed stone next to the board. On their next turn, your opponent must place the stone back on an empty point of the board and then make a regular move.


—description from the designer

Clink, whirl, jingle! The medals look like they're about to fall, but they don't. Get excited about getting lots of them! The excitement of the "medal drop" game, the highlight of game arcades, has been made into a board game.

You're not the only one aiming for the accumulated medals. Moreover, the medals you use will be targeted by other people! When will you use your only 10 medals? How many will you use? Where will you use them? This toy-like board game will have both children and adults excited by the unexpected movement of the medals !

Step into history and alter fate on April 14th, 1865. Will you foil John Wilkes Booth's treacherous plans, or will President Lincoln's life be tragically cut short?

Embark on a thrilling roll-and-write spy adventure, armed only with a marker and a die. As a double agent, your mission is clear: infiltrate Booth's nefarious circle of conspirators and prevent the assassination of President Lincoln. To do that you'll need to make contact at a safe house, perform missions to prove yourself and then finally rendezvous with Booth.

Utilize your die not just for missions, but also to traverse the streets of historic Washington, D.C. With each roll, chart your course and mark your progress. Beware - every second visit to the same building draws unwanted attention. Roll the die to unveil the consequences: lost time, police apprehensions, or suspicions from Booth's crew.

Plot your paths carefully: avoid impediments, seek rerolls and steal treasures to aid your mission. Adapt if your cover is compromised, transitioning to a solo pursuit of Booth.

Will you outmaneuver obstacles and seize the opportunity to rewrite history, or will Booth's machinations succeed?

—description from the designer

The ancient doors to the Underworld are sealed with magical runes and sigils to keep the terrors that lay beyond trapped. Sometimes the magic of these runes falters, and the doors begun to buckle... and break!

The Underworld Doors Terrain Hazard represents one of the eldritch passages that leads to the cold dark of Hel's domain... the Underworld. Traditionally Norse-themed, the Underworld Doors are also great to represent gateways to the Void that have been sealed by occult means, or that are used by the Crimson Hand to unleash demons and creatures into the world, breaking down the runic barriers and squeezing through the cracks that form in the splintered doors.

In addition, on the back side of this Terrain Piece is a new type of dangerous portal that the Heroes can encounter on their Adventures called an Infinity Gate! Larger and more deadly than a normal gateway, the Infinity Gate actually pulls Heroes that stray too close into its swirling vortex, launching them into the world on the other side, and potentially corrupting them with Void energy as they pass through. The Infinity Gate also charges everything beyond it, Heroes and Enemies alike, with Void potential, giving all models in the world it leads to extra Damage on their Attacks. Much like the Hell Mouth Terrain Piece before, this new Infinity Gate can be encountered in any Adventures, not just the Missions included with this set.

This set also comes with 2 brand new Missions -

Mission 1: Horror at the Gates 
One of the gates to the Underworld is breaking open and countless horrors are ready to burst through! The ground shakes and the sky grows dark with each pounding strike against the ancient braces that hold the doors closed. If you cannot find a way to reinforce the runic magik of the seals, the doors will buckle. Even now, some evil has begun to squeeze through the splintered cracks!

This Mission sees the Heroes split up to find 3 Underworld Runes lost across 3 different worlds, all while trying to Hold Back the Horrors that threaten to break through the Underworld Doors. With flickering portals that connect to all other Gates on the board, and a ticking clock, can the Heroes find the Runes and fight their way to the foot of the Underworld Doors to seal them before it's too late?!


Mission 2: Valitore's Calling

Haunted by visions of a hideous fortress with countless tower spires and twisting dungeons of despair and madness, you wake in a cold sweat, terrified. These visions have been plaguing you for days, and the restless sleep is wearing on your fraying mind. But now you know. They are calling to you. One word echoes in your mind...Valitore.

The Heroes must seek out one of the dangerous Infinity Gates deep in the Mines, drawn to it by the call of the Void. Only there can they face a powerful Emissary of Valitore that has come to unleash torment unto our world. This Mission highlights Void Enemies and pits the Heroes against the masters of madness that haunt that eldritch world between worlds.


This deluxe resin terrain piece depicts the ancient and massive Underworld Doors, with a terrifying Infinity Gate on the back side. It is an all-new scenario set piece for Shadows of Brimstone, plus 2 new Missions themed for this new piece!

The Underworld Doors model is cast in high-detail resin and hand-poured in-house by Flying Frog Productions

—description from the publisher

Special Ops #12 has 8 articles, four ASL scenarios, a mini HASL map, and two ASLSK scenarios. The magazine also includes Warriors of England, which is a quick playing game on the Wars of the Roses.

Warriors of England: The War of the Roses is based on Warriors of God: The Wars of England & France, 1135-1453 game and includes a 22” x 34” map, 8 pages of rules, and 162 3/4" counters.

Also includes Historical ASL mini game map: Lysyanka with scenarios O23, O24 and O25


     ASLSK Scenario S95 (S102) - Clay Pigeons of St.Lo
     ASLSK Scenario S96 (S103) - Cats Come Out To Play
     ASL Scenario O26 - Drava Epic


(Note: the ASLSK scenarios were incorrectly numbered.)

Featured articles:

     Warriors of England: origins of the War of the Roses, by Stuart Ellis-Gorman
     Warriors of England: who's who in the War of the Roses, by JR Tracy
     Cracking the code: learning ASLSK is not that hard, by Mark Stevens
     A look at the 3rd die ROF: mathematical analysis of an unofficial ASL variant, by Jim Bishop
     Designer notes: Storm over Jerusalem, by Scott Blanton
     Night moves: initial operations in Race for Bastogne, by Lee Forester
     Harmonious Conflict: the etiquette guide to harmonious OCS play, by Chip Saltsman
family: Series: Advanced Squad Leader Starter Kit

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Traveling Gambler's Boots
description:
Exclusive promo received from ordering $100 or more worth of product from the Flying Frog webstore during the 2025 Spring Sale

Gear - Charm - Clothing - Boots

+ 1 Move

Whenever you roll a natural 5 or 6 for Move, choose a number from 1 to 6 and roll a D6.  If your chosen number was rolled, Recover a Grit.

Promo 1153

Exclusive promo received from ordering $100 or more worth of product from the Flying Frog webstore during the 2025 Spring Sale

Starting Gear Item

Gear - Light Source - Tribal - Fire

Whenever the Hold Back the Darkness roll is successful (not including Depth Events), every Hero may Heal 1 Sanity.

You may start any adventure with this Light Source if all heroes have Keyword Tribal.

Promo 614

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era – Cheeses of Tamriel
description:
Do you like cheese? Do you like ...erm ...lots of cheese? Does that affinity for dairy delights extend to your Betrayal of the Second Era character? Well, we've got that character covered! You can't eat these cheeses, but your fictional characters can. And you can imagine The smile on their faces as they enjoy wheel after wheel! *

The Cheeses of Tamriel offers 20 unique cheeses direct from the world of The Elder Scrolls online! 19 offer unique name and art with an identical effect, while the single, legendary (legendairy?) Shivering Cheese offers just a hair more benefit! Careful though, overconsumption of The Cheeses of Tamriel will leave your character healthy, but very clogged up...with fatigue. What did you think we meant?


     Dairy not approved for Argonian consumption.


—description from the designer
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

description: How well do you know the states? Match state geography cards with fact cards to collect the most matches, and use special challenge cards to steal matches from other players! Trivia on each card helps players learn more about the history and culture of the states. It's the funniest card game this side of the Mississippi (no matter which side you are on)!

Based on the system of "History of the world", this game traces the history of liberal democratic party of Japan (自民党) from Shigeru Yoshida to Shigeru Ishiba.

The game is composed of 9 rounds from "馬鹿野郎解散" to "時代は誰を求めるか？".

It is playable with 2 to 6 players. 5 is recommended.

Grab That Dino is a fast-paced, scream-out-loud, grab-before-your-friends-do kind of card game where only the quickest hands (and loudest voices) survive. Perfect for 3–8 players (or just two very competitive people), this game is all about matching dinos with their zombie counterparts, yelling “COMET COMET” or “LAVA LAVA” when things get wild, and diving for the squeaky dino in the middle, his name is Bunty. Yes, you must name him.

Flip cards. Watch closely. Scream if you have to. Then GRAB THAT DINO.
Mess up? Flinch too early? Shout the wrong thing? Congratulations, you’ve just paid the penalty. The stakes are high, the chaos is higher, and no one is safe from the drama.

With rounds lasting just 10–20 minutes, this game is addictively replayable and dangerously competitive. It’s part memory game, part reflex test, and all-out dino mayhem.

Get ready to yell. Get ready to grab. And get ready to betray your friends in the most delightful way possible.

This isn’t just a game, it’s a prehistoric showdown.
Let the extinction-level fun begin.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Game of Divine: The Spiritual Board Game
description:
Game of Divine is a competitive 2–5 player board game where seekers race to unlock all 108 chakras and attain unity with the Divine.

Navigate across territories, build shrines in sacred abodes, awaken powerful deities and harness spirit forces to alter fate. Use your Karma Score — determined by rolling Good Deeds and Bad Deeds dice — to move across the world, accomplish missions and conquer challenges.

Players must balance strategy, spiritual growth and luck, while facing constant trials: unpredictable dice rolls, divine interventions, and the ever-present threat of Satan who seeks to slow their progress.

With elements of area control, resource management, dice drafting, variable powers and mission completion, Game of Divine offers a dynamic race toward spiritual ascension. Every decision matters: when to build, when to move, when to challenge, and when to call upon sacred powers.

Will you rise through wisdom, resilience, and be daring to prove your worth, or be left behind in the eternal race towards the Divine?

Unlock. Ascend. Become the Divine.

—description from the publisher

Set in the Battletech universe during the Succesion Wars.

A Steiner Scout Lance walks into Dragon Canyon to "assess" the 3 bridges that support Combine forces logistics. Your assignmet as Company Commander is to "Evaluate" the bridges and their defenses and exit the canyon with the information.

A "tongue in cheek" solo game inspired by Bommerz Over Da Sulphur River by Andy Chambers. Using Battletech theme and some BT Classic mechanics to create a new experience.

Enjoy!

The game sets you with 4 mechs entering the map of Dragon Canyon, each turn you'l roll dice to set the movement points of your mech and will move them along the map.

You'll encounter enemies along the route and try to evade or eliminate them in order to reach the bridges that you will try to destroy.

As many wargames, the result of your dice will determine the outcome of your actions, but as Steiner Scout Commander you should assure that Dragon Canyos has no Combine Assets and no usable bridges over it.

A gentle breeze of madness sweeps across the beach, giving you the chance to fish during “Low Tide” and “High Tide.” But be careful – you won’t be catching the same things!

The game begins at “Low Tide.” The tide alternates, based on the “Tide” cards revealed, between low (to fish for clams) and high (to fish for fish). Follow the changes by flipping the “Tide” token to the corresponding side.

With the "Folly" cards, block your opponents, play again, reserve treasures, or change the flow of the game!

The Fishing ends: when all the “Tide” cards have been revealed. The fisherman with the most points (Clams, Fish, Treasure) wins the “Golden Clam”!

—description from the publisher

NOLA: 1895 is an action-point, worker placement game about the spectral hunters who battled the restless spirits haunting New Orleans at the end of the 19th century.

In the year 1895, the French Quarter is a place of mystery and unease. Strange happenings plague the streets, and rumors swirl of spirits too powerful for ordinary folk to handle. Called upon by desperate citizens, you — the players — take on the role of elite Spectral Hunters: investigators, mediums, and scholars tasked with vanquishing spirits, restoring balance, and securing your own reputation as the greatest hunter in the Bayou.

Armed with supplies like holy water, salt, and iron, players will take contracts to cleanse haunted houses, research supernatural entities, and gather essence from vanquished spirits. They must work the streets of the French Quarter, expand their influence through the placement of houses, and rise in fame through newspaper chronicles.

NOLA: 1895 is played over six rounds. Each round, players send their team of hunters across key locations — the Town Center, the Crescent City Chronicle, Secluded Lane, and more — taking actions to gather supplies, gain contracts, research spirits, and perform séances. Workers vary in strength, and essence can be spent to push actions even further. Success depends not just on vanquishing spirits, but also on seizing critical bonuses in the French Quarter and mastering the delicate balance of supplies, essence, and favors.

Victory comes to the hunter who can build their name into legend, gathering the most victory points through contracts, research breakthroughs, street dominance, and spectral essence — securing their place in the ghostly history of New Orleans.

—description from the designer

Strandbüfé ("Food Truck") is a refreshing summer game, the third in the creator's seasonal card game series. You play as a food truck owner on the beach. Your goal is to attract as many customers as possible, serve them quickly and efficiently, and manage your inventory and employees wisely. You also want to earn stars for your business to indicate high quality, as well as various additional bonuses to increase your final score. The winner is the player with the most points after 6 rounds. The game can be played on two difficulty levels and also has a solo version.

A food truck is run by three workers, each with a different action point value. You need to optimize the use of these action points between attracting and serving customers, and restocking your inventory. At the beginning of the round, all players take guest cards by open drafting (bidding in expert mode), then they serve their guests' orders by placing the necessary ingredients onto the cards – in the same turn, or no later than the next one. If you serve a guest properly, the card is worth points at the end of the game: the trickier the order, the more points you get. Meanwhile, you should make sure to replenish your stocks, because if you run out of ingredients and fail to complete an order, the customer will leave, giving your food truck a bad name. Yet, the level of quality and efficiency are decisive.

—description from the publisher (translated)

A Strandbüfé üdítő, nyári kártyajáték, a szerző ÉVSZAKOK sorozatának harmadik darabja.  Egy tengerparti strandon mozgó ételárusként versenyeztek.  A célod, hogy minél több vendéget csábíts a büfédhez, és gyorsan, hatékonyan ki is szolgáld őket, ügyesen gazdálkodva az alapanyagokkal és a munkaerővel. Eközben törekedned kell a magas színvonalra is, amit a kiérdemelt csillagok száma jelez, de a végső pontszámodat további bónuszok is befolyásolják. A játékot az nyeri, aki a legtöbb pontot szerzi a játék 6 fordulója alatt.  A Strandbüfé két nehézségi fokon (kezdő és haladó) játszható, sőt egyszemélyes verzióval is rendelkezik.

Minden büfékocsiban három, eltérő kapacitású munkás dolgozik; ez jelenti az akciópontjaid számát, melyet ügyesen meg kell osztanod a vendégek csábítása, kiszolgálása, és az alapanyagok beszerzése között. A forduló elején draftolással (haladó módban licitálással) vendégkártyákhoz jutsz, melyeken szerepel a vendég rendelése. Ha a megfelelő alapanyagok ráhelyezésével sikerül az adott körben (vagy legkésőbb a következőben) hiánytalanul kiszolgálnod a vendéget, a kártya pontot hoz a játék végén: minél igényesebb a vendég, annál többet. Eközben gondoskodnod kell az alapanyagok pótlásáról is, mert ha készlethiány miatt nem tudsz teljesíteni egy rendelést, a vendég azonnal távozik, rossz hírét keltve a büfédnek. A színvonal és a hatékonyság pedig fontos a győzelemhez  – haladó módban különösen.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Operation Torch: Algiers and Oran Landings – North Africa Campaign
description:
This scenario pack focuses on Operation Torch and the Eastern and Central task forces in 1942 of the North Africa campaign. Torch was the first full scale joint American and British operation of the war. The British had fought the Germans and the Italians widely across North Africa. Now the Americans plunge into the fray at their side in a baptism of fire. This packet includes naval support and even British destroyers, the HMS Brokes and Malcolm, carrying American troops directly ashore. This battalion level scenario pack contains 8 exciting miniatures war game scenarios written in campaign form and playable by 2-4 players.

The scenarios include data sheets for vehicles and uses Fist Full of TOWs 3 rules. Scenarios can be easily converted to your favorite game system.

The eight scenarios can be played as a campaign. A campaign score sheet is included, enabling players to track the campaign progress and final score.

These scenarios put you in the Gasoline Cowboy seat, making you and your gaming partner(s) the tank drivers as you play through Operation Torch - the Algiers and Oran Landings - North Africa Campaign.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Murder on the Midnight Maiden: A Puzzle Magazine
description: You find yourself stepping on board a luxurious ocean liner, bound for New York. Shortly after departure, a gunshot rings out, and a lady in a red dress is discovered dead. All the glamorous passengers appear to harbor dark secrets. Now, you must dig into their pasts to uncover the truth.

boardgame: Pole Position: Circuits Pack 3 Germany & Brazil
description: This is an expansion for Pole Position with two fictional circuits. The circuit Brazil seems to be inspired by the Autódromo José Carlos Pace. Each circuit has a special rule. The base game is required to play. The box contains the folded double-sided board with a circuit on each side.

boardgame: Murder at Shrike Bay: Puzzle Magazine
description: You join a crew of bird enthusiasts on a trip to a paradise island. Everything starts off wonderfully, but then a stuffed bird is stolen from the museum and is soon found accompanied by a threat message to someone. Is there something special about this bird? Is someone in danger? Something on this island feels uncanny...

boardgame: Secret of the Crimson Pendant: A Puzzle Magazine
description: It's late December. You are hired as a private detective to investigate the disappearance of a long lost family heirloom, The Crimson Pendant. The investigation takes a dark turn when the pendant is found on a murder victim! To make matters worse, a fierce snowstorm hits the estate. You are now stranded in a snowstorm... with a killer.

Alright, buckle up, boardgame enthusiasts, because we've stumbled upon something truly groundbreaking in the realm of abstract strategy! Our researchers have unveiled a novel Multi-Layer Abstract Tactical Board Game Framework that's poised to redefine tabletop gaming as we know it!

At its core lies an ingenious rule system operating within a multi-dimensional hexagonal space – yes, you heard that correctly, multiple layers, planes, call them what you will! This isn't your grandma's checkerboard; we're talking about a paradigm shift in spatial reasoning!

Beyond the core mechanics, the framework boasts pre-baked narratives and diverse rule variants, all stemming from the same elegant foundation. Players command their primary factions on a shared layer, engaging in direct strategic conflict. However, the innovation truly shines with the option to simultaneously control non-player factions across these distinct layers, unlocking unprecedented tactical depth and resource acquisition possibilities.

While advanced variants introduce intricate card-driven victory conditions, the default objective remains a compelling race for resource dominance by turn ten or, for the strategically aggressive, the complete elimination of opposing player factions. This framework presents a fascinating exploration of multi-layered interaction and control, offering a rich and complex strategic landscape for tabletop gamers. The implications for abstract game design are significant, potentially paving the way for entirely new genres of spatial strategy. Stay tuned for further developments in this exciting field!

—description from the designer

Control to Catastrophe: Caesar is a miniature wargaming rule set that puts you in command of the great armies of Antiquity and the Middle Ages. 
The rules cover everything you need to know about getting ready for play, the core rules covering all aspects of moving and combat, the effects of terrain, special rules that add histoical flavour to your armies, sample army lists and scenarios, and optional rules. CtC is not meant for tournament play but is written to be unambiguous and complete.

Here are some of the game’s features:

     Gridded playing surface – move your units quickly and precisely without the fuss of rulers and specialized maneuver tools.
     Regardless of the size of table or number of figures, fight battles not just skirmishes.
     Customizable scaling – choose the grid-size that you want and can accommodate using unit sizes that suit your budget and aesthetic preferences.
     Figure scale and basing conventions don’t matter. Fill the grid-box with models that you have and that evoke how you imagine the troops you are representing.
     The core rules are simple and open the way for fast play using D6s.
     Special rules for special units add character and tactical nuance to your armies.
     Sample Army Lists and 3 scenarios.
     Free Army Lists available on the Decisive Wargame Design website.


—description from the publisher
family: History: Wars of Alexander the Great

description: Flora Obscura adds dramatic plants, extreme plant abilities, and riskier equipment with aggressive abilities to the base game.

House Plant Hermit is a focused expansion for Propagation Station that adds a new layer of strategic tension and solitary decision-making to your garden bench. With 21 new cards, this expansion introduces fresh interactions that reward careful planning, timing, and a willingness to commit to your own growing space rather than competing for every resource. House Plant Hermit is designed to seamlessly integrate with the base game and expand its possibilities

A copy of Propagation Station is required to play this expansion.

Unexpected Blooms is a small but impactful expansion for Propagation Station that introduces surprise twists and delightful chaos to your growing collection. Featuring 7 new plant cards and 1 rules card, this expansion focuses on plants that break expectations. Designed to drop seamlessly into any session, Unexpected Blooms adds variety and replayability without increasing complexity, making each draw from the Auction deck filled with surprise!

A copy of Propagation Station is required to play this expansion.
Dirt Baby uses Progation Station's auction mechanics to make a short travel game.

A word search game played using your own Scrabble or Bananagram tiles.

The game board is composed of 36 movable squares, each with four bigrams (2-letter fragments) and/or single letters within boxes.

LASERCUT WOODEN COMPONENTS:
Eight-piece frame and 40 base tiles.
Also included is a list of valid 3-letter words and common acronyms (eg FAQ, VIP).

GOAL: 
Make words from adjacent letters (streaks).
You can play solo or with multiple players.
The game is played in strategic yet simultaneous phases:
- letter tile placement
- word search.

SET UP:
Assemble the game board by connecting the frame and inserting 36 random base tiles in any orientation.
Four base tiles will remain unused.
Flip all your Scrabble (or Bananagram) tiles, except blanks, upside down. 
Grab a paper and pen.

SOLO GAME PLAY:
Draw a tile and place it over a bigram inside a box such that the combination of your tile and the bigram makes a 3-letter word or
your tile and the single letter makes a 2-letter word.
Your letter can be combined to the start or end of the bigram.
For example: F+OX = FOX, while KI+T = KIT or B+E = BE.

Letter placement is the first phase.
In the second phase, look for words.

Attempt to place tiles such that a word can be formed from other adjacent letters in a straight line. The word can be in any of eight directions.

If you cannot place a letter, discard the tile. This will occur when no more bigrams or single letters are available for tile placement.

SOLO SCORING:
Upon finding a word of at least three letters, record its score. With Scrabble tiles, add up the point values. With Bananagram tiles and for younger children, the score is the number of letters in the word.

SOLO END OF GAME:
Play until all tiles have been drawn. Play again to beat your score.

MULTI-PLAYER GAME PLAY:
To avoid waiting, game play is simultaneous. Each player draws one tile and places it over a bigram or letter inside a box such that the combination of the tile and the bigram/letter makes a word. The word must be called out for validation by all players. the letter can be combined to the start or end of the bigram.
For example J+AM = JAM while BO+W = BOW or H+I = HI.

Letter placement is the first phase.
In the second phase, look out for words.

Attempt to place tiles such that a word can be formed from other adjacent letters. The word can be in any of eight directions. Call out the word and score it.

MULTI-PLAYER SCORING:
Upon finding a word of at least three letters, call out the word. With Scrabble tiles, add up the point value. With Bananagram tiles and for younger children, the score is the number of letters in the word.

If you cannot place a letter, discard the tile.

MULTI-PLAYER END OF GAME:
Once all tiles have been placed, the winner has the highest score.

OPTIONAL PLAY: When playing with younger children, allow for them finding two-letter words as well as longer words.

—description from the publisher

The general objective of this work is to create a game with a legal theme to be used as a method of teaching Brazilian criminal procedure, more specifically, a pedagogical tool aimed at teaching/learning the Jury Court procedure according to the Brazilian Code of Criminal Procedure, but in a playful and interesting way.

OBJECTIVE

Convince the majority of the jurors (4 or more out of a total of 7 jurors)

HOW TO PLAY

Starting with the ACCUSATION, players take turns choosing a card from their hand and placing it face up next to a JURORS on their side of the table. It does not have to be the same one.
Once placed, the card cannot be moved to another JURY.
After playing the card from his hand, the player draws a card from the deck of EVIDENCE and passes the turn to his opponent.
If the EVIDENCE deck is empty, the game continues without players drawing any more cards.
A JURY card cannot have more than three EVIDENCE cards on each side of the table.
NOTE: When playing a TEST card on a JURORS who already has another card(s), position the new card so that you can identify all the cards in that formation that influence the JURORS.
The first player to place 3 cards on the same judge will place a chip of his/her color on the judge to have preference in the tiebreaker.
When all the JURY cards have 6 EVIDENCE cards each (3 on each side of the table), the first phase of ORAL DEBATES ends.
At the end of the ORAL DEBATES, the conviction of the JURIES will be assessed.

CONVINCING THE JUDGES

To claim a JUDGE card at the end of the game, you must have a stronger combination of cards than the one played by your opponent.
The possible combinations, from strongest to weakest, are described below:
5 - Suited sequence: three cards of the same suit with strengths in sequence. The order in which the cards are played does not matter.
4 - Three of a Kind: three cards with the same strength.
3 - Same suit: three cards of the same suit.
2 - Simple sequence: three cards of any suit with strengths in sequence. The order in which the cards are played does not matter.
1 - Sum: any three cards.

VICTORY

Whoever has the most JURY cards will be the winner.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Wardens of Morath: Heroic Fantasy Skirmish Combat
description:
Welcome to Morath, a world plunged into chaos as rival factions clash over the use of magic. Conflict is especially fierce in the kingdom of Galbraith, where magic is outlawed and practitioners are subdued swiftly and without mercy. Quazimorphs and Kindled Voidbound flee across the Aardrun Divide to escape growing mistrust and political turmoil, but for those suspected of harboring magical artifacts and tomes, even the border between kingdoms is not enough to stop the agents of Galbraith.

Maribor bolsters for war on multiple fronts, struggling with their support of magic and dealing with the consequences of its misuse. Though a safe haven for Quazimorphs and Kindled Voidbound, a growing number of Mariborian subjects have begun to harbor sympathy for Galbraith’s concern about the dangers of magic.

Far to the South, Velleguard continues to expand their influence across the Ogdian Sea. Boasting an impressive navy and standing army, there are few who wish to engage them in open combat. Rumors abound of Velleguardian agents venturing into the southern reaches of both Galbraith and Maribor, sowing discord and fomenting unrest.

Once unified under a common banner, Warden Archives have fragmented into rival factions as conflict grows among their ranks. Finding profitable work as mercenaries, Archives now clash against former comrades across Morath. Though Galbraith, Maribor, and Velleguard have not yet issued declarations of war, many fear it is only a matter of time.

The Archival Schism has begun, Wardens. Prepare yourselves for the conflict ahead.


Wardens of Morath is a tactical, flintlock fantasy themed miniatures game for two players. Each player creates a Warband consisting of 4 to 10 Wardens that battle on a 3 ft x 3 ft game board to complete objectives. Skirmishes take place across the lands of Morath, as rival Warden Archives fight to control land, acquire valuable resources, reclaim historic relics, and sabotage the enemy. However, rival Archives aren’t the only threat in the wilds of Morath, and Wardens must remain ever vigilant against nefarious Desecrators and the Voidbound resurrected under their control.

—description from the designer

The time is the 14th century. You are one of the nobles in the European city-state Verona. In Verona, two large families were at odds: the Montague and the Capulets. Amidst the conflict continues, Romeo of the Montague family and Juliet of the Capulet family. Rumors have come to mind that they are in love. After hearing this rumor, the noble patriarchs thought: "This is a good opportunity to unravel the two families and take credit for it!"

And you have been ordered by the patriarch of the noble family you belong to. Move different figures cards and make Romeo and Juliet happy before any other nobles.

Each players take turns, following 5 steps:

     Draw one card from the right player. Just like the game "old maid".
     If you have the same type of two cards(set) in your hand, you need to play them faced up using effect or faced down in front of you. (Only play one set in one turn, and you can only have one set in front of you.)
     If there is a faced down set in front of you, you can choose to perform the effect.
     Check whose hand is empty, the player lose the game and no longer take turns.
     Goes to the next player's turn (clockwise).


Repeat these step until one player meets one of winning conditions.

     Only "Romeo" and "Juliet" are in your hand.
     players other than you lose the game.


—description from the publisher (translated)

During the First World War, the British and French made a secret agreement to carve up the Ottoman Empire – literally drawing on a map in red and blue ink – without respect to the peoples, culture, or history of the region. This would have devastating effects over the next century.

In Sykes-Picot, two players take on the roles of infamous diplomats Mark Sykes and Francois Georges-Picot, using red and blue markers to carve out territories to control and to influence on a dry-erase map. Trick-taking card play and table-talk negotiations drive the proceedings, with the winner of each trick choosing one of the two cards to implement, and the loser consoling themselves with the remaining card. Each player has a set of hidden objectives they're trying to achieve. Players may be as co-operative or as competitive as they wish in pursuit of these goals.

Tonally, the game is light, reflecting the distance and cavalier attitude of these imperialist powers. We feel there's something horrifying in that, and this approach captures some of that horror.

—description from the publisher

The gourdineur draws a card, chooses a word that he announces to the players and prepares his gourdin.

All players have 30 seconds to write words related to the chosen theme on their slate.

If the gourdineur manages to find their words, he knocks players with his gourdin.

If the players fond of the gourdineur with common words that he did not find: "It passes".

Cooperate to discover the remains of the past with your Ventouse shovel.

Dig on 4 levels, reconstitute your discoveries and try to get 10 stars. But beware, get rid of your pebbles before they break your shovel.

boardgame: Questions dingues à poser à vos amis
description:
Each turn, a player will play the role of "guessor". He chooses a theme (among the 6 offered: animals, players, numbers, food, objects or celebrities) and draws 5 cards. By choosing a card from the five response cards, the other players answer the question out loud.

The guessor has now heard all the answers (but not the question), he reads the questions and classified the five cards of "the least likely" to "the most likely" on the rule.

When the five questions are classified, the other players reveal the right question.

The guessor marks the number of points indicated by the rule where the card is placed with the right question.
The player (or players) having the most points at the end of the towers earns the game.

Take on the challenges of everyday life in a developing country 
in the Sahel region. As a large family, you must invest your limited resources as skilfully as possible,

to secure your livelihood and at the same time be prepared for risks such as price fluctuations, droughts, political unrest or diseases. Your family is also represented on the village council of, which can support and drive forward the development of the village. But does everyone want to invest in the new community centre? 
Or would the money be better invested in the creation of a veterinary service ? In the end, the winner is the extended family that best protects itself against potential risks with a 
sustainable strategy, utilises existing opportunities and can most successfully improve its living situation.

Welcome to The Slowlympics!

The only race where the competitors nap between strides, and victory is measured in inches per hour! In today’s main event, seven of nature’s most unhurried athletes—including the drowsy sloth, the ever-determined turtle, and the surprisingly spry hedgehog—take their places on the starting line.

As the spectator, you will be betting—watching from the sidelines, dry-erase board in hand, placing your bets with the keen eye of someone who knows how to read a sleepy stare or a hesitant shuffle.

This simultaneous betting game unfolds over three whimsical moments of the day (rounds): Dawn, Noon, and Dusk. Each round, seven movement cards are drawn from a deck where every animal has two cards hidden inside. Some creatures might surge forward twice. Others? They may just snooze through the round entirely. Strategy will emerge from observing the race's slow developments and adjusting bets as new information is revealed.

After each movement is revealed, players mark a bet, hoping their chosen champion will creep ahead. When the sun sets on this slow-motion marathon, the animals are ranked by how far they've gone. Bets on the first-place animal score four points, second place earns three, third place two, while the rest, bless their tiny feet, still bring in one point each.

The player with the most points at the end is crowned the ultimate Slowlympics oracle. So grab your board, place your bets, and get ready for the slowest thrill ride of your life.

The Metromite Game Board Story

Time Travel to Atlantis . Embark on a thrilling and unforgettable journey through time! Set out from modern-day London aboard the extraordinary Obsidian Sphere, an impressive five-story vessel powered by cutting-edge negative energy and Einstein-Rosen Bridge wormhole technology. Your destination: Atlantis, a breathtaking floating city of legend, gracefully suspended above an endless ocean, encircled by mesmerizing waterfalls cascading serenely into the waters below.

Traveling back 12,000 years, you will find Atlantis at its peak—an awe-inspiring utopia powered entirely by the infinite energy of the crystal known as Metromite. At the heart of this spectacular metropolis stands a radiant dome temple, the sacred sanctuary housing this extraordinary crystal, sustaining the city’s unmatched technological advancements and peaceful prosperity.

But Atlantis is doomed to imminent destruction. An unstoppable cataclysm, triggered by a devastating earthquake and colossal tsunami, looms on the horizon, threatening to erase this magnificent civilization forever.

Your mission is daring, strategic, and filled with excitement. Carefully navigate Atlantis’s intricate network of concentric rings, each connected by luminous floating airbridges and elegant walkways. Along the way, strategically acquire futuristic properties, discover powerful artifacts, advanced Atlantean weapons, and incredible technological inventions unlike anything seen in the modern world.

Yet, peril awaits. Beneath Atlantis’s serene exterior lurk treacherous challenges: unexpected storms, powerful ocean currents, giant octopuses hidden beneath the waves, sudden malfunctions of your Obsidian Sphere, and unpredictable anomalies caused by your time-travel journey itself.

But you won't face these trials alone! Meet Helena, the delightful and charming guardian of Atlantis, who holds the honor of being the sole and only Atlantean remaining in this remarkable city. Helena’s wisdom, guidance, and heartwarming humor will provide you with priceless advice and memorable laughs. Her delightful personality ensures you’re always smiling, and she'll reward your progress generously with plenty of coins along the way.

Joining her is Miles Shaw, a dedicated archaeologist and seasoned expert in time-travel expeditions. Miles will guide you to strategically valuable properties, rare artifacts, cutting-edge Atlantean weapons, and extraordinary technologies available for purchase, greatly enhancing your journey and chance of success.

Together, Helena and Miles Shaw form a warm, loving, and playful romantic couple whose mutual affection brightens every step of your adventure. They’re here not just to guide you, but to make your experience joyful, immersive, and full of laughter and companionship.

The clock is ticking relentlessly—the catastrophic tsunami is approaching. Your ultimate goal is clear: reach the majestic dome temple at Atlantis’s core, retrieve the Metromite crystal, and return safely aboard the Obsidian Sphere to modern-day London exactly one hour before disaster strikes, resetting history and safeguarding humanity’s future.

Your courage, wit, teamwork, and strategic thinking will determine the fate of Atlantis—and your own.

Are you ready to journey into history, laughter, friendship, and adventure?

Good luck, explorers—your epic voyage through time starts now!

—description from the designer

The Grand Duchy of Lithuania and the Kingdom of Poland are under siege.
Step into the shoes of real medieval legends in LDK and Poland, a cooperative board game where teamwork and strategy decide the fate of an empire.
Seven enemies close in from every direction — only you and your allies stand between them and the fall of Vilnius and Warsaw. Victory demands unity. Will your legend echo through the ages?
Travel the medieval world freely, complete daring missions, gain experience, and level up your historical characters with new powers.
Travel across medieval Europe freely, complete historical quests, gain experience, and level up your historical characters with new powers. Are you ready to defend the Grand Duchy of Lithuania and the Kingdom of Poland at all costs?
History isn't written yet. Will you save your homeland... or watch it burn?

—description from the designer

Wizard Race is a fast-paced, chaotic card game where Wizards sprint to victory, without breaking a sweat! 
Build a 100 Meter track using Track cards while casting over 20 completely unique and devastating spells to sabotage your rivals. 
Outsmart, outspell, and outpace your opponents to claim the title of the fastest Wizard in the land!

—description from the designer

Magic Number Eleven: Expansion  adds new player cards and new features to the base game. There are 9 new Star Players with the powerful skills which you never have seen before in the base game. With the brand-new Away Team set featuring 10 new Player Cards boosts both the excitement and the replay value to the game. The VAR system, Yellow/Red cards, and the decision of the Referee will bring even more diverse variables to the game. The Corner Kick rule, which conducted on the Corner Kick board, will give you more thrills and tension like never before!

—description from the designer

Can you smell melted cheese? Try to win cheese, potatoes and more, while your opponents ingredients burn. Just make sure you don't mix up your fondue fork and your raclette spatula bvecause that could really mess up your game plan!

The aim of the game is to try and collect the four fondue and raclette ingredient cards with the highest value.

A lighthearted card game where the goal is to take the most tricks! Trick taking game... where the Trump continues to change.

13 cards to each player. Plays like Hearts or Spades, where highest card of suit led takes the trick, but with a major difference: the Trump Change Card is automatically the highest card AND the Trump Change Card changes the Trump Suit/Color for future tricks.

Each trick taken = 1 point. Play to 36 points or a pre-determined number of points.

Deal the cards according to the number of players, create the deck, and start the round!
     The order of ranking for the round is determined by the players who run out of cards first.
     Earn points based on your ranking in each round. If you reach 5 points, you get a "check" (one step away from victory)!
     If you take 1st place during your "check," you win!

boardgame: Krimi al Dente: Weinprobe des Wahnsinns – Das letzte Glas
description:
Players are invited to a wine tasting event in an idyllic vinyard. But the invitation is almost to good to be true, and more than just wine flows on this evening ...

Players gather for a dinner party. Over the course of several 30 minute rounds, they will have to find the player who is lying. In each round, new information becomes available, until at last, the truth comes to light (or not). Aside from trying to find the murderer, each player has side tasks to fulfill that decide their final score.

boardgame: Krimi al Dente: Tödliche Datingshow – Ärger im Paradies
description:
The latest scandalous dating show thrills the audience - but behind the scenes, things aren't as romantic as they seem. Someone has been murdered, and everyone's a suspect.

Players gather for a dinner party. Over the course of several 30 minute rounds, they will have to find the player who is lying. In each round, new information becomes available, until at last, the truth comes to light (or not). Aside from trying to find the murderer, each player has side tasks to fulfill that decide their final score.

boardgame: Krimi al Dente: Dinner for One – Not the Same Procedure
description:
Players play the roles from the famous TV sketch Dinner for One - including the four dead friends of Miss Sophie's (when playing with seven, add Madame Claire, a clairvoyant). Meeting at Miss Sophie's mansion, players will have to find out what happened to the four deceased men.

Over the course of several 30 minute rounds, players will have to find the player who is lying. In each round, new information becomes available, until at last, the truth comes to light (or not). Aside from trying to find the murderer, each player has side tasks to fulfill that decide their final score.
Includes a 2 player variant for seasoned crime-enthusiasts.

Strange things have been happening in Aspen, the scene of Ayla Dade's novel series Winter Dreams. Players must find out who is behind the ocurrences.

Cards in this game contain information about the character depicted on them, plus a one-time ability. Each player holds four cards in their hands. On their turn, they put one of them on the table and announce that they want to use the ability, which will exclude some of the other cards. This is put to a vote, each player can vote yes or no - any no vote will void the ability. But each player only has one no vote throughout the game. When each player has played two cards, a last card is flipped open from the deck and voted on. Afterwards, the player with the best card that has not been excluded, wins the game.

In addition to the character cards and voting tokens, the game contains short scenes written by Ayla Dade. These scenes serve as prompts for the game. Each scene can be played multiple times.

boardgame: 安卡:生命之符 (Ankh: Rune of Life)
description:
Welcome to Ancient Egypt, a land teeming with "deities"! Your destiny is eternally interwined with the gods. Harness their divine power, climb to the pinnacle of the pyramid, and become the Pharaoh's heir!
With stunning art and over 100 Eygptian deity cards, this game delights history buffs, myth lovers, and collectors. A light-to -medium strategy game, it emphasizes strategic buildings, satisfying combos, and player interaction, appealing to a broad audience. Solo and optional rules boost replayability, while exquisite componests and expansions support versatile sales strategies.

Similar to Escape Room: The Game, but 1 game has been replaced with one that were originally sold as expansions.

Secret Agent has replaced Temple of the Aztec.
family: Series: Escape Room – The Game (Identity Games International B.V.)

description:
Time Weavers is an epic fantasy adventure set across multiple time eras!

Time Weavers is a 1-4 player board game that mixes elements of table top gaming and trading cards, which makes for a quick paced action packed game! 

When high school students, Kai, Isha, Eve and Braiden break an ancient artefact they accidentally unleash Elgar, a time travelling outlaw intent on collecting the Era Stones and controlling time!

Now time is all jumbled and they find themselves in a world of medieval battles, dinosaurs, samurai and pirates! Choose your character and collect the Era Stones, then unite them on the shield to restore time!

—description from the publisher

Dark Rift is a solo cyberpunk themed micro game that casts you as a private investigator for Cipher, a shadowy agency contracted by a mega-corp to investigate a mysterious murder aboard the passenger transport vessel Charon. Every clue matters. Every decision counts. And in the Rift, trust no one.

At the start of the game, the player selects one of three unique cases to solve—each offering a different level of difficulty and distinct win conditions. A game board is then generated at random, featuring 12 ship locations. Each location is seeded with an Investigate card, which may reveal helpful clues such as motive or method—or potentially lead you astray with a false lead.

To gather clues, players roll dice and attempt to crack a sequence of five keys (higher, lower, or equal). Using the clues you've uncovered, your goal is to identify the murderer before time runs out on your Case Progression Tracker. Solve the case in time, and you win the game.

—description from the publisher

Dice & Bear is a dice-rolling game themed around bears gathering food for hibernation. Players use polyhedral dice (D4, D6, D8, D10, D12, D20). For each revealed food card, players choose a dice and roll to compete for the highest result. However, if players choose the same dice, they clash and fail to claim the card.

—description from the publisher

Zero-Sum is the exciting trivia game with one rule: Your guesses must add up to exactly 100!

It's the party game with an unmatched rules-to-fun ratio. Teach your friends and family to play in seconds. Then watch as the players start seeing all the interesting decisions they face in every play of Zero-Sum!

Each round, three question cards are dealt to the middle of the table. Players secretly write their guesses on their personal whiteboard.

Since the question cards are chosen at random, there is little chance their answers will actually add up to 100. But each player's guesses still must!

You'll find yourself questioning how confident you are in a given answer. Even if you think you can get close, will another player get closer than you? Maybe it's best to focus on one of the tougher questions this round instead? Or is it worth blowing your budget to guess 80 or 90 on a question, leaving you with no choice but to make ridiculously low guesses on the other questions.

Easy to learn. Fast to play. And rewards a good strategy as much as having all the answers. Zero-Sum is the party game your friends and family will ask to bring out every time you get together!

—description from the designer

You are the artisans shaping a living web of nature, from the tiniest grasses to the mightiest predators.

Use your Symbiosis cards to share information with your teammates, plan your Condition or Species cards and work together to create the fabric of life!
This cooperative game of collective adaptation offers 13 distinct modes for a unique experience each time.
The basic rules are casual and family-friendly, while the game evolves into a more expert experience through the 12 themed modes.

In partnership with France Nature Environnement.

—description from the publisher

"Carve your empire from the ruins of the stars"

In the fractured remains of a forgotten galaxy, nomadic fleets known as Drift Caravans scavenge, trade, and battle across the shifting ruins of the Drift.
Drift Empires is a gritty, tactical strategy game where players lead caravans of ships, command Dustwalker crews, and balance uneasy alliances with deadly competition.
Face the growing Mawspawn threat, control key planets, and rise as the dominant force before the galaxy falls to chaos.

In Drift Empires, players are rival Caravan Leaders, commanding fleets of ships and Dustwalker teams across the remnants of a broken stellar system.

Each round, players deploy units to planets, surface locations, and deep space sectors, competing for precious resources like Iridite and Voidfuel. Control over locations yields rewards — but also risk, as the Mawspawn threat grows with every conflict.

Key features include:

Planet Control and Surface Exploration: Secure orbital dominance while dispatching Dustwalkers to risky surface operations.

Resource Economy: Trade, offload, and burn resources strategically — or seize them from your rivals.

Mawspawn Threat: A growing, semi-cooperative danger. Push your luck for rewards but beware escalating catastrophe.

Fleet and Crew Tactics: Driftships and Dustwalkers operate differently, forcing you to plan layered strategies.

Hidden Opportunities: Mystery tokens, event cards, and secret objectives create unexpected shifts each round.

With asymmetric setups, variable planets, and modular components, every game of Drift Empires offers a unique battlefield where cunning leadership will decide the fate of the Drift.

Will you unite the scattered fleets, or let the Mawspawn consume the last sparks of civilization?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Great Western Trail: El Paso – Los Olvidados Promo
description:
A promo provided free to early buyers of the retail release of the base game in April 2025.

COMPONENTS: 6 brown Bonus tiles (2x each of 3 new types):

     Permanently increase the value of Simmental cattle by 1 for you. 
     Immediately remove any disk from your player board, paying any associated cost and gaining any bonus. 
     Hire 1 Worker (if it’s Wild, pay $4).

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – War of Kings Character and Crisis Card Pack
description:
Confront a new crisis in the cosmos for Marvel: Crisis Protocol! This Character and Crisis Card Pack includes 5 new Extract Crisis Cards and 5 new Secure Crisis Cards all around the events which occurred in the War of Kings storyline. Also included, is the mighty Gladiator! Jump into this campaign and mission set for Marvel: Crisis Protocol!

In addition to all new Crisis Cards players can add to standard Crisis Protocol games, this pack introduces a new campaign system which plays through 5 narrative missions constructed through pairings of the Crisis Cards in this pack.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Inhuman Royal Court
description:
CP166 boosts the ranks of the Inhumans in Marvel: Crisis Protocol with Maximus the Mad, Karnak, and Gorgon, along with a more powerful version of King Black Bolt joining the fight. Exposed to the Terrigen Mist, these Inhumans are another exciting group of heroes and villains in the Marvel Universe.

With Inhumans already having made their initial debut in 
Marvel: Crisis Protocol, this pack will boost the options for players, providing more variety for this affiliation. Players already fielding Inhumans on the table should look to add this pack to their collection. 

—description from the designer

boardgame: Star Trek: Into the Unknown – Rising Tensions Expansion
description:
Broaden your narrative experience and your starship collection with the new Rising Tensions expansion for Star Trek: Into the Unknown! This exciting follow up to the core set brings the Klingon Empire to the conflict, playable as a standalone faction or as allies for Federation or Dominion forces.

Add new officers and equipment to your task forces. Take on new campaign and incident missions designed to test each faction’s battle skill, diplomatic cunning, and thirst for exploration. Engage in evocative and tactical gameplay, realized through new cloaking mechanics and an elegant stealth tracker component!

As an unexpected war rages, the conflicts between the powers of the Alpha Quadrant only intensify. Will you rise to the challenge with actions worthy of song?

—description from the publisher

Spetzi is a two-player domino trick-taking game inspired by Texas 42 and Schnapsen.
The game blends aspects from both games to create a brand new experience.

Each hand is a race to reach 25 points.
The first player to reach 25 points gets 1 mark.
If a player thinks they can win with just the tiles left in hand, they can close the boneyard to try and win another mark.

If a player has already reached 25 points, but they think they can push it further, they can call Another! and try to reach reach 35 points.
Succeeding this, also awards another mark.

—description from the designer

Adventures of Friskies world is a crazy fun game of cat exploration. Adventure through the board to partake in different cat activities including a cat maze, underwater sea fishing, tree climbing, and more! Be the most adventurous cat to win the game!

The goal of Friskies world is to have the highest points at the end of the game. You gain points by adventuring around the board, competing on activity spaces, spending ingredients for meals, bonus points tiles, and more. Cats (players) take turns rolling dice and moving around the board. If a cat (player) lands on an activity space, other cats can pay ingredients to join in the activity.

The end-game is triggered when once one cat (player) has reached 30 points or visited all 5 activity spaces. The game will then end when all players have had the same amount of turns.

Players game points from winning Activities, turning in ingredients for star cards, and landing on star tiles on the board. However, you will lose points if you forget to feed your hungry cats your find along the way!

The game is very simple and straight forward. Go around the map do whatever sounds fun to you. A great game for kids and cat lovers!

Learn about the Ages with this fun card game! Be the first to run out of cards, but be sure to always say hello according to the time of day! If you don't, you'll face the consequences!

A fast-paced card game where the winner will be the first to say goodbye to the special creatures.

Океаны: Глубина("Oceans: The Deep") includes:

     Oceans: 5-6 Player Expansion
     Oceans: Exclusive Promo Cards (normal non-foiled cards)
     Oceans: The Deep – Promo Pack 1
     Oceans: The Deep – Promo Pack 2


•••
Дополнение Океаны: Глубина включает в себя:

     дополнение "Океаны: Расширение для 5-6 игроков"
     дополнение "Океаны: Промо-набор"
     дополнение "Набор карт глубины №1"
     дополнение "Набор карт глубины №2"

Deploy Disasters to disrupt opponents, with effects that change depending on who you target. A disaster deployed at just the right time could disrupt your opponent's plans, forcing them to adapt.

This module features 24 unique cards and is compatible with Earth, Abundance, and Animal Kingdom.

—description from the publisher

Fashion Sketcher is a strategic trick-taking game for 2 to 4 players, where every trick becomes a fashion runway.
Bet boldly and impose your style by dominating trends — or by dazzling with originality.
A tense, elegant, and dynamic card game, crafted for discerning players who aren't afraid to step off the beaten path.
Its system of simultaneous open tricks, combined with a tactical betting phase and the freedom to break suit (may follow), offers a wealth of interesting decisions.
But the real twist lies in the trick resolution: the highest card wins when a colour dominates — but in a tie, the lowest card takes the trick.

boardgame: たべたのだぁれ？(Who Ate It?)
description:
The pugs have eaten all the candy! Which pug ate what...? But don't worry, the pugs' tongues are dyed the color of the candy they ate, and when you press a pug, it will stick out its cute tongue! If you can guess the pug with the same tongue colour as the sides of the dice, it will be your own dog park! The first person to collect six pugs wins! A family game of concentration that can be played with small children from 4 years old.

Find the pug that ate the candy!
たべたのだぁれ？ ("Who Ate It?") is a concentration game using a cute pug figure that sticks out its tongue when pressed.
12 pugs have eaten the candy and ran away.
Find the pug that ate the specified colour of candy and bring it to the dog park!
When you lightly press the pug, its cute tongue will stick out.
The tongue sticking out is so cute that even small children can play without getting bored!

How to play

1. Roll the dice
Roll two dice with colorful candy illustrations. Find a pug with a tongue of the same colour as the candy on the dice!

2. Check the colour of the tongue of the pug you chose!
Choose two pugs and check the color of their tongues.
Press the pug's stomach from the side with your thumb and forefinger, and its cute tongue will pop out!

3. Catch a pug!
When you find a pug with the same colour as the dice, bring it to your dog park. If you get both, you can roll the dice again.
The first person to catch six bugs wins!

—description from the publisher (translated)

To play Battleground Fantasy Warfare, you first build an army from your faction with an agreed-upon points limit. After deploying your units, you give each of them a standing order representing what you would like them to do on the Battlefield.

Your units will follow these orders until you change them or take control of them using command actions. Players get one command action for every 500 points available to build your army. This means players usually will have 3 or 4 command actions per turn. However most armies have between 6-12 units, each following the standing order you gave them at the start of the game. You must be shrewd in the use of your command actions, because you will never have enough.

Orcs feel no compassion, only hatred at those who did not suffer like them. They take gold but place no value in it. They conquer land but do not hold it. They pillage and slaughter for the pain it causes. They are ageless, their souls reborn after each death. They have no cause to struggle for, nor ambition to rule. They have only one driving desire: Burn It All.

—description from the publisher

Salsicha is a modern card game where the winner is the one who best uses their turns to make the smartest card choices and complete the "combina-cães" before their opponents.

At the beginning of each round, each player receives 5 cards, and another 5 are placed face-up in the discard area, along with 3 random "combina-cães" and the "Perfect Salsicha". On their turn, a player can draw 2 cards—either from the discard pile or the deck—and must discard one, checking for any extra effects (if the card is a head or a tail).

When a player has a complete "combina-cão" using all the cards in their hand, they can go out, ending the round, earning points, and starting a new one. If a player manages to present a "Perfect Salsicha", they end and win the game!

Total Blast is a fast-paced, head-to-head card game where players command magical cannons in a fierce back-and-forth battle for victory. Set in the colorful fantasy world of Blastonia, each player drafts and upgrades their cannons, loads them with powerful elemental cannon balls, and unleashes their might against their opponent. Strategic planning and quick thinking are the keys to out-blasting your rival and claiming glory.

Across four intense rounds, you'll:

     Draft cannon balls, upgrades, and abilities
     Load matching colored cannon balls into your cannons
     Upgrade and fortify your firepower
     React to your opponent's moves with smart plays and clever timing
     Use a-symmetric character abilities.
     Win by having the post powerful cannons in the line-up by the end of the game.


Total Blast is easy to learn but full of tactical depth. Players will need to balance offense and defence while adapting to the randomness of the draft. Should you invest in boosting a single powerful cannon or spread your firepower across all your weapons? Should you sabotage your opponent or focus on fortifying your own setup? Every choice matters — and every round can swing the momentum.

Whether you're a casual player looking for a quick, energetic match or a strategist plotting every move, Total Blast invites you to load up, aim true, and fire your way to victory.

—description from the designer

My First Math Dice contains 2 dice with faces 0 to 5, 30 chips and 2 towers for stacking chips. Each player takes a die and a tower. Players take chips equal to the numbers rolled, equal to the greatest number rolled, or equal to the sum of the numbers rolled until all the chips are used. The player with the most chips wins.

In the Section 99 micro-expansion for Brink, upgrading a ship now has a hidden cost - one of the cubes used is pulled from circulation and placed on a mysterious HEX - known as Section 99 - at the edge of the board. Players can choose to travel there to reclaim the lost resources, but doing so comes at the cost of 5 Prestige, a penalty for dealing in questionable territory. In lower player count games, Section 99 starts with one cube of each color, making the risk even more tempting.

—description from the publisher

Tex is a drawless connection game for two players: Red and Blue. It is played with trapezium-shaped pieces on the spaces of an initially empty, loosely square grid. The top and bottom edges of the board are colored red; the left and right edges are colored blue.

Two pieces are point-adjacent if they share nothing more than a vertex, and edge-adjacent if they share an edge segment longer than a vertex.

Red plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a piece of your color on an empty space. After your placement, for any two point-adjacent pieces of your color there must be another piece of your color edge-adjacent to both.

You win if there is a chain of edge-adjacent pieces of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, Blue will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Blue instead of making a regular move.

—description from the designer

In the hidden corridors of power, every favor counts.

Polls may sway the nation’s pulse, but behind every result lie public deals, secret betrayals, and unexpected alliances.

You are the leader of a political faction striving to increase your political capital while dodging scandals, outmaneuvering your rivals, and manipulating public perception.

Manage your resources with cunning, deploy key allies at just the right moment, anticipate your opponents’ moves, and adapt your strategy to the sudden shifts provoked by powerful figures.

Only those who master the delicate dance between influence and political capital will rise victorious in this ruthless game of ambition and strategy.

Welcome to House of cards.

—description from the designer

The big bargain sale starts today! Even if you had a target in mind, the excitement of the sale can make you lose your composure. At the store, you might end up buying cheap items one after another. Who will be the true smart shopper who doesn’t regret their purchases once they get home?

Bargain Rush is a trick-taking game about winning cheap but valuable cards. Play cards of colors not yet in the display and try to beat matching colors already on the table. Winners collect the lowest-valued card of each color from the display. At the end, you score by multiplying the lowest number of one chosen color by how many you collected but cards of other colors cost you points.

You are a minor angel on the verge of falling from grace. To cancel your descent into damnation, you must claim how much protection you’ve received from the archangels! Heaven runs on a presumption of goodness — if you cast doubt on someone else's claims, you might just turn the heavens upside down! But, since you're already scum about to fall, you’ll probably lie without hesitation.

Angelion is a trick-taking game where cards are played face-down and players must declare their number and suit — but lying is allowed.
