Please note that the rules are the same as Dominion of the Spear and Bayonet but used for the above wars in the time of Frederick the Great outside mainland Europe.

These are very very simple rules with armies of 3-6 units and a battle can be fought within 10 minutes.

24 historical battles are included ready to play along with army lists:
Prestonpans 1745, Inverurie 1745, Falkirk Muir 1746, Littleferry 1746, Culloden 1746, Arni 1751, Jumonville Glen 1754, Fort Necessity (Great Meadows) 1754, Monongahela (Braddock’s defeat) 1755, Bloody Morning Scout 1755, Lake George 1755, Plassey 1757, Carillon (Fort Ticonderoga) 1758, Condore 1758, Plains of Abraham 1759, Wandiwash 1760, Sainte-Foy (Quebec) 1760, Echoee Pass 1760, Third Panipat 1761, Martinique 1762, Siege of Havana 1762, Bloody Run 1763, Bushy Run 1763, Buxar 1764.

Use figures or paper and pen as there is no measuring (grid used). Uses 2 six sided dice (not included).

boardgame: Dominion of the Spear and Bayonet: Fight Battles of the Colonial Era – Solo Game
description:
Dominion of the Spear and Bayonet is a very very simple set of solo wargames rules, a standalone set built on the Dominion of the Spear game engine, allowing one to fight two Colonial armies (1800 - 1900 AD) against each other in around ten minutes. 
They are written for the game to generate a plausible and with some imagination an amusing narrative. Although figures can be used, battles can easily be fought on paper (no measuring) using pen or pencil and two different coloured six sided dice.

Armies consist of 3-6 units and the points system is included. There is a complete play through of a battle on the North West Frontier. A Quick Reference Sheet is on the back.

This is easily read on a tablet or mobile phone and can be printed as an A5 booklet (click booklet and fit to page in printing options). 32 A5 pages.

122 army lists are included covering the following conflicts: Second Anglo-Maratha War, Bolivian War of Independence, Argentine War of Independence, Mexican War of Independence, Tecumseh’s War, Fourth Xhosa War, Creek War, First Seminole war, The First Anglo Burmese War, Padri War (Sumatra), First Anglo-Ashanti War (Ghana), Java War, Second Seminole War, First Opium War, French Conquest of Algeria, Mexican-Texan War (Texas War of Independence), First Anglo-Afghan War, British invasion of Sindh, Franco-Moroccan War, Franco-Tahitian War, First Anglo-Sikh War, New Zealand Wars, Mexican-American War, Taiping Rebellion, Anglo-Persian War, Second Opium War, Indian Mutiny, French Intervention in Mexico, Fenian Raids, British Expedition to Abyssinia (Ethiopia), North West Frontier Black Mountain Expeditions, Russian Conquest of Central Asia, Third Anglo-Ashanti War, Aceh War (Indonesia), Great Sioux War, Nez Perce War, Second Anglo-Afghan War, Anglo-Zulu War, First Boer War (The Transvaal War), Belgians in the Congo, Mahdist War, Tonkin Campaign (Northern Vietnam) and Sino-French War, North-West Rebellion, Italo-Ethiopian Wars, Abushiri Revolt (Tanzania), Hehe Resistance, Second Franco-Dahomean War (Benin), First Matabele War (Zimbabwe), North West Frontier Chitral Campaign, Conquest of Gaza (Mozambique), Cuban War of Independence, North West Frontier Tirah and Tochi Campaign, Spanish-American War, Philippine-American War, Boxer Rebellion, Second Boer War.
family: History: Latin American Wars of Independence
History: Opium Wars and Taiping Rebellion

description:
The cold war carries on...

Semper Occultis (Latin for “Always Hidden” and the motto of MI6) is a competitive 2-4 player deckbuilder set in a Cold War gone long - the new “modern day plus one” global powers of Pan-America, Greater China, New Soviet Bloc and European States will be recruiting assets, building bases and subverting their opponents for victory points to take the world for themselves.

Semper Occultis brings a unique blend of mechanics to the deckbuilding genre:


     Hidden information. A face-down card market and the ability to ‘deploy’ Assets to bases without revealing them means you never truly know what your opponents are up to until they reveal it – or until you make a risky push against an Opponent’s base. Is their Asset a bluff, a proper defense, or a trap that steals all of your Intel?
     Closed economy. Players spend Intel to buy cards from the card market, but when they spend Intel it’s passed to an opponent of their choice to take advantage of on their turn. Hold back as much Intel as you can to starve your opponents out, or spend it all and give your opponents an advantage?
     Engine building. Bases provide effects at the start of your turn, with bases from the central deck providing two effects. Pick and choose what resources you gain; go on the offensive, reinforce your hand, steal your opponent’s Intel, or perform any one of multiple unique effects. Ways to score points off of every resource in the game (Espionage, Intel and cards in hand) creates multiple paths to victory, on top of just being able to infiltrate the opponent's bases.
     Highly variable play. A starter deck with no duplicate cards (and a faction exclusive card for each) make games different from the word go. Over 40 unique cards in the central deck leads to new conflicts and new choices every game. To place cards in the field, you need to discard another card from your four card hand, leading to tough dilemmas - build two undefended Bases, build and defend a Base, or infiltrate an opponent’s Base? Multiple hard choices need to be made every turn.
     Compact but deep. Semper Occultis has only 112 cards and rules that fit on two double-sided A4 sheets, making it a compact game but with enough depth for many replays. The two, three and four player games share elements but have entirely different feels - enjoy heavily competitive, calculated two-player games, three player crossfires where no-one wants to blink first, and four player free-for-all slapfights where friends can become enemies at the drop of a hat. Note that while we recommend you bring a certain set of extra components for ease of use, the game can be printed and kept as a pure card game.


Art Disclaimer
All icons were provided through game-icons.net. All art was AI-generated through Craiyon. (As much as we would like to have proper art, proper art is expensive.)

Print and Play Notes
We recommend bringing the following extra components to play:

     Six blue cubes and three lighter blue cubes (to serve as "Intel")
     Four six-sided dice (to use as VP dials)


Replacements for these components are provided via the Extra Cards PDF, intended to have the amount of VP you are on facing right-way up, the edge of the card at the amount of 'real' intel you have, and the number on the VP card being vertically aligned with the amount of Temporary Intel you have.


     A handful of four colors of cubes (to use as ownership markers)


These components are optional, but the table can get quite crowded; it can be hard to tell what Assets, when attached to Bases, belong to a certain player - especially if using print-and-play with opaque sleeves. If you use transparent sleeves or print double sided, you can get away with having the logo on the back being "the right way up" for the owner of a card, but I recommend cubes if you can find them.
The PDFs you will receive on purchase are a PDF of the rules (3 pages), a PDF of the cards with backs in both A4 and Letter, and a zip file with the cards as PNG files for use in platforms such as Tabletop Simulator.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Libertadors: Wargaming The Liberation of Spanish America 1808-1833
description:
Quick-playing wargame rules for the Wars of Spanish-American Liberation. While the colourful uniforms evoke the Napoleonic Wars, these campaigns were markedly different, and the game reflects this at every turn. Follow in the footsteps of Bolívar and San Martín at any scale you prefer, from 2mm to 54mm!

Designed specifically for the Wars of Liberation rather than adapting Napoleonic rules to these smaller, more cutthroat internecine actions, Libertadors forces players to manage force morale and inspire their troops to full and fierce faith in their cause. Commitment to the cause wins more battles than steady supply lines! With practice and the variable bound turn system, battles involving around 5,000 troops a side can be played in almost real time.

Focusing on heroes who lead by example, Libertadors’ straightforward yet thematic mechanics provide meaningful and flavourful tactical choices throughout the game. Included in the book are:

     Flexible army selection system with common troop types and special rules for the diverse armies of South and Central America.
     Special characters and historical units for each faction.
     Historical scenarios with maps and army lists.
     Random events table.
     Scenario generator for pick-up games, with separate missions for each player.
     A campaign system informed by the peculiar realities of the period.


—description from the publisher
family: History: Latin American Wars of Independence

description:
Dive into a world of artistic fun with Abstractable. Challenge your creativity and guesswork skills as you try to decipher what your friends are crafting from a unique collection of magnetic shapes.

Get ready for a game night filled with creativity, laughter, and friendly competition. Who will be the ultimate artist?

—description from the publisher​

Word has gotten out that pirate treasure belonging to Eric “The Terrible” has been discovered in the area and now adventurers are the hunt to get some of the treasure for themselves. The job of your crew is going to the ocean bottom and find the treasure. Underwater currents are constantly changing the landscape of the ocean, so finding the treasure can be somewhat of a challenge. But be careful, you only have enough funds to spend a day searching. If the crew finds all 12 treasures, you win!

Pirate Treasures is a cooperative memory game with a few twists. Each treasure location is identified by two sea life icons. Players will take turn flipping over two tiles. If a player flips over two tiles that matches both icons on the treasure card, it will be placed to the side to show it has been found. If you flip over a tile with a hourglass icon, take a time token and place it near the players. If players flipped over five hourglass tiles five times, the game has been lost. If both players find all 12 treasures, they win the game.

Players start with identical sets of six stones in different shapes and sizes. Players take turns rolling a 12-sided die, which may designate the stone to be placed, or require the removal of stones, or require that a stone or stones be placed in a certain way, etc. The first player to place all of his or her stones without causing a tumble wins the round and receives a point for each stone that the other players still have. Play to 21 points.

boardgame: Эволюция волшебных тварей: Бестии (Evolution: Another World – Bestias)
description:
Эволюция волшебных тварей: Бестии ("Evolution: Another World – Bestias") mini-expansion adds 5 new Evolution cards that can be played only as traits. These are the Aethereal trait cards. Only creatures who have witnessed a transmutation can gain such a trait.

Initially, this set of cards was a stretch goal for pre-order participants in Evolution: Another World on Kickstarter.

The storm howls. The longships set sail. Blood will water the earth before this saga is over.

Ravensong is a free rules supplement for the tabletop wargame Ravenfeas, designed for players who want to weave an ongoing narrative into their battles. This expansion provides simple but engaging rules for campaign play, allowing your warband to grow, suffer losses, and carve their legend into history.

Gather your warriors, chart your path to glory, and fight for your place in the sagas. Each battle has consequences—victories bring renown, while bitter defeats leave scars. 
Will your warband become legendary, or will their names be lost to time?

Features:

     Narrative Campaign Rules: Link your battles into an evolving saga.
     Printable Resources: Includes a campaign map, roster sheets, and tokens for easy play.
     Online Warband Builder: Easily create and manage your warband with the Warband Builder. 
     Solo Play Compatibility: Play Ravensong alone using the Ravenfeast Solo Play Rules! These rules allow you to control enemy warbands or mythical creatures using tactical AI-driven decision-making, ensuring a challenging experience every game.
     Compatible with Core Ravenfeast Rules: Seamlessly integrates with the free Ravenfeast system.


—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The TOURNAMENT comes to us from the World of Bedlam, a dystopian steampunk outpost of humanity containing elemental magic, lost technologies and floating continents.

In preparation of the TOURNAMENT, Players build their 5 member team through a series of card trades. They then build their Mechs through a series of tile draft and placement.

Upon completion of the build, the TOURNAMENT begins- Players score across 5 dimensions; Synergy, Speed, Strength, Scheme, & Segregation. The player with the highest score WINS!

—description from the designer

He Will Never Stop is a solo horror card game. You play as a final girl who is trying to outrun or stop an ensuing malevolent being.

Play occurs mostly within a hand of cards, which represent the terrain between yourself and your pursuer. Every turn, the player will try to manipulate their hand to put more gain more distance (add new cards to the right of the hand), while the pursuer will relentlessly get closer (discard cards from the left of the hand).

Games can end in one of three ways: By exhausting the draw pile (you've gotten away ...for now), by collecting four of a kind (you've built a trap from your surroundings to stop the pursuer), or by falling prey to the pursuer (hand is empty).

He Will Never Stop was released as part of the You Can Play These Games with Cards compendium of card games.

—description from the designer

Deque is a trick-taking game for 3-5 players with a twist: You can only play the cards in the left- or right-most position in your hand, and you may not re-order the hand you were dealt.

Players are dealt a hand of cards from a standard 52 card deck and must place a bid for how many tricks they expect to win. Then, throughout the hand, players are either forced to follow from the ends of their hand, or make the choice to discard from one side of their hand until they hit a card they can follow with, all in the effort of hitting their bid.

Deque is a game of constraint and compromise. It is named after the "double-ended queue" data structure, which is pronounced like "deck [of cards]".

Deque was first published in the You Can Play These Games with Cards compendium.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Race to Reactor, a nuclear power-themed card game for 2-4 players where strategy and sabotage are the keys to victory. Get ready to race against your fellow competitors to build the most powerful nuclear reactors!

However, it’s not just about building your reactors - it's about slowing down and supplanting others to seize the advantage. But beware! Your rivals will be playing cards designed to delay your progress, from component limitations to technical malfunctions. You'll need to strike a balance between advancing your own reactors and disrupting the plans of others. In the battle to power the planet your strength can turn on a single card.

Will you be forced to play a rival's components to get your reactor over the line? Or will you bide your time waiting for the perfect opportunity to strike? Shifting strategies abound in this fast-paced card game, will you win the Race to Reactor?

—description from the publisher

Many years ago, when nature was no longer self-sustainable, a group of world leaders gathered the most brilliant minds to think of viable solutions to renew the planet. Among the many projects, the Zenkai (Restart) Project was established, which consisted of creating synthetic energy-based beings that would have the ability to renew nature by releasing this energy in a clean way.

Nowadays, with the flora renewed and pollution controlled, the beings known as Zenkais are used as pets and engage in competitive battles to release energy. Over the years, it was discovered that these little monsters had the ability to undergo metamorphosis when winning battles, and these metamorphoses were popularly nicknamed evolutions, even though their species and DNA were not changed in the process.

You play as a challenger in the Zenkai League, a regional competition organized so that Zenkais can release their energy in a controlled and safe way. Your goal is to explore the distant cities in different habitats, capturing new monsters and battling them while making new friends along the way. Experience this incredible journey and be the big winner of the Zenkai League!

—description from the publisher

Each card in a deck displays a super element with six attributes of differing values. Six cards are dealt to each player, and any subsequently played card is replenished from the deck. Players take turns rolling two dice, one black and one white. Each die shows one of the attributes on each face. Following the roll, the roller decides which die's attribute will be used. Players simultaneously choose and reveal a card from their hands. The player with the winning card (lowest value of the designated attribute if the black die is used, highest value if white) takes all the cards and places them in the player's own pile. Play until the deck runs out. The player with the most cards in their pile wins.

Hyperdrive Odyssey is a space adventure game, mixing (literal) engine-building, trading, combat and exploration. Set in the “wild-west” of space you are guided by a choose-your-own-adventure style narrative through branching campaign missions to reach a dramatic conclusion!

This is a solo campaign-style space adventure using a few sheets and a handful of dice and tokens, and an adventure booklet/pdf.

Overview

In Hyperdrive Odyssey you will complete a series of missions, each with a different objective. Throughout the missions you will be travelling between planets and space stations: trading, upgrading your ship and fighting space pirates!

To do this you will need to manage Power (produced in the Hyperdrive) which is used to activate the ship systems (weapons, shields and engines,) and mine for natural resources.

The eponymous game module is the Hyperdrive engine: a circuit powered by ‘quantum energy’ dice. The dice move around the track, and each lap they complete the more energy they gain, and the faster they go. You can manipulate the dice to draw power from the engine to the other main systems on the ship.

As your engine spins up over the course of the game you have to carefully balance the actions you need to take each turn, and the amount of energy you need to hold back, to feed back into your engine.

The more you want to do on your turn, the higher the energy cost!

Over the course of a play-through there are branching paths to explore, and each of the missions has a different objective, from trading, exploring, fighting, fixing your ship and even a mystery to solve as you hunt around the galaxy for clues.

In addition to the solo missions are a series of multiplayer scenarios, ranging from a competitive economic sandbox to cooperative tower defense.

—description from the designer

Sevens is fishing game (such as Scopa) that is heavily based on the Spanish classic Escoba.

The game is played with a standard set of double-nine dominoes, with the 8s and 9s removed, and can be played from 2 to 4 players.

The game is played through several rounds in which the dealer deals 2 tiles to each player, and then 2 to 4 tiles to the middle of the table face-up.

On their turn, players play a single tile from their hand, trying to make the total pips on the played tile, together with any one set of tiles on the table, add up to 20. If it does, they capture those tiles. For example: If the table tiles are 2-3, 4-6, and 1-0, and you play the 0-5, you capture the 2-3, 4-6, and 0-5 as the total of all pips on the tiles is 20.

If a player captures all the tiles on the table, it is called a feje and is worth one extra point.

After all tiles are played, the dealer deals another 3 tiles to each player and another round is played. Rounds are played until there are no dominoes left in the stock. At that time, the player who "won" tiles last, "wins" the remaining cards on the table and scores are calculated.

Points are awarded:

1 point for each "Feje"
1 point for the player who won the most tiles
1 point for the player with most tiles that have a 7 on them. (1-7, 2-7, etc.)
1 point for the player who won the Belle Siete, which is the 0-7.
1 point for the player with the most Nikos; dominoes which sum to 7 (1-6, 3-4, etc.)
In case of tie nobody scores the point.

Special event: if, when the dealer places the initial dominoes on the table, they add up 20, the dealer "wins" all of them and scores a "Feje."

The first player or team to reach 11 points at the end of a hand wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Age of Alexander: Using Three Ages of Rome to fight the Wars of Alexander and his Successors
description:
Before the birth of the Roman Empire the history of the Classical world was dominated by the history of Greece, its cities, and kingdoms. The best known of these stories is the achievement of Alexander, son of Philip of Macedon, who would become known to the world as Alexander the Great.

The Age of Alexander starts with Alexander as King of Macedon.  
He convinces the Greek states to join him in the conquest of their traditional enemy, the Persian Empire. Moving into the Persian lands he fought several battles, defeating the Persians, and taking control of what is known today as the Middle East. Alexander forged the largest empire the world had seen but there was more. Moving east he campaigned against the Scythians and crossed the River Indus into India. The army of the Indian King Porus included numerous elephants which was different from anything that Alexander had met before. These beasts impressed the Macedonians and they would come to be a frequent element in the armies of Alexander’s Successors.

Unfortunately, Alexander died before he could achieve his vision for his Empire. The territories were shared between his generals (the Successors). These men then fought against each other, trying to increase their personal power. The competing pressures were too great and Alexander’s Empire broke up. The next century would see many wars between the Successors and their various descendants.

Watching from the west, the Romans saw a chance to take advantage of the weaknesses of the individual Successor states. They started by invading and conquering Macedonia. They followed this success with wars against the Galatians, and the various Successor states across the Middle East. These wars included the conquest of the Ptolemaic dynasty of Queen Cleopatra, which became one of the most famous stories in history.

This book provides an expansion to the Three Ages of Rome wargames’ rules. The six historical scenarios span the Age of Alexander and link directly into the first period of the Three Ages rules. The new army lists introduce armies from across the period of the expansion set. They complement the armies in the original set of rules enabling players to recreate more battles from the ancient period.

As in the Three Ages, in battle nothing is certain but generals that practice their skills are more likely to be victorious.

—description from the designer
family: History: Wars of Alexander the Great
History: Wars of the Diadochi and Syrian Wars

description:
Let the party begin with the TRIVIAL PURSUIT Party Quiz Volume 2!
This entertaining game is packed with brand-new questions from exciting categories such as Music, Travel, Lifestyle, Culinary Delights, Movies, TV Shows, and "Who Am I?".
Get ready for a power-packed quiz experience with an incredible 600 questions.
Test your knowledge, enjoy musical highlights, explore distant countries, and discover culinary pleasures.
With 12 correct answers, you emerge as the winner of the game!
With TRIVIAL PURSUIT Party Quiz Volume 2, every gathering becomes an unforgettable experience.
Whether you're playing with friends, family, or colleagues, this quiz guarantees exciting entertainment and loads of laughs.
Get it now and turn your next party into a quiz spectacle!

–description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

boardgame: RUSE: Bunny Boom Promo Punch Board
description:
Gamefound (Bunny Boom Campaign) Exclusive Promo Punchboard

The tiles provide additional growth points bonuses during the game.

How it works

     If you draw the Carrot Territory tile, add the Carrot token it to your personal board
     If, during another turn, another player draws the Carrot Territory tile, he takes the carrot from your personal board to put it on his own 
     Once the carrot is on a player's board, the Carrot Territory tile is discarded
     During the end of round scoring, at the same time as the cheese step, if a player stil hase the carrot, he can exchange it for 4 additional growth points
     The carrot is then discarded and returned near the global board

boardgame: Campeones: Oliver y Benji – Desafío Otomo
description:
The Desafío Otomo expansion brings even more excitement to your matches in Campeones. Oliver y Benji!

Desafío Otomo introduces new ways to play, including an exciting Solo Mode and a competitive 2 vs 2 Team Mode, so you can enjoy thrilling football duels whether you're playing alone or with friends.

Desafío Otomo also introduces new cards to enhance the original game, featuring additional Match and Motivation cards, along with 8 brand-new player signings to strengthen your team.

boardgame: Team X en het verdwenen geheime recept
description:
In a country far from here, the secret recipe has been stolen from the cellar with the triple lock. The king only wants the best detective team on the case, Team X. The crown witness, the motive, the crime scene, everything has to be researched.

This detective game ("Team X and the lost secret recipe") consists of letters, puzzles, hidden codes and interactive suprises. The mystery can only be solved if the whole family cooperates.

This game is meant to be played by a family, including at least one child from grade 4-8 (age approx. 8-12). It contains 5 envelopes, which a parent should deliver to their own mailbox. Each envelope contains puzzles and riddles, and will take around 30 to 45 minutes to crack the code.

In Coasmon players take on the role of master brewers who challenge each other by composing their own deck of coasters.

Each coaster represents a fantastic monster with unique characteristics and extraordinary powers that, combined with the positioning choices of the players, determine the course and outcome of the game.

Coasmon is easy to learn but requires tactical choices to master, characterized by fast matches (the game modes are 1 vs 1 or 2 vs 2) and can be played anywhere, even in a minimal space.

—description from the designer

When the night falls, toys come to life. New toys and old toys face off in an intense battle! Forgotten game consoles and teddy bears come face to face with fluffy interactive creatures and plastic unicorns. Who will survive the night?

Toys is a two-player game in which old toys and new toys fight each other. In this exciting and epic battle you try to beat your rival by claiming their victory space. Fight each other and eliminate their playing pieces, find the right strategy to move your pieces across the board and use action cards wisely to claim your victory.

Gothic re-imagines the Inner Sphere in a new, grimdark light. Insane experiments in cybernetics and biology have led to the development of Abominations, mutated creatures of flesh and steel capable of competing with BattleMechs on the battlefield. The Great Houses have been reforged into familiar, yet alien, entities that wage a perpetual war against one another in order to claim the entirety of the Human Sphere as their own!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Players create and extend thread-like 'Flukes' that score points for the number of 'Kinks' they contain, but have the power to attack other Flukes depending on the number of other non-Kink stones they contain.

Definitions:

     A Fluke is an isolated stone or a chain of stones connected to one to another, without branches nor triangles of adjacent stones. A Fluke is allowed to form a loop.
     A Kink is a stone at a 120 degree bend in a Fluke.
     The Power of a Fluke is the number of stones it contains that are not Kinks.


Play:
On your turn place a stone on an empty cell, followed by consequential removals according to the following rule:

One at a time, for each triangle of adjacent stones that contains 2 friendly stones, remove the other stone if the fluke containing the friendly stones is more powerful than the fluke containing the enemy stone.

The placement is only allowed if after all the consequential removals, the board is left in a legal state.

A legal state of the board means:

     There are no triangles or adjacent stones, whether mixed or same color.
     All stones are in Flukes: there is no branching.


Ending the game:
Passing is allowed. The game ends when both players pass.

The winner is the player with the most Kinks. -- In the case of a tie, the player with the most powerful fluke LOSES.

Kinks is playable on any hexagonal board, with sufficient stones or markers. Playing time depends on board size: Each player will place onto about 1/3 of the cells of the board.

—description from the designer

You are an explorer in the jungle and you discover a mysterious entrance. You decide to enter and find an extraordinary dungeon, where ancient creatures battle
each other for precious treasures. You wonder how these creatures have managed to survive in here for so long, but you also feel drawn to the treasures that lie within the dungeon. A strange feeling tells you that you must fight for the treasures, although you don’t know if you’ll ever be able to leave the dungeon...

In Mini Dungeon, you and your opponent create a 4x4 grid of adventurer cards, aiming to win the treasures that lie next to the grid. You do this by ensuring that you have the adventurer with the highest strength in each row and column. You must also consider the abilities of the different adventurers. Once the grid is completely filled, you determine per row and column who wins the treasures. The player with the most coins on their treasure cards wins the game!

In Neon Nexus, players are desperate freelancers at a cyberpunk job agency, trying to survive in a futuristic society. They take turns selecting job cards from the job board, bidding or trading cards, and adapting their hands using (red) wild cards. To score, you want to collect high-value job cards, while managing coins strategically.

After several rounds, players score their final sets based on color-based scoring rules and their remaining coins. The highest total wins.

The Fast, Fun, Collectible Battle Card Game Your Kids Will Love!

Ages 6+ (fun for all)
5 minutes to learn (hours of fun)
15 minutes to play
1v1 head-to-head play
Easy travel single deck

Unleash wild power cards. Outmaneuver with strong defenders. Change your fate with a single roll.

Swap battlefields. Destroy or resurrect creatures. Dodge attacks with a lucky roll or double your damage with a perfect one. Outwit your opponent by switching defenders, sneaking past their guard, or powering up with magic at just the right moment. Every move can turn the tide!

—description from the publisher

Who tells Simon what to say?

Which personality will prevail and take control of his mind?

PSIQUIS is a competitive area control and ability-drafting game, where the personalities of a boy named Simon fight for dominance over his mind.

At night, during the dreaming phase, players dive into Simon’s subconscious and battle for control of his mind through his mental functions, spending emotions and neurons to dominate the brain represented on the board.
During the day, they face the crossroads of Simon’s life, in the form of events that alter the course of the game.

In Echo Wave / 青き海の再生, you are a coastal fisherman striving to restore the ocean's ecosystem while making a living. During the game, players release fish to the sea to determine turn order, roll dice to act in ocean areas, and choose to fish, remove trash, or build artificial reefs. The ecosystem changes dynamically through feeding and reproduction each round.

You want to balance ecological protection and manage resources to earn the highest score.

Min-ified pocket-sized version of Not A Real-Word Game. This demo version is adapted for 2 or 3 players.

What's different:
- just 18 cards
- 3 players max (per deck)
- tokens not included

Combining Decks:
- each deck allows 3+ players
- combining decks will forever change your deck

Post-play, cards are split into new unique 18-card decks.

---
The original version can be played with 12+ players.

boardgame: Oranges & Lemons: Here Comes a Candle
description:
Here comes a candle to light you to bed,
And here comes a chopper to chop off your head!
Chip chop chip chop the last man is dead

Overview
Here Comes a Candle is an expansion to the game, Oranges and Lemons which requires the base game to play. There are three separate elements:


     A One Player expansion which is a solo-play version based around the six careers in the base game.  
     ‘Here Comes a Candle’ – an addition to the multi-player game which introduces events which impact gameplay but have variable durations. 
     ‘Here Comes a Chopper’ – a further addition to the multi-player game which incorporates objectives to achieve before a deadline.


In addition there are 10 new Cargo tiles to add variety to the main game and 25 larger bells between 50% and 100% larger that the original bells.

The two multi-player expansions can be used separately or together but cannot be combined with the One Player expansion.

—description from the publisher

In Icefall / アイスフォール, each player controls a pair of climbers — an older brother and a younger brother — and aims to climb to the top of a harsh ice wall while competing against other players.

You need to decide whether to let the older or younger brother climb according to the cards that are dealt in each round, but there may be times when you are in danger of falling or cannot proceed well. Sometimes it is important to replenish chocolate bars to conserve your energy. Which player will be the first to get the brothers to the top?
designers: Takanari Yamaguchi (ヤマグチ タカナリ)

boardgame: I'm the King of the Castle
description:
I'm the king of the castle, and you're the dirty rascal!

A fun, family-friendly dice-rolling and picking game where you fight, elbow, push, and pull your way to the top of the castle. The first to get five pieces in the castle wins and looks down at the rascals below!

At the start of each round the First player rolls all the dice into the dice tray. Starting with the First Player each player takes a turn choosing and using a dice. Play proceeds clockwise round the table until all the dice have been chosen and actioned. At the end of each round the player sitting to the left of the start player takes the dice and becomes the new start player for the next round.

There are 8 symbols on the dice (and two dice types). Symbols define the actions you do with the pieces: Up 1 step, Up 2 steps, Pull up a piece, Give a piece a legup, swap piecs, Leap over a piece, Push a piece down and the Dirty Rascal: Push 1 of each oponents pieces down a step.

When a starting piece or 'kid' reaches  step 3 they are upgraded to a 'King' whose actions are more powerful

First to get five kings to the top of the hill wins.

—description from the designer

Cult'en sac emerges as a tense cooperative struggle for area control and dominance. As a devoted Cult Leader, you must strategically deploy your loyal Cultists to exert your influence over key Locations and claim their potent Artifacts.

On your turn, you face a pivotal decision: Deploy two Cultists to bolster your presence across the map, OR deploy a single Cultist and strategically place a Clue Die upon any Location

But beware, your growing influence does not go unnoticed. Relentless Investigators also seek to unravel the mysteries and challenge your claim. Their presence will force you to carefully consider every placement and anticipate their meddling.

At the close of each round, only the faction with the strongest presence will seize the coveted Artifacts. These are not mere trinkets; they are the essential keys to unlocking unimaginable power and finally summoning a slumbering Deep Ancient Entity into our world. Will your cult prevail against the encroaching light and bring about the age of cosmic revelation?

—description from the designer

In 1871 a fire started at the O'Leary Barn swept through and destroyed the heart of the great city of Chicago.

In this game players both manipulate and move the fire and at the same time try to contain it and defeat it, protecting their own properties and goods and hidden secret objectives.

Can you save the windy city... or just watch it burn?

This game is from the same publisher as The Great Fire of London 1666.

The game uses the same core mechanics as Great Fire of London - wind cards move the fire, Action points move the fire fighters and put out the fire. Players have hidden locations to protect.

What is new? Its a totally new board based on the City of Chicago in 1871. Objectives are historically important locations in Chicago. Art will be 19th century American in style. New mechanics include:

     A representation of the alarm mechanism in city
     A representation of the attempt to get people and possessions across the river.   


description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – The Galaxy's Deadliest Affiliation Pack
description:
In CP223, Gamora, Nebula, Ronan and Drax come together for the Guardians of the Galaxy Affiliation Pack #2. This pack expands players’ collection of Guardians of the Galaxy characters.

As daughters of Thanos, Gamora and Nebula were trained to be the deadliest warriors in the galaxy. Using their abilities as the Guardians of the Galaxy and their own motives and accomplish their goals. Drax the Destroyer continues to fight to protect the innocent and pursue his quest to slay the Mad Titan Thanos. Ronan is a threat rightly feared by all who dare cross the Kree.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Guardians of the Galaxy Affiliation Pack
description:
The Guardians of the Galaxy come together in CP222. Bringing Star-Lord, Rocket, Groot and one of their newest members, the Cosmic Ghost Rider in one pack. Players can use other Guardians of the Galaxy with these characters to grow their collection.

Each character brings their unique abilities, powers and tactics to this affiliation pack. Star-Lord is an expert marksman and a clever tactician. Rocket is a deadly marksman, and cunning explosives expert whose friend Groot has exceptional strength and the ability to regenerate damage. Their bond gives them abilities and bonuses. They are joined by the Cosmic Ghostrider who is a powerhouse on the battlefield.

—description from the publisher

Death Doubt / 死神ダウト is a lying card game in which two players push the Grim Reaper onto one another, trying to wear down their lifespan!

If you think your opponent is lying, you doubt them. You can doubt as many cards as you like, but the more you doubt, the more negative points you will have. Which of the following will help you spot the other person's lies: probability, expression, or strategy?

A Fully Electronic Survival-Horror Board Game — No Moving Parts. Just Pure Tension. Inspired by the classics, this is a love letter to the golden age of zombie horror — drawing heavily from iconic films and survival-horror video games that defined the genre. Featuring a fully original musical score composed exclusively for the game and real voice performances from four professional actors, the experience is rich with cinematic atmosphere and escalating dread.

You're part of a four-person military unit trapped in a remote farmhouse. It’s 6:00 PM. Outside, the undead swarm. Rescue won’t arrive until 6:00 AM. Armed with only what you can scavenge, you must survive 12 relentless in-game hours as the horde breaks through the defenses.

Driven entirely by LEDs, this one-of-a-kind game eliminates moving parts — every action, threat, and shift in the environment is signified through immersive light cues.

Search for ammo. Barricade the doors. Fend off the dead. Scavenge supplies. Make impossible choices. With multiple endings, no two playthroughs are the same — your story of survival is yours alone.

Dawn is your only escape. Can you survive the night?

—description from the designer

Tide of Steel is a dice-driven Solo PNP wargame where time is ticking! Lead your troops, dodge enemy blasts, and take out 3 of the 6 enemy turrets before all your 12 soldiers are eliminated and break through enemy fire to seize the beachhead.

The board is a vertical grid with 8 rows and 6 columns with the Turret Zone above, containing 6 turrets. Your soldiers can move freely in the rows below coming in from the Landing Zone below. It takes 3 successful hits to destroy a turret.
You roll dice to move your soldiers and hit the turrents and in the enemy phase roll dice for the turrent's attack back. After the Enemy Phase, proceed to the next turn until a victory condition is achieved.

—description from the designer

Weltraumpioniere in rostiger Glut is a strategic card game about colonizing the solar system. In this game, players assume the role of pioneers who must establish and sustain colonies on Mars or other celestial bodies.

The game combines elements of resource management with strategic development. Each player must build a functioning colony from various module cards, taking into account various aspects such as energy supply, food, and infrastructure. Players face challenges such as resource scarcity and unforeseen events that can threaten their colonies. The player to reach a preset number of victory points is the winner.

"Space Pioneers in Rusty Embers" is aimed at players who appreciate complex strategic games with a science fiction theme.

boardgame: In the Shadow of Neon Lights
description: A trick-taking game of power boosts and weapons drafting where players, after being dealt a hand of five cards, pick an additional two from a face-up row. The game progresses with each person playing a fighter card to the table followed by a weapon or power boost to add to the fighter's numerical value, with the highest value winning the trick. The goal is to win three tricks.

boardgame: Turf War: Unicorn City – The Expansion
description: This ten-card expansion pack adds special action cards featuring a child in a unicorn-onesie with their collection of unicorn-themed toys that either attract an opponent's card to you or scares them to the discard pile.

Edition used for the 1982 World Championships.
The board and mechanics are those of a traditional Monopoly game.

Ladrillopoly ("Brickpoly") is based on the traditional version of "Monopoly." Set during the real estate boom in Spain, and featuring the humor and satire that characterize the Spanish magazine "El Jueves," will you be able to become the most corrupt owner on the board?

Players each are dealt 10-12 face-down cards as their respective USE piles. Cards not dealt form a face-down Draw deck. The face of a card is red, yellow, blue, or green. Four face-up piles --- one for each color --- will be created as a play area next to the Draw deck.

Players draw five cards from their USE decks to start the game, and they take turns shedding as many cards from their hands as possible. Each card contains a statement about what is visible in the play area. For example, a card may say, "The number of green cards is equal to the number of cards of another color." If the statement is true, the player plays the card from their hand to the pile of the same color. The player continues to play cards from their hand to the play area until no card in their hand displays a true statement. Unplayed cards go into the players STUCK pile and are replenished from the Draw deck. The player draws additional cards from their USE pile if necessary to bring their hand count up to five.

The round ends when one player's USE pile is depleted. All cards remaining in players' hands and USE piles are added to the players' respective STUCK piles and scored as one point each. Start another round and play until a player reaches 100 points, at which time the player with the fewest points wins.

You are an experienced co-driver. Your job is to navigate in rally races. Dazzled by the hefty reward, you decide to navigate an amateur driver in a one-day rally in five days' time.
However, the client is driving a super-powered rally car from yesteryear, which an amateur driver cannot handle.
Sensing danger, you rush to train the driver. The big reward depends on winning the race.
Successfully complete your training and set the best time in the race to win the big reward!

—description from the designer

Solar Eclipse is a trick-taking game of cunning combinations as players play two cards to every trick. There are two suits in this game, sun and moon cards. Sun cards are always played face up, but moon cards can be used to deceive and be played face down. The ultimate combination is a pair of sun and moon cards which share a value.

In order to achieve victory, players must skillfully deceive their opponents and predict how many tricks they'll win each round.

—description from the publisher

Dogface is a solitaire WWII tactical combat RPG in which players take on the role of a single U.S. soldier, known as a "dogface" -- a hard-earned nickname given to the infantrymen who fought "their war" on the ground, on foot, through the mud and snow. Taking inspiration from such great games as Advanced Squad Leader, Combat! and the Combat Commander series', and Patton's Best, Dogface seeks to provide the player with the ultimate WWII man-level RPG experience, intentionally restricting combat to little more than the platoon level so as to avoid counter clutter, excessive unit stacking and tracking, and other difficulties. Depending on your chosen historical outfit, your dogface may fight the war in Europe from 1944 to 1945 from the beaches of Normandy, through the hedgerows of France, across the French countryside at breakneck speed in the mad dash towards the Franco-German border, fight to survive in the snowy Ardennes in the Battle of the Bulge, cross the Rhine, and join the sweeping advance through a ravaged and devastated Germany, ending the war somewhere within that country, or Austria, or Czechoslovakia. The game is dynamic, with missions, maps, enemy units, and so on generated through a mission generator. Whether a combat mission or patrol mission (combat patrol or recon patrol), your "doggie" will resolve his campaign on a week-by-week basis, suffering platoon casualties, losing comrades, maneuvering around German machine gun nests, tanks, halftracks, and any other myriad of enemy unit type. Along the way, he may earn decorations, awards, and experience. He may become the acting platoon leader through catastrophic losses, or he may be the platoon leader by default as a Lieutenant (2d or 1st). His fellow "doughs" may come to like or despise him, and his commanding officer (CO) may trust him or loathe him, depending on his performance. His ultimate goal, of course, is to survive World War II in Europe and return home, one way or another.

Dogface includes comprehensive rules, tables and missions books; a player character information sheet; two mats to track in-mission and in-patrol statistics; three counter sheets including soldiers, machine guns, bazookas, tanks, halftracks, radios, flamethrowers, and so on; seven combat calendars following the historical path of seven chosen units throughout their tenure in World War II in Europe (specifically, the 1st, 2d, 8th, 66th, and 80th Infantry Divisions, as well as the 4th Armored Division and 82d Airborne Division); and fifteen different maps representing hills, "Omaha" beach, rural areas, towns, urban areas, and woods.

Dogface requires two six-sided (d6) dice to play, and may be (but is not required) enhanced by taking notes and keeping a log on a separate sheet of paper, or digitally with any free word-like program, as your soldier's story develops.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: History: World War II (Battle of the Bulge)

description:
Bevrijdingsspel ("Liberation Game") appears to be a relatively scarce WWII Dutch game celebrating the liberation of the Netherlands by the Allies from Nazi Germany.

The game consists of a colorful Gameboard with central images of a liberated Netherlands with local Dutch people celebrating with Allied troops, relief convoys of ships, ambulances and food drops, while German soldiers and spies are seen arrested or fleeing. These pictures are bordered by squares spaces that players advance around.

Before the game starts 48 (numbered 1 -48) "Occupied Netherlands" cards are placed on top of the Liberated Netherlands images. Together the 48 cards depict images of Nazi oppression and occupation of the Netherlands, thus covering the happy celebrations on the board.

Players roll a dice and move around the outer border spaces. If the land on certain squares that show pictures that would aid liberation; such as - illegal papers, smuggle weapons, sabotage, hiding people or help an Aviator the player then removes one of the 48 squares. Players can land on a certain squares which sees them captured and detained but all can be released if another player lands on a "Free Prisoner" square. In return, any freed prisoner gives the liberator one of their 48 "Occupied Netherlands" cards.

When all 48 cards are removed and the entire country is liberated the game ends. Each player adds up the value of their collected cards and the highest total is the winner.

—user summary

Tumbas is an Indonesian card game designed by Tabletoys, which is a popular board game publisher in Indonesia. The game is themed around grocery shopping and tests memory skills. Players must recall and match items on shopping list cards.

Theme:
The game revolves around the scenario of forgetting a shopping list and trying to remember the items.

Gameplay:
Players memorize the items on the cards, then try to recall them while covering the cards. If they succeed, add more items on the table!

Memory Focus:
The game emphasizes memory skills and the ability to recall information.

Target Audience:
The game is suitable for players aged 8 and above, with a difficulty level of 4/10.

The Apollo 8 mission led astronauts Frank Borman, Jim Lovell and Bill Anders to become the first humans to see the far side of the Moon and witness the first rise of the Earth above the horizon of our satellite. The iconic photograph, known as Earthrise, makes us shrink before the immensity of the Cosmos.

In the game you will play several turns to obtain Mission cards and Maneuver tokens while trying to avoid Alerts. You will gain rewards and maybe, go down in History.

If you are lucky enough, you will capture the best photographs of the Mission and whoever earns the most Honors will be the winner of the game.

Earthrise is one of the most iconic images of space exploration. Captured in 1968 during the Apollo 8 mission, it shows the Earth rising over the Moon’s horizon, offering a stunning view of our planet, in contrast to the Moon’s desolate surface.

—description from the publisher

I Die Innocent (2025) is a game about power and betrayal. A perfumed viper’s nest of intrigue and shifting alliances in the Royal Danish Court in the second half of the 18th century.

Players assume the roles of royal physician and de facto ruler of Denmark-Norway, Johann Friedrich Struensee (1737-1772), and Privy Councillor, Ove Høegh-Guldberg (1731-1808), respectively. By deploying whatever resources are available to them, players build their courts and enter into coalitions to ingratiate themselves with the mentally unstable King Christian VII.

The story goes like this: Johann Friedrich Struensee is one of the most controversial figures in the history of Denmark. His 16-month takeover of the government of Denmark-Norway, and his affair with Queen Caroline Matilde, came to a violent end when he was arrested, convicted for crimes against the crown, lése-majesté, and executed in the spring of 1772.

I wanted to make a game about that: the intrigues, the scandals, the power struggles arising from Struensee's liberal reforms, something that felt like the scene from the film, "The Favourite" (2018), where a bunch of wigged Members of Parliament entertain themselves by chucking oranges at a well-nourished naked man wearing only a wig. That is, I wanted to somehow represent these useless upper-class types and nobles – especially the male characters – as incompetent and powerless twits. A game that would feel like being in the palace corridors of candlelit flickers against gilded mirrors and bad teeth; courtiers and snakes whispering in a brutal social environment where everyone is basically untrustworthy. Daggers wrapped in velvet.

And so, luckily, it turned out, the brilliant game designer, Amabel Holland, had already provided a basic mechanic for simulating this. "I Die Innocent" is a hybrid mix of "Reign of Witches" (2020) and "Republic of Virtue" (2021), representing the dramatic historical events during the years of Struensee. A small piece of Scandinavian political history. By using and adjusting Amabel's system to reflect the people, events and crises surrounding Struensee, the cards in the game represent the relations between players and between the cards themselves: some cards, for example, are more or less useless without other cards, etc.

In particular, I wanted to add some complexity and nuance to the otherwise doltish representations of Struensee and Caroline Matilde, offered in works such as writer Per Olov Enquist’s "The Visit of the Royal Physician" (1999) and the film, "A Royal Affair" (2012). In these works, Struensee unites the figures of the reformer, the lover, the thinker, the doctor and the friend in one single incarnation that presents itself against the reactionary forces of the Danish royal court and political landscape of 18th century. The main villain of Per Olov Enquist’s novel is Ove Høegh-Guldberg, the hardheaded, royalist jerk who, in contrast to Struensee, embodies both political reaction and human impoverishment. Conservative, sexually congested and contempt, Guldberg is the archvillain, whose personal shortcomings drive his lust for power. The plot: the natural, the genuine and the human against the barren, degenerate and inhuman. This plot is also replicated in the film "A Royal Affair" (2012), starring Mads Mikkelsen and Alicia Vikander, where the tragic and beautiful hero Struensee (and his equally beautiful lover Caroline Matilde), perishes in the backward and politically primitive system.

In fiction, films and literature, in short, Struensee and Caroline Matilde are typically portrayed as heroes of the Enlightenment in a country immersed in the darkness of feudalism and autocracy. Their story a banal and half-baked mix of Romeo and Juliet and Joan of Arc fighting to change the country and give freedom to the people. In reality, it was not like that. It never really is.

Historically, Struensee and Caroline Matilde were not even in love. They were not real-life Romeo and Juliet. Rather, they were using each other to gain what they both wanted: power. In fact, the couple reflected what everyone else in their segment of society wanted: power. Like all the other inbred degenerates at the top of society - the smug upper classes, the nobility, the aristocracy, the wealthy merchants - Struensee and Caroline Matilde were no different. They all wanted power, and in order to get it, they would exploit and use each other as disposable cutlery.

Like contemporary life in the greatest pyramid scheme and zero-sum game of late capitalism, and our postdemocratic, hyperpolitical moment, there are no real winners: the rich and powerful don't care, as long as profit is to be made on ecological collapse and social and racial injustices. The characters in this game are no different in their blind will to power.

—description from the designer

Legends: WWII  is a card deck based simulation of World War II battles where strategy meets historical authenticity.

Each Country kit includes 65 cards for one nation, allowing you to build and play its unique strategy, along with a set of dice.

Each Event Kit contains at least two Country Kits featuring special units tailored for a specific historical battle, plus a themed map and a set of dice to match the scenario.

—description from the designer

Finally, the popular VGTV series by Mads Hansen is now available as a card game!

You know when you really want to laugh at something funny, but your boss is watching or the teacher has asked you to be quiet? You’re going to experience a lot of that with this game.

Ikke lov å le (“Not Allowed to Laugh”) tests how good you are at holding back your laughter. Invite your funniest friends and try not to laugh yourself to death! Have fun with Imitations, Challenges, Jodel, and watch your friends make fools of themselves trying to do Stand-up. Just remember — No laughing allowed!

In Cat Nap, rival cats sprawl out across a shared blanket, claiming cozy corners and stretching to impossible lengths. Using a charming collection of cat-shaped tiles, players take turns building out tails, body-segments, and faces—completing cats to earn the right to claim a color and snatch tiles from the discard pile for future use.

The board starts blank, but by the end it’s a tangle of overlapping kitties in all shapes and sizes. Will your feline creations outstretch the competition?

—description from the designer

High Adventure: Treasure Hunt is a print-and-play solo expedition into danger, mystery, and the lure of lost treasure. You are the captain of a desperate expedition, guiding a dwindling crew through a deadly jungle in search of a priceless artifact: the Grail. Armed with only a map, your wits, and a pool of dice representing your party’s strength, you'll journey through uncharted terrain—braving illness, injury, and the ever-looming threat of a terrible beast. Chart your path across rivers, jungles, and viper-filled dens. Gather provisions, decipher ancient tablets, and uncover cryptic clues to narrow down the Grail’s location. When you're ready — or when time runs out — attempt a single dig and pray you’ve chosen wisely. But it’s not enough to find the treasure. You must also make it back to base camp alive, carrying enough gold to keep your crew loyal or face defeat even in victory.

Each mission consists of 3 game sheets: hex based Map with over 20 terrain types, Expedition with Exertion, Camp, and Dig sections, Events with Skill tree and Combat sections.  Each mission has unique conditions. Each turn represents 1 day of your mission.  Dice are used to manage both crew and burdens.  
 —description from the designer

Brutal Beatdown - Primal is the third standalone release in the Brutal Beatdown series. You don’t need the first game Brutal Beatdown - Infestation to play. The game offers multiple play modes—including Beatdown, Training, Head-to-Head, and a branching-path Story Mode.

Aside from new fighters, items, enemies and special cards specific to each iteration of Brutal Beatdown, this campaign is introducing more new stuff like Dark Energy Beings to mix!

The food from 'CARRERA A LA NEVERA' has found another way to survive humans: by fleeing! The empty fridge has decided to go after them to be full once again.

Find 5 of the 7 food items and be the first to fill it! Watch out for the Handle, which can steal food from you in the act.

NYAM! It's a family board game for 2 to 6 players involving set collection, visual agility, and interaction between participants. For ages 6 and up, with games lasting between 15 and 20 minutes."

This expansion is all about cynicism, stupidity, and childishness. You'll need to electrify the treatment rooms without electrocuting your patients, add lighting in front of your Clinic, and install a few Food Trucks to accommodate your staff's new eating habits. If that's not enough, you can even pay a few taxis and buses to bring you new fake patients, give a few drops of alcohol to your real patients, or even hydrate your elderly during a heatwave in Clinic Deluxe Edition! New solo objectives have been added at the end of this rulebook.
All modules can be combined endlessly, unless otherwise stated in the rules.

—description from the publisher

This is the 2025 new expansion for Tramways the base game !
Tokyo 1954 simulates the Godzilla attack on the city in 1954
Pittsburgh has been developed with Todd Sanders who is living in this city. It helps the designer of Tramways to get the feeling of the city, the traffic jam and the different roles of each part of the city.
Both expansions include new meeples !
Both maps are solo, duo compatible with map scalings !
Small Print run for this last expansion before a reprint of Tramways to celebrate its 10th anniversary in 2026.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Эволюция волшебных тварей: Бунт стихий (Evolution: Another World – Uprising of Elements)
description:
Эволюция волшебных тварей: Бунт стихий ("Evolution: Another World – Uprising of Elements") is a set of two mini-expansions.


     Epic Beasts module includes 8 cards with 4 Epic Energetic (blue) traits on them (2 copies each). Each player will have only one creature with such a trait.



     Bestias module includes 5 Aethereal trait cards that can be played only as traits. Only creatures who have witnessed a transmutation can gain such a trait.


Initially, this set of cards was a stretch goal for pre-order participants in Evolution: Another World on Kickstarter.

The only thing more dangerous than the denizens of the world of Pathfinder is Golarion’s monstrous dating scene! In Pathfinder Monster Match!, compete with your friends to court all kinds of curious creatures, from vivacious vampires and dashing dragons to… a sentient pile of ooze? Why not! When animus turns to amorousness, a whole new world of romantic possibilities opens up!

Players will take their monstrous romantic prospects on a wide array of dates to get to know them better and give gifts to demonstrate their affection, all in hopes of finding their perfect match!

Pathfinder Monster Match! supports 2 to 6 love-starved adventurers and plays in about 30 minutes. The game includes 80 cards, 90 wooden heart tokens, and 84 MATCH! tokens.

—description from the publisher

Goalkeepers is a turn-based game where players complete actions determined by which dice they roll.
The aim is to KEEP as many GOAL dice as possible to score the most points and take the win.

boardgame: Age of Steam Deluxe Expansion: Beer & Pretzels / Reversteam
description:
Two additional expansion maps for Age of Steam Deluxe as a Kickstarter promo for funding the Deluxe Expansion Volumes V and VI.


    Beer and Pretzels
    Reversteam


The new maps are updated versions of classic Age of Steam maps adapted to the new Deluxe art style. These maps cover a wide range of play counts and styles - each with its own unique twists and gameplay.

In Ruins is a tabletop storytelling and dungeon building game for 1-6 players.

Firstly, everyone generates the history of a castle as it is built around the initial throne room, adding rooms and special items (of varying values) as they go.
Secondly disasters occur and players destroy or damage rooms to disadvantage their opponent's faction in the third stage.
Finally, players use their factions to control the most valuable rooms in the Dungeon, gaining control of the old Throne room and winning the game.

Taking cues from the mechanics of mining colonies, Ship-Tuners have repurposed Mining Drones to fill their customers’ needs. Fitting them with advanced weapons, shield generators, scanners and more. The new drones will give you an edge in the exploration of the galaxy.

—description from the designer

The popular sitcom, Dysfunctional, is preparing to film its final episode of the season. Since there is a lot riding on this episode, the director had the core cast members come in for some extra rehearsal time a few days before filming. What was set to be a normal rehearsal day turned into something much darker
when director, Keaton Baldwin, failed to return to set after lunch break. It turns out Keaton had a good reason for being
late. It's hard to direct a television show when you've been murdered. So, hit the set, turn up the laugh track, remember
all the catchphrases from your favorite shows, and see if you can solve The Sitcom Mystery.

From the hit Netflix series G.L.O.W comes its successor. Airing on CBS. Women of Wrestling (WOW) is one of the most popular women’s wrestling promotions in the world with over 300,000 viewers weekly in the U.S. alone.

The game is a fast-paced, compact, 5-7 minute showdown built for players who love mind games, big combos, and big personalities. It’s quick to set up, incredibly fun, and fits in your pocket.

Players attack opponents using their cards which make up their move set, with the hope of not only dealing damage to their opponent and keeping the initiative, but also to string a combo that would devastate their opponent and end the round. Meanwhile, Defenders attempt to guess their opponents attack and decide whether to attempt to block the attack using dice or guessing their opponent’s attacks correctly so they may deal an awesome counter that would gain them the initiative. The player that reduces their opponent’s health to zero pins their opponent and wins the match!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ANKH: Rune of life – Calamity Befall
description:
You, the new Pharaoh, have faced the first trials—— the evil apostles from the faraway bring the calamity to Egypt and shackle the power of the deities.
You must break through the several obstacles to defeat them,and save your people.
The Great Pharaoh, may the Ra blesses you!

The first time Hasbro dared to make country editions.
The dashboard design is inspired by the "Here & Now" edition.

What is the setting?

In ancient Far East lands, military commanders value honor above all. They compete and cooperate, seeking allies from powerful outside warriors when their nations' forces are limited. The most trusted commanders on the battlefield will be remembered as the most honorable.

What is the goal?

Players collect "Honor" by playing cards on the battlefield. Earn bonus points by creating sets with the same numbers or sequences. The highest point-scorer wins.

How do you play?

Players play cards to the Battlefield (IKUSABA) or Reserve (RONIN) areas. On the Battlefield, the players who have played the most cards of the same color form the winning faction and gain Honor (points). In the Reserve, players with lower-numbered cards can select sets of cards to strengthen their hand.

Season-marked cards trigger special effects that can influence gameplay. The game ends when two or more players have empty hands. Earn points for each card you acquire, with additional bonuses for collecting matching numbers or sequences.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Jigsaw Duels – Darth Vader vs. Obi-Wan Kenobi
description:
COMPETITIVE PUZZLING
Adding an innovative twist to the standard jigsaw, Ridley’s Jigsaw Duels have gameified the puzzle to create a unique play pattern that appeals to all.


     LIGHT SIDE vs DARK SIDE - Who will claim victory this time: Obi-Wan Kenobi or Darth Vader? Each side has a 70-piece jigsaw to complete - but there is only one final piece that fits both puzzles. 
     LET'S DUEL - Choose your side and get ready to race your opponent. The first person to finish their jigsaw and then grab the final, double-sided piece wins the battle for the galaxy!


—description from the publisher

Sci-Me! mechanics is a fast-paced card game that blends dimensional analysis with the competitive world of scientific publishing. Step into the shoes of a scientist racing to publish groundbreaking research by constructing logical formulas from your hand of cards.

In Sci-Me!, publications are your currency. Your goal? Be the first researcher to publish three successful findings. But time is limited—so you’ll need to think strategically, manage risks, and choose your actions wisely.

Each turn, players pick two actions from a set of five, using them to build meaningful formulas by arranging cards in the correct logical order. Once you think you’ve got a valid scientific statement, you can attempt to publish it. But beware—your formula must survive a random peer-review check. The shorter your statement, the tougher the review!

Do you have the logic, timing, and nerve to out-publish your rivals? Get ready to think fast and publish faster in Sci-Me! Mechanics.

—description from the designer

In the midst of the zombie apocalypse, the Rougarou (the Acadian werewolf) has emerged from the Louisiana swamps to terrorize the streets of New Orleans.  You and a group of survivors, ten in all, have set off on a sixty mile journey from New Orleans to Thibodaux, Louisiana in an attempt to escape the bloody rampage of the Crescent City and reach the security of a small, well-armed rural town.  Your group of fleeing humans must first cross the Mississippi River to Algiers, then slush through the marsh to Paradis, then along the Southern Railroad tracks to Des Allemands, next into the swamps until you reach, Raceland, and then up Bayou Lafourche into the safety of Thibodaux.  You are not necessarily safe in numbers, as the Rougarou is in relentless pursuit.  Other lost souls may join you along the route, but the Rougarou will pick off members of your group one by one until you either kill it or reach Thibodaux with at least one surviving member.

—description from the designer

In GameChanger, you experience the journey from a board game enthusiast to a first time designer. Immerse in the wonderfull world of board games. Get help from people all around the globe to enhance your ideas to create the most popular tabletop game for years to come!

Each player has their own bag of 12 2-sided dice which are workers with numbers 1, 2, 3 and 4. They represent the amount of players at a table. During each round, everyone simultaneously draw 6 of these from their bag. Extra chips with special abilities that you draw, which have been collected by playing relations cards, are put aside to use during this round. Each player shakes their 6 workers and let them drop to reveal their value. Action spots and their strenght can be triggered, depending on the amount of workers you place. The value of your workers on the same location has to be either equal or different.

Each player will evolve as a person, by placing workers on the following actions spots: boardgame nights at home or with friends, board game shops/cafés, board game clubs, local game fairs, a prototype spot, social media, world game fairs and a crowdfunding area. You will 

     learn and improve your game explanation skills
     discover new mechanics and concepts
     build up relations and gain fans to help you in your journey
     attract more players
     design and enhance your prototype
     create buzz about your game on social media and game fairs
     run a succesfull crowdfunding campaign
     try to enter the BGH (board game hotlist)


By improving your skills on the available tracks you will be able to gain 'hype' or 'buzz' points. Within a maximum of 6 turns you create as much hype as possible to release the most succesfull game of the year.

—description from the designer

A new challenger enters the Arena: pink space birds with beams that hit even when they miss!

The Glyphon expansion introduces a brand-new faction, plus two hilarious sponsors:


     Void Jugglers, who flip the fight by letting you play an upside-down battle.
     Ray Rebuff, which activates bouncing beams that ricochet off shields!


Mix and match with all base game content. Play with up to 5 players (or 6 if you also have the BlackHole expansion).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dominion of Frederick the Great: War of Austrian Succession & 7 Years War – Solo Game
description:
This the Dominion of Frederick the Great Wargames Rules Solo Game.

This standalone set of rules covers the War of the Austrian Succession and the Seven Years War in mainland Europe. It is built on the Dominion of the Spear game system but is a brand new set of rules with appropriate rule changes and new rules. (Its companion set is Dominion of the Claymore, Tomahawk and Talwar which covers the same time period but outside mainland Europe. This is being sold in a separate listing). These are very very simple rules with armies of 3-6 units and a battle can be fought within 10 minutes.

24 historical battles are included ready to play:

Mollwitz 1741, Chotusitz 1742, Dettingen 1743, Madonna dell’ Olmo (Cuneo) 1744, Fontenoy 1745, Hohenfriedberg 1745, Soor 1745, Kesselsdorf 1745, Lobositz 1756, Prague 1757 AD, Kolin 1757, Hastenbeck 1757, Gross-Jägersdorf 1757, Rossbach 1757, Leuthen 1757, Zorndorf 1758, Hochkirch 1758, Güstow (Tornow) 1758, Minden 1759, Kunersdorf 1759, Liegnitz 1760, Torgau 1760, Burkersdorf 1762, Freiberg 1762.

There are also rules to construct your own armies and some simple random tables to generate Imagi-Nations armies. Use figures or paper and pen as there is no measuring (grid used). Uses 2 six sided dice.

—description from the designer
family: History: War of the Austrian Succession

description:
A new challenger enters the Arena: boarding pirates! Never get too close to a BlackHole!

The BlackHole expansion introduces a brand-new faction, plus two tactical sponsors:


     Safer Space, which makes the richest player even richer.
     Live 4D, which turns shields into a source of points!


Mix and match with all base game content. Play with up to 5 players (or 6 if you also have the Glyphon expansion).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Death Card: Card-Based Vietnam Wargame Rules
description:
Death Card is a set of Vietnam wargaming rules using playing cards instead of dice. 
These rules are multi-scale and can be used for both small-unit actions and large battles, involving both asymmetrical warfare and conventional warfare scenarios. 
There is no need to rebase your units, and the focus is on ease of play and fast-moving games. 
The rules cover use of armoured fighting vehicles, helicopters, artillery fire, air strikes, and riverines. 
Also included are engineers and sappers, civilians, dog teams, medics, mines and booby traps, field defences and combat engineering. 
Card-based play emphasises a key point other Vietnam War rules miss - resupply - which can be applied to different scenarios to reflect your units’ ammo levels. 
No rulers or fixed movement distances are used, and a verbal commands system makes for intuitive play when manoeuvring your units on the tabletop, with direct fire limited only by line of sight; a crucial factor in rumbles in the jungle or close-quarter urban warfare, where any misstep could be fatal for your troops. 
And watch out for the Ace of Spades!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Light Speed: Arena – Kickstarter Sponsored Asteroids
description:
Three exclusive sponsored asteroids to thank our amazing Kickstarter backers!


     Community Asteroid: Can't be hit, but beams passing through it get a boost from the cheering crowd.
     Sorry, I’m Late: Spaceships show up only when it’s time for them to shoot. Classic.
     Space Xmas: All asteroids blink and aren't always there… but they are always festive!


Mix and match with all base game and expansion content.

—description from the publisher

DrawBack - Get ready to be DRAWN into a party game that's BACK-TO-BACK laughs!

Remember when you were in grade school, and friends played that game where they drew letters on someone's back with their finger, and that kid had to guess what they were writing?

No?

Well, that makes sense - it really wasn't that much fun.

But NOW, in Jason K Fisher's latest game DrawBack, that idea has been been turned into a picture-guessing party game that is sure to have everybody HOWLING!

In DrawBack, two teams compete by selecting two participants each: an ILLUSTRATOR, and an IMITATOR. The teams' Imitators sit facing their teams, and the Illustrators stand behind them. The Illustrators are given a shared WORD CARD, which has words from six different categories (e.g., foods, places, etc.), selected by the roll of a die. These aren't hard - a child could draw them, and could guess them even faster! Except...

Only the Illustrators know the word. They must DRAW the picture on the Imitators' BACKS, who must then draw what they are FEELING onto the page (using Drawback's patented 'BACKTRACKER' technology - see below!) for their team to see! Twice as hard, but TEN TIMES as FUNNY! First team to get to 11 wins!

But wait! You ever play those drawing games, and one team seems to have a whole bunch of DA VINCIS on their team?

Sucks, don't it?

Well, they're going to be kicking themselves, because after each round, whichever team is in the LEAD must spin the DRAWBACK LEADER-MISTREATER!, which gives a SPECIAL RULE for the next round (which usually STINKS for the team that's ahead)! Will that team get 'OUTDRAW', which means they're OUT next round (but your team has to guess it in HALF the time)? Or will they get 'DOUBLE-BACK' which means they have TWO Illustrators, one BEHIND the other?! Or maybe they'll get 'COMEBACK': if the losing team WINS the next round, they'll earn TRIPLE POINTS!!! No runaway victories here, folks! It stays tight to the VERY END!

So what are you waiting for? All you need is some tracing paper, some clear clipboards, and a couple of dry-erase markers, to play THE party game of the year!

—description from the publisher

Essence: Explore, Battle, Trade is an open world, loot-driven, dark fantasy game of adventure, conquest, and fortune. Join in a group of up to 4 players or embark on a solo journey as a hero exploring the ruins of Moltania, a land divided by the mystical forests of Myrrh and volcanic crusts of Chasmadia.
