Each hero has a unique background and ability, but it’s up to the player to shape their story - gain experience, level up key skills, equip the right gear, and synergize game-bending abilities to earn victory points!

As one of four heroes, players begin their quest in the humble trading town of Ghoss and take turns completing actions across Moltania. Each round, players can use their actions to move into new lands, seek fortune in cities, battle deadly monsters, or rally allies. New equipment earned along the way gives players freedom in how they explore and confront challenges.

Solo and raid-based combat pits players against intimidating monsters, from the nimble dire roach in Chasmadia to the masterful elder tree sir in Myrrh. Weaker foes appear in random encounters, while stronger enemies lurk on special deadly hexes. Each battle tests a hero’s full array of skills, abilities, and equipment—and, with a bit of luck, those who prevail earn abundant loot.

Essence brings popular video game mechanics back to the table with the progression, loot, and player decision systems of a classic RPG. Skill and luck blend together as players build their heroes into formidable forces, all based on choices presented from both chance and strategy.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Covid-19: Jsme v tom spolu, ksakru!
description:
Players can become the American president, the Hungarian cleaning lady and other characters in the game. At the start of the game, players draw their country of origin and profession, and their salary and status will be directly proportional to this. Each player will gain certain advantages that the others will lack, but also disadvantages from which the other players can benefit.

In Covid-19: Jsme v tom spolu, ksakru! ("Covid-19: We're in this together"), players roll dice, move their pieces the appropriate number of spaces, and complete tasks. For example, you may find yourself in the field of DARE, where Finnish coach Miho Urvi has convinced you to come to the gym to train during a tough lockdown, but the police come to the gym and you have to pay a fine. Similarly, during the game, you can reach the KLIKA field, which symbolizes a brigade in a Chinese restaurant where the two main leaders of the government meet. By informing the journalists about their meeting, you will earn 2,000 Koronas.

The object of the game is to kill the Covid
Part of the game's fields include the shares of pharmaceutical companies in the production of vaccines. Players will thus be able to buy a share of each company, thus gaining a dose of vaccines as they pass through START. These can then be used to defeat the Coviid, the player who contributes the most vaccines will win.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Climb aboard your paddle steamer and race downriver in a competitive quest to become the wealthiest steamboat captain of the era!

In Steamboat Mogul, players deliver colorful cargo cards to fulfill a mix of order cards over five structured rounds. Each round introduces new order combinations and tighter cargo constraints, forcing players to adapt and optimize every turn.

The game blends hand management, open drafting, and timed interaction through:

A dynamic river market with three face-up discard piles. Players may discard matching cards here—or draw from them instead of the deck.

A shared cargo storage pile where players must discard a card each turn—but other players can instantly snatch it by calling out “I want it!”

A tight economy of cargo cards where every card counts: you get exactly what you need, but you’ll need to trade, store, or intercept smartly to succeed.

Simultaneous competition for limited cards and the race to be the first to complete all orders and end the round.

Each decision in Steamboat Mogul is a blend of timing, risk-taking, and reading your opponents. With fast turns and high interaction, it’s a family-friendly game that rewards both clever planning and quick reactions.

—description from the publisher

One player plays the white cubes. The other player plays the blue cylinders. Take turns stacking a block onto the grid or onto another block already in the grid. The first player to get four in a row across, down, or diagonally is the winner.

Originally included with Kickstarter pledges.

6 card promo pack of Landmark cards.

P1 - Calla Lily Valley: You may discard this card to swap a card from your hand for one in the depot.  Then build that card for free.

P2 - Limekiln State Park: When Scoring: Count the highway cards below as every terrain type when scoring stretches.  Does not apply to 2-mile bonuses.

P3 - Mission San Antonio de Padua: When scoring: Double the quantity of Landmark cards in your highway.

P4 - Notely's Landing: Score 1 point for each card in your hand.

P5 - Sea Ranch: You may discard this card to put the cards in the depot at the bottom of the Highway deck then refill the depot.  Score 3 points if not discarded.

P6 - Tanbark Trail and Tin House: Once per turn, you may use one persistent material from another player's Highway.

Munacello is a family card game that's easy to learn and quick-playing with small memory and speed elements. Each player is dealt four face-down cards (in a 2x2 grid) and memorizes the two cards closest to them. On a turn, you draw from the deck and, depending what card you get, either improve your cards or use an ability on one of the power cards. When you think you have the lowest score, call "Munacello!"

Cake or Pie?: A Game of Sweet Allegiances is a trick-taking card game for 3–6 players where dessert is serious business—and loyalty is never guaranteed. Each round, players pledge allegiance to Cake or Pie, then battle over Order cards representing shifting demand.

Play flavored dessert cards—Chocolate, Berries, Lemon, or Pumpkin—to win tricks, sway demand, and collect the most valuable orders. But beware: if your actions betray your chosen dessert, you'll earn a Tart of Treason. Fail to redeem yourself, and you’ll lose your sweetest victory points!

With dynamic demand flipping, strategic flavor-following, and a deliciously pun-filled loyalty system, Cake or Pie? serves up clever decisions and playful sabotage in just 30–45 minutes.

—description form the publisher

Part 4 of the series of “World of Motor Racing” games released by designer Terry Goodchild and published by Lambourne Games in 1998.  This is a stand-alone module in the series, parts 1-3 are not needed to support play if this game.

Primarily intended as a solitaire “replay” game, you race a full set of races from the 1997 Winston Cup Series schedule.  Drivers are rated in grades, which figure into their abilities to pass or defend from being passed.

The game includes 36 plastic cars, several card-stock “track sections”, various card-stock lookup charts, 3 dice and a full set of rules to govern the action.
family: Series: World of Motor Racing (Lambourne / Owzat)

description:
Mobius is a fan-made tabletop wargame based on Archie Comics’ 24-year run of Sonic the Hedgehog, allowing you to fight Large Skirmishes in the gone-but-not-forgotten take on Sonic's World!

It features stats for all your favourite heroes and villains, and rules to suit every playstyle from hordes of Eggman Robots to squads of elite Freedom Fighters. There’s an ever-evolving plethora of army books and supplements to represent the many factions within the pages of the world’s most way past cool comic!

—description from the designer

Battlegroup Kustrin presents a series of World War Two wargame scenarios based on the campaign of January to March 1945 around the town of Küstrin in Germany until its eventual fall to the Red Army. The scenarios are all aimed to be played on average sized wargames tables and with forces that range from a few squads up to a couple of platoons per side and using the Battlegroup rule system.

The last few months of the war in Europe, on both the Western and Eastern front, offers much for the wargamer in terms of historical scenarios to re-fight. They are not always the one-sided affairs that wargamers might expect from this period of the war and often offer up unique and interesting forces, providing both interesting gaming and modelling opportunities. Küstrin offers a number of esoteric items – from the rag-tag Volkssturm units to Hungarian Infantry, to Soviets using lend-lease equipment to King Tigers, to Josef Stalin 2s, to 105mm Flak guns and all manner of defences. This period of the war has an awful lot to offer the gamer compared to the usual fare of ‘Normandy’ games.

With these scenarios for Küstrin you can follow the course of the siege and see how you do in defence, or indeed, in assault of the besieged town.

—description from the publisher

UPDATE: A prequel/sequel is currently undergoing production that will retain some of the same content and core gameplay elements, but feature a new storyline and all hand-drawn artwork. It is recommended to get the current edition, as there is no release date yet planned for the next title, and the storyline is planned to be different but related to the current edition and themes.


Create a Hero from 19 Races and 19 Classes, join the Order, and delve dungeons to discover the heart of evil and destroy it. Order of Eventide is a solo or cooperative dungeon crawling board game & RPG hybrid featuring in-depth combat, hero progression, loot, and procedural generation with a full campaign.
        20-120 minute play sessions
    


While each dungeon is unique, you won't need to block off an entire day to play. String together multiple sessions to complete a grand campaign!


        Roleplay Rules
    


Experience a brilliantly simple ruleset to allow for players to roleplay their own imaginative unique items, skills, and actions and so much more for their heroes in a way that can fit into the balance of the game system.


        More than a system..
    


Tired of dungeon crawlers that only offer a procedural framework and no storyline or deep character development? Order of Eventide features a full campaign storyline to complete, Guild Quests to embark on, no hero level cap, and a brilliantly simple Questing variant so you can continue your own story with homebrew adventures.

- A robust Town System complete with NPC's to interact with, and the ability to invest in shopkeepers for expanded services
- Join a Guild to gain Titles, Rewards, and Perks
- A storyline to discover with limitless potential to continue to grow heroes and embark on new journeys
- A virtually infinite combination of class, race, and equipment compositions for players to discover and synergize in their parties
- Over 100 unique items and weapons to discover, with room for infinitely more

4 Years in the Making...
In their spare time between family and full time jobs, Chad and Chris have compiled four years of game crafting and world building into Order of Eventide, their passion project. Visit OrderOfEventide .com for player resources, printouts, to join the OOE public Discord channel for player meetups and chat with the creators, and to keep an eye out on new content!

—description from the designer

Players build their team by drafting characters from different indie comic factions to battle and defeat their opponent's team. It's a battle-style card game based on a growing list of indie comics. You only need one box to battle your opponent, however, you can mix and match factions with other scattered nexus boxes to multiply the potential deck possibilities. This year, we partnered with a comic group called Nerdanatix to bring 6 of their comics to life.

—description from the designer

IntroductionJotun (from Old Norse: jǫtunn, meaning “devourer” or “giant”) is a drawless territorial game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 5 cells per side, but boards of size 4 or 6 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient number of stackable pieces in their color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A stack is a set of pieces of the same color located in the same cell. A single piece is also a stack.
     The height of a stack is the number of pieces it contains.
     The color of a stack is the color of its top piece.
     The strength of a stack is the number of friendly pieces it contains, ignoring enemy pieces underneath (if any).
     A group is a maximal set of connected stacks of the same color, height, and strength. A single stack is also a group.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, do one of the following actions:


     Place a stack of height 1 in your color on an empty cell adjacent to at least as many enemy stacks as friendly stacks.
     Move a friendly stack to an empty cell by passing through empty cells or jumping over one or more aligned sequences of at least one friendly stack (with or without empty cells before or after each sequence), landing on an empty cell after each sequence, under the following conditions:
         The minimal height among stacks adjacent to the origin cell must be greater than the minimal height among stacks adjacent to the destination cell.
         If the moving stack was not adjacent to any friendly stack at the start, it may end adjacent to at least one friendly stack of equal or lower height.
    


Each time a stack is moved, this procedure is followed:

  1. The stack moves in a straight line through all empty cells until it becomes adjacent to one or more friendly stacks, which it may jump over in sequence. After each jump, it must land on an empty cell adjacent to the opposite end of the sequence. The path may continue in this manner until reaching the destination cell.
  2. On each first cell touched that is adjacent to a new friendly or enemy group, the following interactions are resolved in order:

     Active: If it touches an enemy group of equal or lesser strength, it consumes the top piece of each stack in the group (only one is added beneath the moving stack). Then, if it becomes adjacent to friendly groups, they are fed by adding one piece on top of each.
     Passive: If it becomes adjacent to an enemy stack with greater strength, that stack consumes its top piece (adding it beneath itself). Then, if that enemy stack is adjacent to allied groups, it feeds them by adding one piece on top of each.


Each group may be consumed or fed only once during the journey, and never both in the same cell. Consumption is resolved in ascending order of strength.
  3. After advancing in a straight line at least once (whether or not interactions occurred), the stack may change direction if it touches the lowest adjacent friendly stack, and that stack is equal to or higher than the next lowest friendly stack in the new direction. It cannot touch again any stack it has stopped touching.
  4. If the stack loses its top piece and changes color, the movement stops immediately. A moving stack cannot be completely consumed.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when exactly one player cannot place or move any stacks. The winner is the player who controls more than half of the board, whether by occupying cells with friendly stacks or by controlling empty cells where the opponent cannot place.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stack of height 1 on an empty cell, and the second player chooses their side. This balancing method is known as the pie rule.

—description from the designer

Each round, players secretly lay cards facedown and bid on how many they can flip to match the Capture Card's color. Can you outwit your opponents and call their bluff, or will you up the bid? With every bid, the stakes rise and the suspense soars.
Perfect for game nights, Butterfly Bluff is a thrilling blend of strategy, risk, and deception that's fun for all ages!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Murder Mystery in a Box: The Halloween Murder
description:
To solve the case you need to 1) determine the identify of the killer and 2) uncover the motive for the murder.

Start by reading the assignment letter to learn specifics of the case and then begin examining all the contents found inside the box.

When you have finished your investigation and are ready to reveal the solution and conclusion of the mystery, you will need to go to the publisher's website. There is no hint system online so you will only need internet access for the solution.

Each turn a new company president is selected and a new product is presented to the copywriters who arrange word cards to create descriptive ad copy of a product. At the end of the turn, the product, along with 4 others products, are presented to the president. The company president is tasked with guessing which product the copywriters wrote their ad copy about. If the president guesses correctly they receive the product card as a point and pick one writer to receive a card as well.
publishers: ゲーム工房カコムタク(Game Factory KAKOMUTAKU)

description:
Every player has 12 hidden cards (3x4). Cards are ranked from -2 up to 12. Two are turned face up. On your turn you can take the top card from the discard or draw pile. You can exchange one card (hidden or open) from your display. Round ends when one player has only open cards. (equal turns). All card will be revealed. Add the numbers of the card for scoring. Game ends after one player has 100 or more points. Whoever has the lowest number wins.

Special rule: Whenever one column of 3 cards all have the same value, they will be discarded and no longer scored.
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
Cosplay: The Game is a competitive card game where players embody cosplayers striving to achieve "Cosplay Superstar" status. Players select a fandom—such as Video Games, Anime & TV, Steampunk, or Superheroes—and collect "flair" to enhance their costumes. Through various events and tournaments, players advance their ranks, with the first to reach "Superstar" status emerging victorious.

Designed by cosplayers for cosplayers, the game features original, hand-drawn artwork with a comic-book aesthetic. It's easy to learn, portable, and offers engaging gameplay suitable for conventions or casual gatherings.

—description from the publisher

In the heart of a decaying kingdom, seven dark beings await their verdict.

In Deadly 7, two players engage in a tense, asymmetrical psychological duel. One assumes the role of an ancient Inquisitor, determined to gather seven Seals of Evidence or break the Will of the Damned. The other takes on the role of the Damned, a human or monstrous entity accused of unspeakable crimes, who must bluff, deceive, and endure until the final round.

Easy to learn yet deep to master, Deadly 7 combines declared bluffing, hand management, and unique asymmetrical powers for each character.

Tension escalates with every turn, as players’ decisions weave an emergent narrative in every game.

Experience a dark fantasy gothic world where words can save… or doom.

—description from the publisher

Max and Alice are two courageous siblings who once roamed the wasteland freely — until they were captured by a ruthless gang of raiders. Now forced into hard labor, they must meet the raiders’ relentless demands for tribute. But beneath the surface, rebellion brews. The siblings are growing stronger, biding their time, and preparing to rise up and reclaim their freedom.

Feral State: Revolt is a solo/co-op comic book–style, hack-and-slash roll-and-write game set in a zombie-infested apocalypse. Focused on combat, it blends survival, worker placement, and resource management into one brutal and strategic experience.

Work the base under the watchful eyes of raiders. Sneak out to explore the town, level up, and gather gear. Build your strength - and when the time is right, strike back when they least expect it!

—description from the designer

Welcome to NEON! A vibrant and exciting city full of opportunities, where communication happens through gestures and sounds. Dive into the action and experience NEON in your own unique way!

A party game of gestures and sounds! Describe two secret images using only one noise and one gesture. Others race to guess and grab the right tiles. No words, just pure creativity and chaos — in two fun modes!


     Entertaining fun: combine sounds and gestures in a hilarious way 
     Simple rules and set up to start playing immediately
     Original packaging in a handy carry-along edition


Let’s Talk MORE NEON: The game comes in a variety of color editions, each with different sets of motifs for even more replayability and laughs.

—description from the publisher

Like in a talk show, in this party game you have to face your opponents by supporting unusual and funny opinions, even against your own convictions. The secret to winning? Having good dialectics and being clear and convincing!

Dibattito is a board game inspired by the Debate method, aimed at developing critical and dialectical thinking. Players must defend both a thesis and its opposite, regardless of their personal beliefs, fostering open-mindedness and varied perspectives. The game involves two players debating while others act as jurors. Each debate consists of two 30-second speeches per player, following a structured format. The jury votes, and points are awarded based on the consensus or discrepancy of votes. Bonus and malus tokens can influence the final score. The game continues until all players have participated in a debate, with the winner being the player with the most points.

Can you guess a word without ever saying the letter A, while your opponents... disturb you by counting out loud? With crazy restrictions and obligations, this party game will test your language skills, alertness, and strategy!

Slalom Parola is a fun team game for 4-16 players aged 10-99, lasting about 30 minutes. Players take turns being the "Oratore," who must make their team guess a word within the time of an hourglass while respecting restriction cards. The game includes 60 word cards, 52 restriction cards, 24 score cards, a card board, and an hourglass. Players divide into 2-4 teams, with each team deciding on a color for word pairs. The Oratore picks a word card and restriction cards, then tries to get their team to guess the word while following the restrictions. If the team guesses correctly, they discard their restriction cards and take score cards based on the number of restrictions used. The game ends when a team reaches 25 points or runs out of restriction cards. The team with the most points wins, with higher-value score cards breaking ties. If the Oratore breaks a rule or runs out of time, an opponent can say "Slalom!" to end the turn. The Oratore cannot use made-up words, parts of the word, morphological clues, derived or compound words, show the card, or make gestures. They can use other words, lateral thinking, sounds, or songs to hint at the word.

Choose an intriguing question and answer with a cryptic answer. Are you able to justify your choices... in front of everyone? A brilliant party game that stimulates relationships and dialectics!

Non Chiedermi Questo! ("Don't Ask Me This!") is a psychological party game that fosters entertaining interactions among players, enhancing their dialectical and interpersonal skills. It encourages honest, witty, and sincere conversations among people who already know each other, but not exclusively.

The game is based on a deck of cards featuring provocative questions and ambiguous answers about personal, emotional, behavioral, and habitual topics. It can be played by 2 to 8 players.

Setup: The dealer distributes 4 cards to each player. Each card has a question and an answer.
The oldest player acts as the master for the first round.
Gameplay:

The master selects a question from their hand, reads it aloud, and places it on the table.
Other players choose a response from their hand that they believe is most appropriate and say it aloud in turn.
The master selects the response they find most sincere or fitting and the player who gave that response wins the card.
All played cards (except the one won) go into the discard pile, and each player draws a new card.
The role of master passes to the next player in a clockwise direction, and a new round begins.
When the deck runs out, shuffle the discard pile and continue playing.
The game ends after each player has been the master at least once (or twice, if chosen by the players).

Winning the Game:
The player who collects the most cards wins the game.

boardgame: Skull Tales: Full Sail! – O Captain! My Captain!
description:
O Captain! My Captain! Your time has come. It’s time to take command of your crew and sail in search of trading ports. Smuggle goods boldly, transport passengers to reap profits, boost your Allies' skills with jobs, and play strategies to confront your enemies.

O Captain! My Captain! is a new expansion for Skull Tales (You need the core game, Skull Tales, to play): 45 new cards, 14 tokens and new and exciting rules for your Skull Tales - Full Sail! game.

The expansion includes new cards, Jobs and Strategy cards. Job cards are assignments given to an Ally to provide them with new responsibilities in the crew (in the Port and Voyage Phases). In the Adventure Phase,  Strategy cards allow an Ally to obey orders that help them to improve their capabilities. They can move more spaces, defend themselves better, and even perform new actions.

The strategy cards may use in the Adventure phase. Your Pirate may activate a Strategy if you share a tile with an Ally you control or if you have Line of Sight to them. Doing this has no cost. To do this, follow the instructions on the card text, as long as you meet the requirements specified on the card. These cards can be used in different rounds of the game, each of them indicates when it can be used.

—description from the publisher

(Español)

¡Oh Capitán, mi Capitán! Ha llegado tu hora. Es hora de tomar el mando de tu tripulación y navegar en busca de puertos comerciales.

Haz contrabo de mercancías con audacia, transporta pasajeros para cosechar beneficios, potencia las habilidades de tus aliados con trabajos y juega con estrategias para enfrentarte a tus enemigos.

¡Oh Capitán, mi Capitán! es una nueva expansión para Skull Tales (necesitas el juego base, Skull Tales, para jugar): 45 nuevas cartas, 14 fichas y nuevas y emocionantes reglas para tu juego Skull Tales ¡A toda vela!

La expansión incluye nuevas cartas, cargos y cartas de estrategia. Las cartas de Cargo son asignaciones que se dan a un Aliado para proporcionarle nuevas responsabilidades en la tripulación (en las Fases de Puerto y de Travesía). En la Fase de Aventura, las cartas de Estrategia permiten a un Aliado obedecer órdenes que le ayudan a mejorar sus capacidades. Pueden moverse más espacios, defenderse mejor e incluso realizar nuevas acciones.

Las cartas de Estrategia pueden usarse en la Fase de Aventura. tu Pirata puede activar una Estrategia si comparte una loseta con un Aliado que controle o si tiene Línea de Visión con él. Hacer esto no tiene ningún coste. Para hacerlo, sigue las instrucciones del texto de la carta, siempre que cumplas los requisitos especificados en ella. Estas cartas se pueden utilizar en diferentes rondas del juego, cada una de ellas indica cuándo.

 -descripción de la editorial

Welcome to Pirates! With 6 new characters for your Skull Tales games and a new competitive game mode: Pirate versus Pirate or PvP.

Take on the enemies of each scenario and other pirate gangs to complete the objectives before they do!

Includes cards to use the new characters, along with those from the core game and the Mega Expansion, in the new PvP mode.

CONTENTS: 6 miniatures, 6 Character cards, 6 tokens, 23 PvP cards, 11 Gear cards, Rulebook and scenarios.

Skull Tales: Full Sail! core game required to play this expansion.

(Español)

Bienvenido a Piratas! 6 nuevos personajes para tus partidas de Skull Tales te esperan, y, además, un nuevo modo de juego competitivo: Pirata versus Pirata o PvP.

Enfréntate a los enemigos de cada escenario y al resto de bandas pirata y completa los objetivos antes que ellos.

Incluye las cartas para utilizar los nuevos personajes, además de los del juego base y la mega expansión en el nuevo modo PvP.

CONTENIDO: 6 miniaturas, 6 tarjetas de personaje, 6 marcadores, 23 cartas de modo PvP, 11 cartas de equipo, Reglamento y escenarios.

Se necesita el juego base, Skull Tales, para jugar.

Discover the paths in search of treasure!

In the Enigma do Labirinto, players embark on a fascinating journey through a circular board in search of precious stones.
Each explorer must face the challenge of entering the maze, but they must be very careful, as the walls change position during the game.

They will need to rely on a good strategy and a bit of luck to be able to get their precious stone and the diamond in the center of the maze and escape safely.

With the Enigma do Labirinto, the fun never ends. Challenge your friends and family and find out who is the best explorer!

boardgame: Skull Tales: Full Sail! – Curse of the Caribbean
description:
Skull Tales: Curse of the Caribbean is an exciting expansion that offers a variety of challenges for your fearless pirates.
Dive into a world of adventure where you will find new treasures and legendary items that will give you an incredible advantage on your journey. Discover the amazing power of potions and the new additions to your arsenal that you can find in the Gear cards at Tortuga's Market.

But not everything will be smooth sailing, and you should be wary of the foes lurking in the shadows. The Curse of the Caribbean will take you on a journey to explore new and exciting scenarios in two thrilling campaigns: "The Grimoire of the Dead" and "Monkey Chuck". And to top it all off, an epic Epilogue chapter that will take your breath away.

Embark on your ship and sail the seas to challenge the fearsome Turtle Submarine. Explore the narrow streets of the villages while avoiding the formidable Elite Redcoats.

—description from the publisher

(Español)

Skull Tales: La Maldición del Caribe  es una emocionante expansión que ofrece una gran variedad de desafíos para tus intrépidos piratas.
Sumérgete en un mundo de aventuras donde encontrarás nuevos tesoros y objetos legendarios que te darán una increíble ventaja en tu viaje. Descubre el asombroso poder de las pociones y las nuevas adiciones a tu arsenal que podrás encontrar en las cartas de equipo del Mercado de Tortuga.

Pero no todo será coser y cantar, y deberás tener cuidado con los enemigos que acechan en las sombras. La Maldición del Caribe te llevará a explorar nuevos y emocionantes escenarios en dos trepidantes campañas: «El Grimorio de los Muertos» y «Monkey Chuck». Y como colofón, un épico capítulo Epílogo que te dejará sin aliento.

Embárcate en tu barco y surca los mares para desafiar al temible Submarino Tortuga. Explora las estrechas calles de los pueblos mientras evitas a los formidables Casacas Rojas de Élite.

—Descripción de la editorial

boardgame: Mansions of Madness: Second Edition – Turn of a Page
description:
“…come back…”

The whispers wake you at all hours of the night, breaking through your dreams and dragging you out of bed only to find no one else in the room. But you are certain a voice is calling to you.

Finally, as dawn spreads over your window, you follow the whispers to your library. To one particular shelf that has lain too long forgotten. It’s time to return to the Mansions of Madness.

I am thrilled to welcome new players and old fans alike back to the world of Mansions of Madness: Second Edition with a brand-new scenario! For those unaware, I am Grace Holdinghaus, a senior designer and resident expert on hybrid app games here at FFG. I was hired over 10 years ago to work on a fancy new edition of the Mansions of Madness game line, and then Journeys in Middle-earth and…well history is cyclical and I am back at the beginning, feeling like a world-traveler stepping onto my home soil after years away.

Turn of a Page is a new, wholly digital scenario that requires only the base game of Mansions of Madness: Second Edition to play, but it is also a narrative tie-in with the new The Drowned City campaign for Arkham Horror: The Card Game. In that campaign, a group of investigators returned from an expedition to R’lyeh carrying powerful new artifacts. Now it is up to your investigators to uncover more of the story, claiming those artifacts to defend Arkham against the rising doom. Whether you choose to play as both groups of investigators, or simply join us for Turn of a Page, surely, Arkham will never again be the same.

—description from the designer

The West—preoccupied with its own problems and terrified of becoming infected itself—has attempted to cut itself off from the rest of the world. Can
your crack team break away to discover cures for all diseases? A Pandemic scenario for 4 players by Matt Leacock. - From Publisher

This published scenario gives you a predetermined setup.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In a_way, players take part in the legendary spaceship race of the Quazarys galaxy. Each pilot starts from their own base… which is also an opponent’s finish line. The race track is made of solar paths filled with stations, directional arrows, and strange space phenomena, creating a constantly shifting cosmic circuit where clever navigation matters as much as speed.

On your turn, you combine movement and card play to advance your ship. Players move from station to station, respecting one-way routes and mandatory checkpoints, while avoiding landing on the same station as an opponent. Cards drawn from a shared deck allow players to manipulate the board, trigger special effects, teleport through vortices, or disrupt rivals. You may play single cards or powerful combos from the same faction, discard cards to cycle your hand, and gain an extra movement each turn for well-timed bursts of speed.

Positioning and timing are key: you can leap over opposing ships if conditions are right, anticipate blocked paths, and adapt as the layout changes. The game ends immediately when a player reaches the opposing base (or the base directly across from their own in games with three or four players). a_way is a fast, tactical race where clever card play and sharp movement decisions decide who crosses enemy territory first.

—description from the publisher

Your team is paralyzed by a government shutdown. When your government finally comes to its senses, will it be too late? -- from publisher.

This is a predetermined setup for Pandemic.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Zombicide: Fantasy Quest B59 – The Ancient Tomes
description:
Quest
R.SONNESSO – T.VON GLEHN
6 February 2026
Hard
2+ survivors
90 minutes
Material needed: White Death.
Lost in snowy ruins, just you, your partner, and a few guards. The Legendary Tomes await... but the undead stir. Giving up isn’t an option when you face it together.

It's 2077. Five industries — Augments, Fusion, Quantum, Robots, Skycars — are reshaping the global economy, and the startups racing to dominate them are yours to build, back, and sell at exactly the right moment.

In Pivot, you and your fellow entrepreneurs hold shares across multiple companies. You can operate any company you're invested in — building fabs, extending shipping networks, hiring agents, selling stock to meet regional demand — but your share count caps your spending power and your salary. Invest more to do more; invest everywhere and spread yourself thin. Fortunes shift as players pool their efforts behind the same companies, then diverge when their interests no longer align. The player with the most shares holds CEO control, unlocking partnerships that let two companies share infrastructure and split revenue — and the ability to trigger a manual exit, selling the company at 40–99bn for a 1× to 3× bonus. Wait for the automatic exit at 100bn and the bonus hits 4×, but every action at the table has been moving the market since your last turn.

That market is alive. A shared card display feeds Hype Cards into players' hands after every company action — play them to seed regional demand and shift prices in your favour, or watch your rivals do it first. Every card played moves a visible marker around the board, driving the clock forward. There is no hidden timer - everyone can see how much game remains, and everyone is deciding whether to spend it building or getting out. Your Net Worth at the end of the game — personal cash plus the value of all shares held — determines the winner.

The two maps offer different pressures. Continents is a global network of landmasses and waterways — the canals at Suez and Panama and the Pacific nodes are the vital arteries that make or break a route. Yellow Sea is more precise: each region has two ports with their own attached Fabs, and connecting to the wrong one could set you back. The Bohai Sea is a natural choke point, with tighter, more crowded connections on the west of the map than the east. Continents invites you everywhere; Yellow Sea dares you to commit.

Players who love the mutual dependency of Brass, the hand discipline of Concordia, the share competition of Chicago Express, and the market pressure of Indonesia will find themselves in exactly the right company. 2–5 players, 50–150 minutes.

—Description from the publisher.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Set sail in Rolling Pirates, a modern Roll & Write adventure where cunning navigation, daring decisions, and the thrill of treasure hunting collide! Step into the boots of a bold pirate captain, exploring uncharted waters in search of lost riches. To prove you're the most legendary adventurer of the seas, you'll need to gather clues, outwit your rivals, and be the first to uncover the treasure’s secret location.

Each turn, roll three dice to navigate your ship, battle the wind, and upgrade your crew’s abilities. Explore mysterious skull-shaped rocks, plunder drifting wrecks and merchant ships, and face off against formidable Spanish galleons as you piece together the map to fortune. But treasure isn't found only at sea—bribe dockside sailors in rowdy taverns, trade stolen goods, and stock up on supplies in bustling ports. Multiple paths to victory ensure every journey is unique.

With open-map navigation, escalating tension, and a wealth of pirate-flavored choices, [i]Rolling Pirates[/i] offers a rich, replayable experience for adventurers who crave strategy, risk-taking, and legendary tales. Will you rise to fame or sink into obscurity?

—description from the publihser
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Players travel around Finland, and when they land on a city that has IKH hardware store, player gets a job deal, takes a card and does what the card says. When all players have deals of all four colours, the one with most money wins. Very simple luck-based game.

1490 Doom is a grimdark medieval skirmish game where players lead Doom Companies through vertical battlegrounds, fighting for survival against rivals and the rising death. Featuring brutal action-point movement, a modular 2’x2’ circular board, and campaign-driven progression, 1490 Doom blends fast tactical combat with desperate storytelling. Players battle in a dying world where every climb could be their last, fighting not only the enemy but the encroaching Creeping Death that claims the land below. The game supports solo, cooperative, and competitive modes.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Tower Titans is a fast-paced, roguelite-inspired dungeon-crawling board game for 2–8 players. Players build the map as they explore, gather powerful items, confront deadly traps, and face off against rivals in fierce PvP battles. Deep within the dungeon lurks a colossal Titan—the ultimate test of strength, strategy, and survival.

Only one can claim the Victor's Crown.

In each game, you’ll:

Explore the procedurally built Titan Tower and dungeon

Discover treasures, hazards, and unique events

Fight rivals once the Key is found

Unlock the dungeon and confront the Titan

Win by slaying the Titan or being the last player standing

—description from the designer

Everyone Must Die! is a drafting card game based on the awesome film Everyone Must Die! Everyone starts the game with a hand of twelve cards and drafts cards until they have twelve different cards in hand. Each player then takes a turn playing a card until everyone is out of cards. Cards can be played for points and/or their effects. Once everyone is out of cards you shuffle the deck and do it all again until someone reaches 50, 80, or 150 points depending on the length of the game you want to play.

In Plight, you are the head of an Airship Logistics Company tasked with rekindling civilization after a cataclysmic event that has left the world shattered. A game of Plight lasts for a 4 week cycle, each week broken up into 2 Travel Legs. The winner is the player with the most Influence - essentially points - at the end of the full cycle. Alternatively, you can also play up to a certain influence threshold.

Gain influence by completing missions and by trading in resources that you will pick up and deliver to various settlements.

Your first game of Plight will take about 40 minutes per player. After you play a few times, a game should take about 20 minutes per player.

—description from the designer

Bog Hollow Brigade is a whimsical, push-your-luck deck-building game set in the tangled woods of 1920s England. Players take on the roles of imaginative kids braving Traps, tricky terrain, and the curious Bogborn. Each turn is a gamble—press forward for greater rewards or retreat to regroup. Build your deck, time your tools, and earn glory through bold adventures and clever decisions.

—description from the publisher

Centuries have passed since the old Demon King had been defeated. After a long struggle for influence (playable in Monster Hex), one of the 4 Demon Lords rises to become the new King.

In Dungeon Mahjong players are young adventurers delving into the Royal Dungeon to defeat the Monsters lurking within. Starting with a hand of 6 Guild Cards representing a party of followers from one of 5 classes (Thief, Warrior, Sorcerer, Knight and Monk, corresponding to numbers 1 to 5), each player on their turn chooses one of two actions:


     Discard a Guild Card for its effects
     Reveal a number of cards from the hand to defeat a Monster


The Monster Cards come in 3 levels, each requiring a matching number of Mahjong-like melds (either 3 sequential numbers, or 3 identical numbers), along with additional requirements, to defeat. Defeated monsters join the player's party to bring victory points and helpful special effects. Among the level 3 Monsters, there are the 4 Demon Lords, and to trigger the game end players need to defeat at least one of them (or at least 3 of the other level 3 Monsters). The player with the most victory points at the end of the game wins and is pronounced Hero.

boardgame: STAK: The Sophisticated Simple Strategy Game
description: Each player starts with three stacks of three disks in the player's color. Each stack contains a small, medium, and large disk. Players take turns moving one of their disks onto a 4X4 grid or onto the top of a stack in the grid or from one stack or place in the grid to another. Players must move a disk that is on a grid space or on top of a stack. Players may place a disk onto a grid space or onto a larger disk. The winner is the first to line up 4 of his or her disks in a row.

The Pumpkin Fiend is out for revenge! Who summoned him? No one knows, but only you can stop him! All you know is he's out there and he won't rest until his victims are claimed. Keep the fires burning. Watch the shadows. Survive if you can.

A new villain for Slaughterville. Includes 24 cards for Pumpkin Fiend and 3 new characters.
Coming with 24 booster packs of 7 cards each, it contains 40 new cards from across the color spectrum, and featuring the new Visionaries Ink Witch with her three ink tokens and Mothlyn.

Game Description

Zenji is a mindful card game for 2 to 4 players, where each participant strives to become the Zen Master by collecting Elemental Points (EP).

Throughout the game, players must learn to manage their “Monkey Mind” — a playful metaphor for distraction and impulsivity — while making thoughtful decisions that test their focus, patience, and awareness.

Blending strategy, mental balance, and interactive gameplay, Zenji offers a unique experience that trains essential cognitive skills such as attention, self-control, and concentration — all while having fun.

The "Journey of Remembering" in Zenji is about strategically managing one's Monkey Mind, accumulating Elemental Points, and outsmarting opponents. Through careful card selection, calculated risks, and mindful decisions, players strive to clear their minds and achieve Zen mastery.

Always guided by four mystical allies, the avatars of AIR, EARTH, WATER and FIRE!

www.playzenji.com

boardgame: Twilight of the Soldier Kings: Scenario Book 2 and Regimental Rules – European Battles in the Age of Frederic the Great 1730-1770
description:
Second scenario book for the Twilight of the Soldier Kings rules contains 14 scenarios previously published as PDFs only.

Contains rules for Regimental Scale battles.

Scenarios included are: 
Bassignana, 1745; Fontenoy,1745; Piacenza, 1745; Rocoux, 1746; Lobositz, 1756; Reichenberg, 1757; Hastenbeck, 1757; Gross-Jagersdorf, 1757; Moys, 1757; Bergen, 1759; Kunersdorf, 1759; Strehla, 1760; Clostercamp, 1760; and Reichenbach, 1762.
family: History: War of the Austrian Succession

boardgame: Those Born of Gods: Omen Game 5
description:
Those Born of Gods | Omen Game 5 is a standalone card game for two players. Like the other new Omen games, this standalone game features 22 Unique Unit cards and 6 Unique Krater cards, each offering new Traits and abilities, and is fully compatible with all new (and future) Omen games.

In Omen games, players alternate taking turns gaining and spending Coins to play Unit cards, each with their own unique effects, to their side of three different Cities (each comprised of two City/Krater cards) to trigger effects and sack the Cities; vying to the winner by having the most points when the game ends.

When a City is Sacked, the player with the most Strength in that City gains one of that City's Krater cards. When both of a City's Kraters have been claimed, that City is Razed. When all three Cities are Razed, the game ends and players score points for the Krater Cards and Coveted Units in their hand, as well as the broken Krater Cards and facedown Triumph Units in their Rewards.

Rushing to Sack the Cities, playing towards completing Triumphs, and hopefully hoarding Coveted Units in your hand; Omen offers several paths to scoring points and impressing the deathless, watching Olympians.

While this game reimplements Omen: A Reign of War, it is not compatible with any previous Omen: A Reign of War title or expansion.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: The Omen Games (Small Box Games)

boardgame: Across the Briny Sea: Omen Game 4
description:
Across the Briny Sea | Omen Game 4 is a standalone card game for two players. Like the other new Omen games, this standalone game features 22 Unique Unit cards and 6 Unique Krater cards, each offering new Traits and abilities, and is fully compatible with all new (and future) Omen games.

In Omen games, players alternate taking turns gaining and spending Coins to play Unit cards, each with their own unique effects, to their side of three different Cities (each comprised of two City/Krater cards) to trigger effects and sack the Cities; vying to the winner by having the most points when the game ends.

When a City is Sacked, the player with the most Strength in that City gains one of that City's Krater cards. When both of a City's Kraters have been claimed, that City is Razed. When all three Cities are Razed, the game ends and players score points for the Krater Cards and Coveted Units in their hand, as well as the broken Krater Cards and facedown Triumph Units in their Rewards.

Rushing to Sack the Cities, playing towards completing Triumphs, and hopefully hoarding Coveted Units in your hand; Omen offers several paths to scoring points and impressing the deathless, watching Olympians.

While this game reimplements Omen: A Reign of War, it is not compatible with any previous Omen: A Reign of War title or expansion.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: The Omen Games (Small Box Games)

description:
In this chaotic and mischievous trick-taking game, players must dodge specific point-losing cards and try not to be the one who ends up with the trick, especially when the wrong colors or numbers are involved. Be careful: if a third suit is played in a single trick, it triggers a Run Away, instantly ending the trick and dumping it on the offending player!

Don’t Take Me!!! is a trick-taking game where the highest card in the lead suit wins the trick... unless a third unique suit appears, which ends the trick early and forces the player who caused the color burst to take it. Certain cards carry negative points, and tricks with those cards are dangerous to win. The player with the fewest negative points from tricks and leftover cards wins.

A deck consists of several crops and farm animals. Every kind of animal has a different value, as does every type of crop. The undealt cards form a draw deck from which 3 cards are played face-up to create the "deposit."

Players take turns acquiring cards by drawing from the deck or trading with other players or exchanging cards with the deposit. The exchange must be crop for crop or animal for animal, and the relinquished card(s) must sum to a greater value than the replacement card. Players may play their acquired cards face-up into their farm in front of them at any time but must do so in sequential order, lowest to highest, for both the crops and the animals. The first player to create a farm with one example of each crop and animal wins.

In a strange little kingdom of competitive climbers, each team evolves through five seasonal battles. With each match, new cards are added, and the rules change — demanding clever adaptation and precision planning to reach the top.

Tabletop Climber is a climbing game played over five rounds, where players build a hand from an expanding pool of cards. Each round introduces a new rule that reshapes which plays are strong or weak. Victory depends on crafting the right hand for the moment, managing card strength and combos, and outmaneuvering rival teams through changing conditions.
publishers: ハレルヤロックボーイ (Hallelujah Rockboy)

description:
This is a game based on the traditional Chinese festival Loong Boat Festival!
In the game, players play the role of drummer rowing Loong Boat, and control their Loong Boat to move forward in the river by counting dice or combining results.
There are various effects in the river to help players advance better or collect more Zongzi (traditional food of Loong Boat Festival)
Finally, the game will end when someone crosses the end. The game will calculate scores according to the player's final ranking of Loong Boat and Zongzi collection
The player with the highest score wins the game!
30 minute fast-paced game
Child gets started in 5 minutes: Dice parity ratio, easy to understand at a glance
Parents play with strategies: blocking, hoarding Zongzi, and predicting the timing of the sprint!
Seven double-sided river channels randomly spliced together, each sentence is a brand new track!
Re win the championship and get high scores, or play Zongzi steadily?
Let the classic of Dragon Boat Festival play the laughter of the whole family!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Pixeline Skolehjælp: Lær om søde dyr
description:
Pixeline helps you find, where the animals live and what they eat.
In this game, you compete to find out, who knows the most about animals.
Every time you answer correctly, you get an animal token - and the one with the most animals token at the end wins!

---
(in Danish:)
Pixeline hjælper dig med at finde ud af hvor dyrene bor, og hvad de spiser!

I dette spil konkurrerer i om hvem der ved mest om dyr! I bliver spurgt om hvor man finder alverdens forskellige dyr, og hvad de spiser!

Hver gang du svarer rigtigt får du en dyrebrik, og den der har flest dyrebrikker til sidst vinder!

Successfully align four shapes of your color before anyone else, either in a row (including diagonals) or in a column (stacked).

Each candy consists of two shapes. There are three types of shapes: round, square, and star.
Each shape comes in two sizes: small or large. The small shapes can fit inside the large ones; the large ones can cover the small ones.

On your turn, place one of your candies:

     in an empty space on the board
     inside or on top of another candy already placed on the board.


Only candies of the same shape fit together: a circle inside a circle, a square inside a square, a star inside a star.

You can block an opponent by covering one of their small shapes on the board with one of your large ones. Only use visible shapes to count your lines of four consecutive shapes!

The game ends when one of you manages to make a row or column of four shapes of your color, or when no one has any more candies to place.

A Park In The Desert is a 1-2 player tile-laying game where you will be building a small local desert park while trying to conserve on your water usage.

Take cards from the draft row and add them to your park to form groups of baseball fields, tennis and basketball courts, playgrounds, and grass fields. Build paths to connect your sport terrains and surround playgrounds. But be careful not to use too much water!

You will earn points for how your park is formed, such as earning points for desert patches or groups of grass, but will also be losing points during the game for the water that you use to water your grass. If you water and build wisely however, the points you earn could win you the game if you get the most points.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Bumblebee Dragway where the best slammer door racers gather for some exciting match drag racing or Pink Competitions. You are one of the best drivers around, and your opponent, and you are up for auction to drive one of twelve slammer cars at the track, six each for two class teams, to drive for the owner. This all means you will roll a D6 die to determine which car/setup you will be driving, and what lane, for your match race, and yes, they all want you behind the wheel for your expert driving abilities. 
Get Ready for More Exciting Two Player Drag Racing!

—description from the publisher

Be prepared to unmask the dirty and downright depraved minds of your friends as you compete to invent the most outrageous sexploits - guaranteed to make even the most poker-faced players blush. Simply use the cards to build a sentence - rude, obscene, funny or absurd - and vote on whose creation is the filthiest in this side-splitting, x-rated extravaganza!

Game play: 

     a sentence is built over 5 turns. For each turn, players chose their favourite, filthiest or funniest card from their had and play it facedown on the table. Cards can be selected in any order, but finished sentences must include one card from each deck and must run 1 to 5.
     Once the player has played one of their cards, they must pick up another card of the same number from the draw pile to bring their hand back up to 5 cards.
     Players then pass their whole hand to the player on the left and repeat until everyone has a complete sentence. 
     Players then take turns to reveal their sentence and a winner is voted for. They receive a FILTH card.
     At the end of the round all cards are discarded and a new hand in drawn.


The winner is the player with the most filth cards at the end of the game.

boardgame: Back to the 80s: Il grande quiz degli anni Ottanta
description:
A journey into the legendary 80s!
A great challenge to discover or relive the craziest, happiest and most colorful years of our history, guided by two iconic characters: Rick & Clive!
Songs, cinema and TV, fashion, advertising, meteors and superstars, historical figures and pop culture.
Each card features 80s themed questions. The player whose turn it is chooses the question and asks it to the other players, also specifying what the 4 possible answers are. The player who guesses correctly gets the point and becomes the one who asks the next question. The one with the most points wins (you have to decide at the beginning of the game how many turns the game will consist of).

In A Hilltown in the Mist, 4–6 players take on the roles of explorers trapped in a mysterious, fog-shrouded mountain town. The setting is dark and uncertain, with each player working to collect fragments of memory — the only way to uncover the truth behind the mist and ultimately escape.

The goal of the game is twofold: first, to survive the challenges of the town and gather enough memory fragments to qualify for escape; second, to accumulate the most gold among those who escape, as only the wealthiest survivor will claim victory.

Gameplay unfolds over a series of rounds, each divided into three phases: morning exploration, evening distribution, and night trading. Players cooperatively contribute effort to overcome challenges during exploration, then divide the spoils and memory fragments found. However, while collaboration is necessary for survival, players must also negotiate, trade, and scheme during the night phase to position themselves ahead of their rivals.

A Hilltown in the Mist blends cooperative and competitive elements, forcing players to balance teamwork with individual ambition. Every choice - from how much to contribute in a round, to how and when to trade - shapes both personal success and the group’s survival. The game ends once all memory fragments have been drawn, and among those who manage to escape, the player with the most gold is declared the winner.

A questionnaire is answered by using your voting card from your hand. Once every player have voted, everyone turn their voting card to reveal the results.
In this game, you win by voting like the rest of the group!

The red side of a card displays 8 food items; the blue side features 8 groups of drinks. Place the deck blue-side up and flip the top two cards to show their red sides. The first player to spot the one food item that appears on both cards then flips those cards to their blue sides. The first player to spot the one drink that appears the same number of times on each card takes one card. The player who first spotted the food gets the other card. Play continues until the deck runs out. The player with the most cards wins.

Flip over two cards from a deck. Only one shape will appear the same number of times on both cards. The first player to shout out the shape takes both cards. Play until the deck runs out. The winner is the player with the most cards.

Arson is a 7-Mission campaign created for Zombicide Season 3: Rue Morgue and Angry Neighbors’ Kickstarter. It was designed with all unlocked stretch goals in mind: having extra zombie miniatures (including an extra Abomination) is recommended.
Three Little Wishes is a 3-Mission campaign for Zombicide Season 1, Toxic City Mall, and Prison Outbreak.

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C25 – A Star is Falling
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C25 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Material needed: Season 1.
A mission with a real Hollywood taste. One of your hero is poisoned and the whole team is out to find a cure in a Walker-infested building. True heroes never die, don’t they?

Designed by Paul Cooper

At a glamorous costume party, rival cliques vie to outshine each other with elegance — but lurking among the guests are doppelgängers, creatures who imitate others so perfectly they vanish when confronted with their exact double. Style is everything, but only the real ones survive.

Doppelganger Tricks is a must-follow trick-taking game where special “doppelgänger” cards mimic the lead suit. However, if a doppelgänger encounters a card of the same suit and rank, both vanish from the trick. Players must strategically deploy these deceptive cards to win tricks (or eliminate threats) while anticipating what’s real and what’s a fake.

Get ready to face extreme temperatures, unpredictable weather… and freeze (or heat) your chances of winning!

In Grausº, players embark on a temperate adventure, manipulating temperature cards to balance the climate at zero degrees or move as far away from that mark as possible. Between cute penguins and tense rounds, whoever knows how to challenge the thermometer and accumulate the most temperature points wins. A family game that warms hearts and freezes minds!

—description from the publisher

The year is 1613 and the Globe Theatre has burned to the ground. William Shakespeare, now retired to Stratford-upon-Avon, is rattled by the news. How will his plays live on without the Globe? Will they have a life beyond his own? He calls you, a plucky and ambitious play-house apprentice, to his chambers with a challenge: rebuild the theatre, retell his stories, and ensure his legacy lives forever.

But when the great Bard dies in 1616 the world as you know it is turned on its head. You will have to use all of the skills learned at Shakespeare's knee to prove yourself a master worthy of the page and stage. Can you work with the members of Shakespeare's company, the King's Men, to cement his legacy and earn your place in the famed troupe?


     Visit Shakespeare’s contemporaries to gain valuable resources, experience, and perform actions. 
     Collect the Bard's work into your tableau ahead of peformance seasons in London. 
     Rachet up clamour for your shows by drafting and manipulating valuable genre dice. 


—description from the designer

boardgame: 私だけの晴天 (Watashi dake no Seiten)
description:
Rain pours gently as each player waits for their own clear skies. In a game of restraint and precision, players choose the moment to declare their personal trump color — not to dominate, but to balance. The goal isn’t to win the most, but to win just enough, exactly when the timing feels right.

私だけの晴天 (My Own Sunny Day) is a must-follow trick-taking game where each player can independently declare a personal trump suit mid-round. This declaration (called the “umbrella”) acts as a bid and players must aim to match the number of tricks won before and after it. Overcommit, and you’ll overshoot. Wait too long, and the timing might slip away. The second half flips the challenge by weakening hands while still requiring finesse in timing.

私だけの晴天 is a trick-taking card game with a poetic and thematic twist: each player experiences a "journey" of departure and return home under shifting weather, symbolized by umbrellas. The game adds a whimsical yet strategic layer where players must choose the perfect moment to open and close an umbrella, aligning with their position and the color of the cards they play.

The gameplay centers on traditional trick-taking mechanics—each player plays one card per round, and the highest card in the lead suit typically wins the trick. However, a unique twist occurs when players possess an umbrella that matches the color of the card they play. In that case, they become “Sunny,” gaining a powerful advantage that can override the usual hierarchy. But each player may only open and close their umbrella once per phase, making timing and planning essential.

The game unfolds in two phases: Departure and Return Home, each consisting of 13–14 tricks. In Departure, players must choose a moment to open their umbrella, and in Return Home, they must close it. Each trick won with or without an umbrella contributes differently to the player's final score: tricks won with an umbrella count as positive points, while those without count against you.

The goal is to finish the game with the lowest total score. Success requires balancing risk and restraint—sometimes winning a trick hurts you more than losing it. With its blend of light storytelling and clever card play, My Own Sunny Day is a contemplative game about finding just the right moment to step into the sun.

Inchiki Daifugo is a ladder-climbing card game built around variant rummy-style grouping and strict hand management.

Your goal is simple: be the first to empty your hand.

Despite the name “Cheat,” there is no bluffing—success depends on numerical ordering, combination efficiency, and careful timing.

Low cards and high cards are equally important, and precise hand control is essential.



Normal GroupsCards can be played as standard groups, such as multiple cards of the same number (e.g., 3-3-3-3).
Up to two colors may be mixed within a group.

Variant Groups (“Cheat”)

In a “Cheat” group, the number of cards played defines the minimum value of the set.
Players may include cards equal to or higher than that minimum value, and the highest number among them determines the group’s strength
(e.g., 4-4-6-10 → treated as 10-10-10-10).

There is no deception involved—the structure is entirely numerical.
publishers: 篠原遊戯重工 (Shinohara Yugi Juko)

description:
Helur is about shrinking the playing space from the maximum to the basic land mass.

In Helur, players remove space tiles and add gems and move gems to mark their territory. Players lose if you have 4 of your own pieces or any 5 or 6 pieces in a straight line at the end of your turn.  You also lose if there is no legal move in your turn.

In a culinary contest where moderation is key, players compete to eat just enough — not too little, not too much. Each round, you secretly bid how many dishes you want to eat, then play cards to try to match that number. Overeat, and you’ll need medicine to digest it. Undereat, and you won’t gain anything. Only those who walk the fine line between indulgence and restraint can win.

Fine Line is a must-follow, secret-bid trick-taking game where players earn points based on how close their trick wins are to their hidden bid. Players simultaneously select one card as a “bid” and one as a scoring card. At the end of the round, players score based on the difference between their bid and actual card count adjusting their track accordingly. To prevent overconsumption, players can use medicine cards that neutralize excess but only if taken correctly!

The player becomes the mayor and builds the city.
At the beginning of the game, a policy is randomly selected for each of the three regions (residential, industrial, and commercial) that will be the scoring rule. Seven of the 14 special landmarks that are scored by meeting conditions are randomly selected. Public utilities that are competitive with neighboring players are chosen at random. There are over 200 million combinations of these scoring rules, making every play different.
City building is done by open drafting, where the turn order and building cards are decided at the same time. Early turn players can take building cards as soon as possible. In the late turn, more building cards will be available in the next round.
The building cards taken are placed on the player's place. The cards are placed in an alternating weave, with the cards facing vertically next to the cards facing horizontally, and the cards facing vertically next to the cards facing horizontally. This results in a grid of connected paths written on the top and bottom of the cards.
The shortest way to a high score is to place the buildings well and connect the paths well.

—description from the designer

October 31st, the final night of Halloween! At Spooky Sales, a quirky Halloween store, huge discounts lure customers in for one last shopping spree. But behind the festive decorations lies a dark secret…

The store owner is none other than Count Dracula himself! Each year, on Halloween night, he commands his monstrous employees—disguised as humans—to abduct customers and turn them into new creatures of the night. Their goal: expand the monstrous workforce and keep Dracula’s business thriving.

However, not everything goes according to plan. Suspicious tax inspectors and relentless monster hunters are lurking around, threatening to expose the truth and shut the operation down.

How the Game Works:
Players take on the roles of monstrous employees working under Dracula. They must secretly capture as many customers as possible while avoiding detection. But beware—among them may be undercover inspectors or hunters trying to sabotage the mission! Will you serve the Count loyally, or will the dark truth finally come to light?

—description from the publisher

Prepare to test your resolve, resourcefulness, and resilience just like Gon and friends! Welcome to Hunter x Hunter: The 287th Exams, a dice-rolling adventure that challenges you to experience the high-stakes journey of earning a Hunter License.

In Hunter x Hunter: The 287th Exams, players take calculated risks, face setbacks, and develop their characters’ skills as they roll dice to overcome the gauntlet of trials that define the Hunter Exams. Players work together to get through the exams, using their skills and rolls to help each other out. If your teammates fall, the trial is over in this tough-as-nails adventure.

—description from the publisher

Meet MRS. Jenkins and her chonky cats who love to make a mess! From flying furballs to toppled towers, their home is a wild playground.

HOW TO PLAY: Aim for purr-fect while playing as squads of adorable, sticky-furred felines in a race to stack your chonks onto a growing tower.

Use hook-and-loop magic to launch your cats with precision. The first player to stack all their chonks wins!

—description from the publisher

Condemned to the Veldonian arena for your crimes, you are faced with a single chance to recover your life: fight countless battles to prove your mastery in combat and become Reborn. Carnage & Aether: Revenants of Veldonia is a tactical arena battler set in the world of Ker Nethalas in which your character must face terrible creatures and ruthless warriors as they climb through the ranks, seeking glory and redemption.

Carnage & Aether: Revenants of Veldonia features:

A simple, intuitive D100 ruleset that both veterans and newcomers to tabletop games can grasp in a matter of minutes.

Rich and detailed tactical combat that takes place in a grid map representing the Veldonian arena. Hit locations, multiple critical hit tables, defensive moves, and everything you need to bring brutal arena battles to life!

Near-infinite character builds. Instead of relying on classes, a character’s abilities and capabilities depend on the gear they have equipped. Tired of being a necromancer that shoots fireballs and wields a two-handed axe? Swap up your gear and become an ice-wielding warrior, a deadly sharpshooter, an assassin, or anything you can think of.

All gear is crafted based on the materials you obtain after defeating your enemies. Craft unique sets of gear to take advantage of an enemy’s weaknesses, and slowly but surely unlock the hundreds of different powers available to your character.

Patrons are an important source of benefits and support, so you must make sure you pick the one that will support you on your chosen path!

Dozens of different enemies, from terrible skeletons to enormous lizards, and anything in between, each one with their own behavior tables and actions, so you never know what to expect!

Complex Elite enemy fights that require you to completely change your usual strategies. Make sure you chop off that dangerous tentacle, or get rid of that armored tail, if you want to win the fight! Each Elite has a number of phases that you must go through before defeating them, gaining different abilities and actions!

Seven different types of matches, because not all fights must be to the death!

Written to be played solo or co-op, regardless of how many players there are! The game will throw you the exact challenge you need to have a balanced and fun fight.

—description from the designer

You're a crew of skate punks pulling tricks on the table literally. Each round is a battle not just of cards, but of dexterity. Only those who can stick their flip tricks get to score, so bring your skills, your guts, and your grip.

Tricky Kickflips is a must-follow trick-taking game where the lowest card in the lead suit wins but only if the player can successfully perform a physical "flip trick" by flipping their card from the edge of the table and landing it face-up. If they fail, the next-lowest card gets a chance, and so on. If no one succeeds, the highest card wins instead. The game merges traditional trick-taking structure with physical skill, making pip count and finesse equally important.

Tricky Kickflips can be played with a standard deck of cards, and was published as part of the Games with Cards booklet.

It’s summer break, and your mission is clear: finish your homework, chase after bugs, and write the best summer journal of all. But as you climb the social ladder of insect collecting, only those who take clever risks and manage their energy well will return with the best memories — and the most tokens to prove it.

Enikki is a climbing game for 3–4 players where hands are played out in traditional Daifugō style, but with a betting twist. At the start of each round, players place a hidden or open token bet on their performance. The top performer collects two tokens, middle ranks get one, and the lowest player must give all leftover cards to the winner. The game runs for four rounds, and the player with the most points in collected tokens wins.

boardgame: ルビーアイの大富豪 (Ruby Eye Daifugō)
description:
In this reality-bending version of Daifugō, a mysterious pair of ruby-red glasses changes what you see — and what you can play. Hidden numbers, redacted information, and lucky dice rolls create a party full of surprises as players race to empty their hands while navigating shifting visibility and unexpected turns. It's Daifugō… but with a twist of optical illusion.

Ruby Eye Daifugō is a climbing game where players attempt to shed all their cards using traditional Daifugō mechanics with a twist: some cards contain hidden numbers revealed only by removing or wearing ruby-colored glasses. During your turn, you may roll a die for a chance to perform a “Try” action, allowing you to put on or take off your glasses, potentially unlocking stronger plays. Players must strategically decide when to trigger their Try and adapt to an ever-shifting visual landscape.
publishers: ルビーアイの大富豪 (Ruby Eye's Billionaire)

boardgame: The Coming Wave: Rising Sun Over the East China Sea
description:
‘’’’’Dawn over the East Sea’’’’’, second instalment of ‘’’The Coming Wave’’’ series, moves to portray the intense air-naval conflict centred at the East China Sea and the first island chain.

‘’’The Coming Wave’’’ series are a simulation system that portrays hypothetical conflicts in the near-future. The system focuses on modern and next-generation warfare, where the tempo and lethality of modern warfare are supplemented by a wide array of additional layers: from electronic warfare and cyber warfare, all the way to space warfare. Challenging the players to make decision to utilize all of their assets to the fullest in combined air-land-naval warfare.

This game focuses on the operations around the unification of China, where Chinese gradually clears out the air and naval resistance from Taiwan, while also preparing the eventual showdown with American forces. The American player needs to make the most out of allies from the First island chain to contain Chinese forces and intervene with the Chinese operation, while the Chinese player conducts anti-intervention operations to ensure the smooth progress in the ground war, ensuring total unification. In these scenarios, United State sends three carrier battle groups to East China Sea for intervention, while China concentrates the cream of her naval forces: two carriers sortie out along the new 052DG and 055 class destroyers, tasked with the breakout from first island chain and become the vanguard of this anti-access/area denial operation at an unprecedented scale. Both sides are also supported by some of their best land-based strike assets, from long-range bombers including H-6, B-1B and B-2A to cruise and ballistic missiles, all for a single aim: the complete destruction of their opponent’s combat capability.

The game includes 6 scenarios, with 4 single-map scenarios, and 2 full 3-map scenarios arranged in story-arc format.

Components:
Three 841x594mm Maps
One 420x280mm Taiwan overlay Map
One Decision Book (Rulebook)
One Game Book
One Chart and Table Manual
One pad of fleet display
81 Airfield, port and command display cards
100 command point markers (Acrylic cubes)
180 plastic cube markers
1084 counters (594 18mm, 490 15mm)
Four Player aid cards
Three 10-sided dice

In 1917, a new kind of war broke out in Russia. Instead of nations and rulers competing for territory and power, social classes fought in a deadly struggle for survival. As World War I still raged, the October Revolution sparked a conflagration of Red and White armies clashing across the territory of the former Russian Empire. The world's first socialist government attempted to entrench its position and further the Revolution as Western democracies sent expeditions to stop the spread of communism. Both sides engaged in brutal repression of class opponents as the war dragged on into the 1920s and the Soviet Union came to be.

What Is to be Done? is a card-driven wargame for 2-6 players divided into Reds and Whites. Teammates must coordinate to succeed, but all coordination is public to opponents, so players must be strategic not only about what actions to take but also what information to communicate. The game begins with conflict taking place only in the core of Russia, but as the years pass it will spill over into up to six other regions.

Each year, each faction has only six cards which must be divided among mustering, attack, defense, and governance. Bonus effects on cards are activated using Bread and Industry tokens on the map, which must be protected from capture by the enemy and be carefully allocated among actions taken by the team. Success in combat requires a combination of accurate tactical calculation and reading of opponents' intentions. Each faction has a unique set of cards that tells a story about the nature of the conflict, and players can craft their decks over the course of the game according to necessity and taste. The Reds are made up of the Bolshevik party, the Red Guard revolutionaries, and the nascent Red Army. Opposed to them are the White Armies, the Allied Powers, and the Cossacks.
