Whichever side is able to create and sustain an effective system of governance while holding its own militarily will triumph, or else Russia will descend into chaos and only the strong will survive. WITBD? is the title of Lenin's pamphlet advocating the creation of a vanguard party, and it is also what players will need to ask themselves and their teammates in order to prevail in this grueling conflict.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
It’s double-overtime in the most important game of the season. You’re trailing the opposing team by 3 points. Every single man on your squad is exhausted, frustrated, and nursing some kind of injury. Now you’ve received the ball at your 20 and the last series has begun. Whether by ground or air, you need to move your squad 80 yards downfield and infiltrate the opponents’ end zone. You have 2 minutes left, and nothing short of a touchdown will do. Even a single mistake could mean sudden death.

Sudden Death is a solitaire game based on American gridiron football. Acting simultaneously as "coach," "quarterback," and "ball carrier," you execute a series of offensive plays against a system-controlled defensive team. The object of the game is to move the ball down the field and into the opponent’s end zone, scoring a touchdown, before the game clock runs out. If you do this, you win. If the game time runs out first, or if you lose possession of the ball due to an interception, a safety, a turnover on downs, or a fumble recovered by the opposing team, you lose.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Coming Wave: South China Sea Typhoon
description:
Typhoon over the South Sea', the third installment of The Coming Wave series, featuring the south east Asia and South China Sea as the center stage. For more than 20 years, powers within this diverse region are undergoing constant struggle and corporations, and intervention from superpowers could easily sparked conflict.

‘’’The Coming Wave’’’ series are a simulation system that portrays hypothetical conflicts in the near-future. The system focuses on modern and next-generation warfare, where the tempo and lethality of modern warfare are supplemented by a wide array of additional layers: from electronic warfare and cyber warfare, all the way to space warfare. Challenging the players to make decision to utilize all of their assets to the fullest in combined air-land-naval warfare.

The regional powers of South China Sea had purchased a wealth of assets from multiple superpowers. As a result, in this struggle for island, reefs, and political bargaining chips, players can experience a wide array of equipment from multiple origins: F/A-18D, Su-27SK, F-16A, and even the aged Su-22 are all tossed into the fray.

Unlike the previous installment of this series, it is a common sight for regional powers here to lack the full suite of combat assets. How to take control of the air, while lacking capable AEWAC support? Is it possible to survive an onslaught of bombers while lacking advanced area air defense ships? Players are challenged to use their limited assets to their best to complete their missions.

The game has 5 scenarios, where 4 are single-map scenarios, and 1 double map scenario. Depicting the conflicts between regional powers, and also 2 scenarios are included where U.S. and Chinese forces intervenes and escalates the conflict to a new level.

Components:
Three 841x594mm Maps
One 420x280mm Taiwan overlay Map
One Decision Book (Rulebook)
One Game Book
One Chart and Table Manual
One pad of fleet display
81 Airfield, port and command display cards
100 command point markers (Acrylic cubes)
180 plastic cube markers
1084 counters (594 18mm, 490 15mm)
Four Player aid cards
Three 10-sided dice

In this fast-paced original card game, players split into two teams and race to hit the Duel card when rival members of the Jedi and Sith are drawn to do battle!

The first player to react wins the duel and moves the Force token one step closer to their side, thus moving their team closer to victory. The first team to move the Force all the way to their side wins the battle for the galaxy!

Packaged in a unique Darth Vader mask-shaped portable case, this awesome action game is made to be played wherever you are in the galaxy! Featuring artwork of the most iconic Star Wars characters, including Luke Skywalker, Kylo Ren, and Yoda.

Your mission is to bring the power of the Force over to your side. Lay out the Force Track with the Force token in the middle and divide into two teams - the Light Side and the Dark Side. Place the Duel card in the middle and deal the rest of the deck out evenly to all players. Now, let battle commence. Alternating between Sides, players move quickly, taking turns flipping over one card from their pile. If rival members of the Jedi and Sith appear, then it's time to duel! The first player to smash their hand down on the Duel card wins the round and moves the Force token towards their side of the Track. But if you smash the Duel card incorrectly, then you lose the Force, and it moves back towards the opposition. And you must always stay alert for Action cards because they can help or hinder your quest. The first team to move the Force token all the way to their side wins this epic battle for the galaxy!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Hail Caesar: Rome's Conquest of the East
description:
Fight the Mithridatic Wars across Greece, Anatolia, and beyond with Rome’s Conquest of the East!

This mighty tome contains everything you need to bring the Mithridatic Wars of 88-63 BC to life on the Hail Caesar tabletop. 
Inside its covers you’ll find a wealth of historical background information covering Mithridates VI, ruler of Pontus, and his struggle against the increasing Roman domination of Asia and the Hellenistic world, as well as his allies, enemies, and battles, all accompanied by fantastic miniatures photography, informative maps, and gorgeous artwork.

The real glory of this book comes in its scenarios - a frankly astounding 70 of them - covering the three Mithridatic Wars from start to finish, with all manner of engagements from skirmishes to pitched battles to grand sieges detailed and brought to the gaming table. Full army lists allow you to field the forces of Rome and Pontus, and a host of new special rules lend the battles a truly unique feel. These scenarios are designed to be played either as standalone games, or as part of a linked campaign, and Rome’s Conquest of the East also includes a full map-based campaign system, putting you in command of mighty armies and fleets as war rages across land and sea!

—description from the publisher

In Cocktail Maestro, you must quickly handle customer orders, gather ingredients ,manage your shaker and mix cocktail as fast and accurately as you can . The play who focuses the best and remembers the most and acts the quickest will stand out as the "Cocktail Maestro " of the night! Re you the one ?

Players uses cards to collect ingredients. To mix the drinks, players stack up the cards faced down. The purpose is to remember the cards you draw (ingredients)and and "mix " them according to customer's order and get it right. Once the orders is fulfilled, we can count the points on the cards to see who wins. There is penalty for forgetting your cards and tokens will be assigned with penalty points.

It's a fast pace fun little party game for all.

—description from the publisher

GAME DESCRIPTION
King Krowfell of Krystalia is dead and left no heir. Now it's time to claim the throne by crafting nine Power Krystals to forge the ultimate Krown!
In Krafting Krowns, players deploy dice strategically and manipulate their values using cards from six powerful Klans. Transform Raw Krystals (dice showing 1) into Power Krystals (dice showing 3) through clever combinations of Magic, Master, and Menace cards.
The game features three distinct phases that create a compelling gameplay loop: Gathering (competitive bidding for cards), Snatching (last-chance card acquisition), and Krafting (strategic dice manipulation).
What makes Krafting Krowns unique:

Three-phase gameplay cycle with competitive card bidding using dice as currency
Dual-purpose dice: bid for cards in Gathering, then manipulate them in Krafting
Resource tension: Every Power Krystal you craft reduces your bidding power for future rounds
Self-balancing gameplay: Early leaders face disadvantages in later rounds, keeping games competitive
Immediate victory: Win the moment you place your 9th Power Krystal

VITAL STATS
Player Count: 2-4 players
Playing Time: 30 minutes to 90 minutes
Age: 14+
Complexity: Medium
COMPONENTS:

54 Krafting cards (Magic, Master, Menace across 6 Klans)
36 custom 8-sided dice (9 per player)
6 Klan zone cards
4 Krown cards
3 Turn Marker cards
4 Reminder cards

Core Mechanics:

Dice Rolling & Manipulation
Hand Management
Auction/Bidding
Area Control
Take That

THE SIX KLANS
Master the powers of six distinct magical factions, each with their own Klan zone:

SINGING SIRENS SHORE - Mystical shore dwellers with enchanting abilities
CURSED HUMANS CITY - Resilient village inhabitants with determined spirit
GRAVE UNDEAD YARD - Graveyard dwellers wielding dark powers
ELEMENTAL SPIRITS MOUNTAIN - Elemental mountain forces
MYSTIC FAERIES FOREST - Forest mystics channeling nature's magic
UNSACRIFICEABLE GOATS FIELDS - The stubborn guardians of the realm

GAME FLOW
The game cycles through three phases until a winner emerges:
Gathering Phase: Roll dice one at a time to bid on face-up and face-down cards. Rolling a 3 wins instantly, or accumulate dice totaling 3+. Dice on lost cards become "burnt" and unavailable until Krafting.
Snatching Phase: Re-roll idle dice for a final chance at remaining cards. Choose which dice to keep and which to re-roll, then place them strategically on remaining cards.
Krafting Phase: First allocate all dice to Klan zones (starting value: 1), then play cards to manipulate dice values. Combine Magic cards (flip to 2) with Master cards (add +1) to create Power Krystals. Menace cards disrupt opponents or provide tactical advantages.
Cards carry over between sessions, creating long-term planning opportunities and resource management decisions.

DESIGNER NOTES
"Krafting Krowns emerged from our desire to create a game where dice serve multiple strategic purposes. The dual-resource system means every decision ripples through multiple phases—aggressive bidding wins cards but leaves fewer dice for future rounds.
The self-balancing mechanism naturally prevents runaway victories. Players who craft Power Krystals early have fewer dice for subsequent Gatherings, while conservative players maintain stronger card acquisition potential. Victory goes to whoever best manages the delicate balance between immediate gains and future opportunities."
—Stefano Virgilli & Ernestas Jasilionis

PERFECT FOR

Players seeking meaningful decisions with dice manipulation
Groups that enjoy competitive bidding and resource management
Fans of tactical card play with "take that" elements
Game nights needing something fresh that plays in under an hour
Anyone who appreciates hand-crafted artwork (no AI art here!)

Launched at SPIEL Essen with the magical kingdom of Krystalia awaiting its next ruler. Crown not included, but glory guaranteed!

Mystery Monsters is a fun, deduction card game where each player is assigned a secret monster card — known only to them. Your mission? Figure out the other players' mystery monsters before they guess yours!

With ten unique monsters in five colours, every game is full of sneaky strategies, sharp questions, and hilarious “aha!” moments. It’s easy to learn, great for all ages, and perfect for groups who love a bit of mystery and mind games.

—description from the publisher

Paradise – A Wastepunk Boardgame is a skirmish game set in a post-apocalyptic world.
With stunning artwork and deep tactical gameplay, Paradise blends wasteland survival with a unique "wastepunk" aesthetic in a brutal fight for dominance.

Players lead rival factions fighting for control over a dying land. Each faction consists of four main characters (one of whom is the leader) and two henchmen.

The game is played in rounds, where each player alternates turns, activating one miniature each.

Miniatures can move on the map using some range tools to check distances.

Fights are based on dice rolls, and characters are not eliminated; they don’t die. They get KOed instead.

There is also a deck of cards that helps players in their strategies and fights. Those cards are dealt and drafted at the beginning of the game.

There are some objectives in each scenario, which are represented by points of interest on the map.

There are some terrain features that can be used to players’ own advantage.

Players earn points from controlling those points of interest and knocking out the opponent’s characters.

The first player to get to a specific number of victory points (according to the scenario) is the winner.

—description from the publisher

With the Ecogon: Vögel Expansion ("Ecogon - Birds expansion"), your ecosystem soars to lofty heights! This new adon brings magnificent birds to your game and makes Ecogon more colorful and varied than ever. Look forward to 14 new animals, 2 new plants and 4 uniquely designed habitats that not only visually but also mechanically bring a breath of fresh air to your rounds.

—description from the publisher

Something is burrowing through the ground. Beetle larvae are looking for plant remains and even a wild cat makes an appearance from time to time. Are you ready for the underground? With the Ecogon: Untergrund ("Ecogon Underground") expansion, 13 new animals, 3 new plants and 4 unique habitats enrich your ecosystem.

—description from the publisher

Introducing LOGOLOGIC, the exciting strategy game where kids match the colors and symbols to win.

How To Play: Roll the 21 dice and take turns removing one or more dice of the same color or symbol. The player that has to remove the last dice loses the round and must take a loser token. After seven rounds, the player with the fewest loser tokens wins!

—description from the publisher

A 2-player diceless boardgame covering Anzac Cove battles during the Gallipoli campaign of 1915.  It includes three scenarios: The Invasion, The Counterattack, and The August Offensive.

A turn consists of each player taking three actions to move or attack.  Combat is done quickly by comparing values on the units. The losing side flips a unit over--and the attacker always flips a unit over as well.  Variation comes from random, secret chits each player begins the game with permitting extra actions or combat advantages.

Victory is determined by counting the value of the spaces controlled by the ANZAC player.  This is compared to the scenario's requirements.  Depending on the scenario, the ANZAC will need to control 7, 8, or 11 points to achieve victory while the Ottoman will want to hold the total to 5, 6, or 9, otherwise the game ends in a stalemate.

The scenarios play in under 60 minutes and are suitable for all ages.

—description from the designer
family: History: World War I –  The Gallipoli Campaign
Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Terra Altera: a Song of the Forgotten Kingdoms
description:
In an alternate world where history took a different path, new civilizations are ready to rise. In Terra Altera, you lead your people as they build realm, discover powerful technologies, manage resources, and shape the future of the world. Starting with just a few workers and simple tech, you’ll develop your realm by growing your population, gathering vital resources like energy, rock, gold, culture, and influence, and advancing through a rich technology tree. You'll build wonders, inspire your civilization with legendary leaders, and guide your people through both peaceful and turbulent times. But you won’t be alone. Competing civilizations will challenge your power. Through wars, aggressions, and clever diplomacy, you’ll have to defend what’s yours—or take what you need. Trade routes through caravans and smart planning will help your empire grow even stronger. Terra Altera combines the strategy of worker placement with the flexible choices of imperial cards, offering new actions and diplomatic options every turn. Every decision matters, and no two games will ever be the same. The game is played on a detailed main board along with individual player boards, and is designed for 2 to 4 players. Whether you expand through culture, strength, or influence, your goal is the same: to lead your civilization to greatness in a world unlike any other. The future of Terra Altera is in your hands. Will your empire stand the test of time?

This is a prototype board game. All game mechanics have been thoroughly tested and are approximately 98% complete. For now, we are using AI-generated images to help us move forward with the game design. We understand the concerns surrounding AI-generated artwork and deeply value original artistic expression. I have shared more thoughts on the use of AI in game development and its implications in a dedicated forum post.

—description from the designer

Mosses cover the ground, colorful mushrooms sprout up and the branches of the trees are densely covered with lichen. Expand your ecosystem to include the fascinating world of cryptogams!

—description from the publisher

A 1v1 PVP Roguelite card game where you are tested on your ability to bluff, draft cards and improvise. Choose from 6 classes, each with a 10 card deck and unique ability, then draft 3 spells and build a strategy with Trinkets and Upgrades you find along the way.

Each turn, you roll a pair of dice to determine the effectiveness of the cards you play, but dice value is hidden from opponents, which is where bluffing comes in - do you attack, scout or call for a truce?

Fight your opponent over 15 turns, each represented by a hidden location card that affects your decision making. Spells and Trinkets can swing the tide of battle for you, modifying the cards on the table as well as the dice rolls of players, allowing you to spring nasty surprises on that opponent you baited to attack you.

Every game of Halfwish is different and unlike other CCGs, its built for replayability and has been made difficult to "solve".

—description from the designer

Take the ultimate route filled with four zones-water, road, cloud, and railroad. Feel the rush to be the first to get home by avoiding your opponent's zone in the route!

Driving is an adventure! You can be stuck in traffic jams, encounter everything from a detour to a flat tire, and even see a parade! Be the first to navigate the city streets and reach the finish line before the other players do!

How to play the game:

     The player who rolls the highest number goes first, and the play proceeds clockwise.
     Each player rolls the die to determine how many squares to move.
     If a player lands on a square corresponding to their zone, they move one square forward.
     If a player lands on a square corresponding to another player's zone, they move two squares backwards.
     If a player lands on a star, they move three squares forward.


Winning the game: The player to complete the route and reach their start point first WINS!

—description from publisher

It's a sort of trick taking game, but simplified. Each round a player calls out one of two colours available and plays a card. Others follow with a single card, after which everyone can play aditional special cards. Whoever has the highest sum in the given colour takes all the cards. The game is over when all the cards are spent at which point whoever has the most cards won, wins.

You may get burned, and you may get iced, but you're sure to get hooked on Fire & Ice, the tricky new card game for partners and individuals aged 8 and up.

—description from the back the box

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Indomitable Survivor: Dashing Reaper Paleun
description:
Dashing Reaper - Paleun is packaged in a graphic box that includes:

1 x Dashing Reaper - Paleun hard plastic miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
1 x Gnawed Gastrolith Screaming Antelope indomitable resource card
1 x Dashing Reaper pattern card
2 x Dashing Reaper pattern gear card
1 x Install guide card
1 x Large matte art print

The thunder of hooves was upon her before she could scramble to her feet. Suddenly she was plucked into the air, her armor snagged between two massive gnashing teeth. Bouncing over the stone faced ground, she twisted wildly, searching for a handhold. Grabbing the froth-soaked fur she felt the pulsing, bulging edges of the Screaming Antelope’s straining neck. Her grip wouldn’t last. She lashed out with her bladed fists. The braying of the monster ceased. A shower of hot ichor spilled from its wounded throat. Something hard sailed out of the wound striking her head. Her grip failed and she tumbled to the ground, landing alongside the strange stone. The monster slid past her, gouging the ground with its silent undermaw.

The hunt is on! Big Spot ‘n Bop is the crazy game with the giant inflatable tower of fun! Spin the spinner to see if you’ll race against the clock, go head-to-head, or if everyone plays at once. Pick a color, flip a card and see if you can spot ‘n bop the matching item!

You might be chasing a mouse, a monster or an alien. A pup, a pug or a hot dog! They're all there, hidden in plain sight as the tower wibbles and wobbles!

First to collect 3 picture cards wins!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the world of graffiti! In this exciting R&W game, players will immerse themselves in a tag battle across the city. Their goal is to leave their mark by graffitiing walls and dominating as many surfaces as possible, all while avoiding being caught by the police. The graffiti artist who manages to control the most walls will be proclaimed king of the city! Go ahead, get ready for a competition full of cunning, tactics, and, above all, lots of street art!

—description from the designer

In Disney Lorcana: Reign of Jafar, the eighth set for the Disney Lorcana CCG, Jafar now possesses the Hexwell Crown and, with it, he has corrupted Archazia's Island to his wicked will.

Traps and illusions abound in Reign of Jafar, and glimmers like "Jasmine – Steady Strategist" and "Rapunzel – High Climber" will need to band together to steal back the crown and defeat the evil vizier.

—description from the publisher

Long ago, the King wanted to spice up the spectacle of the Dragon Fighting Pits, to which end his trusted Wizard, a capricious fellow, began to cast enchantments on the dragons during tournaments causing wild and exciting transformations to the participants – Monstrous Mutations!

In Monstrous Mutations you take on the role of a dragon master. Your job is to field the mutations created by the Wizard so they affect your dragons in the optimum way, and then to direct your dragons in battle.

You start the game with a team of three battle dragons, each of which has a similar, but slightly different set of battle statistics. Your goal is to defeat all three of your opponent’s dragons. The game takes place over a maximum of nine rounds where each round comprises a Mutations phase followed by a Combat phase.

Monstrous Mutations has been designed as a two player game, but it is easy to make it playable by more. Three players could take two dragons each and draw two mutations per round, or you could combine multiple sets of Monstrous Mutations for even more players, who could either play in teams or in a frantic, all out Battle Royal to the last dragon standing.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Master of Orion: Ad Astra Stretch Goals
description:
The Stretch goals adds 2 new empires with their own tach tracks:

     Terran Khanate - Ready for war with an economy primed to produce endless warships
     Trilarian Shoal - Trans-dimensional beings that can teleport to any area they control  


Adds new game mechanics:

     Basic Spies - espionage can now start in round 1
     Ship Upgrades - time to get that warp drive
     Forgotten Stars

boardgame: Master of Orion: Ad Astra – Galactic News Network
description:
The Galactic News Network expansion puts the emphasis on galactic expansion over diplomacy and negotiation.

It adds three new empires with their own tech tracks:

     Brood of Sakkra - Born invaders, they reject science and diplomacy for relentless expansion.
     Meklar Combine - Cybernetic organisms who run their empire at peak efficiency.
     Alkari Flock - Aviators who, with their personal fleet of ships, can colonize a world or deploy a fleet at a moment's notice.


Adds new game mechanics:
- Galactic News Network events
- Admirals and governors
- Tech cards, diplomats, discovery tiles, and planets

Step into a world on the brink of ruin with The Fall of Rothenburg, an immersive expansion for 1490 Doom. This epic campaign is designed for both solo and cooperative play, bringing new depth and challenges to the grimdark tabletop experience you love.

Key Features:


     Beautifully Designed Campaign eBook: Follow the gripping story of Wilhelm and his loyal dog, Berengar, as they confront the horrors of a crumbling Rothenburg.
     New “KNIGHT” Warrior Class: Wield the blade of honor and duty with the brand-new class tailored for your Doom Company.


Legendary Doom Company: Assemble a team of iconic characters to aid in your desperate struggle.

     Wilhelm & Berengar STLs: Bring the protagonists to life with stunning miniatures ready for printing and painting.


Official Music – “Wilhelm’s Theme”: Immerse yourself in the haunting soundscape of Rothenburg.

     This isn’t just a game—The Fall of Rothenburg is a harrowing tale of loyalty, survival, and sacrifice. Will you lead your Doom Company to salvation, or will the shadow of Rothenburg consume you?


Prepare your warriors. The fall has begun.

Perfect for fans of grimdark narratives, customizable miniatures, and immersive tabletop campaigns.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Master of Orion: Ad Astra – Galactic council
description:
The Galactic Council expansion adds additional gameplay focused on diplomacy and negotiation.

Adds three unique empires, each with its own tech tree:
- Gnolam League - expert merchants with a mercenary fleet to maintain.
- Mrrshan Pride - proud but lazy warriors, reliant on others for resources.
- Elerian Fiefdom - an all-seeing race capable of manipulating others. They excel at combat and espionage.

Also adds new game mechanics:
- Minor civilizations
- Galactic Council mode
- Tech cards, diplomats, discovery tiles, and planets

boardgame: Master of Orion: Ad Astra – Antarans Revenge
description:
Adds new game mechanics:

     Huge Galaxy Mode
     Antarans Ravage Mode
     Custom Factions


With the custom faction mechanic, players are able to design unique civilizations with tailored traits.

Slip through the shadows. Raid the outposts. Save the captives—before the creeping death claims them all.

The Gates of Rennes is a stealth-focused campaign expansion for 1490 Doom, featuring four intense narrative scenarios centered on Adrian, Father Gregor, and Simon, the Mute Assassin. Designed for solo or cooperative play, this expansion introduces the new PATROL FOCUS system, transforming enemy behavior into a tense, reactive challenge. Visibility, line of sight, and player movement all matter—every step could be your last.

Inside this expansion you’ll find:

    Four linked narrative scenarios, including a prison break, a cathedral siege, and a final showdown with Bishop Charon himself.
    The new PATROL FOCUS AI system, creating smart, sweeping enemies with rotating patrols and dynamic detection rules.
    New mechanics for visibility, stealth, and mission objectives that make every round feel razor-tight.
    The official character stats and abilities for Adrian, Father Gregor, and Simon—each fully playable in narrative or standard games.
    A powerful new warrior class: The Executioner, Bishop Charon’s brutal enforcer.


Plan your ambushes. Rescue the doomed. Strike before the gates close forever.

—description from the publisher

Students of quantum physics professor Heinrich Zeist, you have been transported through space and time following a failed teleportation experiment. And as if that weren’t enough, you soon learn — thanks to the butterfly effect — that you must fix the catastrophes caused by your time jumps in order to come back.
Inside this box: 60 cards featuring surprising twists and a very simple game mechanic will transport you to the year 1900, to a Norman manor, where you’ll face a compromising situation involving Arsène Lupin. Investigate and solve puzzles to restore temporal anomalies!

—description from the publisher

Sobresaliente is a competitive "push your luck" board game, quick, simple, and with tremendous replayability!

In Sobresaliente, players compete to get the highest score in the exam, but it's not about answering academic questions. The game tests your strategy, intuition, and ability to manage risks, while trying to accumulate points without going over the limits.

Objective of the game:

The goal of Sobresaliente is to be the player who gets the highest score in the exam at the end of the game. To achieve this, players must make decisions on when to take risks and when to stop, trying to maximize their points without losing them due to a bad move. The player who manages their decisions the best will be the winner!

How to play:


     Push your luck: On each turn, players must decide whether to keep risking or stop to secure their points. The more they risk, the more points they can get, but they also risk losing everything.
     Manage your resources: Players need to decide how to use their resources (cards, actions, etc.) strategically, always looking to maximize their score without risking it all.
     Get the highest score: At the end of the game, the player with the most points accumulated will have "earned the best grade" and be the winner.


Key features:

Indirect learning: Sobresaliente seeks to bring the world of mathematics closer to its detractors in a fun way, indirectly working on math-related skills without the player noticing. You learn while having fun!

Quick filler with high replayability: A short game that can be played over and over again, with decisions changing each time, keeping the excitement and tension alive.

Familiar theme: The exam theme is known to almost everyone, and the game has been created by a Secondary School Mathematics teacher, giving it an educational and relatable touch.

Game components:

Action cards that allow players to make strategic moves during the game.
Resources that help players make decisions and manage risks.
Point markers to track each player's progress.

Why play Sobresaliente?


     Suitable for all ages: The game is easy to learn, making it ideal for playing with family and friends of all ages.
     Mental challenge and strategy: Sobresaliente tests players' ability to manage risks and make smart decisions on each turn.
     Fun and learning: While it's a competitive game, it also promotes strategic thinking and decision-making, working on math skills indirectly.


Prove your ability to manage risks and get the best grade.

—description from the designer
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

boardgame: The Village Crone: Silver Promo Coaster
description:
Silver promo coasters that were given away during the promotional tour for the game.

They are real drink coasters, and they also have an in-game effect: 1 time per game they can be spent as an extra unit of silver.

boardgame: Wine Cellar: More Clients Promo Cards
description: A pack of 9 Client cards to add to the base game, part of the original KS bonus.

OVERVIEW

The world as we known it was destroyed in a nuclear war. Mankind hiding in underground complexes, managed to survive thanks to new post-nuclear silicon crystals called Serikon. Machines created with this material gained the ability to evolve and became self-aware. Together with Humans they ware able to leave the underground and build new civilnization. With technology breakout and help form the machines, humanity develop implants and the ability to create new synthetic life forms. Over time, however, the machines realized that humans were unable to survive in the new reality without their assistance — and some no longer wished to help them. Society fractured into several Factions — each with its own rights and vision for the future. Choose your Faction. Dominate the Metropolis! Control the Serikon ore and lead the revolution that will shape the new order!

Gears Genesis is a 2 to 6 players strategy game where you compete for control of the Metropolis. Conquer new areas and enlist loyalists before the revolution begins — and make sure you’re the one leading the future!

Note: All artwork is AI-generated, modified, and implemented solely to enhance the gameplay experience. The physical release will feature original artwork created by professional artists.

GAMEPLAY

Each player begins game with three Command Action Cards and one War Action Card in hand. They have also some troops on their Mother Ship and Faction Generator areas, along with Communication Plant on the Generator. During Turn, player can play one or two Action Cards, each on five possible ways:
1. To make Troop and Leader Movement (only Command Action Card).
2. To declare Conflict (only War Action Card).
3. To take Supply Card.
4. To use card Main Action.
5. To use card Minor Action.

As a last step of played card, player have to resolve card Event (presented by icon on the card).
There are five event effects.

1. Time Token Advancing - on Time Board. 
2. Faction Generator Maintenance.
3. Agents Reports.
4. Socciety Tensions.
5. Leadership.

Between actions, player can also:

1. Use Active District Tokens of controlled Areas to gain various benefits.
2. Play Operation Cards.
3. Take Over the Power (only if he control Palace).
4. Use Supply to gain new Action Cards.

If player decide to End Turn, as a last step takes:

1. One Command Action Card to hand.
2. One War Action Card to hand.
3. One Supply Card to Warehouse (if not full already).

Than another player takes Turn (clockwise).

If as a result of Time Token advance it reaches End Game field, game immediately ends and Victory Points calculation begins. Each Faction gain:

1. Victory Points from Factional Area control (amounts depends of Factions Relations on Faction Sheet).
2. Victory Points from Heart Of The City Areas.
3. Victory Points from Palace Area control. 
4. Victory Points from Airspace Area control.
5. Victory Points for Captives.
6. Victory Points from Enemy Faction Generator control.

The game wins the player with the most Victory Points!

—description from the designer

Speed Bac Kids is the perfect fast-paced party game to get kids (and grown-ups) thinking and laughing!

Each player starts with 5 letter cards. A fun and easy theme is revealed — like “A funny animal,” “A color in the rainbow,” or “A silly job.” The 3 quickest players can shout out a word that matches the theme and starts with one of their letters. If it fits, they discard that letter. The first player to get rid of all their letters wins the round!

Simple, quick, and filled with giggles, Speed Bac Kids is a great way to spark creativity, develop vocabulary, and encourage quick thinking. With child-friendly themes and easy rules, it’s ideal for family game night, classroom play, or a fun session with friends.

Recommended from age 7 and up, it’s designed to entertain kids and grown-ups alike — whether you’re playing with the little ones or joining in for some fast-paced fun yourself.

—description from the publisher

Prepare for your next great adventure! Quatermain: Lost Cities adds a new way to gain wealth by defeating the enemies guarding the entrance to ancient cities deep in the African interior. However, only the explorer to discover the most lost cities will be able to score them at the end of the game.

The expansion adds more replayability to Quatermain with additional adventure cards, a new gun, and new animals. These all follow the existing game rules. It also adds the namesake "Lost City" cards to the game. When somebody defeats whatever is guarding a lost city, they claim this new form of wealth card.

Quatermain: Lost Cities continues to support solo rules, along with standard rules for 2-4 players. A major focus of the expansion is on hunting and defeating enemies at long range. A new gun is available to aid in this endeavor, and both the lost city cards and new animals will reward players who are able to impact things at long range.

—description from the publisher

Battle for the Iron Throne in the Monopoly House of Dragon Edition game! A civil war is raging to claim the most powerful seat in the Seven Kingdoms. Pledge loyalty to a house by choosing one of 6 themed golden tokens. Then move around the board to travel through Westeros and across the Narrow Sea to Essos, and take control of strongholds, lands, and bays. The more you own, the more rent you can collect from opponents. Will you secure dragon eggs and fearsome dragons to bring your enemies to their knees? The last player with coins when all others have lost theirs wins! This strategy board game’s board, packaging, tokens, money, cards, and game pieces are all inspired by the popular TV series.

—description from the publisher
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire

description:
Together with the citizens—build the city of your dreams!
In Vientor City, people from all walks of life come together to live and work.
From police officers and firefighters who keep the city safe, to architects and farmers who bring beauty and bounty, and even tram drivers and shop clerks who support everyday life—each citizen plays a vital role.
As the leader of a major city project, you won’t rely on your own power alone. You’ll work side by side with these citizens, leveraging their strengths to overcome challenges and shape a more livable, vibrant city for all.

By adding Desolito: Citizen to Desolito Core Game, you can enjoy a deeper, more strategic gameplay experience.
This expansion includes 160 wooden tokens, of which 80 are used in each game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dune: War for Arrakis – Ecological Testing Stations
description:
EXTENDED MODE

During game preparation, shuffle the 6 Ecological Research Station cards and place them face down next to the game board.

ERWEITERTER MODUS

Mischt bei der Spielvorbereitung die 6 Ökologische Forschungsstation-Karten und legt sie verdeckt neben den
Spielplan.

A mini card game for 2-10 scallywags!

Manoeuvre your pirate or Royal Navy ships to fire your cannons or board other ships in an attempt to steal their treasure.

Place compass cards to move your ship, cannon cards to fire at and sink other ships, helm cards to evade cannon fire and cutlass cards to board other ships.

Sink or board a ship to steal their treasure. When all pirate ships are sunk the player with the most treasure wins.

—description from the designer

Do vegans deserve a tax break?
 Are we living in a simulation?
 Sweaty lovers are better lovers, right?
 Would you ever buy a haunted house?

What are your thoughts here? And more importantly; do you think you know your friends’ opinions on these?

Mindline challenges you to read the minds of the others at the table, before they express their views. Are you sure you know their takes? Or could they surprise you with an entirely unexpected response?

Whatever happens, you’ll end-up knowing a bit more about the people you surround yourself with!

In Mindline, players try to collectively score as many points as possible.

Each round the active player draws a card and reads the Statement printed on it. The others then try to guess the active player's thoughts on that Statements.
If they are correct, the card is placed in the Win pile, otherwise it goes to the Loss pile.

At the end of the game, the score is calculated and players can check out how well they did on the back of the rulebook.

—description from the designer

Crop Rotation is a card game that is fully playable in hand, in which two competing players slip into the roles of vegetable gardeners who share a plot of land and who both strive to outdo each other with their harvests.

In order to achieve this, you will have to cleverly exploit the benefits of efficient companion planting as well as using crop rotation after your opponent’s season to your best advantage to grow your collection of plant shoots into a luscious and verdant garden of delicious vegetables.

Do you have the green thumb necessary to come out ahead in this interactive head-to-head race for a bountiful harvest?


     Crop Rotation was an entry in the 2025 In-Hand Game Design Contest, in which it was voted #1 Best Overall Multiplayer Game


—description from the publisher

Slay to Win is a fast-play  monster-hunting fantasy card game with a touch of light optional roleplaying.

In the game, you and your companions strike out to save your kingdom from the monsters who threaten its peoples. But...each of you also secretly wants to go down in history as the greatest monster hunter of all time. And that means you need to score the killing blows against the monsters to gain as many trophy points as possible.

At the end of the game (or a longer campaign of multiple games), whoever has the most trophy points becomes forever known as the greatest monster hunter who ever lived.

Team up, but also go it alone when needed, so you can Slay to Win!

—description from the designer

In The Masters, each player selects a character and engages in three rounds of duels. A player wins a round by reducing their opponent's HP to 0. The first player to win two duels is the overall winner.

You are about to begin a new story, starting from your earliest memories. Guided by your wishes, you will choose various life experiences. Along the way, others will become part of your journey, turning it into a story that's more than just your own.

In Life Express / 人生列車, players build a life by selecting different experiences. At the end of the game, each fulfilled wish earns points.

To win SHARYUMON, you want to collect the fewest types of dinosaur cards.

To set up, shuffle the sixty cards, create five stacks with 1-2 face-down cards, then deal a card from the deck face up on each stack.

On a turn, flip a card from the deck onto one of the three central stacks, then compare the numbers on both sides. If the card you flip falls between these two numbers, you're safe; if it's outside the range of these two numbers, collect one of the surrounding cards; and if it matches one of the two numbers, collect all of the cards in the stack where you place the card! If a stack lacks a face-up card, reveal its top face-down card or flip a card from the deck face up on this stack.

Every type of dinosaur counts as 1 point, unless you have 4+ cards of a type in which case it counts as 2 points. When a player hits a point threshold, the game ends, and whoever has the fewest points wins.

Some cards feature an egg, and these eggs can trigger special actions if you play with one of the three expansion rule cards.

In Who is this?! / これ、ダレ?!, players want to identify matching characters through description cards.

To set up, deal each player a hand of five description cards and place one face up on the table. On a turn, the active player places a card next to the card(s) already on the table or covers a card on the table. All players then simultaneously write down a name on their personal whiteboard, then reveal them. If anyone named the same person as the active player, this is a success — and if you have two more successes than the number of players before everyone runs out of cards, you all win.

The alarm's been set but nobody knows how long before it goes off. Everyone has their own button and there's no time for fumbling on this one. Using quick-thinking skills and a fast touch, you must answer questions rapidly and push your button before the panic alarm goes off. Whoever's button is lit up when the alarm sounds takes the question card.

Players keep playing for a number of rounds and compete for the fewest cards at the end of the game.
Expand the basic Cities game with a new achievement board. That includes the main attractions of the cities of Hamburg and Berlin for even more variety.

Earn as many points as possible by asking your game partner(s) the right questions. The better you know your partner(s), the better you will choose the questions they can answer, increasing your chances of scoring points and winning.

On each turn, the current player can decide to play a card from their own hand, or draw a card from an opponent, of which they only know the theme. Then, they give a clue about the question to their partner, who responds "Dare" if they think they can answer, or "Chickpea" if they need answer choices to figure it out. In addition to this, there is a die that will either simplify or complicate their task.

boardgame: アッフェンボロウの決戦 (The Battle of Apahehmbollaw)
description:
アッフェンボロウの決戦 (The Battle of Apahehmbollaw) is a single-player game in which you roll a die each round and assign it to a gate to create a hand to defeat the enemy facing the gate and defend the city of Apahehmbollaw.

You win by defeating the enemy boss or eliminating the enemy's deck, but you lose if even one of your monsters is destroyed before that happens.

Grandmaster is an expansion for Kushi Express. It allows up to 6 players to enjoy the game together and introduces a new topping ingredient: leek. New skewer recipes featuring leek have been added, bringing more variety to the gameplay. After the main game, a tournament phase takes place to determine the ultimate Grandmaster.

—description from the publisher

Isn't it magical when we think the same thing without saying a word?...

A game for two players who will try to unite their minds and test their level of harmony. Overcoming communication problems and achieving this in a short amount of time will be essential to winning this game.

Players must pass the numerical and color codes under the pressure of a limited time. Each turn is an adventure in which they will overcome communication problems.

—description from the publisher

So, you and your overconfident penguin friends have decided to prove the world wrong and take flight off an icy cliff. Unsurprisingly, it's not going well. Your only goal? Stay in the air longer than the others because the first penguin to hit the water -well, there are three hungry orcas waiting below.

In Penguins Can't Fly, each player selects a penguin character and begins at the top of the laid-out track cards. Players then take turns drawing cards from the deck. Most cards will send you closer to the water, bad news. But action cards let you mess with your opponents, switch places, look ahead, or force someone else to drop faster. Track cards can cause mid-air chaos or slow some penguins down, while others are unaffected.

One by one, penguins plummet until only one remains, the last true believer in penguin aviation. That player wins. Play cards, avoid falling, sabotage your friends, and most importantly, don’t become orca lunch.

—description from the publisher

Bring your family together with this laugh-out-loud game of fun questions, silly voting, and clever word guessing! Perfect for family game nights, it's packed with surprises, bonding moments, and plenty of opportunities to learn something new about each other!

In Family Guest Fest, each turn the active player picks a card and reads one of three prompts: single word, vote, or question. They think of their answer to the question and then read the prompt to the other players, who then try to be the first one to guess active players answer. The first one to guess the answer correctly gets the card. The first player to 10 cards wins.

La Seconda Guerra del Monferrato ("The Second War of Montferrat") is a historical simulation game designed for two players: the Pro-France Faction and the Pro-Spain Faction. Players command units on the map, engaging and trying to solve all the problems faced by the commanders of the time, their historical counterparts, and trying to reach the victory conditions.

—description from the publisher

The game is released in Para Bellum issue XV and uses a system similar to "La Guerra di Gradisca 1615-1617".
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Ottaggio 1625 is a Tactical level two-player board-wargame that simulates the battle fought on 9th April 1625 between the forces of the Duchy of Savoy, allied to the Kingdom of France, and those of the Republic of Genoa, allied to the Kingdom of Spain, in Ottaggio during the First Genoese-Savoyard War.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Panzerwaffe is a solitaire game that allows you to take command of a German tank on the Eastern Front.

Between 1941 and 1945, some of the mightiest tank battles in History raged between Soviets and Germans, leading to the loss of thousands of machines and millions of lives. You and your crew are only a pawn in the maelström, and the game tries to reflect this by narrowing your perception to your tank’s immediate surroundings. Give orders to your crewmen and coordinate them to express all the potential of your steel wildcat. Stay aware of enemy or friendly actions to take advantage of every situation. Greet your teeth as shells bounce on your armor and hundreds of bells resonate in your ears. Make the right shell find the way to proper retaliation, and never forget to double the shot.

Although this game is a heir to Patton's Best and Nigel Hodges' Guderian's Best variant, it offers a new perspective with a modular map made of variable terrain cards (depending on the scenario) that changes each time you play for the operational mode, variable units (per year-season), identification cards, 20 tank types, etc. The game has a battle mode, an operational mode, a campaign mode, and a grand campaign mode (the full war, linking more than 15 campaigns) with plenty of scenarios from the start of Barbarossa to the fall of Berlin.

—description from the publisher

KUSOGE TRICK is a Hearts-like trick-taking game where the rules for scoring change with each round.
At the end of the round, you receive chips for negative points. There are two types of the negative point chips: 1 point and 5 points. The player with fewest negative chips wins. (Note :it is not the total value of the negative points.)

This game was inspired by an episode of the old toilet situation in Hokkaido, Japan, told by a personality on the Japanese web radio program “GAYA Radio”.
publishers: Bodoge Radio Kenkyukai(BoardGame Radio Study Group)

description:
The aim of the game is to become the MARKET BOSS, playing on the table as many goods as possible, and preventing your opponents from placing theirs: send your Thieves to steal or corrupt Guards to make surprise inspections!
But there will be moments in the game in which you will have to ally yourself with your opponents... will you keep your promises?

—description from the designer

Piazza le tue bancarelle piene di spezie, vino, patate ed altri prodotti, attendo a dosare la giusta dose di bluff: usa i ladri al tuo soldo per rubare le carte altrui, e manda le guardie a fare ispezioni a sorpresa!

—description from the designer (Italian)

In Fenceology（KAKOMIST）, players take turns drawing a tile, then placing one of their four tiles on the table, matching colored dots on the tiles to make valid placements. Whenever you enclose a space, mark it with one of your tokens. Create seven enclosures before your opponent, and you win...but if the opponent can fill an area with a tile of their own, your token will be returned to you and await use once again.

boardgame: Super Banco Imobiliário Estrela: Safra Pay
description:
Super Banco Imobiliário is the most modern property buying and selling game in Brazil, as all transactions are now made by card!
The market is modernizing and you can't be left behind: with the card machine, real companies on the board and updated properties, you'll play as if you were doing business for real! This new game brings all the dynamism of the business world to your home. Gather your family and friends to play! This is your chance to become a great millionaire. Good luck with your business!

boardgame: あののみ: あの飲み会を誰も覚えていない (ANONOMI: The Party Night We All Forgot)
description:
Last night's party was a wild one. When participants woke up in the morning, the room was a complete chaos, featuring piles of pizza, a tuna wearing a futon, a wandering sloth, and even a Moai statue. Who could have caused such a scene?!

In あののみ: あの飲み会を誰も覚えていない (ANONOMI: The Party Night We All Forgot), each player is the "perpetrator" of one incident and the "instigator" of another (or the same) incident — yet no one, including themselves, remembers anything they did. Players drink shijimi (basket clam) soup to gradually recall each other's actions, while they try to "conceal" or "tamper" with the evidence left at the scene to escape responsibility.

See whether you can cleverly dodge the blame and slip away! The player with the lowest responsibility score at the end of the game wins.

     The responsibility score can be reduced by "destroying" or "tampering" with the evidence cards at the scene.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Based on Paolo Mori's Blitzkrieg!, Flintlock!: Global Warfare in 20 Minutes is set during the events of the first full-scale global conflict in the 18th Century. Play one of two different two-player games: the Seven Years War in the theaters of Europe, India, and the Atlantic; or the French and Indian War in North America — or combine both boards for the ultimate 2v2 team-based battle.

Make crucial alliances to bolster your troops, boost your strategic support for more tiles, take down forts for new bonuses, plus more twists and surprises for Blitzkrieg! players, or anyone who loves a head-to-head tactical skirmish.

—description from the publisher

10 Hits Music Trivia Card Game

375 Question Cards - each with 10 possible "Hit" Answers

7 Categories by Card Color

Blue = Name 10 albums by a specific Artist

Yellow = Name 10 Members of a specific Band

Red = Name 10 Songs that fit the category such as a having a specific word in the title

Purple = Name 10 Songs on a specific album

Green = Name 10 Artists or Bnands that fit the Category such as Bands with a certain word in their name

Pink = Name 10 Songs by a Specific Band or Artist

Orange = Potluck

There is no dice, board, timer or game pieces so the game can be played anywhere and any time!

—description from the designer

boardgame: In the Shadow of Neon Lights: the Expansion
description: This ten-card expansion adds four level 10 fighters and six action cards that either double your fighter's power or decreases the strength of opponents.

boardgame: Off the Line: Expansion #1 – The British
description:
From the frozen villages of Norway to the baking sun of Crete and the flooded fields of Normandy, this first expansion for Off the Line presents British and Commonwealth forces.

Players will find eighty individually named, rated, and armed soldiers (including the indomitable Gurkhas) ready to battle the enemy across six new double-sided maps and six new scenarios.

Also introduced is a new "bocage" terrain type that adds both protection and hinderance to soldiers trying to make their way through it and on to victory.

NOTE: This is not a complete game! Ownership of the Off the Line volume Fortress Europe that includes German and American forces
is required for play.

—description from the designer

boardgame: バンババン: 巨大隕石衝突、宇宙人vs幽霊船 (BANG-BA-BANG: Giant Meteorite Collision, Aliens vs. Ghost Ship)
description: In バンババン: 巨大隕石衝突、宇宙人vs幽霊船（BANG-BA-BANG: Giant Meteorite Collision, Aliens vs. Ghost Ship), one player controls the alien side of the battle, while the other takes charge of Earth's defenses.
publishers: らくがきアトリエ (Atelier for Scribblers)

description:
Pets Rescue is an interactive Euro-style card game about pet adoption. Every round, the lead player selects either a row or a column of cards from the central grid. Players take turns grabbing a card from that row or column, starting and ending with the lead player.

Every card can be oriented either vertically, for the scoring opportunities, or horizontally, for the pets, in the player individual tableau. At the end of the game, the points are scored to determine the winner.

—description from the publisher

You have lived (as frogs) in a stagnant puddle your whole life. In the distance is the safety of Fly Pond. But there are six lanes of dangerous freeway traffic between you and the good life.

Hophazard is a two player dice game - each player rolls 1-4 die (depending on how many frogs are in traffic at the moment) on their turn. Then, one at a time those die are used to either either move one frog towards Fly Pond, or set up for a 'hop inbetween' the opponents frogs (and then use one of the remaining die to push that opponents frog back towards the stagnant puddle). Doubles and triples can be used to push opponent's frogs back as well.

The first player to get three of their four frogs to Fly Pond wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Pike and Shotte: Wars of the Samurai
description:
The Sengoku Jidai saw Japan divided into numerous warring states from the mid 15th to the early 17th century.  Starting with the Onin War and ending with the battle of Sekigahara in 1600 the era saw the development of tactics and weapons by the samurai from a medieval system dominated by horse archers and heroic duels to greater use of infantry recruited from the peasant class (ashigaru) to sophisticated command and control techniques and mass use of firearms, It is a period romanticized in films like Ran and Kagemusha.

Wars of the Samurai is a supplement for Pike & Shotte with an extensive description of the history of the Sengoku Jidai,a glossary of terms, eleven historical scenarios, army lists for Daimyo, Shinobi, and Ikko Ikki forces, castle defense rules, and a theme of the Three Unifiers by D.G. Laderoute.  It has special rules for the Sengoku Jidai (including the Jinmaku command enclosure, Sojutsu, Hatamoto, the code of Bushido, and weapons like the Nagae Yari), stratagems (like flank marches and Woodpecker Forces) unit profiles for use with Pike and Shotte including options for fielding cavalry either as small mounted units which can dismount to fight and can have ashigaru follower subunits on foot, or standard size mounted units that focus on charging as depicted in films like Kagemusha, a solo play bot, and a pregame and terrain placement system by Tim Greene which first appeared in a publication called Armies of the Daimyos included free with every boxed Warlord Games Samurai starter army.

Wars of the Samurai allows the battles of the Sengoku Jidai, including the larger ones such as Nagashino or Sekigahara, (scenarios for both appear in the supplement) to be recreated with miniatures using the Pike and Shotte rules.  Or play out siege and castle defense games!

—description from the publisher

萬象魔寶 ("Arcana Wonders") is a competitive strategy game blending secret factions, magical item auctions, and economic management. Designed for 4–8 players, each participant assumes a unique identity and secretly aligns with a magical faction.

Players bid for powerful relics, manage limited resources, and deduce opponents’ alliances through interactive bluffing and deduction. Victory belongs to the faction that best coordinates through clever trades and strategic acquisitions.

With hidden roles, unpredictable bidding wars, and intense player interaction, Arcana Wonders is ideal for players who enjoy deduction games with deep strategic choices and high replay value.

—description from the designer

Let Musse and Helium take the kids on a fast-paced card game! With cards in hand, you must collect four cards of the same suit as quickly as possible. But beware – there are pigeon hunters lurking in the pile of cards who threaten to disrupt the game and put the leader in jeopardy.

—description from the publisher (translated)

A Battalion-Regiment simulation of the Napoleonic Battle of Chiclana-Barrosa in Southern Spain, March 11, 1811. It uses the Sombra del Águila (Shadow of the Eagle) system.

In early 1811, Marshall Soult left Andalusia to support Massena's invasion of Portugal. The Allied command took the opportunity to attack the French forces besieging Cádiz. The expeditionary force achieved a tactical victory, but failed to relieve the siege of the city, that would go on until August 1812.
family: Series: The Shadow of the Eagle

description:
Bounce the Baby Roos into Mama Roo's pouch in this ping-pongesque bouncing game. Help the Baby Roos hop home, but watch out! Silly Dingos will try and block your bounces! Safely bounce the most Baby Roos home in 30 seconds and win!

How To Play

     Take turns bouncing the baby roo ping pong balls into Mama's pouch until the 30-second timer runs out.
     Bounce the most baby roos into the pouch during your turn to win!
     For an added challenge, place the 3 dingo blocks in front of the pouch.


—description from the publisher

Juggernaut is PvP Deck Building Roguelike board game. You and rivals are zealot that invited to the Ritual for choose an offering to the God. To become offering, beat your rival completely. Player must gather your Servant cards and enhance them, or customize the card abilities, generate items for your deck be more stronger, collect Relics, for show your Faith. Time is come...sharpen your deck as your sword.

The aim of Juggernaut is to be the player with the most Faith (Victory Point) at the game’s end. Faith are primarily gained by deal damage to your opponent in the Ritual step, and also Purchase some Relics. Each round, you enhance your deck by customize your card or gain new one from action in Blessing. Then battle your rival for gain Faith in Ritual. Repeat these 2 steps for 6 round, then game is end.

—description from the designer

An animal has escaped the zoo and it's your job to figure out who! Travel the board and take peeks at the hidden animal card to gather clues.

Be the first to get to the end and correctly guess the missing animal and win!

How To Play

     Slide a card under the board without any players seeing it.
     Roll the die to move.
     Open a Peek-a-boo window to peek at part of the secret animal!


—description from the publisher

A unique board game in which one player is Mike, a boy who loves his dog, and the other player is Bruno, Mike's adorable dog.

It is a lovely day to visit the dog park. Mike and Bruno are about to enter when the little pup makes a dash for it! While Bruno enjoys running around the park, pooping away Mike needs to catch the rowdy puppy and clean up after him. So who will prevail?

Object of the game:
For Mike, the objective is to catch Bruno.
Bruno's goal is to poop and escape capture!

Winning the game:
Bruno has to have 4 Poop pieces on the board to win!
If Mike catches Bruno before this happens, Mike wins!

- description from publisher

Contents:
- 1 Game board
- 1 Mike figurine
- 1 Bruno figurine
- 49 Grass tiles
- 1 Mike spinner
- 1 Bruno spinner
- 4 Poop pieces

"Oh no! I totally messed up!"

In ナメプ謝罪 (Troll Apology), you'll be thrown into one disaster after another — the kind that makes you want to shout that — but since everyone at the table is a troll, that is, someone who lives to mess around with others, you will all match each outrageous blunder with the most sarcastic, ridiculous apology possible.

It's not about winning or losing; if you're having fun, you're doing it right.

Board the exciting journey across Europe with the Train Ride Europe escape room game! Made of high-quality plastic, this multicoloured game offers an immersive and medium-heavy experience for 2-4 players. With a playing time of 45 minutes and designed for adventurers aged 8 and above, this game is perfect for families, groups of friends or players looking for an engaging challenge.

Train Ride Europe takes you on an intriguing train journey across the continent, confronting challenging riddles and mysterious twists and turns. The medium difficulty level provides a perfect balance between suspense and accessibility, allowing both novice and experienced escape room enthusiasts to enjoy this game.

This family-friendly game is not only a great addition to game nights, but also an ideal gift for curious adventurers. Escape the everyday routine and experience a captivating journey across Europe as you work together to overcome the challenges of the Train Ride Europe escape room. Prepare for an unforgettable adventure full of excitement, strategy and fun!

Two teams, 400 challenges, 20 seconds, no time to fool around!

In Pas l'temps d'niaiser, two teams go head-to-head in a series of ridiculous challenges. Turn the audible hourglass and complete the task on the map before the hourglass runs out. Flip it over immediately and the other team is on the grill! Every second counts, because as soon as the hourglass stops, the game is lost!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Deux équipes, 400 défis, 20 secondes, pas l’temps d’niaiser!

Dans Pas l’temps d’niaiser, deux équipes s’affrontent pour un face à face de défis complètement ridicules. Tournez le sablier sonore et complétez la tâche sur la carte avant que le sablier ne s’arrête. Retournez-le immédiatement et c’est à l’autre équipe d’être sur le gril! Chaque seconde compte, car dès que le sablier se tait, la partie est perdue!

—description from the publisher

Travel with Tom Waes and his team to all corners of the world and discover the most fantastic places, near and far. Adventurous and educational, that's travelling with Tom. But it certainly isn't easy!
Relive Tom's adventures yourself with this exciting board game and take up the challenge. Become a real world traveller, answer the quiz questions, collect all crew members in time and win!

A letter game flip and write. Every round, chose one of the two letter cards and place it in your 5x5 grid, trying to make the most 5 letter words. You can use a few special actions during the game, erasing a letter, swapping two letters, adding a 6th letter in the margins, but you get a large bonus in the end if you didn't use any.

—description from the designer

Battles of the American Revolution presents 2 separate hex and counter war games from the American Revolution.  The battles in this first volume are Oriskany and Freeman's Farm.

In early 2024, Worthington introduced this innovative box and board size to accommodate our players who wanted the feel of a high-quality board game with the convenience of a game they could take anywhere. We call them travel games because of the small footprint and price, but they are big on gameplay anyplace games are enjoyed.

—description from the publisher

Battles of the War of 1812 presents 2 separate hex and counter war games from the American Revolution.  The battles in this first volume are Lundy's Lane and North Point.

In early 2024, Worthington introduced this innovative box and board size to accommodate our players who wanted the feel of a high-quality board game with the convenience of a game they could take anywhere. We call them travel games because of the small footprint and price, but they are big on gameplay anyplace games are enjoyed.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Battles of the Mexican American War Travel Game
description:
Game:

A hex-and-counter wargame with 2 scenarios about the US-Mexican war.  Both sides race to be the first to accumulate a scenario-specified number of VP, through a combination of occupying key locations and eliminating enemy units.  The active player can use a limited pool of leadership points to boost movement, boost attacks combat, or rally troops during their own turn.  Off turn, the non-active player can use a pool of artillery points to boost defensive combat or make long-ranged attacks.

Publisher Descriptions:

Battles of the Mexican American War presents 2 separate hex and counter war games from the American Revolution.  The battles in this first volume are Buena Vista and NMonterrey.

In early 2024, Worthington introduced this innovative box and board size to accommodate our players who wanted the feel of a high-quality board game with the convenience of a game they could take anywhere. We call them travel games because of the small footprint and price, but they are big on gameplay anyplace games are enjoyed.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Escape Game: Flucht aus dem All
description: A game with cards and many puzzles waiting to be solved so that you can escape the aliens- just like in a real Escape Room! A journey through the outer space; full of puzzles that help develop logical skills, mathematical thinking, observation skills and language. The cards hide useful clues to continue the adventure and get back to the earth. The game includes a set of puzzle cards, a game board and useful items to find the clues.

All Players agree on the Difficulty level, and each Player decides the size of the Village they will rescue (20 to 50 Villagers). For every Villager below 50 the Player begins with, they start 1 Level higher. EG. If a Player has a Village size of 35, they start the game on Level 15.
The game is played in Rounds. Ascending Players take their Turn by selecting a die from the remaining Dice Pool, choosing how many Levels they wish to ascend, and rolling. If the roll is unfavourable, they might be able to reroll before recording any Villagers lost or found. Next, they add the Levels ascended to their current Level, and choose whether or not to Pray.
Safe Players do not Ascend, but roll to see if the Gods are kind to them, before they choose whether or not to Pray.
After all Players have had a Turn, the Round ends with an Act of Gods roll, the Water Level rising dependant on the Difficulty chosen, and new Prayer Tokens distributed to all non-Drowned Players.
As soon as all Players have either reached Safety or been drowned, Players scores are calculated and the winner declared.

—description from the publisher

Talent Agency is a quick and light game blending strategy and luck.  Manage risk with the roll of a die.  Should you play the short or long game?

You own a Talent Agency and can sign talent to your team.  Develop your team with attributes so they can win more auditions.   Every job won earns you a cut.  Be the most successful talent agency to win.

On your turn, sign talent to claim them for your agency.  Play an attribute card (Singing Lessons, Acting Lessons, Nose Job ...) next to the talent to start building their resume.  Finally, send the talent to an audition defined by a job card.  If they lack attributes that the job requires, roll a die on the chance that they may win the job anyway.  But be careful, if they fail to get the job their confidence is shaken, and they lose all their attributes.  Do you try to build up their attributes to increase the odds of winning the job?  Or do you hope that sending them on as many auditions as possible even when the odds are low will ultimately be the winning strategy?

The game is for 2-4 players, but can easily be modified by allowing teams to play instead of individuals.

This is the thirty third game in the Pocket Battle Games series from LPS, Inc.

This game looks at one of the boldest "special attacks" of World War 2, a suicidal raid carried out by Japanese airborne commandos on May 25th, 1945 against the recently captured Yontan airbase at Okinawa.

Historical Wargame Eastern War 1853-1856 is an A4 format book-album, contains 52 pages.

Includes five main sections:
1. Troops
2. Rules
3. Terrain
4. Cards and Markers
5. Game board

Your goal in this game is to capture and hold enemy Victory points.
You can play for a specified number of time periods (in our case, Rounds) or until one of the players earns a certain number of victory points (awarded for occupying checkpoints).
The Round can be taken as 15-20 minutes of real time. Hexagon represents approximately 300m (from flat to flat) of real distance.
The sequense of the game within one Round is similar to chess party. The first player makes a turn with one of his "Regiments", then the second player makes a turn. And so on until all the Orders of the troops are carried out. At this moment the Round ends. In the next Round everything is repeated again.

The Troops
The game is a clash of two divisions.

A small division consists of 6 Regiments, 6 Adjutant officers, 7 order tokens, 6 Special order and 1 Headquarters card.
If players want to increase the division's size to 8 Regiments (full division) each, they can do so, but the number of Order still remains equal to 7.
The set of one of the four national armies consists of 12 regiments:
Each national army in the game set consists of 12 regiments:
2 cavalry Regiments (3 blocks each) 01-02;
12 Adjutant officers (1 block each);
2 elite infantry Regiments (6 blocks each) 03-04;
6 infantry Regiments (4 blocks each) 07-12;
2 artillery Batteries (4 blocks each) 05-06

From this staf, each player recruits his own Division.

This section contains sheets for all four armies, which must be cut up, folded, and glued into game blocks.

The Rules
The rules consist of six sections:
1. GAME BOARD AND BASIC CONCEPTS
2. UNITS PROPERTIES
3. TERRAIN AND TERRAIN EFFECTS
4. CARDS AND MARKERS
5. GAME PREPARING
6. GAME SEQUENCE

Any actions of the Regiments on the battlefield are carried out at the expense of Order markers. The file includes 2 kits of Orders. The second one in case the troops in battle will more numerous than ordinary.

The Cards
This block contains all other auxiliary elements of the game. Such as: cards of "fog of war" mode, Headquarters and Victory points markers

Game Board
The game board is a rectangle of 9 by 9 hexagons.

Scenarios
The scenarios are not included in this set. They are distributed as pdf files separately.
Now you can try the following scenarios:


     Danube Campaign (1853-1854):
         Battle of Oltenitza;
         Battle of Cetate;
         Storming of Fort Arab-Tabiya (Silistra).
    



     Caucasus:
         Battle of Bayandur;
         Battle of Choloki river.
    



     Crimean campaign:
         Battle of the Alma River. The advance of the French division Bosquet;
         Battle of the Alma River. The British advance in the center;
         Battle of Balaclava. 1st stage. «Thin Red Line»;
         Battle of Balaclava. 2nd stage. «Charge of the Light Brigade»;
         Battle of Inkerman

Work your way though location after location, discovering puzzles and riddles or solving a mystery.

The Reuben gang have been robbing stagecoaches and banks around the town of Rattler Gulch for months.  A train carrying a valuable lockbox is set to travel through the area and the boss has asked you to help protect the train from those outlaws.  It seems a pretty straight-forward mission at first.  The bandits don’t seem to be the brightest and the train company even hired undercover agents to help you… but there is something off about your time machine.  Every 20 minutes or so, the world freezes and reverses… restarting you right back at the beginning.  This “time loop” will continue to occur until you figure out what is causing the malfunction.  The events all unfold the same on the train… but how you react to them can change everything.  Can you find a way to stop the robbery and escape the time loop?  If not, you may be forced to re-live the events of the robbery over and over again forever!

—description from the publisher

Re-Quest is a cooperative dungeon crawler where all players act simultaneously, creating a fast-paced and dynamic experience. Each session combines tile-based exploration with strategic deck-building, ensuring fresh challenges every time you play. As bold adventurers, you are sent to complete missions left unfinished by the less fortunate. These “re-quests” are your shot to prove you've got more wit, strength, and sheer audacity than the last guy—literally.

—description from the designer

Pirate O-Zone takes the dice tossing team play of Classic O-Zone and adds a few twists to make a head-to-head battle for gold on from three islands.

First off, it includes all the components needed to play Classic O-Zone. It also has two 12-sided cannonball dice. Three island targets are printed on back of the regular O-Zone targets.

Most O-Zone editions are found on that game's BGG entry, but this one has enough special rules to warrant its own page.

Over the course of four rounds, teams take turns tossing their five 6-sided dice (ships) toward the islands. Then they fire their cannonball dice.

A team gets up to three tosses of the cannonball dice, as long as they don't foul or go off the table.

After tossing the cannonballs, some ships may be sunk depending on the position and value of the cannonballs. Then islands are scored depending on who has the most dice on or around them.

The team with the most doubloons at the end wins!

—description from the designer

They're a 10... but they won't stop flirting with your mom. How far will their rating drop? Be as judgey as you please with They're A 10, But..., the hot or not party game based on the viral TikTok trend. Here's how it works: Flick the spinner to determine your potential partner's initial rating. Depending on how high or low they start, they'll get paired with a corresponding Trait Card. High ratings get bad traits to keep them humble, while lower ratings get good traits to help give them a fighting chance. All players must reveal their new rating at the same time. You'll lose your mind when you see what some friends are willing to put up with — and where others draw the line.

Designed for ages 17+ due to mature language and references.

Ludosaurus is a card game with colorful dinosaurs. Play a card on the table and say the color of the dinosaur, but be careful not to say the last ludosaurus mentioned or one that is already on the table. And watch out for the sneaky puppies, as they will confuse everything!

Be the first to run out of cards and win the game!

Humanity has conquered the stars. You have managed to secure a crippling loan from the Interstellar Banking Corporation to buy your own trader ship. She has been fueled up and the hyperspace jump nodes are fully charged. It is time to fly into the Outer Reaches and make your fortune as a Corsair, and maybe, one day, pay off that loan.

Corsairs of the Outer Reach is a sandbox space trading board game. You explore a solar system, revealing resource rich planets. By trading resources you'll earn the funds to upgrade your spacecraft. You can hire crew to search space stations and scan nearby space to find hidden adventures, all while avoiding pirates and the tax department.
