The first player to hire a crew and pay off the mortgage on their ship wins!

—description from the designer

Fry's Selection Box Rocket Race was a children's space game that was printed on the lid of a chocolate box.

The game uses a spinner to move carboard rockets along a winding path, encountering detours and certain squares that will aid or hinder progress.

Players pass a space station, hostile ships and a Sputnik satellite. First to reach the moon is the winner.

boardgame: A Useless Fight:  The Battle of Trent's Reach, January 23-24, 1865
description:
A Useless Fight details the last major naval battle fought between ironclads during the Civil War. The Confederate vessels of the James River Squadron had been confined by their Union opponents to headwaters above Drewry’s Bluff the previous year. In January the squadron was under growing pressure to do something to help drive off the Yankees that were besieging the Confederate capital. In mid-January such an opportunity seemed to present itself when all but one of the Federal ironclads guarding the James River were withdrawn for their attack on Fort Fisher, and the winter’s rising waters had submerged much of their enemy’s defenses.

Can you do as well or better than your historical counterpart?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Lumin Wars is a strategic card game where anticipating your opponent's next move is key. Be the last ship standing in this intergalactic duel!

Each round, players simultaneously reveal a card from their hand and use it to attack other players, defend their ship, and manage basic resources.

Any player without health is eliminated from the game. If there is no winner, the next round begins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ХАЙП: Битва за популярність (HYPE: Battle for Popularity)
description:
HYPE. Battle for Popularity is themed around modern influencer culture. Players take on the roles of bloggers from different niches — like fashion, pets, or politics — each trying to build a loyal following and rise to social media fame. The game humorously captures the race for internet popularity, from posting viral content to dealing with hate, promoting posts, and even collaborating on podcasts.

Each turn, players post content (to gain likes) or use special actions from their hand of cards to grow their following, block rivals, or boost their own reach. Cards represent content with specific themes (e.g., travel, food, style) and may trigger bonuses depending on the player's avatar niche. Players gain likes, manage their social media feed of up to five posts, and interact with others using cards like "Complaints" (to block a rival’s post) or "What’s Wrong With…?" (to attack and gain likes at their expense). Strategic use of event cards like "Hype Up", "Fan Love", and "Blogger Festival" adds dynamic twists.

The goal is to be the first to accumulate 100,000 likes. A simplified version of the rules is also available for children, focused only on basic posting and collecting likes.

A fast-paced tactical arena game, where you’ll command two legendary warriors—known as the Honorbound—and battle for glory in the ancient "Crimson Colosseum".

Choose your champions from a roster of six, each with signature skills and playstyles. Combine their unique Skill Decks, manage your limited energy each round, and unleash devastating strikes to earn Victory Points and crush your rival.

Each match is a thrilling 20-30 minute duel of strategy, positioning, and timing. Use the battleground, inflict status effects like Cripple, Daze, or Stun, and exploit Burst and Travel skills to outplay your opponent.

When a player ends a round with more than 20 Victory Points, the game ends and the player with the most points is declared the Champion.

—description from the publisher

If two players participate, the 19 white and black pieces are placed on the squares enclosed by the wide green border. If four players participate, 13 pieces of each of the four colors are placed on the squares enclosed by the red line.

From now on, the game is played in the same way as the well-known game of Halma, except that no piece may touch the star field, but must go around it, which can, of course, be done from either side. This makes the game longer and more varied. The winner is, of course, the one who gets all of their pieces into the opponent's court first.

Start from different social backgrounds — each with unique advantages and resource allocations — then compete to climb the economic ladder.
Will you invest your capital for long-term gains or learn new skills to unlock opportunities?
Will you choose the safe route with modest rewards or brave the risky path with potential for greater returns — or devastating setbacks?
In the end, which players will prosper — and which will struggle?

description:
Visit the quacktion-packed pond of Ducks in a Row! In this game of deducktive reasoning, All of the ducks have gotten out of order! It is your job to play cards and learn about the locations of your fellow player's ducks. While swan diving, ducking for cover, and quacking jokes, make sure to keep your ducks in a row, or you might get caught off guard by the goose!

Ducks of Legend! The Conducktor has ordered these ducks in a row, and it is up to you to discover the correct order!

Play cards to find where these Ducks are, and if your guess is closest to the correct order, you win!

But be careful not to draw the Goose card! Or the round might be over quicker than you think!

—description from the designer

A game of Strategy and Chance, two-player traditional boardgame.  There are different levels which means different rules.  Players use cards, dice and a spinner to move their game pieces (called dots) from the starting position to the home position.  The special mechanisms come into play which allows a player to Bank Your 9's, which is important because in this game, the player with the least number of points is the winner.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 大怪獣首都攻撃 (Urban Kaiju Assault)
description:
Protect the capital from rampaging kaiju — or become the monster and unleash destruction upon the city!

大怪獣首都攻撃 (Urban Kaiju Assault) features handcrafted kaiju figurines, each meticulously crafted by the Professor. Every single piece is one-of-a-kind, making this a truly rare and special game experience.
publishers: 教授に訊いてみた (Ask the Professor)

description: Paki te Chocolat is like a chocolate bar mini-game with an addictive cracking action. To play, roll the dice; the winner is the one who gets the chocolate piece they were aiming for.
This fifth expansion adds three new tribes: tritons, artificers and monsters.

Be the hero Gotham City needs! In the Monopoly Batman Edition board game, play as a member of the bat-family and patrol the night, stopping the many notorious villains who have overrun Gotham City. This fun kids board game features Batman artwork, characters, and themes inspired by the Batman comics. In this edition, villains replace properties, and cardboard batarangs replace Monopoly money. Travel the board using batarangs to capture villains, collect their Calling Cards, and return them to Arkham Asylum. Answer the call of the Bat-Signal deck or turn to the Alfred Pennyworth deck for help. The player with the most batarangs when all the villains have been captured wins!

—description from the publisher

What is Mystic Space?

It is a brand new space fantasy game from Nordic Weasel Games.

Featuring great heroes, stonking combat walkers, magical spells and an array of high tech weapons, Mystic Space is set in a remote galaxy where the Star-Born civilisations are collapsing into galaxy wide conflict as each of 8 factions struggle to impose their will on the future.

It is NOT a grimdark world. Its a world of bright colours, cool heroes and exciting adventure, taking as much inspiration and vibes from video games and anime than it does from classic science fiction and comic books.

Mystic Space includes:

     Easy and pretty quick game rules with a unique take on unit movement (measure for the squad leader, then deploy the unit around them)
     Core dice mechanic is 1D10 + character attribute. A 10 or higher is a success. 
     Alternating activations with the ability for heroes to activate alongside a squad by taking command of it.
     Fortune points allowing each player 3 rerolls in a game. 
     Weapons include area weapons that cover a blast radius, bolt weapons that blast through units and chain weapons that arc between targets. 
     Spell casting system for some Heroes with 8 spells in the alpha version.
     Vehicle combat rules. All vehicles in Mystic Space are walkers. 
     8 factions to play, each of which is detailed with history and personality. 


All factions use the same army list to pick from but each has 3 special abilities that set them apart.


     Army list with 26 units to pick from. 
     7 scenarios, each of which calculates victory in different ways. Perfect for pick up play.
     Extensive background information on life in the Mystic Space universe. 


A typical army is 20-30 figures with a couple of vehicles. Anything from 15mm and up will work (but as long as you base individually, you can use smaller scales if you are determined). A typical gaming table should be about 3x3 feet across.

Most importantly (perhaps) the Mystic Space license!

The Fan License allows you to create and distribute fan art, fan fiction, game scenarios, army building apps etc.

Meanwhile the CommercialLicense allows you to create and sell miniatures for Mystic Space. This is particularly aimed at STL creators and lets you create and sell miniatures for the various units and factions in Mystic Space. AND you get both licenses when you buy the game.

Mystic Space is intended to be a living rules system: Not only will we make updates, tweak points values and clarify rules based on player feedback, but over time we are also intending to advance the time line, introducing events (which might then result in new spells, weapons or units being added to the game).

—description from the publisher

Baby Party is a hilarious party and dexterity game where players go head-to-head to complete challenges drawn from a deck filled with baby-themed mini games. The winner is the first player to land all three of their tiny, colorful baby figurines onto an ever-growing dance floor of cards at the same time.

The theme is a chaotic baby nightclub called Bottles & Beats, complete with a mini disco ball, a baby bottle, and a shiny bouncer who might bounce you right out the door.

Each challenge is paired with a well-known baby-related phrase, like “Hasta La Vista, Baby,” where a player flicks a baby off their thumb like a marble, or “Baby Bump,” where two players fist bump and drop one of their babies onto the table. Other challenges, like “Baby Shampoo,” might involve dropping your baby off your forehead, or “Baby Face,” where all players stare at one of their babies — and whoever blinks last gets to drop theirs alongside yours.

After each mini game, the completed challenge card is flipped and added to the table, expanding the dance floor. As the nightclub fills with more cards and more babies, the game crescendos in chaos and fun, building toward a cheer-out-loud finale.

—description from the designer
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
There's a lot to do in this galaxy, might as well get paid. Get the right contract at the right time to maximize your rewards.

—description from the designer

You are one of the admirals of the major belligerents of the Second World War. You must command your navy on the high seas to complete strategic objectives to secure ultimate victory. You will be gathering intelligence, establishing fleets and battle groups, and relying on the courage and valor of your fighting men to win the day. Manage your economy, adjust your tactics, and with a little luck victory may be yours.
This is a game where you will be building a deck of cards. The deck represents a series of ships and aircraft that are available for your use this round. Initially the forces will be evenly matched, but once the initial objective board is selected each side may receive certain advantages be it economic or offensive.
Each admiral will have to choose how best to achieve the objectives marked for their side, be it through heavy fleet assets or swarms of aircraft or a combination of both. The player that reaches the end of each line of objective markers for the second play mat achieves victory.

—description from the designer

Arcadia's Map is a deduction and memory game for 2 to 4 players, where your goal is to uncover and assemble the secret map of Arcadia Island.

Each player has a card with a secret condition that the map must fulfill. Your objective is to ask the right questions to deduce which cards your opponents have, and be the first to complete the secret map, one that satisfies all the conditions of every player.

—description from the publisher

Chicatana Pocket is a fast-reaction game for all ages, where the goal is to be the first to spot the correct combination of colors and shapes.

Help Nocú collect leaves for her colony while you learn, in a fun way, about primary colors and how they combine to create secondary colors.

In addition, on the back of the cards, you’ll find puzzle pieces showing the mushroom that chicatanas feed on. Can you complete it?

—description from the publisher

Welcome to NEON! A vibrant and exciting city full of opportunities, where communication happens through gestures and sounds. Dive into the action and experience NEON in your own unique way!

A party game of gestures and sounds! Describe two secret images using only one noise and one gesture. Others race to guess and grab the right tiles. No words, just pure creativity and chaos — in two fun modes!

Entertaining fun: combine sounds and gestures in a hilarious way
Simple rules and set up to start playing immediately
Original packaging in a handy carry-along edition
Let’s Talk MORE NEON: The game comes in a variety of color editions, each with different sets of motifs for even more replayability and laughs.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to NEON! A vibrant and exciting city full of opportunities, where communication happens through gestures and sounds. Dive into the action and experience NEON in your own unique way!

A party game of gestures and sounds! Describe two secret images using only one noise and one gesture. Others race to guess and grab the right tiles. No words, just pure creativity and chaos — in two fun modes!

Entertaining fun: combine sounds and gestures in a hilarious way

Original packaging in a handy carry-along edition
Let’s Talk MORE NEON: The game comes in a variety of color editions, each with different sets of motifs for even more replayability and laughs.

On the Far Spindle, in the Starward Belt, Spindlejacks are notorious. Slicing across the station with their custom airbikes, they courier messages, packages, and anything else too sensitive to be fed into the station's crumbling and haunted networks. Sharing the cavernous transit tunnels with hulking cargo haulers, they are also widely assumed to have some kind of deathwish. But, as one of their number, you know it's the rush, the competition, and the community that keeps you riding.

Citizen Sleeper: Spindlejack is a solo tabletop roleplaying game (set in the universe of Citizen Sleeper and Citizen Sleeper 2), where you use your airbike to courier deliveries across a ramshackle space-station for cryo, reputation and fun. Taking inspiration from the character Kadet from Citizen Sleeper 2, you'll get to live life as a Spindlejack in the Starward Belt.

This is a print and play game, where you'll navigate randomly rolled intersections, run deliveries, upgrade your bike and become a legend on the station of the Far Spindle. it was released on May 5th (Citizen Sleeper day!) 2025.

To play you'll need to print a document, 10 six-sided dice (3 sets of 3 and 1 spare), a pencil and eraser, and/or some counters.

—description from the publisher

Epic Bluffing & Strategy Game Night – Immerse yourself in "Swindle: The Ultimate Bluffing Game," a high-stakes battle of strategy, bluffing, and quick decision-making. Perfect for competitive poker-style game nights filled with intensity and twists.
Collect Mythical Beasts & Superpowers – Outwit your opponents to claim the most legendary beasts, unlock powerful abilities, and amass gold coins. But beware—sly Goblins lurk around every corner!
Auction, Bargain & Bluff Your Way to Victory – With every round, players must decide whether to bid, bluff, or betray—turning every move into a high-pressure mind game. Do you have the nerves of steel to come out on top?

—description from the publisher

Nabstones is a 2-4 player competitive gem collection card game. It plays like a game of Uno, crossed with a match 3 like Candy Crush. This game is played on a pentagram type spread with 5 piles in play.

The person with the most points wins. In this game, you score points by collecting sets of different gems. A set of gems is three or more piles with the top card having the same color gem or same number of gems. Each gem type is worth a different amount of points.

—description from the designer

Use simple math in unexpected ways to slay monsters, grow stronger, and save the day.

The Scarlet Mage has twisted the once-beautiful forest into a place few dare to enter. Mighty heroes have ventured in—some returning in haste, others never seen again.

Today, you step into the woods. Armed with a handful of basic moves and the belief that every obstacle has a solution, you'll face monsters that test your wits and adaptability. Each battle sharpens your skills, making you smarter, stronger, and more prepared.

But will it be enough to overcome the final challenge—the Scarlet Mage himself?

—description from the designer

The game was designed for the 2025 9-Card Nanogame P&P Design Contest, and it achieved the following awards:

1st place - Best Overall Game
1st place - Best Solitaire Game
1st place - Most Innovative Mechanic
2nd place - Most Thematic Game

description:
TINY GAMES TO PLAY WITH FRENS is a Tabletop games zine series, featuring two games per volume.

A TASTY MORSEL: You're a tasty morsel and the big bad is hungry! Keep your characters away from the monster. Move the monster closer towards other players. Be the last morsel standing!

This game is played on a grid, with player pawns and a die. Players roll the die to move their pawn or the monster depending on the number rolled. If the monster enters the same space as a player, that player is eliminated. The player left standing at the end is the winner.

boardgame: Tiny Games to play with frens Vol 1: Department Store Dash
description:
TINY GAMES TO PLAY WITH FRENS is a series of tabletop games zines. Each volume features to games to play with your frens.

DEPARTMENT STORE DASH: You're a frantic  parent looking for a present for your child on Christmas Eve. You have a description and one minute before the store closes! Be the first parent to find a toy matching the description!

Players flip through a deck of toy cards to find the image that matches the description of the toy their child would like for Christmas. If the toy is not in their deck of cards they put it into the centre and swap for a new deck to search through. The first person to find the toy that matches the description wins the game. No one wins if the timer runs out before anyone locates their toy.

—description from the designer

Space Knights is a first world book in the series for the Hot Lead skirmish miniature game.

The rules system is a D20 skirmish system which could be linked to the GURPS role playing game.

A Science Fantasy background about the Space Knights and their fight against the rival races which includes the Psions and Necrovores ("eaters of the dead") which also fights each other.  The Psions are low tech, big, fast, smart, yet animalistic and live for battle, and have incredibly psi abilities.  The Necrovores and bionic undead, living skeletons with metal claws, cybernetic eye, Crystal brains and cables and wires where they once had flesh.  When the Necrovores capture a human, they flay him alive and turn him into another Necrovore with a hatred of his previous race.  Many of the Necrovores are powerful wizards

The game was support by range of miniatures by Ral Partha Enterprises

(Description based from Steve Jacksons' interview in "The Lost Province Magazine".)

In this print-and-play roll-and-write game, you are a young spellcaster transported to a magical realm to prove your worth. Guided by a wisecracking wizard dog, you'll explore a mystical map by flipping poker cards to determine your movement, gain resources, and perform magical actions.

Each round, players flip three cards and choose one, using its suit for a bonus (like energy or potions), and its value for upgrading actions and determining movement. You'll travel across a magical training ground, collecting gems, potions, trees, and defeating enemies — all while managing limited movement points and building a strategy to maximize your score.

Strategic choices, clever upgrades, and magical encounters shape your journey to wizardly greatness. But be warned: each decision matters, and the path to becoming a Master Wizard is anything but easy.

Space Fleet Commander is a solitaire game of fleet management, exploration, and combat.

Fill in all 70 Score Stars before the game ends, earning the title of Emperor!

The game ends in one of two ways:

     All twelve Turn Boxes are filled.
     It’s impossible to complete any adjacent Hex Missions.


You will spend each turn rolling dice to try to accomplish adjacent Hex Missions, pushing your luck to explore as many hexes as possible in one turn. If you complete one you gain the benefits and deal with the consequences of the hex. For instance, if you rolled a 2,3,4,5,6 you completed a Straight! Mark out the stars on that hex and move your cube into it showing that it’s explored. Once you’ve explored a hex you may not re-enter or pass through it. Your game ends of you back yourself into a corner and leave yourself with no new adjacent hexes to enter.

Community is an expansion to the game Tenby, with new ways to build and score your town. Mayors, visitors, locals, projects and notice boards all offer unique ways to interact, greatly expanding the core gameplay.

This expansion builds on the core game, and continues to support solo play throughout.

Monopoly Mont-Saint-Michel is a board game celebrating one of France's most iconic sites. Players can buy, sell, and trade famous and historical landmarks located on this architectural marvel surrounded by the sea.
     Following traditional Monopoly rules, this game includes elements specific to the world of Mont-Saint-Michel. Players collect cards representing legends, historical events, and anecdotes related to this legendary site, adding an enriching cultural touch.
     This board game for teens and adults is ideal for those interested in French history, architecture, and traditions. Perfect for evenings with friends or family, it guarantees a fun and educational immersion into the fascinating world of Mont-Saint-Michel.
     Thanks to this themed Monopoly, game nights also become educational. While having fun, participants learn financial management, develop their strategic thinking, and improve their communication skills, all while discovering the unique heritage of Mont-Saint-Michel.
     This board game for adults and teens makes a great gift for anyone who loves this French gem. It cleverly combines fun and learning, providing hours of entertainment while exploring the rich culture and history of Mont-Saint-Michel.




     Le Monopoly Mont-Saint-Michel est un jeu de plateau qui célèbre l'un des sites les plus emblématiques de France. Les joueurs peuvent acheter, vendre et échanger des lieux célèbres et historiques situés sur cette merveille architecturale entourée par la mer.
     Suivant les règles traditionnelles du Monopoly, ce jeu inclut des éléments spécifiques à l’univers du Mont-Saint-Michel. Les joueurs collectent des cartes représentant des légendes, des événements historiques et des anecdotes liées à ce site mythique, apportant ainsi une touche culturelle enrichissante.
     Ce jeu de société ado et adulte est idéal pour ceux qui s'intéressent à l'histoire, à l'architecture et aux traditions françaises. Parfait pour les soirées entre amis ou en famille, il garantit une immersion ludique et éducative dans l'univers fascinant du Mont-Saint-Michel.
     Grâce à ce Monopoly thématique, les soirées jeux deviennent également éducatives. Tout en s’amusant, les participants apprennent la gestion financière, développent leur sens de la stratégie et améliorent leurs capacités de communication, tout en découvrant le patrimoine unique du Mont-Saint-Michel.
     Ce jeu de société pour adultes et adolescents constitue un excellent cadeau pour tous les amoureux de ce joyau français. Il combine astucieusement plaisir et apprentissage, permettant de nombreuses heures de divertissement tout en explorant la richesse culturelle et historique du Mont-Saint-Michel.

Monopoly in French with a Ligue 1 theme is a captivating board game that combines the love of football with the fun of classic board games. This adult board game immerses you in the world of French football, buying stadiums and negotiating contracts with other players. It's a board game that follows the classic rules of Monopoly, while adding a sporty and competitive touch.
     The game follows the classic principles of Monopoly: buying properties, developing infrastructure, and avoiding bankruptcy. However, the addition of football-related elements makes this adult board game even more engaging. Players must also deal with unforeseen situations, such as injuries or suspensions, which reinforces the strategic aspect of this logic game.
     This board game is ideal for everyone, from children to teenagers and adults. As a teen board game, it offers an immersive experience that appeals to young football fans, while adults will appreciate the strategic depth and financial challenges. It's also a perfect family game for family gatherings, as it's easy to learn and play, while providing hours of entertainment.
     Monopoly in French Ligue 1 can be used as an adult board game at parties or casual gatherings, but also as an educational tool to teach financial management, strategy, and the art of negotiation. By playing this board game, players develop financial and logic skills while having fun. It's a logic game that stimulates critical thinking and encourages conversations around the table, making each game unique.
     This board game is the perfect gift for any football fan, especially those who follow Ligue 1 with passion. Not only is it a fun adult board game, but it's also a tribute to the iconic clubs and players of the French league. For special occasions or simply as a treat, giving this logic game is a great way to share your passion for football with your loved ones.




     Le Monopoly en français avec une thématique Ligue 1 est un jeux de plateau captivant qui combine l'amour du football et le plaisir des jeux de société classiques. Ce jeu de société adulte vous plonge dans l'univers du football français, acheter des stades et négocier des contrats avec les autres joueurs. C’est un focus jeu de société qui reprend les règles classiques du Monopoly, tout en ajoutant une touche sportive et compétitive.
     Le jeu suit les principes classiques du Monopoly: acheter des propriétés, développer des infrastructures, et éviter la faillite. Cependant, l'ajout des éléments liés au football rend ce jeu de société adulte encore plus engageant. Les joueurs doivent également faire face à des situations imprévues, comme des blessures ou des suspensions, ce qui renforce l'aspect stratégique de ce jeu de logique.
     Ce jeu de plateau est idéal pour tous les publics, des enfants aux adolescents, en passant par les adultes. En tant que jeu de société ado, il offre une expérience immersive qui plaît aux jeunes fans de football, tandis que les adultes apprécieront la profondeur stratégique et les défis financiers. C'est également un jeux d'ambiance famille parfait pour les réunions familiales, car il est facile à apprendre et à jouer, tout en offrant des heures de divertissement.
     Le Monopoly en français Ligue 1 peut être utilisé comme un jeu de société adulte lors de soirées ou de rassemblements décontractés, mais aussi comme un outil éducatif pour enseigner la gestion financière, la stratégie et l'art de la négociation. En jouant à ce focus jeu de société, les joueurs développent des compétences en finance et en logique tout en s'amusant. C’est un jeu de logique qui stimule la réflexion critique et encourage les conversations autour de la table, rendant chaque partie unique.
     Ce jeu de plateau est le cadeau idéal pour tout fan de football, en particulier ceux qui suivent la Ligue 1 avec passion. Non seulement c'est un jeu de société adulte amusant, mais c'est aussi un hommage aux clubs et aux joueurs emblématiques du championnat français. Pour les occasions spéciales ou simplement pour faire plaisir, offrir ce jeux de logique est une excellente manière de partager votre passion pour le football avec vos proches.

Allenby's Gamble (# 117-1), covers the start of the Southern Palestinian Offensive of November 1917.

The EEF, having failed taking Gaza twice, replaced their Commander whom brought a new approach to the matter. Allenby's strategical objective was the defeat of the Ottoman army in Southern Palestine, decisive enough to ensure Ottoman reinforcements being diverted to Palestine. The offensive started with a joint attack on Beersheba at the eastern end of the Gaza - Beersheba line.

The following day, the Battle of Tel el Khuweilfe began, with an advance into the Judean foothills. This move up the road from Beersheba towards Jerusalem not only threatened Hebron and Bethlehem but also forced the Ottoman commanders to deploy their reserves, to hold the EEF threat. The Third Battle of Gaza then took place on the Mediterranean coast, starting with the largest barrage during WW1, outside the European theatre. The Battles of Hareira and Sheria was then launched on the centre to bind as many enemy troops as possible.

Allenby’s Gamble simulates the first 10 days of this Campaign.

Rules and mechanics
Rules are developed from the Tannenberg 1914 game released in 2010 now forming the Standard Rule System Collision of Empires with chit-pull mechanics.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Collision of Empires (Three Crowns Games)

description:
Stalin's Lost Chance: The Soviet Attack on Finland, through Karelia-Ladoga, 1939-1940 depicts the Winter War from 30th November 1939 to 13th March 1940. This is the 9th game in Three Crowns Games WWII Battle Series.

The Finnish defence plan was made on the belief that the Soviet army would target its primary offensive on the Karelian isthmus. An offensive was also expected on the north-western and northern side of Lake Ladoga, where the road network was relatively good. This is the area upon which Stalin’s Lost Chance focuses. Two divisions of the Finnish IV Army Corps were located there. North of Ilomantsi, the entire long eastern frontier was manned only with detached battalions and border companies, when the war broke.

The Soviet Union’s 8th Army, comprising six divisions and a tank brigade, had grouped a division or two on every road up north leading from east to west. Its mission was to execute a flanking manoeuvre around the northern shore of Lake Ladoga to strike at the rear of the Mannerheim Line.

—description from the publisher

The map scale is about 3.7 miles per hex, or about 5.5 kilometres per hex.  Turns represent one week.
Units are Companies and Battalions for the Finns; Battalions and Regiments for the Soviets. Plus the odd armoured train and more ad-hoc Groups.

—Source: Paul Shackleton, 29 May 2025 [on a BGG thread for Stalin's Lost Chance].
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: WWII Battle Series (Three Crowns Game Productions)

boardgame: Desert Duel: A Most Bitter Blow / Fighting Hard
description:
Desert Duel - 2 games set in WWII North Africa.

A Most Bitter Blow - Operation Battleaxe, June 1941
-The famous Tiger Convoy of May 1941 brought over two hundred tanks and fifty Hurricane fighters to Wavell’s command in North Africa – and an increased expectation from Churchill of an imminent offensive. General Wavell had reservations about the operation but reluctantly gave the green light.
After early successes, three days of heavy fighting saw the Allies retreat to their starting points, having lost about 100 tanks. Upon hearing the outcome of the battle, Churchill concluded it was “a most bitter blow”.

Fighting Hard - Battle of Matruh, June 1942
-Following defeat at Gazala, Eighth Army retreated into Egypt in June 1942 with little idea how and where it might stop Rommel. Border defences were quickly rejected as being too weak and so General Ritchie, the Eighth Army commander, sent orders to man the incomplete defences at Matruh.
From the outset, the plans to hold it were confused and then Auchinleck dismissed Ritchie taking command himself. Three days of chaotic fighting occurred in which the Allies grabbed defeat from a possible victory.

-Rules and mechanics
Rules are developed from the Stargard Solstice game, released in 2021, making it the 10th game in the WWII Battle Series, all with chit-pull mechanics.
Game length for A Most Bitter Blow is 6 turns, each turn represents 12 hours.
Game length for Fighting Hard is 9 turns, each turn representing 8 hours.
Counters represent companies, columns, battalions or regiments.

—description from the publisher

The map scale for A Most Bitter Blow is approximately 1.4 miles per hex, or 2.3 kilometres.
The map scale for Fighting Hard is approximately 2 miles per hex, or 3.2 kilometres. [Source: Paul Shackleton, 29 May 2025]
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: WWII Battle Series (Three Crowns Game Productions)

description:
Boletus is a set-collecting game in which you must collect mushrooms with your knife and basket. If you collect them without a license and run into a forest ranger, you'll lose them and receive a hefty fine. Also, be careful not to break your wallet, as this will prevent you from losing your license. Avoid eating the poisonous mushrooms, and you'll survive to enjoy the victory.
Each player has a three-by-three face-down card array that simulates the field. On your turn, you turn over two cards from the field or one from the center pile, which you can place in another opponent's playing area. The cards you turn over can be edible or poisonous mushrooms, or even actions.
It's a filler game with a lot of negative interaction between players, which leads to great moments and constant demands for revenge. You can poison another mushroom picker, makeing them lose their knife, or even steal their license.

—description from the designer

Read the topic on the card you drew and start thinking. Until all the players say “OHOO!”

Then read the card to everyone and start the countdown. The player who makes the longest list under the topic within 30 seconds wins the hand and advances on the game board. The player who advances the fastest and reaches the twelfth stage first wins the game.

Karate Grannies brings the mojo to the dojo in a hilariously high-stakes clash of knitting and martial arts! Outsmart or outpower your opponents to collect Granny’s favorite items, but beware - these grannies mean business. Do you have the skills, strategy, and sass to outwit the competition and claim the ultimate Granny glory?

—description from the designer
肉かるた (Meat KARUTA) is an unprecedentedly strange and fresh meat-based card game.

Font Karuta Black Letter is a real-time competitive game themed on font types. Spread out the cards with the same set of letters in different fonts on the table. After a font name is announced with a clue, players race to find and take the matching card. At the end of the game, whoever has the most cards wins.

Mechanics:
In addition to the players, a "Reader" is required to read out the question card.
The players compete in real time without turns. 
The players race to find and take the card that matches the announced font.
The answer is not stated clearly on the front of each card. In order to win, you need to guess the answer from clues as well as to memorize the information that is gradually revealed.

Karuta is a traditional Japanese card game in which players listen to short descriptions written on a reader's card. Each turn, the players compete with one another at being the first to pick up the player’s card with the corresponding picture or writing, and whoever wins the most cards wins the game.

Font Karuta Black Letter is a version of this traditional card game for people who are obsessed with recognizing the differences between typefaces. The reader reads the font name and description written on the reader's card, and then the players pick up the player's card bearing that font. The player who picks up the most cards wins.

—description from the publisher

Players are dealt six cards from the Gift deck, with a card from the Princess deck placed face up to determine trump. Over five rounds players take turns placing a card on the table, with highest of the lead suit or trump suit winning, and the person who wins the most cards gets the princess card. The sixth card of the hand is used for an optional tie-breaking round. Once seven princess cards have been won the person who has the most wins the game.

Kalypso is a tile-laying survival game where players build floating settlements in a world swallowed by the sea. With Earth’s surface lost beneath rising waters, survivors must salvage what they can to endure storms, overcome scarcity, and adapt to a shifting oceanic landscape.

Each round, players draft tiles and expand their settlement, managing resources, placing structures, and guiding survivors across platforms of rusted metal and driftwood. Strategic placement unlocks new opportunities, while weather events and shared objectives push players to cooperate, or compete, for precious supplies.

Play solo, cooperatively, or competitively. With modular objectives, and escalating difficulty, Kalypso offers a new challenge each time you set sail.

—description from the publisher

In Reminisce Route / 追憶の旅, players go on multiple trips, then attempt to recall something that they experienced along the way.

In more detail, each player takes a hand of cards showing activities or locations from four travel locations. At the start of a round, the lead player chooses the travel destination, plays a matching card face up, then gives a key word or phrase for this image; each other player does so in turn for the same destination, then these cards are flipped face down.

After 6-10 rounds, in reverse player order, each player names a key word or phrase from the final round that was not their card, claiming it if they were correct. (The final player can name their own card if it's the only one left.) Continuing play counter-clockwise, players try to recall cards from the various day trips, and whoever collects the most cards wins.

In Jyugosai, a.k.a. 十五賽 (15 Dice), players take turns placing a die, then placing it on the intersection of lines on a Go-style board. When a player rolls a 6, they can either place it on the board as a 3 or roll it again, placing it on the board only if the result is a 6 and removing it from play otherwise.

As soon as a player has a line of dice that sum to 15 or show three 1s, they win the round. The first player to win two consecutive rounds wins the game.

boardgame: まちまちビルダー (Machimachi Builder)
description: In each game of まちまちビルダー (Machimachi Builder), each player builds their own town, collecting fifteen town cards — wheat, school, financial district, etc. — through drafting and trading, using these cards and a "town symbol" card — a zoo, an amusement park, a football stadium, etc. — to build a 4x4 grid for their town, after which they score.
publishers: まちまちビルダー (Machimachi Builder)

description:
In this game, each round begins with a player designated as the 'Doctor Hue' who draws a word card from the deck. The challenge? Choosing a color card that best captures the essence of that word. The other players then attempt to tap into the 'Doctor Hue's' thoughts and select the same color.  Earn points by matching the 'Doctor Hue's' color choice - the first player to reach 10 points emerges victorious!

Dive into a world where words and colors collide, revealing the unique shades of our imaginations.

The snatch and spell card game

Fun game of letter-taking and word-making.  On your turn, you must steal a card from an opponent to make your own words.  Grab cards from your opponents, build words, and collect the most points to win.

Use higher-value letter cards to form high-scoring words to maximize your points.  You may have a great word in your hand, but your letters are up for Grabs too!

Unleash cunning combos and outwit your rivals in Riftblade, a dynamic deckbuilding game. Forge powerful synergies by acquiring spells from The Rift – a shared, ever-changing marketplace of magic. Fueling these devastating plays are the strategic Riftstone dice; more than just randomizers, these custom dice offer primary powers to activate spells and crucial secondary abilities (like upgrading dice, drawing cards, or refreshing The Rift) for vital tactical flexibility, allowing you to sculpt your hand, control the flow of magic, and gain the upper hand.

The strategic heart of Riftblade lies in managing The Rift itself. Will you purchase powerful spells to build your engine for future turns, or Trigger their effects directly from The Rift for immediate, disruptive impact—perhaps denying a crucial card to an opponent or clearing the way for a better selection? Starter cards grant access to the versatile Void Aspect, essential for weaving diverse spell combinations from different magical Aspects (Infernal, Celestial, Soul, and Chaos), forcing a careful balance between a lean, consistent deck and broader strategic flexibility.

Each turn has 2 phases: Attack and Cleanup.  If your Life reaches 0, you're out of the game. Play until only one player has Life left — that player is the winner!

—description from the designer

boardgame: ロボファクトリー (Robo Factory)
description:
In a quirky robot assembly line, players are factory workers trying to collect valuable robot parts while avoiding faulty components. Sometimes a shiny new unit rolls down the conveyor belt and sometimes it’s a skull-marked hazard. Bluffing, deduction, and a pinch of greed drive your choices... will you grab the card or let it pass?

Robo Factory is a bluffing and set collection game with auction-like resolution. On their turn, a player hides one of two cards and places a face-down chip worth -1, 1, or 2 points on it. The other players decide in turn whether to take the mystery card or pass, risking a skull that could eliminate them from the round. Some cards increase in value when collected in sets, while others can immediately knock players out. Strategic chip placement and calculated risk create a tense loop of reading your opponents and knowing when to fold or grab.

In Payback, cunning is your currency and survival is your only strategy. Each player builds their own stage of play, using card combinations not only to rid their hand but to strike at opponents to the left and right. As players rush to go out first or outlast the chaos, every move could provoke retaliation, or open the door to fortune.

Payback is a climbing and shedding game where each player plays combinations to their own personal board instead of a shared central pile. There are only two types of valid combinations, simplifying choices while sharpening strategic tension. Every play can affect adjacent opponents via "paybacks," which force them into tough decisions or directly attack their hand. The game rewards those who either go out first or survive longest without being eliminated, striking a balance between aggression and endurance.
publishers: シュピールディスオーダー (Spieldisorder)

description:
Pose Mania! is a cooperative game where each round will have a hidden theme and one player who takes on the role of the performer. The goal of each round is for the performer to be able to correctly guess the theme of the round within the time limit based on the reactions the other players give to their poses and gestures.

The performer can move their body in any way to make gestures and poses, while other players should react to show if the performer is on the right track to figuring out the theme or not. However, the players giving the hints are not allowed to give verbal clues. They can only show their excitement or disappointment through whoops, hollers and groans, etc., to let the performer know how close or far they are from the correct answer.

If time runs out and the performer is unable to guess the correct answer, the entire team loses a single life point. Survive 5 rounds with at least 1 life point remaining to win the game!

Keystone Connection is a fast network-/portfolio-building train game with the objective of having the most valuable stock portfolio at the end of the game. Players must start new railroads, they may expand existing railroads, they are able to trade stocks in the railroads, and they may acquire coal by exploiting Pennsylvania’s anthracite and bituminous mining reserves.

There are 5 colors of wooden locomotive tokens, each color representing a railroad, as well as 1 set of black tokens, which represents Coal and is placed on specific locations at setup. Players are dealt random hands of 5-10 (depending on number of players) locomotives, which are essentially a stock portfolio; the rest of each color are placed in a pool. On your turn, you can start a railroad, increase the value of a single railroad by placing 1-5 locomotives from the remaining pool of that company, trade one locomotive in your portfolio for one or two locomotives from the remaining pool in a railroad, or you can increase the value of a railroad by removing Coal from a railroad’s connected coal hex and placing it in an industrial City or into the railroad’s reserve.

The game board is a map of Pennsylvania, with cities worth anywhere from 1-4 points without Coal, and certain cities will increase in value upon receipt/railroad acquisition of Coal. Connecting to a city adds to a company's value. The game holds 3-6 players for about 30 minutes.

The game ends when all but one Railroad Box is devoid of cubes, only a specified number of total cubes remain in Railroad Boxes, or when a certain number of Coal cubes have been acquired into railroad Coal boxes. Players' scores are determined by the value of each railroad at the end of the game, multiplied by the number of stocks the player has for that railroad.

—description from the designer

Players strategically play their cards one at a time on the center pile, adding or subtracting to the top total as they go. The total value must not exceed 100; otherwise, you BUST and lose a token.

The cards are then reshuffled, and the game begins anew. The last player with tokens left is the winner. Strategic use of specialty cards such as “Jump to X Number” or “Switch Directions” keeps each round challenging.

With 5 levels of play, this game is for everyone! After one of the 220 Dolch sight words is called out, be the quickest to to swat the word on the matching fly with your swatter. Collect the most flies and win! The sight-word “flies” are double-sided and color-coded by level (from pre-primer to third grade).  For 1-4 players.  Includes 4 swatters, 110 double-sided flies, instructions.

Naval Mania is a set of rules for naval warfare in the Pre-Dreadnought age.

The rules are neither fast play nor deeply detailed, but rather something in between. For example, Naval Mania is content to know that a shell knocked out the pumps but not that it also destroyed the coffee cup in the engineering officer’s cabin. And while the cup may not become a casualty, it’s owner, the engineering officer, just may.

However, the jettisoning of minute detail does not mean a simplistic game. The mechanics of the game bring about a realistic result based on the actual abilities of each ship in play, not ship ratings based on generalized vessel types.

Included in these 180+ pages is an extensive ship ratings list, featuring nearly every ship from 1880-1905, plus some before and after, from over 30 navies. That totals 2,367 ships, from Argentina to Zanzibar. If you can find a model of it, then you can find it in Naval Mania.

Also included are a generous section of optional rules to mix and match, a QRS with useful information to speed up gameplay and a campaign system complete with a sample what-if campaign.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Holiday Hills: Santa and Krampus Double Expansion
description:
Get ready for the ultimate holiday showdown in this double expansion for Holiday Hills! Will you spread festive joy with Santa's generous gifts, or embrace a bit of mischief under Krampus' watchful eye? With new ways to earn Cheer Points - or cause a little chaos - this expansion adds twice the holiday magic (and mayhem) to your Holiday Hills adventure!

In this expansion, 1-5 players will choose to play on the Nice or Naughty side of the game board, then select to play Santa's Secret Deliveries or Krampus' Holiday Havoc.

In Santa's Secret Deliveries, Ol Saint Nick is dashing from house to house spreading holiday cheer, but can you keep up? Use cookies and milk to catch his attention, load up his sleigh board with dice presents, and score big for helping him complete his midnight mission!

In Krampus' Holiday Havoc, Krampus is on the loose stirring up mischief and tempting players to embrace their naughty side! Swap decorations, add coal under trees, or even toss festive adornments into his bonfire - all while racking up Humbug Points that could turn into unexpected rewards!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Holiday Hills: Wreaths and Wonders Expansion
description:
Step into a world of festive charm with this expansion for Holiday Hills! From beautifully adorned wreaths that enhance your holiday displays to prestigious Mayoral Mandates and dazzling Masterpiece designs, this expansion brings new layers of strategy. Prove your decorating prowess and make Jingle Bell Square shine brighter than ever!

In this expansion, 2-5 players can add up to three different modules to the base game of Holiday Hills.

The Wreaths Module allows for players to strategically add wreaths to their trees in Jingle Bell Square. Each wreath has hidden bonuses that will either enhance or decrease their point total.

The Mayoral Mandates module add detailed objectives for players to accomplish by the end of the game or they will earn negative points.

Similarly, the Gingerbread module also adds positive or negative end-game scoring, but before play begins, player will add their three gingerbread men to different confectionery cards with various requirements. Players must achieve these, or they've burned their cookies and receive negative points!

—description from the publisher

The cooperative card game Cat Rescue is back with an exciting Second Edition — now with a brand-new competitive mode! In this new edition, you'll work to rescue, foster, and adopt adorable cats, giving them a chance at a loving forever home.

Carefully place cats in the shelter, line them up for adoption, and make sure no foster home gets overcrowded. Play cooperatively or compete against friends, adding even more excitement to every rescue.

The game ends when all street cats have been rescued.  The player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

The year is 2199, and the Moon is overrun with kittens. Stray kittens. On the Moon. It’s more of a problem than you’d think.

In Kittens in Space, humanity’s first Lunar Kitten Adoption Center is in crisis, struggling to manage a low-gravity furball of a situation. and it’s up to you to save the day! Wedged between sleek skyscrapers in a bustling lunar metropolis, the shelter makes brilliant use of vertical space—because hey, low gravity means high stacking. What looks cramped by Earth standards is actually a cozy, multi-level paradise for space kittens.

You and your fellow volunteers are working to rescue stray kittens and help them find loving homes. Kittens in Space is a fast and furry-ous card-shedding ladder game with multiple active stacks in play. The direction of the ladder shifts dynamically based on what players put down, and special cards let you restrict opponents’ options or completely change the flow. It’s a tactical race to empty your hand while causing just enough chaos to stay ahead.

The first to shed all their cards wins the round. Win multiple rounds to become the ultimate champion of lunar kitten rescue.

—description from the publisher

Hey there, all you bellycrawlers! Ready yourselves, because it’s time for a fiery mayhem of the highest order!

New islands appear out of toxic waters surrounding Gastropodia and new, powerful leaders join the ferocious fight for domination. They reach for weapons of mass destruction to show their rivals that their faction will prevail. Battle-hardened soldiers join their cause, while a sea breeze carries bullets and grenades further than ever before.

So, get your shells, buckle up, lock and load, and crawl straight to combat!

Fire in the Shell is a huge expansion for Snails – a dynamic game for 2-4 players about squads of pumped up mollusks. It expands upon the base game, adding never-seen-before elements: wholly new factions with unique special abilities and action cards, new terrain tiles enabling you to play even bigger battles, additional rules featuring the wind and soldier specializations in snail squads, and a lot more...

Cyborgs, Mobsters, Druids and Knights enter the fray, while tiles hide new rewards and threats. Thanks to their specialization, each soldier is now worth their weight in gold and the wind makes even the most lousy shot count, with the potential to hit a snail... Even if not the one you wanted to harm.

You thought that playing Snails is crazy? Think twice, because now you will really feel on fire!

—description from the publisher

A family-friendly board game for children aged 6 and up, designed to be fun for both parents and kids! The objective of the game is to rescue a cat from the clutches of mummies.

In the depths of a desert, a pharaoh once hid his most precious treasure — several rare cats. He locked them on an island, leaving the mummies to protect them. However, the cats are unhappy, as they are prisoners and will never have a loving owner. They live in constant fear of the mummies roaming around…

You are the last hope for these helpless cats!


     Reach the center of the map faster than anyone else.
     Take the cat and bring it back to the start!


On your way there will be:

     Game challenges
     Search for artifacts
     Battles with other players (who may try to steal your cat!)
     Encounters with frightening mummies


Good luck on your journey!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Настольная игра для семей с детьми от 6 лет, чтобы и родителям, и детям было интересно играть! Цель игры — спасти котика из цепких лап Мумий.

Где-то в недрах пустыни Фараон спрятал своё самое ценное сокровище — нескольких редких Котов. Он запер их на острове и велел Мумиям стеречь их.
Вот только Котики этому не рады, ведь они пленники, у них никогда не будет доброго хозяина, и они вечно будут жить в страхе перед блуждающими
вокруг Мумиями…

Вы — их последняя надежда!


     Быстрее других доберитесь до центра карты
     Заберите кота и принесите его обратно на Старт!


По пути вас ждут:

     Задания игры
     Поиск Артефактов
     Сражения с другими игроками (они могут украсть вашего Кота!)
     Встречи со страшными Мумиями


Удачи в пути!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Aventuria: Island of the Mummy Lords Team Challenge
description:
Lizard Swamps, 1046 FB

You are on a mission deep in the Lizard Swamps. The humid climate and the many insects give you a hard
time, but that is nothing compared to the dangers you will soon have to face.
The Order of the Golgarites has hired you to track down a temple pyramid hidden in the swamp and destroy the artifacts buried there. These artifacts are the catalyst for a growing undead plague that threatens all of Southern Aventuria, and the island and surrounding swamp are expected to be teeming with undead creatures.
You are supported by a group of brave warrior friars from the Order of the Golgarits as well as a group of
mute cats, who joined you for some unknown reason, to fight against the Mummy Lords and their undead minions.

The game materials in this box allow you to host the cooperative Team Challenge “Island of the Mummy Lords” for up to 20 players.

—description from the publisher

Lizard Swamps, 1047 FB

You have been called back to the Island of the Mummy Lords. This is where, one year ago, you defeated a large horde of the undead. Today, the island is home to a dig site by the Dragon Egg Academy, led by Archmage Rakorium Muntagonus. He suspects a powerful artifact on the island, which the lizard priests want to use in a ritual to reawaken a terrible being by the name of “Tsulu.”
After spending a couple of uneventful weeks at the campsite, on one fateful morning you hear drums from the surrounding jungle, and a humongous army emerges from from the mists. Will you succeed in defending the dig site against their onrush?

The Team Challenge Tsulu is a fully cooperative, multi-parallel group adventure that uses Aventuria rules. All heroes compete together against a horde of enemies commanded by dangerous leaders.

You may also play this adventure as a normal group Adventure with 3 to 4 heroes, but we recommend it as a multi-parallel group Adventure with at least 8 heroes, divided into at least 2 groups. Such a team challenge is usually played as part of a larger game event.

—description from the publisher

The 144 Deck

This  pack  of  144  playing  cards  hits  the  sweet  spot between  compactness  and  versatility.  Its  basic system  includes  18  ranks  in  eight  suits  and  120 sequence numbers. Each suit includes four face cards and three special cards. The bold and light partner-suit design (with the same base-color) creates a four-color double deck or extra-long suits (e.g., for a tarot deck), or it can be used to visually highlight any rank (e.g., for highlighting point value cards).

With this deck, you can play tons of awesome games, making it a great lightweight travel companion and a way to explore and enjoy games that aren't otherwise available. The intuitive design is tested and made for smooth card play, avoiding distracting and confusing labels. Legibility and beauty are more important than including  additional  systems.  This  deck  of  cards marries a unique system of numbers, face cards, and special  cards  with  traditional  playing  card  symbols and characters.

For a complete overview and examples of games to improvise check the Manual in the files section.

—description from the designer

Heureka / ヘウレーカ！ヘウレーカ is a tile-placement puzzle game based around the powers of 2.

Players take turns placing tiles by matching the symbols on the four corners of the tiles. Each time a tile is placed, tokens are placed on the newly placed tile and adjacent tiles. Tiles occupied by a player's tokens become their territory.

In the end, player with the highest total score from their occupied tiles wins.

boardgame: Heroes of Might and Magic: The Card Game
description:
Heroes of Might and Magic: The Card Game is a two-player card game based on the internationally acclaimed video game series. Dive into the world of Erathia, where you'll command iconic Heroes, summon fearsome Monsters, and wield powerful Spells and Artifacts.

At the start of your journey, you’ll face your first primary choice: which Hero will lead your forces? Build a deck tailored to your chosen champion, develop your strategy, and prove you are the most powerful Hero in all of Erathia.

Create unique tactics, summon creatures familiar to fans of the original games, and challenge rival Heroes in tactical battles. Your faction grants you access to specific effects, while your Hero provides you powerful abilities.

Although your Hero and faction will shape your early game, the battlefield is ever-changing. As the game unfolds, powerful Artifacts and Locations may reveal new paths to victory — adapt, outsmart, and conquer!

—description from the designer
family: Video Game Theme: Heroes of Might & Magic

description:
In さくらだんご (Sakura Dango), you want to collect five sakura cards or get your opponent to collect three dango cards — but who will collect which cards will be determined by both of you

The first player in a round takes the deck of ten sakura cards and seven dango cards, then chooses three of them and places them face down in an arrangement. The other player says a number 0-3, then chooses and reveals this many cards, claiming them; the first player reveals and claims the remaining cards. The roles then switch, and play continues until someone collects five sakura cards (winning 2 pistil chips) or three dango cards (with their opponent winning 1 pistil chip).

The loser of a round starts the next round, and play continues until someone has at least three pistil chips.

さくらだんご includes six rule twist cards for use once you're comfortable with the base game.

boardgame: 当方ボーカル! (I'm the Vocalist!)
description:
Touhou Vocal! (当方ボーカル！), translating to "I'm the vocalist", sees players as vocalists trying to get together a band. Each turn, players would either exchange a card with another player or draw a card, before playing a card, showing members, to add to their band.

However, card effects will cause members to leave. For instance, a member may leave if any other member's performance skills are too low, or maybe the entire band would disband if the princess joins.

First to have 5 members signals the last round.

Keep the band together, and put people who like the same music genre to win the most points!

The Hangry Penguins / はらぺこペンギン is a co-operative game in which the goal is to become the best feeders by satisfying a large number of penguins.

In the game, hungry penguins are arriving at a tiny diner, one after another. Work together to serve their favorite fish and make them happy — but if you keep them waiting too long, they might get angry and storm off!
In Sexy Selfiewolf / セクシー自撮り人狼, the person with the highest total value of the three cards becomes the werewolf. However, if the total is less than 18, it is a false accusation and that person wins.

Rome: The Imperial Crisis is Strategy and Intrigue Board Game for 2–4 Players
The 3rd century A.D. a time of chaos and decline for the Roman Empire. Emperors rise and fall at a dizzying pace. The legions turn on each other. Barbarians press the frontiers. The economy crumbles. The power of Rome is now up for grabs.
In Imperial Crisis, each player takes on the role of a powerful Roman general, governor, or senator, seeking to dominate the empire through military campaigns, political machinations, economic influence, and ruthless ambition.

Set in a time when ancient clans and legendary warriors navigate a brutal path of loyalty and honor, Shoshin: The Path of Ascension not only immerses players in the tumultuous lives of Japan’s iconic warrior classes but also explores the mythical elements of the times. From disciplined samurai to mystical yokai, each figure in this realm is bound by fate, fighting for their clan, honor, or perhaps a mysterious higher calling. In Shoshin, feudal Japan’s struggles and spiritual elements are brought to life, blending strategic depth with epic storytelling.

Shoshin: The Path of Ascension is an immersive tabletop skirmish game rooted in the fierce and mystical world of feudal Japan, where combatants battle for honor, prestige, and survival.

Players command teams of warriors, creating distinct rosters from a pool of characters, as they clash across a series of skirmishes. Whether you lead your clan in honor-bound duels or embark on a journey as a band of Ronin, every battle reveals more about the Path of Ascension.

—description from the designer
プラネットインパクト! (Planet Impact!) is an abstract-strategy game in which players attempt to collide their planet tokens into one another to make them larger than those of other players.
みどり (GREEN) is a memory game.

"Bury them quietly."

In 静葬員（Funeral Staff), you're the clean-up crew for the criminal underworld next door. Stack the fallen meeples, balance them with care, and sneak them out without drawing attention.

In this darkly humorous balancing action game, every added meeple brings you closer to disaster.
publishers: ペンタススタジオ (Pentasufide Studio)

description:
Leyendas Ocultas is the first expansion for Héroes y Dados (Heroes and Dice), a fast-paced competitive dice game set in the mythical land of Ymir. This expansion introduces a wealth of new content that adds depth, variety, and new strategic layers to the base game. This is not a standalone game. Héroes y Dados base game is required to play.

In Leyendas Ocultas, strange objects, formidable enemies, and mysterious champions emerge from long-forgotten myths. You’ll find new types of challenges to face, both in solo-like duels and in dynamic free-for-all battles for 3 or 4 players.

This expansion includes:

    New Champions with unique abilities and styles
    A set of powerful and thematic objects
    Titan-class enemies that will test even the strongest teams
    Rules for new game modes: 3-player and 4-player free-for-all combat
    Additional content for enhancing 2-player battles


—description from the publisher

Leyendas Ocultas es la primera expansión de Héroes y Dados, un juego competitivo de dados ambientado en la tierra mítica de Ymir. Esta expansión suma una gran variedad de contenido que aporta nuevas capas de estrategia, más variedad y una mayor rejugabilidad al juego base. Este no es un juego independiente. Se necesita el juego base Héroes y Dados para jugar.

En Leyendas Ocultas, nuevos campeones con habilidades únicas, objetos extraños y enemigos temibles irrumpen desde los rincones más oscuros del pasado. Los desafíos ahora se multiplican: desde duelos intensos hasta batallas todos contra todos para 3 y 4 jugadores.

Esta expansión incluye:

    Nuevos Campeones con estilos y habilidades originales
    Objetos especiales con efectos poderosos y temáticos
    Enemigos de gran escala que pondrán a prueba a los equipos más fuertes
    Reglas para modos de juego de 3 y 4 jugadores (combate libre todos contra todos)
    Contenido adicional que enriquece las partidas a 2 jugadores


—description from the publisher (Spanish)

In Card Gear / カードギア, you want to ditch your hand of cards or use your personal handle gear to move the King Gear that starts in the center of the game board.

Each player starts with a supply of gears and a hand of five cards, with their handle gear at one corner of the hexagonal game board. On a turn, you draw a card from the deck, then either place a gear from your supply on the board or move a gear on the board to an adjacent space; in either case, you must reveal a card from your hand that matches the color of the game board space where the gear will go, then you carry out the effect of your card.

boardgame: Lords & Servants: Holy Land – Supplement for the Crusades
description:
This booklet is a supplement for Lords&Servants skirmish rules by Dadi&Piombo. 
Lords&Servants is a game system designed to recreate skirmish games with 28, 20 or 15mm miniatures.

In this supplement you will find all the information that you need to create your games set during the Crusades in Holy Land from simple clashes involving a few men per side to bigger encounters with 60-80 miniatures per side, ideal for multiplayer games.

You can field your force choosing from Fatimid Egyptians, Seljuk Turks, Sultanate of Rum, Komnean Byzantines, Crusaders, Ciliacian Armenians, Ayyubid Egyptians, Second Bulgar Empire, Latin Empire, Empire of Nicea, Empire of Trebizond, Despotate of Epirus.

—description from the publisher
群星二十一 (The 21st Constellation) is a card game based on blackjack, where players using function cards to get higher star points while not exceeding 21 points.

boardgame: Star Trek: Star Realms – Borg: Invasion Expansion
description:
Star Trek: Star Realms - Borg: Invasion pits players against the menacing foe, the Borg, in two new exciting multiplayer game modes: Cooperative and Player v. Player. Players will work together to overcome the Borg Collective, or a single player will assume the role of the Borg and assimilate all resistance. 
The Borg utilize new and devastating technology to assimilate other ships, powerful forcefields making them impervious to attacks, and legions of Drones to infiltrate and overpower enemy ships.
This expansion to Star Trek: Star Realms adds a new tool to aid in your battles: Gambits, which are powerful ability cards that provide strategic advantages at key moments to change the course of battle.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: All That Remains: A Game Based on the Judgment Seat of Christ
description:
All That Remains is a 2-6 Player, Competitive Card Game, based on the Judgement Seat of Christ, where Players take the role of Saints being judged for their life’s work. Each Player receives a Life Card Deck, containing good works and bad works, which are then thrown into the fire throughout the game. The good works that survive grant rewards; each type of reward is worth a different amount of Points. The Player with the most Points at the end of the game wins.

—description from the designer

Be the first player to Escape the island by collecting ALL five Resources; Or you can play elimination style, through multiple rounds, and the last remaining player is the overall winner.

Players can use action cards and false resources to better their hand and manipulate their opponents.

—description from the designer

Roaring Lions is a 2-4 Player Christian themed board game. The goal of the game is to make it from the start location (salvation) to the finish location (departure) without being slain by the devil. Being slain refers to either physical death (dying before your time), or spiritual death (ruining your walk with the Lord). Players must collect the Armour of God along the way to increase their chances of survival. It’s possible for multiple Players to make it to the finish location, but the Player with the most Armour Cards at the end is the winner!

—description from the publisher

In Favor of the Goddess, players take on the roles of acolytes seeking enlightenment by performing sacred rituals. They must arrange the right combination of offerings at ancient temples to match their ritual cards.

On their turns, players manipulate the available resources, exchanging or placing offerings at the open temple where the priest is present. They can flip the double-sided temple tiles and move the priest between them. When a ritual card’s pattern is matched, they gain favor points and advance on the score track. Scored rituals may also grant resources, coins, or affect opponents.

With careful planning, resources management, and optimizing actions, a player can score multiple ritual cards in a turn. Scroll cards provide special abilities and strategic advantages. The first player to reach the end of the score track wins the game.

Favor of the Goddess blends pattern recognition and building, action optimization, and resource management in a competitive race to victory.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: 2nd Rantemario
description:
This is a scenario for the Tsukuda LOTGH game system that was released in Game Journal no.40.

The scenario deals with the battle between the two "jewels" of the Empire, Mittermeyer (the Wolf) and Reuenthal.

The instructions for the scenario were laid out in the Journal, but all pieces needed to be made or used from the existing parts of the other Tsukuda games.
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description:
Jam Jam Live is a set collection game themed after making bands where each player plays a card, representing a musician, to one of their "bands" (group of cards in their area) each turn. If the cards in one of your bands exactly forms one of the sets, you score them, moving them away to your scoring area, and you may be allowed to play more cards depending on the set.

The game continues until the draw pile runs out, in which case the highest score wins.
publishers: サークルひでばち (Circle Hidebachi)

description:
In a world of fierce battles and spotlight-stealing heroes, even the party's side characters want a chance to shine. Delve into a dungeon full of monsters and loot, where you and your fellow adventurers take turns striking down foes and only the one who lands the final blow gets the coin. Time your attack, read your allies, and snatch victory from the shadows.

Trick Dungeon is a trick-taking game where players cooperate to defeat monsters by exceeding their health with the total value of cards played. However, only the player who delivers the finishing blow claims the coin reward.

boardgame: Conquest: Alexander & Hannibal – A supplement for Strength & Honour
description:
This supplement for Strength & Honour provides the gamer with an opportunity to extend the rules back in time to the world of Alexander the Great and Hannibal Barca.  The young Macedonian conquering the known world and extending its frontiers eastwards, and the preeminent General of Carthage, campaigning in Spain, Gaul and Italy, becoming Rome’s greatest nemesis.

This PDF supplement includes and introduction to the world from 350B to the time of the Marian reforms around 100 BC.  It contains rules for the warfare of the phalanx, hoplites and manipular Legions, extending the main rules back over two centuries of warfare. A dozen army lists are included, covering the early Roman Republic, Carthage, Syracuse, Gallic tribes and Gallatians, early Italian tribes,  India, Achaemenid Persians, Macedonians, early Successor, Selucids and Potolemaic forces.   These can serve as the core lusts for many other more specific historical variants.

Scenarios include the following featuring superb maps of the tables requires

Chaeronea 338BC 32
Granicus 334BC 34
Gaugamela 331BC 36
Hydaspes 326BC 38
Paraitakene 317BC 40
Gaza 312BC 42
Sentinum 295BC 44
Heraklea 280BC 46
Asculum 279BC 49
Beneventum 275BC 50
Telamon 225BC 52
Raphia 218BC 55
Lake Trasimene 217BC 57
Cannae 216BC 59
Beneventum 214BC 62
Ilipa 207BC 64
Zama 202BC 66
Cynoscephalae 197BC 68
Magnesia 190BC 70
Pydna 168BC 72

This 73 page PDF supplement is designed to allow the gamer to print out the individual scenarios and Army lists required for their games.

—description from the publisher
family: History: Wars of Alexander the Great
History: Wars of the Diadochi and Syrian Wars

description:
Deadlock! is an abstract strategy game based off of WW1's battles along the Western front. In the game, the players' ability to understand the duality between simultaneous offensive and defensive operations is crucial for victory.

Victory is achieved when a player breaks through the opposing player's defense and reaches their side of the board. Combat is predetermined on a ratio table and the players' forces start out evenly matched, but despite this gameplay remains fluid and simple. Armed witha. variety of troops, it is up to the player to be able to effectively juggle their operations, not the roll of the die. If they do, they may have a chance at breaking through the deadlock to the greener fields beyond.

The advent of trenches and arrival of reinforcements like infantry, artillery, and even tanks, serve to give both new challenges and possible strategies to the players, as well to keep gameplay overall interesting. Players will have to pick and choose the placement of reinforcements, careful to keep in mind that any one troop committed to a planned attack is one less troop committed to a defence elsewhere. If players aren't careful to maintain a balance between their planned offenses and defenses, they will often find themselves losing more ground than they gain, if not a complete loss of the game. Too close of a balance, and players will end up finding themselves deadlocked and in a stagnated position, with prospects of only a stalemate at best.

The player must understand that there is no perfect route to victory, and that not every casualty will doom them to failure. It is how they react towards and limit their losses, while effectively still maintaining a threat to the enemy, that allows them to perform successful counterattacks and offenses that can then amount to a breakthrough. It is essential that the players understand the balance between offense and defence, and the cat-and-mouse game they must play. If they do, they will be able to create a situation that will ultimately lead to a breakthrough, and then hopefully their victory.

Deadlock! is played on a 12x6 checkered board, with square pieces to represent their troops. Besides the rulebook, the only other components included in the game are tiles to mark entrenched and destroyed positions.

Deadlock! is originally designed as a PnP game for the 2025 Wargame Design Competition, and the files for the game components are under this BGG page. It is designed for two player, but can be played with more. To play with more players, simply double (or triple, etc) the length of the board (from 12x6 to 12x12, etc) as well as double or triple the amount of playing pieces.

—description from the designer

Similar to a hybrid of Stratego/Luzhanqi and Tic tac toe, with a bit of shogi, each player places their 10 cards, representing mostly soldiers (but with also a zombie and civilian), into a 3 by 3 board, with the extra 10th card in the "Standby" area. The cards are placed face-down and you may check them, so that you know what the cards are but your opponent doesn't.

On your turn, you may move a card. Then, you may drop a card from your Standby if you have any vacant spaces on your board. Afterwards, you must declare an attack on an opposing card. The 2 cards are then flipped over, face-up, resolving their effects and comparing their ranks. The lower ranked card is captured.

The first player to have 3 face-up cards in a row, or to capture the opposing General card successfully wins.
