Lone Wolf is a solo Print-And-Play (PNP) game that puts you in the role of a German U-boat commander operating in the Atlantic during World War II. As captain, your mission is to patrol dangerous waters, locate and sink Allied shipping, avoid detection, and survive long enough to return to base.

Each game plays out over a series of turns in which you'll roll dice to perform key actions such as movement, torpedo attacks, evasive maneuvers, system repairs, and spotting enemy convoys. You'll mark your progress and ship status on a custom sheet that track your fuel, torpedoes, hull integrity, and crew readiness. Strategic choices and the unpredictability of dice rolls create a challenging mix of resource management, risk assessment, and tactical navigation.

The modular patrol map system, random events, and mission objectives ensure no two games play the same. From engaging convoys to escaping depth charge barrages, Lone Wolf delivers an immersive and suspenseful experience in under an hour.

Will you return to port a decorated hunter of the seas or join the countless wrecks claimed by the cold Atlantic?

—description from the designer

¡A futbolear!' ("Let's Play Soccer!"), a fun game for soccer lovers. A game about records, stars, legends, anecdotes, great stories... and some embarrassing moments in world soccer. .Questions about soccer players, clubs, World Cups, and European Championships to test your soccer knowledge, surprising quotes from the stars that won't let you stop laughing, and fun quizzes to test your intuition and creativity.

Roll or Stand: Forbidden Adventures is a cooperative and solo roll-and-write, print-at-home game. It combines Blackjack-inspired mechanics with thematic route-building puzzles, offering a fresh twist on the roll-and-write genre. Embark on a replayable narrative campaign featuring 3 comic book-styled scenarios, each with multiple possible outcomes. Explore 12 unique puzzles, each thematically tied to a cinematic scene.

Each turn, you roll 2 dice and add the results to your previous rolls, aiming to get as close as possible to the target values without busting (going over), similar to Blackjack. Once you choose to stand, you extend lines in the active puzzle to work toward fulfilling its objective.

Note: Each scenario is contained on a single sheet of paper, with no crafting required. All you need is one pen per player and two standard six-sided dice to play the game.

—description from the publisher

Fantasy Forces is the family friendly fantasy wargame by Echidna Games.

Designed for gamers looking to inspire the next generation, Fantasy Forces lets you and your Junior General pit small fantasy warbands against each other on your fantasy battleground of choice.

As always Echidna Games encourage you to use figures from any manufacturer to bring your battles to life!

Fantasy Forces is easy to learn and fast to play.  We loved Alien Worlds and it's the game that started it all for us, but we believe Fantasy Forces does an even better job of making wargaming accessible and fun for new players.

—description from the publisher

Ahoy Taverneer is an asymmetric Roll&Write board game telling the story of a retired pirate who doesn't quite want to give up his pirate habits. The game will allow you to freely choose the methods by which you will achieve final success.

You have 8 rounds to gather Fame Stars using Dice rolls and effect cards. You can focus entirely on:

    Running your tavern business.
    Returning to the seas and oceans to plunder, loot, search for treasures, fight merchants and gain fame by hunting mythical sea creatures.
    Trading in other ports, acquiring exotic goods and gold.
    Conducting shady deals in the city of Nassau itself, where a club and a knife in dark alleys solve almost all local problems. 
    Hosting the crews of other pirate ships in your cozy tavern, with suitably pirate-friendly service.
    Or a combination of all of the above.


At the end of the 8th round, the player with the most Fame Stars, wins!

Rules include solo variant.

—description from the designer

Meteor Shower it’s a fresh, fast-paced experience built for players who love smart tactics, unexpected twists, and just a little bit of chaos. Every session is different, thanks to falling meteorites and dynamic strategies that keep everyone guessing (and plotting) until the very last move.

The goal of Meteor Shower is to be the first player to build an unbroken line (curve/polygonal/straight) of meteorites from the starting point to the opposite side of the board. For this purpose, players move the stones available on the game board to the cells they need. Initially, there are no meteorites on the game board, they will fall during the game.

Two gameplay modes: 
Quick Play Mode: Finished in about 10 minutes. 
Extended Play Mode: Runs 30 minutes or more.

—description from the designer

Buchstaben Klacks is a colorful and engaging card game designed to introduce young children to the alphabet. Each card pairs a letter with a corresponding animal to encourage visual and phonetic associations. Players take turns matching cards by color or letter/animal. If no match is possible, they draw from the deck. Special black action cards can be played at any time, adding excitement to each round. The game is simple, educational, and perfect for early learners and families.

—description from the designer

A cosy tessellating tile laying, worker placement game of gems, jewellers, and beautiful treasures crafted by players as they journey to become the greatest jeweller in the Grand Bazaar.

Players take turns collaboratively building the board through placing tessellating tiles, arranging setting pieces to collect gems, hire new workers and fulfil orders from their extravagant clients.

—description from the designer

An ancient game of cosmic skysails and stellar influence, gifted to our ancestors by ancient travellers who sailed the stars on streams of sunlight.

Played on a circular seed of life board, Aster is an abstract strategy game where players take turns building castras (defenses), moving ships along circular streams, planning attacks, and securing places to lay aster petal pieces.

—description from the designer

BACHI×BACHI! is an asymmetric abstract game themed around the battle between Japanese honeybees (nihon mitsubachi) and giant hornets (ōsuzumebachi).

The goal is to collect eight food tokens before your opponent does. The honeybee player gathers nectar from flower fields, while the hornet captures bees as their food source. Unique abilities based on real-life insect behaviors are included to make the gameplay feel natural and exciting.

UMBRELLA / アンブレラ takes places in a world where acidic rain falls and harmful UV rays pour down from the sky. Choosing the right umbrella for the weather can mean the difference between life and death. The rules are simple: Whoever collects the most umbrellas wins!

In this game,the environment is extremely harsh. Whether it's a sunny day or a rainy one, stepping outside empty-handed will cause damage — but to obtain new umbrellas, you'll need to venture outside and sometimes you must use an umbrella you already own to safely bring back a new one.

Predict the weather, and return with umbrellas efficiently and safely! Use item cards to manipulate the weather, steal umbrellas from others...or perhaps even have your umbrellas confiscated by the police?

Who will survive and return with the most umbrellas?

"See you in 10 years, in this cafe." 
Ten years have passed since I parted ways with an old friend. Today is the promised day. I can't wait to see my grown up friend again. But as I walked down the old street, I began to feel uneasy.
"Where was that cafe?"

The goal of each player is to fulfill a promise to reunite with an old friend. To do so, all players must meet in the same cafe. If the field cards in front of each player match and the call is successful, all players win the game.

—description from the publisher
publishers: Mysboard Games (ミスボドゲームズ)

description:
Late at night, a scream echoes through an abandoned building.
There were four mysterious men and women and the dead body of a homeless looking man.
They move around the building and begin to investigate.

This is a Murder Mystery Game. Each player takes on the role of a character in a mystery novel and must complete the missions listed in each handout.
The game can only be played once in a lifetime, as the player will know the truth of the story when he or she plays. The purpose of the game is for all players to cooperate and have fun.

—description from the designer
publishers: Mysboard Games (ミスボドゲームズ)

description:
Fantasy Rank Master is a game in which players rank monsters and people of various occupations that are summoned at random in a fantasy world according to a theme.

On the front of the summon cards are various races and occupations, and on the back are various characteristics. These are arranged on tiles that resemble a summoner's camp.

The player summons a character by randomly combining cards with various characteristics and races, such as "Fulfilled", "Dragon", "Handful", "Knight", "Flying", "Cerberus", and so on.
Then, cards with subjects such as "easily lost" are presented, and each player ranks them, earning points the more they are the same in rank with other players.

What happens when three or more Archons want to access the same vault? A relentless assault where only the fiercest, or most devious, can be victorious!

KeyForge: Vault Assault is a multiplayer variant where 3–5 players compete against each other in a race to be the first to forge three keys. There are no teams in this variant; it’s every Archon for themselves!

To play Vault Assault, each player uses one KeyForge deck from their collection (or opens a new sealed deck). Aside from these decks and the usual KeyForge tokens and counters, the only other components you need are a set of Vault Assault cards. This set of cards contains a First Player card, and six different boon cards.

Extreme Mission / エクストリームミッション is a card game in which players select one of four levels of missions and clear the game by drawing color and number combinations from their hands as instructed, aiming to clear all levels of missions as quickly as possible.

However, some missions are so difficult that it is hard to believe that you can succeed, such as "All the numbers from 1 to 9 must be in the same color, and all the colors must be the same".

Such missions are made possible by the five co-operators that accompany each player. By turning the co-operator cards inside out, they can be used as all-mighty cards, and any number of such cards can be used at once. However, each of them must be paid for by a certain amount of cards to be put back into their normal state.

However, to clear the game, all co-operator cards must be returned to the foreground, in addition to clearing all stages of the mission, so the use of powerful co-operator cards can greatly affect the outcome of the game.

Siamese Mah Jongg aka 2-handed Mah Jongg, is a reimplementation of American Mahjong for 2 players. The goal of the game is to create two completed Mah Jongg hands. The first player to do so wins the game.

From small and cute organisms to big and cool animals, a variety of creatures live in harmony in the sea. In Mini Wild: Blue, the second title in the Mini Wild series, you will create your own little marine ecosystem with these animals. However, please be careful of environmental pollution that is approaching every moment. Remember that it is up to you to keep the ocean beautiful.

On your turn, you may add animal cards to your hand, or use the cards in your hand to create a food chain. The food chains you create gather to form your own ecosystem. The more you can connect different food chains to form a larger ecosystem, the more victory points you get.

An event will occasionally occur when acquiring cards. While some events have positive effects, beware of bad situation, such as plastics accumulating in the ecosystem due to environmental pollution or animals being scattered in a storm. It is also important to properly use the abilities of the animals in your ecosystem.

The goal of the game is to create a rich ecosystem, earn victory points from mission cards, and collect more victory points than other players.

Explorers from all over the world have gathered on an isolated island in the middle of the ocean! You have come to "Dinosaur Island" as an explorer because many dinosaur fossils and DNA have been excavated here.

In Mini Wild: Red, the third title in the Mini Wild series, you will excavate every part of the island, restore various dinosaurs, and build your own ecosystem, then compare its strength with those of other explorers.

Explorers love adventure and competition, but mutant dinosaurs, born from failed restoration attempts, are wandering around the island. Furthermore, volcanic activity on the island has recently started. Create a wonderful dinosaur ecosystem before the volcano erupts, and grab the glory of being the best explorer.

Mini Wild: Red differs from the previous entries in the Mini Wild series by being an engine building game based primarily on set collection and hand management. Gather various dinosaurs, build own ecosystem, and prepare for battles!

boardgame: Pet Names: A Game of Endearments
description:
In Pet Names: A Game of Endearments, you'll be competing to create the best nicknames in each round- first to three wins the game!

There are 400 mini cards snuggled inside a plush fox bag called Charlie that you will use to create endless combinations of playful, charming, or hilarious nicknames. Bonus: Charlie the fox plush also doubles as a cute mini ba g- just attach your favorite strap to carry your hands free crossbody bag.

In each round, every player makes one pet name for the person holding Charlie using 2 to 4 cards from their hand. Make it funny, sweet, or silly—whatever you think fits them best! Players may also choose to draw from the pool of face-up cards on the table or a random card from Charlie. Always replenish the pool of face-up cards to five.

Once everyone has made their pet names, players read them aloud to the subject (the person holding Charlie), going clockwise.  The subject chooses their favorite pet name, and the player who created it wins the round.

The first player to have three winning nicknames chosen wins Pet Names!

—description from the designer

MechaBug Fighters is a strategic card game where 2-4 players control and battle large insect Mechas.

The roll of a d20 decides the success of your attack but it is a players strategy that matters the most.

Install weapons, shields and minion insects to fight with your MechaBug. Use trainers and repairs to keep your MechaBug in the game.

Batteries are key, install as many as you can.

Join the battle!

—description from the designer

Discover and collect various types of flower dragons while trying to earn prestige by planting and claiming beautiful gardens of flowers for the next migration of dragons.

Every round you will choose to plant flowers in the garden by choosing a flower tile. Based on the placement of the tile you will: collect the dragons underneath the flowers you planted, reveal the dragons next to the flowers, and score prestige points based on matching the types of flowers to the other flowers and dragons around it.

Players will then have to choose to keep one of the dragons they collected and the other one will be sent to the greenhouse to grow and will lower the overall prestige earned from collecting dragons of that type. Players will score additional prestige at the end of the game based on the dragons they have collected and their prestige value from the greenhouse.

Each player will have 2-3 gardeners that they may place in the garden, during the game, in order to secure a particular area to earn them prestige.

—description from the publisher

Monsters have invaded the ancient dungeons below ground! How delicious! Your adventuring company is being hired out by the best restaurants and diners to go hunt these monsters for their menus.

As an adventuring company, your goal is to become the most successful business you can be. You will be: hunting monsters, claiming hunting grounds, completing restaurant contracts, and developing your company, all while exploring the dungeon.

—description from the publisher

IntroductionSerpent is a drawless annihilation game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 5 cells per side, although 4 and 6 are also valid. Each player has a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A snake is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color, whose stones are adjacent to no more than two stones of that snake that are not adjacent to each other. A single stone is an egg.
     The length of a snake is the number of stones it contains.
     An end is a stone of the snake that is adjacent to exactly one stone of the same snake.
     The head of a snake is the end with the shortest survival path to the end or egg of another friendly snake. The other end is the tail. If both ends have survival paths of equal length, either one may be the head and the other the tail.
     A survival path is a continuous sequence of empty cells from an egg, head, or tail of a snake to an egg, head, or tail of another friendly snake, such that, by filling the sequence and the ends with friendly stones, a single valid snake would be created.
     An egg or snake is alive if it has a path to survival to another friendly snake's egg, head, or tail; otherwise, it is dead. An egg or snake is also alive if there has never been more than one egg or snake of its color on the board.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty cell adjacent to at most two friendly eggs and to no friendly snake.
     Move a friendly snake's tail to an empty cell adjacent to its head. The move is valid only if, after all the removals, the snake maintains a shorter survival path than the previous one, which can reach 0 cells in length.


At the end of your turn, choose a color and simultaneously remove all dead eggs and snakes of that color, then do the same with the other color. A removal is valid only if its owner cannot play on any of its remaining cells on their next turn, invalidating any dead formations that cannot be removed under this condition. If the only stone removed is the one you just placed, your placement is illegal.

				
				
					End of the game:You win if the last enemy egg or snake is removed from the board.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 異世界アジャイルカードゲーム (Isekai Agile Card Game)
description:
Enter a fantasy world where characters take on roles like Product Owner, Scrum Master, and Developers — all inspired by Agile development.

In 異世界アジャイルカードゲーム (Isekai Agile Card Game), each player draws and plays cards in turn to form role sets, use special abilities, and react to others' discards.

Be the first to form two groups of three matching role cards, while trying to score the highest points based on combinations, bonuses, and timing.
publishers: 異世界アジャイル (Isekai Agile)

description: In CATTLE, one player takes on the role of the UFO trying to abduct SHEEP, while the other represents the SHEEP trying to surround the UFO.
designers: ピーナツデザイン (PEANUTS DESIGN)
artists: ピーナツデザイン (PEANUTS DESIGN)
publishers: ピーナツデザイン (Peanuts Design)

description: In Strategy 5, you will become a basketball league general manager, and manage your team to win the most championships! You will draft, acquire, and release players to build a stronger team that fits your strategy for the basketball seasons.

After the brutal war against the Devot, the Federation, eager to reclaim lost ground and uncover new opportunities, ventures into the Uncharted Sectors. As a seasoned Warp-Lancer, you’ll face the shattered remnants of the Devot menace, hostile new alien factions, ancient space anomalies, and treacherous hazards. Uncover powerful relics, forge uneasy alliances, and battle for survival in a galaxy where every jump could be your last.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Falcon Hero Pack
description:
Sam Wilson has always helped people, serving as a prodigious social worker even before he became a soaring superhero. Once he donned a pair of high-tech vibranium wings, Sam—also known as the Falcon—used his aerial ability and empathic connection with birds to provide his fellow Avengers a powerful tactical advantage, and now he’s flying into your games of Marvel Champions: The Card Game! With his keen eyesight and ability to detect incoming threats, Falcon is an adaptable hero and a great addition to any team. Within this hero pack, you will find Falcon, his fifteen signature cards, and a full assortment of Leadership cards to help you command your allies from the skies. This pack also includes a bonus modular encounter set featuring Fixer of the Thunderbolts!

Anyone looking to take on the agents of A.I.M. will want to add the Falcon Hero Pack to their collection! This expansion comes with a 40-card pre-built deck, giving you the chance to start playing from the moment you open the box.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Winter Soldier Hero Pack
description:
As a young man during World War II, James “Bucky” Barnes served as the loyal sidekick to Captain America. When an explosion took Bucky’s arm and left him on the brink of death, he was captured by Soviet soldiers, fit with a cybernetic arm, and brainwashed into serving as an assassin until Captain America helped him remember his past decades later. Now, Bucky seeks to atone for the horrors he was forced to commit as the Winter Soldier, and he’s blasting his way into your games of Marvel Champions: The Card Game! With his impressive skills as a lethal protector, Winter Soldier is a fierce hero and a great addition to any team. In this hero pack, you will find Winter Soldier, his fifteen signature cards, and a full assortment of Aggression cards inviting you to tear through the enemy forces.

Anyone looking to take on the agents of A.I.M. will want to add the Winter Soldier Hero Pack to their collection! This expansion comes with a 40-card pre-built deck, giving you the chance to start playing from the moment you open the box.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

boardgame: ボドゲの制作者たち (Board Game Creators)
description:
BODOGE creators / ボドゲの制作者たち is a game from Japan. As a BODOGE creator, this is a game where you make BODOGE and aim to sell out. A hand management game, that allows you to freely play cards from your hand within a set cost. The players  battle for majority control over the four elements that make up board games: system, artwork, motif, and components.
pubisher's summary

Classic edition featuring the streets of Barcelona from the famous Monopoly game.


Edición clásica con las calles de Barcelona del famoso juego Monopoly.

HANAMI / 花見 is a game from Japan in which players enjoy the cherry blossom in Spring. All over Japan, parks and gardens are full of cherry trees in full bloom, and Japanese people enjoy Hanami, sitting under the blossom to eat and drink and party.

This is a strategic territory capture game with a new and changing board and easy-to-play rules. Players try to take over as much of the cherry blossom viewing area as possible, using cards to spread out the straw mats while competing with other players. Collect sets of Hanami activities and play them to secure key positions, scoring for your largest contiguous area on the board.

・Manage your hand to create sets of cards, then use those cards to capture the best seating locations under the blossoms
・Score points for your largest chain of consecutive pieces
・Certain sets will allow you to pull the entire tatami row out, shaking up the largest interconnected sections of the board

The player who has captured the largest area is the winner and has enjoyed the cherry blossom viewing to the fullest.

-publisher's summary

Volcanoes: From Chile is a party game for 2 to 6 people, with games lasting 3 to 5 minutes and recommended for ages 8 and up.
It's a fast-paced, turn-free game in which players must search for tension cards that increase the tension of other players' volcanoes.
Tension cards range from 1 to 9 and must be played on top of opposing volcanoes in consecutive order. However, there are double tension cards that are an exception to this rule.
If you find an eruption card and there is a volcano at tension level 9, make it erupt and remove it from the game!
When searching for cards, if you can play them, place them on an enemy volcano; otherwise, return them to the pile in the center of the table, face down. However, there are fixed tension cards that are not returned face down, but face up.
With extremely simple and quick setup, and frenetic gameplay, the player elimination mechanic feels effortless and facilitates the feeling of claiming a fair rematch.
Be the only volcano that doesn't erupt!

—description from the publisher

Volcanes: de Chile es un juego party de 2 a 6 personas, con partidas entre los 3 a los 5 minutos y recomendado para mayores de 8 años hacia arriba.
Es un juego frenético y sin turnos en el que deberemos buscar cartas de tensión que nos sirvan para aumentar la tensión de los volcanes del resto de participantes.
Las cartas de tensión van del 1 al 9 y deben ser jugadas encima de los volcanes rivales en orden consecutivo, sin embargo, hay cartas de tensión dobles que son la excepción a esta regla.
¡Si encuentras una carta de erupción y hay un volcán en nivel 9 de tensión, hazlo erupcionar y sácalo de la partida!
Cuando buscas cartas, si te sirven para jugarlas, las pones sobre un volcán enemigo, de lo contrario las devuelves al montón al centro de la mesa boca abajo. Sin embargo, hay cartas de tensión fijas que no se devuelven boca abajo, sino boca arriba.
Con un seteo en extremo sencillo y rápido, además de un gameplay tan frenético, la mecánica de eliminación de jugadores se siente ligera y facilita la sensación de reclamar una justa revancha.
¡Se el único volcán que no entre en erupción!

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Lastuf Basket is a Basketball Card Game in which players must score 21 points to win.


     Simple rules make this game a great pastime and an entertaining board game of your favorite sport.



     The SPECIFIC ACTION CARDS simulate the rules of basketball to make it even more fun; they must be used skillfully to win. The game includes erasable wildcards to customize them with your own rules for even more fun.



     THE BARAJA SUME 90 cards: 8 player cards in 4 colors. 4 baskets, 24 balls to score points. 4 substitutions, 6 five personal fouls. 4 caps, 4 technical violations. 4 timeouts. 8 24 seconds. Includes 2 joker cards to add your own rules.


—description from the publisher

パンドド！ (Brea-do-do!) is a dexterity game from Japan, with players flicking bread into goals, as in Billiards or Carrom. Each player has a different character, as various Japanese anime schoolchildren, and these are shown in flat acrylic cut-out shapes in dramatic action poses. The player places the figure flat and flicks it with their finger, to strike the bread token and propel them to a goal.

The game board is a cloth, printed with a school lunchroom occupied by lots of kids, and a dinner-lady in the centre dolling out the last lunch, "Here you go! This is the last yakisoba bread of the day!".

Around the four edges, wooden batons are placed, to keep the cloth flat and provide a wall to bounce shots off. A flat loaf of bread is dropped in the centre. Each player enters the arena from their goal in the corner by flicking their character towards the bread. Players continue to take turns, flicking their pieces to drive the bread into another player's corner and so score a goal.

Players can play it as a fun party game, or take turns using each character for added strategy.

--user summary
publishers: ボボン・ボン・ボジワーイ連邦 (The Bobon Bon Bojiwaai Union)

boardgame: こんなせまい池とはおさらばだ (Goodbye to Such a Narrow Pond)
description:
In こんなせまい池とはおさらばだ (Goodbye to Such a Narrow Pond), players become flamingos kept at the zoo, plotting an escape. Praying not to be found by the zookeeper, you want to secure a running distance, spread your wings, and escape into the sky...but maybe I'm happier now, where I can live peacefully rather than living in harsh nature?

This is a puzzle and chicken-race game in which you flip or exchange cards in the common field to secure an escape route and help the flamingo in your hand escape, but things might not go well because of the troublesome keeper.

There are multiple end conditions, and the way points are calculated changes depending on which one is met. The points for each round are added up, and the player with the most points at game's end wins.
publishers: ぷあぷあイデオロギー (Poor Poor Ideology)

description:
The game WHO IS OWNER teaches players how property rights work, how to defend your claims, and how to challenge others — all while having a blast.

The game consists of forty cards, including basic ownership cards (sale, gift, inheritance...), technique cards (security, notarized deed...), penalty cards (tort, breach of contract...), and world cards with game-altering global effects.

Each card carries a strategic legal mechanism, forcing players to think like property lawyers, negotiators, and opportunists. Whether you're proactively securing your rights or waiting for an opponent to slip up, you're constantly engaged in a dynamic legal battlefield.

At the end of the game, whoever holds the Owner Card — or has secured the Paper Owner title (through legal registration) — wins.

boardgame: The Massive-Verse Fighting Card Game: Starter Pack
description:
This $5 booster pack contains everything two players need to play the Massive-Verse Fighting Card Game. It's super-powered dueling in just 10 minutes.

Our theory? You'll play it, you'll love it, and you'll want more characters.

One player is Radiant Black, cosmic-powered superhero.
The other is Premier, who will test the Radiants for the fate of the world.

Will Earth be saved? It will certainly be changed.

Note: The Radiant Black, Challenging Catalyst character in this pack is different than the Radiant Black in the Massive-Verse core box.
—description from the publisher

In this semi-cooperative dice-and-card game, 1-6 cultists traverse New England to collect knowledge and relics to summon Cthulhu from the depths. Roll resource dice to complete and claim cards representing various elements from the Cthulhu Mythos. Resources include tomes, relics, coffee, madness, and elder signs.

Each turn, you will roll and assign the dice until you get your target card or run out of dice. But if you can’t fulfill a resource block, you must send a die to the dice grave.

The sea & stars are never safe, and neither is New England. Investigators and mystics stand in your way, preventing your progress and pushing you toward ruin. Time is short. Sanity is fragile. And the Great Dreamer stirs…

Will you succeed in Summoning Cthulhu, or will your sanity be torn apart by the very forces you seek to unleash?

—description from the publisher

THE BOMB is the explosive expansion to Camarades !

It contains a special card that introduces a bomb to be defused in the middle of the game, adding new tension between players.

It comes with a UNIQUE numbered soft dice tray representing a imaginary country !

This exclusive box contains a myriad of Survivors, Abominations, Necromancers, and more, to upgrade your Zombicide Fantasy experience. Rally your party of Survivors to face off against the fearsome Abominations and scheming Necromancers! Are you prepared to confront the Zombie plague?

A card and dice game based on the arcade game Bombergirl, this game sees players try to play cards and roll dice to destroy the opposing Base before theirs is destroyed.

Players will be playing cards to move from their Starting Point to the opposing Tower, and eventually the opposing Base, rolling dice to deal damage to their gates and the structures directly. Each time you damage the opposing Base, you take a Base card, which can grant a variable amount of points, without flipping them face up to check their point values.

Along the way, players can play cards to help you increase your rolls, kill opponents, block off enemy respawn points, and more.

The first to deal 7 damage to the opposing Base wins, or if the draw pile runs out, any Base cards you obtained will be revealed and counted for points.

Ahoy ye maties - Captain Octo has set sail on the open seas and is looking for booty! All you buccaneers need to load your treasures onto the Captain's boat as it sails by. The first player to stack all their treasures and put the pirate hat onto the Captain wins! Shiver me timbers - the seas are rough, and the boat is wobbling! If any pieces fall down on your turn you need to add them back to your pile. Who will be the master of the high seas, and who will need to walk the plank!?

WOBBLING BOAT CHALLENGE: The game features a wobbling boat that adds an extra layer of difficulty, making it a fun and engaging challenge to keep treasures stacked and balanced as the boat rocks. If any treasures fall during a player’s turn, they must return the pieces to their pile.

—description from publisher

California is a place unlike any other, with sunshine, celebrities, forest fires, earthquakes, and everything in between. Unique situations call for unique heroes. An elite firefighting squad trained to deal with the unusual situations that only a place like this can produce. Operating out of the beachfront city of the sun-drenched SoCal city of Los Aventura, these brave firefighters are ready for anything. When danger arises and requires the utmost skill, determination, and grit, Gold Company gets the call.

Flash Point: Golden State Heroes is a co-operative game of bravery and heroism. Included are six unique game boards representing a gauntlet of challenges for the courageous Gold Company in Southern California.  Additionally, this set provides action-packed boosts and new customizable gear to create countless combinations of unique firefighting heroes.

During each game, all players are on the same team, winning or losing together. To win, the players must rescue trapped victims before the fire gets out of control or the scene breaks down completely.

Flash Point: Golden State Heroes is a standalone game that introduces the "Ignition System", a non-legacy method of creating endless customized firefighters and using thematic event decks tied to each map in the game.

—description from the publisher

The First Mates expansion introduces a ton of modules that you can add to Seas of Havoc including new First Mates cards, Ship Upgrade trees, 2 new captains, new market cards and more. All these new cards and modules offer a whole lot of fun and variability to the core tactical naval skirmish game.

Adds 8 unique First Mates to have even more ways to customize your starting deck. First Mate cards are an additional ability card in your deck. They each have a free action and a paid enhanced version of the action. At the start of the game, you’ll draw and draft them based on player count and place them immediately in your discard pile.

2 New Captains: The Gambler and Fisherman
New Market cards: Wild Flags
Ship Upgrade Tech Trees
Deck Upgrades and Challenge cards
Advanced Sea Monster Cards

—description from the publisher

おもさ感覚 (Weight Sense) is a "sensory game" that anyone can enjoy as they try to detect subtle differences that their fingers can feel!

The game features small boxes with slightly different weights, and you want to guess the number on the back of a box using the weight as a clue. There are also original games based on card games, such as neurasthenia, self-arranging poker, pig's tail, and Indian poker, as well as co-operative games and quick-take games.

おもさ感覚 features simple and profound games that do not rely on memory or strategy and are fun for everyone.
publishers: ちょっとずつ違う (Chottozutsu Chigau)

description: In The Escape code / エスケープコード, you want to collect four or more of the same card to escape before your oxygen runs out. Be the last survivor to win the game!
publishers: ふらっとゲームス (FR@ Games)

boardgame: どすこい！押相撲さん (Dosukoi! Oshizumo-san)
description:
どすこい！押相撲さん (Dosukoi! Oshizumo-san) is a handmade, all-wood abstract board game.

During play, you will position, merge, and move your sumo pieces to push opponents out of the ring! Reduce your opponent to two or fewer wrestlers to win.

the ONE is a tic-tac-toe word puzzle in which you attempt to form the word "ONE" — as well as ZOO, NEO, MOM, and other words — by changing the orientation and arrangement of the pieces!

The combination of matching letters and tic-tac-toe provides for a deep and fulfilling game experience.

In the poker-like card game Amulette, players create a number of poker hands from a five-suit, 12-rank deck and score points according to the hands. The key to the game is the five cards lined up in a common field.

Who was nicknamed "the vulture"?
Who scored the goal nicknamed "the hand of God"?
How often is a World Cup held?

Show that you know the most about the beautiful game.

Advance with your players around the pitch and score more goals than your opponent by answering questions about national, international, and World Cup football.

Deportes Cuatro: The ultimate game for football lovers.

1.2.3ならべ (1.2.3 Arrange) is a brain-busting game in which you freely arrange pieces in a 3x3 grid in an alternating 1, 2, 3 order; move the pieces in 1, 2, 3 order; and win by arranging them in 1, 2, 3 order.
In 多段3目ならべ (Multi-level 3-Row Line-up), you stack pieces alternately within a nine-square grid and win if you can create a 3x3 grid at the same time.

ヤジルシ (Arrow) is a co-operative game in which players try to lay out tiles showing two or more arrows into a grid so that each arrow is pointed at by another arrow. The tiles come in multiple colors, and you can use different sets of arrows to create different sized grids, adjusting the difficulty of the game as desired.

Harness the power of the stars and guide your destiny. In a celestial contest of fate and wit, players use the strength of the zodiac and elemental alignments to outmaneuver their rivals. Cute yet mysterious, this is a cosmic challenge where intuition and timing determine who truly controls the heavens.

Zodiac Trick is a trick-taking game themed around the 12 zodiac signs and four elemental forces that add a rock-paper-scissors layer of strategy, where some elements naturally beat others. In addition, Fate Cards introduce one-time powers that can alter scores or outcomes, creating moments of surprise and tactical reversals. It's a trick-taking game that rewards both planning and flexibility.

Gramma / グラマ is a co-operative board game for two or four players that focuses on the sense of weight and communication. Players work together to make their individual bags weigh the same, describing the weight using everyday objects (basic rule) or your emotions (advanced rule). Success is achieved when all bags balance on a scale, with no individual winners or losers.

In 機械獣 (Mechanical Beast), you want to capture the most valuable beasts, but without getting into too much danger as you risk elimination.

The deck consists of cards numbered 1-7, with 1s and 2s having safety marks and 4-7 having danger marks. More 3s are present than the other numbers.

Each of the three players has a hand of four cards, and they take turns placing cards to form a 3x3 grid. The starting player then declares "vertical" or "horizontal", with the player to their left taking their choice of column or row (depending on the declaration) and the starting player getting whatever is left behind after two players choose.

Rotate the starting player position, deal each player three cards, then play another round. Do this a third time.

Add up each player's danger marks, with each safety mark eliminating a danger and a set of three 3s being worth three safety marks. Whoever has the most danger is eliminated, then the player with the highest sum wins.

In a two-player game, the grid is filled and cards taken based on an algorithm for the third player.

In Ultra-Stupid 300 Raiders, stupid raiders kill each other at the turn of the century. The more dice you roll, the more raiders you have. Expand your camp, devour the seed rice, and earn violence points.

Each player starts with 12 hidden cards: 2 rows with 6 cards each and tries to get the best open display.

At the start, each player already chooses one of his cards in the display and turns them to the open side.

Now all players play alternately.

In his turn, a player can choose a card from the open discard pile and put it open in its display, the replaced card is placed on the discard pile. Alternatively, the player can pull a card from the hidden pile and put it as mentioned or discard it. In the latter case, however, one of the own cards has to be revealed.

The game ends immediately as soon as a player has all his cards open.

Points are now obtained from the printed card symbols. There is one point for two neighboring half coins each, here the position of the cards is important. There are also cards that score points for collecting other cards.

In addition to the scoring cards, there are also a few cards with special effects.

In 枕戯 (Pillow Fight), you want to smash your opponent with pillows...or decorate the futon.

To start, each player chooses one of the five pillow-fighting girls, each of whom has different abilities, including the number of pillows in hand. Draft pillows to fill your hand, then start the fight.

Each player simultaneously chooses and reveals a pillow, with whoever has the higher number placing their pillow in their points area. Depending on the situation, the loser places their pillow on a cushion, gaining a one-time effect, or on the futon.

To fill their hand, each player simultaneously points to one of the four pillows on display, re-choosing if they select the same one. Keep fighting until someone has 20 points in their area or has placed their third item on the futon. If the deck runs out before either situation, the player with the most points wins.

In Dice of Madness / コロコロSAN脈, we'll climb the mountain by rolling dice. Yes, that's (almost) all it will take...

Each player has five dice, and on your turn, you choose how many to roll, with up to two re-rolls, in order to accomplish a task. The more you roll, the higher your success rate. However, when all players have finished their turns, the madness die will fall to the player with the highest total of rolls. The madness die is a nasty one that cancels other dice.

The game ends when a player reaches the top of the mountain after completing a certain task, or when all tasks are completed. The player closest to the top wins.
artists: おーつぼじゅうじん (Jyujin Ohtsubo)
publishers: ぶれけけゲームズ (Brekeke Games)

boardgame: Off the Line: Expansion #2 – The Italians
description:
From the Allied landings on Sicily to the heartland of the boot of Italy, this second expansion for Off the Line presents soldiers of the Italian army.

Players will find eighty individually named, rated, and armed soldiers ready to battle the enemy across six new double-sided maps and six new scenarios.

Also introduced is a new "sunken path" terrain type that adds protection to moving soldiers trying to make their way across the battle zone and on toward victory.

NOTE: This is not a complete game! Ownership of the Off the Line volume Fortress Europe that includes German and American forces
is required for play.

—description from the designer

A game of collecting and dexterity. It’s time to set off on a mission into space in search of precious crystals. The crystals will be more or less rare depending on the planet you land on. Use your rocket launching skills with care to land on the best planets!

—description from the designer

My Magnum Opus is a worker placement, set collection and negotiation game for 2 to 4 players in which each‬ player takes on the role of a Master Painter working to fulfil their animal group’s duty to Mother Nature. In doing‬ so, players will utilise their Apprentices, apply their investment acumen, wield ruthless tongues of negotiation, and‬ collect the necessary resources required to literally paint their paint-by-numbers masterpieces. Who will rise above the other Master‬ Painters and claim Mother Nature’s favour?‬

—description from the designer

Welcome to a drama of fated encounters, emotional tension, and romantic power dynamics. In BL Trick-Taking, players take on the roles of classic boys’ love (BL) archetypes (Tsundere, Do-S, Muscle, and more) forming couples through clever trick resolution. Only the most fitting combinations of aggressive “seme” and vulnerable “uke” will win the hearts (and points) of the fandom. But sometimes the roles reverse... and when a “reversible” card shows up, the whole power balance flips.

BL Trick-Taking is a trick-taking game where each card represents a BL character archetype with a strength value and a role (seme or uke). Players play one card per trick, and the strongest seme is paired with the weakest uke to form a couple. Each resulting couple scores points based on how “shippable” the match is according to a predefined table. Occasionally, “reversible” (リバ) cards shift the suit hierarchy, introducing dynamic power flips mid-game. Bonus points are awarded for thematic consistency—like achieving a “total uke” or “total seme” hand. It’s a campy, strategic game of roleplay, rhythm, and reading the room.

Two rival dentists, one poor patient with three aching teeth. In this charming and deceptively tense card game, you’ll compete to prove your dental prowess. Each round, you’ll declare how many cavities you can cure, but only one of you will walk away with bragging rights. The clinic may look friendly, but the treatment room is a battlefield.

Sanbon no Mushiba ("Three Cavities") is a must-follow for two players built on bold bids and precision play. Using a compact deck of just 10 unique cards, players first declare how many of the three "cavities" (tricks) they believe they can win. The higher bidder reshuffles and redraws a limited number of cards, adding risk to their confidence. Tricks are resolved with a rotating trump system and clever bluffing. To win, you must fulfill your declared number of tricks... no more, no less. Miss your bid, and your rival wins by default.
publishers: 東京なかよしデザイン (Tokyo Nakayoshi Design)

description: The hit Pictionary Man game that combines the fun of drawing and charades is now available in a to go version. Completely portable, the game contains everything you'll need to play including clue cards and a miniature sand timer. Perfect for taking with you on vacation or to a friend's house for game night.

Dungeons & Business Cards is a fantasy role-playing game in business card format. All you need is a pen and a die. You can play it alone or with friends. It's a small and entertaining game that fits in your wallet. Playable at parties and with children.

Roll your character. Disarm traps. Kill monsters. Find treasure. Reach the next levels on the next business cards. Slay the dragon. Find the amulet!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Gold And Glory for DQ: Dragon's Keep Dungeon
description:
In the storied land of Terrestria, crawl the dreaded Dragon's Keep Dungeon with fabled Heroes, face terrifying monsters and risk the wrath of the ancient Grand Dragon Vyrminus Mithorax, risking life and limb for Gold And Glory.

Gold And Glory For DQ: Dragon’s Keep Dungeon is an unofficial expansion that offers a system of alternate rules built on top of the DungeonQuest Revised Edition board game. It adds advanced gameplay concepts to the base game’s core rules, game board and card decks, which are used as a foundation.

—description from the designer

Shot Assemble is a tactical sniper showdown akin to military chess.

To start, arrange your ten cards — which include three snipers — in a grid, then take turns moving, revealing, and activating them to outsmart, uncover, and shoot. Reveal cards to trigger effects, use the spy to gather enemy intel, protect your own with the bodyguard, and strike down the opponent's target with a surprise attack from your sniper to claim victory — and don't forget the ace sniper, a special unit with a deadly finishing move that can turn the tide in an instant!
artists: お風呂場なめくじ (Bathroom Slug)

description:
Each turn in Ocean Lane, reveal cards from the deck as you go. However, if you get the same number as a card you have already turned over, you will be crushed by seasickness. If you get seasick, you will lose all the rewards you were supposed to get.

However, if you can stop the voyage without getting seasick, you will get the reward, so sometimes you need to have the courage to pull out. Rewards can be used as victory points, or they can be converted to money and used to purchase cargo or to prevent seasickness. In the end, the player with the most points wins.

Fuel Loaders / 装填士 is a game of confusion, belief, and co-operation.

Each player starts with a hand of three cards, holding them so that the numbers on them face other players. You will see only the "fuel sphere" on the back of each card, but the larger the sphere, the higher the number on the opposite side.

On a turn, you (1) pull a card from your hand and offer to exchange it with a card chosen by another player, (2) exchange a card in your hand with a card on the table, or (3) reveal a card from the deck and add it to the table. Every exchange must have the new card put into the same place as the old card. Once per game, you can ask your fellow players the state of your hand, and they can respond only "Excellent" or "Good".

After each player has had 2-3 turns, the game ends, and players score their hands: 5 points for a three-of-a-kind, 3 points for a sequence, and 1 point for a pair. If the players have collectively scored enough points, they win.

You’ve always dreamed of writing a novel that captivates readers and lands on the bestseller list. Now’s your chance! In Bestseller, you’ll craft a story from the ground up, developing intriguing characters, weaving unexpected plot twists, and introducing gripping conflicts that keep readers on the edge of their seats.

In Bestseller, each card in the game represents a key storytelling element - a setting, character, plot twist, or conflict. Players will draft cards, manage their hands, and add cards to their binder, weaving together a unique and engaging story.

The core mechanic of Bestseller is binder building, a new variant of engine building.

Players use "ink" to pay for and add unique cards to their individual binders. Each turn, every visible card is active. Because pages are transparent, any blank spaces allow the card beneath to remain visible. As players turn pages to progress through their binder, the set of active cards constantly changes, revealing new abilities while covering others as new cards are added over time.

Using both tactics and strategy, players add cards to their binders, unlocking stronger actions, effects, and powerful combinations.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: LMG: World War Two Infantry Action Wargame Rules
description:
LMG is a set of basic infantry action rules for World War Two wargaming.

The rules are 7 pages long in a 12-point font.

The turn sequence is similar to the WRG 1925-1950 rules: 

     Move-countermove, 
     shoot-then-move. 


The rules are meant for low-level skirmish scenarios.

They only include weapons found at squad or platoon level.

—description from the designer

Do robots dream of electric sheep after a long day of manufacturing widgets and thingamabobs? Or do they sleep at all? Maybe they break off into teams and hop into the machine to blow off a little steam with a race for anodic glory and a chance at upgrading themselves into more skilled bots.

Into the Machine is a highly tactical race in which circle-bots race to one end of the machine to tag in their hex-bot teammates who race in reverse on the same track to win. A pit crew of bots outside the machine are sent to work the terminals, moving and manipulating their teammates in the machine.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Die Maus: Raus aus der Mausefalle
description:
Raus aus der Mausefalle is a simple card placement game for children that features the main character of a popular German children's TV program, "Die Sendung mit der Maus" (The Program with the Mouse), which first aired in 1971.

Players start with five cards each and in each turn draw another card before it is required to play at least a single colored story card in proper order (from 1 to 5) in a common area. If this is not possible a die is rolled which allows depending on the value further card drawings and finally a card has to be discarded.

If a fixed number of complete stories are put on the common area the game is won. A story is characterized by a color and cards with values from 1 to 5.

There exists also joker cards which can be played instead of the proper color card. This joker can be obtained by replacing later with the card it replaces. Another card type, mouse traps, exist and require the player to skip his turn until a special side of the die is rolled within 3 attempts per round.

This game shares its designer with Mausefalle (1980) and re-implements it.
family: TV Shows: Die Sendung mit der Maus

description:
はちゅっと！メイクテン (Hachutto! Make10) is a game from Japan.

In this game, all players reveal a number simultaneously. Using four of the revealed numbers, they compete to form 10 as quickly as possible. 
publisher's summary

Based upon a popular game played in Japan on journeys, players compete to make a total of exactly 10 from 4 of the played cards, using the basic operands + - / *. The cards are numbered 1,2,3,5, and 7, however the 7s come with their own operand which must be used correctly (and only one 7 can be in the formula).

The players are dealt a hand of cards, and each selects one, which are then played simultaneously, at the shout 'Hachutto!'. Out of the played cards, it is a race to declare 'Make 10!'. If the player can correctly compute 10 from four of the cards, they win their card as a point (or 3pts if it is a 7). When the hands are exhausted, most points collected wins the game.
user summary

boardgame: 絵獣コレクション (Kaiju Collection)
description:
絵獣コレクション (Kaiju Collection) is a card game set in a  mysterious world where the power of art comes to life, players become “Kaigaja” heroes and work to seal the rampaging “Eju” (Art Beasts). It is a "friendship x bargaining" card game.

The goal of the game is to collect the most points by successfully sealing powerful Eju cards.

How to Play
    1.    Each player chooses one Eju card to play and simultaneously points to the left, right, or center.
    2.    If two players point at each other and their cards share the same type or color, they seal them together (Joint Seal). If not, both cards are lost.
    3.    If only one side points, the player seals their card alone (Solo Seal).
    4.    If a player chooses the center and no one points at them, they succeed in a Private Seal. If someone points at them, they fail, and the pointer seals their own card instead.
    5.    After 6 rounds, the player with the highest total score from sealed cards in their left and right zones, plus any private seals, wins the game.

--description from publisher

Everyone sees color differently — and says it their own way!

Not Easy to Color-rectly Guess! is a playful and feel-good game in which mismatched color vibes bring laughs. Why? People from different backgrounds often imagine colors in different ways. Ever been surprised by someone’s color choices?
Or walked into IKEA and thought, "Wow, these colors feel so...Scandinavian"?

Through this game, you can connect with people from different backgrounds, discover surprising perspectives, and deepen mutual understanding.
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG
publishers: ゆほつげたゲームズ (Yuhotsugeta Games)

description:
Die On This Hill is a party game about making, refuting, and reacting to the hottest takes anybody has ever seen. It’s like social media, but, y’know, fun.

Each player is dealt a hand of "debate" cards with various things on them, ranging from "A Sandwich" to "Gen Z" to "A Giant Mech" to "Forcing Your Child To Open A Can Of Beans". Players use those cards to form an argument using a "Hill" card that contains a connecting phrase like "Is", "Is Not", or "Is Unable To Stop The Crushing Tide Of". Once the active player creates the phrase, the other players scramble to play their Let Me Cook card to become the devil's advocate: the person trying to argue *against* the phrase the active player created.

Both players get 45 seconds to argue their side, while other players react in real time to the debate by tossing different emoji tokens at them based on how the argument makes them feel. At the end of both debates, the person with the most emoji tokens in their tray wins, and takes the hill card for the round. Oh, and it doesn't matter whether the emotions implied by the emoji tokens are positive or negative. Social media rule #1: Any engagement is good engagement. But wait: you, as a player, can sabotage the person making the argument! If you have a hill card, you can wager it to augment another player's argument, forcing them to argue in favor of an argument that you chose.

Play continues until one player collects 5 hill cards, at which point they immediately have to go outside and touch grass.

—description from the designer

Giddy Up, UNICORN! is a fast-paced, chaos-fueled card game where cute turns deadly and glitter meets madness. Designed for 2–5 players, it combines set collection, "take that" mechanics, and absurd humor in a world where unicorns have gone rogue.

Each turn, players draw and play cards from a 65-card deck filled with magical fruits, annoying shrooms, collectible unicorns, deadly nemesis attacks, and glitter-fueled power-ups. Block attacks, trigger chain reactions, and outsmart your opponents — or die dramatically.

Survive the glitterpocalypse, unleash chaos, and collect the cute unicorns ever drawn. With easy-to-learn rules, strategic combos, and artwork that blends adorable with deranged, Giddy Up, UNICORN! is perfect for fans of games like Exploding Kittens or Unstable Unicorns... but with a darker twist.

Do you have what it takes to be the last unicorn standing?

—description from the publisher

Hexeh is a fast-paced, two-player strategy game played on a compact hexagonal board. Each player commands a team of five distinct piece types, each with its own movement rules and tactical identity. From ricochet attacks to color-based movement paths, every turn offers opportunities for clever setups, surprise plays, and momentum shifts. The goal is simple: outmaneuver your opponent and capture their Empress.

Games last around 10 minutes, making Hexeh perfect for spontaneous rematches, tournament play, or quick showdowns at game cafés and breweries. It’s easy to learn but rewards mastery, offering endless depth in a short, satisfying play window.

Vora is a fast-paced, spatial game where players push and slide wooden cubes to form animal lines and collect points. Set in a vibrant pasture, each cube represents a different creature with its own unique ability: wolves scare others, ducks fly over water and grass, sheep graze grass, and so on.

Players take turns choosing cubes from their farm and pushing them into the shared meadow to manipulate the layout, trigger combos, and score efficient patterns. Every move matters — the pradera is alive and constantly shifting.

The game is quick to teach but offers surprising depth through tactical positioning and chain reactions. It's equally suited for casual players and competitive groups, and plays great across all player counts.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: World War II on the Western Front
description:
World War II on the Western Front is a game of the lightening fast Blitzkrieg that shattered France in 1940, to the massive Allied push from the beaches of Normandy in 1944, the Western Front was a battlefield of strategy, sacrifice, and high stakes.

Take command of German or Allied forces in this dynamic, turn based strategy game where every choice influences the outcome of the war. Can Germany shatter the Allies in France and threaten the United Kingdom itself in 1940? In 1944, will the Allies break out from the beaches and start their relentless drive across France and into the German heartland? High stakes require bold decisions!

Outwit your enemies, manage your resources, and rewrite the story of the West in the ultimate World War II showdown!

—description from the publisher

This game comes with its own rules & game mechanics, map, and components, but consider this to be an unofficial expansion to Axis & Allies.  It includes rules for terrain, such as forests, mountains, rivers, and beaches.  Game mechanics allow for rapid exploitation and reinforcement.  The optional event cards included add unpredicatble events, such as storms, civil unrest, and political interference.

Two scenarios are included in the game:

The Battle of France, May 10th, 1940. The Germans are about to launch their long awaited invasion of France and the Low Countries. The French, British and Belgian armies are positioned north of an expected German attack, but the Germans have their forces ready to smash through the Ardennes forest. As the Allied player, you will need to quickly reposition your forces to blunt the German attacks, and maybe even push them back to Germany. As the German player, you have the initial advantage of the Allies being in the wrong place, but you will need to be fast to reach Paris before the Allies regroup.

D-Day to the Rhine, June 6th, 1944. The Allies land in France. Will the Germans contain the Allied breakout, or are the Germans better to defend behind the Siegfried Line? As the Allies, you will enjoy numerical superiority in planes, tanks, and men. But as you drive deeper into France and then into Germany, you will find that keeping your armies supplied is a real challenge. The German player will need to either contain the Allies, or at least slow the Allied breakout and advance to keep them out of Germany. Taking advantage of the long Allied supply lines and bad weather will be crucial ...

What is included? The components found in the base set are:
- 1 18x27 mounted folding game map
- 1 41 page fully illustrated colour rulebook with index
- 1 double sided battleboard for land and sea combat
- 4 double sided set-up charts & player’s aid
- 30 event cards for The Battle of France
- 30 event cards for D-Day to the Rhine
- 3 Off Board Cards

In the deluxe set, the following is also included:
-Control markers for France, Germany, UK, and US
- Fortress tokens
- Beachhead markers
- Supply tokens
- Strategic Damage markers
- Entrenched markers
- Mulberry Harbour token
- Airfield tokens
- Flooded markers
- mine tokens
- tokens for airborne, trucks, SS tanks, SS mechanized infantry, jets, and air transports
- weather markers
- 6 Dice
- 3 storage bags

https://www.thegamecrafter.com/games/world-war-ii-on-the-western-front-deluxe-set

In addition the the components provided in this game, you will need to supply the following in order to play:

- Miniatures units for France, UK, US, and Germany
- Coloured chips

These miniatures can be found in many popular WW2 games such as Axis & Allies. Specifically, Axis & Allies 1940: Europe

Bone-Zai 5 - The exciting, next-level, high-scoring Dice Game filled with risk and reward. 1 or more players 12 and up. 5 uniquely-sided, colorful dice (D6-D16) 14 FUN New, Challenging categories will appeal to the die-hard "Yacht" lovers!

Included in the game is a D6, D8, D10, D12 and a D16.

On your turn, roll the dice up to three times keeping the values you want each time. There are multiple categories to which you can apply your dice to score. You don't have to apply your dice to a score category before you've finalized your rolls.

Categories include:

Odds - score only odd numbers. If all dice are odd score a bonus of 20 points!
Evens - score only even numbers. If all dice are even score a bonus of 20 points!

Prime numbers - Score only prime numbers. If all dice are prime numbers score a bonus of 25 points!
Starts with "T" - Score numbers that start with T. If all dice apply score 30 bonus points!

Perfect Squares (1, 4, 9, 16) - If all dice are perfect squares score 50 bonus points!

There are also categories for multiples of 3, 4, and 5, dice sequences, and 3, 4, 5 of a kind.

When players have attempted all 14 category boxes players tally their total score and the highest score wins!

Score higher than your opponents by creating words using two rolls of the five unique 'letter dice.' You'll have one minute to utilize the drafted dice to make as many unique words as you can. Good luck!

Gethsemane is a game of social deduction and conversation that thrusts players (from 6 to 63) into the dramatic 24-hour period preceding Jesus’ death on the cross. The game play follows the events and characters of the Easter story as they are described in the gospels. Gethsemane is meant to be played for both pure entertainment and educational purposes, cleverly unpacking the themes and nuances of the Easter story, as well as the dilemmas faced by its characters. 
During the game you will meet Jesus Christ, who was revolutionizing the Jewish faith, and his loyal disciples. You will also meet the High Priest Caiaphas, who considered Jesus and his followers a threat to the established society, thus tempting the disciple Judas to betray Jesus in Gethsemane. In Gethsemane the players are assigned a role from among the key characters of the New Testament account, and they will either be on Jesus’ or Caiaphas’ team. The roles are secret, and only by way of argument, discussion, deception and cunning will players survive and win the game.

—description from the designer

A fast-paced, card-driven game of paradoxes, prediction, and quantum mischief.

In McGuffin: Time Travel Version, players compete to outwit one another across twisted timelines using an identical set of cards — and one mysterious McGuffin whose true nature is hidden until the moment of revelation. Will it bring glory... or disaster?

Each round centres on the McGuffin Card, secretly loaded with either a Stable Timeline (+2 points) or a Paradox (–1 point). Players take turns playing cards that manipulate the McGuffin’s position, interrupt plays with temporal tricks, or unleash legendary devices like the H.G. Wells Time Machine, Professor When’s Time Machine, or the Philadelphia Experiment.

As the McGuffin shuffles between players, you'll need to balance strategic movement, well-timed discards, and bold prediction. Should you risk opening the McGuffin? Or hold on and hope its contents favour you?

For those seeking deeper strategy, the Timeline Prediction variant allows players to secretly wager on the McGuffin’s true identity — with points to gain or lose.

Simple to learn, rich in bluffing and timing, McGuffin: Time Travel Version is a clever blend of deduction, deception, and sci-fi chaos. It’s not just about what's in the box — it’s about when and whether you dare to open it.

—description from the designer

Sandworms ahoy! The big Sandworm Race is about to begin! The crowd cheers as the sandworm teams speed along the racetrack to
reach the reef as quickly as they can. But beware, if you don’t take cover in your hole in time, the tide will wash you back to the beginning.
The winner is the first player to use clever tactics and a bit of luck to get all their sandworms to the finish line.

Fun and playful 3D design brings a real beach atmosphere to the game.

—description from the publisher

Lina: “Listen and be amazed! Slayers has a TCG now!”
Amelia: "Oooh! That world’s strongest, unbeatable, survival hobby that even the neighborhood elderly women regard highly, that TCG right!"

Slayers Fight (Japanese: スレイヤーズふぁいと Romaji: sureiyāzu faito) was a trading card game (or TCG). Cards are categorized as basic, common, uncommon, rare, and special. Players collect cards and build decks out of their collection.

Players build a deck of cards and duel against an opponent's deck. Players are wizards attempting to reduce their opponent's life total to zero. The first player to score 10 or more points (or meet another set win condition) wins the game. Points are earned by fullfilling your clients needs, deveating your opponent's characters or attacking their client.

An important part of the game is deck construction, which is done prior to the actual game by selecting what cards are included in a particular deck. There are over 452 different cards from which to build your deck!

Cards can be clients, which usually generates resources of various types, while characters and events require a certain amount of resources to be used. Incantation cards require characters with enough magical aptitude to chant their incantations before they can be resolved. Attack support cards can be utilized by characters with certain characteristics. Some cards (clients, characters, and subcategories of the other card types) stay on the board and continue to affect the game, while others have a one-time effect.

Players start with their chosen client and randomly draw cards to see what they get and can play each turn. Although this limits your choices, there is a lot of strategy in how you play those cards. A robust list of game mechanics, including intricate rules for reactive card play provide for rich tactics and tough choices each turn.

Though traditionally a two-player duel, there are casual formats that allow more players to play.
artists: あらいずみ るい (Rui Araizumi)

description:
This game calls for brigth minds!
Players have to complete 7 task cards with increasing degrees of difficulty, one after the other. Who will be quickest to uncover the lucky animals they’re looking for? 
A rainbow grows as each task card is completed.

The first player to master all 7 phases and complete their own rainbow wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Eternal Kingdom – Rise & Fall is a dark medieval strategy board game offering both cooperative and competitive gameplay. Set in a rich fantasy realm, this immersive adventure blends asymmetric roles, kingdom-building, betrayal, and tactical decision-making.

The game features nine different map configurations, allowing for fast-paced skirmishes or epic, multi-hour campaigns. With modular boards, asymmetrical factions, and deep decision-making, each playthrough offers a unique path to victory.

As a player, you are responsible for your own kingdom or village. When it's your turn, you draw a Quest Card, which gives you a task. Sometimes, you will need to travel to another kingdom or village to collect something that your own kingdom or village needs.

To complete your journey, you must equip yourself with the right cards (Battle, Magic, Currency, or Provision) and pick up more cards along the way.

—description from the designer

Abbates Bibliotheca – Expand your monastery with knowledge!
This first expansion for Abbates adds a strategic twist in the fourth round. Discover the library, collect extra Cloister Garden cards, and expand your Abbot Portrait more cleverly. Choose your position freely, but sacrifice a Benedictus card for this powerful bonus. More choices, more tactics – perfect for those who want to take Abbates to the next level!

The expansion comes in the form of a stylish postcard – perfect for playing and for sending to friends who also love board games. A fun surprise in the mailbox for every Abbates fan!

The Galaxolotls had a great time on Earth! But now it’s time for them to return to their home planet. However, as soon as they land, the old rivalry between the two big Galaxolotl families rears its head. Who arrived there first? And who will claim the best spots?
Exciting thrills and risky decisions are guaranteed!
The player with the most star points at the end wins the game!


     Thrills through exciting risk-taking decisions
     Simple rules, clever tactics
     Features hilariously alien Axolotl cards


—description from the designer

Intruders have been breaking into your mansion and making off with your treasures.  So, you've decided to hide Mimics - monsters who can disguise themselves as objects - in the mansion to surprise your unwanted guests.

In Mimic Mansion, each player places three cards every round. A set number of gem tokens are distributed among the rooms, and then the player to your right chooses one of your placed cards. They receive the gems in that room but also take mental damage based on the card placed.

Gems acquired can be used to purchase new cards or donated to gain victory points. This process is repeated for 8 rounds.

At the end of the game, players total their remaining mental strength, victory points from donations, and mission points earned by dealing damage to the player on their right. The player with the highest total wins.

HOME is a bone-chilling paranormal investigation game that fits in a mint tin!

The house stands silent, but you are not alone. As a paranormal investigator, you've come to uncover the truth. Identify the ghost, withstand the terror, and cleanse the house of its haunting. Keep your wits sharp, your flashlight steady, and pray you make it out alive.

In HOME, you assume the role of a paranormal investigator, tasked with cleansing a haunted house of its ghostly inhabitant.

Armed with your ghost hunting equipment and a flashlight, you make your way through the dark house, room by room, discovering items, clues, and evidence in order to identify, locate, and expel the ghost. You must be cautious as your flashlight batteries drain quickly, paranormal events test your sanity, and deadly hunts force you to run and hide as the ghost chases you throughout the house.

Once you have gathered enough evidence, found the unique mementos tied to the ghost's former living self, and discovered the room that the ghost is haunting, you must perform a cleansing ritual to rid the house of the apparition once and for all!

—description from the designer

The carnivorous plants are hungry – verrry hungry! So try your best to grow quickly and snap up as many yummy flies as you can. Attract the buzzing tasty treats with your scent and catch them in your traps. 
But beware! Any flies that escape will cost you both points and patience.

The player who’s eaten the most flies while letting the fewest get away wins the game!

A happy snappy arranging game.

     Arranging game for clever and greedy minds
     Score points with 50 wooden flies
     The longer the game, the more beautiful the plants


—description from the publisher

A promotion is on the line, but so is your sanity in Burnout, a chaotic strategic party game.

Burnout drops players into a familiar battlefield: the modern office. Over six relentless weeks, you’ll juggle Projects, navigate company politics, and sabotage or support your coworkers.

But the real challenge? Managing your Mental Health. Push too hard, and you risk burning out. The line between high performance and total collapse is paper-thin, just like in real life.

The player with the highest Reputation wins the promotion. But you’ll need to balance ambition, sabotage, chaos and strategy to make it through the grind.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Gisei (fan expansion for The White Castle)
description:
As your clan strives to gain favor with the Sakai clan it becomes clear that securing your future in the court of the White Heron will require significant personal sacrifices.

Gisei (犠牲, sacrifice) is a fan made expansion for The White Castle. This expansion explores the theme of making a personal sacrifice in order to achieve a greater goal.  It adds 5 stone garden, 5 plant garden, and 2 third floor castle cards. This expansion can be used alongside the Matcha expansion. Standard setup is to mix this expansion with the existing cards.

—description from the designer

Welcome to the world of The King of Antiques! In this game, you will take on the role of a professional antique shop owner, earning profits by trading and appraising antiques. Not only will you manage your own antique shop to attract discerning collectors, but you'll also appraise items for the town bank, curate collections, and host auctions.

Will you use your silver tongue to ignite the desire of potential buyers? Or will you drive up the auction price just to frustrate them? The decision to sell at a high price or secretly hoard stash away treasures depend entirely on your auctioneering skills. You can also activate unique abilities of various antiques, forming powerful auction combos to outsmart wealthy bidders and even disrupt the grand plans of other players, leaving them in regret. Successfully selling antiques to collectors will earn their introductions to better-quality antique sources, skyrocketing your profits! Ultimately, you can transform your shop into a legendary antique shop known to all! Join this medium-light strategy game full of management and auction tactics and compete to become the ultimate antique auction master!

Objective of the Game:

The King of Antiques is a strategic auction game designed for 3 to 4 players. Players can earn profits by buying and selling various antique items through bidding while using their eloquence to host auctions and influence the outcomes. Players can also utilize shop skills and combine them with the unique effects of different item cards to create diverse money-making combos. You can even interfere with the trading plans of other players. Who can earn the most money in seven rounds to claim victory?

—description from the publisher

Escape from Labirynth is a roll-and-write adventure for any number of players. This game is in print and play format. In Escape the Labyrinth you're the fearless leader of six daring heroes, they are all racing to escape a twisted, trap-filled labyrinth. Your mission? Guide your heroes to the exit as cleverly and quickly as possible - and whatever you do, don’t leave anyone behind.

Each turn, you’ll draw a tile onto your game sheet, adding new corridors to the maze. Every path will connect to another - so watch where you place your pieces! One wrong move can block the way or send your heroes spinning in circles forever. Your escape skills will be put to the test - and yes, you'll be judged! So plan smart, draw tiles, and aim for the top score.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 街コロ (新装版) (Machi Koro: Revised Edition)
description:
街コロ (新装版) (Machi Koro: Revised Edition) features the same gameplay as the original Machi Koro, with each player developing their own city and trying to be the first to build all of their landmarks.
