This new edition supports up to five players instead of only up to four, with the card balance being slightly adjusted and the card design and size being updated — which makes it incompatible with previous Machi Koro titles and expansions.
DIVERHOUSE is a card placement game aimed at creating diverse homes.

Each turn in CROWNSWORD, everyone chooses and plays a card from their hand at the same time. The card with the lowest number played takes effect, but if two or more cards are the same, their effect cannot be used.

A player wins if they can use the effect of the sword (No.1) or the crown (No.6).

A blast for families and parties. Challenge each other with 15-second tasks, strategically use special cards, and race to be the first to run out of Challenge Cards to win.

How to play: 1. Select and read one of your challenge cards to an opponent of your choosing, then start the 15-second timer. 2. If the opponent fails the challenge, they must draw a card. - Challenger never draws a card. - Be sure to discard used challenge cards. 3. It’s now the opponent’s turn.


     Fun for all ages (even teenagers)
     Family / Party Game
     2-8 Players
     15-Second timer included
     Stacking blocks included
     72-card deck included


—description from the publisher

RECOLLECT POKER is pretty much what the title tells you: poker with a memory element.

To set up, lay out the thirty cards face down. On a turn, reveal a card from the table; if it has a "dead" icon, your turn ends immediately, and if it has another icon, carry out that action. If you're not dead, repeat until four cards are face up. Either flip those cards face down to end your turn or collect four face-up cards. In the latter case, each player has one final turn, then whoever has the best poker hand wins.

The rules suggest playing 3-5 rounds and keeping points, with the best hand in a round earning its holder 3 points and the secondbest hand worth 1 point.

In irozato / 色里, each player becomes a wealthy merchant in the Edo period. The goal is to buy the freedom of a top-ranking courtesan called a Tayu. The player is tasked with creating a fine kimono and meeting the Tayu repeatedly.

Deep in the heart of the jungle lies a kingdom ruled by a cunning lion king. The carnivorous citizens are fed up with his tyrannical rule. Sensing the growing unrest, the king plots to embezzle the meat from the royal treasury and flee the country...but the subordinates won't stand by quietly. Some plan rebellion, others aim to assassinate the king, and a few pretend loyalty in hopes of a handsome reward.

Law of the Jungle is an asymmetrical bluffing and deduction game in which one player becomes the king and the others act as subordinates. The game lasts five rounds. The king's goal is to survive and ultimately escape with all the meat in the treasury. The subordinates use cards like "Assassination", "Rebellion", "Guard", and "Labor" to co operate — or betray — one another and gain the upper hand.

Will you outwit your rivals and escape with the loot, or seize the throne for yourself? In this game of strategy and deception, the one who ends up with the most meat claims victory.

boardgame: Star Trek: Away Missions – Klingon & Romulan Board Expansion
description:
Upgrade your games to 3 or 4 players with brand new Mission Boards that see you beam over to Klingon and Romulan Warbirds. Will you take on Sela, Gowron and the Duras Sisters in their own backyard? Or will you have all four Federation Away Teams go up against each other and finally settle the Kirk vs Picard debate?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The World's End Runners: Miss Kobayashi's Dragon Maid Board Game
description:
The World's End Runners: Miss Kobayashi's Dragon Maid Board Game is a race game in which each player controls multiple pieces, and the earlier your piece finishes the race, the more points you earn. You can jump over other players' pieces, so creating a line with others' pieces and leaping past them is the key to victory!

Your final score is the total of all your pieces' placements.

boardgame: 有限探偵ミネルバ (Infinity Detective Minerva)
description: In 有限探偵ミネルバ (Infinity Detective Minerva), you and your fellow player will read one another stories, share them, and solve mysteries.
publishers: あひるのソワレ (Ahiru no soiree)

description:
In Dice Poker Duel, each player has their own player sheet with a 5x5 grid and a trash can. On a turn, the active player rolls three dice, then keeps one of them, while the other player takes the other two. Both players then write each of their numbers into an empty space in their grid (or in the trash).

You're trying to create strong poker hands in each row and column and along one diagonal. When you complete a line, circle the letter at the end of the row, then tell your opponent which type of poker hand you completed — straight, full house, etc. — but not the strength of the hand. If you complete adjacent lines, circle the "thumbs up" icon between the letters.

When all spaces are filled, compare the poker hands in each line, with the higher hand earning 3 points. Score 1 point for each circled letter or thumbs up icon. Whoever has the higher score wins.

This is a 2 player paper pen game. There are 5x5 squares on the sheet hidden by each other's tapes. Players fill in the die rolls on their sheets and place them in each row at the end of the game for a poker game.
You cannot see how your opponent is filling in any of the rows. However, the early fill bonus is a hint. If you fill each row quickly, you get bonus points. In return, you have to tell your opponent what the rows are filled with. You can either give the information to your opponent and get bonus points, or you can carefully fill in the rows and win the poker game...
publishers: ぶれけけゲームズ (Brekeke Games)

description:
In Marlo’s Holiday, Princess Marlo has fled her kingdom and is now hiding in the city, seeking freedom.

To play, you talk with the AI-powered Princess Marlo using your voice, as well as the given topic and keyword cards to guide the conversation. Win Princess Marlo's favor by making her enjoy your conversation.

boardgame: Roll Player Adventures: Silver Threads of Fate
description:
As fledgling members of the Starlit Door, your party is tasked with a novice's initiation quest on the eve of their ambush on Taron's Arena in the city of Sabek. The oracle of the Starlit Door foretold your fate: While other members retrieve the Eye of Ulos from its temple, your mission is to steal a mysterious sewing needle from a god.

Silver Threads of Fate is an all-new single chapter storybook board game expansion for Roll Player Adventures. Players will create new characters, either importing from the results of a game of Roll Player or using one of the pre-generated characters in Roll Player Adventures or one of its expansions.

This expansion introduces recruiting allies, exploring locations in a new story, and a unique finale puzzle.

There's no specific time to play it, but you'll get a little more out of the narrative if you've played through at least Eye of Ulos (ADV4)

Silver Threads of Fate requires the base game of Roll Player Adventures.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Roll Player Adventures: Lost Characters Pack
description:
The Lost Character Pack includes twelve all-new pre-generated character cards for use with Roll Player Adventures and any of its expansions.

The characters in this pack were available to be named by backers for the crowdfunding campaign in 2025.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
It’s the year 1922. Your expedition has uncovered an ancient desert tomb. As you enter a large hall, the walls shake and start to slowly close in!

Fortunately, your years as an archeologist alerts you to your surroundings. You notice peculiar parchments lying about and hallowed hieroglyphs etched in a puzzle-like manner on the wall.

You stop for a second. Perhaps studying the parchments and deciphering the hieroglyphs might help you to escape the tomb … before it becomes yours.

There are 2 rotating phases: The Study and Decipher Phases.

In the Study Phase, your goal is to use the cards in your hand to obtain lots of Parchments to prepare for the next phase. But don’t forget to also discard a few cards to reveal more Hieroglyphs!

In the Decipher Phase, your goal is to use your Parchments to decipher Hieroglyphs which are then scored at the end of the game. Hieroglyphs come in two types: symbols and values. You need both types in order to score!

Work your way through 4 rounds to try and get a high score and find out your fate. Will you escape the tomb ... or be entombed?

—description from the designer
SuperBug!!! is a card battle game in which you use antibiotics to defeat microorganisms. However, strong drugs cause them to develop resistance, making them uncontrollable.

boardgame: ビストロヴァンパイア (The Vampire's Bistro)
description:
You're in a bustling bistro on a mysterious night where chefs must work together — while a vampire hides among them.

Your goal in ビストロヴァンパイア (The Vampire's Bistro) is to cook five dishes as a team while identifying and eliminating the hidden vampire before too many dishes fail.

To set up, each player is secretly assigned one of three roles. Each round, use ingredient cards to cook a five-course meal co-operatively. Garlic cards can substitute for any ingredient, but vampires can't use garlic!

After cooking, players debate and vote to eliminate someone. If the team cooks three failed dishes, everyone loses. Who do you trust in the kitchen?
In Philosopher's Ant, players compete to see how efficiently they can produce ants as resources by combining machine tiles in a world of anteaters with advanced science.

In the trick-taking game Oment, players are each secretly a human or a demon, and the scoring of white "holy" cards and black "evil" cards is reversed for the two groups.

Special cards include the "Lightning (1 & 13)" card, which is the strongest but must be passed to other players, the "Hellhound (6)" and "Rosary (8)" cards that double their points as they accumulate, and the "Mirror (7)" card allowing players to exchange their identity card with a designated player. These cards with unique abilities provide a varied and enjoyable gameplay experience.
BACON and EGGS is a drafting, set-collection, and racing game starring eggs on a frying pan.

Bakamon!: Version Alu is a card game set in a parody of Japanese manga.

Play as fantastic creature trainers and duel against each other! Surround them all and beat the crap out of the opposing trainer using the game's 24 cards.

Enjoy the humor of the French parodic comic strip created by Juliette Fournier and Jean-Gaël Deschard, in this fast-paced card game.

What are Micro Games?

Micro Game is a range of portfolio games developed by Matagot. The games are taken from the catalog of publisher Button Shy Games or are exclusive new games. This collection of small games includes a large number of titles based on different universes and mechanics.

However, they all share the same characteristics: short games, high replayability and clean, minimalist mechanics. The Micro Game range includes single-player, two-player, multiplayer and cooperative games.

Tengi is a shogi-styled game with cards as pieces, and themed after angels.

At the start of the game, you use 10 points to buy units, before deploying them onto the field. Each player chooses one of these is to be their King, but in secret. The goal is to be the first to capture the opposing unit chosen as the King.

If the piece you capture turns out to be the one your opponent selected as the King at the start of the game, you win. If your secret King unit reaches the promotional zone, you may also choose to promote it into the King, which gives it more movement options, but outs itself as the target to capture.
publishers: ししゃもゲームズ (Shishamo Games)

description:
Bakamon!: Version Laiton is a card game set in a parody of Japanese manga.

Play as fantastic creature trainers and duel against each other! Surround them all and beat the crap out of the opposing trainer using the game's 24 cards.

Enjoy the humor of the French parodic comic strip created by Juliette Fournier and Jean-Gaël Deschard, in this fast-paced card game.

What are Micro Games?

Micro Game is a range of portfolio games developed by Matagot. The games are taken from the catalog of publisher Button Shy Games or are exclusive new games. This collection of small games includes a large number of titles based on different universes and mechanics.

However, they all share the same characteristics: short games, high replayability and clean, minimalist mechanics. The Micro Game range includes single-player, two-player, multiplayer and cooperative games.

Meeresrauschen is a tactical card game for 2–5 players, ages 8 and up, where players compete to collect and arrange a unique row of nine marine animal cards. Each animal has real-life traits that influence gameplay. Strategic use of action cards allows players to disrupt opponents and protect their own progress. With quick setup, simple rules, and language-independent components, Meeresrauschen offers a fun and educational experience for families and casual gamers alike.

Discover the fascinating abilities of sea creatures with Meeresrauschen. Collect stonefish, octopuses, northern gannets and more, and place them in your row according to their characteristics. Use the action cards cleverly to outsmart the others. Who will be the first to lay a complete row of 9 unique animals and win the game?

     YOUR GAME: Discover the exciting party game ‘Meeresrauschen – Kartenwirbel unter Wasser’ (Roaring Sea – Cards Whirling Underwater). Lay an open row of 9 animal cards and win the game!
     GREAT FUN – for children, adults, friends and family. The card game is ideal for short games in between or when playing for points for a longer game night.
     LEARNING THROUGH PLAY: The characteristics and abilities of the animals in the game are real. You learn something new while playing. If you like, you can find out more about the animals via a link in the game.
     QUICK GAME START: Simple game principle! For 2-5 players, ages 10 and up. Short game instructions with lots of pictures. The game is language-neutral; there are no words on the cards.


—description from the publisher (translated)

Explore The New World in an Age of Discovery!

Ideal for both casual and experienced gamers, with convenient and flexible play times. A mid-weighted game suitable for Family, Friends, Frenemies & Geeks.

"In the Age of Sail your Navigators have been tasked with the challenge of discovering new lands & trade routes beyond the known horizon.

Sail into the unknown... Find exotic new treasures that will be the envy of your adversaries.

Rumour has it that the sumptuous riches of The Indies can be found east or west, the choice my friend is yours... But can you build a global trade Empire to withstand the test of time?

Good luck!"

Explore, Build, Trade and Defend in the world of Empire The Boardgame. Utilise all of your cunning in this grand game of global chess that is easy to play, yet endless to master. Explore the world first for bonuses and settle your Ports for resources, but be sure to avoid the pirates along the way! Players collect money from their markets and conduct trade & purchases before moving their Ships around the world. Build Strongholds to defend your prized assets, stock up on Empire Cards to both defend your Monopolies and shift the balance of power with other players. The winner of the game is the player which advances farthest along the Monopoly tracker to become the most famous and successful in this new modern strategy classic.

—description from the designer

This contains 3 add-ons. Add 1, 2, or all 3 to the SCENERY base game!

LIGHT:

     Setup: Players place a Sun at the geographic South of their play area.
     Endscore: 1SP per tile which is directly behind a lower height tile, in the direction of the sunshine. -1SP for the opposite.


LOOKS:

     Setup: Randomly draw a number of tripods based on player count.
     Gameplay: Each tripod has a combination of 1, 2 or 3 vegetation zones. If an intersection of 3 land tiles matches the tripod tile, place the tripod at that intersection.
     Endscore: Points are shown on the tripod.


LAKES:

     Gameplay: Place a lake on a tile if it has at least 4 higher tiles adjacent.
     Endscore: Add up your lakes' heigths. Compare the sum to other players.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Astro Knights: Velocity Revolver Promo Card
description:
Originally included with the Man Vs. Meeple Season 5 Gamefound campaign.

New Weapon card.

ATTACK: Any ally draws a card.  Then, deal damage equal to the number of cards in that player's hand.

boardgame: Books of Time: Man Vs. Meeple Season 5 Promo Cards
description:
Originally included with the Man Vs. Meeple Season 5 Gamefound campaign.

4 card promo set featuring two cards each of David and Ryan.

CH1: Front ability: Pay 1 File to Remove 1 to remove the top Objective file from Objective stack of your choice.
     Back Ability: Gain 1 File.

CH2: Front ability: Pay 1 Pen to turn 3 pages of a book.
     Back ability: Gain 1 Pen and 1 Point.

CH3: Front ability: Turn 2 pages of a book.
     Back ability: Take one page from the offer and add it to your player board following normal rules.

CH4: Front ability: Pay 2 points to gain 1 File and 1 Pen.
     Back ability: Turn 1 page of a book.

boardgame: Resafa: Man Vs. Meeple 2024 Promo Card
description:
Originally included with the Man Vs. Meeple Season 5 Gamefound campaign.

New sack card.

Pay 1 Coin and 1 Stone for 2 Marbles

boardgame: Earth: Blushing Emily Chrysanthemum Promo Card
description:
Originally included with the Man Vs. Meeple Season 5 Gamefound campaign.

Costs 4 Soil and worth 2 points.

Gain 3 sprouts when planting the card.

Watering action: Discard 3 sprouts for 1 growth.

This game will put your music knowledge to a serious test. You must identify an iconic artist by only a costume and match them with lyrics from one of their most recognizable songs. Nobody will blame you if you victoriously sing in your best “in the shower” voice after winning.

—description from the back of the box

Step into the cockpit of airline strategy with Low Cost Airline Manager, a fast-paced, competitive board game that puts you in control of a budget airline.

Plan Routes. Price Smart. Maximize Profit.
Each round simulates a month in aviation. Players compete to carry passengers between airports by offering the lowest fare — just like in real-world revenue management. But there’s a twist: every flight you operate uses up valuable aircraft capacity, and the network resets with the seasons.

Designed with IATA codes, real-world connectivity rules, and seasonal planning, it’s more than a game — it’s a playful way to explore route strategy, network planning, and pricing dynamics in the aviation world.

Whether you’re an aviation pro or just fascinated by airline operations, Low Cost Airline Manager is your runway to strategic fun.

—description from the publisher

Tell stories! What happened while Bobby Bear was hibernating!

In the first round, players draw an item tile. After showing it to everyone face-up, they place it face-down in ANY empty location on the board, telling a story that involves that item and that place.

Once every location has a tile, players begin rolling the die and moving the "Bobby bear" pawn. Wherever the pawn lands, the player must re-tell the story and recall what item is there. They reveal the item - if they got it wrong, they replace it; but if they got it right, they take the tile for points, and draw a new one from the stack. Then they must come up with a story that relates the NEW item to this location and place it face-down.

Once the tiles run out, continue play until Bobby bear lands on an empty space. Then the player with the most tiles, wins.

You are a lemur.

Like all lemurs, you really want to impress Vice President of Cool, Tamarin Tamarin Price. How? By being super Trendy of course!

Set a Trend that shows you’re on the cutting edge by secretly changing what styles are hot, and press your luck to score big. Some lemurs think hats are super cool, some think ties are super cool, and some think glasses are super cool.

You? You’re going to be the supercoolest.

In this fast-paced game, players will swap cards with a face-down central flop in order to best match their hidden scoring condition. After three rounds and a bonus round, whoever has scored the highest will be deemed not just the coolest, bu the supercoolest.

—description from the designer

Scamper the Squirrel and their woodland friends are ready for race day!

Everyone's scurrying to win points in different competitions. How many can you finish before time runs out?

On your turn, flip the timer and then the top card off the stack. Do the activity on the card as fast as you can, and keep flipping cards and doing their activities until your time runs out:

     Go Home - put the 4 animal pawns in the correct locations on the creature dens board.
     Tree - stack the wooden tree pieces in the order(s) shown.
     Food Match - flip over pairs of food tiles until you find the two that match this card.
     Funny Face - make the funny face shown.
     Animal Action - do the action shown.
     Hide-and-Seek - EVERYONE races to grab the pawn for the animal NOT on the card.


There are also cards that add or subtract "leaf points", let you steal another player's card, or flip the timer.

Keep all the cards you successfully complete. At the end of two rounds, the player with the most "leaf points" on their cards is the winner!

A new version of the original game. Inspired by beautiful oceanside golf courses around the world like the iconic Pebble Beach, this is a complete stand-alone game, that comes with 28 double-sided terrain tiles in both the Family (cardboard tiles) and Pro (wooden tile) Editions.

Using the terrain tiles, you decide the layout of the course, and make it as easy or difficult as you choose. The game comes with a Tee Box and several different "holes". You complete the course by adding tiles that include roughs, sand hazards, trees, water hazards, and a cliff, as well as the fairway tiles. The game includes four balls that are basically mini-shuffleboard pieces that you must flick from tee box to hole, while trying to avoid the hazards just like in real golf.

If you do hit into the hazards, each one carries its own unique mechanism to replicate the challenges of real golf. For example, hitting into the sand means you must hit your next shot using the thumb of your non-dominant hand. Each hazard also limits the distance you can hit your next shot.

The game also includes a weather die and spinner that must be rolled before each shot to determine the wind speed and direction. Golf pro player cards, which players can choose or deal out randomly, each have a different ability players can use once per hole.

Whoever has the lowest score after the final hole wins.

TGA can be played solo, one-on-one, in teams, or as a league.

Par for the Course uses three 8-sided dice, a simple set of "Performance to Par" charts, and player ratings based on historic or current rankings of actual golfers to replay golf tournaments using stroke or match rules. The designer describes it as "a quick-playing but accurate tournament golf simulation."
Each dice roll determines a golfer's score for three holes, a design element that speeds play and allows a 60-player, 4-round tournament to be replayed in about two hours.
The game doesn't account for differences among actual courses, but does include separate performance charts for poor, normal, or perfect playing conditions. The game includes ratings for many "all-time great" golfers, male and female, as well as a chart that allows the user to rate players themselves, by converting current World or European rankings into player ratings.
The game does not involve decisions by the game player; it is observational, allowing the player to watch how the drama of a tournament develops.

boardgame: Massive Darkness 2: Table Leg Promo Card
description:
Table Leg
Hand
Attack: You may count any Suns you rol as Swords instead. If you roll masks, gain +2 swords
2 Swords

One of a line of very simplified mass market games issued by McDonald's as the free toy with Happy Meals in 2022.

Gameplay involves pulling out tweezer-shaped pieces from a board with the goal of getting one of each.  If you pull a duplicate, you put it back in the board.  There are no decisions beyond a  very slight memory aspect.  If you remember where certain pieces are, you can come back for them if you still need them at the end.

boardgame: 静寂または音楽 (Silence or Music)
description:
In this hauntingly poetic take on Daifugō, passing is no longer a retreat, it's a statement. Silence may grant you privilege, and music may trap you in noise. Whether you hold a powerful hand or barely a chance, every move echoes with meaning. Will you claim initiative through restraint, or roar forward into chaos? In this game, the ones who hesitate are the ones who fall.

Silence or Music is a climbing game with a twist on traditional pass mechanics. When a player passes, they gain early access to unique bonuses such as becoming the next starting player or gaining extra cards. The last player to play gains the chance to discard their weakest cards. The game invites bold plays, deceptive restraint, and ruthless punishment of half-measures.

This party game is cooperative and,  while it is mostly focused on storytelling (awkward or hilarious or epic), the rules request binge drinking from the players.

Scoutmasters, taking scout children to camping in the forest.  Every night (round) the players have to tell a scary story to their troops. They need to scare the scouts enough to keep them from wondering away from the camp during the night. The scouts have different personalities and phobias so the stories affect each scout differently. If any child goes missing during the night, a search party phase comes up before the next night's story time.

The goal of the game is to have all the scouts back in the camp after the final search party.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A card game where the players try to empty their drawpiles as fast as possible by moving cards from a pile to pile based on color, shape or number (of elements) in card.

You can play from your own piles and you have a special stolen cards pile where you can put cards that you have stolen from other players piles.

All the players play at the same time, there are no turns.

Fastest player wins.

In Legs Crossed, players communicate without words – using only leg positions! The goal is to collect sets of four matching leg poses by mimicking the exact position shown on the cards. No talking allowed – if a player speaks, they lose their turn! With hilarious physical gestures, creative expression, and multiple ways to play, Legs Crossed offers laughter and connection for players of all ages.

—description from the designer

Corporate Breakfast is a family-friendly, ridiculous, short card game with classic draw and discard mechanics, where players use word and suffix cards to create fun new breakfast cereal names, which they pitch to the other players to earn their votes. It's easy to learn and typically plays in under 20 minutes.

—description from the designer

The Straw Hat Crew and their captain Luffy set out to travel the oceans to faraway islands in search of treasure! With each clue they discover along t he way, they’re one step closer to GLORY! Choose your crew member and see which island holds the coveted treasure. In One Piece Clue, it’s up to you to determine WHO has discovered the treasure, WHAT it is, and WHERE it is hidden
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description: Neon City Runner: One die! One card! One game! Pick your hero and fight your way through Neon city. Upgrade your hero and take back your city.

Greetings, Heroes. Just print, fold, and play.

In Greeting Card Guard you face off against a powerful enemy, manage your heroes' actions, and adapt to deadly patterns in this compact yet strategic solo battle. With a flexible ruleset and unique mechanics for each encounter, every Guard presents a new challenge. Test your skill and luck—can you defeat them all?

The game requires 5 six-sided Action dice, 4 six-sided Guard dice, 2 six-sided Hero dice, 1 six-sided Hazard die, 1 eight-sided Location die, 3 tokens for Focus, Guard Health, and Guard Rage (any small markers or cubes), and 1 Dash disc.

—description from the designer

This family board game turns every night into a fast-paced culinary duel of wits. Plunge into the challenge of flipping tiles and aligning patterns as players bluff, strategize and outwit each other on the delectable grid to win big. Perfect for 2-4 players, the game dishes out flavorful and vibrant fun that keeps everyone coming back for more.


     Feast of fun and strategy provides the perfect ingredient to spice up any game night
     Engage in friendly competition as players race to match patterns, complete lines and rack up points
     Provides excitement for the whole family
     Includes 1 gameboard, 64 square tiles, 48 cards and 20 point tokens


—description from the designer

Ayakashi Harashi is a youkai-themed, shedding-type card game. It shares some similarities with Go Fish. Each player takes a card from either the player on their left or right, adding the drawn card to their hand at the corresponding edge. Players are not allowed to rearrange their hand (except when card effects allow it) and must try to form matches of cards with the same name in a row. Matched cards are discarded. If, as a result of card effects, additional cards at the edge of your hand create further matches, they may also be discarded, potentially forming chains.

The first to get rid of all their cards wins!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
publishers: ししゃもゲームズ (Shishamo Games)

description:
In Foxtrot, players take on the roles of rival commanders in a drill competition. The objective is to be the first to demonstrate the winning formation with their dice. Prior to starting the game, players must agree on which formation will serve as the victory condition:

Variety Formation: All five dice values must be different.
Unity Formation: All five dice values must be the same.
Parity Formation: All five dice values must share the same parity (all odd or all even).

On your turn, you must issue one of the following troop commands: 
Turn: Spin a troop 90 degrees in place, maintaining its top face while rotating the side faces.
Roll: Rotate a troop 90 degrees over its bottom face edge onto an orthogonally adjacent empty space.
Jump: Lift a troop over an orthogonally adjacent troop (of either team) and land it on the empty space directly on the other side, maintaining its original orientation. Optional Jump Restriction — A troop may only jump over another troop if its value is higher than the troop they’re jumping over.

—description from the designer

Kraken Knuckles is designed for waiting at restaurants, rainy-day fun, or anytime you want a 5-minute showdown with a tentacled twist. Perfect for 2 players, ages 5 and up.

One player is Hero. The other is Kraken. Hero wins if Kraken loses three life or by surviving all 7 rounds to escape. Kraken wins if Hero loses two life. Use a small token or coin to represent Hero.

Each Round you place Hero on the current round’s numbered space. Both players hide 1–4 fingers behind their backs.
Say: “1... 2... 3... Krak!” Reveal fingers at the same time:
Match: Kraken loses 1 life, poke Kraken player.
Total=5: Hero loses 1 life, play Hero player's hand.
Neither: no effect.
Slam, shout, and Krak knuckles responsibly!

—description from the designer

New Dog City is the first game in the Tigabloo series starring the eponymous Tigabloo, a tiger detective, and his fellow sleuths. In this game, they must find various stolen treasures hidden in the sewers of New Dog City while avoiding the criminal John Bulldog.

In this memory game, players must match images (à la classic memory) which represent stolen treasures of varying values. The caveat is that John Bulldog moves around the play area, and the players cannot turn tiles in the row and column in which the figure stands.

At the end of the game, the players counts the values of the treasures they found and whoever has the most valuable haul wins.

The game also comes with solo, tactical and cooperative rules.

boardgame: This War of Mine: The Board Game – Second Edition: Companions Pack
description:
A pack of four new companions with unique rules! They work similarly to the dog and cat from the 1st edition of the game.

The Chicken, The Sheep, The Donkey, The Parrot

—description by the publisher
family: Game: This War of Mine (Awaken Realms)

boardgame: This War of Mine: The Board Game – Second Edition: Expanded Incidents
description:
A scenario pack feat. 5 previously released stories—plus 1 brand-new exclusive written by Michał Oracz, never before available for purchase. Includes “Blood on the Snow,” “Epidemic,” “Refugees,” “You and Me,” “Side by Side,” and the all-new “The Escape.”

Product description
Expanded Incidents is an scenario pack add-on for players looking to deepen the narrative experience of This War of Mine: The Board Game. This collection brings together five emotionally charged scenarios, previously available only during the first Kickstarter campaign or in select retail expansions:

Blood on the Snow (from the original Incidents add-on)
Epidemic (from the original Incidents add-on)
Refugees (from the original Incidents add-on)
You and Me (from the Tales from the Ruined City expansion)
Side by Side (from the Tales from the Ruined City expansion)

To complete the set, we’re including a sixth scenario: "The Escape", a story created by Michał Oracz, based on the "Orphans of the War" content. This scenario has never been available for sale—until now.

- description by the publisher
family: Game: This War of Mine (Awaken Realms)

boardgame: This War of Mine: The Board Game – Second Edition: Memories from the Past
description:
This expansion focuses heavily on the storytelling aspect. It is a non-systematic way of checking what the chosen Character is thinking and how he or she would react to the current situation based on the Character’s background and personality.

Product description
This add-on consists of:
- A BOOK OF MEMORIES (works like the book of Scripts, but it’s smaller)
- 13 ADDITIONAL CARDS

The book adds over 200 unique Scripts, all strictly connected to the Characters available in the game. The Scripts themselves can be of two kinds:

- Recollection - just as it was in the original game by 11 Bit Studios, the Characters in different stages of the game can reveal some essential details from their past. These memories or recollections can [1] often be purely story-related, [2] influence the game (for example, Morale), or [3] include a CHOICE made by a Character in the past (a player may also make a choice as if he or she would have traveled in time; The choice can carry consequences reflected in the game’s mechanics, like, for example, a predefined Consequences token waiting to be triggered with another Script just lurking by the corner).

- Current Thoughts - meaning a reaction to the current state of affairs in the game, depending on the chosen Character and its state (hunger, morale, etc.), and sometimes depending on events that took place or the choices already made. These can also be only story-related or influence the game’s mechanics.
Ten cards build the basic engine that allows the Character whose recollections or thoughts are to be revealed to be determined. It also determines what exactly is to be checked and under what conditions (for example, a card might trigger the thought of the Character with the most broken Morale or the one chosen by the players, etc.).

—description by the publisher

boardgame: This War of Mine: The Board Game – Second Edition: The Rubble Die
description:
A totally ASYMMETRICAL die. It has small and large sides and no central center of gravity; it also isn’t perfectly flat; it has bulges and looks like a piece of rubble or debris with some twisted pipes and damaged home equipment parts sticking out of it.

Product description
In the game’s mechanics, this Rubble Die represents a decision to sacrifice some time while searching through a location. We risk more than usual, but instead, we can find something more. You roll the die. In most cases, the roll will mean one of the more probable results, but from time to time, a VERY HARD-to-get result will be rolled, and with it, we will trigger an extremely rare finding or an event.

The description of the symbols on each of the die’s sides will be printed on the cards that will come with it. Not all results of the rolls will be to our liking. The Rubble Die brings certain risks with it and VERY RARELY a highly unpleasant adventure.

—description by the publisher

description:
Spare Parts is a fast-paced and highly interactive drafting game of Robotic Repair that is simple to learn but hard to master.

Using unique drafting mechanics with a sci-fi theme, Spare Parts has players competing with one another to see who can finish the repairs of a set of three Robots first. To do this, players will need to sift through piles of junk to collect critical component cards, while stifling the plans of other players with special action and reaction cards.

Only one may be crowned the Sovereign of the Scrapheap, so race to complete your mechanical menagerie before the others or be left to rust in peace!

—description from the designer

Ever wondered what it's like to manage flight connections, gate assignments, and passenger flow under pressure?
Airport Madness Manager brings airport operations to life in a fast-paced board game where you:

Assign gates to minimize passenger walking distances
Manage passenger stress (literally measured by dice rolls!)
Keep flights on schedule by optimizing passenger movement in real time to avoid costly delays and missed flights

Inspired by real-world hub airport operations, this game blends strategic planning and chaos management — perfect for aviation pros and fans of clever logistics.

Whether you're an airline planner, airport ops leader, or aviation geek, this game will test your nerves and decision-making under pressure.

—description from the publisher

Lerpa is a traditional Portuguese gambling card game, popular in some Portuguese communities, especially among friends and in informal settings. It’s typically played for small stakes and is a fast-paced trick-taking game.

Objective
Win tricks and avoid being the lowest (or win the pot). Avoid being the only player to lose all tricks ("Lerpa’d"), which results in a penalty.

Game Setup
Each round, the dealer contributes 3 of the agreed stake to the pot.

The dealer gives 3 cards to each player. After dealing the other players, the dealer can choose to deal themselves into the hand.
If the dealer deals themselves in, their final card is flipped face up to determine the trump suit.
If the dealer does not deal themselves in, they flip over the top card of the remaining deck to determine the trump suit.

Playing the Hand
Players look at their cards and decide whether to play or fold.
If you fold, you sit out the hand and do not risk any more chips.
If you play, you are obligated to win at least one trick.

Once all players decide, the hand is played out in 3 tricks, with standard trick-taking rules:

Players must follow suit if possible.
If you can’t follow suit, you must play a trump; if you cannot play the highest trump, you can play anything

The highest card in the lead suit wins, unless trump is played — in which case, the highest trump wins.

Scoring and Payout
Each trick won earns 1/3 of the pot.

If a player plays alone (others fold), that player wins the pot by default.

If a player plays but wins no tricks, they are said to be or to have “Lerpa’d”:
That player must pay a penalty equal to the pot for the next hand. If multiple players lerpa'd then they must each pay a penalty equal to the original pot amount at the start of the hand.

Lerpa is traditionally played using a 40-card deck. Some regions, like Germany and surrounding areas, use an Ace-10 deck, created by removing the 7s, 8s, and 9s from a standard 52-card deck. In this game, the cards are ranked from highest to lowest: Ace, 10, King, Queen, Jack, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2. Others, like Italy or Spain, will use an Ace-7 deck, created by removing the 8s, 9s, and 10s from a standard 52-card deck, with the 7 now being the second-highest card behind the Ace.

boardgame: Dungeon Degenerates: Roadside Preacher & Morbid Murderer
description:
Kickstarter Expansion characters for the sixth edition printing.

ROADSIDE PREACHER - Human, Performer, Puritan, Rogue
The Roadside Preacher saw the religion of the Holy Order as his own, spreading his idiosyncratic variation of what is right & wrong to anyone who would listen. The Roadside Preacher’s teachings quickly went so off the Imperial scripture that he had to travel far into the fringes of society. This place of the lawless unwashed is dangerous & it was here that the Roadside Preacher quickly learned to be ready to defend the faith! Eventually brothers from the Holy Order caught up with the Roadside Preacher & threw him in jail for heresy.
 MORBID MURDERER - Human, Puritan, Militant
The Morbid Murderer is a ghoulish accomplice of the Corpse Burners who kills people that aren't completely dead, giving them a push through death's door. She ensures death so that the Corpse Burners can do their jobs of purging the world of dead bodies & the Empire can prevent the dead’s use in heretical witchcraft. Constantly being around the dead & dying & killing hardens the hardiest hearts. The Morbid Murderer killed a cop in a bar who said the wrong thing at the wrong time, she was immediately caught & thrown in the dungeon as an enemy of the state.

boardgame: War of the Three Kingdoms: Return of the Legends
description:
Ancient history of China presents a century's worth of prolonged chaos and war in the story of Three Kingdoms.

The war has also witnessed the birth of numerous warlords in known for their unique abilities, such as Guan Yu and Zhao Yun, generals from the Shu Kingdom, exhibiting the traits of courage, loyalty and righteousness. Military strategists Zhuge Liang and the cunning ruler of Wei Kingdom, Cao Cao, whose brilliance that have inspired many with their philosophies.

War of the Three Kingdoms has allowed players to assume the identities of these warlords in the game. We bring the factors of role play, strategy and deception along with multiple other elements to create an exciting game that can be enjoyed with many other players.

—description from the publisher

This version (WTK) replaced the phased out Legends of the Three Kingdoms (LTK). The alternate gameplay mode of "Kingdom Wars" is only available in English under the WTK name.
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
Titan Tactica is a modern homage to Titan, the classic fantasy wargame in which you command a legion of mythical creatures— centaurs, unicorns, wyverns, giants, and more! At the head of your forces is the Titan who, like the king in chess, must be safeguarded at all costs. The battlelands are teeming with monsters—do you have what it takes to outwit, outlast and conquer your foe?

The game features three types of units:

     Lords— Titans along with their angel (and archangel) support units.
     Legends— Elite units with special orders and abilities. Guardians and wizards are found in towers, but “older and fouler things lurk in the deep places of the world.”
     Creatures— Common units found all over the battlelands. Lions and rangers in plains, gargoyles and gorgons in brush, dragons and colossi in mountains, and many more!


In standard play, the game is won by the player who

     controls the last surviving Titan,
     reaches 100 ἰχ, or
     occupies 6 tower hexes


—description from the designer

boardgame: Dune: War for Arrakis – Exclusive Extras
description: The Exclusive Extras box contains all the Exclusive Stretch Goals unlocked in the Dune: War for Arrakis Kickstarter campaign. If you get the regular core box at retail, this Add-On gives you all the extra content you'd be missing from the Exclusive Edition core box.

Nuara represents a journey through flooded plains, herons and farms, but also to discover the most fun things to do to fully experience the Novara area.

In this fun game, perfect for an evening with friends or a Sunday afternoon with the family, you will have to invest and buy land. Discover yourself as a real estate entrepreneur by putting your negotiating skills to the test and make the city of Novara and the towns in the province rich and full of attractions! In the meantime, you can discover the traditions and food and wine delights of this splendid Piedmont territory.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: There was a Scout...: Clean Edition
description:
This game is cooperative and mostly focused on storytelling.

Scoutmasters, taking scout children to camping in the forest. Every night (round) the players have to tell a scary story to their troops. They need to scare the scouts enough to keep them from wondering away from the camp during the night. The scouts have different personalities and phobias so the stories affect each scout differently. If any child goes missing during the night, a search party phase comes up before the next night's story time.

The goal of the game is to have all the scouts back in the camp after the final search party.

This is the alcohol free, family friendly version of There was a Scout

boardgame: All Time Wrestling: Eddie Guerrero Expansion
description: Celebrate the legacy of Eddie Guerrero with this exclusive limited edition release! Only 1,000 copies available, featuring unique abilities that capture his iconic style. Each set includes 2 profile cards for 2-player mode and solo campaigns, 16 attack cards, 1 player board, 3 referee check cards, and 3 scheme cards. Enhance your experience with an optional solo mini-campaign, adding 6 unique cards, 17 campaign cards, and 10 adapt cards. Don’t miss your chance to play as one of wrestling's legends! Note: this is an add-on product and requires a main ATW product to play.

boardgame: All Time Wrestling: Matt Cardona Expansion
description: Add Matt Cardonna to your pool of playable wrestlers, this mini expansion gives you all his needed cards, including profile card in order to play. Requires ATW All or Nothing or ATW Extreme Edition to play.
Kill Team is an action-packed strategy game of skirmish combat, set in the war-torn 41st Millennium. Kill Team can be played solo or cooperatively with Joint Ops rules, or between 2-4 players.

Bear Beware is a two player competitive game of survival. Collect supplies by drawing from the supply deck to build up your camp. Each set of supplies gives you a new ability in game, but be careful... There's bears out there! The goal of the game is to survive the entire year, as you play through each month (starting in winter). The further along in the game, the more bears awaken and the more dangerous the game becomes! If both players survive the full year, then the winner is decided by points gathered along the way.

—description from the publisher

In Stuck Buddies teams of two take turns drawing cards and holding them between body parts based on the card instructions. The game gets more challenging as you must contort with your partner to hold more and more cards. Drop 2 cards and your team is eliminated. Last team standing wins.

In this quiet solo operation, numbers are your targets. You track them, release them, and capture them at the precise moment to form the perfect sum. Like a codebreaker or a disciplined hunter, you must remain calm and composed. Every turn is a step closer to mastery or a miscalculation waiting to happen.

Hold, Release, Capture is a solo puzzle-style card game where players manipulate a small set of numbered cards (1-7) through three possible actions: hold, release, or capture.

The goal is to capture a combination of cards whose total exactly matches a randomly assigned target number. The player must manage a limited "hand zone" and cycle cards strategically to reach the goal. As difficulty increases, the game introduces ranking levels to track progression, offering both immediate challenges and a sense of long-term growth.

Let's separate plastics, metals, glass and paper to invest in the future of a more sustainable world.

In Recicle card game, discarding products is part of the strategy to achieve victory!

A fast paced game with only four short rounds. The 48 cards show four colors (green for glass, red for plastics, blue for paper and yellow for metals) and eight types of product (glass bottles, cups, PET bottles, plastic cutlery, cardboard boxes, stacks of paper, cans and metal keys).

On your turn, consume a product by buying a card and discard a product by placing a card on the table, forming a line. After each person has discarded two products, it's time to recycle. Each person takes two cards from the line, always from one of the ends, placing them in front of them face up. At the end of the round, each person plays a card from their hand to secretly invest in a color and a product.

At the end of four rounds, everyone will have 8 recycled product cards and 4 secret investment cards in front of them. Investment cards makes the recycled products more valuable.
Whoever gets more points from their investments and recycled products wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Hátsó Gondolatok: Rossz az aki rosszra gondol
description:
Game Rules:

Players place their pawns on the starting space. The question cards are shuffled thoroughly and placed in their designated spot. The first player rolls the dice, and the game begins.

Objective:
The goal of the game is for a player to be the first to assemble the word "TAIL" or "BOOBS," depending on the gender. The letters are placed in separate envelopes, and the current player can draw a letter if they correctly answer the card’s challenge using the provided clues.

Gameplay:
After rolling the dice, the player moves to the appropriate space and draws a card from one of the following types: Guilty, Ambiguous, or Story. The player seated to their right reads the first clue.

Guilty Cards: These cards contain clues that may seem misleading at first, but the correct answer is innocent.

Ambiguous Cards: The clues can be either provocative or innocent, but they always point to an ambiguous word.

Story Cards: These cards feature a short, cheeky story, and the goal is to guess the answer after hearing it.

If the player gives the correct answer, they can draw a letter from the envelope. If they draw a letter they no longer need, they must put it back.

Stealing Solutions:
If a player cannot answer after the first clue, others can attempt to steal the solution. However, if someone gives an incorrect answer, they must return one of their already collected letters.

Special Spaces:
Some spaces allow players to collect two letters at once or steal a letter from another player. In some cases, a player may even be forced to return a previously collected letter to the envelope.

---
Winning:
The game is won by the player who first collects all the letters needed to spell the word "TAIL" or "BOOBS."

‐----‐-------------

A játék szabályai:

A játékosok a start mezőre helyezik a bábuikat. A kérdéskártyákat alaposan összekeverik, majd a kijelölt helyre teszik őket. A kezdő játékos dob a kockával, és megkezdődik a játék.

A cél:
A játék célja, hogy valamelyik játékos elsőként állítsa össze a "FAROK" vagy a "DUDÁK" szót, nemtől függően. A betűk külön borítékokban találhatók, amelyekből a soron lévő játékos akkor húzhat, ha sikeresen megválaszolja a kártyalap feladványát a nyomok segítségével.

Játékmenet:
A játékos a dobás után a megfelelő mezőre lép, majd húz egyet a következő kártyák közül: bűnös, kétértelmű vagy történet kártya. A játékos jobbján ülő társ felolvassa az első nyomot.

Bűnös kártyák: Ezek olyan nyomokat tartalmaznak, amelyek elsőre félreérthetőek, de a megoldás végül ártatlan.

Kétértelmű kártyák: A nyomok sikamlósak vagy ártatlanok lehetnek, de minden esetben egy kétértelmű szóra utalnak.

Történet kártyák: Ezeken rövid, pajzán történetek szerepelnek, amelyeket felolvasva kell kitalálni a megfejtést.

Ha a játékos helyesen válaszol, húzhat egy betűt a borítékból. Amennyiben olyan betűt húz, amelyre már nincs szüksége, azt vissza kell tennie.

Megfejtések elcsenése:
Ha egy játékos nem tudja megoldani a feladványt az első nyom alapján, mások megpróbálhatják elcsenni a megfejtést. Viszont, ha valaki rossz választ ad, vissza kell raknia egy már megszerzett betűt.

Különleges mezők:
Egyes mezőkön lehetőség van két betű megszerzésére egyszerre, vagy akár egy másik játékostól elvenni egy betűt. Ugyanakkor előfordulhat, hogy vissza kell tenni egy korábban megszerzett betűt a borítékba.
---
Győzelem:
Az a játékos nyer, aki elsőként gyűjti össze a "FAROK" vagy "DUDÁK" szó összes betűjét.

boardgame: バラバラ博士の分解大富豪 (Dr. Barabara's Bunkai Daifugō)
description:
In the lab of the eccentric Dr. Barabara, numbers are never what they seem. Each card can be broken down into parts, a 6 might become two 3s, three 2s, or even 1+2+3. In this chaotic experiment of numerical dissection, players scramble to shed their hands using clever recombinations and surprise prime number plays to dominate the discard pile and rise to genius status.

Dr. Barabara's Bunkai Daifugō is a climbing game where every card represents a number that can be "decomposed" into smaller numeric sets. For example, a 6 card may be played as three 2s, two 3s, or a 1-2-3 sequence, giving players tremendous flexibility in play construction.  Special "prime cut" rules allow players to reset the field with cleverly timed prime-number plays, opening windows for sudden victories.

boardgame: Murder on the Rocks: New Blood
description:
New Blood is a chilling expansion for Murder on the Rocks that introduces fresh faces and deadlier deceptions. With new characters, weapons, locations, and roles entering the masquerade, no one is safe—and no deduction is certain.

This expansion adds additional suspects and clue combinations to the core game, increasing replayability and player count options. As new layers of mystery unfold, even seasoned investigators will find themselves second-guessing every whisper from beyond.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Murder on the Rocks: With a Twist
description:
With a Twist is a suspenseful and strategic expansion for Murder on the Rocks that shakes up your investigations with unexpected complications and clever new mechanics. This expansion adds a potent mix of fresh clue cards, new characters, additional weapons and locations—and introduces Scenario Cards, which change the rules of the game each session, ensuring no two mysteries are ever the same.

Will the investigators be forced to share clues in silence? Will the murderer be more powerful, or the survivors more restricted? Each scenario delivers a unique twist, challenging players to rethink their strategies and adapt to evolving threats.

—description from the publisher

The first board game of the Eldertide trilogy has arrived. Each game will be set in the same universe and will unfold across a connected timeline.

In Eldertide: A Thousand Lights, players lead one of five houses of a once-mighty seafaring nation, now rising to reclaim their homeland from a devastating supernatural storm that has raged for decades. With powerful ships at their command, they embark on a mission to rebuild a network of ancient-tech lighthouses across the scattered isles — restoring balance and retaking the archipelago.

In this pick-and-deliver board game, with elements of market fluctuation and civilization building, each player controls a Foundation Ship, sailing across several Ports to collect and transport resources. These resources can be either exported for immediate points, or invested in constructing Facilities on the player’s own Outposts, leading to more explosive endgame opportunities. The Storm acts as a hindrance, breaking Ports that can later be rebuilt, and posing a direct threat to ships themselves. Careful route planning and resource management are essential to succeed.

—description from the publisher

This is a shape transfer game in which you "read" the shape and take the shape.

Apparently one of the tests for astronauts involves transmitting figures, so that is the motif.

Since all karuta figures are made up of six shapes, you can explain in detail their relative positions, sizes, and shapes, or you can explain the figures by comparing them to something.

Each player takes turns rolling two dice, winning or losing coins! You can even take a risk and reroll!
Coins will keep piling up in the 7 squares! If you get a repeating 6, all the coins in the 7 squares are yours!
The game ends when someone gets 21 coins or some players drop out! The player with the most coins wins!

15th anniversary Oink Games Catchphrase Karuta

There are two sets of cards, one with the first half of the poem (yomi-fuda, reading cards) and one with the second half (tori-fuda, taking cards). 
The reader:
One person reads the yomi-fuda, and the other players try to grab the matching tori-fuda. 
The race:
The first player to touch the correct card wins it. 
The goal:
The winner is the player who collects the most cards after all the yomi-fuda have been read.

boardgame: ダンダダダンジョン (Dandada Dungeon)
description:
Go on an adventure with the duck's beat!

A duck guide is your best friend in the cursed ruins?!
Follow the rhythm the duck makes to find the treasure.

Dandada Dungeon is a dungeon exploration game in which players split into a navigator and an explorer team and work together to find the treasure.

The dungeon is made up of tiles that represent rhythms such as quarter notes and eighth notes,and by following a route along a specific rhythm, the location of the treasure will become visible.

The navigator's role is to beat the rhythm of the given topic with a castanet and tell the explorer team the route.
The explorer team relies on the sound to deduce the path to the treasure.

Work together to find the treasure using the sound and deduction as clues.

Afrinopoly is a multi-faceted game, of African awareness for those interested in combining the challenge of attaining wealth, along with the acquisition of knowledge about vital geographic, economic, cultural and political data of the various nations within the African continent. Afrinopoly embraces the principles of mathematics, cultural awareness, economic intuition, negotiating skills, the basic theory of import/export and the Ujamaa principle (Cooperative Economics). Although the game includes the buying and selling the right to represent nations in Africa, no subliminal connotation should be drawn from this principle. The sole purpose of Afrinopoly is to enhance one's all-around knowledge of the geography, culture, economics and politics of nations within the African continent, and to sharpen one's entrepreneurship endeavors via realistic economic scenarios.

—description from the rulebook

Немного Ту-Ту ("Brain Train") is a word association game that can be played cooperatively or competitively. Players are challenged to think creatively within letter constraints.

In cooperative mode, all players work as a team to lead their train along the route by guessing hidden words. Each turn, the active player reveals one letter of their secret word and provides an associated clue starting with that letter. Team members must guess the word in three attempts, losing "gears" (representing train health) for every incorrect guess. If all "gears" are lost, the game ends with defeat.

Players can use two types of limited-use hints: "Idea" (one associated clue with no letter restriction) and "Letter" (shows an additional letter). Route maps add variability due to rules that change the gameplay depending on the segment (for example, they limit the types of hints). For a more difficult task, players can use a timer (sandglass), reduce the number of starting gears, or disable hints.

To win, you need to get to the final station indicated on the route map.
In competitive mode, two teams race to the finish line, guessing the words.
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces
Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
Version 1.5 of EVA Engine represents a significant evolution of the core game. Think of it as a refined and enhanced experience, building upon the foundations of the original while introducing key improvements and additions. In essence, EVA Engine 1.5 is the definitive way to experience the game.

One of the most noticeable changes is the updated rule set. Based on player feedback and further design considerations, the rules have been streamlined for greater clarity and smoother gameplay.  Additionally, Version 1.5 boasts a completely new graphic style. This isn't just a minor facelift; it's a reimagining of the game's aesthetic. And, Version 1.5 may also include the first-ever expansion for EVA Engine (depending on number of backers).

The game consist of doing whatever you can to bring (move) the correct element in ascending numerical order to the position 5 (repairing chamber) and repair it, before your time runs out. If your time is up, or you have to place a heat token while your stock is empty, you lose the game. The game's design incorporates scalability, allowing you to fine-tune the difficulty to perfectly match your personal taste or level of experience.

Beyond new components, the 1.5 upgrade introduces a fundamental shift in how you interact with the game's resources through a new mechanic we call 'Resonance Cascade'.  The energy you generate from one action can now ripple outwards, potentially fueling another.

—description from the designer
