boardgame: My Very First Games: Hello, Vacation!
description:
Rocky the Raccoon is very excited — he’s going on vacation today. But first, he needs to load his luggage into his little red speedster. The first player to load their luggage into the car – with a little lucky die spinning – wins the game and gets to ride along with Rocky.

A clever color matching game with 3D car.

—description from the publisher

In the 16th century, astronomers like Tycho Brahe studied the Moon’s movements and phases using precise measurements and simple instruments. Galileo Galilei’s telescope revealed craters and mountains on the Moon, thereby disproving the ancient hypothesis of its perfect smoothness. These observations changed our understanding of the Moon and of the entire universe.

This expansion for Galileo Galilei introduces the Moon as a new object of observation, adding a new scoring mechanic to explore. Discover four new playable characters, each bringing unique abilities. Additionally, powerful inquisitors enter the game, offering great rewards to those who can successfully persuade them. Map the Moon together and enhance your strategic possibilities!

—description from the publisher

It’s a sunny Sunday morning in a tropical paradise!
You want to surprise Tia the Toucan with a delicious fruit salad for breakfast. Can you collect all the fruit before she wakes up and excitedly hops into the fruit bowl?

A cooperative collecting game.

     The classic HABA game in a tropical new look
     Simple rules to start playing quickly
     Includes 14 large, printed wooden pieces


—description from the publisher

In Crime at Home: Bega Case, you will find yourself tasked with solving the mystery behind an attempted murder. David survived a poisoning, but the culprit is still at large and will strike again unless you find him first.

Escape game that turns you into a detective. Using both physical and digital materials, players will act as real detectives, following the narrative, solving escape room-style riddles, and investigating clues to uncover the truth.

—description from the publisher

4 additional bonus passenger cards for the game.
These can be player instead of the cards in the base game.
They require additional demands to fulfill to be rescued.

In Beaks, swoop, steal, and squawk your way to victory!

In this family dexterity game, players take on the role of gulls swooping in to steal food. Using yellow tweezer “beaks,” you must race against your fellow gulls to grab food from the table and match any food order card before the other players. The quickest gull to squawk wins the card and the points!

You can snatch food from another gull but be careful! Have too much food and you lose points.

The first gull to 10 points, wins!

—description from the publisher

Shout, slam, and laugh your way to victory with Bish Bash Bosh! This fast-paced card game is all about speed, saying sounds, and split-second reactions. Ideal for families, parties, or game night chaos, it’s the ultimate showdown of quick reflexes.

Player’s race to match the word sound chain, ish, ash, and osh in the discard pile with the cards in their hands - in 5 seconds or less! If they can't, they must draw a card and play moves to the next player.  If they lay down an "osh" card, they pick who starts the next sequence.  If they play a card in the wrong order, they must take it back and draw more.

The first player to play all their cards, wins!

—description from the publisher

History Bluff – the game where you and your fellow players journey through time and witness some of history’s greatest moments. Once you arrive, it’s up to you to uncover what era you’ve stepped into. Use your knowledge of history to earn points, or mislead your opponents and score from their mistakes.
During each turn, all players place either a BET card or a BLUFF card somewhere on the shared timeline, between the years of other events. You get a point by placing a BET card correctly, or the opportunity for more points by fooling your opponents with the BLUFF card. After betting or bluffing, you can also play a Bullseye card, a chance for extra points if you spot the correct year, but with the risk of losing points if you miss. You play 15 event cards, and the one with the most points at the end of the game wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Sustainability: An Expansion for Night Express
description: Sustainability is an expansion for Night Express that requires the base game. Players must achieve a positive or neutral environmental balance after four rounds by efficiently managing passengers and avoiding excess wagons. Properly aligning passenger numbers with wagons and using sustainability measures are key to avoiding penalties and winning the game.

Artisticamente is a creative drawing and voting party game designed for 3-6 players aged 8 and up, with a playtime of approximately 30 minutes. The game challenges players to create imaginative drawings based on a given theme while incorporating specific symbols in the required quantities.

Each round, two players face off as challengers, while the others act as judges. The challengers have 2 minutes to draw their "masterpiece" using the revealed theme and symbols. Creativity and adherence to the theme are key, as judges vote for the best drawing.

Judges simultaneously raise their paddles to vote for the chosen drawing:

Unanimous vote: Winner earns 3 points, each judge earns 1 point.
Majority vote: Winner earns 2 points, judges earn no points.
Tie: Both challengers earn 1 point, judges earn no points.

The game continues until all players have had a chance to challenge someone. The player with the highest score at the end wins. Artisticamente combines creativity, strategy, and fun, making it an engaging activity for family and friends.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dark Domains: 2021 Lab Rat Promo Cards
description: This is Laboratory H's 2021 Lab Rat Gift for fans and supporters. It is extra Fortune Cards for Dark Domains. These cards are simply added into the main deck and provide more replayable with no extra rules.

boardgame: A Battle, Furious, Bloody, Repulsive, Crimson, Gory, Boisterous, Manly, Rough, Fierce, Unmerciful, and Hostile
description:
This tenth anniversary celebration of the Shields & Swords series of medieval battle games collects and revises scenarios from all nine previous games, and includes two new scenarios. A dry-erase map allows for twenty-four total scenarios, and leaves the door open for potential expansions in the future.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces
Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
This box includes the SHERIFF edition, i.e. the deluxe edition with all stretch goals, minis AND standees, wooden tokens, custom dice, game board, and player boards. It also includes the pets mini expansion, unlocked as one of our last stretch goals, with the animals in wooden token form (if current backers raise funding to 160k euros during the pledge manager phase, the pets will be upgraded to miniatures).

In the same big box, neatly packed in your custom insert, you will find all the material of the 3 expansions in its deluxe version, i.e. including minis, wooden tokens, player boards and standees. It does not include the physical boxes of the expansions, if you want those, you would have to buy the expansions separately.

The expansions add new game mechanisms and a range of new faction combos, as well as the option to play with up to 5 players or in solo mode (Lone Ranger).
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
What about America is worth fighting for? That’s the question at the heart of Fight for America!, a historical tabletop wargame that examines the events of January 6th through immersive, strategic gameplay.

Fight for America explores how far someone might go to win their version of America. The goal isn’t to recreate January 6th — it’s to use game mechanics to examine how American politics itself has become a game. Red versus Blue. Victory at all costs. This game doesn’t glorify that reality. It immerses players in it—to question what happens when the only objective is to win, and people become pawns in the pursuit of power. Step into the game — and decide for yourself what’s worth fighting for.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Fable of Mabel on the Witch's Table
description:
The Fable of Mabel on the Witch's Table brings playfulness, cuteness, and unmitigated chaos to your tabletop as this two-player board game asks you to do the impawssible: give a cat instructions.

In a simple tug-of-war battle, you must herd Mabel the Cat across the board so that she can push magical trinkets off a witch's table. Each fallen knick-knack earns you points, but don't go feline smug as some thingamajigs can subtract from your score.

Players draw and discard cards to move their cat across the board and earn (or lose) points from objects.  The game ends when 10 objects have been removed or the witch returns (time track moves to zero).  The player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

Die drei ???: Toteninsel, a tabletop adaptation of the movie of the same name, sets players off on an exciting treasure hunt with the detectives from Rocky Beach, challenging them to combine clues and puzzle out what's going on.

boardgame: EXIT: The Game – Family: The Trophy Case/Night at the Carnival
description:
A cool team of animal superheroes – Milo the cat, Häm the hamster, Bella the dog, Nele the parrot, and Luis the bug – has formed a secret club in EXIT: The Game – Family: The Trophy Case/Night at the Carnival in order to solve a pair of puzzly adventures.

In "The Trophy Case", they investigate an animal talent show where a thief is planing to steal the show's trophy. Can the crew enter the talent show undercover, stop the thief, and save the show?

In "Night at the Carnival", mysterious power outages keep occurring, threatening to leave our heroes in the dark. The team suspects sabotage and sets out to solve the mysterious incidents.

Murder in Hollywood! Who killed Olivia Grant, the famous actress who was the sister of a notorious motorcycle gang boss?

In Masters of Crime: Hollywood, players become gang members to investigate the murder by using Google Maps, writing emails, searching websites, and calling phone numbers. What dark secrets are the high society of Hollywood Hills keeping? Together, the gang members decide how the story will continue. Solve puzzles, combine facts, and question them: This is where real detective work comes into play!

Go Viking is a fast-playing, push-your-luck dice game steeped in Norse mythology. As Viking leaders, players seek glory through daring Raids, divine favor, and bold decisions.

Each turn, draw Mythic Powers through sacred carvings in the Runestones—then roll and let the Destiny Dice shape your fate, raid for Loot, and carve your legend in the sagas of the North.

Easy to learn and quick to play, Go Viking is a compact filler game that packs mythic adventure into every turn. When one Jarl’s hoard becomes legendary, the final round begins—and only the richest warrior will have their saga sung.

Raid • Roll • Glory

The title Go Viking comes from the Old Norse “fara i víking”—to leave home, embrace risk, and earn your place in legend.

—description from the designer
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
Sci-Me! arithmetic is a fast-paced card game that combines arithmetic with the thrill of scientific publishing. As a scientist, your task is to craft accurate equations and race to publish your findings before your competitors do. Put your logical thinking to the test and be the first to share your results with the world!

In Sci-Me!, you're a researcher aiming to publish scientific results—because in the world of science, publications are everything. Your objective is to be the first to earn three successful publications. But time is tight, and each decision counts. You'll need to plan carefully, weighing the risk of failure against the time each publication demands.

At the heart of the game is the challenge of arranging cards to form valid mathematical equations. Each turn, players choose two actions from a set of five options to build their formulas. Once you believe your equation is correct, you can attempt to publish it—but beware: every submission must pass a randomized peer-review check, and the shorter your equation, the tougher that check becomes.

Can you balance logic, timing, and ambition to become the most published scientist at the table? Find out in Sci-Me! arithmetic!

—description from the publisher

In Endeavor: Deep Sea, players explore the vast depths of the ocean, hiring dedicated workers and completing objectives in an effort to chart the environment and help protect it.

The Endeavor: Deep Sea – Uncharted Waters expansion pushes players to the edge with challenging new missions, undiscovered depths to explore, and new specialists looking to join your crew and make an impact on the world. Players also get to incorporate new mini-expansions tasking them with saving endangered aquatic wildlife, exploring deeper depths than ever possible, and restoring the fragile balance of the marine ecosystem.

This expansion includes four new scenarios with interesting phenomena such as "Tectonic Split", which divides the sea in two, and "The Fog", which blankets the surface in a mysterious mist. Each of the four scenarios introduces components not found in the base game.

In the distant past, intrepid explorers from the western islands of the great ocean venture into the vast unknown. They hope to find new lands for their families, somewhere to call home.  But they are not alone. Others are racing to settle the islands they discover, islands scattered all over Moana Nui.

In Moana Nui, you and your friends compete for control over a newly-discovered island group. Create the islands as you explore, revealing precious resources along the way. Establish buildings and defend your new community. As you settle new islands, will you develop a peaceful presence or expand to neighbouring shores?  Be careful though, for as you compete for control, peace can turn to conflict.

Moana Nui is a hexagonal tile-placing game where you explore a newly discovered archipelago in the vast Pacific Ocean (Moana Nui). You and your friends will take turns placing tiles and resolving actions. Explore the archipelago, deploy units and move them to defend your new territory, or mobilise your forces to defeat those who stand in your way.

At the end of the game, controlling territory and buildings will score you victory points (VP). The player with the most points wins!

—description from the designer

Welcome to Alchemy 101! You're a first-year student at a school of magic, and it's your very first alchemy class. Your goal: cast spells as quickly as possible. But be careful… you're still learning, and each spell might come with a quirky side effect that will affect your next turn. Laugh, adapt, and try to be the most brilliant (or at least the least clumsy) apprentice!

The game is played simultaneously by all players, we can use both hands to reveal ingredients, but only one ingredient at a time, once we find all the ingredients of a spell, the name of the spell is said, and the spell is taken from the table, as everyone is doing it, more than one person may go for the same spell, so it's a race, but... those spells give us a secondary effect, then, whoever has not completed spells in the round, starts the next round normal, but those who completed spells, start with a secondary effect that gives them the spell

When the spells are finished, the points are counted and whoever has the most points wins.

boardgame: World War II Solitaire: Echoes of War – Allied Edition
description:
World War II Solitaire: Echoes of War (Allied Right Handed Edition) puts you in command of the United States, United Kingdom, and Soviet Union as you stand against the Axis war machine in a gripping solo experience. Spanning the years 1940 to 1945, this unique gamebook delivers 30 individual games, each a full strategic campaign of the Second World War, playable in just 30 minutes.

Don’t be fooled by the quick playtime—each game is packed with tense decisions, critical turning points, and historical flavor. You’ll face a relentless AI-controlled Axis (Germany and Japan) opponent that reacts and adapts, keeping the pressure on from every front.

Armed with only a pen and a single die, you’ll dive into a fast-paced world of strategy, sacrifice, and survival. No two games play the same, and the stakes couldn’t be higher.

Historical events are woven into gameplay, offering unexpected twists and hard choices. Whether you're defending Stalingrad, launching D-Day, or racing toward Berlin, every turn challenges your leadership and changes the outcome.

This is not just a game, it’s a solo war campaign that captures the urgency and scale of the greatest conflict in human history. Simple to learn, tough to master, and impossible to forget.

—description from the publisher

Team based tricktaking.
player role boxer or second
If a team loses stamina, they will be eliminated
If you score a trick, you will get points but lose stamina.  The tricks in the second half are more important.  The player who scores the most points that has not lost stamina wins.

Twelve is a card game that has players attempting to make an ascending line of cards in front of them numbering from 1-12. There are 3 editions of Twelve the first is the regular version with no theme, the second is the Disciple Edition and the third is the 12 Day of Christmas. On your turn you will play a card, draw cards or rearrange your tableau. During the game use action cards to gain an advantage or slow down your opponent. But watch out your opponent may have the same idea. Be the first to have 1-12 in the correct order in your tableau to win the game.

—description from the designer

In Show Me U Know Me, one player picks a category and reads a situation and a list of options, while other players vote on what they think that player would do in that situation. The reader then reveals their answer, and people who voted correctly get one point. First to 10 points wins!

Players also have the option to make the reader explain their answer or challenge the answer if they think the reader is being dishonest or lacking self-awareness. If the reader is forced to change their answer, they lose a point.

ICBRG is a 2 player abstract strategy game played on a hexagonal grid covered by 37 stackable ice tiles.

Players each have 3 penguin pawns in their color which are placed on ice tiles in the corners of the board.

Each turn, a player will do the following actions:


     Slide one of their penguin pawns in a straight line, in any direction, through as many empty ice tiles as possible. 
     Remove all ice tiles from the space they exited, and stack them on top of the smallest existing stack(s) of ice tiles on the board.


When moving a penguin, you may only enter an ice tile stack that is the same height, or one tile shorter, or one tile taller than your penguin's current tile stack. If a penguin ever enters an ice tile stack of a different height, you must end their movement.

Players continue alternating turns in this manner until one player loses by being unable to move at the start of their turn.

Variants are included for 3 player games (using 2 pawns each) and 4 player games (2 v 2 games using 1 pawn each).

—description from the designer

This game was entered in the BGG 2025 Children & Family Game Design Contest, where it was voted #1 Best Family Game

The sheep lost their way! All together help the little shepherd find them and bring them back to their home. Notice why the wolf is lurking? Turn the wheel, move the shepherd to the dashboard and collect in the house as many sheep as the fields that the shepherd moved. You play one by one, but you cooperate. When all the sheep reach the house, you all win the game! A collaborative game for preschool children with many educational values: Counting, correlating numbers with quantities, perceptions, teamwork, collaboration.

Linimasa Card Game: Nusantara Edition is a fast-paced educational card game where players try to guess the chronological order of three historical events and match them with the correct historical figures — all drawn from the rich era of Indonesian kingdoms before the country’s independence. Featuring events and characters from powerful empires like Majapahit, Sriwijaya, and Mataram, the game invites players to explore Indonesia’s pre-colonial past.

Using limited-use Spy Cards to peek at hidden event cards, players secretly arrange timeline guesses and assign matching figure cards. Played over six rounds, points are awarded for correct order and accurate figure matches.

Designed for 1–4 players, ages 8 and up, the game offers a fun and engaging way to learn early Indonesian history in about 15 minutes.

—description from the designer

Buckanjär is a pirate-themed adventure game where players take on the role of daring captains navigating an ever-expanding map. At the start of the game, each player chooses a unique character and ship combination, with differences in speed, cargo capacity, and equipment — like fishing gear or cannons. The game combines exploration, strategy, and a hint of danger as players set sail from their chosen home ports.

As players move across the map by rolling dice (modified by character traits), they uncover new tiles — revealing islands, fishing spots, or open sea. Islands contain resources that can be collected, sold for coins and gems, or used to upgrade your character. Some islands even hide treasure maps, allowing for high-risk, high-reward expeditions. Coins and gems serve as currency and are key to winning the game.

The twist? All valuables remain aboard your ship until you return home — which means you're vulnerable to plundering by other players. Sea tiles also trigger unpredictable event cards, creating moments of opportunity or danger that can turn the tide of the game.

The game ends when the last tile has been revealed. The player with the most coins wins the game, but fortune favors the bold — and the clever. Buckanjär blends light strategy with a strong thematic experience, perfect for fans of exploration, pirate lore, and dynamic board setups.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Would You Still Love Me If...
description:
Check your relationship status and limits by answering if certain wacky traits of your partner crossed the line. Agree on conditions, if you'll accept them or not; to go deeper on your relationships and ask even wackier scenarios.

Each round, a question about a possible dela breaker in a relationship is asked and both players answer if yes or no. If agreeded, they advance in the next stage of their relationship. If they don't agree, they have a miss. Five misses and the players end their relationship. The objective of the game is to reach the "Happily Forever" space.

Pax Romana: Battle for the Provinces is an independent narrative module designed to be played alongside tile-based board games such as Catan. It reimagines the familiar island as the Roman Republic and its neighboring provinces in 201 B.C., adding layers of diplomacy, expansion, and intrigue.

Key Features:
Faction System: Players are divided into two factions—Romans and Barbarians—each with separate starting areas, win conditions, and political goals. Romans pursue territorial expansion, while Barbarians focus on infiltration and destabilization.

Secret Objectives: Each player receives a unique character card with a backstory and personal mission. Achieving these objectives grants additional victory points and enriches the narrative experience.

Province Control: The custom game board is divided into distinct provinces. Players control provinces by having the majority of settlements within them, unlocking province-specific bonuses and extra victory points.

Conquest Mechanics: Players can attempt to conquer opponents’ villages or cities, given road access and payment of military costs. Success is determined by dice rolls, introducing risk and reward to expansion strategies.

Event Deck: A 60-card event deck drives the narrative and dynamically affects resource production, faction conflicts, and the political landscape.

Diplomat Mechanic: The Robber takes on a new role as the Diplomat, doubling resource production instead of blocking it. Players must balance aggression with influence.

Modified Win Conditions: In addition to the standard victory points, each faction has unique win triggers, such as controlling specific regions or achieving collective goals.

Pax Romana combines thematic storytelling with tile-placement and area control mechanics, offering an immersive and strategic experience for players seeking asymmetric objectives and historical flavor.

This module is not affiliated with, authorized, sponsored, or endorsed by the publishers or trademark holders of Catan. A compatible base game is required to play.

Pioneers & Saboteurs is an independent narrative module designed to be played alongside route-building board games such as Ticket to Ride. It introduces asymmetric roles, a reputation system, and a new turn structure to enhance player interaction and strategic variety.

Key Features:
At the start of the game, each player receives a unique character card featuring a secret background and specific scoring objectives. Some characters embody cooperative pioneers who benefit from helping others, while others take on the role of saboteurs who excel in misdirection and disruption.

Each turn, instead of taking an immediate action, players draw a Turn Card containing a one-time event and two optional reputation actions—one supportive (Pioneer) and one disruptive (Saboteur). Choosing an action grants a reputation point along that path and ends the turn; skipping it allows normal play. Reputation bonuses accumulate over time, significantly altering player abilities:

Pioneers may draw additional cards, build extra routes, or earn bonus points.

Saboteurs can hide tickets, block opponents, or even cross existing routes.

Players earn extra points by reaching reputation milestones (+10 or -10), fulfilling secret role-based goals, or leveraging reputation bonuses strategically.

This module includes:
- Five asymmetric character roles with personal objectives
- A dual-path reputation system (positive and negative)
- Thirty-two Turn Cards providing tactical choices each round
- Additional scoring conditions tied to player behavior

Pioneers & Saboteurs maintains the core mechanics of route building while adding bluffing, negotiation, timing, and indirect conflict—ideal for experienced players seeking a more dynamic and social gaming experience.

 This module is not affiliated with, authorized, sponsored, or endorsed by the publishers or trademark holders of Ticket to Ride. A compatible base game is required to play.

Play as a unique civilization from history and join the battle for dominance. Lead your empire to glory — or let it fade into the shadows of time. Develop your civilization through the included advancement card, exploit your civilization’s unique bonuses, and leave your mark on history in this Journey through Catan.

Hooray, the party can begin! And what do we need for it? Lots of colorful confetti, of course! 
Help Panda, Crocodile, and friends spread the cheer and the confetti everywhere.

The first player to scatter the colorful confetti all around their animal wins the game.


     Recognizing and assigning colors and shapes
     Simple rules to start playing quickly


—description from the publisher

"Whoop Whoop"– In the dim disco light, Funkyphant is in his element. His funky dance moves set the beat making him the star of every party.
The first player to correctly predict and uncover all his moves wins the game.


     Simple rules to start playing quickly
     A party game for all ages


—description from the publisher

In the middle of the ocean lies the legendary “Treasure Island,” home to a pirate treasure from ages past. However, no one has managed to claim it. The strong currents of the surrounding whirlpool repeatedly push ships of course, keeping them away from the island. But you won‘t let that stop you!

The first player to skillfully puzzle together the right routes with some lucky rolls of the dice and guide one of their ships onto the treasure island wins the game.


     With dynamic gameplay and a thrilling rotating mechanism
     Fun playing for the whole family


—description from the publisher

Welcome to Mr. Wacky’s Inventor’s Workshop! Here, the eccentric professor and his team of mice are tinkering with extraordinary inventions—currently, the Crazy Copter, a one-of-a-kind flying machine! But they urgently need some help.

Whoever skillfully places the puzzle pieces with a keen eye and a good memory, and moves their mouse the furthest forward, wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dirt & Dust: Ghost Drivers – Promo Pack
description:
Two exciting new variants of Ghost Driver designed for solo players. Each one adds a fresh twist and even more variety to your solo racing experience in the Dirt& Dust board game.

—description from the publisher

Players try to collect matching card pairs. The player left with the Rat King card at the end loses and must chug their drink as a penalty.

Setup:

Remove the 8 regular rat cards; keep only 1 Rat King in the deck.

Shuffle and deal the cards – it’s fine if not everyone gets the same number.

Players check their hands and lay down any pairs face-up. If someone has 3 of the same card, only 2 are laid down, the third stays.

Gameplay:

The player holding the Rat King starts, announces it, and drinks two finger-widths of their drink.

The player to their left randomly draws one card.

If they can form a pair, they lay it down. If they draw the Rat King, they must announce it and drink two finger-widths.

Play continues clockwise.

Every time the Rat King changes hands, the new holder must take a drink.

Winning & Losing:

A player who plays all their cards or has their last card drawn is out and safe.

The player left with the Rat King at the end loses and must chug their entire drink.

Important: If playing with alcohol, drink responsibly, stay hydrated, and NEVER DRINK AND DRIVE!

boardgame: Dirt & Dust: Alternative Stage Cards – Promo Pack
description:
Spice up any race with two brand-new alternative starting stage cards. Each one brings its own challenges and a unique vibe to the track.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: My Very First Games: Block by Block
description:
There‘s a lot going in the HABA Town! There‘s building at every turn.
Floor by floor, block by block new houses are being built. The dump truck brings the required building blocks to the site. But it is up to you to guide the crane and lend a helping hand!

Will you manage to stack all the blocks according to the building plan before your work day ends?

—description from the publisher

Game created as a teaching aid for primary school students.
It teaches how to manage the home budget and support the WOS (Society Knowledge) lessons.

The goal of the game is to have the most Happiness Points at the end of the game, which are gained by responsibly managing your finances.

Each round players draw the top card of the deck and depending on the effect, make a financial decision. The effects include e.g. Opportunities which let you invest your budget and get Points in return and Crises that force you to react in order not to lose the Points. The decisions are taken independently and simultaneously by all the players.

The game ends when a special card is drawn. Then players count their Points, add some extra for the leftover money and the player with the most Points wins.

Expand your adventures with the third expansion for Alpaca!
Add fresh strategies, new characters and exciting modes to your sessions! This expansion introduces a solo mode with two difficulty levels and supports up to 5 players without extending game length, thanks to new streamlined components.

What’s new?

Alpaca Robot – A futuristic alpaca with 4 unique cards

Alpemperor – A noble (and fashionably late) addition to your alpaca herd

Solo Mode Enhancements – 2 bot cards for extra challenge

5-Player Mode – 5 special Baby Alpaca cards to expand the table

Extra rule/FAQ cards for seamless integration

Whether you’re aiming for deeper solo challenges or more fun with friends, this expansion brings even more variety and charm to your Alpaca experience!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Trek: Away Missions – Mortal Q Specialist
description:
It's difficult to work in a group when you're omnipotent! Add the enigmatic Q, previously only available through Organized Play events, to any Away Team by swapping out your Mission Specialist and adding the Mortal Q Cards to the table!

Limited Gamefound Edition Contents:
1 Miniature
1 Rules Card
10 Mortal Q Cards

(Does not include the Immortal Q cards and tokens used during Games 1, 2, and 3 of organized play that are included in the OP Kit.)

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Plastic Fighters is a cooperative strategy game for 2 to 4 players where each player takes on the role of an environmental organization fighting marine pollution. Set in a dynamic coastal ecosystem, players must work together to clean up plastic waste, build eco-friendly structures, and protect marine species from extinction.

Each player leads one of four associations with unique strengths—such as waste removal, wildlife rescue, research, or public awareness—and manages a personal deck of action cards. As the game progresses, players acquire new cards, respond to event cards that simulate environmental threats, and construct permanent structures on the board to mitigate damage and improve their collective impact. The sea and the waste move each round, challenging players to adapt their strategy and coordinate actions effectively.

The game ends after a set number of rounds based on player count, with the final score determined by the number of surviving marine species, built structures, and remaining pollution. Designed to blend fun with ecological awareness, Plastic Fighters combines tactical decision-making with educational depth, offering players an engaging way to reflect on real-world environmental challenges.

The initiative benefits from the support of Beyond Plastic Med.

—description from the designer

October 9, 1982

Old Friend,

I know we have not spoken in a long time, and it's probably unsettling to receive a strange package from me out of the but I'm at my whit's end.

I've been assigned a cold case, the disappearance of Mary Foster from back in ‘71. In this package is a strange book sent station earlier this year and just addressed "In Loving Memory of Mary Foster".

I was at first inclined to dismiss it as the work of some crank, but I've been puzzling over it these last few months and I am more and more convinced that it is genuine and holds the clues to finding out what happened to her.

I am sending it to you because every page is a damned riddle within a maddening riddle, and it has utterly defeated me.

I need your help.

Mary Foster needs your help.

The Eldritch Diary is an escape game consisting of 30+ interlocking puzzles that tell the story of the disappearance of Mary Foster. Solving it will take anywhere from an evening for a group of clever detectives or a week of evenings for a lone-wolf.

The game includes the diary, ephemera, and a set of envelopes containing additional artifacts to unlock.  You will need to think laterally to solve the mystery of Mary's disappearance. In case players get stuck, clues are provided in the accompanying case notes of Detective Mike Ross.

—description from the publisher

In Chesscode, the player takes control of six secret agents during the Cold War, each with a distinct chess-inspired movement. The game consists of two standalone but thematically connected chapters, each offering a different type of solo logic puzzle.

Chapter I – Shadows on the Wall
Each agent can be activated three times. On each turn, the player selects one agent, moves it on a 6×6 map, collects Intel Points by landing on icons, and attempts to reach and close the matching Safehouse.
Each Safehouse requires a specific amount of Intel to be secured, and over-collecting results in time penalties.
Empty spaces may trigger random events. The player has exactly 24 turns (hours) to close all six Safehouses and return to the Operation Base.
Each closed Safehouse grants a one-time special ability.

Chapter II – The Right Code Is the Way Out
The goal is to complete eight logical statements on a Code Sheet, using numbers, logic symbols, and letters collected by moving agents around the map.
Each agent has two uses, and a limited set of symbols and letters.
The logic statements must reference values from other columns and must be immediately verifiable when completed.
Enemy patrols appear each turn, progressively reducing safe movement options.
Planning, deduction, and precise use of agent abilities are key to completing the code and escaping East Berlin.

Both chapters use a minimal set of materials (one printed sheet, one six-sided die, and a pencil), and can be played in under 20 minutes.

—description from the designer

You're a detective and you must find the guilty.

Illustrated Crimes is a collection of gamebooks featuring different illustrated scenes, each of which presents a crime scene that you must investigate to solve the case.

Choose from different cases:
Each scene hides a story and a possible murderer. Analyze each clue and solve the mystery.

Look at each page carefully:
Who is the murderer in this train car?
Each crime scene tells a story, and only by uncovering this story will you be able to find the true perpetrator of the crime.

Access clues through labels:
What did the passengers have in their suitcases?
The different crime scenes are full of clues. Through the labels, you can go to other pages where you'll see even more specific details. You'll be able to figure out what happened if you look closely at all those details (objects, papers, messages, traces, etc.) and then, finally, all the pieces of the puzzle will fit together, and you'll discover the real killer and their motives.

Unleash your creativity by using every resource you can think of:
What does the German text mean? Where do those coordinates lead?
Be very creative in your investigation. You may need to use tools outside the app, such as websites, emails, a translator, Google Maps... Let nothing stop you from solving the case!

Read the QR codes to access interactive clues:
You can hear the suspects themselves explain what they were doing at the time of the crime.
In addition to accessing other interactive clues such as passwords, safes, phone calls, and more,

Once you have a theory about what happened, consult the solution to see if your theory is true:
Step by step, we'll explain how to get to the truth so you can check if your investigation was on the right track.

Purgatory: Reincarnate Souls is a heavy, strategic board game for 2-4 players.

Theme:
In this game, you play as an angel whose job is to reincarnate souls. Each soul carries sins from their past lives, and it's your mission to guide them so they don't repeat the same mistakes. To do this, you give them memories of their past sins through dreams, deja vu, and hallucinations. By making them remember what they did wrong before, you help them live better lives and avoid sinning again.

Gameplay:
You are an angel, trapped in purgatory. 
Your mission? Reincarnate lost souls. You return fragments of their past sins through dreams, déjà vu, and eerie hallucinations. To do this, you must dive deep into the soul, solving lock puzzles.

But every soul comes at a cost.
Each time you take on a new one, the scale of judgment tips out of balance. You’ll need to carefully manage your Heart and Feather resources to avoid losing points...

There are multiple ways to act:

     Play actions directly from cards;
     Swing the mystical pendulum and take actions on the book;
     Bonus action tiles give you extra moves.


To win, you'll earn double-sided Reward Cards. One side gives instant points, the other end-game scoring. Choose wisely. Additionally, you will have Ongoing Benefit tiles, which reward you based on your playstyle.

Each round, Omen Cards are drawn. They may curse your path… or let you curse others.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Mystery Riddles: Adventskalender – Der indische Palast​
description: As with other advent calendars, Mystery Riddles: Adventskalender – Der indische Palast​ features 24 compartments for you to open throughout December — but a tricky puzzle awaits you each day, and you must use the cards and other elements to solve the daily puzzle in order to determine which door to open the following day.
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
One night in the enchanted forest, the witches are brewing their potions. But what was that? Suddenly, a cockroach scurries among the magic cauldrons! If it falls into a potion, it's spoiled.

In KakerlaHex, you must wave your magic wand and find the right magical ingredients! Whoever collects the most points for successfully brewed potions wins.

My Favorite Pizza or Sheep’s Pizzeria (ひつじのピザ屋さん) as it’s called in Japanese, is a speed puzzle game in which players compete to create the correct pizza as quickly as possible by overlaying pizza cards with holes in them.

This magical card game allows players to take on the role of a student of wizardry in Magic School. At the start of the game, players are secretly assigned to either House Rugir or House Ramper, but they don't know which House they belong to. Next, they enter a study phase where they have 5 minutes to memorize as many of the 34 spells and 28 items as possible. After this they are dealt 3 cards from a combined spells/items deck.

Starting with player 1, each player draws a card from the event deck. Depending on the difficulty of the event (represented by a 1-3), the player must creatively use 1-3 of their spells/items to complete the event. The active player tells a brief story on how they've used their spells/items to complete the event. If a majority of players agree that the story makes proper use of the spells/items, the active player keeps the event card as a point. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Note: The instructions don't elaborate on how the different Houses work. It is left to the players to create house rules that handle either a team game or some sort of hidden team mechanic.

In this SOLO DUNGEON CRAWLER you play as an EXPLORER trapped in an EGYPTIAN TOMB pursued by a vengeful UNDEAD MUMMY.

You must run, fight, dodge & disarm traps as you explore the tomb.

Your goal to find and destroy the FOUR CANOPIC JARS housing the Mummy's vital organs. Only then can you defeat the MUMMY and break the CURSE!

Choose between 3 playable characters. Each character has a different primary stat and starting item. Gain gold through COMBAT and EXPLORATION to upgrade your character's stats. Pass skill checks to defeat enemies, disarm traps, or to escape when things go sideways.

The longer you spend in the tomb the greater the effect of the PHARAOH'S CURSE upon you!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dark Domains: 2022 Lab Rat Promo Cards
description: Four new suit cards, the Architects of Wands, Cups, Pentacles, and Swords designed to expand the possibilities in Dark Domains while off-setting the extra Arcana cards offered by the first two promo decks.

PLAYING THE GAME:
The player that goes first will take the dice direction medallion making sure that the arrows on the medallion are going clockwise. They will then roll the one red die. This die is always rolled by itself to get a pawn out of the pit area and onto a start space. If a 2, 4, 6, or 8 is rolled that player would move one pawn of their choice from their color pit area onto their color start space, they will announce which direction (clockwise or counterclockwise) they would like to move their pawn around to board and flip their player direction medallion to reflect that direction. Now their turn is over and they pass the dice and dice direction medallion the to the next clockwise player (player on their left).
After a player has gotten a pawn out of their pit and on to a start space, on their turn they will roll both the black and red dice together and move their pawn doing as instructed by the space landed on. Their turn is then over. Don’t forget to pass the dice direction medallion with the dice.
Players can get another pawn out of their pit only if their preceding pawn is on a bridge space.

WINNING THE GAME: The first player to get all four of their pawns in each sanctuary space wins the game.
A player does not have to make a full revolution around the board to get a pawn in a sanctuary space. Once you leave your start space you can move your pawn to a sanctuary space. However, you must roll the exact number of spaces to get your pawn in a sanctuary space. Also, all saved chaos cards have to be played and discarded before you can get your fourth and final pawn in a sanctuary space to win the game

On the flying island of Ventopia, the protective light shield has collapsed. Nyxara, the ruler of the Dark World, wants to take control of Ventopia and sends out her dark creatures.

In Ventopia: Die fliegende Insel, which features an electronic game board, players need to use their fighting skills and a bit of luck to save the flying island from its imminent fall into the dark depths.

The Nemesis commands frenzied beasts, leaving destruction in their wake. The heroes must answer the call of the hunt and take the beasts down!

Get ready for one-vs-many gameplay in the Talisman: Nemesis – Call of the Hunt expansion. The hero players must work together to overcome three perilous trials, each with its own unique adversary and villain characters. To win, they need to defeat each adversary before the Nemesis player wipes them out. The Nemesis' objective? Each trial brings a new one. As players complete trials, they'll open boxes and discover new cards and figures to add to their game.

boardgame: Betrayal at the Neibolt House: The Evil of Pennywise
description:
You're with a group of friends who survived It's last feeding frenzy in the town of Derry when you were kids. Now, It has stirred again after 27 years, and you've been drawn back to the Neibolt House to confront the savage shapeshifter. As you explore the house and experience nightmarish events, will you turn on your friends? Or will you band together to beat It once and for all?

Inspired by the It Chapter Two movie based on Stephen King's novel It, the Betrayal at the Neibolt House: The Evil of Pennywise expansion features five terrifying haunts and includes three highly-detailed character figures, new rooms to explore, new card, tokens, and more!

Myitkyina was a prolonged torturous affair, but vital to the re-opening of the Ledo/Burma Road to supply the beleaguered Chinese allies.

After an end-around to the north over/through a high mountain ridge, Chinese divisions and US Merrill's Marauders - 5307th Infantry Regiment - descended on the town and its airfield from the north to the surprise - shock - of the Japanese troops on site.

The Marauders were exhausted and disease-ridden - unlike the wise Chinese they didn't boil drinking water, apparently trusting in water purification tablets. Mite-infected typhus killed one of battalion commanders in 1 day!  They finally grabbed the airfield after a fight and flew in supplies and reinforcements, but under-estimating the Japanese troops.

Some of the Japanese were 18th Division which played its part in The Rape of Nanking and was an extremely tough/resilient outfit, as the rules reflect. (Its "1*" units cannot be Disrupted at all, unlike all others.) They defended very aggressively and even had us worried at one point that they might come out of the town and re-take the airfield.

The US Army green book about this is titled Stilwell's Command Problems, and he may have created the worst, despite his brilliant generalship up until this battle.

It appears the British were ready to send in a fresh division - the 36th Indian Division - I'm having it in the game as an option at the cost of the Allied player getting no better than a draw - but Vinegar Joe was uninterested. Did he not want to share a victory with the British?

Note the rail line and roads going down the hex-EDGE - there is a reason. Anybody want to guess what it is? ?

Stacking is 3 battalions, with a regiment (or brigade) counting as 2, and rigid.
11 weekly turns. 8 Japanese units plus a couple decoys. 15 US, Chinese, and British units.

—description from the designer

The HeroQuest: The Crypt of Perpetual Darkness Quest Pack expansion includes 10 epic quests designed by Joe Manganiello, 1 metallic black dragon miniature, 8 black dice, 1 pad of character sheets, 20 foil-embellished game cards, and 2 cardboard tile sheets. With this thrilling strategy game, enjoy limitless replayability and create your own quests and stories.

Anything You Can Do is a family-friendly card game where players attempt to one-up each other at physical and mental challenges.

What makes the game really unique is the surprising combination of challenges that you will need to do during your turn. It might be easy to name 3 books, skip on 1 leg, and do 3 dance moves - but can you do them all within 30 seconds?

Strategise when to do a certain challenge from your hand, cover up a challenge that you want to avoid doing, or put a challenge down that's easy for you but difficult for the next person!

The first person to complete their challenges (empty their hand) wins!

Rules include team and solo modes.

description:
Enter a post-apocalyptic world teetering on the edge of destruction. Tartaria 2030 is a rich, story-driven strategy game that merges the immersive depth of a classic board game with the innovation of cutting-edge digital gameplay.

Lead powerful units, face catastrophic natural disasters, and make game-changing decisions as you fight to survive and conquer in a world consumed by chaos.

Vi är ej maskiner ("We Are Not Machines") is a fast-paced, knife-fight-in-a-phone-booth-type game about the great miner’s strike at the ore fields (Malmfälten) of Norrbotten, Sweden, 1969-1970.

Over the course the game, players engage in a political power struggle to organize and manage the massive wildcat strike that broke out on December 9, 1969, when about 4800 miners at Loussavaara-Kiirunavaara Aktiebolag’s (LKAB) mines in Kiruna, Svappavaara and Malmberget, supported by dockworkers at LKAB’s shipping ports, Narvik and Luleå, halted work for 57 days.

The primary aim of this game is to capture the tensions in collective efforts to organize extra-parliamentary action against capital in the years leading up to the neoliberal counterrevolution of the 1970s and 1980s. To this end, players in this game assume the roles of social democrats, unionists and communists - members of the strike committee who are, in principle, on the same side, but internally split - in an effort to build and maintain a united front against LKAB (the company/management) and the state.

Mechanically, players are at once allies and rivals: on the one side, they must cooperate to prevent LKAB from breaking the strike by exploiting their own internal conflicts, diverging interests and ideological disunity; on the other side, players must use their cunning to individually pursue their own political and ideological agendas in order to win the game. In other words, players are neither social democrats nor unionists or communists, ideologically diverged but politically aligned, and they must bicker, negotiate, coordinate and carefully organize against their oppressors. Yet they must do so without stirring up too much internal conflict to be exploited by the company.

I made this game because I sometimes feel like I was born in the wrong century: born too late to be part of the struggle between labor and capital; born too soon to be a teenage star influencer on social media; born just in time to experience the final stages of planetary collapse under late capitalism. I wanted a game that simultaneously told stories from the side of labor rather than capital. Today, notions of social progress, revolution and class struggle have been replaced by the cursory nomenclature of culture wars, trivial influencer activism and blunt awareness campaigns. No regular, middle-class wage slave, nor any lifestyle-politician or head of government in the global north truly believes in revolution beyond the market. Indeed, they cannot imagine any alternative to capitalism. Nor do they consider any notion of social progress beyond the technological inventions of new apps, synthetic food, anti-obesity prescription drugs, decarbonized electric cars, solar panels and vertical farming. The Western world wants commodities at the expense of life, order before justice.

But what seems to be forgotten today in our crisis of democracy and collective amnesia in the West is that the threat of revolution forced the ruling classes to extend political rights to larger sections of the population. Class war, a term today replaced by superficial culture wars and identity politics, pushed the bourgeoisie to consider wages as an investment rather than a cost, making real material changes to the living conditions of ordinary, working people. But I also had more banal reasons to make this game, simply to convey a fraction of Scandinavian economic history, which is predominantly marginalized in board gaming.

What I hope to show with this game is that without collective organization, struggle and the idea of revolution, strikes, riots, national liberation struggles, even the labor movement’s reformist choreographies with capital, by which compromises were made with the ruling classes, would have made no sense at all. This game revives a historical, somewhat nostalgic fraction of European revolts in the latter half of the 20th century; movements that were crushed, from Paris 1968 to Bologna 1977, together with the notion of revolution during the 1980s, buried and forgotten in the neoliberal hellscape we inhabit today.

—description from the designer
family: Theme: Labour movement / Workers rights / Unionising

boardgame: Star Wars: Unlimited – Legends of the Force
description: The Legends of the Force set features characters and content from across multiple eras of the Star Wars galaxy, with a particular focus on Force users, such as Jedi, Sith, and more.

Every player is a candidate for the canton's (region) executive government. They have characteristics at the beginning of the game, during the game they will win votes and money that will allow them to get to their spot in government.

Game developped in Obwald around mostly local political subjects.

In Vividiem, you shape your day hour by hour, crafting a balance of calm, connection, and adventure.

Each turn, you draw colored cubes — each representing an hour of your time — and place them in a line, forming your daily sequence. Use sets of cubes to complete activity cards, from a quiet morning walk to a lively dinner with friends.

Timing and planning are key: choose when to hold back, when to take risks and how to adapt to what the day throws at you. With each scored card, you fill your day with purpose and move closer to your goal.

The first player to reach 24 points — a full, well-lived day — wins.

Vividiem is a tactical pattern-matching game of tempo, tension, and tiny choices with big impact.

—description from the designer

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: A Fist of Divine Speed.
description:
Significantly boost decks themed on the​ Whole Cake Island and Fish-Man Island Arcs!
Introducing a brand-new theme "SWORD" featuring a host of powerful characters!

—description from the publisher

Dogfight 1916 simulates aerial combat on the Western Front during the First World War. Each flier tries to shoot down his opponent by manoeuvering his aircraft behind and above the enemy, then firing.
Dogfight 1916 is played in rounds. A round consists of each player choosing a manoeuvre card in secret, turning it face up, plotting the manoeuvre card on the chart, and indicating each aircraft's altitude on the respective altimeter "cube". When one aircraft gets behind the other he may fire using one of two firing procedures: either from below the defender OR from above the defender and exhausting ammunition either way.
Play continues until one aircraft is destroyed.

Flank Flip is a 2 or 4 player game integrated within the second Fledgling Wallet. This is a straightforward strategic and tactical war game using 9 or 16 cards. Move your troops to strategic places on the battlefield and use tactics to respond to what your opponents do. You win by totally controlling the battlefield.

The Flank Flip Set includes 18 One Card Mazes from Season 3 and all 18 cards can be use to play Flank Flip with 2 or 4 players.

—description from the publisher

12 card expansion to Hotshot Pickleball which adds an element of silliness to the game.  Cards may direct you to make helicopter noises while the ball is on your side of the court, or yell "It's Volley Time" whenever they hit a volley.  If you fail to follow the directions you lose the point.
Ever had a referee totally change the game?  Add a referee to your game who may decide to ignore some rules or enforce new ones.  Referee remains in play until replaced by a new one.

Quick Paws is a fast, silly game of matching, reacting, and racing to perform tricks.
Players take on the roles of excitable pups playing in a crowded dog park. They will flip cards one at a time to separate piles, keeping their eyes peeled for matching “tricks”. As soon as two matching trick cards appear, it's a frantic dash to be the first to shake, speak, roll over, or fetch! The fastest dog earns a treat card—but its value stays hidden until the end of the game.
Designed for 2 to 6 players, Quick Paws plays in about 15 minutes. It’s easy to teach, great for parties, and turns any table into a tail-wagging showdown!

—description from the publisher

Lay hiking trails along waterfalls, forests, mighty mountains and beautiful lakes. Only if you lay them out skillfully or build over existing ones will you score many points.

Play it alone or with up to five players. In this cooperative game, you want to build at least one long hiking trail and lay lots of attractions around the viewpoints.

Draw a card and place it so that the hiking trail continues from the starting card. You can also place the map in an offset position or build over existing maps. As soon as the hiking trail reaches a viewpoint, it can score burning points. Place attractions such as waterfalls, mountains etc. around the viewpoint.

Each attraction around a viewpoint, the longest hiking trail and refreshment points (small red house) along hiking trails earn you points. At the end of the game, you can check the points table to see how well you have done.

—description from the publisher

Pure adrenaline! Master the slope and reach the finish line before everyone else. Catch your opponents with rockets, bees and pigeons and make their descent a personal hell. Ready to put friendships to the test? Get on your board, set, go!

Choose one of the 8 funny animal characters and start on the first part of the track. You can drive up to 2 spaces in your turn. Pass gift and boost fields and avoid the abysses. You can use the gifts you collect to make faster progress or to attack your opponents. The first to cross the finish line wins.

—description from the publisher

Realms At War is a TTG made for epic multiplayer battles. It is a game of calculated risk.

Portals have opened in multiple Realms, all leading to The Convergence. The king of each Realm has sent a Company into The Convergence, not for it's resources - it has none - but for it being a key strategic position, the gateway to other Realms.

Each king can forge alliances with other Realms and fight 1v1, 2v2, 2v1 or 3-4 player FFA. They decide which of their miniatures, known as units, will join their Company, and how their Company will be structured. Each unit has different strengths and weaknesses, having different stats and traits.  Units and cards come together for powerful combinations to form impenetrable defensives or unstoppable offensives.

The winner will outsmart their opponents by strategic unit placement and movement, casting well timed spells, and laying unsuspecting traps.

With 4 game modes, 55 cards, 60 units, 4 teams, and a huge 12 x 12 board, Realms At War has countless different ways to play!

—description from the publisher

Cooperative drawing & guessing game

Players draw a picture of the same item, but each with a different adjective attached to it. A guesser needs to guess which adjectives go with which pictures.

The city is populated by many "smonbees" (smartphone zombies) who walk around without looking ahead, operating their smartphones.

You've been forced to take part in a game to see which Smonbee will be the last one standing. You come up with the idea of ​​writing symbols on the ground to give instructions to the Smonbees, who are always looking at their feet. But then some of the other participants start imitating you...!?

Place control panels under the feet of your Smonbees to guide them and knock your opponent's Smonbees off the board.

description:
"Smombies" (smartphone zombies) walk around without looking ahead while operating their smartphones.

You've been forced to take part in a game to see which Smonbee will be the last one standing. You come up with the idea of ​​writing symbols on the ground to give instructions to the Smonbees, who are always looking at their feet. But then some of the other participants start imitating you...!?

This is a new edition that includes completely new rules for playing with 2 to 4 players in addition to the traditional rules for 2 players only!

Welcome to Sablé-sur-Mer

Before the war, this small coastal town was peaceful and pleasant to live in.
But since the occupation, the atmosphere is filled with betrayal, self-interest, and assassinations.

Two teams compete to complete four missions:

The Occupants, responsible for maintaining order and eliminating troublemakers, and the Resistants, determined to free Sablé-sur-Mer from the oppressor’s grip.

The game ends when one team completes two missions.

But be careful — a collaborator may be hiding among the Resistants!

If the Occupants win and the collabo has revealed himself or herself during the game, they win together.

If the Resistants complete their two missions first, the game moves to the "Épuration" Phase:
The Resistants must vote to eliminate one of their own, suspected of being the collabo.
The chosen player loses the game — whether guilty or not.

If the collabo survives the vote, the traitor wins alone.

—description from the designer

Ring of Truth is a fast-paced and engaging social deduction game, where players take on secret roles and try to discover who’s on their side—and who’s out to deceive them.

The game is completely free to download and can be assembled using everyday items found at home.

It was designed with accessibility in mind and is fully playable by people with visual impairments.

A party game for 2-6 players. A player read an event and the others have to guess the year it happened. If they do not, they get a "before" or "after" answer and they can try again, getting nearer and nearer until they guess. A celebration for the centenary of Istituto della Enciclopedia Treccani: an agile promotional edition will be followed by a richer commercial one and by an online version of the game.

—description from the designer

Catacombs Alpha is a cooperative dungeon crawler where a party of up to 3 heroes work together against a "dungeon master / DM" player. The DM controls the various monsters the heroes encounter.

To resolve combat, players flick wooden discs representing their heroes across the game board. Ranged shots such as missiles and fireballs are represented by separate discs of various sizes. The heroes must fight through a series of monster-filled rooms to reach and defeat the final boss - a Catacomb Lord.

Catacombs Alpha features a streamlined rule set and introduces all new heroes, monsters, items, and Catacomb Lords. The game remains fully compatible with all existing Catacombs Third Edition expansions.

Really Loud Librarians: Grab & Game has only a deck of 55 cards.


In Really Loud Librarians, your useless knowledge will win you useful points.

Think fast, not hard in this merciless word-shouting board game. Start by drawing a category card. Your team will then shout words from that category that begin with the letter on the race track. Move along the track every time you get a word right and race against your opponents to win. This is a simple game for versatile occasions. Play it as a two-player game for a casual night-in or bring it along as a party game for big groups.

Dexterity game where players take turns getting ingredients, adding them to a cup, and shaking them out based on their secret objectives. Score points based on the flavors left in the cup at the end of the game. But be careful, spilling too much milk and coffee will result in an instant loss.

Table World Wrestling Entertainment is a tabletop simulation of American wrestling matches.

The rules, certainly too long for the target audience, begin with this paragraph which I find delectable: “A player is chosen to be the referee of the game. This referee must make sure that everything (sic) is done according to the rules.”

That says it all!

The board is divided into two zones: the Ring, where battles are fought, and the Paths of Glory, where players prepare to reach the Ring.

Before the game, each player chooses a wrestler, and one player is appointed game referee. The referee enforces the rules, controls the cards and can expel a player who cheats.

Battles are resolved by each player rolling two dice simultaneously. Before rolling the dice, fighting players can use a combat card.

The only goal is to be the last player standing in the ring.

boardgame: Pilsna: A Drink to the Past
description:
Join the Fabled Four Pack in Pilsna: A Drink to the Past! Team up to battle foes, uncover treasures, and defeat the evil Lady Dunkel in this thrilling co-op adventure!

Each turn, a floor card is revealed and challenge tokens are assigned. Players each roll a d6 and attempt to complete each challenge along the way.

Players will progress through the castle until they unlock Lady Dunkel's chambers, in an attempt to defeat the evil queen and remove the darkness from all of New Aleland.

Real world bonuses will also help players along. Gain a bonus to perceptive checks if you are wearing glasses, gain stealth bonuses for wearing black.

—description from the designer

Uh oh! Election day is fast approaching. Have you and your fellow senators passed enough pork projects to satisfy your constituents and be re-elected?

Pork is a cooperative word-association party game for 2-6 players. Each round, one player is the president and the rest are senators. Senators give one-word clues that collectively are used to get the president to guess the Bill Word.

But that's just the first hurdle.

If the Bill passes, the President then guesses each senator's individual Pork Word--using the same clue (and only this clue!) the senator gave for the Bill Word earlier.

Pass enough pork in 6 rounds, and you and your fellow senators are re-elected and win the game!

—description from the designer

Get rid of your cards by completing the line or performing the "Smatchy" or "Matchy" actions.
Be the first player to reach 18 points to win the game.

boardgame: Sherlock Holmes and the Return to Baskerville Hall
description:
A self-contained puzzle box game.

The Story:
A disturbing letter from Sherlock Holmes has arrived at your door.

On his return visit to Baskerville Hall, Holmes has discovered Sir Henry Baskerville dead, slain by someone within its very walls. But danger lurks in the shadows, and his every move is being watched.

Holmes has hidden his findings within his correspondence, entrusting you, Dr. Watson - or rather, you, the player - with the task of deciphering his clues, unmasking the killer, and calling in the authorities before it’s too late.

Will you prove yourself a true detective and save Holmes in time? The fate of the great sleuth is in your hands.

—description from the publisher

Gender Neutral 1920s Murder Mystery Party Kit for home use.

The 1920s. An era of gin, jazz, and rich extravagance. Perched on the shores of Long Island, the grand Gatsby Manor is home to millionaire media mogul Randolf Fitzgerald. With a reputation for hosting the most lavish parties in the state, a night at Gatsby Manor is an excuse for lavish indulgence and glittering revelry. This evening, as the dazzling soirée unfolds, the festivities take a much darker turn. Fitzgerald’s body is discovered amidst all the glamour and it’s clear he didn’t die of natural causes.

Can you discover which New York socialite is to blame?

You play the suspects, one of you is the murderer.

—description from the publisher

A fun, fast, and addictive visual safari through the Tarot! In Fools' Path, 9 Tarot cards are laid out each round in a 3x3 grid to form the Path. Players are assigned Challenge cards (a la Ticket to Ride) which indicate which cards along the Path they need to capture. Each Challenge card presents a different card capture strategy and is victory worth points at the end of the game. Are you trying for cards that feature flowers or fantastic beasts, mountains or stars, or perhaps swords and armor? Look closely, because there's more than meets the eye on every card!

Explore the rich visual imagery of the Tarot like never before, and outscore your opponents using additive bidding mechanics (a la Cribbage) to capture the cards you need before they disappear!

Play with the 33 Rider-Waite inspired Tarot Path cards (Major arcana and King, Queen, Knight of each suit) included with the game, or use your favorite Tarot deck! Game comes with 52 Challenge Cards, 33 Path cards, and 30 standard deck Bid cards.

—description from the designer

Naruto Shippuden card game, a recreation of iconic battles with a card system, chakra tokens, life counters, mission cards, and mission parameters. A player or team wins a set number of rounds with experience points, or the player with the most characters left alive. Experience is gained by completing missions or mission parameters, and you'll receive support for battles.

A party card game of silly noises.

During each turn, the judge turns over a thing (!) card from the draw pile.  All the other players then combines a ? sound card with the ! thing card and makes the noise described. The judge chooses the biggest, daftest one and that player wins a point!  Play moves to the next player/judge and a new turn starts.

The player with the most points wins.

Get ready to Slap the Deck, slap yourself, and maybe even slap your friends! Put on one of the HUGE foam hands and deal out all the cards evenly and face down to form each player's draw pile. Take turns placing cards from your draw pile into the center of the table while counting from 1 to 10. Don't mess up the count or you'll be adding the entire center deck to your draw pile! Not all cards require counting - some will force you to slap to your left, slap to your right, or, you guessed it, slap yourself! You might not have to slap first, but you definitely don't want to slap last! The first player to clear their entire pile wins the game!

How to play:

     It's time to put on a HUGE foam hand and get slap happy!
     Deal out the entire deck evenly to each player, and start flipping cards into the center pile one player at a time
     Watch out for those slap cards — some will have you slapping yourself or even slapping your friends!
     If you're the last to slap, you have to keep the entire center pile — the first player to get rid of all their cards wins!


—description the from publisher

A standalone game from Naruto Shippuden Ninja Battle but fully combinable, two-sided battles of Kages and reanimated Kages with cards and tokens.
Draw a mission card and a mission parameter card, play two sides: the Shinobi Alliance and the Akashuki, use the mission character, and do your best to gain experience by completing both the mission, which only one side can complete, and the mission parameter for each side. The winner or winning team will be the one with the most experience at the end of a set number of rounds or the one with the most characters left alive.

You take on the role of one of the three most iconic figures in the horror genre (Dracula, Victor Frankenstein, and Dr. Jekyll), and face your great antagonist in a thrilling dialogue where wordplay and dice management play a key role.

In this narrative board game, your opponent will ask you a series of questions, and you’ll have to respond. The type and number of words you use will determine how many dice you roll, and how many points you gain.

You win if, by the end of the game, you’ve persuaded your opponent and have more points than answered questions.

Interpretas a una de las tres figuras más icónicas del género de terror (Drácula, Victor Frankenstein y el Dr. Jekyll), y vas a enfrentarte a tu gran antagonista en un apasionante diálogo, en el que la gestión de palabras y dados jugarán un papel fundamental.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Books: Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde

description:
FIRE! Modern Combat is a miniatures wargaming ruleset for playing out intense modern squad-level combat scenarios. Confrontations are exciting using this fast, agile, and extremely realistic game system that keeps both players immersed in the game at all times. The rules use actions and reactions along with fluid initiative while embracing simplicity to reproduce the tension of today’s modern combat. Players will find action galore in games of less than an hour with just twelve miniatures per side.

Play with squads of regular armies, with the composition and weapons identical to those they use in real life or lead irregular troops with a completely variable composition. From US SEALS to revolutionary militias. There are 16 lists to choose from.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dark Domains: 2024 Lab Rat Promo Cards
description: Three new fortune cards utilizing the sciences and education. Add Art, Mathematics, and Science to your game.

Welcome to Divine Duels – A Battle of Light and Darkness!

Step into the pages of Scripture with "Divine Duels," an exciting, faith-filled board game that brings Biblical stories to life. Choose your character from legendary figures like Joseph of Egypt, Noah, or even Adam—each armed with unique abilities and divine relics to help you triumph.

Gather your friends or embark on a solo quest in this 1-4 player adventure. Select your mission scenario, adjust the Darkness deck to match your group’s skill level, and prepare for a journey of faith, strategy, and action.

The gameplay unfolds as you:


     Explore iconic locations, uncovering treasures and challenges.
     Collect relics and miracles to strengthen your faith and combat the forces of evil.
     Battle Sinister Minions while preventing the spread of Darkness.
     Face the Villain, completing mission-specific objectives to secure victory.


But beware—the forces of Darkness are relentless. If they overtake too much of the board, the mission is lost. However, with teamwork, faith, and a bit of strategic brilliance, you can push back the shadows, fulfill your mission, and shine the light of hope for all creation.

Divine Duels combines thrilling gameplay with spiritual depth, offering an unforgettable journey where God’s power shines in every move. Are you ready to fight for the light?

Fly Fight Win: Danger Zone is a hex-based, miniatures rules set for aerial combat from the early Vietnam War through Desert Storm. The game emphasizes the importance of speed, altitude, and pilot skill in the chaos of air combat. It includes statistics for many frontline fighters and a pilot advancement system for campaign games. All games in the Fly Fight Win series are designed for playability but retain sufficient detail to recreate plausibly historical outcomes. Most scenarios can be played in around 90 minutes.

The game differs significantly from its predecessor in that it uses initiative cards only for movement and has a completely revamped combat system that uses opposed die rolls to resolve hits.

—description from the designer

Fly Fight Win: Honcho is a hex-based, miniatures rules set for aerial combat. It emphasizes the importance of speed, altitude, and pilot skill, and minimizes much of the tedium typically associated with air-to-air combat games. It includes statistics for many frontline fighters that saw action during the Korean War and a pilot advancement system for campaign games. The rules differ from MiG Alley with streamlined initiative and combat systems.

—description from the designer

Fly Fight Win: Wingman is a hex-based, miniatures rules set for aerial combat in World War II. The game emphasizes the importance of speed, altitude, and pilot skill in the chaos of air combat. It includes statistics for many frontline fighters and a pilot advancement system for campaign games. All games in the Fly Fight Win series are designed for playability but retain sufficient detail to recreate plausibly historical outcomes. Most scenarios can be played in around 90 minutes. There are enough changes from the Warbirds game to warrant a new title.

—description from the designer

Несказочная заварушка ("The Not Fabulous Mess") is a fast-paced card game that challenges players to strategically place their characters on advantageous cards to score as many points as possible. Players must plan their moves carefully, using the unique abilities of their magical characters to outmaneuver opponents and block them from achieving their goals. With countless strategic possibilities, this small-scale game offers high replay value.

At the start of the game, a kingdom of the required size (based on player count) is randomly laid out using four types of terrain cards. Each player receives an identical set of 10 character cards in their chosen color. They shuffle them and place 3 random cards face down next to them without looking. The remaining 7 cards are kept in hand, hidden from opponents.
