Players take turns clockwise, starting with the first player. Each turn, a player must place one character card from their hand onto any terrain card, following placement rules and using the card’s properties (if any). After playing, they pass the turn. There are two character types:


     Regular characters earn victory points at the end if specific conditions are met (e.g., a Knight placed with a Princess).



     Magical characters do not score points but grant special actions to alter gameplay (e.g., a Witch in a Forest lets you swap two characters on different terrains).


The game ends once all players have placed six characters. Players tally victory points from their regular characters, and the ruler with the highest score proves their worth—seizing the contested lands as their rightful prize.

—description from the publisher (translated)

•••
«Несказочная заварушка» — это быстрая карточная игра, в которой вы попробуете разместить своих персонажей на самых выгодных для них карточках местности, чтобы получить как можно больше очков в конце игры. Планируйте свои действия, пользуйтесь эффектами волшебных персонажей, мешайте соперникам осуществить их планы - в этой небольшой игре довольно много простора для того, чтобы попробовать разные стратегии.

В начале игры из карточек местности случайным образом выкладывается королевство необходимого размера (по числу игроков). Каждый игрок получает одинаковый набор из 10 карточек персонажей выбранного им цвета, перемешивает их и откладывает 3 случайные карточки рядом с собой лицом вниз, не подглядывая. Оставшиеся 7 карточек игрок берёт в руку, не показывая соперникам.

Игроки ходят по очереди, начиная с первого игрока и далее по часовой стрелке. Каждый игрок сделает 6 ходов, сыграв 6 карточек персонажей (1 карточка останется в руке). В свой ход игрок должен разместить 1 карточку персонажа из руки на любой карточке местности, следуя правилам размещения карточек, использовать свойство персонажа (если оно есть) и передать ход следующему игроку.

В игре 2 типа персонажей: обычные и магические.

     Обычные персонажи принесут вам в конце игры победные очки при выполнении определённых условий. Например, если Рыцарь игрока находится на одной карточке местности с любой Принцессой, игрок получит 3 очка в конце игры.
     Магические персонажи не приносят очки, но позволяют игроку выполнить определённые действия, которые могут изменить ход игры. Например, если Ведьма размещена в лес, можете поменять местами двух любых персонажей на разных карточках местности.


Игра заканчивается, после того, как все игроки сыграли по 6 карточек персонажей.
Каждый участник считает свои победные очки, полученные за своих обычных персонажей в королевстве. Выигрывает игрок, который в сумме набрал больше очков. Он побеждает в заварушке и получает эти земли в свои владения.

boardgame: Little Treasures (fan expansion for Silver & Gold)
description:
Yarr, ye scurvy dogs, to arms we go,
With daggers sharp and blades that glow!
Hoist the colors, black and bold,
We chase down ships with pearls and gold!
At dawn we sail, no room fer fear,
Or ye'll mop the deck with yer own beard, hear?

In this expansion, you will be raiding for weapons (gray maps) and pearls (green maps) through a new scoring mechanism. These maps are made to be as lucrative as gold (orange) and gems (purple) maps, opening up fresh strategies and new opportunities as you vie to claim the most treasure.

Setup: Set up the game according to the standard rules. Instead of giving each player the original scoring card, use the new scoring card.

—description from the designer
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description: Chutney is a climbing-shedding card game for three players using a standard deck of cards. In each Deal, one player bids to be the Dipper and plays alone against the Moochers, the other two players in a temporary partnership. The Dipper’s aim is to be the first player to have no cards left. The Moocher’s aim is to stop the Dipper from playing all of their cards first.

Pikel is a domino climbing-shedding game for 2 players.

Players may not rearrange their hand nor rotate dominoes. Unlike other climbers where you can not sort your hands, you can play combinations using any dominoes except the ones that are directly adjacent.

Pikel uses a single set of double 6 dominoes, where players can freely play different combination types on each other, with each combination type having an effect on play.

Dominoes played from your hidden hand get sent to your open hand, where they must be played again (or removed through combination effects). Pickling can be a long process!

Win by shedding out and effectively using combination abilities!

—description from the designer

Congratulations for this once-in-a-lifetime chance. 
You got a mysterious invitation to a magnificent castle that looked like it came straight out of a fairytale.  
But soon the dream turns into a nightmarish horror, when an uninvited guest – a psychopathic murderer – starts going on a killing spree.

Run away without confrontation? or fight with any 
weapon you can find? You decide.

Victory Conditions – Death Invitation
Players must escape from the killer by collecting key items such as a car key and a fuel canister, or by finding a secret door and the secret room key. Another way to win is to eliminate the killer using a gun—but first, you’ll need to figure out who they really are.

—description from the publisher

Uses 6 dice to create combinations of 4 and 5 dice. Players get up to 10 throws of the dice to create 16 different COMBOES. Every time you throw the dice, you get 1 point. So it is to your advantage to throw as few times as possible to get a Comboe (getting a Comboe on your first throw gets you a score of 1!) As the game goes on, you may throw as many 10 times and STILL not get a Comboe - that will result in a score of 10 in some box... not good! Player with the LOWEST score wins!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Spell Arena is a competitive card-based board game where 2 to 6 players take on the roles of apprentice wizards fighting for glory in a mystical arena.

Each player chooses their actions and spells simultaneously, aiming to predict their opponents' moves while managing their hand and surviving the chaos of magical combat. Position in the arena, timing, and bluffing are crucial elements that shape every duel.

With intuitive rules, fast rounds, and a dynamic mix of strategy and chance, Spell Arena offers a thrilling battle experience full of surprises and laughter. Whether you're casting a fireball or dodging a trap, every turn could change the outcome of the match.

Legacy of the Rangers is a cooperative adventure game for 1-4 players. Each player controls a hero from The Rangers: Bloodstone, and works together to protect the villages of the Grimmock Mountains from the Orc raiding parties who are searching for the Bloodstone.

All players have a personal matter they must attend to before they can focus on saving the realm from the Orcs. If the players conclude their business, locate the Bloodstone, and fortify that village from invasion, they win. If the Orcs raze too many villages or secure the Bloodstone themselves, all players lose.

The game is a humorous and interactive party game where players try to avoid laughing while following increasingly absurd rules. The main goal is to keep a straight face while making others laugh. Players sit in a circle and take turns saying "I!" until someone laughs. The first player who laughs draws a card from the "Nouns" deck and must incorporate it into their identity (e.g., "I am a Bun!").

As players continue to laugh, they draw additional cards from different decks: "Adjectives" (e.g., "Chewed"), "Emotions" (e.g., "Pride"), and "Tasks" (which may change the game dynamic). Each new card adds an extra challenge, making the player’s statements more ridiculous (e.g., "I am a Chewed Bun with Pride!"). If a player accumulates four cards and laughs again, they are eliminated. The last remaining player wins the game.

The game is a hilarious and cheeky party experience where players must keep a straight face while delivering absurd phrases composed of random nouns, adjectives, emotions, and tasks drawn from card decks. The adult edition ups the ante with more suggestive, risque, or provocative vocabulary, making it a perfect choice for grown-up gatherings or game nights with friends who enjoy edgy humor.

Players sit in a circle and take turns solemnly saying "I!" while watching each other closely. The first player to break into a smile or laugh draws a card from the Noun deck and must now identify themselves using that word (e.g., "I am a Banana"). Further offenses add layers: a descriptive adjective (e.g., "Hairy"), an emotion to perform (e.g., "Lust"), and eventually a task to carry out (e.g., say the words and ask for applause). The more a player laughs, the more ridiculous and expressive their identity becomes - and they must repeat it with full theatrical flair every round.

The goal is to be the last player remaining who hasn't laughed themselves out of the game. As the word combinations and performances escalate in absurdity (especially in the adult edition), keeping a straight face becomes increasingly difficult - and that’s where the fun lies.
family: Versions & Editions: Adult Versions of Family-Friendly Games

description:
In Heist Night, you play a crew of criminals working together to pull off a series of heists and steal the $100 million you owe the Boss. To succeed in a heist, everyone needs to bring their own set of skills to the table and pull them out of the bag under pressure. Every successful heist nets you a pile of cash, and the aim of the game is to get that pile to $100 million before the SWAT team arrives and puts an end to your plans.

But you can’t trust everyone to be a team player. There’s a chance that one or more of you is an infiltrator: someone who’ll do their very best to stop you in your tracks and bring the Boss down. Infiltrators try to sabotage the heists by slowing them down, gathering evidence and sowing the seeds of distrust. You need to keep your eyes peeled and your ears open. Because as well as getting the money, your mission is to expose the infiltrators and eliminate them.

The criminals need to steal $100 million or eliminate the infiltrator/s before time runs out. Simple as that. The infiltrators work for the cops. They want to stall the criminals before they get the money so that the SWAT team can arrive, or gather as much evidence as they can (filling up the Evidence Track) to take down the gang and the Boss. And any infiltrators who survive a shoot out will have all the evidence they need to snitch on the gang – also leaving them the winners.

Tatort Meer Junior: Schmutziges Verbrechen is a crime game for children, where the children help Manni, the seagull to solve a case:

"Hi, I'm Manni, the seagull. I live in the small coastal town of Nordseil and I need your help to solve a crime.

The seals at the seal sanctuary are healthy and big enough to be released back into the wild. The happy event turns into a disaster when the beach is found to be so polluted that releasing them back into the wild is no longer an option. That would be far too dangerous for the little seals. Help me find out who littered the beach and why, so that the seals can be released safely in the future."

No internet required. No game material is destroyed.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Genshin Impact Liyue Millennial Board Game
description:
The first official board game of Genshin Impact which is related to a game created by Ningguang as mentioned in the lore

It is not to say that the board game version repeats and completely conveys the atmosphere of the original video game, but it amazes with its uniqueness. Of course, here you will not be forced to collect source stones and spin banners, but they will offer to go to Liyue! Liyue millennial board game, or Liyue Millennium, gives the opportunity to play as one of the four merchants of the stone region of Teyvat, whose task is to get rich!

Well, now I will tell you in order:

The mechanics of the board game are similar to the famous monopoly - here you buy shops of different markets (mining, food and entertainment), improve them (unlike monopoly, you do not need to collect "streets"), there is also an alternative to chance cards - a notice board. And yet there are significant differences in the mechanics - there is a fork in the playing field, in addition to the main path, by choosing an alternative you can get additional free upgrades for your shops, there is no prison in the game and there are several ways to move forward faster! Well, the most unusual thing, perhaps, is assembling a puzzle. One of the main goals, besides becoming the richest, is to disassemble the puzzle assembled on a special tablet (according to the lore of this game, we kind of help Ning Guang build a pavilion, and it is considered complete when all the pieces of the puzzle are disassembled), and, as for me, this condition makes it possible to finish the game quickly!

The immersion into the game's atmosphere is also quite pleasant. Those who are familiar with the Genshin Impact universe will be pleased to plunge into nostalgia again! After all, you will come across purchasable stores that you have probably already encountered in the video game, and also visit the Wanshu Inn and the Li Yue Department of Public Affairs and feel like a banker, managing the mora at your own discretion! Also, the order cards for the bulletin board (an alternative to chance) add to the atmosphere: in almost each one, in addition to a specific goal, there is a small insert, as if it were really someone's order!

—user summary

OCTAGION (rhymes with "contagion”) is a fast-paced word-building challenge that uses a specialized deck of cards, each displaying a letter and a number. The catch? You can’t use more than one card with the same number when spelling out a word.

The game accommodates any number of players. Play solo against the clock, go head-to-head with friends, or form teams of two or more. Each team uses its own OCTAGION deck, allowing you to divide and conquer: one teammate can sift through the deck for letters, another can start placing cards to form the word, and a third can spy on opponents to catch any slips.

Gameplay unfolds over eight rounds. At the start of each round, an 8-letter “challenge word” is revealed for all teams to solve. Your goal is to form this word with your OCTAGION cards faster than your opponents without repeating any numbers. As the game progresses, it becomes more challenging after Treatment Cards (wildcards) are used up in earlier rounds.

Included with the game are 800 curated words. Don’t be fooled — finding solutions is trickier than it seems, because many words can only be formed just a few ways without incurring penalties.

Get ready to test your wits, sharpen your word-building skills, and have a blast with OCTAGION. Be warned — it’s contagious!

Be sure to view our How-to-Play videos and reviews on our website octagion.com.

OCTAGION (rhymes with “contagion”) is a fast-paced word-building game that uses special cards, each displaying a letter and a number. The catch is that you can’t use more than one card with the same number when spelling a word. Ideal for solo play, head-to-head matches, or team competitions, each team uses its own deck to strategize. The game consists of eight rounds, presenting an 8-letter challenge word for each round that players must solve quickly. OCTAGION is the ultimate family-friendly speed-spelling game. It’s contagious!

This expansion permits one additional team to play. A team can be one player or several.

There are six different Competition Decks available, each of which has a distinct back design, and different colors for the face cards.

PocketParks is a set collecting card game featuring all 63 US national parks with gameplay inspired by Chinese Mahjong. From Acadia to Zion, it has it all! Each cards features original photography of the park shot by the designer. Cards also include GPS coordinates of the location where each photo was taken to help inspire others to go explore the US national parks.

The goal of the game is to get the most points over 3 rounds and points are earned based on how many and how big your sets are. Sets are created based on 16 different distinguishing theme icons that help define features of the parks (ex desert, volcanoe, island, biggest parks, smallest parks, etc).

At the start of the round players begin with 6 cards, one card is flipped face up to form the discard pile and the rest of the cards are the draw pile. On a players turn, they can do one of three different actions: 1) Play/build on a single set. 2) Draw two then discard one card or play an event card. 3) Pay cards to steal from another players set or hand. The player that has three sets: a set of ≥2, set of ≥3, set of ≥4 will end the round, but all players score points with their sets. sets of 3 are one point, sets of ≥4 are 2 points, and the round winner gets 1 point for each set of 2.

Special rules: 

     Set of 5: with 4-of-a-kind hidden in your hand, once the 5th card is discarded, you can stop the game and claim the set of 5 off turn (play continues on as if you just had your turn)
     Set of 9: if you get 9-of-a-kind in your hand then you can just win from your hand and there is no need play your sets. 


—description from the designer

boardgame: The Summer Job: a Break system gamebook
description:
Summer in Kyoto. The City is Alive… with Danger!

An international syndicate is plotting a heist in the heart of Japan, and YOU are the fledgling thief at the centre of it all. It isn’t just their mission; it’s your adventure.

As the Gion Matsuri festival pulses through the streets, you’ll face unexpected twists that could change everything in the future. Every decision made affects the world, your path… and the end. Dozens of different stories are waiting for you.

Set against the vibrant backdrop of Kyoto’s most famous festival, The Summer Job is the first Break system gamebook. Accessible, fun, and full of surprises, the unexpected is waiting. Ready to roll? The heat — and the heist — is on!

—description from the designer

The prehistoric world is fierce, and only the strongest will dominate. In Megabeast: Last Titans, you control one of Earth’s final dinosaur giants, battling rivals and surviving prehistoric threats. Outfight, outmanoeuvre, and outlast your opponents in this fast-paced skirmish game where every move counts.

In MegaBeast: Last Titans, each player selects a unique megabeast and sets up according to a themed encounter that defines the win conditions and any special rules. Players secretly choose one of five ability cards each turn, then reveal them simultaneously to determine movement, actions, and turn order based on speed. Movement and attack ranges are determined by card stats and current life points, with combat resolved through simultaneous die selection and rolls. Cards cycle through a recovery phase, preventing repeated plays, and the game continues until the encounter is won or only one megabeast remains.

Hobby Next Lavender Promo tiles for Pergola

When you create a pair of lavender leaves, instead of the basic lavender flower or a dragonfly, you may place this new lavender flower between the leaves. It can hold 1 bee, 1 ladybug, and 1 butterfly simultaneously.

The new flower is scored like a normal lavender flower, so it gives 8 points at the end of the game if it has all 3 insects.

boardgame: The Eyes of the Enemy: The Battle for Madrid, November 1936
description:
The Eyes of the Enemy is a moderate complexity level solitaire play game on the battle for the capital city of Madrid during the first months of the Spanish Civil War.

Following liberal victories in Spain’s early 1936 elections the Spanish military in Spanish colonies in North Africa, led notably by Generals Emilio Mola and Francisco Franco, rose in rebellion in July starting a civil war. Aided by planes sent by Adolf Hitler that transported Spanish troops from North Africa to Spain proper, the “Nationalist” regular army soldiers, joined by fascist “Falangist” and royalist “Carlist” volunteers soon began taking control of several Spanish cities and towns in western provinces. These were joined by fascist “volunteers” soldiers and airmen sent by Italian dictator Benito Mussolini, as well as airmen and aircraft from Nazi Germany. The loyal “Republican” Spanish navy and armed forces, as well as some loyal elements of the Spanish Army, were hard-pressed to stop the Nationalist forces, now under the unified command of Franco (General Mola was killed just weeks after the start of the conflict in an airplane crash), who ordered that the capital of Madrid be taken as quickly as possible. However, volunteers and weapons from many democratic nations, as well as the Soviet Union, were also arriving at Spain’s ports and deploying to Madrid. What ensued was the largest battle in Europe since the end of the Great War.

The player takes on the role of the commander of the Nationalist forces seeking to wrest control of key points in and around the city of Madrid that can lead to the capture of the Spanish capital. The rules govern the actions and counterattacks taken by the Republican defenders. As the Nationalist, you will be challenged in taking Madrid as Republican forces fight in terrain well suited for the defense, and as their reinforcements gather. Can you do as well or better?

All infantry units are mostly battalion sized or similarly sized battle groups (Banderas) of 600-800 men, and tank units represent a company of 12 to 15 vehicles. An inch on the map is about three kilometers. Each turn represents 3 days of time.

—description from the publisher

Marches & Batailles is an operational-level Napoleonic wargame played on historical canvas maps with wooden counters. No dice rolling is required - the game play, outcome of battles and morale effects are determined by the drawing of colored tokens.  Belgium - 1815 allows you to relive this epic battle through 6 scenarios and a campaign covering them all!

The map is marked with positions that are more or less distant from one another, where a certain number of units can be deployed, depending on the nature of the terrain. Your units move from one position to another. They are aligned along the connections between positions, giving the impression of marching columns across the map. Units have a different combat value depending on whether they are in column or deployed.

Counters represent around 4,000 infantrymen or 1,600 cavalrymen, and their 3cm front is to the scale of the map. The combat value of a unit varies from 1 to 9, and represents both the morale of the units (militia, line, guard) and the training of the soldiers (conscripts, regulars, veterans).
This value is used to resolve combat situations and morale tests.

The sequence of play makes scrupulous execution of your intentions uncertain. It alternates between marching movements and activations combining offensive, moves and attacks. As with the tests, the order of these actions is determined by colored tokens drawn from an activation bag.

You win by breaking the morale of the opposing army by routing its units, taking its communication points, even capturing its generals in chief.

—description from the publisher

Do you know how many of your friends have never been unfaithful? How many of them bite their nails or pick their noses? And how many of them have ever forged an important signature? Even if you don't know, you can guess.

In the party game Tipni si! Známe se? 18+ (Take a Guess! Do we know each other? 18+) you'll learn the first and last thing about your friends - although you can never be sure who chose the answer. And if you're not shy, you can play an open game and bet directly on a particular player's answer!

There are 900 statements, divided into 6 categories:  Sex, Partying, Relationships, Embarrassments, Vices and Sins. So you'll really learn a lot about your friends - and maybe even things you'd rather not know!

—description from the publisher (translated)
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Versions & Editions: Adult Versions of Family-Friendly Games

description:
Vampire Survivors: The Board Game is a tabletop version of the video game. Brave heroes battle seemingly unending waves of enemies as they hope to survive as long as possible!

This cooperative deck building game has several unique characters, which players will deploy on the gameboard. They must do battle with with fiendish creatures who seek only to exterminate their existence. Players gather weapons and items to increase their powers and gather new abilities so they may outlast the vampire onslaught.

If the players can live long enough, they will make it to the end and win the game, even though Death itself will arrive to destroy them utterly.

But maybe, even Death can die.

—description from the publisher

Exclusive promo card for Russian edition of the game. It allows you to spend 1 victory point in order to place your die on the space on your personal board or on the goal card which was previously occupied by another die. After that you can perform an action from your personal board (second time in the same round) or receive victory points from the goal card. 
Language independent.

Exclusive promo card for Russian edition of the game. It allows you to move your tower to the adjacent empty region. After that at the beginning of the next round you get the bonuses of the new region with your tower.  
Language independent.

In Notebook Nations, you will explore the map to collect encounters, expand your nation via build actions, exploit the map's resources, and work to exterminate other players - or avoid extermination yourself!

4X games can be somewhat intimidating to pick up. Truly, they are big games that seek to satisfy even the most ravenous players. Notebook Nations is a game for those of us who want to have that special 4X experience over lunch, and those of us who seek a gateway that can guide us from being a 4X newbie to a 4X fan.

Players will select a Nation, a map, and a start zone during setup. Then players will take turns selecting initiative bonuses to determine turn order. During their turn, a player will gain their income, move, act, and use Source powers with their Avatar, act with their building tiles to grow their settlements and military, and use their units to fight with their neighbors.

Players will need to make strategic choices in order to win the selected number of victory points from three distinct categories: science VP (by prioritizing research), culture VP (by constructing buildings that increase their culture score), or military VP (by raising and commanding their troops to conquer an opponent's town hall).

Step into the ever-shifting underbelly of a crumbling clockwork city, where factions clash, alliances twist, and the gears never stop turning.

A-Mazing Game of Gears 'N Guffaws is a semi-hidden role, asymmetric strategy game of shifting alliances, sabotage, and steampunk absurdity.

Beneath a crumbling clockwork city, three secret factions battle for control of a labyrinthine maze of rotating tiles and unstable machinery. Players take on the identities of mad inventors, saboteurs, and cunning opportunists, navigating a dynamic hex-tile maze that reshapes itself as the game unfolds, revealing them strategically to activate powerful abilities or conceal their motives just a little longer. Every role is unique, every turn is unpredictable, and every player might be bluffing. Whether you’re a loyal 'Gizmologist', a destructive 'Rabblerouser', or a greedy 'Opportunut', every turn is a chance to outthink (or out-scheme) your rivals.

With clever mechanics and gleefully chaotic expansions, Gears 'N Guffaws rewards adaptability, timing, and a good sense of humor. The game includes two modular expansions and can be played as Semi-Cooperative, Fully Competitive or Solo (includes a three-variant solo mode).

Build the maze, bluff your loyalty, and let the chaos spin in your favor!

A monster-themed version of card game Crazy Eights where what you can play is determined by your place on a board. Players spin the spinner and move their playing piece, landing on a square that determines what rank or suit of card they may play, or whether they must draw cards. The cards have four suits and 11 ranks, with each rank depicting a different monster.

Preface, "hypothesis deductive method" is the earth life evolution on the theme of library Building games (Deck Building Game) It is a game mechanism in which players build their own card decks by playing and evolving cards. The core strategy of the game lies in how to effectively construct and optimize their own card decks while simultaneously restricting the development of their opponents. This type of game usually has high strategic value and replay value because each time a player's deck is unique.
During the game process, players need to flexibly adjust the climate of their habitat, engage in fierce ecological niche battles with other players, and win the victory in a dominant position with outstanding strategies and operations. Or seek opportunities in disasters to enable future generations to thrive in difficult circumstances and write victorious chapters.

前言·《假说演绎法》是以地球生命进化史为主题的牌库构筑类游戏（Deck Building Game）                                                                                                                                                                                                  　是一种玩家通过打出和进化卡牌来构建自己牌库的游戏机制，游戏的核心策略在于如何有效地构建和优化自己的牌库，并同时限制对手的发展。这类游戏通常具有很高的策略性和重玩价值，因为每次游戏玩家的牌库都是独一无二的。
在游戏进程中，玩家需要灵活调整栖息地的气候，与其他玩家展开激烈的生态位争夺，凭借出色的策略和操作，以霸主姿态赢取胜利。或在灾难中寻找机会，让后代在困境中繁荣昌盛，书写胜利篇章。

Cities are going dark! Can you find out why & get the lights back on? Blackout is a thrilling cooperative card game where players band together to save the world from mysterious blackouts targeting the most influential cities.

Whether you're new to strategy games or a seasoned tabletop tactician, Blackout offers an accessible yet deeply engaging experience. Dive into an 18-game story-driven campaign, where each chapter escalates the stakes and evolves your gameplay with new twists, challenges, and ways to play. Alternatively, jump into the Freeplay Mode and select from a range of unique, standalone challenges tailored to your group’s skill and playstyle.

With dynamic teamwork, evolving scenarios, and the world at stake, Blackout delivers an exciting gaming experience you won’t forget. Can your team get the lights back on?

Gameplay
Players take turns strategically playing cards into 2 rows in the center of the playing area. Each mission presents unique challenges for certain cards you want to capture. (or specifically not capture).

Challenges range from kid friendly by simply capturing cards of similar color to a more complex scoring mechanism where your team tries to score specific numbers in each row before the deck runs out.

Other challenges you can expect to see include ranks, runs, playing blind, a timed element, and more.

—description from the designer

Supercards rouba monte animais. Diversão para toda familia com o clássico agora no formato inovado de animais. Contém 54 cartas.
Roube as cartas da mesa e dos outros jogadores. Use cartas com a mesma imagem para roubar. Forme seu monte com as cartas que pegar. Quem tiver o monte maior, vence o jogo.
Dinâmica do Jogo
O jogo segue no sentido horário, e cada jogador, na sua vez, joga uma carta da sua mão. As ações possíveis são:

Baixar uma carta:

Se a carta jogada da sua mão for do mesmo valor de uma carta que está na mesa, você pega a carta da mesa e a sua, formando um pequeno monte. Este monte é seu e fica virado para cima na sua frente.

Se a carta jogada da sua mão não tiver um par na mesa, ela permanece na mesa, virada para cima.

"Roubar" um monte:

Se a carta jogada da sua mão for do mesmo valor de uma carta que está no topo do monte de outro jogador, você pode "roubar" todo o monte daquele jogador, adicionando-o ao seu próprio. Essa é a jogada que dá nome ao jogo!

Se você tiver um monte seu e jogar uma carta de mesmo valor do topo do seu próprio monte, você pode adicionar a carta jogada no seu próprio monte, sem roubar de ninguém. Isso é útil para proteger seu monte, já que ele terá uma nova carta no topo, dificultando que outro jogador o roube.

Fim do Jogo
O jogo continua até que todas as cartas do baralho e das mãos dos jogadores tenham sido jogadas.

Quando o monte de compra acaba, os jogadores continuam jogando as cartas que têm na mão.

Uma vez que todas as cartas tenham sido jogadas, os jogadores contam o número de cartas em seus montes.

O jogador com o maior número de cartas é o vencedor.

Supercards steals lots of animals. Fun for the whole family with the classic now in an innovative animal format. Contains 54 cards. Steal cards from the table and from other players. Use cards with the same image to steal. Form your deck with the cards you take. Whoever has the biggest deck wins the game.

Gameplay
The game proceeds clockwise, and each player, on their turn, plays a card from their hand. The possible actions are:

Play a Card:

If the card played from your hand is the same value as a card on the table, you take that card from the table and your played card, forming a small pile. This pile is yours and stays face-up in front of you.

If the card played from your hand doesn't have a match on the table, it remains on the table, face-up.

"Steal" a Pile:

If the card played from your hand is the same value as a card on top of another player's pile, you can "steal" that entire pile from that player, adding it to your own. This is the move that gives the game its name!

If you have your own pile and play a card of the same value as the top card of your own pile, you can add the played card to your own pile, without stealing from anyone. This is useful for protecting your pile, as it will have a new top card, making it harder for another player to steal it.

End of Game
The game continues until all cards from the deck and players' hands have been played.

When the draw pile runs out, players continue playing the cards they have in their hand.

Once all cards have been played, players count the number of cards in their piles.

The player with the most cards is the winner.

Investigate dinosaur remains and learn all about them!

Nature Expedition is a series of card games for children and families. Each box contains 50 cards with detailed illustrations of each animal, as well as a complete profile, fun facts and quizzes to test everyone's knowledge. A real find for the curious!

Investigate dinosaur remains and learn all about them! This is a real find for those who are passionate about these magnificent creatures!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Investigue os vestígios dos dinossauros e aprenda tudo sobre eles!

Expedição da Natureza é uma série de card games para crianças e famílias e cada caixa contém 50 cartas com ilustrações detalhadas de cada animal, além de um perfil completo, curiosidades divertidas e quizzes para testar o conhecimento de todos. Um verdadeiro achado para os curiosos de plantão!

Investigue os vestígios dos dinossauros e aprenda tudo sobre eles! Este é um verdadeiro achado para aqueles que são apaixonados por essas criaturas magníficas!

—description from the publisher

Step into the shoes of wanderers and explore the land and the Fairy World!
Collect the characters from the film and the magical items! But beware — the evil Mirígy or the mischievous devils might play tricks on you along the way!
Who will be the one to finally collect the most valuable deck of cards?
In this exciting and twist-filled board game recommended for school-age children, the locations and characters from Vörösmarty’s tale come to life through the illustrations of Attila Dargay.


Váljatok vándorokká, és fedezzétek fel a mesés világot, valamint a Föld titokzatos tájait!
Keressétek meg a történet hőseit és a varázslatos kellékeket! Legyetek résen, mert útközben ravasz ördögök vagy a gonosz Mirígy próbára tehetik szerencséteket!
Vajon ki tudja végül megszerezni a legértékesebb kártyakészletet?
Ez a kisiskolás kortól ajánlott, izgalmakkal teli társasjáték Dargay Attila rajzaival eleveníti meg Vörösmarty meséjének szereplőit és helyszíneit.

Experience one of the most beautiful stories in Hungarian literature with the Csongor and Tünde – Card Game! This entertaining game brings Mihály Vörösmarty’s classic to life, offering exciting twists and turns for players. Collect characters and magical items, build the most powerful deck, and become the winner!
The game enhances strategic thinking, memory, and attention, while providing great fun for family or friends during gatherings.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Éld át a magyar irodalom egyik legszebb történetét a Csongor és Tünde - Kártyajáték segítségével! Ez a szórakoztató kártyajáték Vörösmarty Mihály klasszikus művét idézi meg, miközben izgalmas fordulatokat tartogat a játékosok számára. Gyűjtsd össze a szereplőket és varázslatos tárgyakat, alakítsd ki a legerősebb paklit, és légy te a győztes! A játék fejleszti a stratégiai gondolkodást, a memóriát és a figyelmet, miközben remek kikapcsolódást nyújt családi vagy baráti összejöveteleken.

—description from the publisher

The garden is full of delicious vegetables,
but we’re not the only ones who know that—the sneaky caterpillars are on their way to feast on the roots of our precious plants!
Quick, let’s call for help—the mole is our only hope! He’ll chase away the greedy pests in no time.

But which little mole digs the fastest?
Who will be the first to collect all the worms in this cheerful and twist-filled board game?

—description from the publisher (translated)

Tele a veteményes finomabbnál finomabb zöldségekkel. De ezt nem csak mi tudjuk, hanem a huncut hernyók is, amik éppen arra készülnek, hogy a növényeink gyökeréből lakmározzanak. Gyorsan hívjuk segítségül a vakondot! Ő majd megszabadít minket a falánk kártevőktől.

Vajon melyik kis vakond ás a legügyesebben? Melyiknek sikerül elsőként összegyűjtenie a kukacokat ebben a vidám, fordulatos társasjátékban?

—description from the publisher

In Battle Pages you will protect your kingdom from a wave of enemies, using runs of dice to mine gems and tactically place units throughout the map. If you can survive 6 waves and the boss, you win!

Battle Pages is a roll and write dice game that features tactical dice placement, a little bit of press your luck, and strategic map building.

This expands the Battle Pages Core Game with monthly new content.

Blastborn is a post-apocalyptic trading card game (TCG) in a print-and-play format, released for free in May 2025. Players take control of survivor factions in a devastated world, engaging in strategic duels for control of resources, relics, and territory. The game is available for download and home printing through its official website: https://blastborn.com.

Blastborn combines resource management, tactical positioning, and custom deck-building. Each card represents soldiers, machines, upgrades, events, or locations—allowing for a variety of playstyles and both short- and long-term strategies.

Games are played in turns, where every decision matters—from when to deploy troops to when to conserve resources for triggering key abilities. Victory is achieved by destroying all supply cards in the opponent’s logistics line. Once eliminated, the enemy is unable to sustain the conflict, resulting in their automatic defeat—even if units remain on the battlefield.

Although already published, Blastborn remains in active development, with new cards, game modes, and balance updates planned over time. Its ongoing evolution is funded through voluntary donations via its official website, and a professionally printed edition is planned for the future with support from the community.

Some of the artwork in Blastborn was generated using AI tools and later adapted to fit the game's setting. As development progresses, the plan is to gradually expand—and potentially replace—some of the current visuals by collaborating with human illustrators.

—description from the designer

Olé11 is a football inspired tabletop strategy game where you get to build a team of 11 players to score Olé11 goals.

You win Olé11 by scoring more goals than other coaches. The match is played in two halves, with three goals to win in each half:

SPRINT: Be the first to build your team of 11    
TEAM: Have the most players from your team     
STAR: Have the highest value players on your team

The challenge comes with trying to score all three goals at the same time. If you win all three in the first-half, you win the match. If the result is tied at the end of the second half, the tied coaches go to a penalty shootout to see who wins.

Olé11 is family friendly, plays well with 6 people and has depth to master for competitive players. It is suitable for football newbies looking for a new gaming experience and easy to play with friends who don't play many games.

The first edition of Olé11 is called El Classico and features a Play it Your Way section with options to make it easier or harder, shorter or longer. You can also play in teams.

Olé11 launched on Kickstarter on 30 September 2025. A limited release Stadium edition with 7 playing boards and extra gameplay features, and a Top Tactics Expansion + Match Day Experience Pack, was available as part of the Kickstarter.

OQUO is a minimalist, abstract strategy game inspired by the quiet focus of nomadic traditions and the timeless idea of balance. I first encountered the game 2017 during a journey with nomads in the tundra. OQUO uses just 25 “Earthstones” to form the board and 4 “Gamestones” per side. Despite its simplicity, it offers surprising depth, calm tension, and space for shared reflection.

Players take turns moving their stones across a 5×5 grid — in any direction, including diagonally — with the goal of forming a square using all four of their Gamestones. The board never changes, but the possibilities constantly shift. Positioning, awareness, and timing are key.

OQUO is for 2 players, but can also be played in small teams. Teammates quietly plan moves together, whisper strategies, and try to anticipate their opponent’s next step — much like a silent conversation around a fire. The asymmetry of team sizes is allowed and even encouraged, just as it often was in its storytelling origin.

Compact, quiet, and intentional, OQUO fits into any travel pouch and any moment of pause. It’s about presence over pressure — and finding clarity through simple movement.

—description from the publisher

Creature Crash takes the fun-loving water hogs to new levels. If you're looking for brain games and funny games that the whole family will love, these friendly capybaras will do the trick.

How To Play: The first person will roll the die. Using one hand, they'll stack the matching number of capybaras on the tree. The player to the left will go next. If any capybaras fall during your turn, you lose.

—description from the publisher

You are the captain of the three famous Mercy Ships: the Island Mercy, the Africa Mercy and the newest ship, the Global Mercy.
You will be gathering supplies, performing medical procedures on the ships, training local medical staff and collaborating with local governments.

Conquer the tides, plan your strategy and make sure your ships are the first to reach the final location!

Cows In Space is an udderly hilarious adventure that blasts family fun into orbit! It features magnet-powered cows and zany alien challenges designed to delight players of all ages.

Take Moo To Your Leader: Perfect for family game night, this game has one simple goal: get your cows outta the farm, STAT!

How to play:
Use your headband to collect as many magnetic cows as possible. Each cow must be delivered outside of the farm. If cow tipping occurs, reset the game. The player with the most cows at the end wins!

—description from the publisher

Players choose an Associate with unique characteristics, then in each of the 10 rounds, they take turns in the three phases of the game: gathering resources, choosing a compatible Solution card and then expanding their business by acquiring a Territory card accessible from the map, paying resources and taking advantage of those already active and the Conventions due to the chosen Solution. Some Solutions allow the planning of Territories, which can be financed with resources to make them active too. At the end of the round, before organizing the next one, an Event is revealed in random order that applies its double effect, Problem and Opportunity. Some Opportunities allow the planning of Territories.

A Dice-Rolling Race Downstream!

Excitement ripples through the water as fish and fishermen race to the sea! Roll the die to determine your move: will you help a fish swim closer to safety, or will you advance the fishermen's boat, bringing them closer to their catch? The goal is simple but full of suspense—save as many fish as possible before they’re caught! If more fish reach the sea than get captured, the fish team wins. Every roll presents a crucial decision!

With simple rules that can be learned in moments, it’s perfect for introducing young players to board gaming. Once the board is set, the whole family is ready to dive in. And after just a few rounds, children will be ready to enjoy the game independently, making it a gentle and engaging first step into the world of tabletop fun.

How to play:

     Roll the die. 
     Move the matching fish or the boat. 
     Will you be Team Fish or Team Fisher?

boardgame: 6 Richtige: Das Lotto-Spiel ums große Geld!
description:
In this game you are trying to fill out your as much as possible german lottary games on your ticket by collecting the most slides with numbers 1-49 (with seven different frame types) in front of you. 
16 out of 49 shuffeled slides are placed backside up in the middle of the table in 4x4 format for each game. A meeple will be placed on one of the slides and by rolling the dice the meeple will be moved by the current player. He has to guess which frame colour will be located under this slide. If he is correct, he take the slide infront of him. 
By rolling the special signs on the dice, you will get or loose your slides. 
The winner is who gained 6x slides infront of him.

This is a learning game for children about sorting the different arts of rubish. 
46 waste tokens, a box with 6 compartments and a roulette wheel can be combined to 7 different games, activities and tips.
1. identify the materials
2. Which waste belongs to which compartment?
3. Who has cleaned up at first? (two player or team game)
4. Recycle Lotto
5. Recycle Memo
6. Recycle actively at home
7. How to avoid and support recycling

Game I
Setup: 10 pieces each are placed in the squares marked with the pieces and dotted with the same color at the outermost points of the board.
Movement: Each player, in turn, may move one square forward, backward, or sideways, i.e., in all directions along the lines of the board. They may also jump over other players' and their own pieces, as long as there is a free square in front of or behind a piece. Jumped-over pieces are not captured but remain on the board.
The marked squares may not be touched, with the exception of the center square and, of course, the respective goal, into which the pieces are to be moved.
Goal: For each individual player, the goal is the square with the same color dots, located opposite their starting square on the opposite side of the triangle. The winner is the first player to move their pieces to their goal.

Game II.
Setup and Movement: As in Game Type I.
The large marked squares may not be touched.
Goal: To occupy the triangle in the center of the board, which is vacated as soon as the 6 points are occupied and then reoccupied. The winner is the first player to bring all their pieces to the center.

Game III
Setup and Movement: As in No. 1.
The marked triangles on the sides of the board, as well as the one in the center, may not be touched.
Goal: The green pieces must be moved from the green-marked square at the outermost point of the board to the black-marked square at the outermost point. Similarly, black moves to red, and red moves to green. The first player to bring all their pieces to the finish line is the winner.

The children can play on their own to learn the multiplication tables with 1-10 and use it in the game mode against others.
Therefore every player gets a table with multiplication exercises. the result token will be placed in the middle of the table with the number side up. 
In his turn the player says one of his exercises and the result loudly, then he is looking for the result token. If the token shows the symbol of the excersice on the back side, the player was correct and he can place the number token on his table.
The game ends once a player can not place any token on his table anymore. Then every player is counting their collected tokens on the table. The player with the most tokens wins the game.

A game similar to Black Stories. One player reads the riddle in front of the card. The other players try to guess what's happened. The answer on the back of the card is read by the storyteller. The storyteller can only answer yes/no.
The Top secret stories are about 50x top secrect crime cases.

A 2-4 player game, where players play a shield, arrow or sword card to dodge or attack other players. Cards are revealed simultaneously. Card effects and scroll (bonus) cards are calculated and health altered accordingly. Sword beats shield, shield beats arrow and arrow beats sword (Tic Tac Toe). Health is measured using coloured gems. Last player remaining wins. A fun filler game.

This is an expansion for the game Battle Commander Volume I.

It adds six new Battles to that Napoleonic Italian Campaigns game, with two extra  (mounted) maps, 20 new Leader/Commander cards, and a new Playbook.

Volume I Expansion pack will leverage the original components of the main game, adding new Battles, and some extra material. It includes:

A +30 page Playbook with six new Scenarios: MONDOVI 1796 -1st, 2nd and 3rd RIVOLI clashes 1796 -1st CALDIERO 1796 - 2nd CALDIERO 1805.

5 Army Commander and 15 Leader cards, for a total of 20 new cards.

1 double-sided mounted map, featuring the battles of Mondovi and Caldiero.

So you are getting more battles of Napoleon's Italian Campaigns, rounding up nicely Volume I. Three French Defeats, two wins and one draw.

Including the First Battle Napoleon Bonaparte lost; and one 1805 fight, showing the evolution of the Battles towards larger fights.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: Battle of Altum
description:
Battle of Altum is a scenario for use with the Tsukuda Legend of the Galactic Heroes game system.

This is a doujin scenario that needs to be assembled.  It is based off the short story "Thierry Bonnard's Last Battle" in The Legend of the Galactic Heroes Manga. It is a mini-scenario with the siege of the Alliance forces and the private fleet of the Imperial nobility. The Alliance will attack the enemy fleet nestled between the asteroid belt from three directions.

When assembled, the game includes 47 counters, rules, and a map.
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description:
Gadgets is a worker placement, engine-building game highlighted with narrative choice. Set in a steampunk fantasy world, you and your team of artificers help fortify the land against the inevitable dragon attacks. Score the most victory points to become the ultimate victor!

Gadgets is played over three rounds and each round is played on a different map. You may find yourself on a remote island, on an airship high in the skies, or in mysterious tunnels hidden in the earth.  Gameplay is simple and the strategy is engaging, making Gadgets a great medium-weight game.

—description from the designer

Easter is approaching, and the madness begins! The Easter bunnies are competing to create the best production of chocolate eggs. The factory is in chaos! The materials are disorganized, and the stress is palpable. Our construction foreman will give us orders that we must complete as quickly as possible. But we don't just have to worry about the quality and order of our eggs; we also have to watch out for the wolf, the weasel, and even our own colleagues, who are plotting to sabotage our work and steal our eggs. The bunny who finishes their production first wins! Can you handle the stress, outdo your rivals, and emerge victorious?
This game combines the mechanics of "push your luck" and "set collection." A game of skill where you must protect your eggs and steal those of your colleagues. Being the fastest to complete the orders will allow you to win the game, but the chaos of each round can change all your plans.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Lying Judge (うそつきジャッジ)
description:
There is an orange and a light blue god.

The gods are on an equal footing and can each do whatever they want with the universe, but they realize that they want the same planet.

They are omniscient and omnipotent gods, but they are evenly matched, so they cannot be defeated by physical battle.

So they decided to compete for the stars in the bluffing and trick-taking brain card game "Liar Judge"!

The winner gets their favorite star (winning star), but if their lie is exposed, they are forced to live on the trash planet of Liar Moon

Liar Judge is a bluffing and trick-taking game for two players.

If you play a higher number card than your opponent, you win! ... But only one person knows who the real winner is.
Maybe your opponent is trying to claim they won even though they have weak cards?

Get tricks to get "winning stars" , see through the lies and force the "false moon" on them!

A score is written on the card that is played.

Put either the blue or red card face down.

If you are the only one who is red, you will deal great damage to other players!

But if you are the only blue, you will take damage!

Read your opponents mind to escape the Inferno
publishers: Be A Kid Again (オマエんチ)

description:
Number 1 isn't worth it.

Second place is justice and the winner.

Use special cards effectively to take first place and claim second place.
publishers: Be A Kid Again (オマエんチ)

description:
Theme: In Undercover Cop, players dive into a high-stakes heist, secretly taking on the role of either a cunning Thief or a double-crossing Undercover Cop. The Thieves aim to snatch as many valuable diamonds as possible, while the Cop(s) secretly work to sabotage their plans by triggering alarms and getting the loot confiscated by the police. The game's tension builds as players bluff, deduce, and push their luck, never truly knowing who they can trust until the very end.

Gameplay: The game is played over a series of turns where players roll five custom dice, each featuring Diamond, Alarm, Thief, and Cop symbols. On a player's turn, they get up to three rolls, deciding which dice to re-roll (Alarms, once rolled, are locked). The core push-your-luck mechanic centers around the Alarm symbols: if a player rolls three or more Alarms at any point, their turn ends immediately, they steal no diamonds, and the Alarm Marker advances. If the Alarm Marker advances and there are currently more Cops than Thieves in play, diamonds are confiscated from the supply and moved to the Evidence Box. If no alarm is triggered, the player successfully steals diamonds equal to the number of Diamond symbols showing on their dice.

Goal: The game concludes after the Alarm Marker has been moved six times. At this point, a crucial Vote Stage occurs where players discuss and vote on who they believe the Undercover Cop(s) are. Finally, the game moves to scoring: all stolen diamonds accumulated by the Thieves are tallied and compared against the diamonds in the Evidence Box. The team (Thieves or Cops) with the most diamonds wins. In the event of a tie, the Cops win, adding an extra layer of pressure for the Thieves to significantly out-steal the police.

Be the fastest to recreate a loopy-twisty pattern shown on a card to win that card. In a two-player game, you win if you have most cards at the end of the game. In the solo-version you aim to beat your own highscore.

Each player receives a similar set of 5 rectangular blocks.
The pattern cards are shuffled, and players take the a number of pattern cards from the top of the deck corresponding to the nnumber of rounds they want to play. These cards are placed face down in the center of the table. Each round, the top card from the deck is revealed, and the players puzzle to match the pattern with their blocks. The first player to successfully match the pattern wins the card after confirmation.

Нейрогонки Junior ("NeuroRace Junior") is a unique game where everyone is equal. You can gather children of different ages to play and even join in yourself. No need to go easy anymore. Everyone can play to their fullest and enjoy the game to the max.

Each round, each player will have a mission card with a row of requirements and a whole common pool of double-sided tiles to complete it with. Whoever is the first to complete their mission goes up a level that will require even more careful planning and multitasking! As soon as the 10th level is completed, the winner is determined.

There are three game modes: BASIC — for equally skilled players, ALL-ARE-EQUAL — for the players of different gaming skills and ages, and SOLO — for one player.

—description from the publisher

This version of NeuroRace game is designed for younger children and does not include numbers or numerical inequalities on the task cards and tiles.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Pierre Feuille Saumon Ciseaux ("Rock Paper Salmon Scissors") (or RPSS for short) is a ridiculous cooperative game!

Each player draws an RPSS card, and during a round, everyone takes turns saying their first word out loud, then their second, third, and fourth.

Depending on the difficulty level you choose, you’ll have to answer the questions that are asked.

The further you progress through the missions listed in the base rulebook, the more the game will throw wrenches in your plans.

Loki is afoot in the world of Viking Raiders!

Get ready for even more chaos and unpredictability as he messes with the mortals, makes mischief and causes general mayhem for the Viking Chiefs.

He’s having fun, and who knows what he will do next?

In this ever-changing world, who will benefit the most, and who will be quickest to adapt their tactics to the events that Loki unleashes?

—description from the publisher

Have you wisely placed obstacles on the table? Are your characters well positioned, sheltered or ready to attack? Then let's get started! Your spadassin can pounce in force and spread panic in the neighboring camp. Unless you prefer to send in your archer, who is quicker and more discreet. Beware, however, of the opposing mage who will try to cast a spell on your characters. And above all, beware of the fearsome assassin who may approach your king armed with his deadly dagger.

RULES OF THE GAME

Aim of the game: to eliminate the other players by knocking all their pieces off the table.

Equipment: you need a table of any shape and some simple obstacles that can be placed on the table, such as small boxes, books, glasses, bottles, phones, etc. Avoid objects that pieces can slip under, such as shoes, spoons, forks, etc.

Zones: the table is divided into as many zones as there are players. With three players, for example, there will be a red zone, a blue zone, and a green zone. The zones should be equal in size and as large as possible. Choose simple shapes: rectangles, triangles, etc. The zones are only used for setting up the game.

Setup: each player chooses two obstacles and takes all the pieces of their color. In turn, players choose one of their obstacles and place it in their zone. When all the obstacles are placed, do the same with the pieces. The obstacles and pieces must be spaced at least 5 cm apart. Pieces may not be stacked or
placed on obstacles.

Game: Each player takes turns playing one of their pieces by flicking it. If pieces are knocked off the table, they are
eliminated. If pieces are turned over, they are asleep. Obstacles, if hit by pieces, can be moved and even knocked off the table. A player whose pieces are all asleep passes their turn. Each piece has special characteristics explained in the character table in the rules.

Ricochet: If a played piece hits several pieces, then there is a ricochet. If a piece that is hit touches at least one other piece, then there is also a ricochet.

Victory: The winner is the last player who is not eliminated.

boardgame: Bards vs Humility: A Party Game for Spicy Bards
description:
“Cards Against Humanity meets D&D!” Bards vs. Humility is a hilarious and fast-paced party card game for 4-6 players. It takes the wise-cracking fun and adult humour of your favourite party games and straps it into a suit of perfectly-fitted tabletop RPG armour!

In Quick Mode, you play a simple Cards Against Humanity-style game using the Setup and Punchline cards to crack jokes. The player with the best joke gets a point, and first to 10 wins! The rules are familiar, with a few twists and new variants to spice things up!

In Dungeon Mode, the added rules transform the game into a dungeon-crawler, and turns the jokes into a combat mechanic. Slaying dragons with comedy! In this game, everyone is a bard, and each one has a unique ability. Seduce monsters, steal points, screw over your opponents and more! The goal is to explore an ever-changing dungeon, racking up points and fighting monsters by making cringe-inducing jokes. Just like in quick mode, first to 10 points wins.

Bards use inspiration points to pay for their abilities, and can gain inspiration back by delving deeper into the dungeon and from certain Loot cards.

Loot cards give you strategic ways to defeat monsters or screw over your opponents. Items like the Double-Ender and the Spicy Novel can give you an edge, inspire you, or give you bonus points. Iconic Spells like Fireball and Counterspell can be aimed at monsters, or other players for some PVP action.

boardgame: Happy DayZ: Woodstock Fever – Promo Cards
description: These 3 promo cards (Hippie, Children and Scientist) are playable with their respective factions.

In Stars of Ilumeïa, players compete to become the most powerful god in a distant galaxy. To do so, they must win the favour of the many civilisations and fulfil their prophecies.

The players collect star cards and assemble them into constellations according to certain placement rules. For this, they receive victory points or other bonuses from the civilisations. On the star cards, not only the number and position of the stars must be considered, but also other characteristics such as comets, symmetry, flaming stars and much more. New star cards can be acquired from a display. Particularly large constellations score a lot of points, but it can also be advisable to quickly create many small constellations. It is also important to remember to generate additional divine energy with certain constellations, which enables more actions per turn. If a constellation has special characteristics, energy cubes can be used to claim particularly strong bonuses from some civilisations, but the amount of available energy is limited.

—description from the publisher

Shakespeare: The Card Game is a 2 player card game in which players use 52 different characters from across Shakespeare’s plays to race to one of three possible win conditions: Comedy, Tragedy, or History. The games uses an 'I cut, I choose, you play' drafting system in which on player chooses a row of three cards keeping two and passing one, then their opponents has the chance to play first. Tragedy cards all have triggers that will cause them to die - if you kill four of your tragic characters first you win. History cards are played to one of the five lands in a battle for control - if you control 4 lands at once you win. Comedy cards are played once you find both halves of the famous sets of lovers - if you pair 4 couples you win. There are also chaos cards that help (or hinder) the other win condition, with characters like Puck, Bottom, Touchstone, Falstaff and the Witches using mischief and magic to impact the game.

The game is designed with theme first, with each card tied to it's play both visually and mechanically. However, you don’t need to know the plays to enjoy the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Шёл солдат со службы (A soldier was walking home from duty)
description:
Simple roll and move game with some interesting twists. The goal of the game is to be the first soldier who reaches home after retirement.
In the beginning of the journey you have some money, a piece of bread and a flint. But during your trip you can receive some magical items (sush as winged sword, gusli, enchanted comb, invisible hat and so on). All these items could help you to overcome different difficulties and defeat monsters from traditional Russian fairy tales (Serpent Gorynych, Baba Yaga, Leshy and others).

In this game, players follow the guidance of the gods to arrive at "The Shikishigamijima Island" and explore the island, discovering spirits and aiming to win. Players gain victory points by increasing the number of seasonal lands and spirits displayed on their god cards.
publishers: 静岡テーブルゲーム工房 (Shizuoka Table Game Studio）

description:
The three Great Pyramids of Giza suddenly appeared in mid-air, and they were upside down.
No one knows why these pyramids appeared, but apparently, we could enter those inverted pyramids from its' tip.
But, there are also rumors about the existence of some dark pyramids...

In this game, players play cards into 3 pyramids in front of themselves in an inverted shape (The first layer with 1 card, second layer with 2 cards, third layer with 3 cards, and so on).
Cards could only be played in ascending or descending order by layers for each pyramid, or players could discard into the dark pyramid.
During the end of the game, players calculate their score according to their 3 pyramids completed layers with unique multiplying system, and then deduct their score according to their dark pyramid.
The player with the higher score wins the game.

This mischievous game of wit and strategy lets you and 2-5 other players take on the role of leprechauns competing to gather the most gold as quickly as possible. To win, you must hold at least 30 gold at the start of your turn.
Gold is obtained by either the effects of Action Cards and/or Charm Cards, or by skipping a play action on a players own turn (at a rate of 1 gold per skipped play). There are two varieties of cards, Action and Charm. Action Cards' effects are exclusively immediate and single use. Charm Cards have some sort of lasting effect that may be indefinite or limited duration (either for a number of turns or on a specific condition). Cards can be purchased with gold in randomly drawn bundles of 1, 2, and 3 cards (with prices in gold randomly determined by a d4, d6, and d8 respectively). Each player (in order) will have a chance to make 1 purchase among these card bundle options, after which each player takes a turn (also in order), playing up to two cards from their hand. Gameplay continues until any player holds 30 gold at the start of their turn, upon which the game immediately ends.

—user summary

Five Boroughs is a polyomino tile-laying game in which players compete over three rounds to build and grow the best city. Each round, players use cards to obtain and place tiles to expand each of their five boroughs, connect parks and surround skyscrapers to gain bonuses, and score based on how successfully they've built their overall city. The player with the most points at the end of three rounds of scoring wins.

—description from the designer

IntroductionGadur (from Proto-West Germanic: gadur, meaning "together" or "gathered at one place") game is a drawless self-stalling game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 4 cells per side, but boards of 3 or 5 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is the maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform the following actions in the order listed:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty cell not adjacent to any friendly group, unless the group formed by the placed stone touches at least two enemy groups whose total sizes are at most equal to the size of your new group.
     Remove from the board all enemy groups adjacent to your new group whose total sizes are exactly equal to the size of your new group.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when a player cannot place any stones; in that case, that player wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Escape Box: Der Tempel des Drachen
description:
Der Tempel des Drachen is a cooperative Escape Game for Kids where you (... well the siblings Flora and Henry von Stein) have to find the hidden amulett in the temple of the dragon.

You have to 'riddle' your way through 50 cards (... although the majority of the cards are 'non-riddles) to complete your journey.

Nothing is destroyed during the journey as you work your way through the temple using a dry erase pen. So the game can be easily played again by another group.

Game obviously was supposed to be the start of a series as the subtitle is 'Krimigame for Kids - Nr.1', but there never was a Nr.2 and Nr.1 seems to be out of print, too (but the solutions are still available on the publishers page).

boardgame: My very first games: My Home
description: In two sorting games, you get to know and name everyday objects. The first game is all about exploring rooms, while the second focuses on sorting items into categories. What can you find in the house, what can you find, outside?

STORY
Your ship the Leuria Pride 2, a heavy cruiser class ship has been deployed in the Battle of Border Zone 005 when you received an urgent & coded transmission from HQ.  An experimental  enemy ship called the Aquarius Heavy cruiser has been sighted in Federation space conducting espionage mission. Knowing that it has been sighted in open space, it is currently trying the escape and rejoin the main Empire fleet at the border. You must intercept and destroy this ship before it reaches the safety of the main fleet and  relay any tactical information it has gathered from Federation space.

THE OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME
The objective of the game is to destroy the enemy ship before it reaches and joins the enemy fleet. The game is played by going through Movement deck (representing the course plotted for the attack), managing your ship’s various systems and blasting the enemy with your ship’s weapons.

—description from the designer
The dogs jump up joyfully at the huge can of dog treats. Feed them colorful and tasty treats and collect cards. The player with the most cards at the end wins the game.

Twisted Words is a dynamic, ever-changing word game with strategy and creativity. It is fast-paced, fun, exciting and unpredictable.  For 1 to 5 players.

Twist Your Thinking, Shape Your Words, Outplay Your Competition!

COMPONENTS:
30 magnetic handpainted cubes, each laser-etched with …
- a vowel
- a consonant 
- 4 bigrams (2-letter fragments).
Each letter has a Scrabble-like scoring value.

Rules for both Solo and Multiplayer games.

Weave words, pass cubes, and outscore your opponents!

FRIDGE GAME:
Stick the cubes on your fridge (steel) door to compete with your family! A daily vocabulary challenge with fun words.

MULTI-PLAYER GAME: 
Setup – Each player starts with four cubes (or 3 cubes for 5 players).  
Build Your Best Word – Twist and align your cubes to form the highest-scoring word possible - in two minutes.
Score your word.
Pass Your Highest Cube - Shift the game dynamics by passing the most valuable cube to the player on your left.  
Repeat Five Rounds – Adapt, strategize, and maximize your score with shifting cubes.

Best Total Score Wins!

Every round is a dynamic challenge as players craft words, adjusting strategies as cubes move around the table.

SOLO MODE: Use all 16 cubes to construct at least four words and challenge yourself to find the best, highest scoring letter combinations!

—description from the publisher
With a little lucky rolling, your bees will fly to the available flowers. The first player to get their bees back in the beehive wins.

Welcome to the dark future - where chrome shines brighter than the sun, neon never sleeps, and data flows faster than you can blink. Style is survival, and bytes are bigger than bullets. And you? You’re a Crittmancer - part engineer, part artist, all genius. You craft cyber critters: mechanical beasts built either for clients, fame, or just the thrill of bending metal and code into something alive(ish).

Crittmancer is a roll-and-write game for 1 to unlimited players. Players will use dice to construct modules of their critter by filling them with numbers according to module's requirements.

—description from the publisher

Night of Wolves is the first in our "Night Of..." series: a solo storytelling card game of survival, sacrifice, and shattered honour. As the last of the Wolves of the North — betrayed, hunted, and alone — you must navigate a treacherous land filled with haunted ruins, brutal enemies, and desperate strangers. Each turn, choose your path from evocative full-art encounters, flip the card, and face a story-driven decision that could earn you gold, allies, weapons. or curse you with wounds, madness, and ruin.

Tuck cards under your Hero to track your evolving journey — every choice leaves a mark. Will you become a Saviour, a Hunter, a Saint? Or will your body and mind collapse before you reach the end? With myriad branching outcomes, emergent narrative arcs, and a brutal boss at the finish line, Night of Wolves challenges you to survive a dangerous fantasy world and see if one lone howl can still echo loud enough to shake the halls of power.

Objective: Survive the journey by reaching the bottom of the card encounter deck, and overcome the final boss encounter to win. Victory is rare — expect to lose 2 in 3 games until you are more familiar with the game!

—description from the publisher

Railheads: England & Wales is a cube rails game about the Railways Act of 1921. It was introduced as a major reform to address the inefficiencies and fragmentation within Britain’s railway system. The Act aimed to streamline the industry by grouping the existing small and often competing railway companies into four large regional companies, commonly known as the "Big Four", and in doing so improve the efficiency of both passenger and goods transport by consolidating resources and simplifying management across regions.

In addition to grouping the railways, the Act also introduced the concept of "Running Rights", allowing each of the Big Four to run their trains over routes outside their designated regions when needed. Meanwhile, although the Underground railway system was not incorporated into the Big Four, it continued to expand independently, playing a crucial role in urban transit, particularly in London, complementing the regional rail services.

Through the use of a highly interactive and cascading action selection mechanism the players compete by laying tracks, making use of the newly established regional borders, developing industries, and making investments with one ultimate goal in mind, to be the wealthiest magnate when the game ends.

Railheads: England & Wales is the fourth installment in a series of cube rails games that all share a prime action selection mechanism as well as a central theme of both railroad network and industry refinement getting developed in tandem.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Atlas: Explore the World – 5-6 Player Expansion
description:
Expand your game of Atlas: Explore the World even more! 
Includes: 2 new sets of player colored components, 4 new characters, 10 new Personal goals, 1 new bot, 1 additional Flag manual, 1 additional score pad.

HAKO is an award-winning strategy and logic board game set in an exciting, multi-level labyrinth. The goal of the game is for players to move their beads from the bottom level of the box to the top, navigating through various paths, tunnels, and traps. With up to 5,000 different routes to the finish, every playthrough offers a new and unique challenge.

The game is designed for 1–4 players, making it enjoyable both solo and in groups. Players take turns moving their beads through the box, aiming to reach the highest level. Choosing the wrong hole causes the bead to roll back to the bottom of that level, requiring the player to try again. Success relies not only on speed but also on strategic and logical thinking. HAKO is recommended for educational purposes in both physics and mathematics, as it develops problem-solving skills and spatial awareness. The game won a bronze medal at the IENA International Trade Fair for Ideas, Inventions and New Products in Nuremberg, Germany.

HAKO offers endless replayability thanks to its varied labyrinth system, making it entertaining for all ages and enjoyable for the whole family.

—description from the publisher

A deck building game based on the loved indie horror/comedy, CarousHELL! Build your decks using characters from CarousHELL 1 and 2 as well as from the rest of the Silver Spotlight Films universe in order to defeat the stack of Nemesis Cards. Players choose a character to play as that each have their own special power. Players then build their decks through the purchase of cards and each card has a power value/point value on it. Cards are used to defeat the villains in the villain deck and the game ends when this deck runs out. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Take charge of one of the great kingdoms of this era and battle your rivals for control of Italy.
Manage your armies wisely, negotiate with your opponents, fortify your borders, exploit your resources and make the right choices. Only one sovereign will emerge victorious from this adventure... unless the pope manages to save Italy?

Italia 1497 is not only a strategy game, it's also a gateway to the 16th century. 
Through the universe, scenarios and events, you can explore the Italian wars and their stakes, as well as all the major trends of the century: the Reformation, the Great Discoveries, the barbarians, etc...

—description from the publisher (translated)
