Drunken Chonker is a ridiculous, party-ready card game set in the unhinged, snack-filled universe of Chonker Party — only this time, the Chonks are buzzed. Each deck is packed with boozy antics, cute art, and enough party energy to make even a sober Chonk stumble.

Sip, Snack, Score:
Take turns playing cards to win rounds and earn points — or just drink your way to victory. Yes, drinking will give you points.

Flip the Table:
Use Action Cards to turn the game on its head. Make others drink, steal their cards, or force them into outrageously unfair situations. It's fast, it’s foolish, and it’s full of surprises.

Get Tipsy, Get Tactical:
When the deck runs out, the player with the most points wins, whether they played smart, played dirty, or just outdrank everyone else.

—description from the publisher

Rainbow Mancala is a two-player combinatorial sowing game played with 48 seeds, eight in each of six colors, on a Mancala board having two rows of six holes with storehouses at each end.

Games in the Mancala family usually have indistinguishable seeds, but sowing games with distinguishable seeds, while uncommon, are not new. A few examples are listed in the prior work section of the rules document. The mechanisms of Rainbow Mancala mostly also follow precedents set in other traditional sowing games. However, new to Rainbow Mancala, at least as far as the authors know, are non-transitive strength cycles (as in Rock-Paper-Scissors) for harvesting the distinguishable seeds, as well as player choice of whether or not to harvest a second seed (if one is available).

—description from the designer

Chonker Team is a rowdy, team-based version of Chonker Party where cooperation meets chaos. Designed for 4 or 6 players (but can be played individually), this standalone game pits teams of Chonks against each other in a strategic snack-off filled with betrayal, bluffing, and big plays.

Team Up, Snack Hard:
Form teams and work together to win rounds and score points—but be careful, your partner’s wild plays might just backfire on you.

Disrupt the Enemy:
Play Action Cards to mess with your opponents—or even your own teammate. Force discards, steal plays, or flip the round in your favor.

Snack to Victory:
The team with the most points when the deck runs out wins the game. Just don’t lose track of your strategy (or your partner’s).

Each deck features cute art, hilarious combos, and all the unpredictable fun, now with double the drama.

—description from the designer

Trick-taking game in which players must place both a bid for the number of tricks they expect to take and a wager which determines the how many points they will receive if they hit their target bid. The wager is made by discarding cards from a player's hand, meaning that players who wish to score big must play from abbreviated hands.

Pollard originally was published in the You Can Play These Games with Cards card game compendium.

—description from the designer

The defender (Swiss soldiers) may place his 6 red pawns in the middle square (fortress) at his discretion on the black squares.
The 24 attackers position themselves at the bottom edge of the 4 outer squares, on the 6 lowest black squares.

The defender may move his pawns forward and backward on the black squares and must capture enemy pawns by jumping over them if possible. If he fails to capture, the offending piece may be taken prisoner by the attacker. The attackers may only move forward, but may never capture.

One of the attackers begins the game and moves, after which the defender makes his move. After each attacker's move, the defender moves to counter the attack. The red edges of the fortress are walls that can't be moved over.

The game ends:

     When the attackers have occupied both spaces A and B, or when they have surrounded the defender so that he cannot move forward or backward. Pawns on A and B cannot be captured.
     when the defender has defeated the attackers in such a way that only 2 pawns of the enemy remain.


—user summary

boardgame: Mate: The Party Game for Feral Naturalists
description:
Adventure into the world of animal mating! In Mate: The Party Game for Feral Naturalists, your team of fellow feral naturalists will analyze specimens, consider queries, estimate measurements, and craft illustrations — all exploring animal mating behaviors and anatomy.

Players join the Society of Feral Naturalists. Their field guide and notes facilitate a wild adventure into the world of animal mating.  But be careful, this adventure is not for the prudish!

In Mate, teams work together to analyze animal sexual anatomy, conjecture on absurd questions, and sketch ridiculous prompts.

     Sexual Selection: Which absurd choice is correct?
     Mutual Drawer-gasm: Race to sketch a hilarious prompt.
     Mystery Mate: Which creature’s parts are in the photo?
     Size Matters: Guess on a wild number query.


Set sail! The team that navigates around the compass first wins.

Warlords of the Steppes is a competitive, easy to learn and roll to slay game. You play as a legendary warlord with unique powers who wishes to be the next Great Khan. You draw recruits from the deck, train your warriors as dice and slay monsters with rolls.

The game is set in a steppe landscape, enriched with mythological elements. Players aim to increase their fame and glory, and secure the people's support to etch their names in history in golden letters. The steppe is a perilous land, constantly under siege by mythological foes. Warlords send warriors recruited from the youth to face these enemies, earning glory for each defeated foe. However, some enemies are ancient nemesis of the people, and any Warlord who allows them to breathe must bear the burden of shame.

When all threats are eradicated and the legendary creatures of prophecy called omens have emerged, the time comes to crown the next Great Khan.

The game features heroes, creatures, gods, spells, and warlords inspired by real-world steppe mythology and legends.

—description from the publisher

Goal is to own a distillery in each whisky-region. The game ends when one player owns a distillery in each region. Points are given for region properties and distilleries. The players ride through Scotland, either by bus, train or ship at different costs. Dice rolling movements. Arrived on cities or sightseeing areas the player can buy or deal with distillery license cards. The cards confirm the ownership of a distillery. The gameboard shows the most famous whisky distilleries of Scotland on a detailed map of Scotland.
The moving possibilities (bus/car, train or ship)between the different cities or distilleries are shown in different colours. Game ends after a fixed time or when one player has a distillery in each region and the appropriate winning points.

—user summary

28 is a two player trick-taking game using a double six set of dominoes or domino cards. Players receive 13 dominoes as their hand, two of which they will set aside face down to comprise their bid for the number of tricks they expect to take. Bids are calculated by looking at the difference between the ends of each domino, and adding those differences together. The maximum bid therefore is 11 and the minimum bid is 0.

Both players then play out a trick-taking game using trick play reminiscent of Texas 42. At the end of 11 tricks, players reveal their bids and score points for their tricks as well as a bonus if they succeeded in taking as many tricks as they bid.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 帰ってきたウルトリテン (Return of Ultriten)
description:
帰ってきたウルトリテン (Return of Ultriten) adds Evil Monsters to Ultriten, the trick-taking game where heroes battle giant monsters!

Evil Monsters are worth extra victory points when defeated, and they possess a strength halfway between that of Monsters and Heroes. While regular Monsters turn the negative points of fallen cities into positive points, Evil Monsters double the penalty.

Overview:
Burl is an elimination game for two players – Black and White – played on an initially empty hexhex board with side length 4 or 5. It is a game of placements and captures. You only place stones of your own color, and only onto empty spaces, and you only remove stones of your opponent's color. Black goes first.

Definitions:
A permanent stone is a stone that is part of any set of 3 mutually adjacent stones of the same color. All other stones are removable.
To trim a group is to remove all removable stones from it. Permanent stones are never removed.

Play:
1. If a stone was removed in the previous turn, place a permanent stone. If not, place a removable stone.
2a. After placing, trim every enemy group that has no space to add a removable stone.
2b. If you removed any stones in step 2a, go on to also trim every enemy group that has no space to add a permanent stone.

Goal:
If, at any point during your turn, your opponent has no removable stones left, you win.
An exception is made for the very first turn, when White has yet to place their first stone.

—description from the designer

Music-themed escape game. Players work together to solve puzzles in a hybrid physical/web-based experience.

Players work together to solve puzzles in a unique blend of physical and web-based experiences. By solving the puzzles left behind by Alf Stormbringer, legendary adventurer, they uncover pieces of a long-lost musical score. One said to calm the roiling seas. As the story unfolds, so do the harmonies, drawing players deeper into a journey of melody, mystery, and teamwork.

PowerPunch! is a fast-paced boxing board game where players step into the ring as over-the-top fighters from around the globe. Using custom dice and explosive combos, you’ll trade blows, manage stamina, and win over the roaring crowd to build momentum and land the perfect knockout. Outwit your opponent with smart timing, powerful finishers, and crowd-pleasing flair in this high-energy game of fists, fame, and fun.

—description from the designer

In Loom, players take turns weaving vibrant threads into a growing fiber mosaic. By aligning and connecting different color swatches, players form patterns of Loops, Weaves, and Chains. The more intricate the pattern, the more points it earns.

Points are scored as patterns are completed, and the player with the highest score after all swatches have been placed is declared the Master Weaver.

Tank Club is a free fast-play game for platoon-level armoured engagements in which players field one or two platoons of tanks.

Players will need a few tanks each, a measuring tape, d10, bag of damage tokens and an arc tracking device.

Many a history book will tell you that the art of the aerial dogfight was discovered in 1915, as various countries came up with the concept of strapping one or more belt fed machine guns to the front end of powerful, manoeuvrable biplanes. What those history books neglect to tell you is that half a century earlier, before the invention of the aeroplane, far off in the hot deserts of the orient, men were engaging in a similar sport. this time on land, with the newly invented Gatling gun strapped to the front of a ferocious and manoeuvrable Camel. It was these gun-laden beasts who inspired the name Sopwith Camel.

With this rulebook, we aim to show you the true original art of the dogfight, of the Gatling Camel Riders (GCR).

—description from the publisher

Play your cards right to be the first player to reach one million subscribers. Collect success cards and prevent your opponents from beating you by throwing fail cards at them to make them lose internet or blur their video. Grow your channel to win the golden button!

The goal of the game is to score a total of 8 monster points by collecting monster cards and creating rhymes. On your turn, roll the 4 monster dice with the goal of collecting all the monsters shown on one of the 4 face-up cards on the table. If you wish, and only once, you may choose to roll one or more dice again. If you have collected all the monsters shown on one of the cards, keep that card and score the points indicated in the corner. Remember, you must complete the rhyme shown on the card you just earned!
Build the biggest skyscraper. Buy and sell properties, give them utilities and services to give them more value. The player who gets to build the biggest skyscraper with all their parts, wins.

Called Mister Mastro, alluding to master Bentivegna, according to legend the builder of the Ponte del Diavolo in Tolentino, the board game us entirely financed by Confesercenti of the province of Macerata, chaired by Roberto Cartuccia and created by three young professionals from Tolentino in the communication and graphics sector.

The authors are Silvia and Guido Raparo, a close-knit father and daughter who have always been grappling with colors and brushes, the journalists Sauro Ciarapica and Fabiola Caporalini and the graphic designer Andrea Raggi, who is also the creator of Mister Mastro and who coined its name.

The board game recalls the legend of the construction of the Devil's Bridge of Tolentino, as it was written and handed down by the Augustinian, Father Domenico Gentili. The story tells of the providential and miraculous intervention of Saint Nicholas, who, with a stratagem, mocked the devil who wanted to seize the first soul that passed over the bridge. The game also reconstructs the history of Tolentino in 1200 and the life of Father Nicholas.

The game is produced on the occasion of the seventh centenary of the death of the saint, a native of Sant'Angelo in Pontano, but who always lived in Tolentino. The anniversary will fall on September 10, but Mister Mastro will be on sale from the middle of this summer. The box contains, included in the purchase price, a mini guide, with all the synthetic but essential information about Tolentino. Mister Mastro was created with the consent of the Augustinian Community.

Confesercenti intends to promote the territory, especially at a national level, given that, presumably, Mister Mastro will be able to constitute a souvenir for pilgrims, tourists and visitors. The game is created in collaboration with the Mountain Community of Monti Azzurri of San Ginesio, of which San Nicola is the patron saint and whose territory has deep ties with the figure of the saint, who was born and lived in the current district territory. San Ginesio was the center where most of the canonization process of the saint took place.

Players take positions on opposite sides of the game and start at the same end, so you're both competing for the same hook. Then, start swingin' to try and capture the hooks. Once you capture a hook, your opponent can't reach it. If you knock a ring off a hook you already captured, you've got to go back and recapture it (before your opponent does). The first to snag three hooks wins! Fast-paced, uniquely challenging, and foldable for easy storage, Hook Rally is an instant family favorite!

A head-to-head hook and ring race!
Encourages hand-eye coordination, concentration
Swing your rings to try and snag them onto the hooks
Once you capture a hook, it's pulled out of reach of your opponent
Players start on the same end so they're always competing for the same hook
First player to capture 3 hooks wins the game
Frame folds up for easy storage - features hook-and-loop straps for securing the rings
Includes one Hook Rally set
Detailed gameplay and assembly instructions included
Frame made of high-quality wood, chains and rings made of durable metal

—description from the publisher

《大喜临门》是一款支持1-5名玩家的恐怖题材桌游，以风险管理与成套收集为主机制。在一局游戏中，玩家将进行六轮的玉佩收集，最终拥有最多玉佩的玩家获得游戏胜利
The theme is based on horror games, with risk management and set collection as the main mechanics.
"Weddings of Woe" is a horror-themed board game that supports 1-5 players. In a game, players will go through six rounds of collecting jade tokens, and the player with the most jade tokens at the end of the game wins.

Players roll coins to decide which 3 doors to open (based on the coin results, they can open auspicious or inauspicious doors). Opening an inauspicious door has a chance of triggering a monster's appearance, and if a monster appears for the third time, all collected rewards are canceled.

After opening doors each round, players decide whether to stay or leave; those who stay can equally share the rewards obtained (similar to "Incan Gold"). Players continuing forward will roll coins again. At the beginning of the game, players also receive wish cards. Collecting the necessary items for the wish cards within a round can earn points. After 6 rounds, the total score is calculated.

In this thrilling tabletop experience, players will take command of powerful fleets representing iconic nations—Germany, the USA, Great Britain, or Japan—during a pivotal era of naval warfare.

Embark on an exciting journey of naval warfare and strategic dominance-it’s a test of wits, tactics, and creativity as you command your fleet and outmaneuver your opponents in a battle for supremacy on the high seas.

The goal is to accumulate points by demonstrating strategic superiority over your opponent. Players earn points through the following actions:


     Sinking Enemy Ships: Each enemy ship sunk grants a victory point.
     Hitting Opponent's Army: Damaging but not destroying an enemy unit grants a victory point.
     Shooting Down Enemy Plane: Each plane shot down adds 1 to your total points.
     Attacking Opponent's Airfield: Successfully hitting the opponent's airfield yields a point reward.


—description from the designer

Rascally Rabbits is a lighthearted yet strategic trick-writing game for 2 to 6 players. Mischievous bunnies are sneaking into gardens to snatch veggies, but only the cleverest plans will succeed! Each round, players receive a unique garden card showing a different arrangement of numbers from 1 to 16, then secretly customize parts of it to meet their goals.

At the start of each trick, the lead player chooses a crop (suit), and others must follow if they can. The highest number in the lead crop wins the trick and becomes the next lead. If a player matches both the crop and number exactly, they collect a bunny—a bonus that can score points later.

The round ends after 13 tricks or when one player has won seven. Players score points for winning tricks and for accurately predicting their total at the start of the round. Even players who win zero tricks can still earn points based on the bunnies they’ve gathered throughout the game.

After three rounds, the player with the most points from bids, tricks, and bunnies is crowned the most rascally rabbit of all!

—description from the designer

In this portable travel version of the tableau building game Atlas: Explore the World, players will explore and experience the world! Players do this by playing cards to collect different icons that represent what that Location is known for. Explore 125 different locations from all over the world from Antarctica to Greenland, experience parties in Copenhagen to Hiking up Mount Everest. Complete your Goals from a deck of 25 goal cards with 2 different missions on each card.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
You are a medieval spice merchant. It is early morning and you are pushing your rickety wooden cart through the thick mist. You need to carefully navigate the cobbled road. You keep losing containers with precious spice along the way while other merchants jump for the chance to pick up what you have lost along the way. It is a hustle among all the spice merchants to lure just the right customers to pair with their available spices. The richest spice merchant at the end of the day is the winner.

Cobblestone Road is a mancala style card game. On their turn players can either play the mancala to obtain spices or fulfill customer cards to obtain money. Customer cards can alternatively be used for their special actions. Players can score extra money for sets of identical customers served.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Adventurer's Guild Girls: The Board Game
description:
Adventurer's Guild Girls - The Board Game is a deck-building board game created by the doujin circle "PaintCan (缶子/罐子牧場)", active in both Taiwan and Japan. It is the first physical game based on the popular "Adventurer's Guild Girls" series.
Step into the world of "Adventurer's Guild Girls", lead your adventurers to take on the impossible, and rise to become the greatest guild leader of all!

In this game, players take on the role of a guild, recruiting various classes to battle monsters. Each round follows a flow similar to Dominion, using deck-building, bag-building, and pool-building mechanics to strengthen their card pool.

Players must continuously enhance their team by recruiting new classes and acquiring equipment in order to defeat monsters and bosses, and to complete their Bond cards (objectives). All of these contribute to the player's final score. The player with the highest score at the end wins.

In medieval India, the mighty Vijayanagara Empire crumbled after the devastating Battle of Talikota, where King Rama Raya was beheaded and his forces shattered. From this chaos, the southern Nayaka governors claimed independence, establishing their kingdoms amid the empire's ruins. As one of these ambitious Nayakas, you will command your people towards a new golden age. Under your rule, master craftsmen create majestic temples, ingenious water systems, and formidable fortresses—architectural marvels that will endure for centuries as a testament to your visionary leadership. Can you claim victory by taking your kingdom to its prosperity?

In this strategic worker placement game, 2–4 players compete over multiple rounds to build the most prosperous kingdoms. Each round, players take turns placing their two distinct types of workers to gather essential resources (stone, wood, culture, and gold) and recruit skilled artisans or participate in events. Players then take turns to build monuments—temples, water tanks, and forts—claiming prosperity points or bonus tiles.

The game ends when either a pre-determined number of buildings are completed or the events run out. The player with the most prosperity points wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ghost Kitchen: Personalmangel in der Geisterwelt
description:
Chaos in the ghost kitchen: which recipes are on the menu and where are the right ingredients? Good staff is so hard to find and time is running out. Each ghost you hire has their own skills - and you'll need them if you want to gain an advantage in the race for the right ingredients. Some ghosts give you more flexibility when acquiring ingredients, others give you a boost in the final score.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A game of deduction where you are trying to collect 5 different numbers without getting 2 bombs.

Ill-Illan, (いる-いらん, Romanized as Iru-Iran) is a Osaka-dialect for "need" (Iru) and "no need" (Iran).
In this game, players decide to receive or reject a card, thus the name of this game.
During a round, players draw a card and show them to other players without seeing the number themself, and all other players

The ice is finally starting to melt. The freezing winds of the Cais Adarra are behind us now, and the Reveal Season for Whispers from the Maze is finally here. It’s time for our expeditions to move forward!
The ascent through the Cais Adarra was a challenge for our expeditions, but the effort was worth it, if only to witness Kuraokami, the Ice Dragon, take flight again and soar over the highest peaks of the mountain. Their destination is now clear—the enigmatic City of Scholars—a place that might hold the answers they’re looking for and their next stop on their search for the source of the Tumult.

When the expeditions get there, the city, or what is left of it, is a strange sight to behold. Only crumbling ruins remain, surrounding a colossal chasm that plunges into unfathomable darkness. While a part of our expeditions establishes a settlement on the surface, the other begins its descent to discover an enigmatic labyrinth in the city’s depths.

Many treasures may lie within those ancient halls, but be careful not to delve too far—some things are better left in the dark.

Drive is a competitive card game built around the concept of canine “Drives,” each represented by a unique symbol and ability on a card. Players embody dogs tapping into instincts like “Herding,” “Survival,” or “Possessiveness,” using these Drives to fulfill Missions laid out at the center of the table. The thematic focus on dog behavior gives each card both a flavor and a mechanical twist, encouraging strategic use of special abilities to outmaneuver opponents while racing to complete tasks.

Gazebo: Elevation Expansion includes two additional garden maps — the Botanical Garden and Jungle Garden. Each map introduces new features and strategies!

Botanical Garden introduces raised patios — a player needs two adjacent gazebos (instead of one) in order to claim them. Because these patios are harder to claim, this increases the competition and interaction between players as they race to connect two gazebos first.

Jungle Garden is the ultimate map for experienced players — it combines the unique features of the other three maps into one epic competition! Jungle Garden also introduces pedestal spaces — players can cover these spaces with a domino to trigger the vase feature (play a second domino in the same turn).

—description from the publisher

Centuries after the crystal network of Azar shattered, four ancient houses rise to restore its lost harmony. Each player becomes a channeler of power — with a unique ability, a secret target shape, and a power number (0, 3, 4 or 5) that alters the energy flow of the board.

This narrative expansion transforms classic Triominos into a strategic battle of pattern mastery and elemental influence. Dynamic Event Cards reshape the board each round, while players chase hidden geometries for massive bonus points. Your legacy isn't just about points — it's about restoring Azar’s ancient grid.


     New Mechanics
     Unique character abilities tied to your power number
     Secret shape objectives worth up to +50 points
     Dynamic events every round that shake up the rules
     No point deductions at game end — every tile matters


Can you master the energy of Azar before the network collapses again?

—description from the publisher

Ever wonder what happens when weeds strike back? Sprout, grow, and thrive as you compete to rule the garden! In Weeds Garden Takeover players will use their wits to analyze the size and quantity spread of opposing weeds. The environment around you and your unique abilities will help to spread your weeds and prevent others from shriveling your roots in the process.

Weeds blends familiar mechanics that both new and seasoned gamers enjoy, such as:

Strategic analysis inspired by Liar’s Dice

Catch-up mechanics reminiscent of Quacks of Quedlinburg

Action card chaos similar to Exploding Kittens

Do you have what it takes to bloom above the competition and be the last weed standing?

—description from the publisher

In Villainous: Treacherous Tides, each player takes control of one of two Disney characters, each a villain in a different Disney movie, specifically Davy Jones (The Pirates of the Caribbean) and Tamatoa (Moana). Each player has their own villain deck, fate deck, player board, and 3D character.

On a turn, the active player moves their character to a different location on their player board, takes one or more of the actions visible on that space (often by playing cards from their hand), then refills their hand to four cards. Cards are allies, items, effects, and conditions. You need to use your cards to fulfill your unique win condition.

One of the actions allows you to choose another player, draw two cards from that player's fate deck, then play one of them on that player's board, covering two of the four action spaces on one of that player's locations. The fate deck contains heroes, items, and effects from that villain's movie, and these cards allow other players to mess with that particular villain.

Villainous: Treacherous Tides is playable on its own, and its characters can also face off against those in the other Disney Villainous games.

Adds 3 dungeon and 7 encounter cards to the base game.

New mechanics added are Companions and Shops.

This expansion adds a Companion and 7 Shop items.

Adds 1 dungeon and 8 encounter cards to the base game.

Builds on the added concepts of Companions and Shops from Season One.

This expansion adds 2 Companions and 5 Shop items.

The Hope Diamond has just been stolen! But with so many suspects and briefcases being passed around, who is the REAL THIEF and which briefcase holds the diamond? Can the detectives capture the REAL THIEF before he escapes?

Diamond Heist is a cat-and-mouse deduction game for 2 players that plays in 40-60 minutes, with each player playing one game as the thief and one as the detective.

As the Thief, you have just stolen the Hope Diamond and stashed it in one of several briefcases to confuse the detectives. You and your Accomplice are trying to escape with the Diamond, and have hired 5 actors (innocent Suspects) to help with your plan. Each turn, you'll play a card, which will allow you to move suspects and take actions such as toss briefcases, leap over obstacles, drop smoke bombs to slow down the detectives or slip through a hidden vent to appear on the other side of a wall.

The Detective’s goal is to recover the Diamond and catch the Thief and their Accomplice before they can escape with this priceless gem. Each turn, the detective will play a card, which will allow them to move in on the suspects and take actions like topple a display, block a door, and lay traps to slow down the suspects. If you catch a suspect, you'll get to open their briefcase and hopefully recover the diamond. Then, you can question the suspect to get more information to narrow down your search.

The game is played over 2 rounds. Once both the Thief and Accomplice have either escaped or been captured, you'll swap roles and play the 2nd round. The player who has the most points at the end will be the winner.

—description from the designer

Inis is a game deeply rooted in Celtic history and lore in which players win by being elected King of the Island (Inis).

Careful drafting, hand management, bluffing (especially once players understand the importance of passing their turn), good timing, and a precise understanding of the balance of power are the keys to victory. After a discovery game, you'll be ready for a full and epic game, where an undisputed player will be king by the Assembly for his merit and wisdom.

This new 3rd edition of Inis features all-new artwork, along with the Seasons of Inis and Nemed expansions.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Step into the sandals of an ancient Greek god. As Zeus, Athena, Hades, and other deities, raise armies, spark conflicts, erect temples, and unleash unique special powers — all to outmaneuver your divine rivals.

Mortals are your pawns, their devotion to you is a weapon, and betrayal is divine tradition. The throne of Olympus is yours... if you can seize it.

—description from the publisher

"Königarde" is a German word meaning 'king's guard'. During the medieval period, the king's guard was an elite and highly trained group of men which composed a kingdom's first line of defense. Today, many countries still have such special troops as part of their armed forces -- though perhaps under a different name. Königsgarde has been written to enable the serious wargamer to recreate as realistically as possible the days of chivalry when knights and peasants alike fought for their king."

Königsgard is a skirmish level miniatures ruleset suitable for the High and Late Middle Ages. Combatants are represented by peasants, men at arms, and knights (foot and mounted) with combat modifiers for training, weapons, and armor. Limited rules for siege weapons, gunpowder, jousting, and naval warfare are included.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: From the Shadows: Hero Booster Pack – Bard
description: An additional hero "The Bard" whose power to exchange opportunities helps her get exactly what she is looking for. With songs in her deck that not only help the other players, but doubly help herself.

boardgame: From the Shadows: Hero Booster Pack – The Chef
description: A new hero to choose from. The Chef is capable of using powerful food combinations with spices as an attack.

boardgame: From the Shadows: Hero Booster Pack – The Illusionist
description: The Illusionist uses his mystical powers to change any monster weakness to their benefit. They also use similar attack cards as the Conjurer with multiple bubbles, the difference being that the Illusionist chooses one of the bubbles as their main attack rather than having to use all three bubbles.

boardgame: From the Shadows: Hero Booster Pack – The Druid
description: Though the Druid takes an additional damage in combat, nothing measures up to his power to generate mana. Each round he gains a mana token that he can choose to use or store for another round. These tokens can stack up to three, and when released may be used as either Health, Defense, or Attack.

boardgame: Danse de la Joie Avec Lou!
description:
In this rapid-fire matching game, match character cards to the scene cards they are featured in to score points. Special cards like Cat allow you to obtain scenes in unique ways. When four scenes with Dance of Joy on them are claimed, the game ends and the player with the most points wins. You must perform a Dance of Joy to claim any scene showing it, so fun is mandatory!

Un jeu de cartes pour s'amuser en famille ou entre amis, et retrouver les personnages de la fameuse BD Lou ! Ce jeu contient 30 cartes représentant chacune un lieu, et 60 cartes personnages (Lou, Mina, Maman, Tristan...). Ce jeu est adapté à la fois aux connaisseurs de Lou, et à ceux pour qui elle est moins familière. Le jeu « niveau 1 » consiste à créer des couples entre les personnages tandis que le « niveau 2 » demande aux joueurs de réunir les lieux aux personnages de la BD !

In the party game Casque Chèque Chat, players work in teams to give clues that sound like nonsense to their teammates, but ideally when these teammates repeat the clues aloud multiple times, the phrase will converge in meaningful words or expressions.

An expansion map for Trains depicting Touhoku region of Japan.
There are 2 maps in the game, one map featuring Aomori-prefecture, Akita-prefecture, and Iwate-prefecture, and another map featuring Miyagi-prefecture, Yamagata-prefecture, and Fukushima-prefecture.
And it is possible to combine both maps to play on a larger map.

Monsters take a form as dice in this game, and your goal is to keep your monster alive with higher number and prevent them from falling into pitfalls.
Player rolls their die in turn order into the 3D-Hexagon shaped colosseum, and may hit other monster to change their number!
Dice with more faces tends to get higher strength, but their are easier to fall into pitfalls since they are heavier.
It's a fun party game with a twist of luck and most importantly, aiming.
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
Players are dealt with cards showing buses and trains running throughout Kansai (West Japan).
During their turn, players must play a card from their hand, and that card must be a transportation which could be transited from the current transportation.
The player who managed to played all of their cards wins the game.
Since this is an old game, quite a number of changes had been introduced into the transportation service in Kansai, and some of the transportation shown differs from the current transportation service.
publishers: スルッとKANSAI協議会 (Surutto Kansai Kyougikai)

boardgame: Bring Bone-Bone-Bone（ブリング ボンボンボン）
description:
Arclight Game Award Honorable Mention + Work by an 11-year-old designer who placed 17th in the Japanese National Recommendation Board Game Designer General Election

A set collection in which a boneless man hunts and breaks bones in order to become a complete bone-man!

Players collect bone cards so that they can place all 11 bones on their human body diagram.

The game progresses in the order of [Bone Hunting Time] and [Surgery Time].

In [Bone Hunting Time], players hunt for bones in their respective hunting grounds, and in the following [Surgery Time], players place bone cards on their human body diagram.
Bone cards can only be placed on parts of the body that are already placed.

There are cards with high points for the same part of the body, and cards with the same color that increase the score when connected, so make a strategy and aim for a high score.

However!
If you collect too well, you may be targeted and your important bones may be broken...!

boardgame: World War II Solitaire: Echoes of War – Axis Edition
description:
World War II Solitaire: Echoes of War: Axis Edition is a standalone, solo-only wargame book that puts the player in command of Axis forces across 24 different WWII campaigns. Using just a pencil and a standard six-sided die, players will fight their way through key theaters including Europe, North Africa, and the Pacific.

Each game features a map and action page, custom event table, and battle objectives. Gameplay blends tactical decision-making with dice-driven outcomes, offering fast turns, tough choices, and high replayability. Victory depends on careful use of limited actions, reading enemy momentum, and pushing your campaign track forward before time runs out.

There are no components to cut or assemble — everything you need is printed directly in the book. Scenarios are designed to play in under 30 minutes and can be enjoyed in any order.

This edition is part of the Echoes of War series, which includes a separate Allied Edition. Both books can be played independently or together for a full dual-perspective experience of the global conflict.

—description from the designer

Pass the Word is a cooperative party game. Everyone plays together to get the best score!

Each player takes a card from the deck of the chosen category and a card from the numbers deck. Your number card indicates which of the secret mystery words on your category card another player must guess. Players check their cards and then place them face down beside them.

Each player writes their name at the bottom of their clue sheet. Then, simultaneously each player writes on the top line of their clue sheet a word that gives a clue to their mystery word. Then they pass their clue sheet to the player to their left.
Read the word on your new clue sheet, then turn the page to cover the word. Directly below the covered word, write a new word that is a clue to the covered word and belongs to the chosen category. Then pass your clue sheet back to the player on your left.

Repeat this phase three times until each clue sheet contains three words, two of which are covered.
In the next phase, each player takes the category card from the player whose name is on the clue sheet they were just dealt. Using the last revealed word on their current clue sheet, the player attempts to guess the mystery word on the drawn category card.

Players work together to guess as many mystery words as possible!
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces
Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
Calling all elves!! 
It’s that time of the year again! The battle to be crowned Santa’s Head Elf is upon us. In Present Problem! each elf (that’s you) must gift, steal or swap their way to the most prosperous bag of loot they can muster.  Play your cards right to make sure you pocket more presents than coal, to be in with a chance. But be warned, it’s every elf for themselves and nothing counts until it is in your loot bag.

Starting with the Head Elf, and moving clockwise, each elf has two choices to make. 
The first, will they play naughty (take a coal and take two cards) or nice (take a present and discard two cards)? 
The second, what card will they choose to play? Once chosen each elf lays their card face down, cards are then flipped in order and the actions followed. 
Play continues over multiple rounds, with elves being able to draw new cards that allow them to swap piles, exchange items for more favourable ones, downright steal from other elves, treat themselves and/or make elves pocket their current loot. Once the last present is taken, elves have one final chance to alter their fate before loot bags are added up, and the new Head Elf is crowned.

—description from the publisher

If you always anticipate what's going to happen when you watch a crime series. If you binge-watch true crime on Fridays. If you're crazy about board games. If you've already played every escape room in your city.

This is the book for you.

WHO'S LYING?

One ordinary afternoon, in an apartment building, one of the tenants falls from his window, crashing onto the sidewalk. In a penitentiary, an inmate is found murdered with a broken mirror stuck in his stomach. In a residential neighborhood, a neighbor has been run over while crossing the street, and the perpetrator has fled.

This book brings together twelve scenes, twelve unsolved cases, which must be analyzed with meticulous care and a detective spirit until the truth is discovered. Observe the details of the crime scene, use cell phones, sound clues, maps, witness statements, and, above all, use deductive thinking to find the killer. All of these elements can be important.

Illustrated Crimes, a phenomenon that has already captivated hundreds of thousands of readers around the world.

Readers have said:
"It transports the reader to the crime scene, making them the protagonist of the investigation."

"A real joy for any fan of the genre."

"Ideal for playing with friends, family, or a partner. We made many theories and laughed a lot at each other's ideas."

"The plots are very well thought out and it's very dynamic: you have to translate texts, decipher, use Google Maps, etc. 100% recommended."

"I finished it in 3 days and I'm left wanting more! It's fabulous."

"Unexpected, incredible, and impressive."

boardgame: Pequeños Crímenes ilustrados: ¿Quién es el culpable?

Into the Vortex is a co-operative spellcasting card game. You play as witches called Alhamis. The world of Zinan is plagued by Chaos Vortexes that threaten to consume the land. You and your friends are the only people who can avert this catastrophe.

The aim of Into The Vortex is to cast a series of spells before three Blood Moons become active. You do this by drawing from anywhere in a deck of cards called the Vortex. As long as you draw ingredient cards, it's smooth sailing. However, the Vortex will not go down without a fight. It will try to scatter your ingredients and hex you. Remember: If an Alhami suffers 3 Hexes simultaneously, they're dragged into the vortex!

There are a variety of special items and allies that you can rely on, too. Such as Mimic Mercury and the Black Cats. These make it easier for you to cast spells and enable deeper co-operation.

The magic that every Alhami relies on is their intuition. Tap into it. Allow it to guide your hand towards drawing the right ingredients and avoid the hexes. And whatever you do... beware the Blood Moon!

—description from the designer

description:
Welcome to the World of Tomorrow!

State-of-the-art magnetic levitation technology has allowed for newer, faster, and stealthier transportation for both humans and robots. Civilization is expanding to modern cities across the globe, distant planets, and even into the quantum realm. Only one maglev railway company can lead the 22nd Century to a bright new dawn!


    This expansion includes a double-sided triple layered map with Chicago on one side and Barcelona on the other.


BARCELONA EXPANSION
Transform the aging metro system by upgrading rail lines and shutting down unneeded stations in this Gaudi-inspired expansion. For each station you shut down, obtain a Unit Extension which provides additional permanent robots and 
allows each action to be upgraded up to 5 robots!

CHICAGO EXPANSION
Revitalize downtown Chicago by upgrading the L trains to Maglev technology. Obtain unlimited bonus cards by dropping off passengers at the Loop. This Windy City expansion introduces new cards and strategies. Collect action tokens to be used on future turns!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Maglev Metro: Nanobots, Inc. & Nanotech University
description:
Welcome to the World of Tomorrow!

State-of-the-art magnetic levitation technology has allowed for newer, faster, and stealthier transportation for both humans and robots. Civilization is expanding to modern cities across the globe, distant planets, and even into the quantum realm. Only one maglev railway company can lead the 22nd Century to a bright new dawn!


    This expansion includes a double-sided triple layered map with Nanobots, Inc. on one side and Nanotech University on the other. This expansion also includes nanobot tokens and nanobot boards  for each player, granting them gamebreaking abilities!


NANOBOTS, INC. EXPANSION
Enter the Nanobots! Gain and add Nanobots to your Nanobots Player Board to unlock special powers! Use other players’ track, ignore a color when refilling, or even teleport to a different location! Anything is possible with nanotechnology!

NANOTECH UNIVERSITY EXPANSION
Unlike the Nanobots, Inc. Expansion, at Nanotech University, players begin with their special abilities unlocked! Leverage your gamebreaking abilities to your heart’s content, but you’ll eventually have to graduate your nanobots and lose your abilities - all while building Nanotech University!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroes of the Sanctum: The Strategy Card Game – Legends of the Damned
description:
Return of the Remnant!

“The tides of fate have turned against us. A loyal Remnant resists, yet many Warlocks have fallen to the Rift’s corruption. The Temple of the Third Eye is lost, where demons construct vile Riftways to hasten our doom. We must shatter their works and guide the Remnant toward redemption. Should we falter, only desolation awaits us all…” - The Sanctum of the Scribes.

Heroes of the Sanctum: The Strategy Card Game - Legends of the Damned introduces a massive campaign and the all-new Quest Deck system! Also included are Proximity Lines, Sanctum Banner upgrades, Advanced Loot, Trait cards, new threats, and more!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Maglev Metro: Ghost Train & The Outback
description:
Welcome to the World of Tomorrow!

State-of-the-art magnetic levitation technology has allowed for newer, faster, and stealthier transportation for both humans and robots. Civilization is expanding to modern cities across the globe, distant planets, and even into the quantum realm. Only one maglev railway company can lead the 22nd Century to a bright new dawn!


    This expansion includes a double-sided triple layered map with Ghost Train on one side and The Outback on the other. The Outback is a modular map with millions of possible maps; unlike other expansions in the Maglev Metro series, the map is built during the game!


GHOST TRAIN EXPANSION
Ghosts roam around freely on Skull Island. You must Summon them onto your Ghost Train and deliver them into the afterlife. Beware. Skull Island is full of cracks, making it difficult to traverse for both trains and spirits!

THE OUTBACK EXPANSION
The Outback needs to be explored! Build the map as you play! Determine which stations should be placed where and how close (or how far). If you don’t forge your future in The Outback, your competitors will!

—description from the publisher

Descend And Rise Again! “It is beneath the earth, in the grim depths of underground realms, that the sacred seal of the Doomcrawler is earned. Only those who descend into the darkness and rise again are worthy of claiming the mark.” - The Sanctum of the Scribes.

Choose your champion and venture alone into the dark universe of Heroes of the Sanctum: Doomcrawler. Traverse perilous dungeons, claim forbidden loot, and face the relentless horrors that lie in wait. Overcome the darkness to earn the legendary seal of the Doomcrawler! Dare you claim the sacred seal?

Heroes of the Sanctum: Doomcrawler is a standalone solo card-and-dice dungeon crawler. This standalone experience includes 4 secret Reward Packs  that unlock bonus content for both Heroes of the Sanctum: Doomcrawler and Heroes of the Sanctum: The Strategy Card Game

If you can escape from all dungeons in the Dungeon Stack, you are victorious.

—description from the publisher

It’s Halloween night, and a group of friends finds themselves trapped in an old house far from town. But something isn’t right. One of them is no longer themselves.

Puppet Killer is a hidden role horror party game for 5 to 10 players, inspired by cult B-movies and slasher films.

Each player takes on the role of a character trapped inside a cursed house on Halloween night. Most are Survivors, trying to reveal safe rooms and escape. A few, however, are secretly Possessed—controlled by a malevolent Puppet hiding among them. One of them is the Puppet itself, pulling the strings behind the scenes.

Each round, the current Leader chooses an Explorer, and the group votes using secret Courage or Suspicion cards. If approved, the pair draws and selects from hidden Room cards. Some are Safe, others are Perilous or even Lethal.

Perilous and Lethal rooms require overcoming challenges by secretly contributing cards from each player’s Ability hand—cards themed around Wit, Stealth, and Willpower. But beware: if the group fails a challenge, terror increases. If it reaches the breaking point, the Puppet wins.

Lethal rooms can cause the death of Survivors or the exposure of Possessed players. Players must deduce whom to trust, manage their limited resources, and survive the Puppet’s influence before it’s too late.

Puppet Killer offers high replayability, intense deduction, and a terrifyingly fun experience with just a deck of cards—and a sinister grin behind every corner.

— description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Match up the dogs and pretend to wag your tail in this hilarious matching game!

     Pair doggy heads and bodies in this hilarious card game
     2 ways to play! For younger players complete dogs on your kennel board, for older players try to collect as many complete, matching dogs as possible!
     Chunky, wipe-clean cards are ideal for little hands


Doggy Doggy Woof Woof! is a lively doggy-themed matching and memory game with double-sided boards and two fun ways to play.

     In game 1, players try to complete three dogs on their kennel board by matching dog heads to bodies, shouting “Doggy Doggy Woof Woof” after each successful match. Keep your eyes peeled for the waggy tail card - when you find it, it's time to stand up, wag your tail, and shout “Waggy Waggy Woof Woof” and play passes to the next player!
     For game 2, turn over the kennel board and try to collect as many complete, matching dogs as possible by taking it in turns to pick a card from the pile of dog heads and bodies and try to match with any of your face up cards on the table! With 13 different adorable pups to complete, this game will have children barking with excitement!


-description from the publisher

As the wobbly tower grows higher and higher, tension builds as players wait to find out which one will make it fall! Roll the dice to see what symbols come up, then put a beaver on each matching symbol on the Building Tiles. Once a tile is full, it must be placed on a tower. But BE CAREFUL, if the tower falls over that ends the game and you will lose! The player with the steadiest hand and luck WINS!

How to play:

     In this board game for kids and family, adorable beaver characters in hard hats are used to build each level.
     The player who rolls the dice decides where to place the beavers.
     Place beavers on other player’s Building Tiles to fill them up and force them to build the tower.
     If another player causes the Tower to fall on your turn, you WIN!


—description from the publisher

Sort all your recyclables before the truck arrives for pickup in this game of Rush to Recycle from Petit Collage! Metal, glass, paper, plastic, or compost? This eco-minded game makes recycling fun! Play cooperatively to recycle together or competitively and be the first to sort all your recyclables to win! This game will get your little ones excited about recycling!

TWO WAYS TO PLAY! - The object of the game is to sort all the recyclables before the truck arrives for pickup. Kids can either play cooperatively to recycle together or competitively and be the first to sort all your recyclables to win!

THOUGHTFULLY DESIGNED - Not only does it promote eco-friendly practices, but it also was made using over 50% recycled card and printed with vegetable inks, with the health of your child and the planet in mind.

—description from the publisher

Quiltable: OverSewn is a printable quilting game from the designers of Quiltable: Paper Quilts and Quiltable: The Card Game.

Feeling ambitious this spring, you’ve decided to grow a garden! However, your expertise lies in quilting, not gardening, and you must work hard to balance your efforts in both hobbies. Throughout the game, you will make progress on a new spring quilt, while at the same time keeping up with your home garden, which is becoming ever more overgrown.

Throughout the game, assign dice to various actions as you grow your sewing ability and increase your gardening prowess.

—description from the publisher

Quiltable: Garden Patch is a printable quilting game from the designers of Quiltable: Paper Quilts and Quiltable: The Card Game.

Hearing about all your newfound gardening experience, a rich uncle on your father’s mother’s cousin’s sister’s side decided to retire and entrust you with the family farm. You started small with only a few garden patches and crops, but you’ll still need to work hard to keep up with all the tasks that need to be done. As you plant, harvest, and more, make sure to grow your abilities, take time to be inspired with new quilt patch designs, and make progress on your latest spring quilt!

Simultaneously use your dice rolls to take any actions cultivating your new family farm and putting together a fresh quilting masterpiece.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Arkham Asylum – Shadows Expansion
description:
DC Deck-Building Game: Arkham Asylum – Shadows Expansion is optimized for use with DC Deck-Building Game: Arkham Asylum, but can be played with any DC Deck-Building Game base game.

This expansion adds dozens of new cards to the game and lets you play as various members of the Bat-Family or as part of the League of Assassins.

Playing with the Banner of Glory expansion, each player gets two banners, each of which they add to one of their armies. When the player moves at least 1 Unit from a Region that contains a Banner of Glory, it has to be moved with it.

The Banner of Glory helps the defending player during the Battle. When a player attacks a Region with a Banner of Glory, the defender immediately draws 1 Support Card for each Unit that exceeds the number of Units in the defending Army. For example, when an Army of 3 Units attacks an Army of 1 Unit, the defender draws 2 Support Cards. If the attacker manages to win the Battle against the Units with the Banner, he receives 2 additional Victory Points.

Disconnect by guessing words as a team: MIME, DRAWING, SOUND...

The goal is to collect the maximum number of cards with your team in each round. To do this, the team must guess the greatest number of words following the rules indicated in each round.

HOW TO PLAY?

Form two teams.
The teams decide who will be the spokespersons.
THE ROUND STARTS: Each team has 40 seconds, alternating between guessing the greatest number of words described by their spokespersons until the pile of cards is empty.

The round ends when the pile of cards is empty. At the end of each round, the number of cards collected by each team is recorded in the scorebook.

The game consists of 5 or 6 rounds, and the same pile of cards is always used for all rounds.
It's important to shuffle the cards after each round, making sure all the top and bottom sides are together, meaning that some cards aren't left face down and others face up.

Horus, son of Osiris, presented his claim to the throne before a court of three of the most powerful deities in the Egyptian pantheon: Ra,the god of Sun; Thoth, the god of wisdom; and Shu, the god of air.  Thoth and Shu declared Horus the rightful ruler of Egypt, bus Ra argued that Seth was stronger and therefore more deserving of the throne.
"The Throne is mine by virtue of my strengh!" said Seth!
"Challenge me with whatever you want.  I will prove that you are the weaker!" declared Horus.
Horus and Seth assembled a veritable army for the battles and several bloody confrontations lasted for a long time.  There are several versions of their violent and grotesque fights.  In one of the most famous and legendary, both were transformed into hippos and fought in the Nile River, whithout a clear victor, thus establishing the ancient dynamic between good and evil.
A new challenge... a new opportunity... a new battle for Horus and Seth presents itself in this millennium! A new chance to define the great winner.
In the Pyramid of Hawara lies the gigantic LAbyrinth of Meni, entirely unexplored and difficult to reach, where Meni, the legendary guardian of immense power, sleeps dormant and forgotten.  Every 10,000 years, portals open to this labyrinth.  Portals that remain open for only 40 days.  It would be the great opportunity for on of the gods, Seth ou Horus, to enter, defeat the ancient guardian, and claim his powers.  Whoever accomplishes this feat will become the most poweful god in Egypt and earn the right to reign over this dynasty.
Horus and Seth have gathered their armies and are await at opposite entrances of the pyramid for the portals to open on the first day of the eight battles.  And so begins the great race to defeat Meni.  Which god will win this battle?  Horus or Seth?

In Hawara, players will have up to 40 turns to explore the pyramid labyrinth, equivalent to 40 days that the portals remain open. To explore this labyrinth, you will use movement dice. In each corridor opened on your exploratory journey, you will find enemies to fight and clues on how to open a special room.

With each battle won, you will be able to advance specific paths on the board that will help increase your attack power, range, movement and/or strength of the hero character.

In this labyrinth, the player will also find clues on how to open the special room of the ancient pyramid, Meni, by searching the labyrinth for the 5 hidden keys that will give access to a secret room. The first player to find and decipher the sequence of the keys will be able to enter the room and defeat the ancient pyramid in a final battle, thus winning the game.

Let it begin... for the portals have opened... and may the strongest, tith the best strategy, win!

Who didn’t pick out pasta letters from their soup as a child and arrange them into words? :-)

In this fun party word game, players try to come up with original words that fit a given theme. The game lasts 6 rounds. With each new round, more letters are added and new categories are revealed.

Your goal is to write a one-word answer that:


     fits the theme
     is different from what others write
     uses as many of the drawn letters as possible
     uses high-value letters more often


Write your answer on your score sheet, count your points, and see how you did compared to the others!

—description from the publisher

Just when all seems calm in Twilight Lake, the creepiest monsters crawl out of the caverns of the Underworld. Fortunately, there are Keelin, Vlad and Wilko of the Night's Watch. They are always on standby and ready to defeat the monsters. They even make it a competition! Will you help them? Choose a Night Watchman and set off across the cube. Try to get on a monster square to collect the most points. But watch out, before you know it, the cube tilts and the monster has escaped before your eyes. Or maybe your own pawn has ended up in the Underworld! Ad inferos! Let the fighting begin!

—description from the publisher (translated)
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
Campus 12: De mysterieuze kubus ("Campus 12 – The Mysterious Cube") is a competitive sticker-based board game where players take on the role of characters attempting to unlock the secrets of the cube through a series of duels. The game is played in multiple rounds, and the goal is to earn as many points as possible by winning duels and strategically placing stickers on a cube grid.

How to play and win:


     Each round starts with a duel: Two players face off by selecting duel cards with varying strengths (values from 1 to 5, plus a special “secret weapon” card). These cards are played simultaneously and the higher value wins, with a few special rules (e.g., the secret weapon beats everything except a 5).
     The winner chooses a colored sticker and places it on a shared cube grid on the table. Players attempt to form specific cube patterns with their stickers to gain points.
     Sticker placement is strategic: The cube can be built up in 3D and players must respect specific placement rules.
     Bonus rounds are triggered using special sticker cards (like the “extra sticker card”).
     The game ends when all sticker sheets are used or the cube is full. Points are counted based on the completed colored sticker patterns. When 4 stickers are placed on the same image you gain that planet.


Regarding reusability

Although stickers are used in the game, the stickers are reusable. They can be removed and repositioned, allowing players to replay the game multiple times.

This set contains four unique, illustrated games of various levels, for children starting from preschool age. The main goal is to enjoy playing a game while also learning to count and read.

The Secret of Atlantis is an adventurous expedition through the depths of the ocean, where you must solve mathematical puzzles by dueling sea creatures or collecting knowledge crystals. A multiplication stone is hidden in the abyss.

Clever Cats is a game to help young children become familiar with the letters of the alphabet.

Alphabet Pyramid is based on the well-known word association game, where you find the treasure by identifying the correct words.

Journey with the Dinosaurs is a game full of traps and shortcuts to reach your destination faster; also suitable for the very youngest players.

TIP: You can also hide a candy among the knowledge crystals or dinosaur eggs and offer a reward to the winner as an incentive.

Make up your own rules!

—description from the back of the box (translated)

Old friends who haven't seen each other for years go on vacation to a forest house, but they learn that some of their friends are now ghouls. Every night, the ghouls eat one of their friends, while in the morning, the friends lynch someone they believe is a ghoul!

There are two teams in this game: the ghouls and the friends. While the ghouls know each other, the friends have no idea about anyone's identity. If you are a ghoul, act like a friend during the day, and eat another friend with pleasure in the evening. If you are a friend, be suspicious of everyone and convince your friends that you are not a ghoul, otherwise your real friends may lynch you during the day.

boardgame: Unsolved Mysteries: Sekten – Den enda vägen
description:
The pastor of a cult has been murdered, but the police can't solve the case as it's buried in a labyrinth of lies, bitter betrayals and abuse. Everyone has something to hide, there are terrible secrets and it seems like the cult is covering everything up.

You get the opportunity to reopen the case. Find the killer by reviewing old case files, photos, interviews, articles from the paper and more. There are clues that need you to go online to find evidence, and if you can unlock the victims phone you're sure to get some helpful clues.

Works great to play solo or in a party of crime-solving friends. Difficulty 5/5.

Zaczarowana misktura ("Enchanted Potion") is an educational math game designed to help students master the multiplication table. The game introduces children to a world of magical spells, mythical recipes, and extraordinary elixirs, offering algebra practice in the form of great fun. It’s both enjoyable and beneficial. On one hand, it offers exciting playtime with peers; on the other, it teaches quick problem-solving skills.

The gameplay is structured to allow players to practice only selected operations based on their needs and skill levels. Three colors of tiles indicate different levels of difficulty:

Green tiles feature addition and subtraction up to 20.

Orange tiles include addition and subtraction up to 50.

Purple tiles involve more advanced multiplication and division up to 50.

At the start of the game, players lay the tiles face down on the table and place their cauldron-shaped boards in front of them. A bell is placed in the center – pressing it signals a player’s intention to answer. Speed is key in this magical math duel!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: I'm a Genius Talent School: Wielka gra – Polska
description:
Thanks to this game, you'll discover many fascinating facts about Poland. Challenge your friends to a duel and play the “Photo Quiz”!

The heart of the game lies in the unique photo cards, divided into three thematic categories:


     Flora and fauna
     Geography
     History and modern times


Each card features a photo related to Poland, such as landmarks, cities, famous people, plants, animals, traditions, and more. On the back of each card, there are four questions with multiple-choice answers, arranged by difficulty level.

To win, you must answer the questions correctly and collect four puzzle pieces, which are needed to complete the map of Poland.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A wonderful blend of learning and fun – Carotina: 1000 pytań ("Carotin 1000 Questions")

Talented little preschooler, get ready for a delicious dose of fun! The doors to a magical world of letters, numbers, and shapes are opening just for you – a place where new skills are waiting to be discovered. On the 1000 Questions cards, plenty of challenges await. Will you give the right answers? Carotina cheers with joy when you choose correctly and encourages you to keep playing. Before you know it, you’ll be building your first words and be surprised to find out—you can already count!

With Carotina 1000 Questions by Lisciani, children learn to form words and count in a simple, accessible way. During play, the child must choose the correct answer in a quiz format. Thanks to repetition, they can keep trying and learning. This complete progressive learning program, supported by richly illustrated cards, is a unique educational tool. As they play, children develop observation and logical thinking skills.

The set also includes a fun and engaging game designed especially for the youngest players. All components are made from high-quality materials, ensuring safe and long-lasting use.

Get ready for a delightful learning adventure with Carotina: 1000 pytań by Lisciani!

—description from the publisher

It's 1996. Reality is cracking, and the timeline is infected with deadly anomalies. Your mission? Travel back to 1943, hunt down the source of corruption, and restore the timeline before it collapses for good. Fight the undead, fix the timeline, and BLAST your way through history in this, retro, tactical, chibi-style game!

Players join an elite Time Commando unit to destroy a zombie army. Play solo against a smart enemy AI, cooperate to take down the horde together, or jump into asymmetrical mode where one player turns traitor and takes command of the zombie horde.

Each round consists of 4 phases: Spawn, Commando, Enemy, Clean-up.  Players process through a deck of Mission cards. When you reveal the final Mission Card, It's time for the final Boss Battle.  Win - or die!

Malevolent spirits, a furtive burden.
Passing from hand to hand
Offer defeat to the next.

Akuma is a trick-avoidance game in which you try to give the worst tricks to other players. The "lead" suit is actually determined by the last player to play into the trick.
The player with the least amount of points wins.

These games for school-age children offer a fun way to gain geography knowledge. The games teach children what it is like to win and lose and allow for making up their own stories and game rules. The games are played with a die and with coins. The coins allow players to play strategically and make different choices. Journey of discovery - Explore famous places around the world while earning coins, fighting pirates and robbing them of their treasures. Crisscross Europe - Visit a number of capital cities in Europe. When most cities are occupied, things get really exciting! Which city can you visit before it's too late? The Silk Road - Earn money while travelling along the historic trade route through Asia. Buy a boat with your earned money and sail back to your home port. Earn bonuses as you travel and watch out for traps, but also engage in combat, which will sometimes earn you extra coins. The four mountaineers - Four in a row in a different guise. The game can be played in pairs. Choose a strategy and think logically while playing this game.

Les as du casse is a cooperative action game where players work together as spies on a daring mission to steal a valuable blue diamond from a heavily guarded vault. The theme revolves around classic heist movie elements: security cameras, boobytraps, a high-tech vault, and a dramatic escape—all under pressure from an impending police response.

The gameplay is centered around physically maneuvering a magnetic spy figure using strings controlled by each player. Players must use coordination and precision to guide the spy across the board to complete a sequence of tasks: disabling surveillance cameras in a specific order, neutralizing a boobytrap, opening the vault by manipulating a red wire, and retrieving the diamond. Each task requires different interactions using the spy’s hook or body to manipulate objects on the board.

Once the diamond is secured, the team must quickly drop it into the getaway car and release it before the police arrive. The game adds tension by making the vault’s opening the trigger for the police countdown, meaning the final steps must be completed under time pressure.

The goal of the game is to successfully complete all mission steps and escape with the diamond before time runs out. Success relies on teamwork, dexterity, and timing. If the team manages to escape in time, they win the game—otherwise, the police arrive and the mission fails.

Your vision for a megapark promises fun for all ages. Now you’ve got to deliver!

Family Fun 3-card expansion introduces family icons to incorporate into your megapark.

Use a single Family Fun card as the basis for your megapark. At least two of its icons must be visible at the end of play to win.

The amusements of yesteryear can still entertain modern audiences…if you can get them working!

Restorations 5-card expansion introduces rust icons that prevent progress and ruin your grand designs.

Restoration icons prevent you from completing normal parks and lower your score if present in your megapark.

Modernized version of an educative game from 1991 named "Le jeu de l’oie de l’immigration" made by the French NGO "La Cimade".
The game is inspired by the Game of Goose. Here players take on the role of migrants trying to cross borders, get help and proper papers, make difficult choices and deal with luck (by throwing a D6) and their background.

This is a promo card for the deckbuilding game Albedo. It is added to the standard deck of planets. While having fairly low amount of victory points it provides plenty opportunities to improve your deck.
This promo contains two cards for the deckbuilding game Albedo. These represent alternatives for the endgame planet card provided with the game.

In the party game Prinsessia Prinsessendiploma by Studio 100, players take on the role of princesses participating in Prinsessia's Princess School.  The goal is to be the first to collect five diamonds and reach the classroom with a decorated crown, earning the princess diploma.

Game progression and Trials

The game board represents a 3D castle, on which players move by drawing cards.  Each card shows 1, 2 or 3 crowns, which determines the number of steps.  Note that some cards show Prince; if you draw these, your turn ends without progress.

When a player lands on a coloured square, she draws an assignment card.  These cards contain various trials, such as:

Object game: "Challenge someone and be the first to put a green object on the table."

Clothing check: "If you wear something pink, you earn a diamond."

Skill test: "Walk with a book on your head without dropping it."

Craft task: "Decorate your crown with stickers and glitter."

Upon successful completion of a task, the player receives a diamond to place on her crown.  The first player to collect five diamonds and reach the classroom wins the game and receives the princess diploma.

Enter the exciting world of the "Amusement Park" escape room game! Made of high-quality plastic, this colourful and durable multicolour game offers an immersive experience for 2-4 players. With a playing time of 45 minutes and a difficulty level tailored to a broad target group of 8 years and above, this game is ideal for families and groups of friends.

"Amusement Park" promises a simple yet engaging challenge, requiring players to use their logical thinking skills and collaborative talents to solve riddles and puzzles. The game invites hours of fun, while appealing to beginners and experienced escape room enthusiasts alike.

This family-friendly game is not only a great addition to game nights, but also an ideal gift for curious minds. Escape the daily routine and enter the "Amusement Park" for an exciting adventure full of intriguing challenges and joyful entertainment. Experience unforgettable moments together as you make your way through this colourful escape room game that guarantees fun and excitement!

The game refers to how old, medieval tournament were organised. The game is a fast paced, 1v1 card game with Deck Building components.

You will be competing, trying to gather points with help of three characters, Damsel, Herald and Knight. These points are used to purchase better cards - but spend wisely! The same points will determine a winner, and having too many cards will render the deck inconsistent.

The Joust features different card types, like Misfortunes that focus on disrupting the opponent instead of gaining points for yourself.

—description from the publisher

First City is a flip and write card drafting game for two players.

Compete as city planners on behalf of the gods of Ancient Greece - whomever among you earns the most favour will be crowned the ruler of their newest island!

Despite their cooperation in creating the isle, each god is secretly working to sabotage or succeed the others, and they're relying on you to make that happen.

—description from the publisher

Howdy Partner! TNT is an action-packed explosive gold rush game for children aged 6 and up. Blow up the mine with dynamite and be the fastest to get the gold! Watch out gold diggers: there is only one correct card to find each time.

Giddy up! Set up the Dynamite Launcher and place all the Blast Cards on top. Spread them correctly on top of the Launcher, don't stack the cards vertically! - That way your explosions are much better and bigger.
The first player turns the two spinner arrows and communicates the selected character and jewel colour to the other players. Ignite the Dynamite Launcher by pushing the handle of the TNT Igniter down hard! Once all Blast Cards have landed, ALL players search for the Blast Card with the correct jewel colour and character. There is only one card with the correct match!
Whoever finds the correct Blast Card first wins the round and earns a Gold Coin! Then put the Blast Card back on the pile with the remaining cards. The game continues until all seven Gold Coins are earned - whoever has the most wins!

The official game from the Blocks TV show. Answer the questions and put the pieces down on the game board to fill as many rows as possible. Who will be the best? A fun game ideal for all family members with questions for old and young.

Plan de Table is a placement and optimization game in which you take on the role of bridesmaids or groomsmen, tasked with creating the perfect seating plan for a wedding… and keeping every guest happy! Can you juggle the whims of family, your friends’ sometimes quirky requests, dietary restrictions, and unexpected events? Each turn, place your guests, move them around, or swap them to maximize your points: every guest has their own wishes, and each table has its own objectives! But beware—events, reserved seats, or settling old scores can throw everything into chaos… Who will manage to satisfy the most guests and become the ultimate organizer? Excitement and family drama guaranteed… but always in good spirits!

—description from the designer

The first open-ended expandable collectible trading card game bringing geocaching to the tabletop. Cards in the game may be collected, traded, or be used as geocache swag themselves, placed in containers for others to find and collect.

CH has a deep backstory, and is built upon a growing storyworld which is evolving in the form of an 'Alternate Reality Game' - there are places and characters and lore that is unfolding over time as a layer over our real world.  The game is enhanced by the story, giving peaks into the content of the game - portals, parallel (and fictional) dimensions, transportation through the Cache Line Dimension ('the Cloud') where the positive energy that's most often felt from geocaching successes and finds is tangible – as well that of failure, negative in nature, an antagonist.

A company “Cache Line HQ” is exploring and analyzing this dimension. They have teams of agents tasked with various missions: Firstly, the Treasure Troops for whom a short audio drama from December 2022 expands on the backstory of a few characters, including the likes of (Sir) Tiny Tim, learning about the CLD, himself, and leading the troops in their mission to keep geocaches well stocked with treasures and swag in an effort to keep the positive energy abundant when the caches are next found.
