Secondly, Arch Team, a couple of hired toy figurines who are archaeologist in profession and exploring the 'frontier' of the cloud (a primary channel for releasing news and discoveries).  Line Guardians are people/geocachers who possess Artifacts - cipher coins that were launched with a successful Indiegogo campaign in 2023.  Through their website, CLHQ will be reporting encrypted data received from their analysis of the cloud, which may take the form of messages and puzzles that can be decoded for digital and physical rewards and prizes (including expansion cards), once that module of the website is completed.

This upcoming geocaching trading card game has a rich backstory, an ever-expanding collection of cards to collect, and numerous rabbit holes to dive in for the deeper, fuller experience.

FROM CREATOR’S WEBSITE:
Humanity has discovered a new frontier - a parallel dimension, the "Cache Line Dimension", where a unique form of energy is bolstered by success and drained by failure. Where the modern activity of geocaching has a unique and significant influence from its millions of players worldwide, their moments of excitement from adventures hunting geocaches and hidden treasures pulsating through this frontier - it’s the very heartbeat of geocaching!

But, a threat is growing.... In the Cache Line Dimension (the Cloud), failure, like success, is tangible - and here it is hungry for power. It is drawn to the energy, but it cannot be allowed to grow and find a way to cross over to our Prime dimension!

Your mission:
You've been recruited by Cache Line HQ, an ageless covert organization that guards the threshold to a new geocaching frontier... Recruit your team of Cache Hunters, and construct epic adventures which explore the world, visiting countries and locations you may only dream of - even parallel dimensions! Hunt down and find geocaches and collect treasures, targeting energy believed to be sought also by the mysterious threat in the Cloud. Find them first, successfully, and seal off any way for the threat to cross over!

The "Cloud" frontier:
The Cache Line Dimension is not yet fully understood. It holds many mysteries, and its ability to allow transit through space and time are still being investigated. Your team may not only travel this world - but others also. You may visit realms and meet - even recruit - characters only concocted in the minds of eons of humanity - where success and triumph have also shown a powerful influence.

Transdimensional travel is at your fingertips, and it may have strange effects on what you find, who joins you, and where you go. So build your team, construct your adventure, find treasures, and protect our world - triumph over the Blue!

The TCG:
"Cache Hunters" is a collectible Trading Card Game, where you construct your adventures and set out to find success.  It includes many real world locations and elements of geocaching translated to this ever-expanding collectible series of cards:

     Collect signature cards, personalized by real life Cache Hunters (geocachers), with special abilities and attributes that may help your team on its adventure.
     Collect fictional characters for your team to hunt geocaches with, and some of their iconic possessions.
     Dodge and resolve hazards that may reduce your chances for success.
     Fulfill challenges to boost your successes - and distract the Blue.
     Locate memorable and highly sought geocaches.
     Help along trackable items in their travels.
     Find and collect swag and treasures along the way - and they may help you too!


The bigger and better your adventure, the more successful your mission to thwart the Blue will be! AND, while you yourself are out geocaching in this the Prime dimension, you improve your odds for playing Cache Hunters:

     Trade your CH cards with friends, especially your Swag treasures
     Find and collect cards that may have been placed in geocaches, to increase your collection
     Place cards in geocaches as treasures for others to find
     Purchase the Cache Line Artifacts (geocoins) to solve puzzles and decode messages from the Cloud as they're discovered on CLHQ.net.
     Earn and collect exclusive expansion cards attained through real-world challenges and puzzle-solving


Collect every card in the Cache Hunters expanding card series for the most variety of fun in crafting your future adventures!

Remember, you are helping to protect the world when you hunt caches!

What will your next adventure look like?
A quiz game about the Swabian dialect.

Fast. Ruthless. Replayable.

Welcome to Neoblazer: Card Wars — a pocket-sized, high-stakes Print & Play game set in a brutal cyberpunk galaxy where every draw could be your rise to power… or your annihilation.

In this ultra-portable card game, each player assumes the role of a unique operative tangled in a web of sabotage, subterfuge, and secret missions. With only 8 cards and a minute to set up, every round is a deadly race to complete your mission or eliminate your rivals.

—description from the designer

Aspirations 5-card solo expansion allows you to compete against an automated rival troupe. Push your performance to the utmost with advanced and expert options to become a master of the lion dance!

Each of the 5 included rival cards creates a different experience, as the rival always builds a platform and then uses their listed improvement effect. They may also have a powerful endgame effect to up their score. Beat their total to win! Up the difficulty to advanced or expert by simply using the appropriate information on the chosen rival card.

Many lion dances feature large scrolls conveying wishes for good fortune to the audience.

Prosperity 6-card expansion introduces scrolls. Work these into your dance to earn immediate upgrades and score more points!

New scroll cards have specific conditions to be met in order to upgrade their platform. Score extra points at the end of the game for adjacent platforms of increasing value and for unfurled scrolls.

Turn the page to the complex world of Modern Diplomacy, the ultimate solitaire bookgame that challenges you while navigating the complex landscape of global pressure and flash points. As the leader of the United States, you'll engage in strategic power plays using Economic, Diplomatic, and Military abilities on the world stage. Competing against a formidable AI opponent representing the combined might of Russia and China, you'll need to outsmart, outplan, and ultimately outmaneuver them to achieve world domination.

This game offers a unique perspective into modern political struggles, where every move counts and alliances can shift in the roll of a die. With immersive gameplay that blends strategy and diplomacy, Modern Diplomacy is perfect for those who appreciate a challenge and want to explore the intricacies of international relations in under 30 minutes. Each book provides 20 game plays.

Will you rise as the global superpower, or will you fall to the cunning strategies of your rivals? The world's fate rests in your hands; all you need is a die, a pen, and a sense of adventure.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: The Balkan League: A Matrix Game campaign including the Portable Balkan Wars Wargame rules
description:
This book contains a Matrix Game about the Balkan Wars of 1912 and 1913 and two sets of tabletop rules (one for land warfare and one for naval warfare) based on the popular Portable Wargame system.

This book is divided into three parts and a set of Appendices.
Introduction

     Part One: The Matrix Game Concept (including a short history of the Matrix Game concept and an explanation about to play one)
     Part Two: The Balkan League Matrix Game (including an historical introduction, maps, player briefings, rules, and how to resolve battles that occur)
     Part Three: The Portable Balkan League Wargame Rules (including land and sea warfare rules)
     Appendices (including map sections, a copy of the Matrix Game Playsheet/Prompt Board, order counters, and Orders of Battle and notes about the armies and navies that took part) 
     Bibliography

This is a Black and White version of the Original Bookgame Coral Sea Solitaire with additional game plays of each mission, and:


     Color throughout the bookgame has been optimized for black and white reproduction.
     The original 4 game sets had 5 different missions in the color version. Each game set now has 2-3 times as many plays.
     The color version is 48 pages, this version is 108 pages.


The naval battle of Coral Sea in May of 1942 was the first carrier battle in naval history. Combat occurred with planes from aircraft carriers for the opposing sides. The involved ships never sighted each other.

At the time of the battle, the Americans were still reeling from Pearl Harbor and Japanese naval control of the Pacific. A Japanese invasion convoy steamed to Port Moresby to expand its control closer to Australia. The Japanese had 3 aircraft carriers against 2 for the Americans. The Japanese would sink the Lexington and damage the Yorktown, while the Americans would sink the Shoho and damage the Shokaku.

The battle was considered a tactical draw, but the Japanese were surprised by their losses and the American tenacity. As a result, the Japanese invasion convoy to Port Moresby was recalled, the first of the war to be stopped and a huge strategic victory for the Americans.

Now you are in command, and the decisions are yours…

Game Summary:

Coral Sea Solitaire places you in command of American carriers in the Pacific in May 1942 against an opposing Japanese autonomous player side, referred to as the BOT side. The object of the game for the American player is to earn the victory points of the mission goal BEFORE the Japanese invasion convoy reaches Port Moresby.

You lead the aircraft carriers Lexington and Yorktown as they sortie into the Coral Sea to stop the Japanese invasion that threatens Australia. You must contend with 3 Japanese carriers roaming the Coral Sea determined to finish off the US Navy. The Bookgame contains 4 game sets, and each game set is against a different Japanese carrier strategy. There are 5 distinct missions within each game set for you to win and defeat the Japanese.

This Bookgame contains 4 game sets, and each game set is against a different Japanese carrier strategy. There are 5 distinct missions within each game set for you to win and defeat the Japanese. Making a total of 20 distinct game plays.

You will need to provide two 6-sided dice to play the game.

Coral Sea Solitaire can also be played cooperatively. For example, one player commands the Lexington, and the other commands the Yorktown.

—description from the publisher

A quick flip-and-write game in which we build a medieval city.

In the game we have 10 turns, in each turn we draw a new card and choose a side, which determines what we will build. It is always any of the 10 buildings, but also elements such as crops, farms, a wall, and in addition we have to draw a threat on the map: wolves, bandits, insects, swamps. Each element of the set has certain rules, according to which we must draw it on the map and are dangerous to our different town elements.

Before the game, we draw 2 objectives that we must complete. To win the game, we have to fulfill them and get a certain number of points. The game is designed for 1 player, with no upper limit.

You can choose whether to print the action cards (16x) or use the app (no installation, works immediately after scanning the QR code). If you choose the second option, all the printing needed for the game is only 1 sheet of paper!

Vanaheim: Level 1 Adventures is a new route to experiencing Vanaheim adventures! This box contains everything you need to make and explore your first dungeons!

"Vanaheim Level 1 Adventures" is entirely stand-alone, but can be extended to match the wealth of content in the original box by purchasing the later level packs.

A stream-lined version of the full v1.1 box, this edition focuses on level 1 adventures in order to give people a more affordable entrance into the World of Vanaheim. You can 'level up' this game with the next set "Vanaheim level 2-3 Fame and Fortune" which continues this set into the midgame.

Vanaheim: Level 2&3 Fame and Fortune is the second in the series of the introductory Vanaheim level packs, requiring the initial pack "Level 1 Adventuring".

This pack is focused on developing your character into level 2 and 3, as well as expanding your town further. Use skills and spells along with new items to explore more dynamic adventures using decision cards that give difficult choices during your adventure.

Vanaheim: Level 4&5 Legendary Heroesis the final set in the series of the Vanaheim level packs and requires "Level 2-3 Fame and Fortune".

This pack is focused on advancing your character into level 4 and 5 and completes your set to the same as both the Vanaheim boxed set and the "Decisions and Domains" expansion.

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Das ferne Land – Der Mhourl
description:
Official rules of the mini expansion:
The Mhourl is considered a "normal" creature. If it moves onto a space occupied by another creature, it is immediately moved along the arrow (or along the path toward the Steppe People) to the next free space.
The Mhourl has the same Willpower and Strength points as the currently strongest (normal) creature on the game board. It always rolls red dice (a number equal to its Willpower points).
If it is defeated, you receive a reward equal to its Strength point value.
The Mhourl always moves when the strongest creature type would move.

An easy-to-learn, fast-paced, 1v1 card game where you get to see your stickmen duke it out.

Use charges to power up your characters and strike blows against your enemy.

Use items to help your characters get more charges, strengthen your defense, or boost your attack.

Three stickmen per team... last stickman standing wins!

boardgame: Saint-Lô: The Capital of the Ruins
description:
Saint-Lô: The Capital of the Ruins is a 2 player World War II Tactical level board-wargame. 
Immerse yourself in the world of Battle for Saint-Lô, an immersive game where history meets strategy. Battle for Saint-Lô invites you to relive the intensity of the fighting in the summer of 1944, at the heart of one of the decisive battles of the Normandy campaign.

The Allied forces face the German army in a confrontation where every move, every decision, can change the course of history.
The realism of Battle for Saint-Lô is based on a meticulous reconstruction of the terrain and the orders of battle of the two armies.

Thanks to in-depth documentation, the game boards have been designed based on period photographs, historical maps and topographical surveys, immersing you in a setting that is as authentic as it is detailed.

Each hexagon on the field represents a portion of 80 to 125 meters. Each game round simulates between 10 and 120 minutes of combat. Each scenario offers you a new battlefield and a series of events that can change the course of the battle. It's up to you to anticipate them and, above all, to adapt to them.

You will not be commanding large formations, but small units, at platoon/squad level. The beauty of the game lies in this detailed management of your soldiers: each decision has a weight, each movement has consequences and each fight can be the one that tips the balance in your favor.

The game's dynamic activation system brings a dose of uncertainty, faithful to the reality of a constantly evolving battlefield. The sometimes unpredictable order of actions gives way to tactical choices as complex as the confrontation itself. Anticipation, the ability to adapt to events and exploit the enemy's weaknesses are essential to victory. The terrain, a true player in the confrontation, becomes a major strategic factor. The bocage, orchard, ruined buildings and natural obstacles modify the line of sight, cover and movement,
inviting each unit to play with its direct environment.

Command is an essential component of Battle for Saint-Lô. As the leader of your units, you will have to manage not only the tactical maneuvers, but also the morale of your troops. The stress of war, the chaos of combat, the disorganization and the hazards of war will influence the effectiveness of your soldiers.

You can also count on tactical support, such as machine guns and bazookas, to be deployed with discernment to compensate for the weaknesses of your formations. Each part of Battle for Saint-Lô is a constant challenge, a subtle mix of risk-taking, anticipation and tactical mastery. This game is not just
a simple military confrontation: it is a plunge into history, a journey where each choice, each movement and each shot resonates like an echo of the historical events that marked this crucial moment in the Second World War.

Prepare your strategies, adapt to the unexpected and lead your
troops to victory. May luck be with you, Commander!

—description form the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: The Game of Emotions: From the Movie Disney/Pixar Inside Out 2
description:
Play with your emotions! Inside Out 2 – The Game of Emotions is an educational board game designed for helping children to understand and express their emotions in a creative way. Featuring 9 emotion tokens and an emotion console, the game presents fun challenges through engaging cards and story-based prompts. Children spin the arrow to discover which emotion drives their story, then complete imaginative tasks that build empathy, communication skills, and emotional awareness.

Be the first to complete your console with the 9 different tokens that represent the emotions shown in the film and win!

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

boardgame: Wizarding World of Harry Potter: Escape from Azkaban
description:
In this exciting game, you and your fellow witches and wizards must solve challenging tasks and puzzles to escape from the notorious Azkaban prison!

Twelve years ago, you were sent to the notorious Azkaban prison for a crime you didn't commit. You've been planning your escape ever since and now the moment has come to put your plan into action.

Only by finding the missing puzzle pieces and completing the map of Azkaban can you escape the prison. But to find them you need to solve eight challenging puzzles designed to keep even the cleverest of witches and wizards locked up!

—description from the publisher

In the strategy game Manila, you explore the Philippine archipelago, cultivate fields, establish trading posts and factories, and seek influence in Manila — all while preparing for typhoons.

The game combines popular mechanisms from Amerigo with a novel action selection system and dice tower that creates excitement, strategy, and moments of surprise. The game plays over three rounds, with each round consisting of seven turns per players, then ends with an additional bonus round.

Points are awarded for Island, Manila tracks and tiles, Sets of Plants, Coins. The player with the most points wins.

Beijing is a strategy game in which players aim to maximize the prosperity and prestige of their province. They must call upon diverse courtiers like scholars, monks, warriors, and craftsmen to help them manage their province and withstand various unfortunate events. After twelve rounds, which represent the twelve months of a year, the player who best manages their province and endures these challenging times will earn the most honor and victory points.

The game consists of exactly 12 rounds, symbolizing the 12 months in a year. After the event and any decay, players move their scoring marker forward for each of their streets, each musician, and each spiral on their scrolls. The game ends after the scoring phase of the 12th round.

Additional Victory Points are awarded person tiles, Monks, and money. The player with the most victory points wins.

Beijing revitalizes In the Year of the Dragon, staying true to its core while introducing fresh improvements and increased variability. With refined mechanisms and new strategic options, Beijing enhances the tension and depth of the original.

Dragon? Dragon! is a cooperative adventure game for ages 8+ that’s quick to learn, easy to play, and simple for parents to guide. With streamlined rules and a kid-friendly design, it’s the perfect gateway to tabletop gaming for young players and families alike.

Goal:

Work together as a family to explore, fight enemies, collect loot, and defeat two bosses (a bear and a dragon) across two levels.

Gameplay:

     Roll Dice: Determine movement, encounters, surprises, and time of day.
     Take Actions:
         Move heroes on the board.
         Encounter monsters and fight using D20 rolls and deal damage by rolling D4.
         Draw Surprise Cards for random effects such as one time use weapons, advantages and disadvantages and a visit to the merchant to buy food and potions. 
    
     Share Loot and help each other: During daytime only.
     Eat at Dawn: Or lose health.


Boss Battles:

When all heroes reach the boss tile, fight begins. Defeat the bear (Level 1) and the dragon (Level 2) to win.

—description from the publisher

Gusher is a press your luck area control game that drills deep into the theme of the oil business. The game is in years broken out by Four seasons with unique modified requirements given the time of the year. You battle the harsh terrain and weather with weather event cards and deal with Incident Reports on the job through Incident cards that affect your production of your drilled towers.  Drilling consists of choosing how deep you wish to drill and pull for the chance to hit a GUSHER, Full flow, or light flow production.  There is a auction phase where Barons & Baroness bid on prime locations on the map and even manipulate the price of oil through a market phase.   A Baron & Baroness can strategically use those parcels of land they own to either build a tower to drill or build a refinery that will DOUBLE the value of your production when the INCOME Phase of the Season occurs.   The Baron or Baroness with the most money at the end of a set number of years. (1, 3, or legacy up to 10 years) WINS THE GAME.

—description from the designer

SAMÉ: A fast-paced card game of shark attacks, sudden combos, and underwater strategy.

SAMÉ is a compact card game using a three-suited deck. Players race to build powerful combos on the table while watching for chances to strike with a shark attack — a surprise move that lets you steal already played cards. Each turn you may also gain a card from a neighbor - but ask carefully or you may be stopped by their secret blocker card.

Each turn has 3 phases:

     Draw – Pull a card and retrieve your blocker.
     Ask – Request any card from the player to your left.
     Play – Lay down combos or launch a shark attack if you have an exact match.


-
Score by forming:

     Sets (same number, 3+ cards)
     Runs (same suit, 3+ consecutive numbers)
     Exact pairs (including shark attacks)


Shark attacks let you snatch cards from the table, unless they are protected.  End the round at the right moment by calling “that’s it!” — players get one last chance to respond, and whoever has the highest score after resolving shark attacks wins the round.

—description from the designer

This is a 1-4 player Dungeon Crawl using press your luck, card management and deck building to resolve quests for 4 characters in a solo or Co-op vintage-style card game.
You win the game by completing 6 mini-quests for each character.
You must do this as a team in multiplayer or control all 4 characters in solo mode.
You will draw a dungeon card from a deck of 150 cards and try to resolve it.
The card will have a puzzle or encounter, or, maybe a nice bed to rest in… There are many things to do in the deck.
Movement and combat are controlled using an action deck of 100 cards. You will be building up actions from this deck to use on the main Dungeon Cards.

—description from the designer

In Mindmaze Mysterious, you will discover dark stories that will make your heart beat faster. With only a small introduction, you try to ask the right questions to receive points for naming the key words. Your questions may only be answered with ""Yes"", ""No"" or ""Not important"". Think carefully about your questions as the game leader will receive points if a hint is read out. The player who managed to ask the most crucial questions and thus earned the most points wins the game! In Mindmaze, let your brain work and think outside the box!

Poseidon is sea-themed, with players being pirates trying to collect Poseidon's treasures.

Game mechanics: The game features a unique mechanism with Poseidon's head that shakes the tray, adding a fun twist and strategy.

Aim of the game: The player who is the first to bring his set of 4 unique treasures from Poseidon back to his port wins the game.

Enter the mysterious world of the "Haunted House" escape room game - an exciting adventure for 2-4 players that guarantees excitement and fun! Made of high-quality plastic, this multi-coloured game offers an immersive experience full of challenges and riddles to be solved within a 45-minute time frame.
Whether you are a seasoned escape room enthusiast or just starting to explore this genre, "Haunted House" is suitable for players aged 3 and above. The colourful elements and child-friendly puzzles make for an engaging gaming experience, focusing on teamwork and problem-solving.
The game comes with detailed instructions and an exciting story set in a haunted house. Players are challenged to decipher clues, discover hidden objects and solve clever puzzles to escape from the oppressive situation before the clock strikes zero.

boardgame: Dungeon Crawler: The Black Wall – Shadow Hold
description:
This expansion has been divided in to 8 equal packs called Chapters; Shadow Hold, Gul'Matok, Shadow Spear, Zhira, Shadow Spiral, Noszrath, Shadow Tier and Sorrowheart.  Each pack is 60 cards.

Shadow Hold brings some incredibly dangerous encounters to the game, while providing some new tricks for the adventurers!

Light up your Hanukkah with this game of knowledge, chance, and treats for the whole mishpocha!

Answer holiday trivia and follow festive prompts to collect perks and treats. Race to the shamash to keep your goodies safe. Everyone leaves a winner!

Players take on the role of Octorun, an octopus creature who loves to run, and race to become the fastest Octorunner in the world. By drinking energy drinks from vending machines, the Octans can run while using various abilities. But be careful, if the energy drink you're aiming for overlaps with another player's, you won't be able to drink it.

During the drafting phase, players secretly select a drink from a vending machine.  Starting in reverse order, players reveal their choice one at a time and use the effect for that drink.  If two players pick the same drink, the second player must choose a random drink on his turn. After all players have used their drink, a new round starts.

There are also many other gimmicks to give you an advantage in the race, such as placing gum on the course to hinder the progress of other Octans, or receiving special shoes from sponsors to gain special abilities.

The game ends when a player crosses the finish line, starting off a last minute sprint to the finish.  The player who sprints ahead the most, wins the game.
This promo pack includes 4 powerful special action cards (2 per player) that can be substituted into the game for variety.

boardgame: I'm a Genius Talent School: Quiz ciało człowieka
description:
A quiz-based game developed according to the primary school curriculum, designed to help children learn about the structure of the human body and its organs. The game is divided into three levels of difficulty.

—description from the publisher

Discover the wonderful world of learning and fun with the 1000 Questions Talking Pen from Clementoni!

This educational set includes 32 cards and 64 exercises that help children learn about letters, words, numbers, sizes, shapes, and colors.

With over 1000 quiz questions, kids can test their knowledge, and the board game for 2–4 players offers a chance to play with friends. The talking pen confirms the answers, ensuring an interactive and enjoyable learning experience.

Don't miss the chance to give your child the gift of educational fun!
The set is suitable for school-aged children.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Post-apocalyptic RPG and story generator.

Randomly generated map and goal.

A different story every time.

Lunatic Larry has ate too many mushrooms and riled up the local cannibals and bikers. Stop him from distributing kiloliters of his cult's shroom tea to create an army of hallucinating marauders. One way or another.

Fight your way through strongmen, cannibals, and feral beasts as you survive the remains of a poisoned world as randomly generated maps and goals ensure no two games are the same.

Playtime ranges from 15 mins-2 hours, depending on if you're chased down and killed by the first enemy you encounter or if the task turns into a grueling enterprise. ~30 mins is average.

Requires one ten-sided die, tokens, and pen and paper to play.

Rated 17+ for drug use and adult situations.

—description from the designer

Players take a role of photographers working for the "Landmarks" magazine. They are on a mission to write articles about five Vietnamese cities. But wait, there's a time limit. They have only a month to do it before the new issue of the  magazine is published. There will be only one winner - the best photographer of the month. Will it be you?

boardgame: Corps of Discovery: Sacrifice to the Demon Promo Card
description:
Sacrifice to the Demon is a promo misfortune threat card. When in play, only paths adjacent to the most recently explored path may be explored.

It can be removed by removing one character card from the game. That player is still in the game, but they won't have access to their ability any more.

boardgame: Feet First Into Hell: Orbital Drop Combat In The War Future – Rain Hell
description:
All hell has broken loose. What was supposed to be a stealthed drop to exfil a VIP out of the ruins of Prios City on Illium IX has instead become a waking nightmare. Heavy ground fire destroyed half your descent burn vehicles in-atmo, and left the rest of your forces scattered and confused.

Regroup your squads, locate your lost gear, and fight for your lives across four interlinking co-op missions!

—description from the publisher

The goal of Starwalk is to travel through space, selecting the best routes, harvesting and deploying resources strategically, and deciding when to move your two spaceships. You’ll have three major missions to develop, each representing the core goals of space exploration 1. Become a Multiplanetary Species (Dedicated to humanity’s goal of becoming a multiplanetary species) 2. Search for Life in the Universe(Focused on the search for life beyond Earth) 3. Uncover the Fundamentals of Physics(Exploring the deepest mysteries of the universe and the laws of physics).

—description from the designer

boardgame: Monopoly: Edición Especial Act For Food
description:
A themed monopoly that doesn't come with the properties nor 4 chance/community chest includee, you must buy them separately as trading cards at Spanish Carrefour stores in order to actually play.

Each property is a non existent place named after either a food item, or some other "action to eat better"

The gameplay and goal is exactly the same as regular Monopoly

In the Explorers supplement you will be able to commission an explorer to search the island for hidden locations and treasures.  You can gain supplies, victory points and other advantages with some persistence and a little luck.

—description from the rulebook

Living deep within the scorched wastes of the desert lies the bane of all civilizations: The Dragon of Catan.  The fearsome beast sleeps quietly upon the top of its hoard, sometimes.  But from time to time the Dragon wakes.  Then the monster rampages across the island, laying waste to all in his path.  To combat this monster each of the civilizations has recruited a wizard to protect the realm and hopefully destroy the beast.  The wizards harvest spell components from around the island, preparing to defend their land, kill the dragon, and perhaps hinder a few neighbors in his spare time.  Touring the island is a roving band of gypsies.  Seeking wealth, the are always willing to trade spell components for the right price.

—description from the rulebook

The Random Events and Mercenaries [sic] supplement contains two additions to your Settlers of Catan game.  The Random Events Chart will add periodic events that may help or hurt you or other players during the game.  The Mercenaries addition allows every soldier to have an identifying role in the game.  While one soldier may be a regular soldier, another may be an Infiltrator, Mercenary, Spy, or other options.  These supplements will add variety and excitement to your game without altering the integrity or the flow of the game.

—description from the rulebook

In the Combat/Politics supplement, the game turns are broken up into three segments.

     Opening segment of rolling, moving the robber, harvesting, and building.
     Military segment - deployment, movement, or combat.
     Political segment - City count, campaign, and elections.


—description from the rulebook

In this nine-card game, vegetable knights are duking it out with kitchen tools. Flip and rotate your hero card accordingly; while a proper flip or rotation can remove enemies from the row, a bad decision can get you eliminated. On the chopping board, there can only be one.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Hydra Wrangler: a solitaire grid manipulation game
description:
A Hydra is like a rowdy class, with one untamed head being sufficient to get the others rowdy. In this nine-card game, flip any three cards in a row or column to tame the Hydra. But beware: when a Hydra's head flips, it can move the other heads or even flip them back to their wild side. Tame the Hydra. All its nine heads.

—description from the designer

Summer Season Promo 2025 of FryxGames, which you can buy on its own, or get for free if you order something at their webstore from June 1st to August 31st.

Promo card X75.
Blue card
Microbe and Animal tag
Cost 8 M€
Effect: Whenever a City is placed, add 1 Animal here. 
Action: Add 1 Animal here, or add 1 Microbe to another card.

End of the Game: If there are at least 10 Animals on this card, EACH player loses 1 VP for each of his cities.

LID-VA! is a cooperative game created by the Chair in Leadership and Values at UManresa – UVic-UCC that invites you to become part of the management team of an NGO developing humanitarian projects around the world. Faced with real-life challenges such as emergencies, social campaigns or educational initiatives, you will have to make decisions, select teams and lead with emotional intelligence.


     Game objective: complete missions by collaborating with people of diverse profiles, complementary skills and different perspectives.
     A playful experience with an educational background


LID-VA! is a window into leadership dynamics, decision-making, communication and teamwork. Ideal for educational, training, social and business environments, the game encourages reflection on how we lead, how we cooperate and how we engage in collective projects.

     Versatile and adaptable: Includes suggestions for rule variations to experiment with communication and competitiveness, fostering the analysis of roles, attitudes and values in different game scenarios.


—description from the publisher

LID-VA! és un joc cooperatiu creat per la Càtedra de Lideratge en Valors d’UManresa – UVic-UCC que et convida a formar part de l’equip directiu d’una ONG que desenvolupa projectes humanitaris arreu del món. Davant de reptes reals com emergències, campanyes socials o iniciatives educatives, hauràs de prendre decisions, seleccionar equips i liderar amb intel·ligència emocional.


     Objectiu del joc: completar missions col·laborant amb persones amb perfils diversos, habilitats complementàries i visions diferents.
     Una experiència lúdica amb rerefons pedagògic


Més enllà de la diversió, LID-VA! és una finestra per observar dinàmiques de lideratge, presa de decisions, comunicació i treball en equip. Ideal per a entorns educatius, formatius, socials i empresarials, el joc facilita la reflexió sobre com liderem, com cooperem i com ens impliquem en projectes col·lectius.

     Versàtil i adaptable: Inclou propostes de variació de les normes per experimentar amb la comunicació i la competitivitat, afavorint l’anàlisi de rols, actituds i valors en diferents escenaris de joc.


—description from the publisher (Catalan)

Strategy game. Who will become the master of dragons? Pick the most powerful dragons to win the duels, not forgetting g that fire beats forest, forest beats water and water beats fire!
First to 6 trophy cards wins

Similar mechanisms to rock, paper, scissors but using card that players select with different numbers at the start of the game

A cave delving expansion with a new hero, new loot and powerful artifacts but equally new dangers and encounters.

4 Cave Exploration Tokens
Powerful Artifact Cards
New Encounter Cards
Player Board and 5-player mode
Hero Standee
Hero Mini

boardgame: Ascendia: The Glade of Wrath Mini Expansion
description:
New event cards making every boss more dynamic
A new boss type: The enigmatic guardians of Tel-Ul
Elder Stones

A genie has arrived at the party, bearing treasure and offering each guest three wishes. He’s generous with his riches—but keeps a sharp eye on mortals who get too greedy.

Three Wishes is played over three rounds. In each round, every player makes a wish—anything they can imagine, including the genie’s treasure. But beware: the greediest wish each round earns a greed token. Players may also challenge illegal wishes by convincing the group that a wish breaks the genie’s rules and shouldn’t be granted. After three rounds, the player with the most greed tokens is eliminated, and the remaining player with the most treasure wins.

Antlers is a minimalist solo dice game played using only eight custom dice. As a lone hunter moving through the wilderness, you’ll track deer by rolling tracking dice, mitigate rolls using focus, and take precision shots by rolling a range die and shot die. Bucks are rarer than does and worth more points, while does offer more frequent targets but yield fewer points. With limited resources and dice that dwindle over time, you must balance the hunt carefully. The hunt ends if you miss only one shot. Can you reach the high score of 18 points?

Simple to learn, portable, and immersive, Antlers offers a quick yet meaningful experience of patience, strategy, and instinct.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Cubyria: Architects of a Lost City
description:
Cubyria is a 3D tile-laying game for 2 to 5 players. Players take turns drawing cards which correspond to physical Building Components, and add them to the structure to maximize their point total and stave off the Vizier’s displeasure. The game board is on a rondel/lazy susan so players are able to look at the structure from every angle.

Players are competing architects who are vying for the Vizier's approval by adding rooms, stairs and roofs to a castle structure on the game board. Each Building component card has a number of points to be awarded which aligns with the preference for where the Vizier would like the room to be. After all, who would want the dungeon on the top floor of their castle? Or the grand suite underneath all the other rooms without a view?

The game ends once all cards have been drawn and each player has played all cards they are able to. Whoever contributed the most to the Vizier's tastes wins!

There's only one acorn card in the deck, but who has it? Between stealing cards from others or taking a peek at their hand, you can try to get that acorn and be the last one holding it! Featuring artwork of 10 different squirrel species.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Reign in Iron, the stand-alone sequel to Reign in Hell. 
In this miniatures-agnostic tabletop wargame, you take the role of rampaging demons who have escaped from Hell to conquer Earth, or the artificially intelligent robots trying to defend it.
Designed to play solo, co-op, or as an adversarial skirmish game, Reign in Iron is created by Adam Loper and Vincent Venturella for 1-4 players.

From the book: “Barzobel had dreamed of once again reaping the souls of the foolish mortals that occupied this dimension. Now though, looking over the landscape all that could be seen was endless metal and plastic. Artificial creatures occupying an artificial world.”

Use any fantasy or sci-fi miniatures you like in the war for Earth. Enjoy a deep warband creation system that lets you customize your experience. 
Play through a robust campaign system discovering unique treasures, powerful abilities and gaining control of critical territories that all enhance your group’s capabilities. 
The game is fast-paced and fun, with a relentless focus on making each game dynamic and memorable.

For five centuries, the planes have been locked in an unending struggle. The noble elves and fey of the High Plane wield ancient magic to defend their mystical realms. Men and dwarves of the Lower Plane forge steel and strategy, standing resolute in their mountain strongholds. From the Deep Plane, goblins, orcs, and demons rise in endless hordes, driven by shadow and chaos.

The war is relentless. The alliances are fragile. The battlefield is yours.

From the creator of Battlespace comes War of the Alliances, a fast-paced tabletop skirmish game where strategy, cunning, and raw power collide. 
Build your battle roster using any miniatures in your collection and command your forces in tense, action-packed engagements.
With streamlined rules designed for quick skirmishes, you can wage multiple battles in a single session.

Glory awaits—will your alliance seize victory, or be lost to legend?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Feet First Into Hell: Orbital Drop Combat In The War Future – Operation Phoenix
description:
Cooperative Campaign

Eight missions. Seven hours. Rescue Pyrrhos Station by fighting to secure the Damoklon Reactors, restoring the security forces, denying the hive-mind its endless supply of digital slaves, and more.

With a high degree of flexibility—and four possible endings—each playthrough of Operation Phoenix is sure to play out uniquely.

—description from the publisher
Two Bonus Ally promotional cards for Here Lies: Alan R. Moon and Bobby West.

boardgame: Feet First Into Hell: Orbital Drop Combat In The War Future – Basic Guide
description:
THE ARIES SECTOR is a wartorn archipelago of ruined worlds and collapsing battlefronts. The Dynamic Reconnaissance and Orbital Paratroopers (DROP Troops) are critical in executing surgical strikes to hold back the teeming hordes of xenos horrors and stem the tide of inssurectionists. As bleak as the darkening galaxy seems, hope is not lost!

Seize command of a Drop Team and take your fight to the enemy! Recruit, equip, and drop your team into hellish battlefields to outmaneuver the enemy and engage in hectic, deadly firefights!

New players and veteran wargamers will find everything they need to go head to head or jump into dynamic cooperative missions in this quick to learn, fast to play sci-fi skirmish game!

The new and improved Basic Manual includes:
The complete ruleset, which fits on one sheet of paper, front and back
New to v1.2: the Core Rules, Unit Recruitment, and Equipment pages all occupy their own two-page spreads for improved reading! 
Improved aesthetics throughout the book, including a whole slew of military sci-fi visuals!
Unique rules for dynamic orbital drop-in deployment of units
Highly customizable options for creating your drop teams
Explosive modifiers
Three unique competitive scenarios
One last stand cooperative scenario
Drop team rosters and cards
Full color, printable tokens, rules, and explosion templates
Note: The Basic Guide is a simplified version of the full Field Manual supported by backers on Kickstarter.

To play Feet First Into Hell, you'll need: 
The rules
Some minis to represent player units and/or enemy units
A tape measure or ruler
A flat play ~2x3' playspace and some terrain
A handful of six sided dice

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Feet First Into Hell: Dark Ice
description:
Mission: NavSec has identified a rebel listening outpost on Elox-C hidden under the moon's ice. The Eloxi rebels are utilizing unknown recon technology to operate a campaign of sabotage and bombings against naval traffic with rapidly climbing casualties. You and your DROP team are tasked with descending to the surface and disabling by any means necessary their deep-system recon operation.

A KILLER NEW ADVENTURE
Dark Ice is a three chapter narrative co-op campaign that builds on the existing Feet First Into Hell game system. It is designed for up to four players, and utilizes a narrative-management system called Mission Logs to dole out the story. This frees up a player from having to act as game master. However, the mission will still work if one player decides to act as a Game Master and facilitate the adventure for others!

Each chapter includes maps to illustrate the battlefield, narrative developments, technical challenges, roleplaying options, and more!  Additionally, a bevy of art by LR Knight and Hugh Stockton fill the book to the brim, including all new page elements that make every page pop off your screen!

ORBITAL DROP COMBAT
During the course of the mission, you’ll get the full DROP Corps experience: a dangerous orbital deployment deep into enemy terrain; overwhelming odds; a central, critical mission objective; tough choices; and ultimately, if you survive all that, a risky escape! DROP Corps missions are not for the feint of heart. DARK ICE is designed to challenge players: once you drop onto the surface of Elox-C, you will not be able to reinforce your DROP Team. So choose and equip your men wisely, Drop Sergeant: this one is straight into the fires.

EXPANDED, EXPLOSIVE FORMAT
For new players, Dark Ice adds several pages and illustrations that explain the ruleset in greater detail. These include, for the first time, full-color photography of FFIH battlefields using custom-made models produced by Lionborn Publishing. Enhance your battlefield aptitude by diving deep into the ruleset.

Additionally, on page 46, you’ll find a link to a Google Drive folder containing a number of player pintables and STL objects modeled for FFIH-specific terrain, including drop pods, a DROP Corpsman, game tokens, and more! Learning to model in 3D is one of the avenues opened up by sales of the previous books, and I’m happy to give back with new stuff to bring your games to life!

—description from the publisher

As you carve out colonies in the New World, you’ll face competition from the native inhabitants defending their homes and European powers on the same quest as you. Whether your colony establishes peaceful trade or wages war with these rivals, the choice is yours.

But beware — as your power grows, your king’s goodwill turns to jealousy, and soon your people will demand freedom from his rule. If you’re bold enough to declare independence, prepare for a fierce battle. Only those who can outwit and outfight the king’s formidable army will secure victory, forge a new nation, and win the game!

Each turn, players work to strengthen their foothold by gathering resources, forging alliances (through a robust treaty system), and developing their settlements — all while facing off against native tribes, royal forces, or even rival players. When a player believes their colonies are strong enough, they can risk it all and declare independence. If they manage to defeat the King’s Expeditionary Force, they’ll break free from tyranny and claim victory in the game!

—description from the designer

Use your quick wit and silver tongue to talk, argue, coerce and bluff your way out of a medieval telling-off! Throw your fellow monks under the cart and ensure that they, not you, take the fall for all these very silly margin doodles!

Each player is dealt a Very Silly Guy card featuring a baffling marginalia illustration, and a Very Serious Quote card featuring a genuine bible quote: your silly drawing and the serious quote you drew it next to.

Thus begins a round-table, free-for-all debate as each player desperately tries to justify their pairing of outlandish illustration and serious quote!

Once this debate reaches a natural conclusion, the least justified pairing is chosen by majority vote. Sent off to the Abbot to take the fall for all this rampant doodle, this loser receives a Chastisement (point) and the round begins anew - least points at the end of the game wins!

boardgame: Into The Vortex: The Fate's Favour
description:
This expansion brings the flavour of the ancient Ibanxara, a nomadic tribe who roamed the desert of Zinan to the Base Set of Into The Vortex.
It includes new action cards steeped in the Iban's unshakeable faith in the Fates. It was in this faith, the scholars of Esterfah noted, that we find the oldest accounts of magic we have.
The Expansion also includes additional Mimic Mercuries, more Hexes and unique Spells to add to your Base Set games and take on the Chaos Vortex!

—description from the designer

A board game in which you become a mythical creature of Slavic mythology.
Simple and exciting with fast moves and deep strategy, the Gnostic board game is the perfect pastime for you and your friends!
A dynamic map that changes at the start and during each game, each new game is different from the last. There is always a new experience and challenge waiting for you!
On beginning of your turn roll a dice to apply it's effect on world. Use your actions points to move or activate your creature ability. First team to defeat both mythic creatures of opposite team is a winner. Strategies your team actions, reduced yours opponents hp to 0 and be the best mythic creature team in this fast game.

Build the most beautiful sandcastle by wisely spending your action minutes across 5 locations: construction, protection, water collection, crab fishing, and tantrums.
Anticipate the waves to prevent them from destroying your creations and collect shells, crabs, and equipment to maximize your score.
After 6 rounds, the player with the most points (castle, wall, bonuses, collections) wins the sandcastle contest!

—description from the publisher

Artalis is a competitive arena game for 2–4 players, set in a world where an ancient magical disaster has become a public spectacle. Centuries ago, a power-hungry mage attempted to unify all forms of magic, but the spell tore open a rift from which monsters still pour into the world. Now, each new wave marks the start of the Artalis Festival, where warriors fight for glory, riches, and the crowd's adoration. But the rift corrupts everything it touches—and its influence is growing.

At the heart of the game is a fast-paced dice-bidding system using a full set of polyhedral dice (like those in Dungeons & Dragons). Each round, players use their dice to claim monsters, recruit companions, and acquire equipment. Conflicts over cards are resolved through clever positioning: higher rolls beat lower ones, but in case of a tie, the larger die (d12 beats d8, for example) prevails.

Games last 4 to 7 rounds, with each player having 6 minutes per round to take their actions. Players earn Favor Points by defeating monsters and optimizing their builds. The player with the most Favor at the end of the game becomes the Festival Champion. But beware—nothing in Artalis stays pure for long.

—description from the designer

Dive into the fascinating world of bees!
Imker: Das Spiel ("Imker – The Beekeeping Board Game") is a family-friendly strategy game for 2 to 4 players. Whether you're a beekeeper or just curious about nature, this game offers fun and education in equal measure.

Each player runs their own apiary through a full beekeeping year. You’ll face realistic challenges like selling honey, feeding your colonies, creating new colonies by splitting hives, and fighting the deadly Varroa mite. But beware – changing weather and unexpected events can ruin your plans!

With event cards and seasonal changes, every round is unique. The player who manages their apiary most successfully by the end of the year wins – but greed can lead to collapse!


     Educational and entertaining
     Realistic beekeeping year with strategic decisions
     Designed for the whole family – no prior knowledge required


—description from the designer

Is “Chäs und Brot” really a place in Switzerland? In which year did Simon Ammann fly to Olympic gold in Salt Lake City?

And who is known for shouting “Bü Bü Bü Bündnerfleisch!”?
This edition of the bestselling quiz Smart 10 is all about Switzerland and its quirks.

Score with Helvetic knowledge, go (un)Swiss-style all in, and crown yourself the king of quizzing!

Discover the world of the Rooklings, adorable but determined little heroes of stone and steel! Choose your squad of 3, and face off with their unique special moves that change the battlefield around them - with patches of ice, underground tunnels and more!

Rookling Heroes is a short and snappy battling card game for 2-players. This ‘Training Grounds’ starter set is the start of your Rookling battle training and everything you need to start duelling with your Rookling teams!

Players set-up opposite each other with their 3 Rooklings in a row, facing off against their opponent's row. In between players there is the shared Battlefield Deck, where energy is drawn from by both players. When a Rookling's Valor runs out, their card is turned over.

Once you flip over all your Rooklings, you lose the game.

—description from the designer

Clank!: Catacombs – Underworld, the second expansion to this deck-building, dungeon-crawling game, sees the thieves going deeper than ever before with special new tiles offering entry to a mysterious new realm.

Underworld expands upon the push-your-luck experience of Clank!: Catacombs, with even better treasures awaiting in the deepest areas - if you can escape!

—description from the publisher

In The Nights of Kind Ludd players direct secret cabals of English Luddites at the dawn of the Industrial Revolution. Together, they will build underground trade networks, lead covert raiding parties, conspire, take oaths, and smash the infernal machines. But beware the Crown's reprisals! Government spies, hired militias, and vindictive judges are marshaling their forces against the laboring classes.

The core game plays in 30-45 minutes and revolves around a shared tableau of cards called "the Commons." Each multi-use card in the Commons has a unique and highly impactful effect - allowing players to mobilise workers, infiltrate shops with saboteurs, and run sabotage operations. By carefully coordinating card play, a powerful shared engine takes shape. With barely 3 rounds to go, everyone must pitch in for a robust attack on the Machines to achieve victory.

—description from the designer

Jackpot: Set the Reel puts you in control of your own luck! This 'slotbuilder' challenges you to master the art of hand manipulation as you aim to make lines of three of the same symbol. But the twist is, you never shuffle your cards! Instead, through clever planning, tactical upgrades, and precise card swaps, you’ll manipulate your hand to strike massive payouts.

Gain victory points by matching symbols, with bigger combos scoring bigger payouts. Upgrade cards to unlock rewards, and claim bonus tiles to boost your score or gain special abilities. Every choice matters.

The game is played simultaneously and with each round, you’ll place three adjacent cards from your hand into your slot machine, forming a row. You'll repeat this twice more, building a grid and claiming points for completed matches. Then, pass your last card to the player on your left while receiving a card from the right, and placing it strategically to optimise your next move.

Next, collect coins from one column of your slot machine, then reorder your columns in your hand while keeping each column’s cards in the sequence you just played them in. With your hand reset, you're ready for another round.

Will you manipulate the cards, stack the odds, and hit the jackpot?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Nanga Parbat: Alone in the Wilderness
description:
Alone in the wilderness is a Print-aNd-Play game based on Nanga Parbat from Steve Finn.
You are exploring the mountain to collect animals and score base on your collection over 12 turns. Each turn you will select an animal in the section of the mountain matching the dice you rolled.
Each type of animal is having a different way of scoring points.
You will try to do the best possible score.

First available from the publisher at UK Games Expo 2025.

Scores 2 points, plus an additional 3 points if two Jellyfish are turned face-down and tucked under Flippers.

Flippers is available in three variants with blue, green or red headgear. The designer has confirmed that only one copy of Flippers should be added to the deck as this gives the best balance of card ratios.

Two objective cards included for Kickstarter backers.

Fill your parking lot in the shape of K or S to gain 5 or 3 points and a scooter for completing the objective 1st and 2nd, respectively or only 3 points for completing the objective 3rd+.

boardgame: Merchants of the Dark Road: Bathos the Blowan Promo Card
description: This promo card was included in the Onboarding Packet that came with the fantasy travelog "Smoking Bones Travel Guide: Brandimir" written and illustrated by Andrew and Willard Bosley, who designed the art for Merchants of the Dark Road. This Companion allows a player to reroll any one die, then Bathos must move to the player on the left.

Inspired by the joy of spotting shapes in clouds and the Japanese Wabi Sabi philosophy, Wabi Blobi invites players to draw, swamp, and turn blobs into imaginative doodles and absurd stories. Let go of perfection, embrace the unexpected, and co-create moments that'll have you cry-laughing. Spark joy, connection, and surprise--no art skills needed.

WHY PEOPLE LOVE IT:

     Sparks Creativity & Connection
     Quick, Easy Fun
     Wellness & Self-Expression
     Make postcards & art; yet no art skills needed
     Solo, Pairs, Group Play


BONUS PERKS:

     Travel & Gift Size
     Eco-Friendly
     AAPI Women-Designed
     Made in California
     4 Variations: unique card sets available.


SPECS FOR EACH VARIETY:

     12 Blob postcards w/ unique watercolor designs and inspirational quotes
     6 Prompt cards to spark imagination
     1 Quick-start guide


INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Draw what you see
Pick a Blob Card, grab a pen, and doodle what your imagination sees. Swap and add to each other’s drawings for unexpected twists.

2. Spark a story
Use a Spark Card to inspire a weird, whimsical, or heartfelt tale. Collaborate, riff, and see where the story goes.

3. Invite connections
Pick a Heart Card to share a silly confession or sweet reflection. It's about light-hearted bonding—not being “deep.”

4. Play it your way 
Solo or group. Rules or no rules. 5 minutes or an hour. There’s no wrong way to blob.

What's Inside?
1. Blob Cards
Unique watercolor blob designs that we created by hand, which also double as postcards. One blank card; infinite possibility. Make it. Keep it. Mail it.

2. Spark Cards + Heart Cards
Story prompts that inspire ridiculous, delicious tales.
Playful questions for real connection.

3. Instruction Cards
A quick, friendly guide to get you started

Why Wabi Blobi?

Perfectly Imperfect
Inspired by the Japanese concept of wabi-sabi, we celebrate the beauty of mistakes, surprises, and creative mess. Let go—and let magic happen.

Everyone's Creative
No skills needed. Wabi Blobi sparks a playful back-and-forth of doodles and stories that unlock surprising genius—together.

Purposeful Play
Art boosts mental health—and this game makes it effortless. It taps into pareidolia (that brain quirk where we see faces in clouds or sockets), which researchers say can enhance focus, mood, and creativity. Think of it as laughter therapy, with no copay =)

A cooperative card based table top game. You are survivors after a zombie apocalypse and are out searching for supplies when you are surprised and must Flee the Horde back to your Home.

Players Draw and Play cards to lay out a clear route, but beware: every turn the Horde will advance first and if you're caught you will be turned into a zombie. Each card holds a resource you need to keep so plan your card use appropriately.

As well as beneficial cards there are hazards to navigate, challenges to overcome, and sometimes it will seem the very ground you're walking on is conspiring against you.

A quick to play, and replay, card game for two to four players.

—description from the designer

Whirl-A-Word is a fast-paced word game for two or more players. Compete to make the best word from your set of magnetic letter cubes. The ideal strategy for winning involves quickly forming the highest-valued word from the target group.

Setup: 
Give each player their coloured set of seven letter cubes. Shuffle the word cards and place face down between the players. Grab a pen and paper for scoring.

Game Play: 
A player flips over a word card and rolls the die to select a target word group. Players manipulate their cubes to form any word from that group.

TIP: Attempt the make the highest scoring word. Remembering which cube has which letters is very helpful.

The first person to make their word yells “STOP!” and records their score.

Winning:
Repeat the game play until someone hits 100 points.

—description from the publisher

In this hilarious social game, players compete by putting their random celebrity and historical figure knowledge to the test–while trying to remember what year people died (or if they even have). When did Albert Einstein pass away? 1897? 1986? Don’t worry, we’ll provide a series of clever clues to help inform your guess–and the closest scores points. We’ve included everyone from Genghis Khan to Bob Saget and hundreds more.

Tucked above the hum of the city, where rooftops kiss the sky, hidden gardens are beginning to bloom.

In Sky Bloom, you step into the role of a rooftop gardener, transforming an overlooked corner of the cityscape into a vibrant garden filled with flowers and pollinators.

Sky Bloom is a strategic game of resource management, set collection, and thoughtful planning. Over the course of a summer season, players gather Seeds, fill Planters with blooming flowers, attract Pollinators for valuable end-game points, and draft Cultivator Cards that enhance their growing strategy.

The flowers, pollinators, and seasonal rhythms in Sky Bloom are inspired by real plants and ecosystems, grounding the game’s garden-building in the natural relationships that allow gardens to thrive.

With personal objectives, shifting weather Forecasts, and limited rooftop space, every decision matters as players compete to grow the most flourishing garden before the season slips away and the first frost claims the final bloom.

Designed to be easy to learn yet rewarding to master, Sky Bloom offers multiple paths to victory and simple turns with surprising strategic depth.

— description from the publisher.

This game is the first installment in a trilogy supplement to be played using the counters from La Bataille d’Eggmühl 1809 as published by Marshal Enterprises in 2024.

The package includes

     a new map; supplement detail; 
     28-page special rules; 
     information about formations and special situations. 
     Other information associated with the Rohr 1809 campaign is located on the Marshal Enterprises website.


Per the designers:

The size of the Rohr map is 24 inches high and 36 inches wide...most of the action is on a diagonal line going from just above Bachl near the north edge of the map southeast to Rohr and a Benedictine Abby near the southeast corner of the map... printing tips as part of the purchased downloaded documents are included.
family: Series: La Bataille (Marshal Enterprises / Clash of Arms Games)

description:
Up Down 30 is a climbing / shedding card game where the direction of the "better" hand can change throughout the round.

Cards 1 to 30 are shuffled, and six dealt to each player. The direction card starts as "Up". The lead player plays either a single card or a set of sequential cards, and other players must play a matching but higher set of the same size. Each time a player any number of cards showing "UpDown" are played, the direction card is flipped, and the "better" hand will now be the opposite direction. If a player can't or doesn't want to play, they pass and draw a card from the deck, if there are any.

When all players but one pass, the played cards are flipped over on top of the deck. The last player to play a card leads next, and may play any single card or sequence. Play continues this way until all players but one have emptied their hands, and points are awarded based on the order players went out.

In Après-vous, be courteous and make sure your respectable opponents have the opportunity to play!

At the start of each round, the player holding the Etiquette Token has the honor of opening the round by stating a rule based on a card characteristic. Each player must then play a card from their hand that matches the rule and, if applicable, trigger the associated effects. However, if a player cannot play a matching card, they must politely excuse themselves, reveal their hand, and receive a Courtesy Token.

And remember: always remain polite whenever you make a request—any request—to another player. If someone notices a lack of manners, they may reward you with a card from the draw pile!

—description from the publisher

Dans Après-vous, soyez courtois et faires en sortes que vos respectables adversaires puissent jouer !

À chaque manche, le joueur possédant le jeton de bienséance à l’honneur de bien vouloir ouvrir la manche en énonçant une règle qui porte sur une caractéristique de carte. Chaque joueur a l’obligation de jouer une carte de sa main qui réponde à la règle et éventuellement de déclencher les effets associés, mais s’il ne peut pas, il s’en excuse, révèle sa main et gagne un jeton de courtoisie. Et veillez à rester poli à chaque fois que vous formulez une demande, quelle qu’elle soit, à un autre joueur, car si quelqu’un remarque votre impolitesse, elle peut vous donner une carte de la pioche !

—description from the publisher (French)

A hilarious game! Try to imitate the funny face on the card
and complete a 4 of a kind set without using words!

Game Variants:
Memory Game – Match pairs of funny faces, but keep them only if you can correctly imitate the face.

Broken Telephone – Pass the face around the circle silently by mimicking—see how distorted it gets!

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Trickster Takeover Scenario Pack
description:
This 78-card scenario pack —guest designed by former Marvel Champions designer Michael Boggs— slinks into the scene with two brand-new scenarios featuring the Enchantress and Loki, two conniving villains with a ton of tricks up their sleeves.

In addition to being able to play these scenarios in the traditional standalone way, the Loki, God of Lies scenario can also be played in a brand-new Epic Multiplayer Mode, a Marvel Champions first! Also included is a special modular encounter set featuring enthralled minions you can recruit to your side.

Can you survive this brutal battle of wits?

—description from publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

description:
Plan out the perfect week of cozy activities! In Cozy Days you have an empty week in front of you, ready for lots of cozy hobbies. Each day also represents a room in the house and you must get the right hobbies in the right rooms to fill it up! Work fast because as soon as someone's week is full you will score up and see who is the winner!

Each card has unique rules of play, and can create some super cozy plans or mess with someone else's! There are a lot of cozy hobbies; gaming, knitting, reading, baking and more! But sometimes work will give you overtime or you'll make a mess, and you will need to rearrange your week to get it back in order!

Cozy Days is part of the Days series from Farplace Animal Rescue and in collaboration with Cozy Boardgames. One deck is perfect for two players, but you can add more to go higher!

The Hamsters are back! In this chaotic expansion, your favourite furry pals explore the world in a brand new way. With optional team variants and enough cards to play with up to 8 people, this expansion will ensure no two games are the same!

Even during the 19th century alchemists couldn’t secure their labs from the mischievous pets. Animals require a lot of attention, and it is well-known that when bribed with some shiny objects, they would calm down. However, familiars are not just charming common pets - when tamed, they can aid you in your work, lending their unique abilities!

This expansion introduces to the game Familiars - tiles gained at the end of the first and second round. Familiars will influence the Transmutations in the section they occupy. At first they show their mischievous side bringing a disadvantage for a player. However, if players decide to spend a few resources the Familiars require, they will gain powerful positive effects.

—description from the publisher

My Puzzle Adventure, the puzzle-game that tells stories!

It is a collection of puzzles in which children live thrilling adventures. They look for the pieces to turn over one by one and discover an incredible story by putting the puzzle away. Will you succeed in the adventure?

Ochre Land is the second opus of My Puzzle Adventure Collection.

Welcome to the Ochre Land!
As the sounds of night begin to echo across the savannah, the air seems charged with an unusual energy. Choose between the animal spirit, the ancestral totem, or the plant spirit to embark on your adventure. You sense that a great quest awaits you!

Jeszcze jeden lato 1914-1918 is a cooperative board game that takes players to the realities of World War I. Taking on the role of members of one of the selected families from three different partitions, they must face the hardships of life during the war together. The heroes will face challenges, make difficult choices and collect mementos that will ultimately become their priceless family archive.

The inspiration for the creation of the game was a series of diaries and memoirs from the period of World War I, published in the publishing series "Wielka Wojna – codzienność niedzienności". The experiences and problems of their authors are loosely reflected in the game's plot - in the form of literary fiction (on the "Challenge" cards that players face and in the "Diary" that defines the consequences of their choices). The mementos collected by players in a family album are illustrated with archival materials from the resources of the State Archives.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Jeszcze jedno lato 1914–1918 to kooperacyjna gra planszowa, która przenosi graczy w realia I wojny światowej. Wcielając się w członków jednej z wybranych rodzin z trzech różnych zaborów, muszą wspólnie stawić czoła trudom życia w czasie wojny. Bohaterowie zmierzą się z wyzwaniami, dokonają trudnych wyborów i zgromadzą pamiątki, które ostatecznie staną się ich bezcennym archiwum rodzinnym.

Inspiracją do powstania gry była seria dzienników i pamiętników z okresu I wojny światowej, opublikowanych w serii wydawniczej „Wielka Wojna – codzienność niecodzienności”. Przeżycia, doświadczenia i problemy ich autorów zostały luźno oddane w fabule gry – w formie literackiej fikcji (na kartach „Wyzwań”, z którymi mierzą się gracze i w „Dzienniku”, który określa konsekwencje ich wyborów). Pamiątki gromadzone przez graczy w rodzinnym albumie, ilustrowane są archiwaliami pochodzącymi z zasobu Archiwów Państwowych.

—description from the publisher

Overview:
A newly discovered planet - untouched, uninhabited, and teeming with the rare resource known as Winium - becomes the flashpoint for four rival Empires. Seeking pre-eminence, they each descend upon its surface, deploying their forces to stake their claim.

But their mining rouses a dark foe.

A foe which inspires unmatched terror and wields incomprehensible power.

A foe that can only be described as… a God!

Gameplay:
Hunted by a God is a thematic, competitive, tactical resource collection board game for 1-4 players. Command one of four asymmetric Empires as you vie for galactic dominance by gathering the most Winium from a planet which is home to vengeful gods.

Engage in tense moments of risk and reward as you mine precious resources, deploy your Ranger on covert missions or provide battle support, and carefully manage your multi-use cards for maximum impact. The ever dwindling resources mean the inevitability of intense tactical battles between Empires, but nothing will save you in a confrontation with a God!

What is Night Quest?
Lose yourself in an epic fantasy adventure full of quests and challenges, to keep you laughing all knight long.

Hilarious Challenges
Night Quest is like 50 mini games all packed into one, designed to keep everyone on their toes.

Magnificently Simple
No need to battle your way through a lengthy rule book. Pick up, play and learn on the way.

Ultimate Icebreaker
The perfect tool to bring people together. Jump out of your comfort zone and into an adventure. Night Quest is ideal to play at pre-drinks, or bring out at barbecues.

—description from the designer

Players take turns drawing cards from a deck. When a player draws a foe card, they must complete some challenge or win a mini-game to defeat it.

Other cards are quests, enchantments, or curses, which will make it easier or more difficult to fight.

At the end of the game, the player that defeated the most foes wins the game.

Fast play miniatures wargame rules for the Napoleonic era, with an emphasis on morale: the title references Napoleon's dictum that the moral is to the physical as three to one.

Individual units represent regiments or battalions, each turn corresponds to about 30-60 minutes of real time. Basing is not critical as long as both sides use the same conventions.

The rules are free online.

A dexterity-communication game of herding or stealing sheep.
4 players work in 2 teams to either shephard sheep into pens or steel them away by an eagle.  The shephards have a stick with a hoop on the end, and the Eagles have a stick with a dangling, flying eagle with a small magnet on the bottom.  All 10 sheep have a small magnet on their back.
The twist is that those controlling the props are blindfolded (included) and guided verbally by their team mates
Goal is to have herded or stolen the most sheep after two rounds, swapping the blindfolded teammate for each round.

Make your small business boutique the talk of the town. Buy and sell material, hire experts and create outfits to get your social media trending and gain thousands of followers.

Run a fashion boutique and gain points by making looks, earning money and collecting influencers to increase your end game score.

Each round, all players draft action points to allocate towards their boutique actions. Once all action points have been drafted, players then take turn taking actions in the aim to gain clothing to their boutique in the aim to create fashionable outfits.

Players gain victory points through creating matching style outfits, fulfilling design briefs and progressing outfits to the trending board.

The game ends once all round have been player or when one player fulfils 4 design briefs.

—description from the publisher

国旗王 (こっきんぐ) is a card game from Japan (meaning Flag King), with the ENG title Flagenius.

Flag King is a casual party game that incorporates a trick-taking mechanism into a flag game, in which players are forced to compare data from 197 countries around the world, relying only on information from the flags.

The rules are very simple: each player is dealt eight flag cards from the deck of 197, with only the flag pattern on the front and the country data on the back.

Over seven rounds, the player is presented with questions such as:
Which country has the largest area?
Which country has the smallest population?
Which country has the longest official name?
The winner is the country with the card that most closely matches the question.

国旗王は、国旗の情報だけを頼りに世界197ヶ国のデータを比較させて勝負する、トリックテイキングの仕組みを国旗ゲームに取り入れたカジュアルパーティーゲームです。

ルールは非常にシンプルで、表面に国旗柄のみ、裏面に国のデータが載った全197枚の国旗カードを1人8枚配り、全7ラウンド毎に提示される
「面積の大きい国は？」
「人口の少ない国は？」
「正式名称の長い国は？」
などのお題に該当すると”思う”ものを裏のデータを見ずに出し、それぞれ最もお題に沿ったカードを出せた人が勝者となります。
publisher's summary

Once, happy critters thrived in peace until one day a fire dragon claimed their treasures and homes. Now, hope rises! Assemble your band of fearless critters, sneak into the dragon’s fiery domain, and snatch back the riches of your ancestors!

In Crispy Critter, players send their critters deep into the dragon’s cave, stacking any duplicate cards to grab the most treasure. The more critters in your stack, the bigger the haul. But beware! The longer you stay in the cave the greater the risk of waking a hungry dragon, eager to turn your brave adventurers into a crispy snack!

Play your cards wisely, activate powerful abilities, and balance risk and reward in this high stakes, push your luck adventure. If you escape with the most treasure before the deck runs out, you will be crowned the winner.

Will you outsmart the dragons and claim victory, or will your luck run out in a blaze of fire?

—description from the publisher

The final exams have brought you and your fellow planetary scouting cadets to an uninhabited planet with a toxic atmosphere that you’ve been tasked with analyzing remotely. You’ll unlock new skills throughout the game as you build your proficiency with the available reconnaissance equipment - rovers, drones, hovercraft, and buggies. If you are having issues with the equipment, you can also utilize the state of the art SCOUT canine companion droids in a pinch! Claim the most strategic sections of the map and score points for charting the alien terrain to become top of your class!

Use the unique number generator system to select which spots to claim on the map as you explore Kharsibecc. But watch out for what your fellow students are up to because the planet surface is limited and you're each vying for similar objectives.

オニトコ (Onitoko) is a deduction game from Japan, based upon Hide and Seek. The game has a tableau of cards showing various places in a playground for children to hide (a sand pit, vending machine, slides, and so on).
