As the Oni stalks the playground, they ask questions of the players, to deduce where that child is hiding.

publisher's summary
Oni (ogres) ask questions to the hidden children and try to find them before their hearts and motivation reach zero.

The game ends after a number of rounds, dependant on the number of players, so that each player has experienced being the ogre once. The person with the highest score wins.

このゲームは伝統的な“かくれんぼ”をモチーフにしたゲームです。鬼は隠れているタローたちに質問をし、ハートやモチベーションが「0」になる前に見つけましょう。人数分ラウンドを繰り返し、１人１回ずつ鬼を経験すればゲーム終了です。得点が１番高い人の勝ちとなります。
publishers: Umeda Printing (うめだ印刷株式会社)

description:
Long time coming but it’s finally here; An updated edition with new illustrations, adjustments, and options for Agents as well as Hostile Security. Packed full of information, the Exploit Zero Omnibus is still a set of fast-play rules designed to help players get miniatures on the table and into combat quickly and easily.

Corporate wars, shadow ops, cranial jacks, cyber-augmentations… you’ve been here before. You know how this goes.

But familiarity breeds sloppiness and sloppiness will get you dead with a capital ‘P’ for Permanent. So you’ve got no excuses. You have a team, you have a boss, and that boss just gave you a job.

Take whatever you need: weapons, micro-drones, dark-ware, pharma-derms, stealth gear... Just get it done. Because the particulars may change but the bottom line is the same: bring the package back or don’t come back at all. Welcome to New Kowloon, 2069. Now get to work.

Exploit Zero - Cyberpunk Espionage and Mayhem.

A table top war game set during the Corporate Wars of 2069 in the mega-city of New Kowloon. Miniatures agnostic.

1 - 6 Players, Co-op or Solo Mode. Made for 15mm - 28mm miniatures.

Grab your friends, create a team of elite Agents, and select a mission. It's time to run-n-gun.

Exploit Zero uses four types of polyhedral dice: D4, D6, D8, and D10. The D4 Die is used exclusively for H-SEC spawning at the game’s Arrival Locations. The D6, D8 and D10 are used in game to determine the success of a model’s actions. For Agents, these three dice constitute their Dice Pool. These dice are used over the course of the Agent’s activation to attempt Actions and the Dice Pool is renewed every turn. The die types can be used in any order the player desires, however each type can only be used once.

THE DICE POOL, ACTIONS, AND DIMINISHING POSSIBILITIES
Except for special circumstances, Agents are allowed Three (3) Actions per Phase. The Target Number to perform an Action successfully is a 4+. To act, simply declare the intended Action (Move, Shoot, Activate CAP, etc) select one of the die types in the Dice Pool and try for a 4+, accounting for modifiers and penalties. Every 4+ is a Success. Less is a Failure.

To represent the stress and diminishing possibilities in a tense, frenetic combat situation, each Die Type can only be used once. One chance with a D10, one with a D8, and one with a D6. That’s it. Although Specialization Protocols permit an Agent to roll two of a given die type in certain circumstances, the Die Type can only be used once per AGENT PHASE. In either case, once the Agent’s Dice Pool is depleted, they can no longer act that Game Turn. This means Agents must prioritize their intended actions and allocate whichever Die Type they feel gives them the best chance at success. Die types do not have to be used in any particular order.

TWO DICE BONUS
Most action rolls are attempted with a single die type, however certain Agents stand a better chance at succeeding at certain Actions, ones linked to their Specialization Protocol. Depending on their SP, Agents can roll TWO dice of a particular type and choose the best result when attempting certain actions. This ‘Two Dice’ bonus also applies to activating those specific Combat Augmentation Programs related to their area of expertise.

All this really means is that while all Agents can attempt any Action or try to activate a CAP, a Razor is far more deadly in a Close Combat situation, and when it comes to hijacking the Cerberus security drone that just stomped into the room, you’ll be thanking the Dark Net loa you’ve got a decent Splicer at your side.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: New York Crisis: The Body Cloners
description:
The Smiths felt something was off since they set foot on the city. Everything seemed normal at first sight, but some of the citizens were acting strangely...

This expansion adds; 
Special Character: Informants
Special Enemies: Tattletales
Special Buildings: Press Building

Shooting Stars is a real-time speed game in which you have to find the highest scoring meteors from a complex route of meteors as quickly as possible. If there is a star in the middle of the route, the score will increase. If there is a meteor in the middle of the route, the route is eliminated. If you select the highest scoring meteor, you get 1 point, and if you get 5 points, you win the game. Or, the player who scores the most points when the deck runs out wins.

複雑に絡み合う流星のルートから、最も得点の高い流星をいち早く見つけ出すリアルタイムのスピードゲームです。ルートの途中に星があれば得点は増えます。ルートの途中に隕石があればそのルートは脱落になります。最も得点の高い流星を選択すれば1点を獲得し、５点取れば勝利です。または、山札が無くなった時に最も多くの点数を獲得したプレイヤーの勝利です。
publisher's summary
publishers: 新ボードゲーム党 (New Board Game Party)

description:
Step into the thrilling world of Portamentals, where you take on the role of a daring Portamental guild leader, venturing through mystical elemental portals. Engage in epic battles, recruit awe-inspiring dragon-like creatures known as Portamentals, and harness their powers to dominate the elements. In this dice-battling, engine-building, and worker-placement board game, every move you make is a step closer to earning victory points and achieving the ultimate title: Master of the Elements. Strategize, upgrade your abilities, and command your team of Portamentals to outwit your rivals. Adventure, strategy, and a world of elemental power await—do you have what it takes to rise to the challenge? Let the portal adventures begin!

—description from the publisher

The coolest skaters have confidence and swagger, pushing their skills to the limit to land the newest, raddest trick.

Sick Tricks is a must-follow trick-taking game with four suits (numbered 1-12), with one being the trump suit. Instead of bidding on how many tricks you'll win, you bid on how many different "tricks" you'll land, tricks with conditions like "Win with an even number", "Lose with an 8 or higher", or "Win 4 tricks in a row".

When a round starts, players place cubes to predict the number of trick cards they think they'll complete. Skaters will score big for completing tricks, but lose points for biffing. Confidence is cool only if you can back it up!

—description from the publisher

A memory game for the blind.
Challenge your listening skills

The Crazy Eggs each sing a different song. 
Can you find their match?
This is a simple memory game where players choose 2 eggs from the grid and listen to see if they match.

The carton is filled with a dozen matching eggs, each with a unique 'noise' inside.  Players choose 2 eggs and listen closely, if they match you keep them.  If not, replace them for the next turn.
This game is designed for Blind players, but sighted can enjoy as well.
Sighted players will be asked to wear an eye mask.
Challenge your perception!

—description from the designer

Mirianth Crystal is a bluffing and deduction card game set in the mystical world of Mirianth, where players take on the role of cunning scoundrels trying to amass wealth by trading magical crystals in the notorious tavern Espólio do Goblin. Each round is filled with misdirection, calculated guesses, and risk management, as players attempt to outwit each other while avoiding the cursed Onyx crystals that can ruin their reputation.

Game Overview
The game is played over 5 rounds, each divided into three phases:

Selection Phase
Players secretly choose one crystal card to contribute to the round's “Loot Pile.” Cards range in value from 0 to 5, with lower and higher-value cards being rarer. Players draft these cards through a pass-and-pick system before placing one face down for the round.

Guessing Phase
Each player makes a secret bid, guessing the total value of all hidden crystal cards in the loot pile. Before guessing, players may use a Character card from their hand — either truthfully or as a bluff — to manipulate the game. Other players can challenge this claim by shouting “I doubt it!”, introducing a risk-reward system of deduction and bluffing.

Loot Phase
Once all guesses are made, the total value is revealed. The player whose guess is closest (or exact) is rewarded as the Lucky One, gaining first choice of crystal cards and secretly adding two more to the loot before selecting their own. Other players choose from the remaining loot based on proximity of their guesses.

Throughout the game, players build a personal collection of crystals to score points. Sets of identical crystals earn bonuses, while collecting diverse types adds extra value. But beware: every Onyx token (earned from being caught lying or taking risks) deducts points at the end of the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 20 Questions: The Game of People, Places and Things
description:
Be the first player to guess the mystery person, place or thing topic and move closer to victory!
The 20 Questions Trivia Game is loaded with 300 unique mystery topics and 6,000 clues across sports, history, literature, pop culture and more so you can keep game night going all night long.
All players are engaged on every turn - as more clues are read on every player's turn, all players move closer to identifying the mystery topic.
All mystery topics are familiar and well-known people, places and things, supported by witty and clever clues to help players identify the mystery topic correctly.

Harvest season has begun!
The garden is in chaos and the forest animals have come to help you… although they might just make things worse!

In this fun set collection game, each player tries to collect their favorite vegetable—but must do so in secret. Your goal is to make your favorite vegetable the most common one in the groups being formed… without letting anyone else figure out which one it is!

Can you confuse your rivals, make the best combinations, and win the harvest? Grab your basket, plan your strategy, and get ready to laugh with the garden animals!

Game Objective
The player who earns the most points wins.

In case of a tie, the winner is the player with the most units of their favorite vegetable.

If the tie persists, the winner is the player with the fewest vegetables containing Larvae.
If there's still a tie, both players share the victory.


¡La temporada de cosecha ha comenzado! El huerto está patas arriba y los animales del bosque han venido a ayudarte… ¡aunque quizás solo lo compliquen más!

En este divertido juego de colección de sets, cada jugador intenta recolectar su vegetal favorito, pero debe hacerlo en secreto. Tu objetivo es que tu vegetal preferido sea el más común en los grupos que se formen… ¡sin que los demás descubran cuál es!

¿Podrás confundir a tus rivales, hacer las mejores combinaciones y ganar la cosecha? Toma tu canasta, cuida tu estrategia y ¡prepárate para reír con los animales del huerto!

Objetivo del juego

El jugador que acumule la mayor
cantidad de puntos, gana.

Si hubiera un empate gana el juego él
jugador que tenga más unidades de
su vegetal favorito.

Si persiste el empate, gana quien
tenga menos vegetales con Larvas y si
aún persiste el empate ambos
comparten la victoria.

boardgame: Quest Calendar 2026: The Souls of Saraku
description:
Souls of Saraku is a solo tabletop RPG disguised as a daily calendar, where you take on the role of a villain rising to power through a year-long fantasy adventure.

Heroes have killed the Overlord. Will you rise to claim the throne and seek vengeance on the heroes?

Each day reveals a new page with story-driven encounters, dice-based challenges, moral decisions, and darkly satisfying rewards. You’ll level up, collect loot, recruit minions, and face off against heroes, monsters, and rival overlords—one bite-sized session at a time. With simple mechanics, immersive writing, and epic choices that ripple through the story, it’s a dangerously fun way to conquer your desk and your destiny.

—description from the publisher

The Game of Gods is a strategic card game in which players compete for victory using the power of mythological figures. Each player starts with 100 life points and a hand drawn from the main deck of 120 cards, which everyone shares throughout the game.

Use energy to pay the cost of your attacks and powers. Attacks deal direct damage to your opponents. Powers allow you to heal, defend yourself, draw cards, and much more. Resource management is key. As the game progresses, special benefits are activated that speed up the game and generate intense tactical decisions. You can forge temporary alliances with other players to eliminate a common opponent… but remember: only one will survive. The last player standing wins.

In addition to the main mode, the game includes four alternative modes where you can play as a team, solo, and cooperatively without needing to purchase expansions. The entire experience comes in the base game!

—description from the publisher

Desde tiempos inmemoriales, las mitologías han coexistido en un frágil equilibrio, amenazado por antiguas rivalidades. Para evitar una guerra eterna, las deidades primordiales establecieron una única vía para preservar la paz: El Juego de Dioses. Este desafío reúne a los participantes para canalizar el poder de figuras legendarias y enfrentarse en una arena de estrategia y fuerza.

El Juego de Dioses es un juego de cartas estratégico que combina habilidades y recursos para vencer. Usa ataques, energías y poderes; forma alianzas temporales, pero ten cuidado, la traición puede cambiarlo todo. En el modo principal, Batalla, de 3 a 5 jugadores compiten en una batalla tipo "Battle Royale", donde el último en pie es el vencedor.

La edición griega incluye cinco mazos —héroes y semidioses— cada uno con su propio estilo y habilidades, con opción de juego para hasta 10 jugadores. Además, el juego ofrece modos alternativos, como duelos uno a uno, equipos, alianzas y desafíos cooperativos o solitarios contra "el jefe final". La estrategia y la diversión están garantizadas: defiéndete a la cara o sorprende con un combo devastador. El destino de los dioses está en tus manos.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

A manifold gaming platform designed to host a wealth of games. The Annuli Board comes with Drift, a game of abstract strategy for two to six players. The object is to be the first player to retrieve and secure one of each type Drift coin. In order to accomplish this players will need to seize and transport coins, defend repositories, tactically outmaneuver opponents and navigate the motion of moving rivers.

boardgame: Great War at Sea: Jutland – Baltic Sea
description:
In 1905, the Imperial Russian Navy suffered a decisive defeat at the hands of the Japanese, with the Pacific Fleet (including much of the Baltic Fleet, sent around the world to reinforce the Far East) annihilated. In the years that followed, soaring economic growth allowed the Navy to begin rebuilding, a process that had not borne fruit when the First World War erupted in 1914.

The Russian Baltic Fleet never had the chance to fight the Germans on even terms, as the war came too soon. But what if the guns of August had waited, or the rebuilding program moved slightly faster?

Jutland: Baltic Sea is an expansion book for Great War at Sea: Jutland, looking at the naval building programs of Russia as well as Sweden and Germany. There are 70 new playing pieces: 50 of them double-sized ship pieces, 20 of them standard square pieces, and all of them die-cut and silky-smooth. And there are 36 new scenarios for Jutland so you can play with them (you’ll need Jutland 2e and Risk Fleet).

While the Swedes get their 1916 fleet expansion program (with new cruisers, armored cruisers and coast defense ships) and the Germans get their proposed coast defense ships, it’s the Russians who get most of the attention. Admiral Nikolai von Essen’s 1914 proposal to hasten the process by building additional battleships in British yards and purchasing foreign dreadnoughts gets full attention, but we also look at Italian-designed battleships proposed for the fleet and the original, full-sized designs for powerful fast cruisers.

It’s a fleet capable of standing up to the Germans, and now you can lead it into battle the way Russia’s admirals wished.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
Legendary magician Elvis Boone is back for one last show before retiring. However, during the grand finale, Elvis vanishes but never reappears. His body is found backstage, with a blood covered sword from a previous trick lying next to him. Boone had no shortage of enemies, from rival magicians to disgruntled employees, but with seemingly no way for the killer to escape, it's as if they performed their own vanishing trick.

34 PIECES OF PHYSICAL EVIDENCE - Inside the case file, you'll find newspaper articles, crime scene police reports, photographs, blueprints, witness statements, and more.

SEARCH THROUGH AN ONLINE INBOX - In addition to the physical evidence supplied inside of the case file, you'll need to access an online inbox to help you decide which suspects are innocent and which are guilty - but it's not easy...you'll need to be a smart investigator to gain access!

A GREAT NIGHT IN - With approximately 2.5 hours of game play (depending on your skill level) and a difficulty rating of 3.5/5 this "whodunnit" style thriller will provide a fun night in for all who choose to participate. If you can't crack the case without a secret clue, hints and answers are available online.

—description from the publisher

Chess Joker brings elegant chaos to the classic game of Chess. The rules are quite simple: Play a normal game of chess, but with one extra twist — after each turn, flip the top card of the deck face up next to you. Each card depicts a Joker move, such as "Move your King like a Knight" or "Warp your Queen to deliver check". On your next turn, you can take a normal chess move, or use your card. If you don't use the card, it's gone, and you draw a new one. If you do use it, you score it for points. Accumulate 800 Elo worth of completed cards, and you win immediately — or exploit your cards' powers to deliver checkmate and win traditionally.

Each of the 256 cards in the game is unique, and they are divided into two 128 card sets: basic and advanced. Learning players can start with the basic cards, and introduce the rest when they're ready.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Bring the ice sport of curling to your tabletop, with dice! In the dexterity game Mystic Curling Club, instead of 44 pounds of granite down a 150 foot sheet of ice, you'll be sliding shiny 12-sided dice down a three-foot board trying to reach the center of the rings. As in the sport of curling, you can bump opposing stones, throw guard stones to defend your own, and mix strategy with skill to win.

But Mystic Curling Club isn't just dice curling; it's magical dice curling. Each player chooses a witch and her familiar to lead their curling team. On certain shots, you'll be able to change the playing field: adding, moving, or even removing stones!

—description from the publisher

One morning, a mysterious package breaks the silence. Inside is a diary written in 1925 by a student at Miskatonic University—a school that, to your knowledge, does not exist. Yet as you read deeper, secrets begin to surface. Every decision you make seems to reach back in time, rewriting a destiny from nearly a century ago.

Abyss Echo: The Forbidden Rite is a narrative-driven tabletop game for 1-4 players (fully playable solo). It seamlessly blends puzzle-solving, novel reading, dice combat, and physical artifact investigation to present a dark cosmic horror adventure.

				
				
					Gameplay Mechanics
     The game core revolves around a unique binder system. As the story progresses, you must personally unseal "Sealed Files" and add new pages, allowing the plot to evolve in real-time in your hands.



     Your mental state is tracked via the SANscope. You must carefully manage your sanity when performing checks with the 8-sided Fracture Dice.



     Sift through ultra-realistic narrative artifacts to solve mysteries. Your progress is visually recorded on the "Trophies of Fate" wall using achievement stickers.



     Combat is not just about comparing stats. You must physically drop your attack dice from a designated height into a specific zone. If the dice miss the zone entirely, the consequences will be dire.


				
				
					Legacy Elements & Replayability
     As the story unfolds, scratch off the surface of character cards to reveal hidden traits, secret bonds, and shadows of the past.



     Your choices within the story will lead you to one of 13 unique endings.


Can you save Miskatonic University from destruction, or will you be swallowed by it? Remember, when you gaze into the abyss, the abyss also gazes into you.

—description from the designer

Kata Emak (which means "Mother's Words" in Indonesian) is a fast-paced card game inspired by a classic everyday experience in Indonesian households—being sent by your mom to shop at the local traditional market!

In this game, players become kids tasked by Emak (Mom) to buy specific items from the market: fresh meat, tempeh, eggs, crackers, water spinach (kangkung), and fish. But be careful—buying the wrong item or spoiled groceries will get you scolded by Emak!

Kata Emak is a game of speed, focus, and accuracy. Players must race to gather the correct items as instructed. The first player to complete Emak’s shopping list correctly wins the game.

Can you keep up with Emak’s orders and shop like a pro? Let’s help Emak!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Murder Mystery Party: Cocktail for Murder
description:
It's the roaring '20s: bring on the glitz and glamor! You've gained entry into one of most exciting events of the year: opening night at a hidden, jazzy speakeasy, accessible only if you know the secret password. You're enjoying a cocktail on a raucous Friday evening when some shocking news emerges. Amongst all the excitement and boozy conversation, local politician Alicia Capone has been found dead at the bar, having drank a poisoned martini! Suspicions fall on all of the guests. But who would have wanted to kill this charming champion of the people? No one's leaving until the murder is solved!

Cocktail for Murder is an interactive murder mystery game, where your guests for the evening become characters suspected of the crime in question. The crime must be solved before the evening is out. It is in each guest's best interest to prove their innocence by interrogating their fellow suspects until the murderer is exposed.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: I'm a Genius Talent School: Quiz dinozaury i prehistoria
description:
I'm a Genius Talent School: Quiz dinozaury i prehistoria (Dinosaurs and Prehistory) is a fantastic game that takes us back to prehistoric times.

The quiz has been created based on the primary school curriculum.

It is divided into three levels of difficulty.

The quiz provides knowledge about dinosaurs, marine and flying reptiles, as well as hominids.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: I'm a Genius Talent School: Quiz planety i gwiazdy
description:
I'm a Genius Talent School: Quiz planety i gwiazdy (Planets and Stars) is a scientific board game with an astronomy theme.

It has been developed based on the primary school curriculum.

Players move around the board by answering questions and taking part in fun mini-games related to astronomy.

Two levels of difficulty ensure that even the youngest players can have a great time.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Deception Inc. is a hybrid analog-digital board game designed as part of a Master’s thesis in Interaction Design. It explores how persuasive technologies and deceptive design strategies (known as dark patterns) influence user behavior. In the game, these tactics become mechanics that players must recognize, navigate, and sometimes weaponize.

The game is built around real-world manipulation tactics found in websites and apps, like disguised ads, pre-selected options, and endless scroll. Each round throws players into scenarios inspired by actual UI/UX dark patterns, presented through a mix of physical components and interactive minigames (built in Figma).

Players face both cooperative and competitive challenges. You can “attack” others using specific dark patterns, trigger events, or resist manipulation through strategic choices. The experience is grounded in research on behavioral nudging and data ethics, refined through iterative prototyping and playtesting.

At its core, Deception Inc. is designed to educate players about the subtle, often invisible ways digital interfaces influence behavior. By turning these manipulative tactics into game mechanics, the experience encourages critical reflection and builds awareness, helping players recognize, question, and navigate digital environments more safely and consciously in everyday life.

—description from the designer

boardgame: There was a Scout...: Dual Edition
description:
There was a Scout is a cooperative game mostly focused on storytelling.

Scoutmasters are taking scout kids to camping in the forest.

Every night the scoutmasters have to tell a scary story to their troops. They need to scare them enough to keep them from wondering away from the camp, to the woods during the night. The scouts have different personalities and phobias so the stories affect each scout differently.
If any child goes missing during the night, a search party phase comes up before the next night's story time.

The goal of the game is to have all the scouts back in the camp after the final search party.

The Dual edition includes a family friendly version and an adult, drinking game version.

—description from the publisher

A game about collecting and posting posters relating to various UK-centric things, such as products or locations.

Players compete to post posters by rolling and navigating the board, the player that manages to place all their posters first wins!

boardgame: Miles Shaw and the Temple of Jade
description:
Miles Shaw and the Temple of Jade is an adrenaline-fueled adventure board game based on the captivating character Miles Shaw from Haja Mo's sci-fi techno-thriller novel, Atlantis Protocol. Designed by Haja Mo, the game plunges you directly into a cinematic journey filled with pulse-racing action and thrilling exploration.

As Miles Shaw, a daring and brilliant British archaeologist, you embark on a high-stakes expedition into the dense heart of the Amazon jungle. Your mission: discover and retrieve the legendary Jade Tablet concealed deep within the enigmatic Temple of Jade. But you're not alone—Scarlett Spire and her ruthless band of mercenaries are hot on your trail, determined to seize the artifact for their own sinister purposes.

Navigate perilous terrain tile by tile, uncover hidden ruins, and decipher ancient glyphs. Every step brings new challenges—solve intricate puzzles, avoid deadly traps, and survive encounters with fierce jungle creatures, including venomous snakes and lurking crocodiles. Face dramatic trials such as precarious rope bridges, roaring rivers, and daring parachute jumps.

Engage in intense battles with cunning villains and interact with mysterious tribal people, taking part in mystic Kali sacrifice rituals to gain powerful insights and valuable artifacts. Every card drawn and every dice rolled immerses you deeper into an unforgettable cinematic experience.

The fate of your expedition hangs in the balance with every decision. Will you succeed in claiming the Jade Tablet and return as a celebrated hero, or will the jungle’s hidden dangers and treacherous villains spell your doom?

Miles Shaw and the Temple of Jade—it's not just a board game; it's your chance to star in an epic adventure.

Are you Clued Up or Clueless? ! The fast-paced race to place your four cups in a stack. Get rid of one cup every time you show you know an answer. But be careful You can't use the same cup twice! Hear the clues then stack the Cups! Work out answers from just four clues – then slam down a cup to win a point.

Are you Clued Up or Clueless?

Hear the Clues, stack the cups!

Clued Up is a fast-paced race to place your four cups in a stack.

Get rid of one cup every time you show you know an answer.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Finders Seekers: Florence – The Art of Escape
description:
Solve puzzles that have trapped students inside an ancient crypt before it's too late. Crack codes and ciphers, figure out escape room elements, and solve this mystery game that's perfect for game nights and date nights.

How To Play
1. Read the introduction letter (it says 'START HERE'). It'll explain the situation you find yourself in and the stakes of the case.
2. Work through the evidence in any order that makes sense to you. Read everything, take notes, and flag anything that seems suspicious.
3. Beat the ciphers and puzzles scattered throughout the game. Each answer leads you to solving the overall mystery.
4. Consult the hint sheet if you need a push in the right direction.
5. Submit the answer in the website given in the introduction letter. And reveal the truth!

PERFECT FOR FAMILY GATHERINGS. The #1 rated mystery game with over 700,000 games sold! Finders Seekers “transports” you around the world, exploring new destinations while solving a mystery! It’s like an Escape Room but can be played anywhere!

FAMILY FRIENDLY CONTENT but challenging for adults! (No violence or murders.)

BEAUTIFUL ARTWORK. Our immersive game comes with beautifully designed case documents, travel posters, souvenirs and puzzles.

LIKE AN ESCAPE ROOM. Solve puzzles, decode ciphers and follow the trail of clues to solve the mystery.

EXTRA IMMERSION. Includes authentic food/drink recipes and a music playlist that go with the game theme. Take your game night to the next level!

HELP WHEN YOU NEED IT. Hints and answers provided to keep things moving if you get stuck.

MANY WAYS TO PLAY. A thrilling adventure for date night, game night, solo play or team building exercises!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Banzai!: Japan Beyond the Indian Ocean, April 1942
description:
This covers the Japanese naval foray into the Indian Ocean in April 1942 with five of their carriers. The Japanese were on a roll, seemingly invincible after a string of victories since Dec. 1941, including the taking of Singapore and the Dutch East Indies. Ceylon was next objective due to its strategic location astride the shipping lanes.

The Japanese hoped that by menacing Ceylon, they could force the smaller British fleet (with three carriers) defending it to fight and cripple it, then go on to demolish Britain’s vital shipping ports located in India. The map scale 50 miles to a hex, and the game uses the land-air-sea system first showcased in Perry Moore’s War in the Aegean game.

boardgame: Finders Seekers: National Parks – Trail of Secrets
description:
Investigate the disappearance of 9 park rangers from around the country, who all left behind clues. Crack codes and ciphers, figure out escape room elements, and solve this mystery game that's perfect for game nights and date nights.

How To Play
1. Read the introduction letter (it says 'START HERE'). It'll explain the situation you find yourself in and the stakes of the case.
2. Work through the evidence in any order that makes sense to you. Read everything, take notes, and flag anything that seems suspicious.
3. Beat the ciphers and puzzles scattered throughout the game. Each answer leads you to solving the overall mystery.

boardgame: Finders Seekers: The Outback – Legends of Gold
description:
While investigating a missing persons case in the Outback, clues of a legendary treasure emerge. Crack codes and ciphers, figure out escape room elements, and solve this mystery game that's perfect for game nights and date nights.

How To Play
1. Read the introduction letter (it says 'START HERE'). It'll explain the situation you find yourself in and the stakes of the case.
2. Work through the evidence in any order that makes sense to you. Read everything, take notes, and flag anything that seems suspicious.
3. Beat the ciphers and puzzles scattered throughout the game. Each answer leads you to solving the overall mystery.
4. Consult the hint sheet if you need a push in the right direction.
5. Submit the answer in the website given in the introduction letter. And reveal the truth!

HELP WHEN YOU NEED IT. Hints and answers provided to keep things moving if you get stuck.

MANY WAYS TO PLAY. A thrilling adventure for date night, game night, solo play or team building exercises!

I’m lost here!

Test your wit by adding impossible phrases without losing the thread. Pass the problem to someone else with a RT, use FAVS to get out of trouble, or break the chain with UNFOLLOW. A fast, fun game that’s perfect for challenging your friends and family. Can you keep up with the thread?

Each turn, you play a card to add to a common sentence. Each player should repeat, without errors, the previous players additions. Each turn will be more difficult! In addition, you have special cards that helps you.

—description from the publisher (translated)

¡No te sigo!
Pon a prueba tu ingenio añadiendo frases imposibles sin perder el hilo. Pásale el marrón a otro con RT, usa FAVS para salir del embrollo o rompe la cadena con UNFOLLOW. Pero cuidado: si fallas, te quedas con todas las cartas. Un juego rápido, divertido y perfecto para desafiar a tus amigos y familia.

¿Podrás seguir el hilo?

Cada turno, debes jugar una carta que añades a una frase en común. Cada jugador debe repetir, sin errores, las cartas que han añadido los anteriores jugadores. ¡Cada turno será más difícil! Además, tienes cartas especiales que te ayudarán.

—description from the publisher

Tagline: "The Quick-Fire Card Game Guaranteed for a Smashing Good Time!

Each player has seven cards, most of which have play restrictions. Players take turns; objective is to empty hand.

Cards have a variety of crawling and flying bugs, and special cars to force your opponent to take the discard pile, to clear the discard pile, to change playing direction and so on.

Calling all strategists and music moguls! If you love strategy, dice battles, and card management, then get ready to rock the charts in ONE MILLION ALBUMS! Your mission: conquer the music industry and be the first to sell one million albums.

The journey to platinum status unfolds in two distinct phases. First, the creative hustle: amass cash, the lifeblood of your next hit record. Navigate the unpredictable music scene with Event cards that can either propel you forward or throw a chaotic spin into your plans. Then, the main event: the intense race to sell your album. But beware! Unexpected industry shifts and rival maneuvers can derail even the best strategies. Alliances can be forged, but trust is a fragile commodity on the path to a million.

Only one will claim the ultimate prize: becoming the first to reach the coveted ONE MILLION ALBUM milestone. Are you ready to make music history?

—description from the publisher

This pack contains 4 instruments and 6 patrons (3 A + 3 Royal) voted on by supporters of the crowdfunding campaign in 2024.

—description from the publisher

Freezers is a paper and pencil game that can be played on a small piece of paper at the pub or cafe. Players alternate claiming (placing a stone of their colour on) an empty square of a square grid; no passing. If you have no legal placements, you lose.

A group touching any strictly larger enemy group, or lacking an empty adjacent space, is said to be 'frozen'.
To 'grow' is to claim an empty square orthogonally adjacent to a friendly group.

If you can claim a square with no orthogonal neighbours, you must. After such squares are depleted, it is time to grow. If all your groups are the same size, you can grow any unfrozen group. Otherwise, grow an unfrozen group without growing your largest groups (unless only your largest groups remain unfrozen).

Touching (and thus freezing) your or enemy groups is allowed; only the starting group must be unfrozen.

—description from the designer

Six Greek warriors have fallen into the middle of an ancient underground temple, and the Gods of Olympus must manipulate all of them to achieve the most glory.

In Fate of the Mortals, God cards show players which mortals they want to perish in the dangerous temple, and which they want to escape with a Gift from the Gods. Players place chamber cards to the expanding temple, and manuever the mortals through the maze of corridors. The player to claim the most glory points on their God card is the winner!

You are newly resurrected combatants in Xerce's Arena; fighting to keep the new life you have been given. Soul Survivor is a head to head gladiator-style mage arena game where you actually aim and dodge the spells you cast. A unique take on the Turn Order: Role Order mechanic creates the sense of really aiming and dodging spells. At the start of the game, players draft a combination of Magic Guild and Species to create their unique combatant. There are 384 possible combinations of Guild and Species for fresh and exciting asymmetric play! Soul Survivor is fast paced and extremely unforgiving. The careless won't last long in Xerce's Arena.

—description from the designer

The Black Rider is a story-driven journey set on a fixed map with pre-printed terrain. It introduces secret objectives, asymmetric characters, and event-driven gameplay, while a mysterious rider roams the land—displacing meeples and disrupting scoring wherever he passes.

What’s New in This Module?
Fixed Map Gameplay: Instead of building the landscape tile by tile, players place tiles only on predetermined open spaces on a beautifully illustrated map of medieval France. Space is limited—every move matters.

Asymmetric Characters with Secret Goals: Each player assumes the role of a unique character, such as Mette the Farmer-Queen or Ser Dorian the Merchant. Every character has a backstory and a hidden personal goal that rewards points if completed by game’s end.

Royal Decrees: Some tiles feature an envelope icon. When placed, the player draws a Royal Decree—a powerful event card that can dramatically change the game, from tax breaks to plagues and sudden scoring shifts.

The Black Rider Mechanic: At the start of each turn, the active player rolls a die to move the Black Rider across the board. Any meeples he passes are returned to their owners’ hands. At game’s end, areas haunted by the Rider are blocked from scoring. This adds constant tension and forces players to adapt strategically.

Why Is This a Unique Tile-Laying Experience?
The Black Rider blends classic tile placement and area control with narrative depth and dynamic threats. It adds roleplaying elements without slowing gameplay, encouraging both strategic planning and immersive storytelling.

Whether you’re a seasoned tile-based game fan or a narrative gamer seeking more depth in your board game nights, The Black Rider offers a fresh, suspenseful twist that’s easy to learn and hard to forget.

—description from the publisher

Take your emotional journey to the next level with Expressions: Missions!

Featuring 60 unique challenges, Expressions: Missions introduces new ways to play that twist, stretch, and deepen the original game’s cooperative puzzle. From subtle constraints to dramatic rule shifts, each mission adds a fresh layer of strategy, replayability, and emotional chaos.

Whether you’re decoding silent signals under stricter limitations or racing against new timing pressures, each session now begins with a mission card that changes the rules – and changes how you connect.

Some missions reward clever teamwork. Others punish overconfidence. All of them demand that your group stays in sync – emotionally and mentally.

Can your team still maintain Harmony when the rules of the game keep changing?

boardgame: The Battle of the Divas: Palco Reale
description:
Palco Reale expands on The Battle of the Divas by intensifying the duel and pushing the drama up a notch or two. While the legendary rivalry between Callas and Tebaldi reaches fever pitch on stage, all eyes turn to the private boxes. The procession of celebrities, aristocrats and jet-setters turns each performance into a show within a show.

In Palco Reale you will not only perform for the audience; you must also keep in mind that in the Royal Box are some of the most influential people of the twentieth century. Each of them has their own tastes and whims, so satisfying their expectations will be crucial for promoting your artistic vision to the world. The spotlight is on you. Can you live up to it?

—description from the publisher

The Manhattan Affair
The Manhattan Affair is an independent, story-driven module designed to complement classic real estate board games set in urban environments. Set in the glamorous and cutthroat world of 1920s New York, this narrative experience introduces secret agendas, influence mechanics, and high-stakes decision-making where charm and cunning matter just as much as cash.

Key Features:
Unique Characters & Secret Agendas
Each player receives a character card representing a figure from elite New York society—each with a hidden personal goal. Some seek real estate empires, others control over nightlife, or a chain of hostile takeovers.

Public Fund Mechanic
A shared fund grows from rent, taxes, and trades. Players may draw from it for subsidies or special bonuses—if the dice and city hall allow.

Subsidy System
Urban development becomes more dynamic: players can apply for funding to build, with dice rolls determining permit approvals or rejections. Strategy and risk replace the old buy-and-build routine.

Nightclubs
Go beyond hotels. Nightclubs are elite venues that trap visitors for multiple turns—unless they charm or pay their way out. Think velvet ropes, not just rent.

Hostile Takeovers
Forget friendly trades. Land on an opponent’s property, roll higher, and seize it at a premium. Fail, and the public fund cashes in on your ambition.

Flexible Building Rules
You no longer need full color sets to build. Development is faster, more tactical, and shaped by opportunity.

The Manhattan Affair adds bluffing, negotiation, and narrative depth to the familiar city-building formula—perfect for players who want social drama and strategic surprises on top of their game nights.

This module is not affiliated with, authorized, sponsored, or endorsed by the publishers or trademark holders of Monopoly™. A compatible real estate board game is required to play.

A tricky game for in between or on the go with high replay value, new game mechanics, driven by 72 “Faunumber“ cards.

You have play/lay out one cards after the other to build diagonal lines to create value of line of the same category of animals or numbers or streets of numbers. The winner is the player with the highest points at the end of the game.

The cards have two diagonal sides, one side with an animal and side with a number.

—description from the publisher

Ein kniffliges Spiel für zwischendurch oder Unterwegs mit hohem Wiederspielwert, neue Spielmechanik, getrieben durch
72 Faunumber-Karten

—description from the publisher (German)

Positano: The Crowded Cliffside includes components in two new colors so that now up to six players can bid for building spaces in Positano.

To give players more room in which to build, the expansion includes a new set of cliffside steps that expand the playing area to a 5x5 area instead of 4x4. This larger cliffside also allows for a four-player variant game that lasts six rounds instead of the usual four.

This expansion also includes a solo mode since the cliffside needs so much construction that you and Otto each need two colors to handle it all!

A series of puzzle games on postcards.

In late 19th-century France, two lovers hide secret messages in a series of three postcards. Will you be observant and clever enough to decode them? Scan the QR code and let the game begin!

Each episode of *Mystery Cards* contains 3 postcards, all linked by a single story. Will you be able to solve the mysteries hidden in the correspondence?

The Witcher: No Man’s Land is a thrilling miniature skirmish game set in the brutal and mysterious world of The Witcher, drawing from both CD PROJEKT S.A.’s acclaimed video game series and Andrzej Sapkowski’s legendary books. Officially licensed and rich with lore, the game plunges players into gritty battles across the war-torn lands of the Continent, where monsters roam and survival is never guaranteed.

Designed for two players, the game begins with an epic starter set that makes it easy for both newcomers and seasoned skirmish veterans to dive into the action. Players command warbands composed of iconic characters and factions from The Witcher universe, engaging in fast-paced, tactical combat across diverse and treacherous terrain.

Gameplay is driven by the innovative [RE]action System, which eliminates traditional rounds and phases in favor of a dynamic flow where players act and react in real-time. Characters can be wounded mid-battle, reducing their effectiveness and creating dramatic tension and urgency. This system promotes cinematic, narrative-rich moments where every move counts.

The ultimate goal? Outfight and outwit your opponent in scenarios full of danger, strategy, and dark fantasy flavor, proving your dominance in a world where no one is safe and every choice has consequences.

—description from the publisher

8th Star is a 4X strategic sci-fi board game where players expand their territory to acquire vital resources, unlock unique technological advancements, and engage in massive fleet combat.

Play as one of six unique alien factions to achieve victory through Conquest, Sanctuary, or Ascension.

Conquest: Conquer another player's home world and prove yourself a force to be reckoned with.

Sanctuary: Seize control of all 7 Sanctuary locations to gain access to the 8th Star.

Ascension: Build all 6 of your Nexus to achieve your Ascension goal.

Players take turns expanding their territory control via transport ships and combat, exploiting tile resources for the development of new fleets and technologies, and exterminating their rival's ships and stations. Each faction is tooled towards a different path to victory, but that doesn't mean it's their only way. The first player to achieve one of the victory types wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Flash Point: Legacy of Flame – Game Boards
description:
7 double sided boards (530x425 mm)
These boards replace the spiral bound maps of Flash Point: Legacy of Flame, but also serve as expansion maps for the original game Flash Point: Fire Rescue.

boardgame: Greedy Merchants from the Jagiellonian Trade Route
description:
Dynamic board game, full of negative interaction and gameplay twists. Good planning and cunning will be your best friends in this game.

Immerse yourself in the realities of the medieval Jagiellonian Trade Route, running for centuries between the largest cities of Poland and Lithuania, Cracow and Vilnius, and make a fortune, not always fairly.

Send transports with hidden cargo, but also with hidden guards and special traps. Protect your transports with guard units and use all opportunitie to attack and capture the transports of your rivals. Take care of the royal cargo as if they were your own head and beware of robbers if you decide to shorten your route through the dangerous forest.

Only the most cunning and greedy merchant of the Jagiellonian Trade Route will win. Are you ready for an adventure like you have never experienced before?

Yo Ho! A pirate's life isn't all it's cracked up to be!

In this scurvy card game designed by Scott Rogers, you play numbered cards to walk your pirates across the plank, ever closer towards the sea creatures that wait for a meal below.

Each game takes about 30-45 minutes to play, making it a perfect bite for your game night!

Feed a sea beast and your reward is in shiny gold coins! The more coins you collect, the closer you get to winning!

The mutinous pirate who collects the most coins, wins the game!

—description from the designer

boardgame: YES or NO: The Game Dating and Relationships Expansion Pack
description:
The newest addition to Yes or No: The Game! With more than +125 new cards, find out even more about your significant other, friends, and family. Judge their stances on love, relationships, and modern dating to figure out what makes the world go 'round.

Topics such as:

     Interfaith marriages are doomed to fail.
     Watching porn is cheating.
     “Fur-babies” are replacing children.
     It's okay to date multiple people at once when you’re not “exclusive”.


—description from the publisher

A simplified and accessible version of our Ecologies game, Ecologies Junior: Ocean allows younger children to practice building food webs with 64 sturdy tiles.

There are 32 pairs of matching organism tiles for a total of 64 tiles.

The game can be played as a simple memory game with children flipping tiles or extended to include food web building and trophic level practice.

It is flexible enough to be played cooperatively or competitively, depending on the audience.

It contains much of the art from the original Ecologies card game, with a few bonus additions as well.

Designed by a biology teacher, it can be played for fun or used in preschools, outdoor schools, homeschools, and elementary schools to teach children about ocean ecology.

This game also shares a common back with Ecologies Junior: Forest, meaning you can mix and match the tiles from both games if you like!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: kCaloria: Namib Desert & Mouth of the Congo River
description:
Introduces 2 new sets of tiles with organisms from two new ecosystems: the Namib Desert and the Mouth of the Congo River. This expansion is standalone but can also be incorporated into any other biome set of kCaloria.

kCaloria gives you the chance to build an expansive and intricate food chain that must overcome obstacles at every turn!

The objective of kCaloria is to play greater and more complex tiles in order to increase your final score. You will alternate placing tiles with your opponents and expand your respective food chains to dominate the table!

Note: Each player needs their own 60-tile set with KCalorie counters.

Feel the excitement of basketball with the BASKET Card Game from Dadral Games. Experience the intensity of a basketball game, scoring points and blocking your opponents. Ideal for family reunions, birthdays, or simply for a good time.
     Suitable for ages 6 and up, this game is suitable for both children and adults, and is a must-have for those who are passionate about basketball. Even if you're not a fan, we encourage you to give it a try, as you don't need to know the rules of the sport. You'll be totally captivated!
     With 67 cards and 48 scoring tokens, this board game offers a variety of strategies and tactics. Each card has its own role, from scoring points to blocking the opposing team's players. It includes two game modes: you can play for a set time or to a specific score. Your goal will always be the same: to score more points than your opponents.

In Sept. 2024, chemical engineer Noémie Boljvic died under suspicious circumstances. You’ve received the full correspondence that raised red flags. Fuzzy bumblebees buzz through the blooming meadow, flying from flower to flower and collecting pollen from the colorful blossoms. Can you help your bumblebee pollinate as many flowers of the same color as possible in one flight? And will you manage to spot all the meadow dwellers hidden among the grass?

The goal is to collect as many victory points as possible over six rounds. Each player builds their own meadow from cards, trying to form the longest continuous rows of flowers of the same color to pollinate with their bumblebee. In each round, players choose two out of three cards to add to their meadow, then fly their bee along a selected row of flowers. But watch out — you can’t fly along the same row two rounds in a row!

—description from the publisher

PAWS is a tableau- and engine-building game for 1–5 players.

You step into the boots of a wildlife conservationist, rescuing Africa’s endangered animals and building the most biodiverse sanctuary. Restore habitats and complete shared goals across legendary regions like the Serengeti and Okavango Delta.

On your turn, draft a die from the shared pool to perform one of five conservation actions everyone resolves together simultaneously:


    Adopt: Draw cards, set adoption fees, and adopt animals from each other to find the best home for each species. 
    Place: Place animals in fitting habitats like Grasslands or Waterholes, or tuck under others for synergies.
    Feed: Feed your adopted animals their specific diets to trigger abilities and build toward endgame Conservation Points.
    Protect Land: Draft land tiles (revealing more underneath), pursue goals, and score at game end when complete.
    Collect Reserve: Reclaim your stashed food from reserves back to your food bank for that satisfying payoff!


Use these actions to race for Region goals and trigger the Rain Dance. The conservationist with the most Conservation Points wins!

PAWS is for you if:


     You love positive player interaction without mean-spirited take-that.
     You enjoy low-downtime games with simultaneous actions by all players.
     You crave building powerful engines with satisfying combos.
     You want meaningful themes woven into every rule, not just pasted on.
     You appreciate flexible game length with tension in the race to trigger the finale.
     You like games that play well at every player count, including an Automa-style solo mode against wildlife ranger Naledi.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Verbrechen von nebenan: Das Krimispiel zum Nr. 1-Podcast
description:
The crime game based on the No. 1 podcast ‘Crime next door’ by Philipp Fleiter! 
In this co-operative mystery game, the gripping case of the missing Jack the Ripper knife, which could be Philipp's undoing, awaits you! Visit the locations connected to the case, collect clues and evidence, check the suspects' alibis and check your guesses on the accompanying website. Will you think creatively enough and draw the right conclusions to save Philipp in time, or will you follow the wrong trail and let him down? Only in the multimedia finale will you see whether your conclusions are correct. A game for real detectives!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Crime & Dine: Das Krimi-Dinner-Set – Mörderisches Südtirol
description:
The murder mystery dinner for all South Tyrol fans!
Invite your guests to an exciting dinner evening in the picturesque South Tyrolean village of Gundl am See. But be warned: murder is on todays menu! As the players, you are the main suspects in a mysterious death case: Time and again, obituaries of people who are still alive appear in Gundl's daily newspaper. The common time of death draws ever closer. Perplexed and desperate, the victims turn to the police, who take the potential victims into protective custody on the day in question. Together they want to get to the bottom of the matter, but at the end of the evening it is not one of the people seeking protection but the local policeman who is found dead. It soon becomes clear that the murderer is among the people seeking protection! And a race against time begins before there are any more victims ...
Together you take on the many different roles of the villagers and try to track down the murderer. Who will be creative enough to uncover the truth? Will you manage to solve the case in the end, or will you let the perpetrator get away unscathed?

—description from the publisher

Vampire: The Masquerade - Palermo Conspiracies is a cooperative narrative game for 1-4 players set in the World of Darkness, exclusive for the Teburu game system.

Take the role of Camarilla Archons, agents sent to investigate the disappearance of the Prince of Palermo. Follow investigative leads and choose your approach taking advantage of your character's strengths, and don't let one Camarilla faction overcome the other, until you decide who is worthy. Discover unspeakable secrets in this narrative driven campaign set in the fascinating city of Palermo.

Palermo Conspiracies is not a mere sequel of Milan Uprising. It’s a refined narrative experience that introduces new systems, shifts the balance between cooperation and rivalry, and deepens the strategic layers around Influence and personal power: each Archon pursues a secret personal agenda, will be able to manage their own resources and access to personal Havens. Through new, specific Encounters they will shape the Camarilla’s influence within the city and advance the main story, both in solo or as a coterie.

The innovative gameplay powered by the Teburu System—which revolutionarily integrates physical and digital components—features smart miniatures and a smart game board that automatically track movements and actions, a free dedicated app that manages enemies, events, and narrative twists, dynamic artificial intelligence for unpredictable opponents and ever-changing challenges, and immersive audiovisual effects that enrich the atmosphere with sounds, images, and cinematic moments.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Expand your Riftlands experience with the March 2025 release! This content drop introduces Fenrir, The Unteamed Beast, a new Hero embodying the primal power of the forests. As a Hunter, Fenrir brings unique skills to track and ambush foes, adding a predatory edge to your strategy. His pack includes a miniature (40 & 100mm STL), player board, passive card, and four skill cards, offering exciting new ways to dominate the battlefield.

Face a terrifying new challenge with the Blood Colossus, the first monster miniature in Riftlands, and new Monster Difficulty Level cards. This brutal creatures come with new conditions like POISON, CONFUSION, and IMMOBILIZE, demanding new tactical approaches. The March release also enhances gameplay with the new Hunter role cards and token, five new Hero Skill Reaction cards (one for each hero), twenty new Solo Mode cards for increased replayability, and two Global Cooldown Boards for smoother ability tracking. Dive into the March content and unleash new strategies in the ever-evolving world of Riftlands!

—description from the publisher

Match 3 of a kind or get a special Steal card to take rocks from other players, before they take yours! The more rocks you steal, the safer you'll feel! Be the last player standing to WIN!

—description from the back of the box

The Floor is Lava is a card game in which players try to avoid falling into the lava.

At the start of the game, each player gets three rock cards. During the game, they steal these rock cards from each other. Each player has a hand of three cards. There are three types of cards: regular cards (numbered 1, 2, or 3), wild cards, and steal cards.

On their turn, a player can play a set of three regular cards with the same value to steal a rock card from another player. Wild cards can be used to substitute for a regular card in order to make a set. Alternatively, a player can play a single steal card, which also allows them to steal a rock card (or sometimes two) from another player.
Is a player unable to play a set or a steal card, they simply discard one card without stealing a rock.
At the end of their turn, the player refills their hand to five cards.

Once a player loses their last rock, they are eliminated from the game. They fall into the lava, and turn in a lava monster. Despite being eliminated, they still participate in the game. On their turn, they can remove one of the rocks from one of the remaining players, removing that rock from the game.

When only one player remains, that player wins the game, and the game ends.

—user summary
family: Mechanism: Eliminated Players Continue to Participate (But Cannot Win)

description:
Chickens 'n Aliens is a fun card game for 2 - 4 players. The object of the game is to get 3 aliens laid down from your hand before other players can. Corn is used a resource - and aliens can only be placed by 'paying' a corn to the central pool. As well as alien cards, there are action cards and special cards that will aid the player in doing everything they can to stop the other players - stealing their aliens, cards, corn or the shirt off their back!(not really)
It's a 'one action per turn' game, so very little down time, and quite often a very rapid back and forth, which gets funnier the longer it goes on... Each win of a round earns a part of the spaceship, and 3 parts wins the game.
30-60 mins and a great pub game as it fits in a pocket.

—descripton from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Armchair Quarterback: The Ultimate Football Game
description:
Armchair Quarterback: The Ultimate Football Game combines football excitement with super snack strategy. Players collect yardage cards by using snack and penalty cards to score touchdowns and flick field goals, all while competing to collect a piece of the pot filled with pretzels. Choose your strategy and play your way to victory.


     Strategic Football Card Game – Play your way to victory by combining tactical decision-making and thrilling gameplay.
     Includes Authentic Football Components – Enjoy the immersive feel of real football with a foam football, goalpost, field, and themed tokens for touchdowns and field goals in this tabletop football game.
     Unique Blend of Cards & Action – Mix strategy and luck with 52 Snack Cards and 25 Penalty Cards to outsmart opponents.

Can you put these works of art in order of when they were created? Caravaggio's The Musicians, a bronze statue of Eros sleeping and Rembrandt's Aristotle with a Bust of Homer.

The Metropolitan Museum of Art is one of the world's largest and finest art museums with a collection that spans 5,000 years of world culture, from prehistory to the present and from every part of the globe.

Explore the treasures of The Met with this timeline, piecing together classics from throughout history and discovering pieces from around the world. Featuring 25 works of art and including a short booklet with information on all of the artists, this unique new memory game is the perfect gift for any art fan wanting to discover the treasures of The Met.

This artwork timeline game will amaze fans of artworks, from statues and ceramics to paintings and portraits, can you place when works of art were created in relation to each other? With easy-to-learn rules this addictive game is quick to learn and easy to repeat, and will reveal historical insights to the world of art every time you play.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

description:
ConShot ‘25 is a limited edition expansion for SAS Rogue Regiment. It features 5 new missions, a new game mode, new mechanics, massive new locations & much more. ConShot ‘25 is fully compatible with all existing SAS Rogue Regiment content.

Dam Busting! - In this nod to the famous "Operation Musketoon" raid of 1942, Operators must infiltrate a hydroelectric power plant and sabotage the turbines, bringing Axis manufacturing in the region to a grinding halt!

Where Eagles Win! - In this daring rescue mission, Operators must free one of their comrades from a fortified mountaintop base, accessible only by cable car!

The Argentinian Job! - With the war coming to an unfavourable end, high ranking Nazis are making preparations to flee the continent. Gold is being stockpiled at the dam awaiting transportation via U-Boat to South America... that’s if our Operators don't get to it first!

—description from the publisher

Lunovos Industries, a company at the forefront of space exploration, is ready to send their brand new rocket into outer space.

But a secret plot to disrupt the launch has been revealed...

Can you find the spy and uncover the diabolical disruption they have planned before it's too late?! Become a true detective in this thrilling standalone prequel to 'Murder On The Moon', an immersive escape game adventure.

—description from the publisher

A prickly twist on the classic game of pick-up sticks! Collect sticks without disturbing the wooden hedgehog to earn points. The player with the highest score when the hedgehog falls is the winner! Roll the die to determine the color you’re playing with, and then ever so gently remove a stick of that color from the bunch. But be very careful because with each stick that’s removed, the balancing hedgehog will become more and more unsteady, and when it drops, the game is over.

And there’s an extra prickle! If the die lands on the hedgehog icon, you can invent a rule for the next player to follow on their turn. Something like “Keep one eye closed” or “Sing a song”— it’s totally up to you!

boardgame: 100% Logique: La réponse est sous vos yeux
description:
100% Logique  – The answer is in front of you!

Forget trivia, history dates, and trick questions—here, only logic matters! Inspired by the hit French TV show, 100% Logic challenges your reasoning skills through a series of clever and surprising puzzles.

Each question invites you to observe carefully, think outside the box, and trust your instincts. No need to be a quiz champion or a higly smart person: The answer is always hidden in plain sight. You just have to look at things differently to find it.

Easy to learn, endlessly fun, and highly addictive, 100% Logique delivers fast-paced, engaging rounds where everyone has a chance to shine, regardless of age or experience. Perfect for game nights with friends or family!

A smart, feel-good game where logic meets laughter.
Can you spot what others miss?

Never have I ever... - the popular game classic for adults over 18!
Watch out, it's about to get hot! Whether on the move, at home or as an exciting party game - this card game is guaranteed to get hot. Sex, Drugs & Crime - over 60 erotic and criminally good statements are guaranteed to provide fun and variety at parties, bridal showers and birthdays. The perfect game for finding out sexy secrets or provoking uproarious laughter.

description:
Jump into action with Quick Draw, where players race against time to draw prompts and guess correctly, making every round a thrilling challenge.

It’s all pens at the ready in this fast-paced fun-filled drawing game! One player wears the shades with a prompt card. It’s up to the other players to draw the prompt as quickly as they can and slam their drawing down in the first place spot on the board. Second to finish drawing puts their scribble on 2nd place, and so on. The guesser then removes the shades and tries to figure out what on earth’s been drawn!

You can test your knowledge of sex, parties and crime in 52 categories and put your creativity to the test. The game pad features a cool design and spicy themes - perfect for all city, country and river fans. From simple questions to tricky categories - it's all here!
