description:
Now it's getting spicy!
Weed, tequila and doggy style without words? Anyone can do that! But can you guess the words when the roles are reversed? The classic pantomime game is reinvented here, according to the motto: if you act, you have to guess! The person performing follows the instructions of the others and has to find out the term they are looking for themselves!
Simple rules and forbidden good terms: The perfect combination of hot and funny for the next party!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pantomime: Switch it up! – Family Edition
description:
Depicting a mobile phone, dog and baker without words? Anyone can do that! 
But can you also guess the terms when the roles are reversed? The classic pantomime game is reinvented here, according to the motto: if you act, you have to guess! The person performing follows the instructions of the others and has to find out the term they are looking for themselves!
Simple rules and a colourful mix of terms: great fun for the whole family!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Few Truths and a Lie
description:
How well do you know your party guests? Blast beyond small talk with new friends or share stories with old besties as you go from the ridiculous to the fascinating in this hilarious get-to-know-you game.

Pick your favorite prompt, listen to your friends' answers, and win points by correctly guessing who drew the 'LIE card! With 200 Prompts, no game will be the same as you try to find the liar in the laughs!


     Figure out who you can trust and who is a dirty liar (and who has the best stories!)
     Whether you have to lie your heart out or track down the fibber, it’s not going to be easy
     Be the first player to earn 5 Win Tokens and claim your victory!


—description from the publisher

The Rise of EnGarde: Winter Season of War

In the winter of 2025, the world changed—not just in our reality, but in the growing, thundering realm of EnGarde, the epic CCG (Collectible Card Game) that brought brutal strategy and living storytelling into a single explosive force. This was the beginning of the Winter Season Cards - a special release that cracked open a new era of myth, metal, and global warfare, all set in the frostbitten, battle-hardened world of EnGarde.

Across continents, the clamor of blades and the roar of monsters echoed not only in decks and tournaments, but across airwaves and online platforms, as players from around the world submitted their own EnGarde battle stories—each of them a spark that could become canon, a scene rendered live on air for all to see.

The World of EnGarde: Ice and Iron

The world of EnGarde is not one of gentle alliances or noble quests. It is a world seared by conflict—its lands split between war-born nations like Ruthgar, the fire-wielding mountain empire; Selvara, the frostbound kingdom of spies and witches; Thonmar, a shattered desert realm driven by blood duels; and Kyvren, an island nation where beasts walk beside men. Each country brings its own flavor of power, its own battle doctrines, and its own heroes—now immortalized in the Winter Season Cards.

Where summer brought stealth and subterfuge, winter brought brutality. The bight of the season turned swords colder and hearts harder. Every card in the Winter Season deck reflects this merciless chill: the Frostblade Duelists, their strikes silent as snow; Soulbound Archpriests, who channel blizzards through divine fury; and the Gorewolves of Yrn, feral monsters that tear through armies and leave frostbitten corpses in their wake.

Cards That Move – And So Do You

But EnGarde was never just about the deck in your hand—it was about where that deck went.

For the first time in CCG history, EnGarde introduced Global Deck Registration: players who built their character decks online could not only enter the global leaderboard, but track their card deck location and view its battle history as it traveled. Whether shipped to a tournament in Tokyo or facing down a rival’s deck in Berlin, your cards were no longer just paper—they were warriors in a global saga.

Each registered deck comes with a unique digital ID tied to your character. Your deck could be challenged in any certified match, and if victorious, it climbed the ranks and etched your name into the Hall of Steel, EnGarde’s global archive of battle-hardened champions.

You might be sitting in a café in Buenos Aires while your deck—anchored by your proudest champion, Vraskin the Crag-Helm—faced off against a demon horde in an exhibition match in Seoul, streamed live on the EnGarde Battle Channel. Through the online interface, you could watch as your strategy unfolded in the hands of pro-tier players, commentators shouting out your deck’s name as your war cards carved their legacy into the cold, wintry soil.

Submit Your Story, Shape the World

Perhaps the most revolutionary feature of EnGarde in 2025 was the Battle Story Submission System. Every registered player could submit the story of a battle they fought—detailing their character’s decisions, sacrifices, victories, and defeats. These stories were reviewed, selected, and adapted into the official EnGarde world narrative.

Once chosen, your tale would be performed live on air—re-enacted by AI-driven cinematic engines, voice actors, and animated sequences broadcast through EnGarde Chronicles, the game's weekly lore series. Your champion could become the next legendary commander. Your tactical ploy might be memorialized in a new limited-edition spell card. If your monster slayed a king, you might even trigger a political shift in the world map for the next season.

Each battle wasn't just a game—it was lore in motion, a choice that could echo through expansions to come.

The Flow of Brutality

The slogan for Winter Season 2025 was etched into every card pack and campaign poster:

“In winter, the world bleeds.”

The flow of brutality surged not just in card stats, but in the rhythm of competition. Players organized regional warfronts—Selvara vs. Ruthgar face-offs that became week-long grudge matches. Storylines emerged naturally: a defeated deck might flee north to recruit reinforcements, triggering new story arcs. Players formed mercenary guilds or royal courts, choosing whether their deck fought as a rebel, a ruler, or a beast-wielder without master.

Even monsters evolved. Some decks leaned into the Monstrosity Mechanic, which let decks grow more feral as they lost life, gaining unpredictable powers. A Frost Basilisk might start slow, but if left alive too long, it could freeze entire rows of the battlefield, disrupting even the best-laid plans.

A New Era of CCGs

EnGarde wasn’t just a card game anymore—it was a living world driven by player choices, deck evolution, and narrative consequence. With every new submission, match, and registration, the brutal winter world expanded. By spring, alliances would shatter, monsters would rise, and a new class of player-kings would emerge.

But for now—winter reigns.

So if you haven’t yet—register your card deck. Choose your champion. Tell your tale. Because the world of EnGarde waits for no one. And in this game?

You don’t just play the story. You become it.

Get dirty, silly & nowhere fast in this intuitive, crazy game that perfectly captures the skids, scents and sidequests of bikepacking. A roll and move cycle camping board game with a co-operative and comedic flavour. To succeed you must help each other solve problems but players can also play practical jokes on one another.

You ride along through the woods of some non-specific forest world with a group of other riders and collaborate and co-operate to get to the next camp spot. You can ride together, share resources and face disasters together to collect points. You can also play dirty tricks on each other by slipping your friends Cringe cards.

Each round covers 1 day.  After 3 days, the game ends when all riders arrive at the finish point.  Points are then totaled  and the player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ランカーフィッシュ (Lunker Fish)
description:
Become an angler, gain experience through repeated fishing, and use that experience to acquire new equipment to become the best angler! This is a new type of deck-building fishing game that is based on deck-building elements, but also has the fun of flipping over a deck.
The cards you turn over become your shopping funds, so the more fishing equipment you have, the more you can do. But sometimes you catch the Earth and can't draw any more cards...

Even if you catch a fish, it's a difficult choice to make: should you use it as victory points or make it into a large fund? Also, no matter how many tools you have, the battle with the fish is a matter of luck. Depending on the roll of the dice, the fish may escape...

But don't worry, if you run out of decks, you can shuffle the discarded cards and get another chance. Don't give up, cast your lure again and again!

In the early Heian period, plum blossoms were a popular flower viewing spot. Create a beautiful garden while admiring the birds fluttering about and the fragrant plum blossoms.

This game has three features:

     Simple tile placement game
     Open draft system including turn order
     The game changes dramatically when the common goal "trend" changes!

Exclusive fauna promo card made for Our Family Plays Games, featuring a Red Fox on one side and Badger on the other.

A single, double-sided card.

Red Fox (Vulpes vulpes):
2+ sets of sunny, wet, rocky, cold (count only 1 sunny, wet, rocky, cold per card)

Badger (Meles meles):
2+ sets of trees, bushes, herbs, mushrooms (count only 1 trees, bushes, herbs, mushrooms per card)

A set of two identical preview decks that, when played against each other, introduce the basic mechanics and gameplay of Hubworld: Aidalon. Choose from four different Seekers to try different strategies with the same cards.

boardgame: Penguins Can't Fly: The Jurassic Flap
description:
In Penguins Can't Fly, penguins fought to be the final survivor.  Now we time travel into the past and see how prehistoric penguins face their ultimate predator....DINOSAURS!

In The Jurassic Flap, each player selects a penguin character and begins at the top of the laid-out track cards. Players then take turns drawing cards from the deck. Most cards will send you closer to the water, bad news. But action cards let you mess with your opponents, switch places, look ahead, or force someone else to drop faster. Track cards can cause mid-air chaos or slow some penguins down, while others are unaffected.

One by one, penguins plummet until only one remains. That player wins. Play cards, avoid falling, sabotage your friends, and most importantly, don’t become dinosaur lunch.

This edition introduces new actions, track cards and dinosaurs.
The expansion to Penguins' Can't Fly adds a new character, 3 new action cards, 3 new direction cards and expands the game to 5 players.

GOLF Extraordinaire is designed for the golfer as well as non golfer. Several players of all ages can play at the same time for hours of fun. The large board represents an eighteen hold golf course layout with water, trees and sand traps. A variation of instructional cards and dice will determine the length and quality of shot making.

GOLF Extraordinaire is a great alternative to computer games and television, an an activity for the whole family to enjoy. If it's raining or for some reason you can't play a real round of golf, GOLF Extraordinaire will fulfill your golf appetite. It will also teach the beginner how to play and prepare them for the real game.

A puzzle game where you arrange hexagonal cubes to recreate a given shape.
The rules are very simple, but you won’t be able to complete the shape unless you carefully consider the orientation and overlap of the cubes.
Rotate the cubes, fit them together, and use your imagination to complete the shape as quickly as possible.

boardgame: モンスタービオトープ (Monster Biotope)
description:
This cooperative card game challenges players to construct a stable food chain pyramid, stacking monsters in the right order so the King Monster can be born at the top. Monsters at lower levels become nutrients for those above, and their traits are passed upward like evolving genes. Only through teamwork and careful planning can players bring about the birth of the King Monster and save the world’s fragile ecosystem.

Work together, strategize your monster placements, and aim for ecological harmony in this one-of-a-kind fantasy puzzle!

Simplex Profession Lotto. Made in Holland. All wood. 8 sets of 5 small boards. One board gives the indication to which the other 4 boards belong. A game which learns, while playing, the words of the several items. 
For ages 3 to 7.

Project SONO is a one-of-a-kind tabletop experience for 4 players, where each player explores a post-apocalyptic world from a unique and secret perspective. With personal objectives and asymmetric gameplay, everyone shares the same physical space, but their actions influence each other in mysterious ways. Set in the ruins of a collapsed society ravaged by radiation, players must observe, take notes, use and discard items, and alter both their personal and shared location sheets through drawings and decisions. The goal is to survive—and earn the most victory points through your choices. But beware: trust is a luxury, allies are uncertain, and the city hides more than it reveals. Adapt, endure… and above all, don’t die.

Proyecto SONO es una experiencia de juego única para 4 jugadores. Cada uno explorará una aventura desde una perspectiva completamente distinta, con objetivos y formas de jugar secretas y personales. Aunque todos compartirán el mismo espacio de juego, sus decisiones influirán en el mundo y en los demás jugadores… sin que sepan exactamente cómo.

Este es un juego de observación y escritura. En cada turno podrás realizar una acción y registrar todo en tu hoja de personaje. También usarás y descartarás objetos tachándolos, además de dibujar tanto en tu hoja como en las hojas de localización, según lo que hagas durante la partida.

La sociedad ha colapsado tras un evento catastrófico. Poco se sabe con certeza sobre lo que ocurrió, pero una propagación masiva de radiación acabó con la mayoría de la población. Solo unos pocos grupos han logrado sobrevivir, y ahora intentan retomar el control de los restos de las ciudades. Aún no está claro cómo sobrevivió cada grupo… ni cuáles son sus verdaderas intenciones para el futuro.

Tu misión es continuar, adaptarte y sobrevivir. Pero recuerda: posiblemente nadie en esta ciudad sea tu aliado. No confíes en nadie. Y, sobre todo… no mueras.

boardgame: Assyria: Second Edition – Scythians and Hurrians
description:
Two Promo Civilisation Boards, first released during the June 2025 Kickstarter for Assyria: Second Edition.

Scythians Tribute Bonus: Spend 1 Farmer or 1 Gold to gain a Food Card from the top of the Draw Pile.

Scythians Roof Immediate Effect: Gain 1 VP.

Hurrians Tribute Bonus: Spend 1 Camel or 1 Ship to gain 1 Gold.

Hurrians Roof Immediate Effect: Draw 2 Food Cards from the top of the Draw Pile, keep 1 and discard the other.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to FLAT OUT FOOTY!

A fast paced, board game version of Rugby League!
Players take turns using PLAYER CARDS with unique ACTIONS to gain meters, build pressure and score tries!

Every PASS, TACKLE, LINE BREAK and KICK, 
each roll of the dice has you on the edge of your seat!

—description from the designer

Welcome to Kotam: Conquest. The term "Kotam" finds its inspiration in the Sanskrit word for ancient fortresses. In ages past, formidable rulers from distant lands cast their gaze upon vast territories renowned for their fertile lands, abundant resources, and thriving economies. These rulers were resolute in their ambition to conquer these lands and make them their own.

In Kotam: Conquest, you will step into the shoes of one of the land's greatest rulers with aspirations of becoming the ultimate conqueror. Your journey takes place in a vast, diverse, and ancient mythical land filled with hexagonal tiles representing territories, cards representing warriors, and tokens symbolizing valuable resources. To succeed, you must gather warriors under your banner, conquer territories, manage resources, and make strategic decision to become the most powerful ruler in all of Kotam: Conquest.

Will you rise to power, claim victory, and emerge as the dominant force in the realm of Kotam? Are you prepared to lead your warriors to triumph and shape the destiny of this ancient land?

—description from the designer

A joyful and funny memory game where players build an ever-growing sequence of character names—Poppy, Lolly, and Tix—until surprise cards like Boom and Loop shake things up.
Remember, react, and laugh your way to victory!
Poppy Lolly Tix develops auditory memory, attention, and concentration in a light and social gameplay format.
Great for families, classrooms, and game nights.

Auditory memory, Attention and Concentration

—description from the designer

Trial by Fire is a fast-paced party card game in which players become chefs vying to impress a rotating restaurant critic. Each round the critic sets a culinary brief by selecting a thematic Restaurant Critic card. Chefs simultaneously refine their hands of Ingredient cards—discarding and redrawing in search of the perfect flavour combination—before calling “Service!” to present a dish.

Judgement hinges on three criteria: creativity, effective use of ingredients, and overall execution. The critic awards their card to the chef whose dish best meets the brief; roles then rotate and a new round begins. Play continues until one chef has amassed the agreed number of critic cards, thereby earning victory.

Optional variants (Speed Round, Restaurant Wars, Secret Ingredient, and Mystery Box) add timed play, team menus, compulsory ingredients, or surprise components, keeping the game fresh across sessions.

—description from the designer

Take command of magical creatures in a fierce battle for control over ancient power fields. Bagabazos is a tactical dice-and-cards game set on a modular arena board, where players summon and maneuver their creatures—imps (d4), golems (d6), and djinns (d8)—to outwit rivals and dominate key zones.

Every turn is a strategic puzzle: move your figure, cast a unique spell, claim a power field or launch a bold attack. But beware—even the smallest mistake may turn the tide.

Bagabazos blends tactical movement, spellcasting, dice-driven randomness, and area control into a fast-paced magical showdown for 2–4 players. Six different arena boards and dozens of unique spells ensure high replayability and evolving strategies.


     3 imps (d4), 2 golems (d6), 1 djinn (d8) per player
     21 unique spell cards in each deck
     Modular board with 6 double-sided maps
     Tense, dynamic battles with surprising twists!


—description from the designer

boardgame: Hamilcar Imperator: La Guerre des Mercenaires
description:
Hamilcar Imperator: La Guerre des Mercenaires is an Operational level Solitaire-Only board-wargame set in Carthage after the First Punic War.

At the end of the First Punic War, Carthage ceded Sicily to the Roman legions. In a gesture of magnanimity, Rome allowed the Punic garrisons holding the last strongholds to return home. Among them were Hamilcar Barca—the father of the famous Hannibal—and thousands of Celtic, Greek, and Iberian mercenaries.

Once disembarked in Africa, the soldiers began demanding their overdue pay and grew restless. The Libyans, vassals of Carthage, quickly realized that their tribute would be increased to cover the mercenaries’ wages. They made a bold gamble: they armed themselves, allied with the Numidians, poured their gold into the mercenaries’ hands, and proposed a daring plan—march on Carthage!

The player must suppress this uprising, which will quickly escalate into a bitter and unforgiving war if not contained. To do so, they will command several Punic generals—who often disagree and whose ambitions are closely watched by the suffetes. Fortunately, war elephants will help literally crush the rebellion.

Optional rules allow players to incorporate Gustave Flaubert’s Salammbô or simulate flaming pigs on the battlefield.

Scale


     1 game turn = 1 year (divided into 4 seasons)  
     1 unit = 500 to 2,000 infantry, 500 to 1,000 cavalry, or 10 elephants/siege weapons  
     Complexity: 6/10  
     Solo game


—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
'The Shadows of the Abbey'  is a deck of 14 additional cards designed to enhance the gameplay experience of The Mystery of the Abbey, enriching the dynamics with new challenges, twists, and deeper insights.

This expansion introduces new elements and mechanics that give players the opportunity to further explore the mysteries and secrets of the abbey. Each card is crafted to make the atmosphere even more immersive, increasing the difficulty and expanding the range of possible strategies.

—description from the publisher (translated)

The Crypt Expansion adds new gameplay elements, introducing a darker and more mysterious dimension to the experience of The Mystery of the Abbey. With the arrival of the Crypt, players will face new challenges and uncover hidden secrets.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Nanty Narking: Rise of Cthulhu – Dagon Expansion
description: This expansion adds another Elder One, Dagon, to Nanty Narking: Rise of Cthulhu. During the course of the game you can summon Dagon just like all the other Elders contained in Rise of Cthulhu. His special ability is "Move Dagon to another area connected by river OR remove a building from an area with Dagon."

The Dutch rebellion against Spain; English privateering in the Spanish Main; naval armadas large and small plying the coasts of western Europe; and France's religious civil wars: they all set the stage for this hemisphere-wide struggle between the mighty Catholic Empire of Spain and the nascent power of Protestant England.

As Spain, you are the aggressor and must impose (or maintain) Catholicism throughout France and Netherlands, or take London and dethrone Protestant Queen Elizabeth. As England, avoid a Spanish victory and you win. Sounds harder for Spain, right? Except Spain has all the wealth, with riches pouring in from the Spanish Main!

The Armada is a low-complexity game, where the tide can turn quite rapidly! Each turn is a year, and the units are capital ships and army regiments, as well as local levees and caravels.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
7 Wonders: Great Wall – first available through the 7 Wonders: Wonder Pack, and now available on its own – is a wonder that can be used with the 7 Wonders base game in order for players to have more choices of wonders available to them.

This is an updated version of the Great Wall wonder, designed to match the artwork found in 7 Wonders: 2nd Edition (printed from 2020 onwards), but is backwards compatible with the first edition of the game.

Although this version is visually updated to match the second edition, the rulebook from the original Wonder Pack is still the best guide to learn the rules for this promo wonder.

Unlike others wonders that need to be constructed from the first level upwards, the owner of the Great Wall can build the stages of this wonder in any order (since the wall is, of course, a long horizontal wonder and not a primarily vertical one)

Day side -
        Stage 1: The player takes 8 coins from the bank and adds them to their treasury
        Stage 2: At the end of the game, the player gains an extra scientific symbol of their choice
        Stage 3: The player adds 2 Shields to their total during each conflict resolution
        Stage 4 : The player can choose, at the end of the current turn, an Age card in the discard pile and put it in play for free.
    Night side (this side requires the Cities expansion to play)
        Stage 1: The player takes 8 coins from the bank. Both adjacent players each take 2 coins from the bank.
        Stage 2: At the end of the game, the mask copies the scientific symbol of a green card present in one of the two neighboring cities.
        Stage 3: At the end of the current Age, the player does not take part in the conflict resolution. The city to their left thus faces off against the city to their right. Moreover, each player, other than the owner of the Wonder, must pay 2 coins to the bank.
        Stage 4 : This Stage allows the player to benefit, each turn, from a resource, of the player’s choice, from among those not produced by its city from among their brown, gray and the initial resources of their board.
- from the BGG store listing of this promo

A fast-paced, fantasy-flavored card game of chaos, sabotage, and dungeon-building.

Dunjen is a quick and chaotic card game where players dive into a cursed dungeon and race to be the first to escape. Each turn, you’ll play a card that matches the current biome or twist the game with powerful spells and special effects.

But beware! Not all players want to help. Hidden among you may be adventurers with their own agendas, curses ready to strike, and monster cards that change the tides. Strategy, timing, and a touch of sabotage are your best weapons.

Dhift the biome. Reverse turns. Force others to draw. And when the time is right… drop your final card to seal the dungeon and win.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Flamecraft: Dragons of the Past Promo Pack
description:
ALL THE PREVIOUS FLAMECRAFT PROMOS!
We have bundled together all the previous Flamecraft promos released to date in this amazing deal. Includes:

     Alternate Starter Shops
     The Spice Tower
     Flamestarter Statue
     Lucky Lake
     Cosmic Scale
     Stuff the Magic Dragon
     The Rare Medium
     Space Craft
     Freshscale Artisan Dragons


—description from the publisher

Nobi Nobi TRPG Cyberpunk is like a tabletop RPG in a boardgame format - you can sit down without preparation or a game master and start playing right away.

First everyone picks a character card. Then the first GM picks a scene card and reads it to the scene player, and then roleplay the scene, typically ending with an ability test. Depending on how the scene is resolved the scene player gets a Light or Dark card added to their character and then the GM and scene player cards move to the next players. After three passes around the table you draw a conclusion card and figure out how your story ends.

Nobi Nobi is designed to be a lighthearted introduction to roleplaying for newcomers or an easy-to-use alternative for seasonsed roleplayers.

Drop Zone: Chef-du-Pont (DZ: CdP) is a Historical Advanced Squad Leader (HASL) module covering the first three days of the battles around the important Norman bridge at Chef-du-Pont and the heights of Hill 30. Itis the second in a series exploring Airborne operations on D-Day and shortly thereafter in Normandy as part of Operation OVERLORD. It focuses on the key actions to control the bridge across the Merderet River just west of the French village of Chef-du-Pont, whose capture was assigned to the 508th Parachute Infantry Regiment (PIR). Matched against the Americans were the German infantry of Grenadier-Regiment 1057, Luftlande-Infanterie-Division 91, assisted at times by the re-purposed French armor of Panzer-Ersatz-Abteilung 100.

Drop Zone: Chef-du-Pont comes with an historically accurate map of the town of Chef-du-Pont and its surrounding environs jointly crafted by designer Ken Dunn and artist Charlie Kibler. There are six individual scenarios each recreating a portion of the battle, as well as a Campaign Game of the full three-day battle.

DZ: CdP follows in the footsteps of the successful DROP-ZONE: SAINTE-MÉRE-ÈGLISE (which is not required to play DZ: CdP), but it is a much different experience. It is much more compact, with only one mapsheet, one simplified Campaign Game, and no new counters (and no cows). It requires no box and is much easier to access. Slope rules (from earlier HASL modules) are introduced, but their use is limited, and a comprehensive LOS Example of how slopes interact with bocage on the historical map is included. The only other ASL modules needed to play are BEYOND VALOR and YANKS.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Sadowa 1866: a name that sounded like a thunderclap at the turn of the 19th century. Also known as Königgrätz in the German world, it was the largest battle in the Western world after the Napoleonic Wars and up until World War I. It also marked, and contrary to what all observers expected, the fall of the power that had dominated the Germanic world for four centuries. After barely more than a week of campaigning and a series of minor battles that had severely hampered the Austrian army, 220,000 Prussians faced 180,000 Austrians allied with 20,000 Saxons positioned on high ground, their backs to the Elbe. The defensive situation chosen was both interesting and perilous.

The Prussians were not yet regrouped at the start of the day, but they nevertheless launched the attack. The victory was resounding and final for the Prussian troops. It would forever change the face of Europe. By the end of July 3, 1866, 21,000 Austro-Saxons were out of action and another 20,000 were taken prisoner: a quarter of the Habsburg Empire's northern army was eliminated, and the rest retreated in great disorder to cover Vienna. The Prussians suffered "only" 9,000 losses despite a tense situation at midday. Austria was definitively rejected from the Germanic world, which would finish building German unity around Prussia four years later.

This wargame represents the final battle in the series dedicated to the 1866 war in Bohemia. A campaign system — available for download from the website  — allows players to play through the first six battles (VaeVictis issues 157, 166, and 174) in succession, resulting in a potentially different situation from the historical one.  Based on the series The Great Battles of Napoleon III (VaeVictis issues 55, 73, 138, 157, 166, and 174), the system is simple and dynamic, emphasizing the engagement of troops in the face of deadly defensive fire.

Game turn: one hour
Units: regiments and battalions
Map: one hex. = 750m
Complexity: 5/10
Solitaire: ​​7/10
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Les Grandes Batailles de Napoleon III (Vae Victis)

description:
The worst criminals gather in a secret lair and in a confrontation with each other decide which of them is the real master of evil!

In Battle of Villains, you take control of a gang of villains. Step by step, you use the abilities of various bad guys to take power (Points of Villainy) from your opponents

—description from the publisher

It's Halloween night, the year is 2025 in Blya* World. You and your friends are offered a simple job: go to an abandoned residential area and grab some items. Easy enough, right? But what you don't know is that some maniacs have decided to have fun on Halloween night, and their hobby is killing everyone in sight.

In Sons of Blyat, some players will lead assassins who will have to kill the other non-assassin players, while the others will be Blyats and will have to collect the items. Based on the post-apocalyptic nuclear world of Blyat World, where the characters are different types of snacks.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Heroscape: Crimson Widow and Dreadnoughts of Caraway Cavern Army Expansion
description:
Seemingly capable of convincing even the most devoted crew to turn pirate and fight with newfound ferocity, the Crimson Widow is counted among the best captains to have ever lived.

One such group she swayed from Utgar’s forces are the Dreadnoughts of Caraway Cavern. A truly fearsome crew, they are unyieldingly loyal to their captain, and will gladly crush the skull of anyone who suggests otherwise...

This army expansion contains one unique hero and one unique 3-figure squad, giving players more options for customizing their armies. These figures synergize with the pirates found in the Battle for the Wellspring Battle Box and the Age of Annihilation Master Set! Turn any Unique Squad into Pirates under the leadership of Crimson Widow, destroy your enemies, and take combo turns with the loyal and brutal Dreadnoughts!

This is add-on content. A Battle Box and/or Master Set is required for gameplay.

Features

Turn any Unique Squad into Pirates with Crimson Widow and lead them with brutal efficiency. 
Buff your melee Pirates, reposition them with Midnight Raid, and unleash surprise strikes across the map. 
The Dreadnoughts gain defense near their Captain and can take combo turns with Captain’s Orders.
Punish enemies with the Dreadnought’s Mortal Blow ability for devastating follow-up damage.
Combine the Dreadnoughts and Crimson Widow with figures like Killian Vane from the Battle for the Wellspring Battle Box or the Exiles of the Sundered Sea from the Age of Annihilation Master Set to create terrifying pirate armies.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroscape: Greatbow Archers and Scions of Icaria Common Army Expansion
description:
The great dragons of Icaria are revered as majestic, regal, or even godly. Their princelings… less so. Imperious and prone to squabbling, the scions of the great dragon houses are sent to ward with the lords of other houses in the hope that they will gain some measure of regal bearing.

Not all of Icaria’s inhabitants are impressed with their draconic overlords. Some join a growing resistance of fighters trained in the art of dragonslaying. Wielding gargantuan bows larger even than their impressive frames, greatbow archers stand ready for the day their reptilian oppressors must be deposed. Even the scales of a lord can be broken.

This common army expansion contains two common squads, a 2-figure common squad of Greatbow Archers and a 3-figure common squad, Scions of Icaria, giving players more options for customizing their armies. Strike hard from a distance with the Archers or unleash Draconic Breath through coordinated strikes with the Scions!

This is add-on content. A Battle Box and/or Master Set is required for gameplay.

Features

Field multiple copies of these common squads for creative army builds.
The farther the shot, the deadlier the strike of the Greatbow Archers.
Use the Greatbow Archers as deadly support units for map control and long-range pressure.
Fly into battle and unleash Draconic Breath on nearby enemies with the Scions of Icaria.
The Scions of Icaria are fast, synergistic, and built for coordinated strikes.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroscape: Heroes of Arboreas Army Expansion
description:
The wizards of Arboreas are not a monolith. Each seeks to refine their power and connection to the natural world, but their methods could not be more diverse. While some cultivate groves and are themselves cultivated in return, others exert their arcane might to break the minds of beast and person alike.

This army expansion contains five Unique Heroes players can use to customize their armies! These Heroes synergize with the figures from the Battle for the Wellspring Battle Box and the Age of Annihilation Master Set. Command beasts, turn enemies into allies, summon sharp thorns, heal your allies, and soar high above the battlefield with these Unique Heroes.

This is add-on content. A Battle Box and/or Master Set is required for gameplay.

Features

Activate multiple beasts per turn, strike at range or up close, and fly across the battlefield with Haluchott, Corruptor of Beasts.
Marachott, Mind Whisperer grants players the powerful ability to turn enemies into allies using mind control.
Control the battlefield using Vrono the Bramblebooth sharp thorns that punish enemy movement.
Harness the power of the forest to attack with sharp thorns, support your armies’ beasts, and heal your allies with Girusha, Grove Keeper.
The swift and supportive Chana the Zenithwing heals nearby allies and dodges danger with stealth flying.
These Heroes synergize with the figures like Ewashia, Master of Tides and Onshu the Welkineye from the Battle for the Wellspring Battle Box and Raakchott, Steward of Death from the Age of Annihilation Master Set.

—description from the publisher
